PN CN213475929-U
TI Biological fermentation system for loquat, has cover plate whose bottom is connected with exhaust hydraulic cylinder whose bottom is connected with elastic plate, where top of elastic plate is connected with spring.
AU ZHANG H
   ZHANG Y
   ZHANG X
   YANG Q
AE HONGHE YUNSHANGTITIAN FOOD CO LTD (HONG-Non-standard)
GA 202173649L
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model belongs to the mechanical technical field, especially a biological fermentation system of loquat, comprising a base, the bottom of the base is connected with a supporting leg; the bottom of the supporting leg is connected with a wheel; the top of the base is connected with a cooling box; the inner wall of the cooling box is connected with a water pump; the top of the water pump is connected with a cooling pipe; the top of the base is connected with a drain hydraulic cylinder; one end of the drain hydraulic cylinder is connected with a sliding block; the side of the sliding block is connected with a baffle; the top of the base is connected with a motor. the motor stirring fan the raw material to slowly stir, the solution concentration and temperature are consistent, ensuring the normal fermentation; the cooling liquid in the cooling tank under the action of the water pump evaporating heat in the cooling pipe to take away the high temperature generated by fermentation, keeping the normal growth of the microorganism; carbon dioxide is discharged from the exhaust port, reducing the pressure in the fermentation barrel, so as to avoid the explosion phenomenon generated by fermentation.

PN CN213407558-U
TI Multifunctional flame-retardant sponge, has air bag whose inner part is connected with inner part of box body through connecting pipe, where box body is located at two sides of paraffin clapboard, respectively.
AU GU L
AE YANGZHOU ZHONGTIAN SPONGE CO LTD (YANG-Non-standard)
GA 202173313D
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model relates to the technical field of sponge, and claims a multifunctional flame-retardant sponge, comprising a pad-shaped sponge main body; the front surface of the sponge main body is provided with a plurality of hemispherical bumps; and the back of the sponge main body is provided with an adhesive layer; the sponge main body is embedded with a plurality of box body matched with the hemispherical convex position; and the top wall of the box body is connected with a plate body through connecting pipe; the inner part of the hemispherical bump is a hollow structure; and the plate body is located in the hollow part of the hemispherical bump. The multifunctional flame-retardant sponge; the sponge main body is embedded with a box body with a paraffin clapboard; the paraffin clapboard will melt under the high temperature of the fire; the aluminium sulphate water solution and sodium bicarbonate water solution are fused to generate carbon dioxide; and the foaming agent is combined to form foam; the surface of the sponge is attached to the sponge; It has good covering effect and cooling effect; it can isolate the inflammable substance and oxygen; it has the function of inflaming retarding and extinguishing fire.
DC P35 (Life-saving, fire-fighting (A62).)
MC P35-C01A3; P35-C03

The bridge guardrail structure is composed of a bracket, a pre-embedded pile and a cylinder; the cross rod and the environment beautifying mechanism; firstly embedding the embedded pile at the bottom end of the bracket in the inner part of the base surface; inserting the cross rod into the inner part of the cylinder for mounting; at this time, hanging the hook and the cross rod at the top end; then finishing the whole installation of the guardrail structure; in the using process, planting green plant in the basin can obviously improve the beauty of the whole guardrail, at the same time, photosynthesis of green plant can absorb carbon dioxide in the tail gas, and has adsorption effect on the dust in the tail gas, and also can reduce noise, so that the functionality of the guardrail structure is improved.

The                first membrane module divides the culture chamber                into a first culture chamber (205) and a second                culture chamber (206). The first membrane module is                used to intercept and/or penetrate portion of the                components in the culture solution. A pre-mixing                chamber (101) delivers culture solution to the                first culture chamber. The second culture chamber                is in communication with the pre-mixing chamber.                The culture solution in the second culture chamber,                flows back into the premix chamber. The first                culture chamber or the second culture chamber is                configured for holding cultures, and culturing the                cultures.
   USE - In vitro life-sustaining perfusion culture                system.
   ADVANTAGE - The consumption of nutrient solution, is                reduced, and the cost of cultivation, is reduced.                The dissolved oxygen concentration makes the system                get rid of the constraints of the carbon dioxide                incubator, and the scale of cultivation can be                expanded arbitrarily.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an in                vitro life-sustaining perfusion culture system                control method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic diagram of an                in vitro life-sustaining perfusion culture                system.

PN CN112798558-A; CN214794456-U
TI Automatic focusing laser gas telemetry device has laser light source set with multiple single wavelength telemetry laser sources and distance measuring laser source, and microprocessor control unit for controlling telemetry laser source.
AU LIU Z
   FAN L
   LIU Y
AE NANYANG INST TECHNOLOGY (UNYI-C)
GA 202156506M
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a microprocessor control unit provided with an automatic focusing unit, a control and signal processing unit, a signal demodulation unit and a microprocessor. A laser light source is provided with multiple single wavelength telemetry laser sources and a distance measuring laser source. The microprocessor control unit controls the telemetry laser source through a multi-path current switch. The automatic focusing unit is provided with a driving motor and an adjusting diaphragm. The microprocessor control unit calculates the gas concentration in sight distance and the whole gas concentration in three-dimensional space area. The wavelength of telemetry laser source comprises central wavelength corresponding to characteristic absorption peak value of methane (CH4), carbon dioxide (CO2), carbon monoxide (CO), hydrogen sulfide (H2S), nitrogen dioxide (NO2), ethylene (C2H4), acetylene (C2H2), and ammonia (NH3).
   USE - Used as automatic focusing laser gas telemetry device.
   ADVANTAGE - The device: utilizes semiconductor laser for directly modulating injected current as emitted measuring light source, coaxial structure of transmitting and receiving and automatic focusing, and harmonic detection for demodulating and processing gas absorption signal; and emits laser in different direction through laser transmitter to monitor gas concentration and distance.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the automatic focusing laser gas telemetry device.
DC S03 (Scientific Instrumentation); T01 (Digital Computers)
MC S03-E04A5; T01-J08A
IP G01N-021/01; G01N-021/39

PN CN112768559-A
TI Method for preparing solar cell grid lines based on screen printing, involves performing surface treatment to form coupling coating on surface of sheet, using screen printing, printing metal paste, and sintering to form battery grid lines.
AU LI D
   LIN Y
   LU L
   WANG J
AE SHANGHAI ADVANCED RES INST CHINESE ACAD (CAAR-C)
GA 202152536R
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves providing batteries. A first surface treatment is performed to form a coupling medium on the surface of the battery sheet. A second surface treatment is performed to form a coupling coating on the surface of the battery sheet through the coupling medium. The screen printing, printing metal paste and sintering are used to form battery grid lines. The first surface treatment includes oxygen plasma surface treatment or carbon dioxide plasma surface treatment.
   USE - Method for preparing solar cell grid lines of solar cell structure (claimed) based on screen printing.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables improving screen printing effect, reducing width of the battery grid line, battery grid line edge expanding metal slurry residue, dosage of the metal slurry and shading area of the battery grid line, increasing battery light receiving area, improving photoelectric conversion efficiency of the battery, realizing compatible preparation process with the production line and adding corresponding device in the production line to finish production line upgrading and strong applicability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a solar cell structure.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating a method for preparing solar cell grid lines based on screen printing. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - The organosilane comprises chlorosilane (RaSiCl), fluorosilane ((CF3)aSi(OR)b), polysiloxane alkane (R(OSi(R)2)nCH3), dealcoholized siloxane (RaSi(OR)b).

PN CN112707424-A; WO2022156076-A1
TI Producing alumina by slag metallurgy technology            comprises adding high calcium oxide metallurgical slag,            adding e.g. aluminum ore, blowing oxidizing gas,            separating slag iron, steel-making, adding sodium            carbonate solution and calcining.
AU ZHANG L
   ZHANG Y
   ZOU X
   ZHANG W
AE UNIV NORTHEASTERN (UNEN-C)
   UNIV NORTHEASTERN (UNEN-C)
GA 2021512607
AB    NOVELTY - Producing alumina by slag metallurgy                technology comprises (a) adding the high calcium                oxide metallurgical slag, adding into the melting                reactor, forming reaction slag, adding aluminum ore                and reducing agent, keeping the molten state,                obtaining molten slag, blowing oxidizing gas into                the molten slag, or oxidizing gas and nitrogen                mixed gas, performing slag bath melting reduction,                (b) melting and reducing slag bath, separating slag                iron, obtaining lower molten iron, (c) directly                using the lower layer molten iron as steel-making                raw material, steel-making, obtaining molten steel                slag, directly returning to step (a) into the                melting reactor and (d) cooling the upper layer                calcium aluminate slag, adding sodium carbonate                solution into the self-powdered calcium aluminate                slag to leach, obtaining sodium aluminate solution                and leaching residue, introducing carbon dioxide in                the sodium aluminate solution to obtain aluminum                hydroxide and calcining to obtain alumina.
   USE - The method is useful for producing alumina by                slag metallurgy technology.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has strong raw material                adaptability, low energy consumption, low                consumption of flux calcium oxide, multi-component                recovery, no solid waste discharge, short process,                low cost, environmentally friendly, and realizes                high calcium oxide metallurgical slag and aluminum                ore, recycling and production of aluminum                components.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The reducing agent is                  at least one of anthracite, bituminous coal,                  lignite, pulverized coal, natural gas, coal-bed                  methane, and coal gas. The reducing agent gas is                  nitrogen and/or oxidizing gas. The oxidizing gas                  is at least one of air, oxygen-enriched air,                  oxygen, and carbon dioxide. The molten iron is                  one of ordinary molten iron, vanadium-containing                  molten iron, niobium-containing molten iron, and                  vanadium-containing chromium-containing molten                  iron. The slag is one of vanadium slag, niobium                  slag, and vanadium-chromium slag. The sodium                  carbonate solution is a solution with a mass                  concentration of 70-110 g/l. The liquid-solid                  ratio of sodium carbonate solution and                  self-powdering calcium aluminate slag is (3-11)                  ml: 1 g.
DC E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.); M25 (Production and refining of metals other than iron - including ore treatment, extraction, working up scrap, obtaining specific metals, control testing methods (C22B).); M24 (Metallurgy of iron and steel - including manufacture and processing, treatment of steel melts and changing the physical properties of iron and steel, control/testing methods, blast furnaces and converters. Metallurgical coking processes (C21, C10B).)

PT P
PN WO2021057022-A1; CN112569172-A; TW202114634-A; TW764304-B1
TI Preparing pearl preparation product useful in anti-aging product, preferably anti-aging cosmetic or anti-aging health care product comprises e.g. crushing pearls with airflow crusher to obtain ultra-fine pearl powder.
AU WANG J
   YANG A
   MO J
   ZHANG L
AE OSM BIOLOGY CO LTD (OSMB-Non-standard)
   OSMUN BIOLOGICAL CO LTD (OSMU-Non-standard)
GA 202133029J
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing pearl preparation product comprises (i) crushing the pearls with airflow crusher to obtain ultra-fine pearl powder with a particle size of less than 6 mu m, preferably 0.3-1 mu m; (ii) mixing pearl powder with deionized water to obtain a mixed solution; (iii) passing excess carbon dioxide into the bottom of the mixed solution, or adding weak acid solution to dissolve the mixed solution; (iv) sterilizing solution obtained in step (iii) and inoculating microbial expansion broth for fermentation culture; and (v) freeze-drying fermentation broth obtained in step (iv) to obtain freeze-dried powder of pearl preparation product.
   USE - The pearl preparation product or composition is useful for preparing an anti-aging product, preferably anti-aging cosmetic or an anti-aging health care product (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The pearl preparation has a very significant anti-aging ability, and acts on mitochondria by means of a PI3K/AKT pathway to play an anti-aging effect; method is capable of scattering the false agglomeration of pearl powder, extracts acidic solution components in pearls in a gentle and non-destructive manner through weak acid dissolution, and does not need to add preservative.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) pearl preparation product prepared by the above mentioned method; and
   (2) composition, comprising pearl preparation product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: In the step (iv), microbial expansion culture solution accounts for 1-10 vol.% of the solution to be connected, preferably microorganisms used is at least one of Saccharomyces cerevisiae, grape juice yeast, lactic acid bacteria, Bacillus subtilis, and Bacillus licheniformis, preferably culture condition of fermentation tank is 300-500 rotations/minute, temperature is 25-38 degrees C, ventilation for 3-5 l/minutes and culture time is 24-72 hours.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method before step (i) comprises cleaning, grinding, and spray drying the pearls. In the step (ii), mixing mass ratio of the pearl powder and the deionized water is 1:5-20. In the step (v), obtaining sterile filtrate by filter press filtration after the fermentation broth is inactivated, and mixing sterile broth and freeze-dried protective agent at a volume/mass ratio of 100:0.5-2, in the above volume/mass ratio, unit of volume is ml, and unit of mass is gram, using liquid nitrogen or pre-freezing at -70 to 85 degrees C for 3-10 hours, and then placing in a vacuum freeze-drying bin for 24-72 hours. The method after step (v), further comprises storing freeze-dried powder in a brown vial or a vacuum-packed plastic bag, preferably at 1-10 degrees C. Preferred Components: In the step (iii), volume ratio of the weak acid solution and the mixed solution is 1:4-10, preferably 1:5, weak acid solution is at least one of acetic acid, citric acid, lactic acid, and malic acid.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC D21 (Preparations for dental or toilet purposes - including filling alloys, compositions for dentures or dental impressions, anti-caries chewing gum, plaque disclosing compositions, toothpastes, cosmetics, shampoos, topical anti-sunburn compositions and toilet soaps (A61K).); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); A96 (Medical, dental, veterinary, cosmetic.)

PT P
PN CN112551571-A
TI Preparing ultra-thin nano-sheet micro-unit hollow            indium zinc sulfide nano-cage for photocatalysis, by            preparing ZIF-8 polyhedron, reacting, dispersing,            adding zinc nitrate hexahydrate and reacting.
AU GAO W
   PENG J
   ZENG S
   LU F
   SONG Y
   CHEN Y
AE UNIV SHANTOU (USHT-C)
GA 202134386K
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation method of ultra-thin nano-sheet                micro-unit hollow indium zinc sulfide nano-cage                involves (i) preparing ZIF-8 polyhedron, (ii)                reacting ZIF-8 polyhedron with tannic acid to                synthesize a core-shell ZIF-8 polyhedron, and (iii)                dispersing the core-shell ZIF-8 polyhedron into                solution to obtain a core-shell ZIF-8 suspension,                adding zinc nitrate hexahydrate and indium(III)                chloride tetrahydrate, stirring, adding                thioacetamide, continuing to stir, putting into                reaction kettle, carrying out temperature-rising                reaction to obtain precipitate, washing and drying                to obtain the product.
   USE - The method is useful for ultra-thin nano-sheet                micro-unit hollow indium zinc sulfide nano-cage for                photocatalysis or photoelectrochemical sensing (all                claimed), photocatalytic degradation, carbon                dioxide reduction, hydrogen production,                photosynthesis and photoelectric sensing.
   ADVANTAGE - The nano-cage is ultra-thin nano-sheet                micro-unit nano-cage, and has large specific                surface area, high light utilization efficiency,                thin shell and nanosheets. The method eliminates                the tedious process of removing the template, is                extremely easy to operate, and has short reaction                time.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) the hollow indium zinc sulfide nanocage                obtained by the above method; and
   (2) the use of the hollow indium zinc sulfide                nanocage for photocatalysis or photoelectrochemical                sensing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (iii) involves                  adding core-shell ZIF-8 to a mixed solution of                  ethanol and glycerol, ultrasonically dispersing                  to obtain core-shell ZIF-8 suspension,                  magnetically stirring ethanol solution of zinc                  nitrate hexahydrate and indium(III) chloride                  tetrahydrate for 10 minutes, adding ethanol                  solution of thioacetamide, magnetically stirring                  for 30 minutes, putting into a PTFE reactor, and                  gradually raising the temperature to 150-180                  degrees C, reacting for 1-6 hours, and raising                  the temperature at a rate of 5 degrees C/hour,                  washing the obtained light yellow precipitate 3                  times with ethanol, and drying in a vacuum                  environment at 60 degrees C for 12 hours to                  obtain the product, where the volume ratio of the                  total amount of ethanol to the total amount of                  glycerol is 4:1. The step (ii) involves                  dispersing the ZIF-8 polyhedron in a tannic acid                  ethanol solution, magnetically stirring to react,                  and performing centrifugal washing after the                  reaction is completed to obtain core-shell-shaped                  ZIF-polyhedron. The ZIF-8 polyhedron is a rhombus                  dodecahedron, and the step (i) involves                  configuring zinc nitrate hexahydrate into a 0.08                  mol/l methanol solution, and 2-methylimidazole                  into a 0.16 mol/l methanol solution, adding                  methanol solution of 2-methylimidazole and                  methanol solution of zinc nitrate hexahydrate in                  a volume ratio of 1:1 under magnetic stirring,                  leaving still and aging for 12 hours,                  centrifuging and washing to obtain a ZIF-8                  rhombic dodecahedron with a regular shape.                  Preferred Conditions: The reaction time is 2                  hours. The step of magnetic stirring to react is                  carried out at 20-30 degrees C for 30 minutes.                  The aging temperature is 20-30 degrees C. The                  photocatalysis comprises photodegradation, carbon                  dioxide reduction, hydrogen production, and/or                  photosynthesis. The photoelectrochemical sensing                  includes quantitative detection of lincomycin.                  Preferred Composition: The added amount of the                  core-shell ZIF-8 is 20-60 mg, and the amount of                  glycerol is 2-8 ml, the molar ratio of zinc                  nitrate hexahydrate, indium(III) chloride                  tetrahydrate and thioacetamide is 1:1:2. The                  concentration of the tannic acid ethanol solution                  is 5 g/l. The molar ratio of zinc nitrate                  hexahydrate and 2-methylimidazole is 1:2.

PN CN112537995-A; CN112537995-B
TI Recovering ethane from high-pressure natural gas useful for ethane recovery device comprises e.g. cooling raw material gas by main cold box and enters cryogenic separator for gas-liquid separation, and separating part or all of liquid phase.
AU JIANG H
   WANG J
   HUANG J
   LI H
   YANG T
AE UNIV SOUTHWEST PETROLEUM (USWP-C)
GA 2021314320
AB    NOVELTY - Recovering ethane from high-pressure natural gas comprises cooling raw material gas by main cold box and enters cryogenic separator for gas-liquid separation, separating part or all of liquid phase by cryogenic separator enters middle and lower part of demethanizer after being pressure-regulated, dividing gas phase separated by cryogenic separator into two streams, gas phase enters bottom of high-pressure absorption tower after cooling down at expansion end of expansion unit, other gaseous phase passes through cold box for heat exchange and temperature reduction, and enters middle of high-pressure absorption tower after adjusting pressure, dividing bottom liquid phase of high-pressure absorption tower into two streams, One liquid phase enters subcooled cold box to cool down and then enters middle and upper part of demethanizer, and other liquid phase enters top of demethanizer after pressure adjustment.
   USE - The method is useful for useful for ethane recovery device with the feed gas pressure higher than 6Mpa.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: increases the gas phase discharge pressure at the top of the high-pressure absorption tower; reduces the compression work of the external gas; increases the carbon dioxide blockage margin, and the demethanizer operates at a lower pressure; and saves energy.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further comprises using gas phase at top of high-pressure absorption tower as external gas to exchange heat with subcooling cold box and main cold box and passing through booster end of expansion unit, pressurizing external compressor and sending out, transferring part of external gas to top of high-pressure absorption tower after pressure is adjusted through main cold box and super-cooled cold box after heat exchange and temperature reduction, pressurizing gas phase at top of demethanizer by top compressor, reducing temperature of subcooling box, and adjusting pressure to enter upper middle of high-pressure absorption tower, liquid phase at bottom of demethanizer enters subsequent fractionation unit, independently setting pressures of high-pressure absorption tower and demethanizer, pressure of high pressure absorption tower is 0.6-1 MPa higher than pressure of demethanizer. The return flow of the external gas transmission accounts for 10-20% of the total external gas transmission. After the main cold box and the supercooling cold box exchange heat and supercool, the pressure is adjusted and enters the top of the high-pressure absorption tower. When the carbon dioxide freeze-up margin is small, the liquid phase separated by the cryogenic separator is divided into two parts, part of the liquid phase (30-50%) is mixed with a gas phase at the top of the cryogenic separator, entering the subcooling cold box for heat exchange and subcooling, the pressure will be adjusted to enter the middle of the high pressure absorption tower, and the remaining liquid phase will be adjusted to enter the lower part of the high pressure absorption tower top gas phase of the demethanizer is pressurized to 4-4.5 MPa by the top compressor, then passes through the cold box for heat exchange and supercooling, and then the pressure is adjusted to enter the upper part of the high pressure absorption tower 10-20% of the gas phase at the top of the cryogenic separator, passing through the cold box, heat exchange and supercooling, the pressure will enter the middle and upper part of the high-pressure absorption tower. 80-90% of the gas phase at the top of the cryogenic separator enters the middle and lower part of the high pressure absorption tower after the pressure drop at the expansion end of the expansion unit. Both the main cold box and the subcooled cold box adopt multi-strand plate-fin heat exchangers. Two heat flows and five cold flows, three heat flows and two cold flows are respectively integrated in the main cold box and the supercooled cold box. The refrigeration process uses a combination of propane refrigerant refrigeration and expander refrigeration. The propane refrigeration process provides refrigeration for the main cold box and subsequent fractionation units.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); Q75 (Refrigeration, liquefaction (F25))

TI Usage method for solid electrolyte sensor detecting density of gas in industrial furnace, involves utilizing zirconia containing platinum wires as lead wires in state of being connected to outer and inner electrodes.
AU TSUNEYOSHI T
AE TOKYO YOGYO KK (TOLY-C)
GA 202126775F
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves providing a sensor device (10) which consists of a solid electrolyte. An outer electrode (21) is provided in the surface of sensor device and arranged in an outer space (S1). An inner electrode (22) is provided in the surface of sensor device and arranged in an inner space (S2). The outer space is utilized as the measurement space. The zirconia containing platinum wires are utilized as the lead wires (41,42) connected to the outer and inner electrodes. The EMF generated in between the electrodes due to ion conduction in the solid electrolyte is measured through the lead wires.
   USE - Usage method for solid electrolyte sensor (claimed) detecting density of gas in industrial furnace used in glass melting during manufacture of glass fiber. Uses include but are not limited to detection of density of hydrogen, oxygen, carbon dioxide, and water vapor.
   ADVANTAGE - The generation of EMF is satisfactorily detected over a long time period by suppressing the disconnection of lead wire at high temperature.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the longitudinal cross-sectional views of a solid electrolyte sensor.

   Outer electrode (21)
   Inner electrode (22)
   Lead wires (41,42)

   Inner space (S2)

PN CN112499586-A; CN112499586-B
TI Realizing steam reforming hydrogen production by            heating and realizing steam reforming by            water-intrusion stratum heating comprises e.g.            selecting production wells and drilling number of side            boreholes from wellbore to surrounding.
AU LIU H
   DAI P
   YAO D
   GUO P
   DU J
   WANG Z
   LI Q
   TUMENGGELE
AE UNIV SOUTHWEST PETROLEUM (USWP-C)
GA 2021312247
AB    NOVELTY - Realizing steam reforming hydrogen production                by heating and realizing steam reforming by                water-intrusion stratum heating comprises (1)                selecting production wells and drilling number of                side boreholes from the wellbore to surrounding in                corresponding target zone, (2) implanting electric                heating rods into side boreholes and introducing                catalysts for natural gas steam reforming, and (3)                vacuum extracting to extract natural gas from gas                reservoir, opening switch of ground distributor to                supply power to electric heating rod when water                content of fluid at outlet of production well                reaches 30%, establishing high-temperature wall                around production well bore of the target zone, and                at this time, existing liquid water in                high-temperature wall and subsequent flowing into                high-temperature wall quickly heats up and                vaporizing into natural gas phase, water vapor and                high temperature natural gas realize steam                reforming reaction under action of high temperature                wall and catalyst.
   USE - The method is useful for realizing steam                reforming hydrogen production by heating and                realizing steam reforming by water-intrusion                stratum heating.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: eliminates the harm caused by the                water invasion of the gas reservoir; obtains                high-purity hydrogen at the wellhead by                decarbonizing; and directly injects carbon dioxide                into the gas reservoir, buries or uses for other                purposes through the remote injection well.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further                  comprises generating carbon dioxide and hydrogen,                  inhibiting or eliminating water lock and wellbore                  flooding in near-well zone, and improving gas                  reservoir recovery, (4) extracting mixed gas                  containing carbon dioxide and hydrogen from the                  production well and entering the wellhead                  decarbonization device, and (5) using gas storage                  tank to collect the purified hydrogen,                  andinjecting the separated carbon dioxide from                  the gas injection well into the gas reservoir to                  displace natural gas or be directly stored.                  Preferred Components: The method uses                  nickel/aluminum oxide alcohol amine                  solution.

PN DE102019211942-A1; WO2021023435-A1
TI Electrochemically converting feed gas at gas diffusion electrode, comprises e.g. determining differential pressure, modulating differential pressure.
AU HANEBUTH M
   HARTMANN D
   KRAUSE R
   MAGORI E
   PASTUSIAK R
   WIESNER-FLEISCHER K
AE SIEMENS AG (SIEI-C)
GA 202114697S
AB    NOVELTY - Electrochemically converting feed gas at a gas diffusion electrode (GDK), comprises determining the differential pressure, modulating the differential pressure, where at least a distinction is made between an operating differential pressure and a regeneration differential pressure and the regeneration differential pressure is lower than the operating differential pressure.
   USE - The method is useful for electrochemically converting feed gas at a gas diffusion electrode.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has reduced maintenance effort and ensures operation with optimal product yield close to the limit of salinization.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for device for the electrochemical conversion of a feed gas on a gas diffusion electrode, comprising at least one electrolysis cell, one electrolyte line, at least one pump, an anode and a cathode, the cathode is designed as a gas diffusion electrode (GDK), arranged in a cathode space which is divided by the cathode into a cathode gas space (I) and a catholyte space (II), comprising a first gas inlet into the cathode gas space (I) for a feed gas containing carbon dioxide, and comprising a differential pressure sensor (1) for determining the pressure difference between the cathode gas space (I) and catholyte space (II) and a differential pressure control unit and/or regulating unit (5) for modulating the differential pressure.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the device.
   Differential pressure sensor (1)
   Water column container (2)
   Electrolyte reservoir (3)
   Pump (4)
   Differential pressure control unit (5)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The regeneration differential pressure is set as a function of time after a predefinable operating time. The differential pressure control unit (5) is connected to at least one data line via which signals can be sent to a pump (4). The device comprises at least one actuator having a water column container (2) with a connecting line between the water column container and the gas outlet of the cathode gas space. The differential pressure control unit (5) is designed to set at least one operating differential pressure and a regeneration differential pressure, the regeneration differential pressure being less than the operating differential pressure. The device comprises catholyte feed line and anolyte feed line, separator (SEP) which separates the cathode compartment (I/II) and anode compartment (III) from one another, and an electrolyte reservoir (3). The cathode gas space (I) is designed as a pressure chamber. The cathode gas space (I) is designed as a gas gap. The device comprises a controllable valve for adjusting the flow rate of the feed gas, which valve is connected to the differential pressure control unit (5) via at least one data line. Preferred Method: The method further comprises determining the Faraday efficiency by measuring the electrolysis yield, where the regeneration differential pressure is adjusted after the electrolysis yield falls below a limit value, and detecting the time intervals between the regeneration processes triggered and the determination of an ideal time interval for setting the regeneration differential pressure. The regeneration differential pressure is set after a presettable pressure value has been exceeded on the gas side of the cathode (GDK).
DC J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).); X25 (Industrial Electric Equipment)

PT P
PN CN112225215-A
TI Preparing high specific surface area and multi-stage porous hollow carbon microspheres using lignin raw material, by adding lignin and sulfite to water, stirring, heating lignin microspheres and mixing carbonized microspheres and activator.
AU WU S
   LIU S
   CHENG H
   WEI W
   ZHANG F
AE UNIV SOUTH CHINA TECHNOLOGY (UYSC-C)
GA 202109197U
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing high specific surface area and multi-stage porous hollow carbon microspheres using lignin as a raw material, involves (i) adding lignin and sulfite to water, heating up and stirring to obtain a mixed solution, adjusting the pH of the mixed solution to 7-9 with acid solution, carbon dioxide gas or sulfite, and drying to obtain lignin micro ball, (ii) heating the lignin microspheres under an inert atmosphere for carbonization treatment to obtain carbonized microspheres, (iii) mixing the carbonized microspheres and activator and adding it to water, uniformly shaking and mixing, leaving still, and drying to obtain a solid mixture of carbonized microspheres and activator, and (iv) heating the solid mixture of carbonized microspheres and activator under an inert atmosphere for activation treatment to obtain activated carbon microspheres, washing the filtrate with water until the filtrate is neutral and drying to obtain the product.
   USE - The method is used for preparing high specific surface area and multi-stage porous hollow carbon microspheres using lignin as a raw material, which is used in electronics and chemical industries.
   ADVANTAGE - The method ensures excellent pore structure and surface chemical, is environmentally-friendly, has simple preparation process, convenient operation, low cost and excellent industrial application value.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (i), the lignin comprises sodium lignosulfonate and/or alkali lignin, and the sulfite comprises sodium sulfite and/or potassium sulfite. In step (iii), the activator comprises potassium hydroxide and/or potassium carbonate solid. The inert atmosphere in step (ii) and step (iv) comprises nitrogen, argon and/or mixed gas of both, and the flow rate of the inert atmosphere is 200-500 ml/minutes. Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of the lignin to sulfite is 5:1-1:1. In the mixed liquid, the total solid content of lignin and sulfite is 30-36%. In step (iii), the mass ratio of the carbonized microspheres to activator is 1:1-1:4. Preferred Conditions: In step (i), the temperature of the stirring treatment is 120-220 degrees C, and the time of the stirring treatment is 2-6 hours. In step (i), the drying method is spray drying, the air inlet temperature of the spray drying is 250-350 degrees C, the air outlet temperature is 100-150 degrees C, the evaporation of water per hour is 1.5-2.5 tons, and the moisture content of the lignin microspheres is less than 6%. In step (ii), the rate of heating is 5-10 degrees C/minutes, the temperature of the carbonization treatment is 400-500 degrees C, and the time of the carbonization treatment is 50-80 minutes. In step (iii), the leaving still time is 2-4 hours. In step (iv), the rate of temperature increase is 5-20 degrees C/minutes, the temperature of the activation treatment is 700-900 degrees C, and the time of the activation treatment is 40-100 minutes.
DC J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)
MC J04-X
IP C01B-032/324; C01B-032/348

AU M NOH M G B

   NOH M M G

   PETRONAS PETROLIAM NASIONAL BERHAD (PETR-Non-standard)

GA 2021064580
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacturing calcium hydroxide solution                involves adding water and calcium oxide to a                slaking reactor (5) comprising stirring element (7)                at an upper end, and one or multiple outlets. The                water and calcium oxide are stirred such that a                concentration gradient of calcium ions can be                formed within the reactor. An outlet is selected to                collect the resultant calcium hydroxide solution                depending on the concentration of calcium ions                required.
   USE - Method for manufacturing calcium hydroxide                solution.
   ADVANTAGE - The method manufactures the calcium hydroxide                solution in a simple, cost-effective and                eco-friendly manner with increased solubility of                the calcium oxide, and minimized any shut down time                during continuous flow.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system                for sequestering carbon, which comprises a slaking                reactor comprising stirring element at an upper end                for forming a concentration gradient of calcium                ions within the slaking reactor when water and                calcium oxide are added, and a carbonation reactor                for receiving a solution from an outlet of the                slaking reactor, where the carbonation reactor                comprises an element for introducing supercritical                carbon dioxide and an injector nozzle for spraying                the solution.

PN CN112108145-A
TI Alumina-supported iridium cluster catalyst useful for formaldehyde elimination reaction, and NJ-DT-3 propellant catalytic decomposition reaction with high reaction rate comprises alumina as carrier, and noble metal iridium active component.
AU WANG X
   SUN X
   LIN J
   ZHANG T
   LV F
   XIA L
   LI T
   ZHAO X
   WU H
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 2020C9942K
AB    NOVELTY - Alumina-supported iridium cluster catalyst comprises alumina as a carrier, and noble metal iridium an active component. The iridium content is 0.1-5%, preferably 0.5-2% of the total mass of the catalyst. The noble metal iridium is highly dispersed on the alumina carrier in the form of clusters, where the cluster size is 0.5-5 nm, preferably 0.8-2 nm.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for formaldehyde elimination reaction, and NJ-DT-3 propellant catalytic decomposition reaction with high reaction rate (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst: shows excellent catalytic activity; and completely catalyzes and oxidizes high concentration formaldehyde (180 ppm) into carbon dioxide and water at room temperature. The iridium cluster of the catalyst serves as the only catalytic active center.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the catalyst, comprising using a two-step synthesis method of first cluster colloid formation and subsequent deposition and loading, where the specific process comprising firstly obtaining iridium sol as a precursor by a lower alcohol reduction method, dropping into the alumina carrier lower carbon alcohol suspension, stirring, allowing to stand, aging, depositing the nanoclusters in the iridium sol on the carrier alumina, filtering, washing, and drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The precursor iridium sol by the lower carbon alcohol reduction method is prepared by dissolving the noble metal chloroiridic acid in 50-100 ml lower alcohol to prepare a solution, where the concentration of metal iridium ions in the solution is 3.9-39 mmol/l, adding 50-100 ml sodium hydroxide and/or potassium hydroxide lower alcohol solution with a concentration of 0.03-0.75 mol/l to the above solution, stirring at room temperature for 0.5-3 hours, uniformly mixing, moving into oil bath at 100-160 degrees C, carrying out the reaction under the protection of argon or nitrogen atmosphere for 1-5 hours to obtain iridium cluster sol. The stirring is carried out at 500-1000 revolutions/minute, the reaction is carried out for 2-6 hours, the aging is carried out at 25-80 degrees C for aging 1-5 hours. The drying of the catalyst is carried out at 60-100 degrees C for 12-48 hours. Preferred Components: The lower alcohol comprises methanol, glycol, propanetriol, and/or 1,4-butanediol. The mass concentration of the alumina carrier lower carbon alcohol suspension is 2-10 g/l. The alumina particle size used is 10-12 nm. The raw material gas composition comprises 30-300 ppm formaldehyde, 5-40 vol.% oxygen, helium balance gas, the relative air humidity is 0-75%, the space velocity is 6x 103-6x 105 ml g/cat/hour, using atmospheric fixed bed reactor, and the test temperature at 20-80 degrees C, preferably room temperature.

GA 2020B3808F
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon monoxide automatically monitoring and                removal involves arranging carbon monoxide sensor                at corner of the working surface of the return air                tunnel to realize the automatic monitoring of                carbon monoxide. The monitoring data is connected                to the display through the data line to observe the                relevant data at the fan. The control circuit is                used to switch the fan on and off linked with the                concentration of carbon monoxide. The fan will be                automatically turned on when the upper corner                concentration is detected to exceed the limit. The                gas collecting hoods are arranged in the upper                corner to connect the carbon monoxide reaction                chamber and the fan through the air pipe. The                corresponding particulate platinum-palladium alloy                catalysts are arranged in the reaction chamber to                form a porous medium under action of the catalyst,                where carbon monoxide is oxidized to carbon                dioxide, which is finally discharged through a fan                and a gas discharge pipeline.

PT P
PN CN111830216-A
TI Quickly predicting swelling of edible vinegar involves putting vinegar product into a sample bottle containing culture medium, performing short-term constant temperature incubation, and using a gas detection device to detect change.
AU WANG D
   WANG C
   HAO J
   LUAN C
AE CHINA NAT RES INST FOOD & FERMENTATION I (POYG-C)
GA 2020A75550
AB    NOVELTY - Quickly predicting swelling of edible vinegar involves putting vinegar product into a sample bottle containing a culture medium, performing short-term constant temperature incubation for a period of time, using a gas detection device to detect change of headspace gas composition in sample bottle, and then judging gasification of sample. The sample's swelling is judged, and if gas composition ratio has changed then ratio of carbon dioxide (CO2) has increased significantly, gas component is produced and if two meets one of them then it is predicted that product has risk of swelling.
   USE - Method for quickly predicting swelling of edible vinegar used in solid and semi-solid food, such as milk and dairy product, grain and grain product, meat and meat product, aquatic product, condiment and beverage (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to quickly predict swelling of edible vinegar, which has simple operation, fast prediction and accurate result, quickly determines that there is a risk of swelling during shelf life of vinegar product, is applied to rapid prediction of gas swelling in solid, semi-solid and liquid food products, which is good for reducing food safety risks of enterprises and ensuring safety and quality of product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The culture medium is dissolved in pure water, pH is adjusted to 4.0-8.0, sterilized at 115-121 degrees C under steam for 10-20 minutes, and cooled to room temperature. The vinegar is put into a sample bottle containing culture medium under aseptic conditions. The amount of vinegar is 10-50%, and culture temperature is 20-45 degrees C. The headspace gas detection device is used to detect headspace in the sample bottle, and data is recorded. The sample is incubated for a period of time, headspace gas composition is tested again, and data is recorded, and then data of two tests are compared and it is predicted that sample swells during the shelf life. The constant temperature culture time range is 24-120 hours. The standard for judging sample's swelling is compared with first prediction, where proportion of gas components has changed and proportion of carbon dioxide has increased by more than 1.5%, compared with first prediction and new gas components are produced, and if it meets condition then product is judged to be swelling. Preferred Components: The headspace gas components of sample bottle includes oxygen, carbon dioxide, hydrogen sulfide and methane. The sample bottle materials contains glass, stainless steel, polyethylene terephthalate, high-density polyethylene, polyethylene, polypropylene and polystyrene.

PT P
PN CN111375871-A
TI Method for welding steel structure with narrow gap cable welding wire, involves performing penetrant inspection on welded joints of base material, and calculating deposition efficiency by measuring penetration depth and width after welding.
AU ZHU D
   GUO L
AE JINGYE HUACHENG TIANJIN STEEL STRUCTURE (JING-Non-standard)
GA 202065089H
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves selecting the cable welding wire specification according to the thickness of the welding steel structure base material and groove width for the center welding wire and peripheral welding wire wound around the center welding wire at a spiral angle. The steel structure base material is polished and derusted. The welding torch is connected to the welding machine, shielding gas and circulating coolant tank. The current and voltage of the welding machine is set according to the specifications of the cable-type welding wire, and current is selected to increase as the diameter of the cable-type welding wire increases during welding. The penetrant inspection is performed on the welded joints of the steel structure base material after 24 hours of welding, and deposition efficiency is calculated by measuring the penetration depth and penetration width after welding. The shielding gas adopts a volume ratio of 82% argon and 18% carbon dioxide gas.
   USE - Method for welding steel structure with narrow gap cable-type welding wire.
   ADVANTAGE - The groove filling amount is small, and cable-type welding wire has high current density and high deposition efficiency under the same welding parameters, and consumption of welding materials is greatly reduced.

PN CN110747164-A
TI Preparing dental pulp stem cell by disinfecting, rinsing dental pulp with physiological saline, cutting dental pulp, digesting with collagenase I and dispase II, centrifuging, washing, and culturing cells using mesenchymal stem cell medium.
AU CHE X
   CHEN Q
AE BEIJING HONGXIN STEM CELL BIOTECHNOLOGY (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 2020116823
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing dental pulp stem cell involves (a) disinfecting, (b) taking the dental pulp, rinsing with physiological saline containing 1000 IU/ml streptomycin and 1000 IU/ml penicillin and cutting the dental pulp into 1 mm3 pieces, (c) adding 0.1-0.5% collagenase I, 0.1-0.5% dispase II and physiological saline as digestive fluid into a digestive tube containing dental pulp, sealing the opening of the digestive tube and digesting at 37 plus minus 5 degrees C while shaking at 145-167 revolutions per minute (rpm) for 15-30 minutes, (d) centrifuging the digestion solution, discarding the supernatant, washing with physiological saline, again centrifuging and discarding the supernatant and (e) culturing the cells using mesenchymal stem cell culture medium containing 5% platelet lysate and 95% basal medium at 37 plus minus 5 degrees C under 5% carbon dioxide.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing dental pulp stem cell (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method effectively obtains a single-cell suspension of dental pulp stem cells or soften dental pulp tissue, which is more conducive to dental pulp stem cells crawling out, improves the rate of dental pulp stem cells acquisition, shortens the cell culture cycle, and guarantees cell quality.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The step (a) involves immersing and soaking teeth in 75% ethanol for 30 seconds-2 minutes under aseptic operation. The step (a) further involves washing the ethanol-soaked teeth with physiological saline containing antibiotics 3-5 times, irradiating the clean room with an UV lamp for 30 minutes, disinfecting and placing the tooth storage bottles, sterilized surgical instrument boxes and 50 ml centrifuge tubes with 75% medical ethanol and placing the sterilized product with physiological saline and 75% medical ethanol into a biosafety cabinet. The step (b) involves wrapping the teeth with a sterile dust-free cloth, clamping the teeth with a vice, and removing the dental pulp with ophthalmic forceps. The step (c) involves adding 0.1% collagenase I, 0.1% dispase II and physiological saline as digestive fluid. The step (d) involves centrifuging at 1000-1500 revolutions per minute (rpm) for 5-8 minutes. The step (e) involves resuspending the pellet in a mesenchymal stem cell culture medium, evenly inoculating the resuspended tissue pieces into a petri dish and culturing in an incubator. In step (e), the platelet lysate comprises 1.8 g/dL globulin, 3.3 g/dL albumin, 128 mg/dL cholesterol, 0.2 mg/dL bilirubin, 208 mg/dL glucose, 80 mg/dL triglyceride, 159 U/l creatinine phosphokinase (CPK), 0.92 mg/dL creatinine, 26 U/l gamma -glutamyltransferase, 43 U/l aspartate aminotransferase (AST), 14.2 meq/l bicarbonate, 140 meq/L sodium, 3.9 mg/dL phosphorus, 61.5 mg/dL calcium, 2.1 mg/dL magnesium, 4.4 meq/l potassium, 109 meq/l chloride and 64 mu g/dL iron. The step (e) further involves performing rehydration, changing medium once in every 3.5 days, and passaging the cells until the degree of cell fusion reaches 30-60%.

Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. PN GB2575630-A; WO2020016559-A1; EP3821108-A1; US2021324730-A1; US11384631-B2; GB2575630-B; EP3821108-B1; EA39438-B1
TI Method for facilitating condition monitoring of            tubing in injection well, involves analyzing monitored            measurement, and using increase in friction loss over            time period as indicator of deterioration in condition            of tubing.
AU SANTARELLI F J
AE GEOMEC ENG LTD (GEOM-Non-standard)
   GEOMEC ENG LTD (GEOM-Non-standard)
   GEOMEC ENG LTD (GEOM-Non-standard)
GA 202009011Q
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves measuring a bottom hole                pressure response and a wellhead pressure response                for a monitored injection rate of fluid in a well.                Friction loss is determined in a tubing (10)                between the bottom hole location and a wellhead                (34) from the wellhead pressure response and the                bottom hole pressure response at monitored                injection rate to provide a monitored measurement                of friction loss in tubing at monitored injection                rate. Monitored measurement is analyzed by making a                comparison with the baseline measurement to                determine change in friction loss over the first                time period. Increase in friction loss over the                first time period is used as an indicator of                deterioration in the condition of the tubing.
   USE - Method for facilitating condition monitoring                of tubing in an injection well to inject liquids                into porous rock formations underground. Uses                include but are not limited to wastewater, brine,                chemicals and carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables reducing friction loss in                tubing automatically at shut-in and monitoring                condition of tubing during life of injection well                and eliminating risk providing fracture network                extending to an aquifer.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic block diagram                of a well.

TI Preparing antioxidant margarine vegetable fat base oil by mixing extremely hydrogenated extruder-pressed soybean oil and cold-pressed corn oil, adding lipase, stirring, sealing, heating, filtering, removing free fatty acid and storing.

   LUO S

   WANG L
   XIA L

   HU Y
   LIU L
   YU D
AE HEILONGJIANG JINGYI TESTING CO LTD (HEIL-Non-standard)
GA 202007830M
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing antioxidant margarine vegetable fat base oil involves mixing extremely hydrogenated extruder-pressed soybean oil and cold-pressed corn oil in ratio of 30:70, 40:60 and 50:50, adding 100 g mixed oil to reactor, adding 6 wt.%/wt.% lipase Lipozyme RMIM (RTM: lipase enzyme) and rotor, stirring to prepare immobilized lipase evenly distributed in fat, sealing reactor, passing 8 MPa carbon dioxide to reach supercritical state, placing reaction kettle in magnetic stirring water bath to heat at 110 degrees C, 120 degrees C and 130 degrees C, adjusting rotation speed to 300 rpm, reacting for 1 hour, 2 hours and 3 hours respectively, removing lipase by suction filtration to obtain transesterified product, removing free fatty acid in transesterified product by secondary molecular distillation, storing in refrigerator at 4 degrees C, and studying fatty acid composition, triglyceride composition and bioactive substances of fat before and after transesterification.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing antioxidant margarine vegetable fat base oil.

TI Manufacture of glass foam used for producing insulating material, involves obtaining dried glass precursor gel comprising nanometer-sized pores and bulk amorphous oxide-based matrix, and heating and expanding dried glass precursor gel.
AU COOPER S
   MUKHEJA V
   GULLINKALA T
AE OWENS BROCKWAY GLASS CONTAINER INC (OWEI-C)
GA 201997047A
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of glass foam involves obtaining a dried glass precursor gel (40) comprising nanometer-sized pores and a bulk amorphous oxide-based matrix having an inorganic network of primary constituent oxides, heating the dried glass precursor gel to a temperature below the melting point of the gel, maintaining the temperature for a time sufficient to expand the dried glass precursor gel, and expanding the dried glass precursor gel to form a glass foam. The primary constituent oxides comprises 30-90 mol% silicon dioxide, and 0.1-25 mol% of alkali oxides (total) (A), 0.1-30 mol% alkaline earth oxides (total) (B), 1-25 mol% boric oxide (C), 5-80 mol% lead oxide (D), or 0.1-15 mol% aluminum oxide (E).
   USE - Manufacture of glass foam used for producing insulating material (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables manufacture of glass foam has excellent durability, and recycleability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for manufacture of insulating material, which involves incorporating the glass foam into the insulating material.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart of method of preparing and using dried glass precursor gel.
   Sand (22)
   Water (22b)
   Hydrothermal reactor (28)
   Mixer (32)
   Drying (36)
   Carbon dioxide source (38)
   Gel (40)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Components: The dried glass precursor gel is free of pre-melted glass, recycled glass, rock, clay, ore, or ash, and carbon dioxide, and comprising bulk amorphous oxide-based matrix is free of polymer. The glass foam having several closed pores. Preferred Process: The glass foam method further involves obtaining wet glass precursor gel comprising bulk amorphous oxide-based matrix is homogeneously chemically mixed and having gel density of less than 2.0 g/cm3, drying the wet glass precursor gel (36) to form the dried glass precursor gel before the step of obtaining a dried glass precursor gel, and expanding the dried glass precursor gel comprises glass foam having foam density is less than gel density. The glass foam method does not using separate flowing agent, and maintaining the temperature and expanding the dried glass precursor gel are simultaneously performed. Preferred Condition: The heating step is performed at 650-850 degrees C for 0.5-6 minutes. Preferred Properties: The dried glass precursor gel has temperature above softening point of the gel. The expanding the dried glass precursor gel to final volume is greater than initial volume of dried glass precursor gel.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The primary constituent oxides contains 60-85 mol% silicon dioxide, 8-18 mol% sodium oxide, and 5-15 mol% calcium oxide and bulk amorphous oxide-based matrix is homogeneously chemically mixed. The dried glass precursor gel comprises 1-2 mol% aluminum oxide. The molar ratio of alkali oxide to alkaline earth oxide to silica is 0.8:0.8:6-1.4:1.3:6.

AB    NOVELTY - Sound absorbing polyurethane material comprises first component and a second component, where the first component is isocyanate derivative, and the second component comprises 60-100 pts. wt. polyether polyol, 7-16 pts. wt. flame retardant, 1.8-3.5 pts. wt. inorganic filler, 2.2-8.6 pts. wt. auxiliary agent, 1.2-3.8pts. wt. foaming agent and 0.5-1.65 pts. wt. catalyst.

The polyether polyol is a phosphorus-nitrogen flame retardant polyether polyol, where the nitrogen content is 3-15 wt.% relative to the mass of the phosphorus-nitrogen flame retardant polyether polyol and phosphorus content is 15-42 wt.% based on the mass of the phosphorus-nitrogen flame retardant polyether polyol. The isocyanate derivative is nitrophenyl isocyanate, 3, 4 - dichlorobenzene isocyanate, phenyl isocyanate and 3,- fluorine benzyl isocyanate.The auxiliary agent is 30-65 pts. wt. ethylene glycol and 16~28 pts. wt. sodium dodecyl benzene sulfonate.The mass ratio of the first component and second component is 1: 1-1.5.

PN CN109867849-A
TI Modified atmosphere storage fresh-keeping bag for dragon fruit comprises e.g. low-density polyethylene resin, porous polymer, biodegradable polymer material, dispersant, organic antibacterial agent, and inorganic antibacterial agent.
AU YOU J
   WU S
   ZHOU M
AE JIANGSU LAIDUN BORUI BIOTECHNOLOGY CO (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019555121
AB    NOVELTY - Modified atmosphere storage fresh-keeping bag for dragon fruit comprises 60-100 pts. wt. low-density polyethylene resin, 10-20 pts. wt. porous polymer, 20-40 pts. wt. biodegradable polymer material, 5-10 pts. wt. plasticizer, 1-3 pts. wt. dispersant, 0.1-1 pts. wt. organic antibacterial agent, 0.1-1 pts. wt. inorganic antibacterial agent, 1-3 pts. wt. ethylene absorption and decomposition agent, 0.5-3 pts. wt. inorganic filler, 0.1-1 pts. wt. antioxidant and 0.1-0.5 pts. wt. antifoaming agent.
   USE - Used as modified atmosphere storage fresh-keeping bag for dragon fruit.
   ADVANTAGE - The bag can exchange gas with the outside world, automatically adjusts content and proportion of carbon dioxide and oxygen compared with common commercially available poly ethylene fresh-keeping bag, decreases rate of rotten fruit of dragon fruit, reduces ethanol content of dragon fruit pulp, and has good antibacterial effect against Escherichia coli and Staphylococcus aureus.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing modified atmosphere storage fresh-keeping bag, comprising (i) preparing mixture, (ii) preparing master batch, (iii) preparing modified atmosphere protective film, and (iv) thermo compressively bonding and cutting the modified atmosphere-preserving film obtained in the step (iii) to obtain modified atmosphere storage bag.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The porous polymer is polytetrafluoroethylene, polyamide, polycarbonate, polyurethane and/or polyethylene terephthalate, and the biodegradable polymer material is polyhydroxyacid, poly-L-lactic acid, poly-DL-lactic acid, polyglycolic acid, polycaprolactone, chitosan and/or gelatin. The organic antibacterial agent is 2-n-octyl-4-isothiazol-3-one, n-butylbenzisothiazolinone, 4,5-dichloro-2-n-octyl-4-isothiazoline-3-ketone, 3-iodo-2-propargylbutylcarbamate, o-phenylphenol, zinc pyridine sulfate and/or polyhexamethylene guanidine hydrochloride, and the inorganic antibacterial agent is zeolite silver-loaded antibacterial agent, zirconium phosphate-loaded zinc antibacterial agent, silicate glass-silver antibacterial agent, silicate glass-loaded zinc antibacterial agent, phosphate glass silver-loaded antibacterial agent and/or hydroxyapatite zinc-loaded antibacterial agents. The ethylene absorption and decomposition agent is potassium permanganate, potassium dichromate, potassium iodide and/or calcium peroxide, and the plasticizer is dioctyl phthalate, diethyl phthalate, diisononyl phthalate, epoxidized soybean oil, epoxy butyl oleate, dioctyl adipate, dioctyl sebacate and/or triisopropylphenyl phosphate. The inorganic filler is calcium carbonate, talc, oya stone, calcite, silicon dioxide, diatomite and/or ceramic powder, the antioxidant is antioxidant 1076 (n-octadecyl- beta -(4-hydroxy-3,5-di-tert-butyl-phenyl)) and/or antioxidant 168 (tris(2,4-ditert-butylphenyl) phosphite), the dispersant is BYK190 dispersing agent and/or TH-904 dispersing agent, and the antifoaming agent is BYK022 antifoaming agent and/or BYK024 antifoaming agent. Preferred Method: The preparation method specifically comprises (1) adding low-density polyethylene resin, porous polymer, biodegradable polymer material, plasticizer, dispersant, organic antibacterial agent, inorganic antibacterial agent, ethylene absorption and decomposition agent, inorganic filler, antioxidant and anti-foaming agent into high-speed mixer, stirring, and uniformly mixing to obtain mixture, (2) extruding the mixture obtained in the step (i), and pelletizing by twin-screw extrusion granulator to obtain master batch, (iii) uniformly mixing master batch obtained in the step (ii), and performing blow molding to form film by single-screw extrusion blow molding machine to obtain modified atmosphere protective film, and (iv) thermo compressively bonding the modified atmosphere protective film obtained in the step (iii), and cutting into modified atmosphere fresh-keeping bag of a desired specification. The preparation method more specifically comprises: (a) adding 60-100 pts. wt. low-density polyethylene resin, 10-20 pts. wt. porous polymer, 20-40 pts. wt. biodegradable polymer material, 5-10 pts. wt. plasticizer, 1-3 pts. wt. dispersant, 0.1-1 pts. wt. organic antibacterial, 0.1-1 pts. wt. inorganic antibacterial agent, 1-3 pts. wt. ethylene absorption and decomposition agent, 0.5-3 pts. wt. inorganic filler, 0.1-1 pts. wt. antioxidant and 0.1-0.5 pts. wt. antifoaming agent into the high-speed mixer, stirring, and mixing evenly to obtain a mixture, (b) extruding the mixture obtained in the step (a), and pelletizing by a twin-screw extrusion granulator to obtain master batch, where the temperature of the twin-screw extrusion granulator is 150-190 degrees C, the feed rate of the hopper is 8-15 m/mi, the rotation speed of the twin screw is 100-150 revolutions/minute, and the pelletizing speed is 3-7 m/minutes, (c) uniformly mixing the master batch obtained in the step (ii), performing blow molding to form film by single-screw extrusion blow molding machine to obtain modified atmosphere protective film, where the temperature of the single-screw extrusion blow molding machine is 150-190 degrees C, and the single screw speed is 40-60 revolutions/minute, and (d) thermo compressively bonding the modified atmosphere protective film obtained in the step (c) at 150-280 degrees C, and cutting into a modified atmosphere fresh-keeping bag of a desired specification. In the step (1), the mixture is stirred at a low speed of 50-100 revolutions/minute for 1-3 minutes, and stirred at a high speed of 200-300 revolutions/minute for 3-5 minutes. In the method, the thickness of the modified atmosphere protective film is 5-100 mu m, the oxygen transmission rate is 5000-15000 ml/m2x dx atm, the carbon dioxide transmission rate is 8000-20000 mL/m2x dx atm, and the water vapor transmission rate is 10-30 g/m2x day.

GA 201946782E
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus (10) has a washer column (100) for passing mixed gas. A main adsorber column (200) passes the mixed gas that passes through the washer column. The washer column is one of a spray column, a bubble column, a packed column, and a plate column. An upper end of the washer column is provided with a demister (120) and a condenser (122). The washer column comprises washing solution that is water or acid solution, where the acid solution is one of sulfuric acid, hydrochloric acid, and phosphoric acid. A reservoir recovers amine discharged from the washer column and supplies the recovered amine to a carbon dioxide capture facility for supplementing amine. An adsorbent is placed in the adsorber column and selected from a group consisting of zeolite, activated carbon, silica, alumina, a carbon molecular sieve, silicate, and activated alumina.
   USE - Apparatus for separating amine gas from mixed gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The apparatus effectively removes amine gas in mixed gas by consecutively passing the mixed gas through the washer column and the adsorber column.

PN CN109663499-A
TI Four-efficiency denitration agent composition, comprises denitrifying agent, catalyst, oxidizing agent, surfactant, titanium dioxide, sulfur fixing agent, sulfur additive and sulfur auxiliary agent in specific weight percentage.
AU WU X
AE SHANDONG SHOUFENG ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTI (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019400206
AB    NOVELTY - A four-efficiency denitration agent composition comprises 25-45 wt.% denitrifying agent, 10-15 wt.% catalyst, 10-12 wt.% oxidizing agent, 5-8 wt.% surfactant, 8-12 wt.% titanium dioxide, 20-30 wt.% sulfur fixing agent, 1-3 wt.% sulfur additive and 1-2 wt.% sulfur auxiliary agent.
   USE - Four-efficiency denitration agent composition.
   ADVANTAGE - The four-efficiency denitration agent composition has improved denitration efficiency, product quality, sorbent utilization in simple and cost effective manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a method for denitration of denitration agent composition, which involves taking a materials, putting the denitrification agent, catalyst, titanium dioxide, sulfur-fixing agent, sulfur-fixing additive and sulfur-fixing additive into a vacuum drying oven, and controlling the drying temperature to be 70-90 degrees C, after drying for 1 hour, taking out and mixing, putting the denitrifying agent, catalyst, oxidant, surfactant, titanium dioxide, sulfur-fixing agent, sulfur-fixing additive and sulfur-fixing additive into the grinder, grinding, sieving and mixing to obtain a four-effect denitrification composition, injecting the four-effect denitrification composition into the denitration reaction zone of the furnace, and reacting the four-effect denitrification composition with nitrogen oxide in the flue gas at a temperature of 500-800 degrees C to form carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, nitrogen, water, calcium sulfate and sodium sulfate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The denitration agent is urea, ammonium chloride, ammonium formate and ammonium acetate. The catalyst is vanadium pentaoxide, titanium dioxide and cobalt. The oxidizing agent is potassium manganate and potassium permanganate. The sulfur-fixing agent is calcium hydroxide. The sulfur-fixing additive is sodium carbonate. The sulfur auxiliary agent is sodium, magnesium, aluminum, zinc, cobalt and titanium.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The surfactant is sodium dioctyl succinate sulfonate, sodium dodecyl benzene sulfonate, sodium glycocholate, sodium lauryl sulfate, sulfonated castor oil and alkyl amide.

PN WO2019070289-A1; US2020291761-A1
TI Method for treating wellbore in subterranean formation, involves generating heated inert gas, combining well treatment chemical with heated inert gas to form matrix injection gas, and delivering matrix injection gas to formation.
AU MCCOLPIN G R
AE SURGITECH INC (SURG-Non-standard)
   SURGITECH INC (SURG-Non-standard)
GA 201934325R
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves generating heated inert gas using an inert gas generator (102), where the inert gas comprises carbon dioxide and nitrogen. The heated inert gas is placed from the inert gas generator into the annular space. The heated inert gas is circulated from a surface down an annulus. The heated inert gas is returned up through a tubular element back to the surface for period of time until the tubular element is partially cleaned. Well treatment chemical with the heated inert gas is combined to form matrix injection gas, where the well treatment chemical is one of suspended in the heated inert gas as droplet in a vapor state of the chemical. The matrix injection gas is delivered to the formation.
   USE - Method for treating a wellbore in a subterranean formation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables designing an end plate, so that an exhaust pipe slides inside a reactor body to expand and contract without causing stresses to normally break welds and damage the system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for the following:
   (1) a well treatment apparatus
   (2) a well treatment system.

PN CN109534495-A; CN109534495-B
TI Sewage treatment involves mixing domestic sewage            with activated sludge microorganisms in reactor, and            then causing pollutants to cause microbial            transcription of microorganisms in activated sludge for            generating active enzyme.
AU ZHANG X
   FAN X
   LIU C
AE UNIV TSINGHUA SHENZHEN GRADUATE SCHOOL (THIG-C)
   SHENZHEN HUAYUAN ENVIRONMENT TECHNOLOGY CO              LTD (SHEN-Non-standard)
   ZHANGJIAGANG HUAYUAN ENVIRONMENTAL TECHN (ZHAN-Non-standard)
   SHENZHEN HUAYUAN ENVIRONMENT TECHNOLOGY (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 2019317775
AB    NOVELTY - Sewage treatment involves mixing domestic                sewage with activated sludge microorganisms in a                reactor, and then causing pollutants to cause                microbial transcription of the microorganisms in                the activated sludge for generating an active                enzyme capable of degrading the pollutants into                carbon dioxide or nitrogen. A mathematical model of                gene transcriptional dynamics is used to calculate                active enzyme level online for correcting activity                level of the activated sludge. A mathematical model                of activated sludge degradation kinetics is used to                calculate the degradation rate of pollutants and                predict the electron donor or acceptor required for                pollutant degradation based on the activated sludge                activity level for determining and accurately                controlling degradation of activated sludge.
   USE - Method for treating sewage based on a                microbial gene and an active enzyme.
   ADVANTAGE - The method treats the sewage in a simple,                cost-effective and eco-friendly manner with                characteristics of intelligent dynamic control for                urban domestic sewage treatment plants.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a sewage                treatment system, which comprises a biochemical                reaction cell for domestic sewage based on gene                transcription and active enzyme.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                biological treatment reaction tank using domestic                sewage. (Drawing includes non-English language                text).
   Sewage pump (1)
   Electric valve (2)
   Flow meter (3)
   Water distribution tank (4)
   Aeration reaction tank (5)

PN CN109481724-A
TI Antibacterial odor superabsorbent sanitary napkin useful for inhibiting growth of menstrual causative organisms, contains antibacterial cotton soft layer, warming womb layer, and bacterial cellulose superabsorbent layer.
AU LI Y
   KONG X
   ZHI Q
   WANG X
   WANG N
AE QINGDAO MINGYAOTANG MEDICAL CO LTD (QING-Non-standard)
GA 201927293N
AB    NOVELTY - Antibacterial odor superabsorbent sanitary napkin comprises antibacterial cotton soft layer, warming womb layer, chlorine dioxide nano odor removing layer, bacterial cellulose superabsorbent layer, water-lock leakage layer and breathable base film layer from top to bottom.
   USE - The antibacterial odor superabsorbent sanitary napkin is useful for inhibiting growth of menstrual causative organisms, promoting blood circulation, vaginal and uterine relaxation, and providing antibacterial effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for preparing antibacterial warming superabsorbent sanitary napkin, which involves pressing the chitosan fiber and the cotton fiber to obtain antibacterial soft layer, extracting gingerol by carbon dioxide supercritical method at extraction pressure of 20 mPa, extraction temperature at 30 degrees C, extraction time of 10-20 minutes, separation pressure of 8 mPa and separation temperature of 55 degrees C, spraying gingerol on the surface of the non-woven substrate, preparing chlorine dioxide nano-deodorizing layer and a bacterial cellulose superabsorbent layer, crushing the chlorine dioxide particles and the bacterial cellulose particles into nanoparticles having a particle diameter of 500 nm-50 mu m, crushing the particles on the non-woven substrate, stacking antibacterial cotton soft layer, warming layer, chlorine dioxide nano odor removing layer, bacterial cellulose superabsorbent layer, water lock leakage layer and breathable base film layer from top to bottom, pressing by hot pressing and weekly sealing, and packaging the product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The antibacterial cotton soft layer is formed by pressing chitosan fibers and cotton fibers. The amount of the chitosan fiber in the antibacterial cotton soft layer is 5-30% of the antibacterial cotton soft layer. The chlorine dioxide nano odor removing layer is prepared by spraying chlorine dioxide nanoparticles on the non-woven substrate, where the mass of the chlorine dioxide nanoparticles is 0.2-1% of the mass of the nonwoven fabric substrate. The bacterial cellulose superabsorbent layer is prepared by spraying bacterial cellulose particles on a nonwoven fabric substrate, where the amount of the bacterial cellulose is 1-5% of the nonwoven fabric substrate. The non-woven fabric substrate is a plant fiber nonwoven fabric. The sanitary napkin further comprises a release paper layer. The temperature of the hot pressing is 100 degrees C.
