PN DE102018104146-A1
TI Method for treating tube interior of pipeline in e.g. drinking water system, involves draining sealant after predetermined exposure time, and drying and curing coating of inorganic sealant layer formed in tube interior.
AU SCHMELZ U
AE SCHMELZ GMBH (SCHM-Non-standard)
GA 2019218040
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves introducing an inorganic sealant into a tube interior of a pipeline (R), where the pipeline is partially made of metal. The inorganic sealant is left for predetermined exposure time in the tube interior subsequently so that a covering of the inorganic sealant is formed on an inner surface (R1) of the tube interior. The sealant is drained after the predetermined exposure time. Coating of inorganic sealant layer formed in the tube interior is dried and cured.
   USE - Method for treating a tube interior of a pipeline in a fluid transport system e.g. drinking water system, heating circuit system or cooling circuit system.
   ADVANTAGE - The inorganic sealant is drained after the predetermined exposure time, and the coating of inorganic sealant layer formed in the tube interior is dried and cured, thus largely prohibiting utilization of epoxy resin for rehabilitation of drinking water system, and hence treating water in a harmless manner. The method utilizes inorganic sealant and carbon dioxide-added gas to make the treatment in a simple, quick and effective manner.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a device for pretreatment of a pipeline.

   IDO H
   NISHIKIORI M
   MIKI Y
AE JFE STEEL CORP (JFES-C)
GA 201889858D
AB    NOVELTY - Converter steelmaking involves adding molten iron (5) to a converter (1), supplying oxygen gas (8) from a top blowing lance (2), desiliconizing the molten iron, supplying calcium oxide-based flux, stirring by blowing bottom blow gas, performing slag-exhausting (s1) from converter by exhausting portion(s) of the slag by tilting the converter, returning converter to upright position, adding calcium oxide-based flux, dephosphorizing by supplying oxygen gas from a top blowing lance under stirring condition, performing slag-exhausting (s2) from the converter by exhausting portion(s) of the slag by tilting the converter, recycling resultant slag as calcium oxide-based flux charge for desiliconization process, returning the converter to upright position, adding calcium oxide-based flux, performing decarbonization refinement by supplying oxygen gas from a top blowing lance under stirring condition, and recycling resultant slag as calcium oxide-based flux charge for dephosphorization treatment.

   ADVANTAGE - The method enables converter steelmaking with high productivity, improved dissolution efficiency of the cold iron source, and reduced generation of slag and carbon dioxide emission. The slag generated during desiliconization has low basicity. The swelling of the slag caused by undissolved calcium oxide is suppressed.

AB    NOVELTY - The method involves reducing an amount of carbon dioxide emitted by the blast furnace system. The air passing from the mixer air inlet into the hot blast main portion (1008) is heated to mix with the hot blast air by a jet of plasma heated gas from a plasma torch (1006A,1006B) mounted in the mixer air inlet (1005A,1005B). The heat transferred to a hot blast main refractory material is reduced by providing a shroud of cooling air surrounding the jet of plasma heated gas exiting the plasma torch, for controlling temperature for the hot blast main refractory material associated with the hot blast main portion. The cooling air is provided with non-diatomic gas that comprises water. PN US2018311707-A1; KR2018121829-A; CN108971078-A
TI Method for cleaning chamber of substrate processing system, involves providing fluid from fluid source through nozzle assembly without substrate, injecting fluid into chamber through nozzle assembly, and purging chamber.
AU TAYLOR T R
   BATEMAN A
AE LAM RES CORP (LRES-C)
   LAM RES CORP (LRES-C)
GA 201885438B
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves maintaining a chamber (204) at first predetermined pressure. Fluid is provided from a fluid source (208) through a nozzle assembly (212) without a substrate present within the chamber, where the fluid source is maintained at second predetermined pressure that is greater than the first predetermined pressure. The fluid is injected into the chamber through the nozzle assembly, where injection of the fluid into the chamber maintained at the first predetermined pressure causes the fluid to aerosolize into a mixture of gas and solid particles. The chamber is purged, where the fluid includes carbon dioxide, argon, sulfur hexafluoride, butane, propane, ethylene, nitrous oxide, ammonia, krypton, xenon, radon, nitrogen trifluoride, sulfur dioxide, and hydrogen chloride.
   USE - Method for cleaning a chamber of a substrate processing system.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables allowing a computer to enable a remote access to a system to monitor current progress of fabrication operations, examine a history of past fabrication operations, examine trends or performance metrics from a set of fabrication operations, to change parameters of current processing, to set processing steps to follow a current processing, or to start a new-process in a reliable manner.

PT P
PN CN108607571-A
TI Polynary metal oxide catalyst used for catalytic passive regeneration of diesel particulate filter at low temperatures, comprises zirconium dioxide, copper(II) oxide, cerium(IV) oxide and manganese(IV) oxide.
AU LI X
AE SHANGHAI BEILV ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018804736
AB    NOVELTY - A polynary metal oxide catalyst comprises base component comprising zirconium dioxide, and active component comprising copper(II) oxide, cerium(IV) oxide and manganese(IV) oxide.
   USE - Polynary metal oxide catalyst used for catalytic passive regeneration of diesel particulate filter at low temperatures (claimed) in high-sulfur diesel and city road traffic conditions.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has strong sulfur poisoning resistance, improves low-temperature catalytic oxidation activity and carbon dioxide selectivity, and removes soot particles in diesel engine gas exhaust temperature range of 150-400 degrees C.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the polynary metal oxide catalyst comprising dissolving zirconium nitrate, cerium nitrate hexahydrate, copper nitrate trihydrate and 50% manganese nitrate solution in 500 ml deionized water, adding glycine solution, regulating pH value, heating and dissolving at 60 degrees C, stirring on magnetic stirrer for 1 hour to obtain uniform and clear precursor solution, coating DPF carrier into precursor solution for 1 minute, clamping soaked DPF sample carrier with nipper, dipping in 100 ml crucible, laying crucible into muffle furnace (advanced pre-heating of furnace to 350 degrees c for 1 hour), quick combustion synthesizing for 8 minutes, taking out crucible from muffle furnace, quickly cooling carrier for 1 minute, combusting, and sintering in muffle furnace at 450 degrees C for 5 hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst comprises 5 wt.% zirconium dioxide, 9-81 wt.% copper(II) oxide, 5 wt.% cerium(IV) oxide and 9-81 wt.% manganese(IV) oxide. The precursor of zirconium dioxide is pentahydrate nitrate zirconium. The precursor of copper(II) oxide is trihydrate copper nitrate. The precursor of cerium(IV) oxide is hexahydrate cerium nitrate. The precursor of manganese(IV) oxide is 50% manganese nitrate solution. The catalyst has specific surface area of 60-100 m2/g.

TI Fire emergency treatment system for freight car compartment, has indicator light which enters alarm state and lights up red, when parameters of compartment are normal, and display that displays various parameters of compartment.
AU WANG S
AE WANG S (WANG-Individual)
GA 201878722B
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a fire monitoring module which is used for real-time monitoring various parameters related to fire in a compartment (2). The smoke sensor and the carbon dioxide gas detecting unit are both mounted on the upper top end of the tail of the compartment. Multiple temperature sensors are evenly distributed on the inner surfaces of the compartment. The light intensity detecting unit is installed at a center top end in the compartment. The indicator light and the display are installed in the cab. The indicator light is green, when the parameters of the compartment are normal. The indicator light enters the alarm state and lights up red, when the parameters of the compartment are normal. The display displays various parameters of the compartment detected by the fire monitoring module.
   USE - Fire emergency treatment system for freight car compartment.
   ADVANTAGE - The safety of the automobile and the driver is ensured. The possibility of the automobile fuel tank exploding is avoided.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a fire emergency processing method for a freight car compartment.

   SONG H
   SONG X
AE ECO BIO HOLDINGS CO LTD (ECOB-Non-standard)
GA 201874103Y
AB    NOVELTY - Solidifying biogas, comprises (a) compressing biogas to 7-10 barg using an oil-free type screw compressor (3) which lubricates with water, (b) separating the biogas and water discharged from the compressor, (c) draining 20-40% of the total weight of the separated water, (d) cooling the separated biogas to 5-20 degrees C, (e) removing condensate generated from the cooled biogas, (f) removing water using a water filter (8) for removing moisture remaining in the biogas from which the condensed water is removed, and (g) removing impurities including moisture, siloxane, sulfide, vegetable oil and volatile organic compound remain in trace amount biogas from gravel layer, activated alumina layer, molecular sieve layer and activated carbon layer by an adsorptive dryer (12).
   USE - The method is useful for solidifying biogas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method removes water, siloxane, sulfide, vegetable oil and volatile organic compounds contained in biogas generated from organic wastes by anaerobic digestion; has improved carbon dioxide removal efficiency; prevents contamination of membrane surface and adsorbent due to oil used as lubricant; eliminates additional filtering or adsorbent; and reduces operating load of impurity removal step.

TI Skin care product for pregnant woman, comprises active mineral factor stock solution, motherwort, seaweed, casein, olive oil, octopamine, sea urchin, liquid yeast extract, water, where stock solution is obtained by extracting from peony.
AU LI H
   YU S
   NAME N P
AE CHANGSHA XIAORU INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY (CHAN-Non-standard)
   FOSHAN BAIFENZHIXIU COSMETICS CO LTD (FOSH-Non-standard)
GA 201864739P
AB    NOVELTY - Skin care product comprises active mineral factor stock solution, motherwort, seaweed, casein, olive oil, octopamine, sea urchin, liquid yeast extract and water. The ratio of octopamine, casein and sea urchin is (0.1-0.3):(2-6):(2-4). The active mineral factor stock solution is obtained by extraction from peony, purslane, red guava. The activated mineral is prepared by mixing factor stock solution, peony purslane, red guava and then placing in extract kettle, and adding entrainer absolute ethyl alcohol. The 400 g mixture is added into 150 ml absolute ethyl alcohol with 22kg/hour flow of carbon dioxide and pressure of 35 megapascal at 55 degrees C for 2 hours. The ethanol is taken to volatilize completely.
   USE - Skin care for pregnant woman.
   ADVANTAGE - The skin care has skin improvement effect and moisture lasting degree and synergistic effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing skin care, which involves:
   (A) extracting active mineral factor stock solution from peony, purslane, red guava, motherwort, seaweed, sea urchin, liquid yeast extract into water for soaking;
   (B) pouring into ceramic earthen pot, adding olive oil, simmering for 30 minutes, and cooling; and
   (C) filtering liquid, octopamine, casein, after uniformly stirring, evaporating and concentrating liquid into paste.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Condition: The ratio of octopamine, casein and sea urchin is 0.2:4:3. Preferred Composition: The skin care product comprises 20-50 pts. wt. active mineral factor stock solution, 3-5 pts. wt. motherwort, 2-5 pts. wt. seaweed, 20-50 pts. wt. olive oil, 0.1-0.3 pts. wt. octopamine, 2-6 pts. wt. casein, 2-4 pts. wt. sea urchin, 4-8 pts. wt. liquid yeast extract, and 40-50 pts. wt. water. Preferred Process: The liquid yeast extract is prepared by adding brewer's yeast with enzymes, catalyzing cell breakage, and then subjecting to deep liquid fermentation, separating and concentrating. Preferred Condition: The raw material is soaked in the clean water is 1-5 hours. The evaporation temperature is 25-30 degrees C.

AE SOL SPA (SOLS-Non-standard)
GA 201861471T

The first tube is provided with multiple holes (13) on outer surface. The second tube is internal to the first tube. Each one of first and second tubes is provided with an intake (4,5) with a corresponding valve (6,7).
   USE - Diffuser used in treatment of drinking water and runoff water for mixing two fluids inside pipes in which carrier fluid flows.
   ADVANTAGE - The diffuser produces micro-bubbles of carbon-dioxide in the water to be treated by virtue of effects of high mixing capacity and rapid homogenization of porous diffusion, thus a precise control of potential of hydrogen (pH) is ensured with very little free pipe length to have an optimal coagulation reaction. The diffuser enables an ease of maintenance. The fluid is extracted from the pipe holding the carrier fluid even under pressure and without having to stop the flow, thus plant shutdowns is avoided. The diffuser is highly reliable, easily and practically implemented, and low cost.

TI Producing biogas in two stages to fuel from prickly pear involves collecting prickly pear, grinding prickly pear having high bacterial content with water, and supplementing mixture with nitrogen-phosphorus-potassium nutrient.

AE AKE M M A (AKEM-Individual)
   LOPEZ R S (LOPE-Individual)
GA 2018A5498Q
AB    NOVELTY - Producing biogas in two stages to fuel from prickly pear involves collecting the prickly pear, grinding the prickly pear having high bacterial content with water, where pear and water are taken in weight ratio of 1:1 to 1:3, supplementing the mixture with 1 kg of nitrogen-phosphorus-potassium (NPK) nutrient by per ton of pear, transporting the mixture to one first tank of one first stage and stop fermenting for one of residence time of 24-72 hours to pH between 6.0-6.5; transporting the fermented pear to a tank of anaerobic reactor of one second stage, where the temperature in the anaerobic tank are maintained at 35 degrees C plus minus 5 degrees C and pH between 7.3-7.5, removing the biogas produced in the tank from the anaerobic reactor and transporting to chamber for treatment of gasses for taking out hydrogen sulfide, carbon dioxide and moisture and storing the purged and compressed gas to high pressure for future use, including motor fuel.

   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to produce biogas in two stages to fuel from prickly pear in reduced time.

GA 201840936C

   USE - The method is useful for silencing a negative regulatory receptor of an immune cell which is used for preparing leukocyte preparation (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares leukocyte preparation which is suitable for laboratory research on immune status e.g. immunotherapy for healthy people, tumor patients and tumor patients, supporting development of treatment plans for cancer patients, after treatment evaluation, studying killing effect of leukocytes on tumor cells under different blocking effects, and performing clinical treatment trials, on tumor patients, that need to be blocked after PD-1 blocking of their own T lymphocytes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Component: Each 1 ml mixture of plasma and leukocytes contains 2x 106 leukocyte. Preferred Method: The step (a) involves taking anticoagulated blood, adding into 50 ml centrifuge tube, centrifuging at 2600 revolutions/minute (rpm) for 10 minutes, pipetting the intermediate cell layer, transferring into another centrifuge tube, repeating the centrifugation twice again, aspirating the intermediate cell layer and mixing with plasma to obtain a mixture of plasma and leukocytes. The anticoagulated blood is obtained by collecting 120 ml peripheral blood from a blood collection bag containing sodium citrate as an anticoagulant and storing at 4-8 degrees C.

   DYAS K E
AE APOLLO CHEM LTD (APOL-Non-standard)
GA 201829580H
AB    NOVELTY - A spraying system comprises a pressurized container and a liquid formulation within the container. The formulation comprises 30-80 pph one or more moisture-cure aliphatic polyurethane pre-polymer, 1-15 pph crosslinking agent and polyurethane-compatible liquid propellant.
   USE - Spraying system is used for dispensing coating composition for restoring block paved or resin bound paved surface (claimed). Uses include but are not limited to driveways, pathways, walkways, car parks and other light traffic areas.
   ADVANTAGE - The spraying system is capable of preventing the formation of bubbles and foam, thereby preventing the formation of superficial defects of the polyurethane coating and improving visual impact of the coating. The spraying system aids in easy and control application of coating composition. The ultraviolet-protector or an antioxidant in the spraying system is capable of preserving esthetic features of the coating for a longer time, thereby reducing the frequent restoring treatments of the surface. The bisoxazolidine in the polyurethane system prevent the unwanted reaction between isocyanate functionalities and moisture which causes coating defects due to carbon dioxide release. The crosslinking agent controls the properties of the coating.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for restoring a block paved or resin bound paved surface using the spraying system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The crosslinking agent comprises a bisoxazolidine. The propellant is chosen from dimethyl ether, propane/butane mixture, liquefied nitrogen, and 134a propellants. The propellant-to-liquid-formulation ratio is 1:4-2:3.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The liquid formulation preferably comprises 40-70 pph moisture-cure aliphatic polyurethane pre-polymer(s), and 5-12 pph crosslinking agent. The formulation further comprises a deaerator, 10-50 pph polar solvent, an organotin catalyst, an ultraviolet-protector and/or antioxidant, and an acidifying agent. The aliphatic polyurethane pre-polymer is chosen from aliphatic di-isocyanates, aliphatic polyisocyanates, and isocyanate-terminated prepolymers. The formulation comprises a fungicide. Preferred Property: The pressure within the pressurized container is 10-20 bar.

PN CN107854973-A; CN107854973-B
TI Producing modify polyphenylene ether gas separation membrane for gas purifying, involves performing bromination of 2,6-dimethyl polyphenylene ether and bromine to obtain brominated polyphenylene ether to perform coupling reaction.
AU CONG H
   YU B
   GENG Z
   SONG Q
AE UNIV QINGDAO (UNQD-C)
   UNIV QINGDAO (UNQD-C)
GA 2018264832
AB    NOVELTY - Producing modify polyphenylene ether gas separation membrane involves performing bromination of 2,6-dimethyl polyphenylene ether and bromine to obtain brominated polyphenylene ether. The coupling reaction of brominated polyphenylene ether with 3,5-bis(trifluoromethyl)phenylboronic acid to give 5-3,5-bis(trifluoromethyl)-2,6-dimethylpolyphenylene ether. The dissolving of 5-3,5-bis(trifluoromethyl)-2,6-dimethylpolyphenylene ether in chloroform to obtain a solution. The obtained solution is coated on the template, after the solvent is volatilized, and template is dried.
   USE - Method for producing modify polyphenylene ether gas separation membrane use for gas purifying.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to produce modify polyphenylene ether gas separation membrane, which effectively realize carbon dioxide and separate nitrogen.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing modify polyphenylene ether gas separation membrane involves performing bromination of 2,6-dimethyl polyphenylene ether and bromine to obtain brominated polyphenylene ether. The coupling reaction of brominated polyphenylene ether with 3,5-bis(trifluoromethyl)phenylboronic acid to give 5-3,5-bis(trifluoromethyl)-2,6-dimethylpolyphenylene ether. The dissolving of 5-3,5-bis(trifluoromethyl)-2,6-dimethylpolyphenylene ether in chloroform to obtain a solution. The obtained solution is coated on the template, after the solvent is volatilized, and template is dried to obtain a modify polyphenylene ether gas separation membrane.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Conditions: The method performed in which bromine is dissolved in chloroform, formulated as a solution A, and 2,6-dimethyl polyphenylene ether is dissolved in chloroform to prepare a solution. The obtained one solution to another solution, are mixed and stirred under reaction, and then drop the reaction solution into methanol and stirred, suction-filtered, washed and dried to obtain brominated polyphenylene ether. The performed step of mixing toluene, ethanol, sodium carbonate solution, 3,5-bis(trifluoromethyl)phenylboronic acid, tetrakis(triphenylphosphine)palladium and brominated polyphenylene ether, under nitrogen protection, and reaction is carried out through heating to reflux, and the reacted solution is successively dropped into an ethanol/hydrochloric acid solution, an ethanol/ammonia solution, and the product is precipitated. The product is filtered off with suction, washed, and dried to obtain 5-3,5-bis(trifluoromethyl)-2,6-dimethyl polyphenylene ether. The concentration of sodium carbonate solution is 0.20-0.25 g/ml. The concentration of the solution obtained is 300-500 mg/ml. Preferred Components: The template is a Teflon template. The polyphenylene ether is 5-3,5-bis(trifluoromethyl)-2,6-dimethyl polyphenylene ether.

   JATZWAUK C
   SCHARNAGL V
   SCHMIDT C
   WITTMANN C
AE HORN GLASS IND AG (HORN-Non-standard)
GA 201820338C
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves generating a diffusion flame (1) in a burner (2). A fuel gas (3) is supplied through a fuel gas supply. The fuel gas is partially added to the component (5) for lowering the flame kernel temperature of the diffusion flame. The component is water vapor and/or oxygen and/or carbon dioxide and/or inert gas and /or aerosol and/or oil. The burner is provided with an inner nozzle and an outer nozzle. The component is ejected predominantly through the inner nozzle.
   USE - Method for operating burner for glass melting tank used for manufacture of glasses.
   ADVANTAGE - The burner of the glass melting tank is operated simply, so that the reaction for the formation of nitrogen oxides is minimized, the emission of the nitrogen oxides in glass melting processes is reduced and the environmental protection requirements are increasingly satisfied. The energy input into the molten glass is improved. The relative position of the inner nozzle to the outer nozzle is optimized.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a burning device for glass melting tank.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawings show the schematic diagrams illustrating the process of operating burner for glass melting tank and burning device.

PN CN112126943-A; CN112126943-B
TI Preparing mesoporous silver electrode used for reducing carbon dioxide, involves placing silver in zinc salt solution for electrochemical deposition, heating silver with galvanized surface and soaking obtained silver in acid.
AU CHEN M
   MA X
   HUANG J
AE FOSHAN FANGZHU TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (FOSH-Non-standard)
GA 202104655T
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing mesoporous silver electrode, involves (a) placing silver in a zinc salt solution for electrochemical deposition to obtain silver with galvanized surface, (b) heating the silver with galvanized surface under the protection of inert gas to obtain silver and zinc alloy on the silver surface, and (c) soaking the obtained silver in acid to obtain the product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing mesoporous silver electrode for reducing carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The electrode increases surface area and mechanical strength of silver, has high current density and charge transfer electron speed under the same voltage, excellent electrocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction performance, and high catalytic activity and efficiency, reduces carbon dioxide under normal temperature and pressure and low overpotential and efficient catalyst for electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the mesoporous silver electrode comprising pores with a size of 100-300 nm.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further involves polishing and cleaning the silver before step (a). The step of polishing involves adopting physical polishing. The step of cleaning and impurity removal process involves adopting ultrasonic cleaning of silver using acetone, absolute ethanol and deionized water. The step (a) involves using platinum wire or zinc wire as counter electrode, silver as working electrode, silver/silver chloride as reference electrode during electrochemical deposition, where the potential of silver is -1.1 V. The step of electrochemical deposition duration in step (a) is 1.5-2.5 hours, preferably 2 hours. The step (b) involves heating adopting three stages of heating from 20 degrees C to 180-280 degrees C for 20-40 minutes, constant temperature at 180-280 degrees C for 2-15 hours and cooling to 20 degrees C for 35-45 minutes. The soaking time is 43-55 hours. The soaking process in step (c) involves soaking silver electrode obtained in step (b) in 5 wt.% acid for 3-10 hours and transferring to 1 wt.% acid for 40-45 hours. Preferred Components: The zinc salt solution is chosen from zinc sulfate solution and/or zinc nitrate solution. The acid is chosen from dilute sulfuric acid and/or dilute hydrochloric acid. Preferred Composition: The concentration of the zinc salt solution is 1.5-2.5 mol/l, preferably 2 mol/l. The mass fraction of the acid is 1-5 wt.%.

   YOSHINO J
AE TOYOTA CHUO KENKYUSHO KK (TOYW-C)
   UNIV TOKYO RIKA GH (UNTS-C)
GA 2020A7146B
AB    NOVELTY - Z scheme type photocatalytic system comprises an aqueous medium, oxidation photocatalytic particles that oxidize water, and reduced photocatalytic particles for reducing carbon dioxide, including semiconductor particles and a substrate that chemically bonds to the semiconductor particles by a coupling linking group that includes spacers.
   USE - As Z scheme type photocatalytic system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system reduces carbon dioxide by using water as an electron donor in an aqueous medium, and improves the selectivity of the carbon dioxide reduction reaction that suppresses the hydrogen production reaction. The system synthesizes useful carbon compound by reducing carbon dioxide, and improves carbon dioxide reduction performance. The system is excellent in scaling up and suppresses the manufacturing cost as compared with systems using oxidation catalyst electrodes and reduction catalyst electrodes. The system uses light including UV rays. The systems obtains sufficient photocatalytic activity in visible light having a wavelength lambda longer than 360 nm, and effectively utilizes light energy such as sunlight.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The system further comprises a redox mediator that transfers electrons between the oxidation photocatalyst particles and the reduced photocatalytic particles, where the redox mediator is a metal complex compound. Preferred Components: The substrate contains a metal complex catalyst and has an ability to reduce carbon dioxide. The metal complex catalyst is a complex of at least one metal chosen from metals belonging to any of Group 6 to Group 12 of the periodic table. The metal complex compound is a complex of at least one metal chosen from cobalt, iron and copper. The semiconductor particles contained in the reduced photocatalytic particles are composed of sulfide semiconductors. The sulfide semiconductor contains zinc. The aqueous medium is an aqueous solution of an electrolyte. The electrolyte is at least one selected from the group consisting of carbonates, bicarbonates, borates and phosphates. The oxidation photocatalytic particles contain a bismuth vanadate semiconductor. Preferred Properties: The level at the lower end of the conduction band of the semiconductor particles is lower than the level of the lowest empty orbit of the metal complex catalyst. The electric potential at the lower end of the conduction band of the semiconductor particles contained in the reduced photocatalytic particles is -0.3 V or less with respect to the potential of the standard hydrogen electrode at pH of 0. The electric potential at the lower end of the conduction band of the oxidation photocatalytic particles is lower than the potential at the upper end of the valence band of the semiconductor particles contained in the reduced photocatalytic particles.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The linking group comprises at least one chosen from a carboxyl group, a phosphonic acid group, a phosphonic acid ester group, a phosphoryl group, a sulfonic acid group, a silanol group, and their derivatives, and including a connecting portion that connects the semiconductor particles and the spacer. The spacer contains an alkylene group or its derivatives.

TI Preparing micron-grade spherical lithium-based carbon dioxide adsorbent, involves adding surfactant and lithium source to alcohol solution, adding tetraethyl orthosilicate and aqueous ammonia, ultrasonically treating and calcining.
AU YANG Y
   LIU W
   XU M
   TONG X
   LI Q
   CAO J
   YAO S
AE UNIV HUAZHONG SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYHZ-C)
GA 202064846G
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing micron-grade spherical lithium-based carbon dioxide adsorbent, involves (a) adding surfactant and lithium source to alcohol solution to obtain mixed solution, (b) adding tetraethyl orthosilicate and aqueous ammonia to mixed solution, and ultrasonically treating for preset time to obtain white suspension, and (c) calcining obtained white suspension for preset time to obtain solid product, where the solid product is micron-grade spherical lithium-based carbon dioxide adsorbent.
   USE - The method is used for preparing micron-grade spherical lithium-based carbon dioxide adsorbent (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method ensures short synthesis period, is convenient to operate and economical, and avoids the problem of serious sintering of products caused by high temperature calcination, and the prepared adsorbent has excellent adsorption and circulation stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a micron-grade spherical lithium-based carbon dioxide adsorbent prepared using above-mentioned method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The lithium source in step (a) comprises lithium hydroxide, lithium nitrate and/or lithium organic acid. Preferred Composition: In step (b), the molar ratio of silicon element and lithium element in white suspension is 1:4.1-1:4.3.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alcohol solution in step (a) comprises methanol solution, ethanol solution and/or isopropanol solution. The surfactant in step (a) comprises cationic surfactant, anionic surfactant, nonionic surfactant and/or zwitterionic surfactant. The adsorbent is core-shell type. Preferred Composition: In step (a), the concentration of surfactant in mixed solution is 15-50 times of critical micelle concentration. Preferred Conditions: The step (b) involves ultrasonically treating for 15-30 minutes. The step (c) involves calcining at 600-900 degrees C at a increase rate of 5-25 degrees C/minute for 4-6 hours.

PN CN108554456-A; CN108554456-B
TI Use of rare earth imidazolium salt compound as catalyst for catalyzing reaction of aziridine derivative with carbon dioxide during manufacture of 5-substituted oxazolidinone.
AU LU C
   ZHAO B
AE UNIV SOOCHOW (USWZ-C)
GA 2018780641
AB    NOVELTY - Use of a rare earth imidazolium salt compound (I) as a catalyst for catalyzing the reaction of an aziridine derivative with carbon dioxide, is claimed.
   USE - Use of rare earth imidazolium salt compound as catalyst for catalyzing reaction of aziridine derivative with carbon dioxide during manufacture of 5-substituted oxazolidinone (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The rare earth imidazolium salt compound having high catalytic activity can be prepared by simple method with high yield. The catalyst enables preparation of oxazolidinone with high selectivity under mild reaction conditions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Use of a rare earth imidazolium salt compound of formula (I) as a catalyst for catalyzing the reaction of an aziridine derivative with carbon dioxide, is claimed.
   RE = rare earth metal.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of 5-substituted oxazolidinone, which involves adding rare earth imidazolium salt compound to a reaction container under the protection of inert gas in the absence of oxygen, exhausting the inert gas, introducing carbon dioxide and aziridine compound, and reacting.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The rare earth imidazolium compound is prepared by reacting 2,6-diisopropylaniline and glyoxal as raw materials in the presence of an acid solution as a catalyst to prepare bis(2,6-diisopropylphenyl)diazepine, producing 1,3-bis(2,6-diisopropylphenyl)imidazolium chloride salt from obtained compound, paraformaldehyde and hydrochloric acid, and reacting resulting compound and trichloro rare earth compound in tetrahydrofuran. The bis(2,6-diisopropylphenyl)diazepine is prepared by adding 2,6-diisopropylaniline, glyoxal and absolute ethanol to a reaction flask, adding an aqueous solution of formic acid, stirring at room temperature for 48 hours, and filtering. The prepared diazepine compound is dissolved in toluene, and powdered paraformaldehyde is added and stirred at 50 degrees C to prepare 1,3-bis(2,6-diisopropylphenyl)imidazolium chloride salt. Hydrochloric acid solution is then added and reacted for 40 hours. The reaction product is then reacted with trichloro rare earth compound at 50 degrees C in tetrahydrofuran, centrifuged, and supernatant is concentrated and crystallized to obtain rare earth imidazolium salt compound. Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of the aziridine derivative to the catalyst is 100:(0.5-1). The usage amount of the catalyst for preparing 5-substituted oxazolidinone is 0.5-1 mol%.

PN JP7136523-B1
TI System for manufacturing liquid fuel for use in            carbon-neutral methane, has heat transfer device that            heats reaction heat generated and that heats carbon            dioxide-containing absorption liquid by heat transfer            to carbon dioxide gas collection device.
AU ITO S
AE EUREKA ENG KK (EURE-Non-standard)
GA 2022C1243V
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a methanation device (30)                equipped with a cooling unit (32) for sending the                condensed water to the high temperature water and                sending it to the boiler (20). The molar ratio of                the hydrogen gas to the carbon monoxide gas from                the carbon-neutral methane gas supplied from the                reaction tube (31) of the methanation device are                approximately 2. The synthetic gas producing                apparatus (40) for producing a synthetic gas 1 and                a liquid fuel synthesizing apparatus (50) for                synthesizing the liquid fuel crude oil by reacting                the synthetic gas supplied from the synthetic gas                producing apparatus with a catalyst in a prescribed                temperature and pressure environment are provided.                A heat transfer device (58) heats the reaction heat                generated when the synthesis gas is synthesized                with the liquid fuel crude oil by the liquid fuel                synthesis device and heats the carbon                dioxide-containing absorption liquid by heat                transfer to the carbon dioxide gas collection                device (25).
   USE - System for manufacturing liquid fuel for use                in carbon-neutral methane generated by a biomass                power generation and a carbon-neutral methane gas                produced from a low carbon hydrogen gas in the                power generation plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The system manufactures the green power and                the liquid fuel crude oil by organically bonding                the established technology to carbon neutral and                energy saving. The green power and the sustainable                aviation fuel is manufactured by energy saving in a                carbon neutral state in all manufacturing                processes. The power generation efficiency is                improved. The boiler corrosion is prevented. The                system manufactures the green power and the liquid                fuel crude oil by carbon neutral and energy saving                in any process of the system without restricting                the production scale by organically coupling the                established technology.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The generator (10) is driven by a condensate                turbine (11). A boiler burns the biomass supplied                from the biomass supply device (23), heats the high                temperature water and supplies it to the condensate                turbine as superheated steam. A condenser (15)                condenses the low pressure steam discharged from                the condensate turbine into condensed water. The                condensate turbine generator and the exhaust gas                are supplied from the boiler. The carbon dioxide                contained in the exhaust gas is absorbed in the                absorption liquid to generate a CO2- containing                absorption liquid, and the carbon dioxide                containing absorbent is supplied from the                absorption tower (26). The carbon dioxide recovery                apparatus is provided with a regeneration tower                (27) for heating the carbon dioxide-containing                absorption liquid to discharge the carbon dioxide                gas. The carbon dioxide gas is supplied from the                carbon dioxide gas collection device. A low carbon                hydrogen gas having a prescribed ratio of mass flow                rate is supplied to the mass flow rate of the                carbon dioxide gas from the low carbon hydrogen                supply device. The reaction tube is constituted so                that the carbon dioxide gas and the low carbon                hydrogen gas are fed into carbon neutral methane                gas methanation reaction in a prescribed                temperature under a prescribed pressure by a                methanation reaction catalyst filled in the inside                of the reaction tube. The condensed water is                supplied from the condensate turbine generator, and                the reaction heat generated by the methanation                reaction is transferred to the condensed water to                keep the inside of the reaction tube at the                prescribed temperature showing the activity of the                methanation catalyst. The liquid carbon-neutral                methane crude oil supplied from the liquid fuel                synthesizing device is upgraded with the low carbon                hydrogen gas supplied from the low-carbon hydrogen                supply device to produce the sustainable aviation                fuel. Either the steam generated by the heat                transfer of the reaction heat generated in the                liquid fuel synthesizing device to a unit of the                condensed water sent from the condenser and the                water vapor extracted from the condensate turbine                are circulated to the carbon dioxide recovery                device to heat the CO2-containing absorption                liquid. A unit of the carbon-neutral methane gas                sent out from the reaction tube of the methanation                device is supplied. A unit of the carbon-neutral                methane gas is burned to provide an independent                overheating device for overheating the superheated                steam sent out from the boiler. The reaction heat                generated in the liquid fuel synthesizing device is                heat-transferred to a unit of the condensed water                sent from the carbon dioxide collecting device. The                other unit of the condensed water sent from the                carbon dioxide collecting device is added by the                exhaust heat recovery boiler for recovering the                exhaust heat from the exhaust gas discharged from                the independent overheating device. The carbon                dioxide-containing absorbent by circulating the                heated steam to the carbon dioxide collection                device is heated.

TI Portable biocontainment device for collecting and storing hazardous or infectious agent, has liquid sample container that is fluidically connected to distal end of tube for storing liquid sample from liquid-phase biological sample.
AU BENNET D
   ZENHAUSERN F
AE ARIZONA BOARD OF REGENTS (UYAZ-C)
GA 202218483D
AB    NOVELTY - The portable biocontainment device has a container having a side wall, a bottom wall and a top wall to define a container reservoir. A condensing spiral tube is positioned in the container reservoir and has a spiral tube proximal end fluidically connected to the gas biological sample inlet. A condensation sample container is fluidically connected to a distal end of the condensing spiral tube for storing a condensed liquid sample from the gas-phase biological sample. A tube is positioned in the container reservoir and has a tube proximal end fluidically connected to the liquid biological sample inlet. A liquid sample container is fluidically connected to a distal end of the tube for storing a liquid sample from the liquid-phase biological sample.
   USE - Portable biocontainment device for collecting and storing hazardous or infectious agent.
   ADVANTAGE - The safe and easy to use collection system that is compatible with gas and liquid samples, while ensuring the storage is safe and robust, all while maintaining viability of biological organisms in the stored sample is provided.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A fluid port traverses the side wall of the container and extends between the container reservoir and an external environment outside the container reservoir. The fluid port comprises one or more openings configured to receive a liquid-phase biological sample and/or a gas-phase biological sample. One or more openings are externally positioned relative to the container reservoir and each of the gas biological sample inlet and the liquid biological sample inlet is positioned in the container reservoir. A compressible absorbent pad has a longitudinal axis extending between a first end and a second end. A liquid indicator is aligned with the compressible absorbent pad longitudinal axis. The proximal end of a hand bar connected to the compressible absorbent pad second end and/or the liquid indicator. The compressible absorbent pad is configured for insertion into the insert port and the hand bar is accessible to a subject to control compression of the compressible absorbent pad in the insert port. The insertable liquid collector comprises a compressible absorbent pad having a longitudinal axis extending between a first end and a second end. A removable liquid collection conduit has a distal end configured to fluidically connect to the liquid biological sample inlet. A liquid indicator is aligned with the compressible absorbent pad longitudinal axis. Multiple apertures are positioned through the insert port for conveying liquid sample from the gas-phase opening to the liquid biological sample inlet. An excess liquid collection container is connected to the fluid port to collect excess liquid from the introduced liquid-phase biological sample. The container reservoir is pressurizable to a negative pressure. The negative pressure is generated in the container reservoir by a pressure pump assembly operably connected to the container reservoir. The compressible absorbent pad is configured for insertion into the removable liquid collection conduit and the hand bar is accessible to a subject to control compression of the compressible absorbent pad in the insert port. The liquid sample container has a container volume to accommodate a liquid sample volume less than or equal to 3 ml, and the condensation sample container has a container volume to accommodate a condensed liquid sample volume less than or equal to 3 ml. The liquid biological sample inlet receives an oral fluid and/or the gas biological sample inlet receives an exhaled breath from a patient to-be-tested for a symptom of the infectious agent, and includes a biomarker of the infectious agent. A filtration system is positioned between the liquid biological sample inlet and the tube to filter the liquid-phase biological sample. The infectious agent in the liquid sample container and/or the condensation sample container remains viable, during biocontainment storage. An exhaust controller is connected to the exhaust line to control a flowrate of gas from the container reservoir to the external environment; wherein the side wall, the bottom wall and the top wall of the container are configured to contain pressure levels up to 2500 psi and temperature levels up to 60 degrees C for a period of between 10 minutes to 60 minutes configured for the carbon-dioxide transformation to supercritical carbon-dioxide Supercritical carbon dioxide in the container reservoir to sterilize any contagion accessible to the Supercritical carbon dioxide. One of the one or more disinfectant storage containers is configured to store a disinfectant additive. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method of storing one or more viable biological samples, involves providing the portable biocontainment device. The liquid-phase biological sample is introduced into the liquid biological sample inlet. The liquid-phase biological sample is collected into the liquid sample container. The portion of the gas-phase biological sample in the condensing spiral tube is condensed to the condensed liquid sample. The condensed liquid sample is collected into the condensation sample container. One or more viable biological samples are stored. The contagious material to be decontaminated into the portable biocontainment device is collected. The carbon-dioxide is introduced to the portable biocontainment device. The pressure and temperature within the portable biocontainment device is adjusted to at least 1500 psi and 31 degrees C to 60 degrees C, respectively, for supercritical transformation of the carbon-dioxide. The pressure and temperature levels are maintained for 10 to 60 minutes. The contagious material is decontaminated. The contagious material comprises a pathogen to be sterilized. The decontaminating of contagious material occurs prior to any hazardous release for medical and/or nonmedical applications.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the portable biocontainment direct-access device for collecting and storing a hazardous or an infectious agent.

TI Preparing sulfonated tetrafluoroethylene based            fluoropolymer-copolymer-loaded covalent organic            framework-316 organic carbon dioxide reduction            photocatalyst, by dispersing sulfonated            tetrafluoroethylene based fluoropolymer-copolymer            polymer solution, performing freeze-pump-thaw, and            vacuum drying.
AU GAO H
   ZHANG F
AE UNIV HARBIN SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYHS-C)
GA 2022A4001A
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing Nafion(RTM: Sulfonated                tetrafluoroethylene based                fluoropolymer-copolymer)-loaded covalent organic                framework (COF)-316 organic photocatalyst carbon                dioxide reduction photocatalyst, involves (i)                dispersing Nafion(RTM: Sulfonated                tetrafluoroethylene based fluoropolymer-copolymer)                polymer solution in the ethanol solution,                dispersing the Nafion(RTM: Sulfonated                tetrafluoroethylene based fluoropolymer-copolymer)                polymer solution, placing dry and activated COF-316                in a Schlank tube, adding 1,4-dioxane solution and                Nafion(RTM: Sulfonated tetrafluoroethylene based                fluoropolymer-copolymer) standard solution, placing                the Schlank tube containing the sample in liquid                nitrogen at -196&#8451; for rapid freezing, and                performing four freeze-pump-thaw processes to                ensure that the air in the tube is evacuated,                sealing the Schlank tube under vacuum, and                naturally cooling to room temperature, performing                atmospheric filtration, washing precipitate, and                vacuum drying.
   USE - The method is used for preparing Nafion (RTM:                Sulfonated tetrafluoroethylene based                fluoropolymer-copolymer)-loaded covalent organic                framework (COF)-316 organic photocatalyst carbon                dioxide reduction photocatalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The method ensures low consumption of reagents                and high yield. The photocatalyst improves the                problem of low efficiency of COF-316 photocatalytic                reduction of carbon dioxide to produce carbon                monoxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing Nafion(RTM: Sulfonated                tetrafluoroethylene based                fluoropolymer-copolymer)-loaded covalent organic                framework (COF)-316 organic photocatalyst carbon                dioxide reduction photocatalyst, involves (i)                dispersing Nafion(RTM: Sulfonated                tetrafluoroethylene based fluoropolymer-copolymer)                polymer solution in the ethanol solution,                dispersing the Nafion(RTM: Sulfonated                tetrafluoroethylene based fluoropolymer-copolymer)                polymer solution in an ultrasonic frequency of                35-45 kHz for 10-20 minutes, transferring to a                magnetic stirrer at 200-300 rpm for 5-6 hours to                obtain a uniformly dispersed Nafion(RTM: Sulfonated                tetrafluoroethylene based fluoropolymer-copolymer)                standard solution, placing dry and activated                COF-316 in a Schlank tube, adding 1,4-dioxane                solution and Nafion(RTM: Sulfonated                tetrafluoroethylene based fluoropolymer-copolymer)                standard solution, placing the Schlank tube                containing the sample in liquid nitrogen at                -196&#8451; for rapid freezing, and performing                four freeze-pump-thaw processes to ensure that the                air in the tube is evacuated, sealing the Schlank                tube under vacuum at 100-120&#8451; for 24-48                hours, and naturally cooling to room temperature,                performing atmospheric filtration to obtain the                precipitate, washing the precipitate with                1,4-dioxane solution for 3-4 times, with absolute                ethanol for 3-4 times, and vacuum drying at                80-120&#8451; for 12-24 hours to obtain                Nafion(RTM: Sulfonated tetrafluoroethylene based                fluoropolymer-copolymer)-loaded COF-316 organic                photocatalyst carbon dioxide reduction                photocatalyst, where the volume ratio of ethanol to                the volume of Nafion(RTM: Sulfonated                tetrafluoroethylene based fluoropolymer-copolymer)                polymer is 1 ml:0.001 ml-1 mg:0.01 ml, the mass                ratio of COF-316 to the volume of 1,4-dioxane is 1                mg:0.01 ml-1 mg:0.05 ml, and the volume ratio of                the mass of COF-316 to the Nafion(RTM: Sulfonated                tetrafluoroethylene based fluoropolymer-copolymer)                standard solution is 1 mg:0.01 ml-1 mg:0.05                ml.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The Nafion(RTM:                  Sulfonated tetrafluoroethylene based                  fluoropolymer-copolymer) standard solution is an                  ethanol solution. The dry and activated COF316 is                  placed in a Schlank tube and the reaction solvent                  is 1,4-dioxane solution.Preferred Composition: The volume ratio of                  the ethanol to the volume of Nafion(RTM:                  Sulfonated tetrafluoroethylene based                  fluoropolymer-copolymer) polymer is 1                  ml:0.001ml-1mg:0.01 ml. The mass ratio of the                  COF-316 to the volume ratio of 1,4-dioxane is 1                  mg:0.01 ml-1 mg:0.05 ml. The mass ratio of the                  COF-316 to the volume ratio of the Nafion(RTM:                  Sulfonated tetrafluoroethylene based                  fluoropolymer-copolymer) standard solution is 1                  mg:0.01ml-1 mg:0.05 ml.Preferred Conditions: The heating                  temperature of the Schlank tube is maintained at                  100-120&#8451;, and the reaction is carried                  out for 24-48 hours.

PT P
PN CN110201711-A; CN110201711-B
TI Preparing catalyst for hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to synthesize low-carbon mixed alcohol by dissolving aluminum salt, adding materials comprising silicon source, crystallizing, dripping zinc salt, heating cake, grinding and roasting.
AU WANG X
   HE Z
   WANG Q
   LIU R
   GAO S
   XU S
AE CHINA HUANENG GROUP CO LTD (CHHN-C)
   CHINA HUANENG CLEAN ENERGY RES INST (CHHN-C)
GA 2019796619
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing catalyst for hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to synthesize low-carbon mixed alcohol involves (i) weighing aluminum salt, copper salt, zinc salt, potassium salt, tetraethylenepentamine (TEPA), sodium hydroxide and a silicon source, (ii) dissolving aluminum salt, stirring, adding copper salt, TEPA, sodium hydroxide and silicon source, mixing and stirring to obtain a slurry mixture, (iii) putting the slurry mixture into a hydrothermal synthesis kettle and carrying out crystallization reaction to obtain a crystallized product, (iv) weighing metered zinc salt, dissolving, dripping into the crystallized product, cooling, carrying out suction filtration and washing to obtain a washed filter cake, (v) heating and drying to obtain a dried product, (vi) roasting to obtain a zinc oxide/copper-SSZ-13 catalyst and grinding the catalyst into powder and (vii) weighing metered potassium salt, impregnating into the above fixed powder, carrying out microwave drying and roasting.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing catalyst for hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to synthesize low-carbon mixed alcohol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves dispersion of the active component and avoids generation of hot spot temperature in the process of synthesizing low-carbon mixed alcohol resulting in the carbon inactivation catalyst by utilizing SSZ-13 molecular sieve having strong hydrothermal stability, can introduce copper source simultaneously in the synthesis process of the molecular sieve, provides high selectivity of the catalytic synthesis of low-carbon alcohol and adopts simple synthesis method and easy large-scale production. The catalyst is suitable for the catalytic hydrogenation reaction of carbon dioxide synthesis low-carbon mixed alcohol and has high added value and excellent application prospect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing catalyst for hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to synthesize low-carbon mixed alcohol involves (i) weighing aluminum salt, copper salt, zinc salt, potassium salt, tetraethylenepentamine (TEPA), sodium hydroxide and a silicon source in a molar ratio of 1:(0.2-3):(0.2-2):(0.1-1):(1-5):(3-10), (ii) dissolving aluminum salt in deionized water, uniformly stirring, sequentially adding copper salt, TEPA, sodium hydroxide and silicon source, mixing and uniformly stirring to obtain a slurry mixture, (iii) putting the slurry mixture into a hydrothermal synthesis kettle and carrying out crystallization reaction to obtain a crystallized product, (iv) weighing metered zinc salt, dissolving in deionized water completely, dripping into the crystallized product for ion exchange to obtain a mixture, cooling the mixture, carrying out suction filtration and washing with deionized water until the pH value is 7-7.5 to obtain a washed filter cake, (v) heating and drying the filter cake under a microwave condition to obtain a dried product, (vi) roasting the dried product in a muffle furnace to obtain a zinc oxide/copper-SSZ-13 catalyst and grinding the catalyst into powder and (vii) weighing metered potassium salt, impregnating into the above fixed powder by an equivalent volume impregnation method, carrying out microwave drying and roasting. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the catalyst for hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to synthesize low-carbon mixed alcohol.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The aluminum salt is sodium aluminate, pseudoboehmite, aluminum isopropoxide or their mixture. The silicon source is large-particle silica sol, small-particle silica sol, tetraethoxysilane or their mixture. The copper salt is copper nitrate, copper acetate, copper sulfate or their mixture. The potassium salt is potassium hydroxide, potassium bicarbonate, potassium carbonate or their mixture. Preferred Properties: The large-particle silica sol has a particle size of 50-100 nm and the small-particle silica sol has the particle size of 2-10 nm.

Side wall of deoxygenating chamber has inlet pipe.                The deoxygenating chamber is connected with                nitrogen removal cavity that is connected with                filter cavity through a through hole. The filter                chamber has outlet pipe. The filter net is arranged                in the filter cavity. The upper end side wall of                filter net is provided with pull rod.
   USE - Carbon dioxide gas manufacturing device.
   ADVANTAGE - The device is convenient to disassemble and                replace the filter screen; quickens manufacture                rate of carbon dioxide; improves production                efficiency; and increases filtration effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Carbon dioxide gas manufacturing device                comprises shell (1), deoxygenation chamber (2),                nitrogen removal chamber (3), filter chamber (4),                inlet pipe (5), ventilator, through holes, outlet                pipe, filter net, pull rod, first rotating piece,                worm, second rotating piece, threaded rod, worm                wheel, nuts, first connecting rod, bar, slot,                second connecting rod, chute, slider and partition.                The shell is successively provided with                deoxygenation chamber, nitrogen removal chamber and                filtering chamber. The partition plate is arranged                between the two sides. The side wall of                deoxygenating chamber is provided with an inlet                pipe. The deoxygenating chamber is connected with                nitrogen removal cavity through a ventilator. The                nitrogen removal cavity is connected with the                filter cavity through a through hole. The filter                chamber is provided with outlet pipe. The filter                net is arranged vertically in the filter cavity.                The upper end side wall of filter net is vertically                provided with pull rod. The pull rod is a hollow                structure. The inner wall of the lower end of pull                rod is connected with vertically arranged worm                through the first rotating member. The worm is                arranged away from one end of the first rotating                member through the upper side wall of the pull rod                through which the inner wall of the pull rod passes                through. The second rotation member rotates and                connects two transversely arranged threaded rods.                The two threaded rods are respectively located on                both sides of the worm. Each of the threaded rods                is provided with a worm gear at one end of the two                threaded rods far from the second rotational                member. The side walls of the two threaded rods are                fitted with matching nuts. The lower side wall of                the nut is vertically provided with first one. The                first connecting rod is provided with a clamp bar                at one end of the first connecting rod away from                the nut. The clamp rod is arranged through the side                wall of the pull rod, and the side wall of the                shell is provided with a slot matching the clamp                rod.

PN CN114904479-A
TI Preparing carbon dioxide adsorbent by utilizing            coal-fired solid waste fly ash comprises e.g. adding            propylene oxide to filtrate, soaking wet gel in ethanol            solution for under heating condition of water bath,            grinding and sieving sample, and selecting sample            within certain particle size range.
AU JIN Y
   ZHANG J
   WANG Y
   QIAO X
   GUO B
AE UNIV TAIYUAN TECHNOLOGY (UYTL-C)
GA 2022A95607
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing carbon dioxide adsorbent by                utilizing coal-fired solid waste fly ash comprises                (1) fully grinding and mixing the fly ash and                sodium carbonate, placing the mixed powder into                muffle furnace, and calcining at                700-1000&#8451; for 2 hours, (2) adding                hydrochloric acid to the calcined product, stirring                with magnetic stirrer for 1 hour, filtering, and                leaving the filtrate, (3) adding propylene oxide to                the filtrate, adjusting the pH of the filtrate to                9-10, and indicating that wet gel has been formed,                (4) soaking the wet gel in 50% ethanol solution for                12 hours under the heating condition of water bath                at 40&#8451;, (5) drying the fly ash-based wet                gel in oven, (6) taking certain theoretical load of                potassium carbonate as the active component, and                drying it at 150&#8451;, and (7) grinding and                sieving the sample, and selecting sample within                certain particle size range to obtain carbon                dioxide adsorbent.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing carbon                dioxide adsorbent by utilizing coal-fired solid                waste fly ash.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: realizes the high value-added                utilization of the coal-fired solid waste fly ash;                can greatly reduce the cost of carbon capture;                directly uses the fly ash produced on site in the                coal-fired power plant; does not require logistics                transportation; and reduces transportation                costs.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing carbon dioxide adsorbent by                utilizing coal-fired solid waste fly ash comprises                (1) fully grinding and mixing the fly ash and                sodium carbonate, placing the mixed powder into                muffle furnace, and calcining at                700-1000&#8451; for 2 hours, (2) adding                hydrochloric acid to the calcined product obtained                in the step (1), stirring with magnetic stirrer for                1 hour, filtering, and leaving the filtrate, (3)                adding propylene oxide to the filtrate obtained in                the step (2), adjusting the pH of the filtrate to                9-10, allowing to stand for 1 hour, tilting the                beaker at 45&#176; and no liquid flows out, and                indicating that wet gel has been formed, (4)                soaking the wet gel obtained in the step (3) in 50%                ethanol solution for 12 hours under the heating                condition of water bath at 40&#8451;, soaking                in absolute ethanol for 12 hours and 5%                trimethylchlorosilane (TMCS) in n-butanol solution                for 24 hours to obtain fly ash-based wet gel, (5)                drying the fly ash-based wet gel obtained in the                step (4) in oven, placing the dried gel into muffle                furnace, and roasting for 2 hours at 500&#8451;                to obtain dry fly ash-based aerogel carrier, (6)                taking certain theoretical load of potassium                carbonate as the active component, taking the fly                ash-based aerogel obtained in the step (5) as                carrier, using magnetic stirrer to stir for 12                hours at room temperature, placing it into oven,                and drying it at 150&#8451;, and (7) grinding                and sieving the sample obtained in the step (6),                and selecting sample within certain particle size                range to obtain carbon dioxide adsorbent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (1), the                  mixing mass ratio of fly ash and sodium carbonate                  is 1:0.6-1.2. In the step (2), the concentration                  of hydrochloric acid is 3-6 mol/l. In the step                  (6), the theoretical load of potassium carbonate                  is 30%. In the step (7), the particle size range                  of the carbon dioxide adsorbent is 120-160                  &#181;m.Preferred Method: In the step (5), the                  drying condition is drying at 90&#8451; for                  12 hours, and drying at 110&#8451; for 12                  hours.

TI Preparation of cubic superfine light calcium carbonate involves crushing limestone ore raw material by crusher, and crushing limestone through high temperature furnace for calcining to obtain calcium oxide raw material.
AU XIONG G
   RAN Q
   LI J
   LUO C
   MO Z
AE GUANGXI DONGTAI IND CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 202226704H
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of cubic superfine light calcium carbonate, comprises (1) crushing limestone ore raw material by crusher; (2) crushing limestone through high temperature furnace for calcining to obtain calcium oxide raw material, and collecting carbon dioxide gas generated by limestone decomposition; (3) adding calcium oxide raw material and water into a reaction kettle, stirring and generating digestion reaction, obtaining first reaction liquid; (4) filtering, removing residue not participating in the reaction and calcium oxide raw material not completely reacted, obtaining second reaction liquid; (5) taking liquid as bottom liquid, preparing calcium hydroxide concentration; (6) introducing calcium hydroxide suspension liquid into bubbler, introducing carbon dioxide gas mixed air, introducing crystal form control agent, and stirring to obtain fourth reaction liquid; and (7) dehydrating and constant temperature drying.
   USE - Preparation method of cubic superfine light calcium carbonate. Uses include but are not limited to rubber, paper making, coating, ink and cable.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides cubical superfine light calcium carbonate with good particle dispersibility, uniform and regular shape, and has simple operation, low cost, which is suitable for industrial production, and improves the economic benefit. The reaction is fast, and the production period is short.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of cubic superfine light calcium carbonate, comprises (1) crushing limestone ore raw material by crusher, where mesh number level after crushing is more than 10 meshes and the diameter of crushed ore is less than 2 mm; (2) crushing limestone through high temperature furnace for calcining to obtain calcium oxide raw material, and collecting carbon dioxide gas generated by limestone decomposition, where the calcining furnace environment temperature is controlled at 600-850degrees Celsius; (3) adding calcium oxide raw material and water into a reaction kettle, stirring and generating digestion reaction, obtaining the first reaction liquid, where the internal environment temperature of the reaction kettle is controlled at 10-20degrees Celsius, and the ratio of calcium oxide raw material to water is controlled to be 1: 0.35-1: 0.5; (4) filtering the first reaction liquid, removing the residue not participating in the reaction and the calcium oxide raw material not completely reacted, obtaining the second reaction liquid; (5) taking the second reaction liquid as the bottom liquid, preparing the calcium hydroxide concentration to obtain third reaction liquid, namely the calcium hydroxide suspension with standard concentration, where the concentration of calcium hydroxide suspension is 7-10%; (6) introducing the calcium hydroxide suspension liquid into the special bubbler, introducing carbon dioxide gas mixed air from high temperature furnace into the special bubbler by air inlet, introducing the crystal form control agent into the bubbler, stirring to obtain fourth reaction liquid under heating and pressurizing environment, where the mixing ratio of the carbon dioxide gas and the air is 1: 4, the ventilation speed of the carbon dioxide mixed gas is 0.05 m/s, the reaction environment temperature of the special bubbler is kept at 16-20degrees Celsius, the pH value of the fourth reaction liquid is 6.5-7, the crystalline form control agent is sulfuric acid, the mol ratio of the sulfuric acid and the calcium hydroxide is 0.018: 1; and (7) dehydrating the fourth reaction liquid, and constant temperature drying.

TI Tobacco extract used in non-combustion electronic cigarette, is obtained by taking tobacco raw material, drying, crushing nicotine, chlorogenic acid, tobacco essential oil and neophytadiene, and extracting solanesol and carotenoid.
AU CHEN Y
   ZOU Y
   ZOU J
AE SHENZHEN ZHENWEI BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 2021E9607L
AB    NOVELTY - Tobacco extract is a mixture of extract (A), extract (B) and extract (C) respectively obtained through the extraction process: taking tobacco raw materials, drying and crushing; extraction of nicotine, chlorogenic acid, tobacco essential oil and neophytadiene by mixing the crushed tobacco raw material and entrainer to obtain mixture (A); performing carbon dioxide supercritical extraction to mixture (A) to obtain extract (A); solanesol extraction by mixing the crushed tobacco raw material and the entrainer to obtain mixture (B); performing carbon dioxide supercritical extraction to mixture (B) to obtain extract (B); carotenoid extraction by mixing the pulverized tobacco raw material and the entrainer in a weight ratio of 1:3.5 to obtain mixture (C); performing carbon dioxide supercritical extraction to mixture (C) to obtain crude carotenoid; and subjecting the crude carotenoid to alumina column chromatography with gradient elution of petroleum ether to obtain extract (C).
   USE - The tobacco extract is useful for heating and non-combustion of electronic cigarette (claimed). The tobacco extracts are useful in electronic cigarette heating.
   ADVANTAGE - The tobacco extract has improved heating non-combustion electronic cigarette flavor and taste, sensory index score of electronic cigarette added with tobacco flavor are generally higher than the application ratio of 1-4 of sensory index scores, electronic cigarette fragrance fraction at 8.34-9.52, and the flavor fraction of the electronic cigarette is 7.15-8.30 (preferably 9-23) with more full flavor. The tobacco essence improves the fragrance and palatability of the heating and non-combustion electronic cigarette.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Tobacco extract is a mixture of extract (A), extract (B) and extract (C), and the extract (A), extract (B) and extract (C) are respectively obtained through the extraction process: taking tobacco raw materials, drying at 90degrees Celsius, and crushing; extraction of nicotine, chlorogenic acid, tobacco essential oil and neophytadiene by mixing the above-mentioned crushed tobacco raw material and entrainer with a weight ratio of 1:4 to obtain mixture (A); performing carbon dioxide supercritical extraction to mixture (A) to obtain extract (A); solanesol extraction by mixing the above-mentioned crushed tobacco raw material and the entrainer in a weight ratio of 1:3.5 to obtain mixture (B); performing carbon dioxide supercritical extraction to mixture (B) to obtain extract (B); carotenoid extraction by mixing the above-mentioned pulverized tobacco raw material and the entrainer in a weight ratio of 1:3.5 to obtain mixture (C); performing carbon dioxide supercritical extraction to mixture (C) to obtain crude carotenoid; and subjecting the crude carotenoid to alumina column chromatography with gradient elution of petroleum ether to obtain extract (C).
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) a tobacco flavor prepared from 35-45 pts. wt. extract (A), 18-22 pts. wt. extract (B) and 18-22 pts. wt. extract (C), in the tobacco extract, 7-11 pts. wt. flavoring agent and 5-9 pts. wt. Pu'er ripe tea extract; and
   (2) an application of the tobacco flavor in heating and non-combustion, involving sparing the tobacco flavor and the cut tobacco in the cut tobacco in a perfuming manner based on the weight ratio of 1:(90-100), and adding the shredded tobacco to the electronic cigarette to obtain a heating and non-combustion electronic cigarette.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The tobacco raw material is dried to a moisture content of 25-30%, and then crushed.Preferred Composition: The flavoring agent is prepared from 7-9 pts. wt. rose oil, 7-9 pts. wt. tuberose oil, 8-12 pts. wt. jasmine oil, 5-9 pts. wt. leaf alcohol, 3-7 pts. wt. maltitol, 3-5 pts. wt. Ambergris ether and 27-31 pts. wt. propylene glycol. The weight ratio of the maltitol and propylene glycol is 1:(6-8).Preferred Components: The entrainer is 80-95 %mass ethanol. The entrainer further includes sorbitol, and the weight ratio of the sorbitol and ethanol is 1:(3-5). The weight ratio of the extract (C), the extract (B) and the extract (A) is 1:1:(1.9-2.1).
