TI Preparing iron phosphide/nitrogen vacancy graphitic carbon nitride nano-sheet photocatalyst useful for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide comprises e.g. preparing nitrogen vacancy graphitic carbon nitride nano-sheet and ferric oxide nano-particle precursor and calcining.
AU ZHOU Y
   LI D
   SONG Q
   JIANG D
AE UNIV JIANGSU (UYJS-C)
GA 2021C9077R
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing iron phosphide/nitrogen vacancy graphitic carbon nitride nano-sheet photocatalyst comprises (i) preparing nitrogen vacancy graphitic carbon nitride nano-sheet (Nv-CN), (ii) preparing ferric oxide nano-particle precursor, (iii) preparing non-noble metal promoter iron phosphide nanoparticles iron phosphide including grinding the ferric oxide nanoparticles with sodium hypophosphite monohydrate, mixing, introducing in crucible, calcining, naturally cooling, taking out, washing resulting black solid product with deionized water and absolute ethanol, introducing obtained product in oven for drying, grinding and drying black solid, and (iv) dispersing specific amount of iron phosphide and Nv-CN in deionized water, ultrasonically processing and stirring treatment to obtain uniform and stable suspension, stirring suspension, centrifuging, washing with deionized water to obtain solid product, placing absolute ethanol in vacuum oven and drying under vacuum.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing iron phosphide/nitrogen vacancy graphitic carbon nitride nano-sheet photocatalyst, which is useful for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: narrows the energy band gap by introduction of nitrogen vacancies; adjusts the electronic structure of graphitic carbon nitride; provides electron-rich regions as carbon dioxide adsorption activation sites; preferentially adsorbs carbon dioxide instead of hydrogen by metal iron sites in iron phosphide; inhibits the hydrogen evolution reaction response; improves the selectivity of photocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction; forms a built-in electric field to accelerate carrier migration; and promotes the separation of photogenerated electrons and holes. The complex photocatalytic material has improved photocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction activity and selectivity, which is of great significance to the development of clean energy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing iron phosphide/nitrogen vacancy graphitic carbon nitride nano-sheet photocatalyst comprises (i) preparing nitrogen vacancy graphitic carbon nitride nano-sheet (Nv-CN), (ii) preparing ferric oxide nano-particle precursor, (iii) preparing non-noble metal promoter iron phosphide nanoparticles iron phosphide including grinding the ferric oxide nanoparticles obtained in step (ii) with sodium hypophosphite monohydrate, mixing, introducing in crucible, calcining at 250-300degrees Celsius under nitrogen protection for 0.5-2 hours, naturally cooling at room temperature, taking it out, washing resulting black solid product with deionized water and absolute ethanol, introducing obtained product in oven for drying, grinding and drying black solid to obtain iron phosphide nanoparticles as black powder, and (iv) synthesizing of iron phosphide nanoparticles/nitrogen vacancy graphitic carbon nitride nano-sheets by self-assembly method namely iron phosphide/Nv-CN including dispersing a specific amount of iron phosphide and Nv-CN in deionized water, ultrasonically processing and stirring treatment to obtain uniform and stable suspension, stirring suspension for 0.5-26 hours, centrifuging, washing with deionized water to obtain solid product, placing absolute ethanol in vacuum oven and drying under vacuum.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for an iron phosphide/nitrogen vacancy graphitic carbon nitride nano-sheet photocatalyst prepared by above mentioned method, where the mass percentage of iron phosphide is 15-30 wt.%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the nitrogen-vacancy graphitic carbon nitride nano-sheet Nv-CN is prepared by taking dicyandiamide (DCDA) and ammonium chloride in a mass ratio of 1:5, adding water, uniformly stirring, introducing the resulting clear solution in a refrigerator, freezing at -4degreesC for 12-18 hours, freezing and drying in a vacuum freeze dryer to obtain white DCDA and ammonium chloride mixed crystals, placing the mixed crystal in a semi-closed crucible, transferring the crucible to an automatic program temperature-controlled heating tubular furnace, calcining at 500-600degrees Celsius under the protection of nitrogen2 gas for 4-6 hours, naturally cooling at room temperature, taking out, and grinding it to powder with a mortar to obtain graphitic carbon nitride nano-sheets with nitrogen vacancies. In the step (iii), the calcination is carried out to transfer the crucible to the automatic program temperature control heating tube furnace, where the heating rate is 2-5degreesC/minute, and drying is carried out at 60degrees Celsius for 12 hours. In the step (iv), the power of the ultrasonic machine used for the ultrasonic dispersion is 250 W, and the ultrasonic treatment time is 0.5 hour-2 days. In step (4), the drying is carried at 60degreesC out under vacuum for 12 hours. Preferred Components: In the step (iii), the mass ratio of ferric oxide nanoparticles to sodium hypophosphite monohydrate is 1:10.

AB    NOVELTY - Simulating and researching performance of                carbon dioxide intelligent response type                temperature-resistant salt-resistant tertiary amine                foam system comprises (i) constructing initial                model by using Sketch tool in Materials Studio                software to construct tertiary amine surfactant                molecules, traditional anionic surfactant                molecules, water molecules and inorganic salt ions,                adding temperature-resistant groups, modifying the                tertiary amine surfactant molecule with                salt-resistant groups and changing number of                groups, mixing obtained compound mixture with water                molecules, inorganic salt ions, and counter ions to                construct an initial interface model of foam                system, (ii) optimizing initial interface model of                foam system, (iii) calculating equilibrium                configuration by using dynamics in the calculation                module of the Forceite module, and (iv)                comprehensive analysis by using Analysis tool in                Forcite module to analyze radial distribution                function of surfactant head group and the water                molecule.
   USE - The method is useful for simulating and                researching performance of carbon dioxide                intelligent response type temperature-resistant                salt-resistant tertiary amine foam system.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: can explain the mechanism of                surfactant stabilizing foam, realizing response,                temperature and salt resistance from a microscopic                point of view; and provides certain theoretical                guidance for the experimental research of the                carbon dioxide intelligent response                temperature-resistant and salt-resistant foam                system and the construction of oil and gas fields                under different working conditions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Simulating and researching performance of                carbon dioxide intelligent response type                temperature-resistant salt-resistant tertiary amine                foam system comprises (i) constructing initial                model by using Sketch tool in Materials Studio                software to construct tertiary amine surfactant                molecules, traditional anionic surfactant                molecules, water molecules and inorganic salt ions,                adding temperature-resistant groups, modifying the                tertiary amine surfactant molecule with                salt-resistant groups and changing the number of                groups, using Amorphous Cell tool and build layer                tool to mix the modified and designed tertiary                amine surfactant molecules with the traditional                anionic surfactant molecules in a ratio of 1:1,                mixing obtained compound mixture with water                molecules, inorganic salt ions, and counter ions to                construct an initial interface model of the foam                system, (ii) optimizing initial interface model of                foam system by using Geometry Optimization in the                Forcite module Calculation to minimize the energy                of the initial interface model constructed in step                (i) to obtain the interface model of the foam                system after energy minimization, (iii) calculating                the equilibrium configuration by using dynamics in                the calculation module of the Forceite module to                perform molecular dynamics simulation on the                interface model of step (ii) to obtain the                equilibrium configuration, and (iv) comprehensive                analysis by using the Analysis tool in the Forcite                module to analyze the radial distribution function                of the surfactant head group and the water                molecule, mean square displacement curve of                surfactant head group in the foam liquid film, and                relative concentration distribution curve of the                surfactant head group according to trajectory file                of the equilibrium configuration obtained in step                (iii), analysing parameters e.g. change of the                interaction energy between surfactants for the foam                performance, response performance and mechanism,                temperature and salt resistance of carbon dioxide                intelligent response tertiary amine foam                system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the                  tertiary amine surfactant molecule is                  N-dodecyl-N,N-dimethyl tertiary amine. The                  temperature-resistant group is benzene ring or                  thiophene group. The salt-resistant group is                  hydroxy, carboxy or ethoxy group. The adding                  number of thiophene group is 1-3. The adding                  number of the ethoxy group is 1-5.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the                  traditional anionic surfactant molecules are                  alkyl sulfate surfactant molecules. The alkyl                  sulfate surfactant molecules are sodium lauryl                  sulfate, sodium tetradecyl sulfate, sodium                  hexadecyl sulfate or sodium octadecyl sulfate.                  The inorganic salt ions mixed with tertiary amine                  surfactant molecules and traditional anionic                  surfactant molecules are cations or anions in                  sodium chloride, magnesium chloride or calcium                  chloride.

PN CN112921402-A; CN112921402-B
TI Improving frequency doubling efficiency of carbon            dioxide laser involves performing wafer polishing,            bonding operation and heat treatment, where wafer            polishing is performed by using gallium arsenide as            quasi-phase-matched crystal.
AU LI Q
   BIAN H
   WANG C
   LEI H
   HUI Y
   ZHU Z
AE UNIV BEIJING TECHNOLOGY (UYBT-C)
GA 202165658M
AB    NOVELTY - Improving frequency doubling efficiency of                carbon dioxide laser involves performing wafer                polishing, bonding operation in a vacuum                environment, and heat treatment. The wafer                polishing is performed by using gallium arsenide as                a quasi-phase-matched frequency-doubling crystal,                cutting and polishing crystal to a 328-micron wafer                perpendicular to (0-11) crystal direction. The                328-micron wafer is beneficial to use of existing                processes to achieve high-quality wafers, where                thickness deviation between the wafers is 0.5                lambda =0.3 mu m, roughness Ra=0.41nm, flatness                peak-to-valley value is 0.056 lambda , and root                average square value is 0.009 lambda , which is                better than the quality of the first-order                quasi-phase matching crystal. The bonding operation                in a vacuum environment is performed by using                acetone, alcohol, and deionized water to remove                wafer surface polishing liquid from the wafer,                pickling it in a mixed solution of hydrochloric                acid for 2 minutes to remove oxides on the wafer                surface.
   USE - Method for improving frequency doubling                efficiency of carbon dioxide laser.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to improve frequency                doubling efficiency of carbon dioxide laser, which                has small thickness error, improves wafer                processing quality and accuracy, reduces roughness                and flatness, reaches number of bonding layer to                more than 50, low average single-layer interface                loss after thermal bonding, large clear aperture of                up to 20mm, and effectively improves output power                and conversion efficiency of carbon dioxide laser                frequency doubling.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Improving frequency doubling efficiency of                carbon dioxide laser involves performing wafer                polishing, bonding operation in a vacuum                environment, and heat treatment. The wafer                polishing is performed by using gallium arsenide as                a quasi-phase-matched frequency-doubling crystal,                cutting and polishing crystal to a 328-micron wafer                perpendicular to (0-11) crystal direction. The                328-micron wafer is beneficial to use of existing                processes to achieve high-quality wafers, where                thickness deviation between the wafers is 0.5                lambda =0.3 mu m, roughness Ra=0.41nm, flatness                peak-to-valley value is 0.056 lambda , and root                average square value is 0.009 lambda , which is                better than the quality of the first-order                quasi-phase matching crystal. The bonding operation                in a vacuum environment is performed by using                acetone, alcohol, and deionized water to remove                wafer surface polishing liquid from the wafer,                pickling it in a mixed solution of hydrochloric                acid for 2 minutes to remove oxides on the wafer                surface, and rotating it to 180 degrees in a vacuum                environment according to polarization direction,                carrying out the polarization, and vacuum treatment                in the vacuum ball at vacuum degree of 10-7Pa to                obtain crystal. The crystal is placed in a vacuum                hot-pressing furnace for heat treatment, where                temperature is increased to 560 degrees C at a                heating rate of 0.37 degrees C/minute for 20 hours,                and the holding process maintains a constant                pressure of 0.27kgf/mm2, and the temperature is                lowered to room temperature of 25 degrees C, where                wafers between the layers are tightly combined to                obtain 47 layers of low-loss quasi-phase-matched                gallium arsenide crystals. The average single-layer                interface loss is less than 0.11%, which is better                than quality of first-order quasi-phase                crystal.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The quality and                  accuracy of wafer processing are improved by                  increasing the thickness of the wafer, and                  odd-order matching method, such as third, fifth,                  and seventh-order quasi-phase matching are used                  according to the quasi-phase matching theory. The                  third-order quasi-phase matching chip is used for                  frequency doubling experiment to minimize                  absorption of fundamental frequency light and                  frequency doubled light. The hydrochloric acid                  with a purity of 38% and ionized water are mixed                  in a volume ratio of 1:1. The polished crystals                  are stacked according to the polarization                  direction of adjacent wafers reversed by 180                  degrees , and wafers are bonded together through                  a high-temperature pressure thermal bonding                  process in a vacuum environment.

Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. PN CN112126622-A
TI Separating and culturing of umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells comprises e.g. placing umbilical cord tissue in petri dish, washing with normal saline, cutting off, and discarding umbilical cord at both ends of umbilical cord.
AU CHENG R
AE SHAANXI BIO STEM CELLS REGENERATION MEDI (SHAA-Non-standard)
GA 2021016329
AB    NOVELTY - Separating and culturing of umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells comprises e.g. (1) placing umbilical cord tissue in a petri dish with 75% alcohol, and washing with normal saline 3-5 times, cutting off and discarding umbilical cord at the inner side 1 cm at both ends of umbilical cord, dividing umbilical cord tissue into 2-3 cm sections, and washing with saline 3-5 times to removing residual blood, cutting umbilical cord along umbilical vein, removing umbilical vein and umbilical artery, peeling off Wharton glue, washing for 2-3 times, and cutting to a tissue size of (1-2mm)x(1-2mm)x(1-2mm) piece, (2) evenly inoculating chopped tissue pieces in a T175 culture flask, adding culture solution, and placing in an incubator for culture, the culture temperature is 37 degrees C, the carbon dioxide concentration is 1-2%, and the inner monolayer is flattening, (3) replacing fresh culture medium on third day of culture, placing in incubator and culturing, the culture temperature is 37 degrees C.
   USE - The method is useful for separating and culturing of umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple, economical, and easy to industrialize, and increase yield of umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Separating and culturing of umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells comprises (1) placing umbilical cord tissue in a petri dish with 75% alcohol, and washing with normal saline 3-5 times, cutting off and discarding umbilical cord at the inner side 1 cm at both ends of umbilical cord, dividing umbilical cord tissue into 2-3 cm sections, and washing with saline 3-5 times to removing residual blood, cutting umbilical cord along umbilical vein, removing umbilical vein and umbilical artery, peeling off Wharton glue, washing for 2-3 times, and cutting to a tissue size of (1-2mm)x(1-2mm)x(1-2mm) piece, (2) evenly inoculating chopped tissue pieces in a T175 culture flask, adding culture solution, and placing in an incubator for culture, the culture temperature is 37 degrees C, the carbon dioxide concentration is 1-2%, and the inner monolayer is flattening, (3) replacing fresh culture medium on third day of culture, placing in incubator and culturing, the culture temperature is 37 degrees C, adjusting carbon dioxide concentration to 5%, and the inner single layer is flattened culture, (4) change medium for the second time on the 6th day of cultivation, after changing medium, placing in an incubator for cultivation at 37 degrees C and a carbon dioxide concentration of 5%, (5) on the 9th day of culture, performing third medium change, after changing medium, placing in an incubator and cultivating at 37 degrees C and a carbon dioxide concentration of 5%, (6) culturing until cell confluence reaches not greater than 60-70%, removing supernatant to obtain primary mesenchymal stem cells, (7) digesting primary mesenchymal stem cells, inoculating in T175 culture flasks, adding culture solution, and placing in an incubator for culture at 37 degrees C, the carbon dioxide concentration is 1-2%, and the inner monolayer flat culture for 3 days, change the medium, culturing until cell confluence reaches not greater than 60-70%, removing supernatant, and obtain passaged mesenchymal stem cells, the culture solution includes basic culture solution, fetal bovine serum, melatonin and Vitamin C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The basic culture medium is a-minimal essential medium medium. The content of the fetal bovine serum is 10%. The content of the melatonin is 20 nM. The content of the Vitamin C is 20 mu g/ml.

PN WO2020208008-A1; AU2020272084-A1; CA3127978-A1; CN113544087-A; IN202117034079-A; KR2021151778-A; EP3953295-A1; US2022081289-A1
TI Chemical synthesis plant has syngas stage            comprising methanation section and/or reverse water gas            shift section, and autothermal reforming (ATR) section,            and synthesis stage in which the plant does not            comprise external feed of hydrocarbons.
AU DE SARKAR S
   CHRISTENSEN T S
   OSTBERG M
   AASBERG-PETERSEN K
   DESARCARSUDIP
   WSTBERG M
AE HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
   HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
   HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
   HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
GA 2020A0283W
AB    NOVELTY - A chemical synthesis plant (X) comprises                syngas stage (A) comprising methanation section                and/or reverse water gas shift (rWGS) section, and                an autothermal reforming (ATR) section; and                synthesis stage (B). A 1st feed (1) comprising                hydrogen is introduced to syngas stage (A); a 2nd                feed (2) comprising carbon dioxide is introduced to                syngas stage (A); and a 3rd feed (3) comprising                oxygen is introduced to ATR section. Syngas stage                (A) is arranged to provide syngas stream (100) and                feed the syngas stream to the synthesis stage (B),                where the plant does not comprise an external feed                of hydrocarbons.
   USE - A chemical synthesis plant.
   ADVANTAGE - Carbon dioxide (CO2), hydrogen (H2), and                oxygen (O2) can be converted to syngas with a                desired H2:CO ratio, without using any external                hydrocarbon feed to the plant. Any hydrocarbon                streams generated in the synthesis stage for                synthesis gas production can be utilized. A higher                utilization of the carbon dioxide feed is possible                compared to a stand-alone rWGS section, e.g. more                CO2 feed is utilized instead of external                hydrocarbon feed as a source of carbon. If an                electrolyzer is used as a portion or all of the                source of the hydrogen feed to the process, a                portion or all of the oxygen, generated in the                electrolyzer along with H2, can be used as the                oxygen source that is required in the plant. The                combination of rWGS and ATR allows utilizing                internal recycle streams that can be added to the                ATR section and utilized for additional synthesis                gas production compared to what is possible with a                stand-alone and selective rWGS. Furthermore, the                outlet temperature from the ATR reactor in the ATR                section will typically be in the range of 900-1100                degrees C, which is, in most cases, higher than is                possible with a stand-alone rWGS unit. This                increases the production of carbon monoxide as this                is thermodynamically favored by higher temperature.                Converting a portion of the carbon dioxide in the                rWGS section may reduce the overall oxygen                consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                production of product stream which involves                providing plant (X), supplying 1st feed comprising                hydrogen to syngas stage (A), supplying 2nd feed                (2) comprising carbon dioxide to syngas stage (A),                supplying 3rd feed comprising oxygen to ATR                section, and converting 1st, 2nd, 3rd, and                optionally, 4th feeds in syngas stage (A) to a                syngas stream and feeding syngas stream to the                synthesis stage (B); converting syngas stream in                the synthesis stage (B) into at least a product                stream and a hydrocarbon-containing stream; and                optionally, feeding at least a portion of the                hydrocarbon-containing stream or at least a portion                of product stream to the syngas stage (A) as 4th                feed comprising hydrocarbons, upstream of ATR                section, and preferably between methanation section                and/or rWGS section and the ATR section.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the                chemical synthesis plant.
   1st feed comprising hydrogen (1)
   2nd feed comprising carbon dioxide (2)
   3rd feed comprising oxygen (3)
   Syngas stream (100)
   Syngas stage (A)
   Synthesis stage (B)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Compositions: Syngas stream at the                  inlet of synthesis stage (B) has a                  hydrogen/carbon monoxide ratio of 1-4, preferably                  1.5-2.1. Hydrogen:carbon dioxide (H2:CO2) ratio                  provided at the plant inlet is 1-9, preferably                  3-7. Synthesis stage (B) is a Fischer-Tropsch                  (FT) synthesis stage, where H2:CO2-ratio at the                  plant inlet is 3-7, preferably 3-5, and is                  arranged to convert syngas stream into at least a                  hydrocarbon product stream and a                  hydrocarbon-containing off-gas stream.

PN CN110076459-A; CN110076459-B
TI Processing lampshade decorative circle laser engraving device comprises e.g. spraying outer surface of lampshade decorative ring with ultraviolet paint layer, leveling, curing by using ultraviolet light, and spraying vacuum coating layer.
AU XUE Y
AE SUZHOU MOTERI AUTO PARTS CO LTD (SUZH-Non-standard)
GA 201969530F
AB    NOVELTY - Processing lampshade decorative circle laser engraving device comprises e.g. (i) spraying the surface of the outer surface of the lampshade decorative ring with a ultraviolet (UV) paint layer, baking and leveling in an oven, and curing by using UV light, (ii) spraying a vacuum coating layer on the surface of the UV color lacquer layer, and placing the lampshade decorative ring after applying the UV color lacquer layer in a vacuum plating furnace to perform evaporation plating of the target, (iii) placing the lampshade decorative ring in the positioning fixture, and removing the area of the vacuum coating layer on the surface of the UV paint layer for laser engraving, where the depth of the laser engraving is greater than the thickness of the vacuum coating layer, and (iv) removing the surface paint surface of the outer ring of the lampshade decorative ring with a carbon dioxide laser, and adjusting the focus of the carbon dioxide laser by positioning the base point in the fixture.
   USE - The method is useful for processing lampshade decorative circle laser engraving device.
   ADVANTAGE - The method realizes smooth and complete paint surface of the product.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Processing lampshade decorative circle laser engraving device comprises (i) spraying the surface of the outer surface of the lampshade decorative ring with a ultraviolet (UV) paint layer, baking and leveling in an oven, and curing by using UV light, (ii) spraying a vacuum coating layer on the surface of the UV color lacquer layer, and placing the lampshade decorative ring after applying the UV color lacquer layer in a vacuum plating furnace to perform evaporation plating of the target, (iii) placing the lampshade decorative ring in the positioning fixture, and removing the area of the vacuum coating layer on the surface of the UV paint layer for laser engraving, where the depth of the laser engraving is greater than the thickness of the vacuum coating layer, (iv) removing the surface paint surface of the outer ring of the lampshade decorative ring with a carbon dioxide laser, and adjusting the focus of the carbon dioxide laser by positioning the base point in the fixture, and (v) moving the carbon dioxide laser along the set trajectory, so that the focus point of the laser moves along the vacuum coating layer of the outer ring of the lampshade decorative ring, laser-processing to form a groove structure, moving the focus point of the carbon dioxide laser, where the laser beam is obliquely incident on the vacuum coating layer by polarization control, so that an angle is formed between the sidewall of the groove structure and the surface of the UV paint layer, and (vi) laser-engraving, and cleaning the lampshade decorative ring. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for lampshade decorative ring positioning fixture, comprising e.g. a bottom plate, an annular groove set in the bottom plate, and multiple positioning components set inside the annular groove.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic representation of the lampshade decorative ring positioning fixture.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the thickness of the UV color lacquer layer is 20-35 mu m, and the UV color lacquer layer is tempered by a UV transparent plating primer and a UV solvent in a color paste ratio to spray the product. In the step (ii), the thickness of the vacuum coating layer is 4-8 mu m, and the lampshade decorative ring coated with the UV color paint layer is placed in a vacuum plating furnace, and the target material is placed, then the air in the furnace is extracted to reach a preset vacuum value for evaporation plating. In the step (iv), in the correction software of the carbon dioxide laser, the carbon dioxide laser is corrected according to the difference between the theoretical value coordinate and the actual value coordinate, and the carbon dioxide laser frequency is set according to the thickness of the paint layer on the surface of the lampshade decorative ring, and the carbon dioxide laser frequency is 40000-45000 Hz., and the emission time is 1.5-2 mu s, the fill line width of the carbon dioxide laser is 0.05-0.1 mm, and the carbon dioxide laser scanning speed is 700-1200 mm/second. In the step (v), the side wall of the groove structure forms an angle with the surface of the UV paint layer, and the angle is 45-60 degrees .

PT P
PN CN108794299-A
TI Purifying solanesol comprises e.g. taking tobacco leaf materials, extracting the crude extract from the tobacco leaf material using supercritical carbon dioxide and the crude extract comprises a solanesol component and mixed component.
AU LIANG M
   BAO X
   HONG Z
AE UNIV I-SHOU (UYIU-C)
GA 201892729S
AB    NOVELTY - Purifying solanesol comprises e.g. taking tobacco leaf materials, extracting the crude extract from the tobacco leaf material using supercritical carbon dioxide, where the crude extract comprises a solanesol component and a mixed component, separating the solanesol component of the crude extract by simulated moving bed chromatography, where the simulated moving bed chromatography method comprises (i) taking a simulated moving bed, where the simulated moving bed sequentially comprises a first section, a second section, and a third section, and is composed of a mobile phase and a stationary phase, where the mobile phase comprises a polyester solution, the interior of the stationary phase particles has pores, the movement is between the first section, the second section, and the third section in the same direction relative to the simulated moving bed and the stationary phase being relative to the simulating movement in the opposite direction.
   USE - The method is useful for purifying solanesol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: separates the solanesol component of the crude extract using simulated moving bed chromatography which can produce high purity solanesol.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Purifying solanesol comprises taking tobacco leaf materials, extracting the crude extract from the tobacco leaf material using supercritical carbon dioxide, where the crude extract comprises a solanesol component and a mixed component, separating the solanesol component of the crude extract by simulated moving bed chromatography, where the simulated moving bed chromatography method comprises (i) taking a simulated moving bed, where the simulated moving bed sequentially comprises a first section, a second section, and a third section, and is composed of a mobile phase and a stationary phase, where the mobile phase comprises a polyester solution, the interior of the stationary phase particles has pores, the movement is between the first section, the second section, and the third section in the same direction relative to the simulated moving bed and the stationary phase being relative to the simulating movement in the opposite direction, (ii) injecting the crude extract between the second section and the third section of the simulated moving bed, moving the solanesol component to the extraction end between the first section and the second section with the stationary phase and moving the mixed component to the first phase with the moving phase a raffinate end of the three sections or moving the mixed component to the extraction end between the first section and the second section with the stationary phase and moving mobile phase to the raffinate end of the third section to separate and purify the solanesol component. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for extracting the crude extract from the tobacco leaf material by supercritical carbon dioxide by loading the tobacco leaf material into an extraction tank contained with pure carbon dioxide for extraction for 6 hours, adding the ethanol auxiliary solvent, performing extraction for 2 hours, pumping the tobacco leaf material into the back pressure valve of the separation tank to extract the crude extract under 35 MPa pressure at 50 degrees C, where the flow rate of the carbon dioxide is 60 g/minute, and flow rate of the ethanol is 4.99 cc/minute.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The first section, the second section, and the third section each comprise two pipe columns and each pipe column is filled with the stationary phase particles with pores inside. The solanesol component moves with the stationary phase to the extraction end between the first section and the second section, and the mixed component moves to the mobile phase to the raffinate end of the third section. The separation conditions used in the first separation step is as follows: the mobile phase is a solution having ethanol/water volume ratio of 95/5, the stationary phase is 18C, the flow rate of the flushing inlet is 8 ml/minute, the feed inlet has a flow rate of 0.4 ml/minute, the flow rate at the extraction end is 4.5 ml/minute, the flow rate of the raffinate end is 3.95 ml/minute, the switching time of the simulated moving bed is 5 minutes and 15 seconds. The separation conditions used in the second separation step are as follows: the mobile phase is a solution having ethanol/water volume ratio of 95/5, the stationary phase is 18C, the flow rate of the flushing inlet is 6 ml/minute, the feed inlet has a flow rate of 0.4 ml/minute, the flow rate at the extraction end is 2.5 ml/minute, the flow rate of the raffinate end is 3.9 ml/minute, the switching time of the simulated moving bed is 6 minutes and 35 seconds. When the solanesol component is moved with the stationary phase to the extraction end between the first section and the second section, and then the mixing component is the moving phase moves to the raffinate end of the third segment, the separation conditions for the simulated moving bed are follows: the mobile phase end pressure is set to 180 bar, the raffinate pressure is set to 160 bar, the temperature is set to 40 degrees C, the set carbon dioxide flow rate has 8.4 g/minute of the mobile phase end, the raffinate end is 5.4 g/minute, the feed inlet is 0.48 g/minute. The set auxiliary solvent flow rate is 1.176 ml/minute of the mobile phase end, the feed inlet is 0.06 ml/minute, the stationary phase is silica gel, and the switching time of the simulated moving bed is 6 minutes. When the mixed component moves with the stationary phase to the extraction end between the first segment and the second segment, then the solanesol component is mobile phase moves to the raffinate end of the third segment. The separation conditions for the simulated moving bed are as follows: the mobile phase is a mixed solution having n-hexane/isopropanol according to the volume ratio of 95/5, the stationary phase is silica gel, the flow rate at the inlet of the rinsing end is 5.4 ml/minute, the flow rate of the feed inlet is 0.2 ml/minute, the flow rate of the raffinate outlet end is 3.3 ml/minute, the flow rate of the raffinate end is 2.3 ml/minute, and the switching time of the stimulating moving bed is 9 minutes. The solanesol component content separated is more than 74%. The solanesol component and the mixed component can have a first retention constant or a second retention constant respectively. The first retention constant is greater than the second retention constant and the first relative flow rate ratio m1 of the first segment should be greater than the first retention constant and the relative flow rate ratio m2 of the second section and the flow rate ratio m3 of the third section are between the first retention constant and the second retention constant. Preferred Method: The method of purifying solanesol further comprises a second separation step of the solanesol component collected at the extraction end to separate strong retention impurities in the solanesol component, where the second separating step comprises injecting the solanesol component collected by the extraction end and the strong retention impurities into the second section and the third portion of the simulated moving bed between the sections, moving the strong retention impurities along the stationary phase to the extraction end between the first section and the second section and moving the solanesol component along with the mobile phase to the raffinate end of the third section.

TI Synthesizing carbon nanotubes with high yield and            good conductivity for preparing lithium-ion battery,            involves mixing water, ferric nitrate nonahydrate,            cobalt nitrate hexahydrate, aluminum nitrate            nonahydrate and magnesium nitrate hexahydrate            uniformly, heating, evaporating and concentrating.

   SHEN Y
AE WUXI DONGHENG NEW ENERGY TECHNOLOGY CO (WUXI-Non-standard)
GA 202277768R
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing carbon nanotubes involves mixing                water, ferric nitrate nonahydrate, cobalt nitrate                hexahydrate, aluminum nitrate nonahydrate and                magnesium nitrate hexahydrate uniformly, heating,                evaporating and concentrating until density of                solution is 1.33 grams/milliliter (g/mL) to obtain                solution, roasting the solution for 180 minutes at                640degrees Celsius by using air atmosphere                continuous pushed slab kiln, and crushing and                sieving to obtain catalyst. The catalyst is added                into fluidized bed reactor, and hydrogen is                introduced to reduce for 10 minutes at 660degrees                Celsius. The supply of hydrogen is stopped,                nitrogen and propylene are introduced, and reacted                at 660degrees Celsius for 40 minutes. The supply of                propylene is stopped, nitrogen and carbon dioxide                are introduced, and heated at 760-800degrees                Celsius within 30 minutes. The supply of nitrogen                and carbon dioxide is stopped, and methane and                hydrogen are introduced to react for 40 minutes at                760-800degrees Celsius. The ventilation is stopped,                and product is output to obtain carbon nano                tube.
   USE - Method for synthesizing carbon nanotubes with                high yield and good conductivity is used in the                fields of electronics, energy, and communications                for the preparation of lithium-ion batteries (all                claimed).

AB    NOVELTY - Solid carbon material comprises calcium                compound, ferric sulfate and biological carbon. The                mass ratio of the compound of calcium to ferric                sulfate is 1.5-2.5: 1. The compound of calcium is                calcium oxide or calcium hydroxide. The solid                carbon material is prepared by mixing the mixture                of calcium compound and ferric sulphate with water,                the mass ratio of the mixture of calcium compound                and ferric sulfate and water is 1: 4-5, mixing the                mixture of calcium compound and ferric sulfate with                water, raising the temperature to 80-85degrees                Celsius, stirring at high speed for 4-6 hours to                obtain calcium, iron layered double metal hydroxide                slurry, preparing biological carbon, mixing the                prepared biological carbon with calcium, iron                layered double metal hydroxide slurry uniformly to                obtain the solid carbon material, the mass ratio of                the biological carbon and calcium, iron layered                double metal hydroxide slurry is 1: 2-3.

It not only can passivate heavy metal                ion in polluted farmland, but also can hold organic                carbon in soil, also can absorb large amounts of                carbon dioxide in air, realize carbon                neutralization, and achieve the purpose of carbon                fixation and carbon reduction.

PN CN114097776-A
TI Preparing plant material-based mixed extract involves subjecting cattail peach peel to dry distillation, separating transparent layer liquid by siphon method, and mixing with lemon citronella and chinaberry leaf extracts prepared by supercritical carbon dioxide extraction and deionized water.
AU CUI B
   WU C
   SONG J
   GAO Y
AE SUZHOU POLYTECHNIC AGRIC INST (SUZH-Non-standard)
GA 2022382617
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing plant material-based mixed extract, comprises (1) placing cattail peach peel in dry distillation kettle, heating to 160 degreesC, and constant temperature processing for 1 hour, then reacting at 240 degreesC for 2 hours, collecting dry distillation liquid of each period, mixing, allowing to stand for 3 days, settling and separating, and separating transparent layer liquid by siphon method, (2) drying lemon citronella and chinaberry leaves, crushing, and sieving, (3) subjecting lemon citronella and chinaberry leaves to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction to obtain lemon citronella extract solution and chinaberry leaf extract solution, (4) concentrating the lemon citronella extract solution and chinaberry leaf extract solution, drying, crushing and sieving, and (5) mixing the obtained lemon citronella and chinaberry leaf powders with deionized water, adding the transparent layer liquid of step (1), depositing at 20-22 degrees Celsius, filtering, and preparing into mixed extract.
   USE - The plant material-based mixed extract is useful in antifungal processing of seeds (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method extracts plant essential oil to use in antifungal processing of seeds.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for use method of the plant material-based mixed extract in antifungal processing of seeds, comprising soaking seeds in the mixed extract, placing at 20-22 degrees Celsius under dark environment and storing overnight, and placing the processed seeds on the cheese cloth and drying, where the mixed extract includes cattail peach peel, lemon citronella, chinaberry leaves, deionized water and alcohol.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Method: The step (1) is replaced by the following step comprising crushing the cattail peach peel into micro powder, soaking for 1-3 hours, extracting by continuous reflux technology under the ultrasonic environment, and separating to obtain transparent layer liquid. The extraction conditions in the step (3) includes extraction temperature of 38-40 degrees Celsius, extracting pressure of 30 MPa, extracting time of 120 minutes, flow rate of the solvent carbon dioxide is 16 L/hour, and cosolvent of 70% ethanol. The filtering process in the step (5) is carried out by filtering using four layers of cheese cloth, and then performing sterile filtration through 0.2 microm filter. The outer surface of the dried seeds is formed with a layer of coat, the coat is further added with slow release agent, the slow release agent is capable of slowly releasing the mixed extract to the soil around the seed.Preferred Components: The transparent layer liquid is capable of acting on the adhesion or absorption force of the soluble inhibitory compound of lemon citronella and the chinaberry leaves. The concentration of lemon citronella is 30%. The lemon citronella extract does not affect the development of the seedling, and has the effect of antifungal. The chinaberry leaf extract can increase the survival rate of the seedling. The seeds are Sorghum seeds.

AB    NOVELTY - The method involves perforating a vertical                well or a horizontal well. A metal sleeve is                penetrated in a wellbore. A                channel is established for fluid exchange between                the shale reservoir and the well bore. The shale                gas is flow in the stratum into the well. Methane                gas or rocket propellant is injected into a well.                High pressure gas is formed to carry a supporting                agent to a crack deep portion in a crack forming                process. The crack is closed on the supporting                agent after well cylinder (1) pressure is reduced.                A scale of fracturing is reached until a design                requirement. Secondary detonation is performed.                Stress waves and high-pressure gas are formed such                that the cracks continue to expand. Liquid carbon                dioxide is used to carry proppant into the                formation to support a fractured zone near a                wellbore.

PN CN113820289-A
TI Measuring oxygen and nitrogen content in titanium material as aircraft engine and body material and automobile valve rocker material,, involves using multipoint calibration method respectively independently establish oxygen standard curve.
AU FU R
   DONG S
   MA C
   MA J
   LI Y
   ZHU J
AE TIANJIN MEDICAL DEVICE QUALITY SUPERVISI (TIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202206647E
AB    NOVELTY - Measuring oxygen and nitrogen content in titanium material involves using multi-point calibration method respectively independently establish oxygen standard curve and nitrogen standard curve, wrapping the titanium material to be tested by using the nickel material to form the wrapping body putting the wrap body obtained in a graphite container, introducing inert gas and heating the molten wrap body, releasing carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and nitrogen, detecting the carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide, measuring the oxygen content in the titanium material according to the oxygen standard curve, detecting the nitrogen obtained and measuring the nitrogen content in the titanium material according to the nitrogen standard curve.
   USE - Method for measuring oxygen and nitrogen content in titanium material used as aircraft engine and body material and automobile valve rocker material, and in biological medicine and automobile industry.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables measuring oxygen nitrogen element with high content in titanium material, and the analysis method is accurate and reliable, so that the analysis result is stable, thus improving the accuracy and stability of the measuring result, overcoming the problem of large deviation of the measurement result obtained by the conventional calibration method. The method improves the measuring range of oxygen content to 1-5 wt.% and the measuring ranges of nitrogen content to 0.5-1 wt., and the oxygen/nitrogen element quality of the sample to be tested is similar to the oxygen standard curve and nitrogen standard curve of the standard sample, thus greatly improving the measuring accuracy.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The method involves selecting at least 3 different oxygen content of the first standard sample, where each of the first standard sample at least analyzing oxygen content of 3 times, taking the average value, using multi-point calibration program to establish oxygen standard curve, the correlation coefficient R2 of the oxygen standard curve is 0.99. The oxygen standard curve after establishing the first accuracy test, the specific process of the first accuracy inspection incldues measuring the first standard sample of the intermediate oxygen content according to the oxygen standard curve, when the analysis result is in the range of uncertainty, indicating that the accuracy of the oxygen standard curve is good, otherwise, performing correction processing. The method involves selecting at least 3 different nitrogen content of the second standard sample, where each of the second standard sample analyzing at least 3 nitrogen content, taking the average value, using multi-point calibration program to establish nitrogen standard curve, the correlation coefficient R2 of the nitrogen standard curve is 0.99. The nitrogen standard curve after establishing the second accuracy inspection, then second accuracy inspection incldues measuring the second standard sample of the intermediate nitrogen content according to the nitrogen standard curve, when the analysis result is in the range of uncertainty, indicating that the accuracy of the nitrogen standard curve is good, otherwise, performing correction processing. The mass ratio of the nickel material and the titanium material to be tested is (7-17):1, the mass of the titanium material to be measured is 87mg. The graphite container comprises a graphite crucible, the inert gas comprises helium gas or argon gas, the heating melting temperature is 2200-2300degrees Celsius, the oxygen in the heating and melting to the titanium material is completely released with the nitrogen element. The detection is carried out by an infrared detector.    USE - The genetically modified organism is useful for producing trehalose and/or glucose, optionally isotope-labelled trehalose and/or isotope labelled glucose (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The genetically modified organism: provides an efficient process that can be performed with whole cells providing an efficient process that can be performed with whole cells i.e. without the need for enzyme purification, and that uses easily available substrates e.g. gaseous substrates e.g. a mixture of hydrogen gas, carbon dioxide and oxygen; provides a increased secretion of sugars; avoids feedstock e.g. means for an area-efficient production of sugars are provided; provides more sustainable thancommon methods of producing trehalose; and provides more cost-efficient than existing methods for trehalose production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for producing trehalose and/or glucose, comprising (i) providing a genetically modified organism and/or a genetically modified organism expressing a sugar efflux transporter, preferably SetA, optionally further overexpressing at least one trehalose synthesis gene(s), (ii) culturing the genetically modified organism of step (i) in a cell culture, the culturing comprising supplying aninorganic electron source, preferably hydrogen in a gaseous mixture comprising hydrogen, oxygen, and carbon dioxide, and/or phosphite, and/or supplying any of nitrate as electron acceptor, carbonate, or an organic carbon source, e.g. fructose,succinic acid, acetic acid, and/or glycerol, and (iii) obtaining trehalose and/or glucose from the cell culture.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preparation: No preparation method given. Preferred Components: The expression optionally overexpression, comprises an expression of an exogenous gene of the sugar efflux transporter, optionally at least one trehalose synthesis gene(s), in the organism, and/or comprises an increased expression of an endogenous gene of the sugar efflux transporter, optionally the trehalose synthesis gene(s), in the organism. The organism is a chemolithotrophic organism, preferably comprises hydrogen oxidizing bacteria, autotrophic phosphite oxidizing bacteria, autotrophic nitrate/nitrite or sulfate respiring microorganisms, and genetically modified organisms having obtained chemolithotrophic characteristics by genetic modification, more preferably Cupriavidus necator. The organism is a methylotrophic organism, preferably any organism comprising: Cupriavidus species, e.g. ; Cupriavidus necator H16; Bacillusspecies, e.g.Bacillus methanolicus;Methylobacterium species, e.g. Methylobacterium extorquens; Hydrogenophaga species, e.g. Hydrogenophaga pseudoflava; Rhodococcus species, e.g. Rhodococcus opacus; Ralstonia species; Pichia species, e.g. Pichia pastoris; Komagatella species; and Paracoccus species, e.g. Paracoccus denitrificans; or genetically modified organisms having obtained methylotrophic characteristics by genetic modification. The sugar efflux transporteris any of a sugar uniporter, a H+ or Na+-antiporter, and/or an ATP-dependent efflux transport protein, preferably isa sugar uniporter or H+/sugar-antiporter comprises the sugar efflux transporter A (SetA) from Escherichia coli (SEQ ID NO: 1 (comprising a fully defined sequence of 392 nucleotides as given in the specification)), the glucose facilitator Glf from Zymomonas mobilis (SEQ ID NO: 2 (comprising a fully defined sequence of 473 nucleotides as given in the specification)), the transport protein YdeAfrom Escherichia coli (SEQ ID NO: 3 (comprising a fully defined sequence of 395 nucleotides as given in the specification)), a protein of the SWEET transport protein family, the Tret1-1 trehalose facilitator from Drosophila melanogaster(SEQ ID NO: 4 (comprising a fully defined sequence of 857 nucleotides as given in the specification)), a protein from the (GLUT)/SLC2A transport protein family, and homologuesand variants thereof, more preferably is SetA. The SetA is derived from Escherichia coli, preferably SetA having SEQ ID NO: 1, and/or is a SetA homologue or variant. The trehalose synthesis gene(s) comprises phosphoglucosemutase, UTP-glucose-i-phosphate uridylyltransferase, trehalose phosphate synthetase, trehalose-phosphate phosphatase, maltooligosyl trehalose synthase, and maltooligosyltrehalose hydrolase. The organism is transformedwith pSEVA228-setA having SEQ ID NO: 5 (comprising a fully defined sequence of 6946 nucleotides as given in the specification). The organism is Cupriavidus necator Cn-H16-setA deposited at the Deutsche Sammlung von Mikro organismen und Zellkulturen GmbH having accession number DSM 33415. The gaseous mixture comprises 20-99 vol.% hydrogen, 0.1-20 vol.% oxygen, and 0.1-20 vol.% carbon dioxide, where hydrogen, oxygen, and carbon dioxide are supplied in a ratio (v/v)of 6:1:1, respectively, to the cell culture. Preferred Method: The trehalose and/or glucose is/are obtained from a supernatant of the cell culture. The step of obtaining of glucose further comprises enzymatic cleavage of trehalose by trehalase to obtain glucose. The step of producing isotope-labelled trehalose and/or isotope-labelled glucose comprising supplying an inorganic electron source, preferably hydrogen in a gaseous mixture comprising hydrogen, isotope labelled carbon dioxide, and oxygen, and/or phosphite, and/or supplying nitrate as an electron acceptor, and/or supplying anyof isotope-labelled carbonate, fructose, succinic acid, acetic acid, and/or glycerol, in step (iii), obtaining isotope-labelled trehalose and/or isotope-labelled glucose, preferably isotope-labelled glucose.

PT P
PN CN113501518-A
TI Modified biochar useful for repairing heavy metal pollution in soil , prepared by air-drying corn straw, crushing, adding water, stirring, feeding into carbonizing furnace, closing, introducing carbon dioxide gas, discharging air, heating, carbonizing, cooling , heating and grinding by ball mill.
AU WANG X
   CHEN J
   TIAN C
   SHI H
AE JIANGSU SUHUAN ECOLOGICAL TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021C4525U
AB    NOVELTY - Modified biochar, prepared by: (i) air-drying corn straw; (ii) crushing corn straw to obtain straw powder; (iii) adding water into straw powder, and stirring until moisture content of straw powder is 20-30%; (iv) feeding straw powder into carbonizing furnace, and closing carbonizing furnace; (v) introducing carbon dioxide gas, and discharging air in carbonizing furnace; (vi) heating at 100degrees Celsius for 15-30 minutes; (vii) carbonizing at 680-750degrees Celsius for 1-2 hours to obtain modified biochar; (viii) cooling modified biochar to room temperature; and (xi) heating and grinding modified biochar by using ball mill to obtain modified biochar powder with grain diameter of 10-20 nm.
   USE - The modified biochar is useful for repairing heavy metal pollution in soil (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The modified biocarbon has larger specific area, pore volume, richer oxygen-containing functional group and stronger pollutant adsorption capacity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for repairing heavy metal pollution in soil, comprising: (i) adding not less than 20 wt.% modified biochar powder into mulching film; (ii) paving mulching film on top of soil or burying mulching film below soil; where use time of mulching film is not less than 3 months, and it must go through rain; (iii) recycling mulching film after mulching film reaches set use requirement; (iv) burning mulching film at high temperature to obtaining residue containing heavy metal; and (v) processing residue in a harmless manner.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Method: In the step (vi), the heating speed is 5-10degrees Celsius/minute. In the step (vii), the heating speed is 10-20degrees Celsius/minute. In the step (ii), the straw powder is sieved through 0.8-1 cm sieve. In the step (viii), the carbon dioxide gas is introduced when cooling, and the gas supply is stopped when temperature is reduced to less than 600degrees Celsius. When mulching film is buried below soil, the soil at position of 4-6 cm above ground surface is plowed and buried in upper part of mulching film, and the mulching film is opened with multiple ventilation holes, and the plant is grown through mulching film. The adding amount of modified biochar powder to soil is 30-50 kg/mu.

PN US2021271877-A1; US11195015-B2
TI Agricultural method involves providing a positive            air pressure chamber to prevent outside contaminants            from entering the chamber, growing crops in multiple            cells in the chamber and maintaining pre-set            temperature, humidity, carbon dioxide.
AU TRAN H
   TRAN B
AE TRAN H (TRAN-Individual)
   TRAN B (TRAN-Individual)
GA 2021A18184
AB    NOVELTY - The agricultural method involves providing a                positive air pressure chamber to prevent outside                contaminants from entering the chamber. The crops                are grown in many cells in the chamber, where each                cell has multi-grow benches or levels, and further                has connectors to vertical hoists for vertical                movements. The pre-set temperature, humidity,                carbon dioxide, watering and lighting levels are                maintained to achieve predetermined plant growth.                The motorized transport rails are used to deliver                benches for operations including seeding,                harvesting, grow media recovery, and bench wash,                and the seeds are dispensed in the cell with a                mechanical seeder coupled to the transport                rails.
   USE - Agricultural method for growing food and                blockchain to track progress through supply chain                to consumers.
   ADVANTAGE - The microbe-powered technology can be directly                applied to soil or incorporated into fertilizer or                embedded as seed coatings, and such delivery modes                can increase nutrient delivery, provide pest                protection, boost crop quality, and supplement the                plant microbiome. The system provides growers the                ability to significantly increase their profits and                allow them to grow their crops in a sustainable                manner. A de-risked business model creates a strong                incentive alignment and pushes the technology to                the farm at no risk to the grower. The data                collected during the planting and growing seasons                provide for visibility into the ag supply chain and                continued improvement of the core microbiome                solutions. By partnering with growers at the time                of planting and providing agronomic support                throughout the growing season, the artificial                intelligence (AI) engine can predict local yield                data and supply volume well before harvest.

GA 2021A2416V

The reaction tank is provided with a vortex mechanism. The left end of the reaction tank is provided with an inflating mechanism matched with the vortex mechanism. The lower end of the reaction box is provided with flux mechanism located between the four base.

PN CN113045441-A
TI Producing feed and food-grade glycine, comprises e.g. adding aqueous solution of hydroxyacetonitrile to aqueous solution of ammonium carbonate, heating, reacting in presence of zirconium raw material, and removing ammonia and carbon dioxide.
AU LI X
   YING G
   GENG H
AE BOZUN INVESTMENT GROUP CO LTD (BOZU-Non-standard)
GA 202178938T
AB    NOVELTY - Producing feed and food-grade glycine, comprises (i) adding an aqueous solution of hydroxyacetonitrile to aqueous solution of ammonium carbonate, heating the resulting reaction system to 100-135 degrees C, reacting in presence of zirconium raw material, then heating to 150-160 degrees C, continuously reacting, controlling liquid level of the reaction to be above 75%, where the zirconium raw material comprises zirconium, zirconium-containing alloy, zirconium salt and/or zirconium oxide, (ii) in the presence of the zirconium raw material, concentrating the reaction liquid under heating, removing ammonia and carbon dioxide, and adding water to obtain an aqueous solution of glycine, and (iii) decoloring aqueous glycine solution, in the presence of the zirconium raw material, concentrating the decolorizing solution under reduced pressure, cooling concentrated liquid, and performing crystallization to obtain glycine and a crystallization mother liquor.
   USE - The method is useful for producing feed and food-grade glycine.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has no three wastes generation; is clean, environmentally friendly and economical; does not utilize organic solvents, and multiple recrystallizations; and has high feed-grade and food-grade glycine yield, and high degree of automation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for device for carrying out the feed and food-grade glycine production method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of device for carrying out feed and food-grade glycine production method (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further comprises dissolving the glycine obtained in the step (iii) with water, performing decolorization, and cooling and performing recrystallization to separate the recrystallized glycine and the recrystallization mother liquor. In the step (i), before adding the hydroxyacetonitrile aqueous solution, the ammonium carbonate aqueous solution and the hydroxyacetonitrile aqueous solution are respectively preheated to 50-70 degrees C. In the step (i), the method comprises reacting at 100-135 degrees C for 10-60 minutes. In the step (i), the reaction is continued for 150-240 minutes at a pressure of 3.5-5.5 MPa at 150-160 degrees C. In the step (i), the liquid level of the reaction is controlled to be above 85%. In the step (ii), the concentration is performed under heating to 50-110 degrees C and ammonia and carbon dioxide are removed. In the step (ii), the concentration and removal of ammonia and carbon dioxide are performed by stripping, or the concentration and the removal of ammonia and carbon dioxide are performed by separate concentration and stripping. In the step (iii), activated carbon or nanofiltration membrane is used for the decolorization. In the step (iii), the concentrated liquid is cooled to 10-40 degrees C for crystallization. The method further comprises recycling part or all of the crystallization mother liquor and optional recrystallization mother liquor to the step (i), and 10-50 wt.% of the crystallization mother liquor and optional recrystallization mother liquor are recycled to the step (i). The method specifically comprises desalting the crystallization mother liquor and optional recrystallization mother liquor, subjecting the crystallization mother liquor and optional recrystallization mother liquor to electrodialysis desalination or chromatographic desalination, performing the desalination of the crystallization mother liquor and the optional recrystallization mother liquor with a salt content higher than 5 wt.%, after the desalination, the salt content in the crystallization mother liquor and the optional recrystallization mother liquor is less than 0.5 wt.%, and performing desalination using heterogeneous membrane electrodialysis. The production method is batch, semi-continuous and/or continuous operation methods. Preferred Components: In the step (i), the molar ratio of the hydroxyacetonitrile aqueous solution and the ammonium carbonate aqueous solution is 1:3. The molar ratio of water and hydroxyacetonitrile in the reaction system of the step (i) is (43-50):1. In the step (i), the concentration of the aqueous solution of hydroxyacetonitrile is 20-65 wt.%, and the pH is 2-4. In the step (i), the concentration of the aqueous ammonium carbonate solution is 24-33 wt.%. The zirconium raw material is in the form of lining, block or powder. The zirconium-containing alloy is an alloy with a zirconium content of 5-30 wt.%. The zirconium-containing alloy is zirconium-iron alloy, zirconium-cobalt alloy, zirconium-copper alloy, zirconium-tin alloy, zirconium aluminum alloy, and/or zirconium-niobium alloy. The zirconium salt is an inorganic zirconium salt. The inorganic zirconium salt comprises zirconium acid, zirconium chloride, zirconium carbonate, zirconium nitrate, zirconium phosphate, and/or zirconium acetate. In the step (ii), the content of glycine in the reaction feed liquid is 4.5-7.5 wt.%. In the step (ii), relative to the mass of the reaction feed liquid, the mass ratio of the zirconium raw material is 300-1000 ppm. In the step (ii), the concentration of the aqueous glycine solution is 8-20 wt.%. In the step (iii), the decolorizing solution is concentrated under reduced pressure to a glycine content of 30-50 wt.%.

   Turbine (72)
   Shaft (73)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Components: At least two reactor of the compression reactors are arranged one behind the other in terms of flow, so at least one product from the reactor space (1) serves as starting material for other reactor space. The downstream of the reaction space, partial product stream or at least one product or at least one starting material is withdrawn from the total stream and fed back to the reaction space. When the reactor inlets and outlets are closed, additional energy, preferably in the form of electrical sparks, corona discharge, laser pulses or microwaves, is supplied to the reaction chamber without depending the compression energy. The compression reactors work as reciprocating compressors for conveying the products (8). The nitrogen is additionally fed to the compression reactors. The hydrogen and oxygen are produced by splitting water using an electrolyzer. The hydrogen is fed to the reactor spaces of the compression reactor and/or the oxygen is fed to the reactor. The electrolyzer and/or the supplied water is thermally coupled to the product stream and/or the cooling medium of the compression reactors.

TI Preparing catalyst useful for removing formaldehyde, comprises mixing and dripping solution containing precious metal salt and permanganate with porous carrier, adding reducing agent to reduce precious metal salt and permanganate and drying.
AU HU K
   WANG X
   LIU J
   JIN X
   LIN P
   LI Q
AE GREE ELECTRIC APPLIANCES INC (GREZ-C)
GA 202138198V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing catalyst comprises (i) mixing and dripping the solution containing precious metal salt and permanganate with porous carrier to obtain the dipping system; (ii) adding reducing agent into the dipping system to reduce the precious metal salt and permanganate to obtain the catalyst precursor; and (iii) drying the catalyst precursor.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for removing formaldehyde at normal temperature (claimed), by decomposing formaldehyde into carbon dioxide and water.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: does not require heating during impregnation or calcination; reduces the precious metal ions and permanganate by adding a reducing agent to the liquid system after immersion; can complete the successful loading of precious metal and manganese oxides on the support; is simple to operate; utilizes easily available raw materials which is suitable for large-scale production in the field of air purification; and produces catalyst which decomposes formaldehyde into carbon dioxide and water, and does not produce intermediate products which can cause secondary pollution.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) catalyst, prepared as mentioned above;
   (2) catalyst comprises a porous carrier and manganese oxide and noble metal supported on the porous carrier, where the loading amount of the manganese oxide is 5-10 wt.%, and the loading amount of the precious metal is 0.01-1 wt.%;
   (3) kit for removing aldehydes comprises a gas-permeable container and aldehyde removing materials, where the material for removing formaldehyde is the catalyst; and
   (4) an air purifying device comprises a filter screen provided with an aldehyde removal material is the normal temperature formaldehyde removal catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solution is a mixed solution of the precious metal salt and the permanganate at mass ratio of 0.01-0.1:1. The precious metal salt comprises at least one of platinum salt, gold salt, palladium salt, silver salt or rhodium salt. The permanganate comprises at least one of potassium permanganate, sodium permanganate, ammonium permanganate, calcium permanganate, zinc permanganate or magnesium permanganate. Preferred Method: The method comprises carrying out first stirring during the mixing and dipping process, preferably, the first stirring speed is 300-1000 revolutions/minute, and the mixing and immersion time is 40-120 minutes. The drying temperature is 80-110 degrees C, and the drying time is 0.5-3 hours.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The porous carrier comprises at least one of activated carbon, silicon dioxide, alumina, zeolite or cordierite, and the mass ratio of the permanganate to the porous carrier is 0.1-1:1. The reducing agent comprises at least one of ascorbic acid, oxalic acid, ammonium oxalate, sodium oxalate, potassium oxalate, calcium oxalate, citric acid or trisodium citrate. The mass ratio of the reducing agent to the potassium permanganate is 3-1:1. Preferred Method: The time for the reduction is 60-150 minutes and it is preferable to perform the second stirring during the reduction process, and the second stirring speed is 300-1000 revolutions/minute.

TI Device for extraction of organic stream from plant            or plant material comprises product feeder,            conditioning module linked to tank containing carbon            dioxide, continuous extraction module comprising            removable tubes, and filtration module.
AU COX S
   PICKETT D
AE DALRADA HEALTH PROD (DALR-Non-standard)
   DALRADA HEALTH PROD (DALR-Non-standard)
GA 2021281080
AB    NOVELTY - A device for extraction of organic stream from                a plant or plant material comprises product feeder;                conditioning module comprising an oven; continuous                extraction module comprising greater than or equal                to 1 removable tube, where continuous extraction                module is operably linked to a tank containing                carbon dioxide (CO2); and filtration module.
   USE - A device for extraction of organic stream from                a plant or plant material.
   ADVANTAGE - The plant oil extraction system provides a                continuous-flow extraction process for extraction                and purification of plant oil. The continuous-flow                extraction process allows for processing much                greater volumes of raw material without having to                stop and restart material flows, and allows for                scaling-up to higher throughputs easily, without                the need for costly additional machine time and                human labor. Continuous-flow extraction allows for                better control over extraction time and                temperature, so that the negative effects typically                associated with heating are minimized.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) extraction of organic stream from plant or                plant material which involves feeding pre-treated                plant material into the product feeder of the                device; and
   (2) extraction of organic stream from a plant                or plant material which involves (a) providing a                prepared biomass, (b) receiving the prepared                biomass into an extraction vessel, (c) extracting a                product from the biomass by contacting the biomass                in the extraction vessel with a solvent and CO2 to                provide an extract, and (d) providing a membrane                separation process to produce a wax product and the                organic stream.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows an illustration of the                plant oil extraction system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Filtration module                  comprises a membrane. Conditioning module is                  configured to condition and heat the biomass.                  Continuous extraction module is configured to                  receive biomass tube, purge, and fill with CO2;                  circulate CO2 in a first pass; circulate CO2 in a                  second pass; and vent, open, and remove the spent                  biomass tube. Filtration module is configured to                  include organic solvent nanoseparation in an                  integrated membrane separation process.                  Extraction vessel includes a set of tubes                  configured to provide output. Continuous                  extraction vessel is configured to receive                  biomass tube, purge, and fill with CO2; circulate                  supercritical CO2 in a first pass; circulate                  supercritical CO2 in a second pass; and vent,                  open, and remove the spent biomass tube.                  Preferred Compositions: Organic stream comprises                  an oil. Organic stream comprises cannabidiol                  (CBD). Organic stream is substantially free from                  waxes. Organic stream is substantially free from                  terpenes. Product feeder is configured to receive                  plant material, biomass, or other product into a                  receiving receptacle to be fed into a basket                  filling machine for product processing. Organic                  oil product comprises greater than or equal to 1                  cannabinoids. Preferred Processes: Prepared                  biomass is de-lumped and/or milled, prior to                  being received into the system. Organic solvent                  nanoseparation includes in an integrated membrane                  separation process following the separation of                  crude oils from supercritical CO2. Supercritical                  CO2 flow is continuous and between about 1500                  kg/hour and about 3500 kg/hour. Membrane                  purification of the extract involves receiving                  raw biomass extract from continuous extraction                  process; combining raw biomass extract with                  solvent and stirring vigorously until completely                  mixed; recirculating the mixture through organic                  solvent nano-filtration membranes; collecting a                  wax stream and an oil-rich stream; and purifying                  the solvent by distillation and returning to the                  process. Distillation of the purified product                  produces terpenes and oil having greater than or                  equal to 80%, preferably greater than or equal to                  90% purity of the oil.
