PN US2021071861-A1; CA3092298-A1; EP3792217-A1; CN112484008-A
TI Method for producing steam in steam-hydrocarbon reforming facility, involves heating carbon dioxide-depleted process condensate and oxygen-depleted demineralized make-up water by indirect heat exchange with reformate introduced into drum.
AU BELDON A S
   GENKIN E S
AE AIR PROD & CHEM INC (AIRP-C)
   AIR PROD & CHEM INC (AIRP-C)
   AIR PROD & CHEM INC (AIRP-C)
GA 202126816E
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves separating the condensed water from the water-depleted reformate in a separator (90) for forming a water-depleted reformate gas and a process condensate (96). The process condensate is passed to a steam stripping unit, stripping carbon dioxide (CO2) from the process condensate. A CO2 -depleted process condensate is withdrawn from the steam stripping unit. The CO2 -depleted process condensate is passed to a steam drum to produce a product steam. The demineralized make-up water is passed to a membrane separation unit to remove oxygen (O2) from the demineralized make-up water to form an O2 -depleted demineralized make-up water. A first portion or all of the O2 -depleted demineralized make-up water is passed to the steam drum to produce the product steam. The CO2 -depleted process condensate and the O2 -depleted demineralized make-up water are each heated by indirect heat exchange with reformate prior to being introduced into the steam drum.
   USE - Method for producing steam in steam-hydrocarbon reforming facility.
   ADVANTAGE - The membrane separation unit requires less integration with the steam-hydrocarbon reforming process and provides more flexibility in the overall system design. The nergy and water losses associated with the deaerator steam vent are eliminated.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a steam system in steam-hydrocarbon reforming facility.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a steam-hydrocarbon facility that includes an integrated steam system.
   Fuel (5)
   Catalytic steam reformer (10)
   Reformer feedgas mixture (15)
   Separator (90)
   Process condensate (96)

TI Snow burial mask for personal survival device in event of accidental snow burial by avalanche or tree well encounter, has power source and processing unit that is operatively connected to burial sensor and air pump.
AU BROOKES S
AE BROOKES S (BROO-Individual)
GA 202118321E
AB    NOVELTY - The mask has a headgear (1) including a breathing portion below a nose portion of a user and adjacent to a mouth portion of the user. An exhaust tube (4) including an intake tube portion connected to the headgear and positioned adjacently to the breathing portion. An air pump fluidly connected to the exhaust tube and activated by the burial sensor and adapted to force air away from the breathing portion and opposite the face of the user. A power source and a processing unit operatively connected to the burial sensor and the air pump.
   USE - Snow burial mask for personal survival device in event of accidental snow burial by avalanche or tree well encounter.
   ADVANTAGE - The mask can detect a burial event and automatically trigger a pumping mechanism to move air from the user's immediate breathing area to another area, such as behind the user's back, thus removing carbon dioxide buildup around the user's nose and mouth and extending survival time. The user may then bite on the mouthpiece to keep the mask in place during an avalanche or other burial event, avoiding displacement of the mouthpiece and ensuring carbon dioxide displacement functionality.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a snow burial mask for personal survival device in event of accidental snow burial by avalanche or tree well encounter.

   HUNAN HEZHOU ECOLOGICAL SOFTSHELL TURTLE (HUNA-Non-standard)
GA 202115211W
AB    NOVELTY - Chinese herbal medicine liquid comprises 3-6 pts. wt. white andpowder, 8-12 pts. wt. glycerol, 6-8 pts. wt. snake oil, 3-5 pts. wt. Artemisia leaf, 1-2 pts. wt. menthol, 4-7 pts. wt. cortex dictamni, 2-4 pts. wt. rhubarb powder, 5-7 pts. wt. Forsythia powder, 3-5 pts. wt. borneol, 2-4 pts. wt. pseudo-ginseng, 4-5 pts. wt. radix sophorae flavescentis and 800 pts. wt. water.
   USE - The liquid is useful for treating turtleskin rot disease.
   ADVANTAGE - The liquid prevents fall off from the soft-shelled turtle skin ensuring effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing Chinese herbal medicine liquid comprising (i) stir-frying the raw materials of traditional Chinese medicine with a slow flame, beating the powder and sieve, and storing; (ii) mixing Artemisia leaf, cortex dictamni, pseudo-ginseng and radix sophorae flavescentis for supercritical carbon dioxide extraction. The extraction parameters are extraction temperature 40-50 degrees C under extraction pressure 25-30 MPa under analytical pressure 4-8 MPa, analytical temperature 35-45 C, where the carbon dioxide flow rate is 220-260 l/hours under extraction time 2-4 hours, and the extract is obtained; (iii) taking menthol, rhubarb powder, Forsythia powder and water to a boil, adding extract, turning to low heat and heating to 20-40% water content, cooling to room temperature to obtain mixture A; and (iv) mixing white powder, borneol, glycerol, and snake oil into mix A, grinding and mixing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The size of the sieve used for powdering and sieving the raw materials of traditional Chinese medicine is 130-150 mesh. The size of the sieveused for sieving the traditional Chinese medicine rawmaterial is 140 meshes. Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the Artemisia leaf, cortex dictamni, pseudo-ginseng and radix sophorae flavescentis are mixed for supercritical carbon dioxide extraction under extraction parameters 40 degrees C, extraction pressure 25 MPa, analytical pressure 4 MPa, analytical temperature 35 degrees C, and carbon dioxide flow rate of 220 l/hours for 2 hours to obtain extract. In the step (iii), the method comprises menthol, rhubarb powder, Forsythia powder and water are boiled and then poured into the extract, turned to low heat and heated to 20%, preferably 30% water content, cooled to room temperature to obtain mixture A.

PN US2020308519-A1; US11312931-B2
TI Supporting material that is configured to be mounted on 1st frame in 1st mounted geometry, for algal growth system comprises flexible sheet material (having substantially vertical orientation) configured to facilitate growth of biofilm.
AU GROSS M A
   WEN Z
AE GROSS-WEN TECHNOLOGIES INC (GROS-Non-standard)
   UNIV IOWA STATE RES FOUND INC (IOWA-C)
   GROSS-WEN TECHNOLOGIES INC (GROS-Non-standard)
GA 202097062J
AB    NOVELTY - Supporting material (12) for an algal growth system comprises a flexible sheet material configured to facilitate growth of a biofilm. The flexible sheet material is configured to be mounted on a 1st frame in a 1st mounted geometry, the flexible sheet material having a substantially vertical orientation when mounted on the 1st frame such that a 1st height of the 1st mounted geometry is greater than a 1st width of the 1st mounted geometry. The flexible sheet material is configured to be in contact with a contacting liquid (14).
   USE - The supporting material is useful for an algal growth system (claimed); and for photobioreactor for stimulating algal growth and simplified biomass harvesting.
   ADVANTAGE - The photobioreactor or system can utilize minimal growth medium, where the triangular or vertical design may reduce the total water needed for the growth and the chemical costs of growth medium. The advantages may be accomplished by submerging only the lowest portion of bioreactor, supporting material, algal growth system, or mechanized harvesting system into the medium. The algal growth system or mechanized harvesting system can remove concentrated algae in-situ from an attachment material and can minimize the amount of de-watering needed post-harvest; and can optimize gas mass transfer due to the algae cells coming in direct contact with gaseous carbon dioxide when the algae are rotated through the open air.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a partial cutaway perspective view of a revolving algal biofilm photobioreactor.
   Photobioreactor (10)
   Supporting material (12)
   Contacting liquid (14)
   Nutrient-rich liquid phase (15)
   Carbon dioxide-rich gaseous phase (16)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Material: Specifically, supporting material for an algal growth system comprises a flexible sheet material in the form of a contiguous loop, the flexible sheet material being operably configured to grow algae selected from Nannochloropsis, Scenedesmus, Haematococcus, Botryococcus, Spirulina, Dunaliella, Arthrospira, Porphyridium, Phaeodactylum, Nitzschia, Crypthecodinium, and Schizochytrium, where the flexible sheet material is polyester, where the flexible sheet material is hydrophobic and has a positive surface charge, and where a 1st portion of the flexible sheet material is in a nutrient-rich liquid phase (15) and a 2nd portion of the flexible sheet material is in a carbon dioxide (CO2)-rich gaseous phase (16). Preferred Components: The flexible sheet material is a contiguous band having a loop configuration supported by the 1st frame, the contiguous band having a 1st surface and a 2nd surface. The 1st surface is an inner surface of the contiguous band configured to engage at least one roller associated with the 1st frame for movement of the contiguous band and the 2nd surface is an outer surface of the contiguous band configured for the growth of algae. The supporting material further comprises the biofilm attached to the flexible sheet material. The flexible sheet material has a high surface roughness, is hydrophobic, and has a positive surface charge. It includes ribs. It is finned. The flexible sheet material is textured such that rotation of the supporting material agitates the contacting liquid. The flexible sheet material includes at least one flexible region and at least one rigid region. It is a hinged belt. It includes at least one removable section. The flexible sheet material includes a rotating belt, and the removable section is a section of material removably attached to the rotating belt with a fastener, where the section of material is configured to withstand a harvesting mechanism harvesting algae off the section of material. The flexible sheet material comprises a 1st layer and a 2nd layer, the 1st layer having a higher strength than the 2nd layer, the 2nd layer being more easily degraded by water than the 1st layer. The 2nd layer is a cell growth layer. The supporting material further comprises a coating provided on the flexible sheet material for facilitating algal attachment. A 1st portion of the flexible sheet material is in a nutrient-rich liquid phase and a 2nd portion of the flexible sheet material is in CO2-rich gaseous phase. The flexible sheet material is selected from cheesecloth, fiberglass, porous polytetrafluoroethylene (PTFE) coated fiberglass, chamois, vermiculite, microfiber, synthetic chamois, burlap, cotton duck, velvet, poly-lactic acid, abraised poly-lactic acid, vinyl laminated nylon, polyester, wool, acrylic, lanolin, woolen, cashmere, leather, silk, lyocell, hemp fabric, polyurethane, olefin fiber, polylactide, carbon fiber, and combination. It is operably configured to grow algae selected from Nannochloropsis, Scenedesmus, Haematococcus, Botryococcus, Spirulina, Dunaliella, Arthrospira, Porphyridium, Phaeodactylum, Nitzschia, Crypthecodinium, and Schizochytrium. The flexible sheet material is operably configured to withstand a harvesting mechanism harvesting the algae from the biofilm.

The method                  involves replacing the pluripotent stem cells                  with activation concentration of 50 ng/ml, where                  the final concentration of bone marrow                  morphogenetic protein 4 is 40 ng/ml and a                  hematopoietic differentiation medium with a final                  concentration of 2.5 mu g/ml of anti-mycoplasmal                  preparation, two days after hematopoietic                  induction, replacing vascular endothelial growth                  with a final concentration of 40 ng/ml, where                  basic concentration of basic fibroblast growth                  factor is 50 ng/ml, and a final concentration of                  anti-mycoplasmal preparation of hematopoietic                  differentiation medium is 2.5 mu g/ml. The                  megakaryocyte differentiation inducing factor                  comprises thrombopoietin TPO or TPO agonist, stem                  cell factor (SCF), interleukin (IL)-3, IL-6 or                  IL-11. PN CN110179101-A
TI Sauce of Areca bead comprises filling layer and gel coating layer comprising gelatinous cellulose gel material made from coconut water fermented with Acetobacter xylinum and maltose syrup and filling layer includes e.g. concentrated jam.
AU YUAN X
   YANG Y
   FENG H
AE HUNAN PANGGE FOOD CO LTD (HUNA-Non-standard)
GA 2019761284
AB    NOVELTY - Sauce of Areca bead comprises gel coating layer and filling layer, where gel coating comprises a gelatinous cellulose gel material made from coconut water fermented with Acetobacter xylinum and maltose syrup and filling layer includes concentrated jam, lemon juice, peppermint oil and carbon dioxide gas.
   USE - Used as sauce of Areca bead.
   ADVANTAGE - The sauce of Areca bead: enhances and mouthfeel on the basis of original Areca.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for manufacturing Areca bead sauce, comprising (i) mixing gelatinous cellulose gel material and maltose syrup uniformly, slowly adding to hot water at 90-95 degrees C, stirring for 5-10 minutes, cooling to 60-65 degrees C and filtering to obtain gel coating solution; (ii) mixing above concentrated jam, peppermint oil and water, heating and stirring until the solution is evenly mixed, adding lemon juice to adjust the pH to 2.5-3, heating and stirring until the solution is viscous; (iii) applying prepared gel coating solution evenly on a flat panel, slowly heating to concentrate the flowing gel solution, obtaining a gel layer having certain toughness and elasticity after internal moisture slowly evaporates and adding a filling layer and carbon dioxide gas into the gel layer by using an automatic rotary rolling capsule to obtain blasting bead; and (iv) boiling Areca fruit in brine, soaking in water, slicing and removing core to obtain dried Areca, where brine accounts for 0.05-0.1% of the body weight of the Areca and spotting Areca bead on the brine.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: The quality of gel coating layer is 0.5-1% of the mass of the Areca and quality of filling layer is 0.5-6% of the mass of the Areca.The fill layer comprises 10-35% concentrated jam, 5-15% lemon juice and 3-8% peppermint oil. Preferred Method: In the step (iii) specific operation of automatic rotary rolling bag machine comprises rolling both ends of machine slowly into gel layer and pressing filling layer and carbon dioxide down through the middle tube, pressing rotating gear with two pieces of gel and seals the filling layer to obtain Areca beads. In the step (iv) brine is prepared by mixing 100 pts. wt. calcium hydroxide, 100 pts. wt. water, 200 pts. wt. sucrose, 5 pts. wt. sodium cyclamate, 1 pts. wt. sodium saccharin, 0.5 pts. wt. sucralose and 1 pts. wt. flavor.

On the surface of the output cone of the Laval nozzle, a microwave camera with a microwave energy generator is installed to transmit radio waves of millimeter range, at a frequency of 65 GHz, with a power of 10 mW/cm2, and on the inner surface of the cone along the helix at an angle to the generatrix perpendicular to the cone surface, (n) guide rods are fixed, the width of which is equal to half the input diameter of the large cone. Upper part of the large cone is communicated with the mixing chamber, and nozzles with a root angle are installed on its cover . Angle () is 3045, angle is 2530, n=24 number of guiding turns. With the simultaneous action of microwave energy on chemical elements in the air and fine spraying of liquid carbon dioxide, the amount of finely dispersed centers of water crystallization increases, which intensifies the process of snow formation.
   USE - Machine building.

   USE - Fire-proof mechanical electric component cabinet.
   ADVANTAGE - The cabinet can send measurement data and set data by PLC controller when the measurement data is close to the set data, PLC controller makes the warning light and the buzzer work so as to worker can reminded to achieve fire prevention.

PN CN109136455-A
TI Rapidly removing converter air refining final slag            comprises e.g. stopping converter post-blowing,            accompanying furnace opening little slag overflowing by            gas discharged, adding dolomite, reducing oxygen lance            nozzle and inclining converter.
AU DENG N
   PAN J
   ZHANG X
   ZHAO B
   SONG L
AE MA'ANSHAN IRON & STEEL CO LTD (MGJT-C)
GA 201905454M
AB    NOVELTY - Rapidly removing converter air refining final                slag comprises (i) stopping converter after                blowing, accompanying furnace opening little slag                overflowing by a large amount of gas discharged,                when the foaming slag in the furnace is serious,                the operation mode of the oxygen lance is quickly                selected as manual; (ii) adding dolomite to the                furnace by a high-level silo of the converter;                (iii) reducing the oxygen lance nozzle below the                horizontal line of the furnace mouth, and manually                opening the nitrogen gas to ensure that the                nitrogen gas jet completely covers the slag surface                and stopping blowing nitrogen after blowing                nitrogen for 30 seconds; (iv) inclining converter                to the tapping side by 30 degrees after the oxygen                lance is automatically lifted to the waiting point                position; and (v) performing the temperature                measurement sampling and tapping operation once the                converter is tilted normally.
   USE - The method is useful for rapidly removing                converter air refining final slag.
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilized dolomite which rapidly                decomposes under high temperature, produces calcium                oxide and magnesium oxide as high melting point                substances having function of adjusting slag, and                does not require to modify the oxidized furnace                slag before the slag splashing protection, where                the generated carbon-dioxide gas increases the gas                nucleation in the center of steel, carbon-monoxide                gas escapes with a large amount of carbon-dioxide                gas, and is supplemented by a strong agitation of                nitrogen gas to rapidly discharge the                carbon-monoxide gas in a short time to achieve                rapid defoaming.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The dolomite is added                  in an amount of 1.5-3 kg/ton of steel. Preferred                  Method: The nitrogen pressure is 1.3-1.5 MPa and                  nitrogen flow rate is 14000-16000 m3 /hour.

PN US2018365955-A1; US10366590-B2
TI Method for operating smoke detector in residential building, involves generating warning alarm in response to comparing determined smoke concentration to smoke concentration warning threshold.
AU BAJAJ K K
   KORCHAK A
AE GOOGLE LLC (GOOG-C)
GA 2018A1731B
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves measuring a voltage output (140) by a light sensor (130) of the smoke detector (100), where the voltage is based on a measurement of an electromagnetic signal emitted by an illuminator (108) of the smoke detector. A carbon monoxide concentration (160) is determined using a carbon monoxide sensor (120) of the smoke detector. A smoke concentration is determined using the measured voltage. A smoke concentration warning threshold is determined based on the determined carbon monoxide concentration. The determined smoke concentration is compared to the smoke concentration warning threshold. A warning alarm is generated in response to comparing the determined smoke concentration to the smoke concentration warning threshold.
   USE - Method for operating smoke detector (claimed) e.g. photoelectric light obscuration smoke detector in residential and commercial buildings.
   ADVANTAGE - The event monitor utilizes the average rate of increase of smoke to resolve the ambiguity, when smoke concentration and the carbon monoxide concentration do not allow for an event to be unambiguously categorized as one of heads-up event and warning event. The smoke rate of rise and the carbon dioxide rate of increase are evaluated in the smoke detector in parallel to reduce false positives. The smoke increases more rapidly in a warning event as compared to a heads-up event, and the carbon dioxide concentration also increases more rapidly in warning event as compared to heads-up event.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a smoke detector.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the smoke detector.
   Smoke detector (100)
   Illuminator (108)
   Carbon monoxide sensor (120)
   Light sensor (130)
   Voltage output (140)
   Carbon monoxide concentration (160)

TI Antibacterial food freshness indicator material for detecting carbon dioxide, is blended on carrier by membrane liquid A, membrane liquid B, and membrane liquid C, and then ultrasonically and heat-treatment is performed.
AU LIU X
   MIN W
   SHI S
   BAI H
   ZHOU S
AE UNIV JILIN AGRIC (UJIL-C)
GA 201889541G
AB    NOVELTY - Antibacterial food freshness indicator material is blended on the carrier by the membrane liquid A, the membrane liquid B, and the membrane liquid C in a weight ratio of 2:1-1.5:0.1-0.3, and then ultrasonically and heat-treatment is performed after vacuum drying operation to obtain a film. The membrane liquid A compries starch, titanium dioxide dispersion, calcium hydroxide, glycerol, urea, xanthan gum, and water. The membrane liquid B comprises polyvinyl alcohol solution. The membrane liquid C comprises methyl red solution, and bromothymol blue solution.
   USE - Antibacterial food freshness indicator material for detecting carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The antibacterial food freshness indicator material inhibits growth of bacteria in food.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing an antibacterial food freshness indicator material, which involves:
   (A) mixing starch, titanium dioxide dispersion, calcium hydroxide, glycerol, urea, xanthan gum, and water, then mixing, stirring and carrying out sonication, heating at 70-90 degrees C to obtain a film liquid A;
   (B) mixing polyvinyl alcohol, water and mixing, heating at 80-100 degrees C to obtain a membrane liquid B;
   (C) mixing methyl red solution and bromothymol blue solution, and performing sonication to obtain a composite indicator; and
   (D) mixing membrane liquid A, the membrane liquid B and obtained composite indicator to weight ratio of 2:1-1.5:0.1-0.3, carrying out ultrasonic treatment and heating, thn spreading on a glass carrier, drying under vacuum at 45-65 degrees oC, for 4-8 hours to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Compositions: The membrane liquid A comprises 2-6 pts. wt. starch, 0.1-0.5 pts. wt. titanium dioxide dispersion, 1-5 pts. wt. calcium hydroxide, 1-4 pts. wt. glycerin, 1-3 pts. wt. urea, 0.05-0.2 pts. wt. xanthan gum and 80-90 pts. wt. water. The membrane liquid B 3-8 pts. wt. polyvinyl alcohol solution. The membrane liquid C comprises 1-3 pts. wt. methyl red solution having a concentration of 0.05-0.15%, and 2-7.5 pts. wt. bromothymol blue solution having concentration of 0.1-0.25%. The mass ratio of starch, calcium hydroxide, glycerol, urea, xanthan gum, water, polyvinyl alcohol solution, methyl red solution, and bromothymol blue solution is 2-6:1-5:1-4:1-3:0.05-0.2:80-90:3-8:1-3:2-7.5. The concentration of the methyl red solution is 0.05-0.15%, and the concentration of the bromothymol blue solution is 0.1-0.25%. Preferred Conditions: The content by weight of the titanium dioxide according to the content of the parts by weight of the starch, and weight fraction of titanium dioxide is represented by n = 4.21+1.6e/1000+45.1-0.26W/7.091n(6.58 omega)-2.41W+3.093. The titanium dioxide dispersion is nano titanium dioxide dispersion liquid. The heating temperature is 80 degrees C, and the heating temperature is 90 degrees C, the vacuum drying temperature is 55 degrees C, and the drying time is 6 hours. The weight ratio of the methyl red solution and bromothymol blue solution is 1:2.3.
   ACTIVITY - Antibacterial.

PN CN108726625-A
TI Degrading and removing psychotropic antidepressant in water using electron beam radiation, comprises using free radical scavengers and pH adjusters under normal temperature conditions and adjusting active group composition and pH in water.
AU HU M
   YAO C
   JIANG Y
   WANG Y
AE UNIV SHANGHAI (USHN-C)
GA 2018872632
AB    NOVELTY - Degrading and removing psychotropic antidepressant in water using electron beam radiation, comprises using free radical scavengers and pH adjusters under normal temperature conditions, adjusting the active group composition and pH in the water of the antidepressant drug to be treated, bombarding antidepressant drug in water by high energy electron beam to obtain secondary particles, carrying out redox reaction of secondary particles by in-situ mineralization of psychotropic antidepressants into carbon-dioxide, water and small molecules, so as to achieve the removal of psychotropic antidepressants.
   USE - The method is useful for degrading and removing psychotropic antidepressant in water using electron beam radiation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple and easy operation procedure and low energy consumption; is economical and suitable for processing low concentration environmental organic dyes, hormone and antibiotic; does not produce secondary pollution; and promotes high-speed and efficient removal of organic pollutants e.g. organic additives.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Degrading and removing psychotropic antidepressant in water using electron beam radiation, comprises using free radical scavengers and pH adjusters under normal temperature conditions, adjusting the active group composition and pH in the water of the antidepressant drug to be treated, bombarding antidepressant drug in water by high energy electron beam to obtain secondary particles, carrying out redox reaction of secondary particles by in-situ mineralization of psychotropic antidepressants into carbon-dioxide, water and small molecules, so as to achieve the removal of psychotropic antidepressants.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The concentration of the antidepressant in the water body of the antidepressant to be treated is not greater than 75 mg/l. The psychotropic water-contaminating drug in the water body of the antidepressant drug to be treated is sertraline. The irradiation dose for controlling high energy rays is not greater than 3-8 kGy. The pH of the antidepressant drug to be treated is adjusted to a pH of 3-6 using a pH adjuster. The pH adjuster used is adipic acid, acetic acid, tris-hydrochloride, iminodiacetic acid or triethanolamine. The radical scavenger can remove hydroxy radical groups in the water body of the antidepressant drug to be treated, where the radical scavenger used is a hydroxy radical scavenger, isopropanol, tert-butanol, o-hydroxybenzoic acid, terephthalic acid or nitrogen.

PN CN108442099-A; CN108442099-B
TI Anti-UV nano-zinc oxide composite textile fabric            comprises zinc salt, ethanol, carbon dioxide,            cosolvent, acetone and textile fabric.
AU DING Z
   ZHU Y
   CAO Y
   REN Y
   DONG Z
   YANG Y
   FAN H
AE UNIV NANTONG (UYNT-C)
GA 201868163J
AB    NOVELTY - Anti-UV nano-zinc oxide composite textile                fabric comprises 0.5-40 wt.% zinc salt, ethanol,                carbon dioxide (CO2), 0.5-80 wt.% cosolvent,                acetone and 20-99 wt.% textile fabric.
   USE - Anti-UV nano-zinc oxide composite textile                fabric.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has green environment-protection,                good uniformity, high bonding fastness, and good                repeatability, and provides anti-UV nano-zinc oxide                composite textile fabric, which has anti-UV,                antibacterial, disinfecting, hydrophobic and                antistatic functions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                preparation of anti-UV nano-zinc oxide composite                textile fabric comprising supercritical fluid                assisted infiltration method and thermal reduction                method comprising adding 20-99 g textile fabric in                0.5-80 g ethanol and acetone solution,                ultrasonically cleaning for 10-20 minutes, cleaning                the textile fabric in deionized water, oven drying                at 50-70 degrees C, adding 0.5-40 pts. wt. zinc                salt dissolved in 0.5-80 g cosolvent, adding in                supercritical fluid device, putting the dried                textile fabric in supercritical fluid device,                introducing CO2 gas into the supercritical fluid                device, where zinc salt and supercritical CO2 enter                inside the textile fabric fiber, conducting                supercritical osmotic reaction at 8-40 MPa and                32-100 degrees C for 1-12 hours, discharging CO2                gas, taking out the textile fabric, where zinc salt                is solidified on the textile fabric fiber, putting                the supercritical fluid-permeated textile fabric                into hydrothermal reaction kettle, heat reducing at                100-200 degrees C for 1-12 hours, taking out the                textile fabric, washing with water, and                drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The zinc salt is zinc                  acetylacetonate, zinc carbonate, or zinc acetate.                  The cosolvent is methanol, ethanol, n-butanol,                  isobutanol, acetone, glycol or triethylene                  glycol.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Component: The textile fabric is                  natural fiber and/or synthetic fiber. It is                  cotton, fur, silk, hemp, Terylene (RTM:                  polyester), polyamide, acrylic and/or                  polypropylene. Preferred Method: The method                  further comprises before the first step,                  conducting textile fabric reduction processing by                  preparing 3-20 mass% sodium hydroxide solution,                  soaking the textile fabric in sodium hydroxide                  solution at solid-to-liquid ratio of 1:30, 60-80                  degrees C water bath heating, washing with                  deionized water, and oven drying at 60 degrees C                  for 5-20 minutes, and textile fabric plasma                  processing comprising starting a plasma                  apparatus, connecting argon gas supply, removing                  air for 2 minutes, putting the textile fabric                  into the plasma apparatus, adjusting gas flow                  rate at 1 L/minute, adjusting the output power of                  plasma apparatus at 800-1200 W, conducting                  atmospheric plasma treatment for 2-6 minutes,                  taking out the textile fabric, and closing the                  plasma apparatus. It further comprises after the                  supercritical osmotic reaction step, conducting                  textile fabric plasma treatment step comprising                  starting a plasma apparatus, connecting argon gas                  supply, removing air for 2 minutes, putting the                  textile fabric into the plasma apparatus,                  adjusting gas flow rate at 1 L/minute, adjusting                  the output power of plasma apparatus at 1000 W,                  conducting atmospheric plasma treatment for 1-2                  minutes, taking out the textile fabric and                  closing the plasma apparatus.
DC A94 (Semi-finished materials - fibres, films, foams.); F06 (Chemical-type treatment of textiles (D06B, L, M, P, Q).); F07 (Other textile applications - include mechanical treatment of fabrics (D06C, F, G, H, J, L, M).); E17 (Other aliphatics.); E32 (Compounds of Ti, Zr, Hf, Cu, Ag, Au, Zn, Cd, Hg, Ga, In, Te, Ge, Sn, Pb, As, Sb, Bi.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).)

TI Scroll compressor using carbon dioxide as refrigerant, has compression chamber comprised by fixed swirl wrap and turning swirl wrap, and suction path is currently formed in fixed scroll.
AU NOBA K
   FUNAKOSI D
   WATANABE K
AE PANASONIC INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY MANAGEME (MATU-C)
GA 201841200Q
AB    NOVELTY - The scroll compressor has an electric motor accommodated in the airtight container. The compression mechanism portion (10) has a fixed scroll (12) with a fixed swirl wrap, and the turning scroll (13) has turning swirl wrap engaged with fixed swirl wrap. The compression chamber (15) is comprised by the fixed swirl wrap and turning swirl wrap. The suction path (21) is currently formed in the fixed scroll. The refrigerant is introduced into the compression chamber from the exterior of the airtight container, and the suction liner (3) is inserted in the suction path. The edge portion of the compression chamber entrance side of the suction liner is an edge portion of the same position as the edge portion of the compression chamber entrance side of the suction path. The scroll compressor main portion is arranged between the starting positions of the turning swirl wrap that forms the compression chamber.
   USE - Scroll compressor using R32 or a carbon dioxide as refrigerant.
   ADVANTAGE - The thermal insulation performance with respect to the suction refrigerant is improved. The energy efficiency of a refrigerating cycle is set to optimum conditions, and the scroll compressor is applied using a refrigerant with high temperature of the gas discharged from a compressor and useful.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the enlarged view of the insertion portion of the suction liner in a scroll compressor.

PN CN107988162-A
TI Enriching cancer stem cells, involves adding            heated liquid into mold in mold base material,            solidifying to obtain carrier, adding specific medium,            incubating, removing, adding cancer cell suspension and            culture medium and culturing cells.
AU GUO X
   GE R
   CHEN Y
   LI C
   CHEN X
AE UNIV WENZHOU MEDICAL SECOND AFFILIATED (WEZN-C)
GA 2018366276
AB    NOVELTY - Method for enriching cancer stem cells,                involves adding a heated liquid into a mold in mold                base material, reducing temperature, solidifying                the mold base material to obtain carrier with                cavity for enriching cancer stem cell, placing the                cavity of the carrier upward in a cell culture                plate, adding Dulbecco's modified Eagle                medium-nutrient mixture F-12 (DMEM/F-12) culture                medium containing 100 units/ml penicillin and 1                g/ml streptomycin to immerse the carrier, then                placing in a carbon dioxide incubator, incubating                at 37 plus minus 0.5 degrees C for at least 24                hours, then removing the culture medium, adding                200-300 mu l cancer cell suspension having cell                concentration of 5x 105 cells/ml to the cavity of                each carrier, maintaining under static condition at                bottom of cavity at room temperature for at least                30 minutes, then adding the culture medium for                enriching the cancer cells in each cavity of the                cell culture plate, and then culturing in a carbon                dioxide incubator at 37 plus minus 0.5 degrees C                for 4 days.
   USE - The method is useful for enriching cancer stem                cells.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enriches cancer stem cells rapidly                and conveniently, reduces requirement for cell                concentration in the sample solution, enables to                obtain uniform sized and controllable cancer cell                spheres with high activity, increases cancer stem                cell enrichment ratio, and improves dryness-related                index octamer-binding transcription factor 4 (OCT4)                expression levels.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The mold is a silica                  gel sphere having a porous structure and 81 holes                  with hole diameter of 400 mu m. The inoculum                  density of the cancer cell suspension is 1x                  105-1.5 greater than or equal to asterisk105                  cells/cavity. The mold base material comprises a                  DMEM/F12 medium, and 2-3 weight/volume% agarose.                  Preferred Medium: The medium for enriching cancer                  stem cells is DMEM/F12 medium, added with 10 mu M                  rho-associated kinase-1 (ROCK1) inhibitor                  Y-27632, 5 volume/volume% fetal bovine serum, 2                  volume/volume% horse serum, 100 units/ml                  penicillin and 1 g/ml streptomycin, where the                  dosage amount of the medium is 2-3 ml. Preferred                  Cell: The cancer cells contained in the sample                  solution are chosen from human ovarian cancer                  cell lines, human colorectal cancer cell lines,                  human pancreatic cancer cell lines, and human                  prostate cancer cell lines.

PN CN107954623-A; CN107954623-B
TI Performing in-situ growth of nanoparticles on solid waste surface involves preparing carbonizable salt solution, adding untreated or treated solid waste thereto, and adding surfactant, emulsifier or crystal form modifier.
AU PAN G
   WANG Y
   MEI R
   PAN W
   MI R
AE UNIV SOUTHEAST (UYSE-C)
GA 201833031J
AB    NOVELTY - Performing in-situ growth of nanoparticles on a solid waste surface involves preparing a carbonizable salt solution, adding untreated or treated solid waste thereto, and adding a surfactant, an emulsifier or a crystal form modifier, and stirring uniformly to obtain solution. The obtained solution is placed in a carbonization reactor, carbon dioxide gas is introduced, and the reaction is stirred, and the pH of the solution is reduced to 7-8, the reaction is stopped to obtain reaction product. The reaction product is washed, the solid liquid phase is separated, and resulting solid is dried.
   USE - Method for performing in-situ growth of nanoparticles on a solid waste surface.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to perform in-situ growth of nanoparticles on a solid waste surface, which has simple, environmentally friendly, and inexpensive, and recycled byproducts.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Performing in-situ growth of nanoparticles on a solid waste surface involves preparing a carbonizable salt solution, adding untreated or treated solid waste thereto, and adding a surfactant, an emulsifier or a crystal form modifier, and stirring uniformly to obtain solution. The obtained solution is placed in a carbonization reactor, carbon dioxide gas is introduced, and the reaction is stirred, and the pH of the solution is reduced to 7-8, the reaction is stopped to obtain reaction product. The reaction product is washed, the solid liquid phase is separated, and resulting solid is dried to obtain the final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The solid waste is added in an amount of 50-250 g per liter of the carbonizable salt solution. The carbonizable salt solution is using calcium oxide or magnesium oxide as a raw material constant boiling dissolved to form a supersaturated solution, after standing by physical methods of filtration, through a 200 mesh sieve to remove the remaining solid residue calcium hydroxide or magnesium hydroxide solution. The calcium hydroxide or magnesium hydroxide suspension prepared by dissolving calcium hydroxide and magnesium hydroxide in water, calcium salt or magnesium salt solution, water glass or sodium metasilicate solution and sodium meta aluminate solution. The treated solid waste is a solid waste that is soaked in a dilute acid, alkali or ammonium salt solution at 20-80 degrees C for 8-24 hours. The ammonia, sodium hydroxide or potassium hydroxide solution is added to adjust the initial pH of the reaction system to 10-14. The ethanol and water are added to obtain an ethanol-water mixed solvent for regulating the crystal morphology and colloidal agglomeration process in the ethanol-water mixed solvent, the volume ratio of ethanol to water is 1:3-1:8. The stirring reaction conditions are carried at 10-80 degrees C, the stirring rotation speed is 400-1000 revolution per minute, the carbon dioxide gas flow rate is 10-60 L/hour, and the reaction time is 15 minutes to 4 hours. The product is washed three times with ethanol and deionized water, respectively, and the solid is isolated by centrifugation or vacuum filtration and dried at 110 degrees C for 8-24 hours. Preferred Components: The solid waste is selected from fly ash, ground blast furnace slag powder, silica fume, desulfurized gypsum, building reclaimed fine powder or furnace bottom slag. The surfactant is cetyltrimethylammonium bromide, crystal regulator is polyethylene glycol or polyacrylic acid, emulsifier is selected from Span and Twen emulsifiers and commercial polycarboxylic acid water-reducing agent, where surfactant, crystal modifier or emulsifier is added in an amount of 0.5-5 wt.% of the mass of the carbonizable salt solution.

TI Performing efficient polymer particle purging for            reducing or removing volatile organic compounds            generated during production of polymeric material,            involves polymerizing olefin monomers and optionally            comonomers in first reactor vessel.
AU VAN EGMOND J W
   GOAD J D
   KAARTO J K
   CHISMAR D J
   VAN E J W
   VAN E YA W
   GOAD Y D
   KAARTO Y K
   VANEGMOND J W
AE GRACE & CO-CONN W R (GRAC-C)
   GRACE & CO-CONN W R (GRAC-C)
   GRACE & CO-CONN W R (GRAC-C)
   GRACE & CO-CONN W R (GRAC-C)
   GRACE & CO W R (GRAC-C)
   GRACE & CO-CONN W R (GRAC-C)
GA 201823205C
AB    NOVELTY - Performing efficient polymer particle purging                involves polymerizing olefin monomers and                optionally comonomers in a first reactor vessel,                for forming a raw product stream which comprises                polymerized solids, unreacted monomer and                optionally comonomer, where the polymerized solids                include olefin polymer, volatile organic compounds                (VOCs) and a catalyst system, contacting the                polymerized solids with a catalyst poison selected                from carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, oxygen,                water, alcohols, amines, or mixtures for forming a                passivated stream and maintaining the passivated                stream in an agitated state.
   USE - Method for performing efficient polymer                particle purging for reducing or removing volatile                organic compounds generated during the production                of polymeric material (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Performing efficient polymer particle purging                involves polymerizing olefin monomers and                optionally comonomers in a first reactor vessel,                for forming a raw product stream which comprises                polymerized solids, unreacted monomer and                optionally comonomer, where the polymerized solids                include olefin polymer, volatile organic compounds                (VOCs) and a catalyst system, contacting the                polymerized solids with a catalyst poison selected                from carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, oxygen,                water, alcohols, amines, or mixtures for forming a                passivated stream, maintaining the passivated                stream in an agitated state within a second reactor                and contacting the passivated stream within the                second reactor with a circulating gas which                includes unreacted monomer for a residence time,                for reducing the concentration of VOC in the                polymerized solids by 10 wt.% compared to the level                before entering the second reactor for forming a                purified olefin polymer solids stream.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The unreacted monomer                  is selected from propylene, ethylene, 4-10C                  alpha-olefins or mixtures. The catalyst poison is                  conveyed into the second reactor vessel with a                  conveying fluid selected from nitrogen, hydrogen,                  propylene, ethylene and mixtures. Preferred                  Compositions: The VOC is present in the                  polymerized solids in an amount from 50-6000                  parts per million based on the total weight of                  the polymerized solids. Preferred Process: The                  efficient polymer particle purging also involves                  conveying the raw product stream to the second                  reactor. The passivated stream is maintained in                  the agitated state at a temperature greater than                  the temperature of the first reactor, where the                  temperature is 20-140 degrees C, preferably,                  90-120 degrees C. Preferred Conditions: The                  catalyst poison is contacted with the polymerized                  solids during conveyance of the raw product                  stream to the second reactor. The residence time                  in the second reactor is 0.1-5.0 hours. The                  polymerization is terminated by the injection of                  the catalyst poison. The catalyst system                  comprises a co-catalyst which is routed to the                  first reactor, and the catalyst poison is                  injected to the second reactor vessel at a rate                  to achieve a molar ratio of poison to co-catalyst                  of 0.5. The circulating gas circulates through                  the second reactor vessel at a superficial                  velocity of greater than 0.3 m/second. The                  polymerized solids to be contacted with a                  catalyst poison further comprises a second                  polymerized solids stream from a storage vessel.                  The first reactor is selected from a fluidized                  bed gas phase reactor, a loop reactor, vertical                  stirred bed reactor, horizontal stirred gas phase                  reactor, multizone circulating reactor, or                  stirred autoclave. The second reactor vessel is a                  reactor vessel that is normally in service as a                  fluidized bed gas phase polymerization reactor or                  a horizontal or vertical stirred gas phase                  polymerization reactor.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

AB    NOVELTY - The system has a premix tank (1) includes an air pump (2) and a carbon-dioxide (CO2) tank (4). The seawater in the premix tank is connected to a test tank (11) by gravity through a shunt tube (10). The premix tank includes a pH controller (3), a submersible pump (6) and an overflow port (5). A trim valve is provided on the pipe connected with a gas stone (8). The submersible pump is located on the pool wall of the premix tank. The overflow port is located at the top edge of the premix tank, and the test tank has a water level control pipe (12) for controlling the water level.
   USE - Streamlined flow-type pH control system for biological research device.
   ADVANTAGE - The system can not only stably control the pH of the water but also reflect the acidification of the real marine environment for a long time, and the method of use is simple and quick, and is suitable for the research of marine acidification in laboratory.

PT P
PN CN107722253-A; CN107722253-B
TI New heterocyclic organic zinc complex used as catalyst for preparing poly(cyclohexene carbonate).
AU WANG Y
   WANG N
   ZHAO K
   ZHANG Y
AE HENAN INST ENG (HEAN-C)
   HENAN INST ENG (HEAN-C)
GA 2018171594
AB    NOVELTY - A heterocyclic organic zinc complex (I), is new.
   USE - New heterocyclic organic zinc complex is used as catalyst for preparing poly(cyclohexene carbonate) (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The heterocyclic organic zinc complex is easy to use, and enables preparation of poly(cyclohexene carbonate) comprising more than 95% polycarbonate, under mild reaction condition, with high catalytic efficiency of 1912 g polymer/g zinc, without using organic solvents, enabling simple separation of products.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A heterocyclic organic zinc complex of formula (I), is new. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) preparation of complex (I), which involves dissolving 2,6-bis(2-formyl phenoxymethyl)pyridine and 3-aminothiophene in methanol, stirring resultant mixture at room temperature, heating, refluxing, stirring for 0.5-2 hours, filtering, washing precipitate with ether, drying, obtaining 2,6-bis(2-formyl phenoxymethyl)pyridine-3-aminothiophene ligand, adding resultant to a vacuum container, filling nitrogen, adding diethylzinc in toluene solution, dissolving, placing resultant mixture in ice bath, cooling, stirring for 0.5-1 hour, stirring at room temperature for 1 hour, and removing toluene and unreacted diethylzinc under reduced pressure; and
   (2) preparation of poly(cyclohexene carbonate), which involves adding complex (I) to a high-pressure reactor, vacuum-drying at 100 degrees C for 1 hour, charging carbon dioxide and cyclohexene oxide, heating resultant mixture to 60-150 degrees C, filling carbon dioxide at 1-6 MPa, stirring, reacting for 6-36 hours, cooling to room temperature, distilling, removing unreacted cyclohexene oxide, dissolving resultant residue in dichloromethane, filtering, mixing filtrate with a dilute acid, washing using a dilute alkali and deionized water, adding methanol, precipitating, filtering and vacuum-drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of 3-aminothiophene and 2,6-bis(2-formyl phenoxymethyl)pyridine is 1.8-2.2:1. The molar ratio of ligand and diethylzinc is 1:1-1.25. The mass ratio of catalyst and cyclohexene oxide is 1:100-1000. The usage amount of methanol is 200-500 %mass with respect to amount of the filtrate.
DC A23 (Polyamides; polyesters. (including polycarbonates, polyesteramides); alkyds; other unsaturated polymers.); E13 (Heterocyclics.)
MC A02-A06; A05-E06A; A10-D03; A10-D05; E05-L03D; E11-F07D; E11-F11

PN CN107624554-A
TI High-yield planting method of strawberry by ditching and ridging soil in greenhouse, plowing, fertilizing base fertilizer, interplanting strawberry, cordate houttuynia and clover at ridge and supplementing special organic fertilizer.
AU YILING L
AE BOBAI FUSHA FRUIT CULTIVATION COOP (BOBA-Non-standard)
GA 201810263V
AB    NOVELTY - High-yield planting method of strawberry comprises building greenhouse comprises multiple arched supporting frames covered with layer of transparent film, ditching and ridging soil in greenhouse, plowing, fertilizing base fertilizer, interplanting strawberry, cordate houttuynia and clover at ridge, planting leek in trench, irrigating, supplementing 150-200 kg/acre special organic fertilizer, and operating carbon dioxide gas generator 5-10 hours every day. The special organic fertilizer is prepared by mixing crop straw, feces, rapeseed meal, plant ash and animal urine and fermenting.
   USE - Method for high-yield planting of strawberry (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method has early strawberry maturity of 8-10 days in advance, and has high economic benefit.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - High-yield planting method of strawberry comprises building greenhouse comprises multiple arched supporting frames covered with layer of transparent film, ditching and ridging soil in greenhouse, plowing, fertilizing base fertilizer, interplanting strawberry, cordate houttuynia and clover at ridge, planting leek in trench, irrigating, supplementing 150-200 kg/acre special organic fertilizer, and operating carbon dioxide gas generator 5-10 hours every day. The special organic fertilizer is prepared by mixing 100-150 pts. wt. crop straw, 60-80 pts. wt. feces, 20-30 pts. wt. rapeseed meal, 30-40 pts. wt. plant ash and 40-60 pts. wt. animal urine, piling, covering film, fermenting for 15-20 days to obtain fermented material, and uniformly mixing and stirring with 10-15 pts. wt. salicylic acid and 5-10 pts. wt. polyamine.

PT P
PN WO2022230861-A1; JP2022168616-A
TI Processing waste lithium ion battery by subjecting            lithium ion battery to discharging, heat treatment,            pulverization and classification to obtain active            material powder, combusting its carbon content,            absorbing, carbonating,wet-dissolving, forming solution            of valuable metal, and carbonating lithium.
AU KEITA Y
   YUKIO S
   TARO H
   JUN N
   YAMADA K
   SAKUMA Y
   HIRAOKA T
   NAKAZAWA J
AE ASAKA RIKEN CO LTD (ASAK-Non-standard)
GA 2022D7742X
AB    NOVELTY - Method for processing a waste lithium ion                battery comprises: obtaining an active material                powder by subjecting a waste lithium ion battery to                operations such as discharging, heat treatment,                pulverization and classification; combusting the                carbon content in the active material powder so as                to be converted into carbon dioxide; a carbon                dioxide recovering step in which the carbon dioxide                generated during the combustion of the active                material in the combustion step is absorbed and                carbonated; a wet treatment step in which the                active material powder combusted in the combustion                step is wet dissolved, and forming a solution of a                valuable metal in the active material; and a                lithium carbonation step in which lithium carbonate                is obtained by adding carbon dioxide, which has                been absorbed in the carbon dioxide recovering                step, into a lithium solution.
   USE - Method for processing a waste lithium ion                battery.
   ADVANTAGE - With respect to recovery of a valuable metal                from a waste lithium ion battery (LiB) and a waste                material from the LiB production, the method solves                that the problem of recovering a valuable metal                from an active material powder that is obtained by                subjecting the waste LiB and the waste material                from the LiB production to a pretreatment                (discharging, heat treatment, pulverization and                classification), and the problem of recovering and                circulating carbon dioxide. The method produces                powder containing the active material at a high                yield. The method provides improved recovery rate                of valuable metal elements (lithium, manganese,                cobalt, and nickel) from waste LiB and LiB                manufacturing waste materials (90% or more yield),                and provides a carbon dioxide recycling process in                which the carbon dioxide generated when the active                material powder is burned is recovered and utilized                as a carbonic acid source for producing lithium                carbonate.

Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).); M25 (Production and refining of metals other than iron - including ore treatment, extraction, working up scrap, obtaining specific metals, control testing methods (C22B).); X16 (Electrochemical Storage)

AU YAO B
   CHEN Y
   JIANG Y
   KUANG J
   DUAN T
   LAI J
AE PETROCHINA CO LTD (CNPC-C)
GA 2022C6481C
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a primary combustion device                whose outlet is connected with an inlet of a                secondary combustion device. The main combustion                device generates carbon dioxide by combustion of                carbon monoxide. A discharging device discharges                smoke discharged from an outlet of the secondary                combustion devices. The primary combustion devices                are provided with a first combustion temperature                and a first air distribution amount. The secondary                combustion equipment generates a second combustion                temperature under the second combustion and a                second air distribution volume to generate carbon                dioxide.
   USE - Combustion controlling device.
   ADVANTAGE - The device reduces the content of nitrogen                oxide in the tail gas, improves the conversion rate                of ammonia gas generating nitrogen gas reaction,                inhibits the reaction of ammonia combustion                generating nitrogen oxide, and effectively solves                the problem of the discharge of the nitrogen oxides                in the flue gas. The nitrogen oxide reduction                reaction is performed under the first air                distribution amount, thus reducing the nitrogen                oxide content of the carbon monoxide generated by                the reduction reaction to generate carbon dioxide,                and improving the conversion of the ammonia gas to                nitrogen gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) a combustion controlling method; and
   (2) a computer-readable storage medium.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of a                combustion controlling device. (Drawing includes                non-English language text).

AB    NOVELTY - Preparing naphthyl hypercrosslinked microporous polymer comprises (i) mixing naphthalene and dimethylformal in a molar ratio of 1:2.9-3.1, fully dissolving in an organic solvent at room temperature, then adding a catalyst to the reaction solution, and fully dispersing under the protection of an inert atmosphere, (ii) carrying out the hypercrosslinking reaction in stages: firstly heating to 45-50degreesC for 5-6 hours, then heating to 70-90degreesC for 5-6 hours, and finally heating to 100-120degreesC for 20-30 hours, and (iii) cooling the reaction to room temperature after finishing, and filtering, and washing the crude product with methanol and hydrochloric acid respectively, and finally washing with water until the filtrate is clarified, and (iv) placing the washed crude product in methanol for extracting, vacuum drying to obtain naphthyl hypercrosslinked microporous polymer.
   USE - The polymer is used as solid carbon dioxide adsorption.
   ADVANTAGE - The polymer has large specific surface area and high thermal stability, strong carbon dioxide capture ability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for naphthyl hypercrosslinked microporous polymer prepared by above mentioned method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the catalyst includes anhydrous ferric chloride. The organic solvent includes nitrobenzene or dichloromethane.Preferred Method: In the step (iv), the extraction time is 20-30 hours. The temperature of vacuum drying is 60-80degreesC, and the drying time is 20-30 hours.

TI Preparing copper-P-100 catalyst used in carbon dioxide electro-catalysis reduction comprises e.g. adding hydrochloric acid to submersed carbon paper, ultrasonically washing and taking out paper, and putting tannic acid and copper nitrate in beaker.
AU XIA L
   LI J
   JIANG Y
AE UNIV LIAONING (UNLN-C)
GA 202238757U
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing copper-p-100 catalyst comprises (1) placing carbon paper in a beaker, adding hydrochloric acid, making the hydrochlorical acid submersed carbon paper, ultrasonically washing for 10degrees Celsius, taking out the carbon paper and using deionized water for ultrasonic washing for two times, using acetone ultrasonic to wash for 10-20 minutes, using de-ionizing water for two times and taking out carbon paper for drying, (2) placing tannic acid and copper nitrate in the beaker and adding 20 ml deionization water, ultrasonic stirring to make it completely dissolved, obtaining electro-deposition liquid, and (3) adding magneton in the beaker with electro- deposition liquid to keep stirring at constant rotating speed, preventing the electro-deposition liquid from layering, taking silver/silver chloride as a reference electrode, taking graphite rod as a counter electrode, clamping the processed carbon.
   USE - Method for preparing copper-p-100 catalyst, which is used in carbon dioxide electro-catalytic reduction for reducing carbon dioxide into acetic acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The copper-p-100 catalyst has high Faraday efficiency of acetic acid, and can be prepared in simple, clean and environmentally friendly manner.

   (2) Method for enhancing aroma of a non-alcoholic carbonated beverage containing an aroma component, which is a difference between the pH value of the non-alcoholic carbonated beverage and the pH value measured after removing carbon dioxide from the non-alcoholic carbonated beverage is adjusted to 0.04 or more, and amount of aroma component in the non-alcoholic carbonated beverage measured is 20 or more.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: The non-alcoholic carbonated beverage contain a flavor and a sweetener. PN CN113441160-A
TI Preparing nickel hydroxide/titanium carbide photothermal catalytic material used in the production of methane by hydro-reduction of photo-thermal carbon dioxide comprises e.g. etching titanium-aluminum carbide, mixing, subjecting precursor liquid to a hydrothermal reaction and drying.
AU LIU Y
   YANG Q
   LIU W
   XIA A
   REN H
   ZHANG B
   WANG M
   WANG Y
   FENG S
   TAN G
AE UNIV SHAANXI SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYSK-C)
GA 2021B5769N
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nickel hydroxide/titanium carbide photothermal catalytic material comprises (1) etching titanium-aluminum carbide with hydrogen fluoride (HF) solution and sodium hydroxide solution in sequence, and then separating the resultant product and drying to obtain multilayer titanium carbide, (2) mixing the nickel source and the multilayer titanium carbide uniformly in deionized water at a molar ratio of 0.29-0.86:1, then adding aqueous ammonia and continuously mixing uniformly to obtain a precursor, (3) subjecting the precursor liquid to a hydrothermal reaction at 140-220degrees Celsius, separating the product in the obtained reaction liquid and drying to obtain a nickel hydroxide/titanium carbide photothermal catalytic material.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing nickel hydroxide/titanium carbide photothermal catalytic material, which is useful in in the production of methane by hydro-reduction of photo-thermal carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the stability of hexagonal nickel hydroxide and the conversion rate of photothermal catalytic reduction of carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a nickel hydroxide/titanium carbide photothermal catalytic material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The volume ratio of the sodium hydroxide solution to the hydrogen fluoride solution is 36-50:25. The nickel source is nickel nitrate, nickel acetate, nickel oxalate or nickel acetylacetonate. In step (2), the mass fraction of the aqueous ammonia is 28%. The volume ratio of the aqueous ammonia to the hydrogen fluoride solution is 0.24:25.Preferred Method: In step (1), etching titanium-aluminum carbide using hydrogen fluoride (HF) solution includes adding titanium-aluminum carbide to the 49% hydrogen fluoride solution according to the ratio of 10.3 micromol: 25 ml, stirring for 1-4 hours, centrifuging the resulting mixed system, washing the precipitate with deionized water, centrifuging, and drying the obtained precipitate until the pH of the obtained supernatant is 6.2-7 to complete the etching of titanium-aluminum carbide. The method specifically comprises add a 0.5-1.0 mol/l sodium hydroxide solution to the precipitate, stirring for 15-48 hours, centrifuging the resulting mixed system, washing the precipitate with deionized water, and then centrifuging, and drying the obtained precipitate until the pH of the obtained supernatant is 7.2-7.6 to obtain multilayer titanium carbide. In step (3), the precursor liquid is hydrothermally reacted at the temperature for 10-24 hours. In step (3), washing the reaction solution successively with absolute ethanol and deionized water, and centrifuging 3-5 times, and drying the precipitate after centrifugation at 65-80degrees Celsius to obtain a nickel hydroxide/titanium carbide photothermal catalytic material.

PT P
PN US2021291150-A1
TI Bifunctional oxidative dehydrogenation catalyst            used in catalytic membrane reactor for dehydrogenating            alkane using carbon dioxide as a soft oxidant comprises            metal oxide and redox active ceramic support promoting            water-gas shift process.
AU DEUTSCHMANN O
   KEE R J
   KARAKAYA C
AE DEUTSCHMANN O (DEUT-Individual)
   KEE R J (KEER-Individual)
   KARAKAYA C (KARA-Individual)
GA 2021B01182
AB    NOVELTY - Bifunctional oxidative dehydrogenation                catalyst comprises a metal oxide and a redox active                ceramic support, where the redox active ceramic                support promotes a water-gas shift process.
   USE - The catalyst is useful in a catalytic membrane                reactor for dehydrogenating alkane (all claimed)                using carbon dioxide as a soft oxidant.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst is highly olefin selective,                economical and coke-free.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) alkane dehydrogenation, comprising                providing a catalytic membrane reactor comprising a                catalyst bed and a hydrogen selective membrane,                where the catalyst bed comprises a bifunctional                catalyst comprising a metal oxide and a redox                active ceramic support, contacting an input gas                stream comprising at least one alkane and carbon                dioxide with the bifunctional catalyst to form a                product gas stream comprising at least one olefin                and hydrogen and separating hydrogen from the                product gas stream; and
   (2) a catalytic membrane reactor comprising                (a1) a bifunctional catalyst comprising a metal                oxide and a redox active ceramic support, and (b) a                hydrogen selective membrane, where the bifunctional                catalyst is contained in a catalyst bed i.e. at                least partially enclosed by the hydrogen selective                membrane.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal oxide                  comprises chromium(III) oxide, vanadium(V) oxide,                  indium oxide and/or iron oxide, where the metal                  oxide is mixed with or impregnated in the redox                  active ceramic support. The redox active ceramic                  support comprises at least one of aluminum oxide,                  cerium oxide, zirconium oxide,                  barium-cesium-zirconium-yttrium-niobium-oxygen                  (BaCe0.7Zr0.1Y0.1Nb0.1O2.95) and                  barium-zirconium-yttrium oxide (BaZr1-xYxO3-delta                  ) (x is 0-0.2). The hydrogen selective membrane                  comprises a material comprising a metal, a                  ceramic and/or a polymer.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Components: The redox active                  ceramic support comprises a doped perovskite                  ceramic.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The metal oxide promotes                  alkane dehydrogenation via a Mars-van Krevelen                  process with carbon dioxide as an oxidant. The                  alkane dehydrogenation occurs at least partially                  via a Mars-van Krevelen process, where a                  water-gas shift process forms at least some of                  the carbon dioxide. The alkane comprises ethane,                  propane and/or butane. A molar ratio of the                  alkane to carbon dioxide in the input gas stream                  is greater than 1. The contacting step occurs at                  a temperature of 450-550degrees Celsius. A rate                  of olefin production is greater than or equal to                  1x10-2 mol g/cat/hour.Preferred Components: The catalytic membrane                  reactor further comprises an input gas stream                  comprising carbon dioxide and at least one alkane                  and first and second output gas streams, where                  the first output gas stream comprises at least                  one olefin and the second output gas stream                  comprises hydrogen.
   EXAMPLE - Barium zirconium-yttrium-oxide (BYZ)                (BaZr0.85Y0.15O3-delta) support was prepared using                calcination of nitrate precursors. The metal                precursors were barium nitrate, zirconyl nitrate                and yttrium oxide. Complexing agents were EDTA,                citric acid, nitric acid and ammonium hydroxide.                Powders were produced utilizing a slightly modified                EDTA-citrate complexing synthesis method. In this                method, stoichiometric amounts of nitrate metal                cation precursors were combined with a sufficient                amount of EDTA and citric acid to ensure complete                cation mixing. The molar ratio of EDTA to citric                acid to BZY powder was 2.5:1.2:1. After adding                yttrium that was dissolved in a heated solution of                water and nitric acid, the solution was heated to                325degrees Celsius while adding ammonium hydroxide                to reduce the pH of the solution. The solution was                then stirred and heated continuously until a sticky                gel was formed. The gel was subsequently                transferred to a drying oven at 150degrees Celsius                for 12 hours. The result was a BZY char that was                then calcined at 900degrees Celsius for 10 hours,                producing as-calcined powder with an average                particle diameter of 40 nm. Bifunctional catalysts                were prepared using the incipient wetness                impregnation technique. Metal nitrate salts and                either ~c alumina powder or BZY powder were                premixed and diluted with water. The resulting                solution was dried overnight at 80degrees Celsius                while stirring. Finally, the catalyst was calcined                at 500degrees Celsius for 5 hours in air.

PN CN113292724-A; CN113292724-B
TI Preparing pyridine-rich cationic covalent triazine polymer comprises e.g. carrying out anion exchange of T-Covalent Triazine Frameworks-chlorine with sodium bromide solution to prepare T-covalent Triazine Frameworks-bromine.
AU HUANG H
   ZHAO Y
   ZHONG C
AE UNIV TIANJIN POLYTECHNIC (UNTP-C)
GA 2021A05738
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing pyridine-rich cationic covalent triazine polymer comprises (i) dissolving 1,3, 5-tris(bromomethyl) benzene and 4-cyano-pyridine in N-methyl-2-pyrrolidone and carrying out solvothermal reaction to produce high-purity 1,3,5-tris(4-cyanopyridine-1-methylene)benzene-tribromide; (ii) mixing the 1,3,5-tris(4-cyanopyridine-1-methylene)benzene-tribromide with anhydrous zinc chloride according to a certain proportion, placing in the heat-resistant glass tube, vacuum sealing under the condition of high temperature and carrying out ionic heat cyanotrimerization reaction to synthesize Covalent Triazine Frameworks-chlorine (T-CTF-Cl); and (iii) using 1 mol/liter sodium bromide solution and preparing Covalent Triazine Frameworks-bromine (T-CTF-Br) by anion exchange method.
   USE - The pyridine-rich cationic covalent triazine polymer is useful capturing and fixation of carbon dioxide, and preparation of cyclic carbonate (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: prepares pyridine-rich cationic covalent triazine polymer with exhibits high carbon dioxide adsorption capacity and good catalytic performance for the addition reaction of carbon dioxide and epoxy compounds thus provides rich and intuitive experience for the design and construction of ionic porous materials for sustainable catalysis and gas adsorption and separation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (i) comprises dissolving 1,3, 5-tris(bromomethyl) benzene and 4-cyano-pyridine in N-methyl-2-pyrrolidone, reacting under nitrogen protection at 80 degrees C for 20 hours, where the ratio of 4-cyanopyridine to 1,3,5-tris(bromomethyl)benzene is greater than 3.1. The step (i) comprises extracting 1,3,5-tris(4-cyanopyridine-1-methylene)benzene-tribromide by Soxhlet with a relatively low polarity solvent to wash away the raw materials that are not involved in the reaction. The step (ii) comprises using anhydrous zinc chloride as a catalyst and reaction medium in the reaction, and the molar ratio of 1,3,5-tris(4-cyanopyridine-1-methylene)benzene-tribromide to anhydrous zinc chloride is 1:9. The step (ii) comprises calcination at 400 degrees C and 500 degrees C respectively, heating by 1 degrees /minute, the calcination is 40 hours and cooling by 5 degrees /minute. The step (ii) comprises washing the crude product obtained with 1 mol/liter hydrochloric acid, deionized water, dichloromethane, methanol, and drying at 60 degrees C to obtain T-CTF-400-Cl and T-CTF-500-Cl. The step (iii) comprises carrying out anion replacement to prove that T-CTF-400-Cl only has free chloride ions and in addition to free chloride ions the T-CTF-500-Cl also has C-chlorine bonds.

TI Producing guava essential oil emulsion, comprises            primary supercritical extraction of guava branches or            leaves with supercritical carbon dioxide, secondary            extraction, and extracting the emulsion in form in            which guava branch or leaf essential oil is            emulsified.

   PARK S H
   PARKJUNGHOON
AE LEE H Y (LEEH-Individual)
GA 202182681F
AB    NOVELTY - A guava essential oil emulsified water                preparation method involves performing primary                supercritical extraction of guava branches or                leaves with supercritical carbon dioxide at 40-50                degrees C under pressure of 100-200 bar, and                performing secondary extraction of                supercritical-carbon dioxide at 45-55 degrees C at                pressure of 200-100 bar. The guava branch or leaf                essential oil is extracted by emulsifying the guava                in a form of eucalyptol at high concentration,                where the oil is beta -caryophyllene, azulene,                nerolidol or Eudesma-4. The extract is aged at low                temperature for 10-30 days at 5-10 degrees C. The                extracted oil is concentrated under reduced                pressure at 10-70 degrees C until volume of the                extract is 1-80% by volume. A partial concentrate                is treated with a hydrocarbon solvent to remove                water-insoluble component from the partial                concentrate.
   USE - The method is useful for producing guava                essential oil emulsified water.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare guava essential                oil emulsified water, which can be used and stored                for a long time without layer separation, and can                be maintained without change in flavor, function                and color.

AB    NOVELTY - Treatment method of Bi-2212 precursor powder                involves placing Bi-2212 precursor powder into                first quartz tube (1) with one end closed and                sodium peroxide powder into second quartz tube (2)                with one closed end, putting first quartz tube into                second quartz tube, vacuumizing first quartz tube                and second quartz tube by vacuum pump, sealing                second opening end to obtain combined quartz tube,                putting combined quartz tube into heat treatment                furnace for heat treatment so that carbon and                hydrogen elements in Bi-2212 precursor powder                diffuse and escape in the form of carbon dioxide                and water after being heated, reacting carbon                dioxide and hydrogen with sodium peroxide powder to                be absorbed, putting heat treated combined quartz                tube into glove box filled with argon gas, taking                out Bi-2212 powder, and storing.    USE - The method is used for treating Bi-2212                precursor powder.
   ADVANTAGE - The powder has simple operation and low cost,                greatly reduces carbon and hydrogen element                content, carries out whole process under isolating                air condition, and avoids possibility of external                carbon and hydrogen element invasion.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The atomic ratio of                  each metal element in Bi-2212 precursor powder is                  bismuth:strontium:calcium:copper=2:2:1:2. The                  mass of Bi-2212 precursor powder and sodium                  peroxide powder is 100-500 g and 10-20 g,                  respectively. Preferred Method: The vacuum degree                  in combined quartz tube is 10-5 Pa. Heat                  treatment is done at 400-450 degrees C for 10-20                  hours.
