PN CN111573620-A
TI Modular hydrogen production method using copper oxide-magnesium hydroxide circulating carrier to perform reaction regeneration cycle, configuring aqueous solution, passing mixed gas of methanol and water vapor and reforming.
AU ZENG L
   SUN C
AE UNIV TIANJIN (UTIJ-C)
GA 2020847985
AB    NOVELTY - Modular hydrogen production method by (i) using copper oxide-magnesium hydroxide circulating carrier to perform reaction-regeneration cycle, configuring aqueous solution of methanol as a reactant, which is preheated to obtain a mixed gas of methanol and water vapor, (ii) passing the mixed gas of methanol and water vapor obtained in the step (i) into the fuel reactor, copper oxide participates in the partial oxidation and catalytic reforming reaction of methanol, where at the same time magnesium hydroxide absorbs carbon dioxide to generate hydrogen and copper-magnesium carbonate (Cu-MgCO3), and (iii) transferring the oxygen or air into the regeneration reactor to realize the regeneration of the copper oxide-magnesium hydroxide circulating carrier while capturing carbon dioxide or obtaining carbon dioxide and nitrogen mixed gas, cyclically repeating the step (ii) and step (iii) to realize the autothermal reforming of methanol based on the chemical chain circulation mode.
   USE - Modular hydrogen production method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method combines the medium and low temperature copper oxide chemical chain circulation with the magnesium hydroxide absorption enhancement reforming, shortens the overall process, provides new method and ways for the efficient conversion of modular methanol to high-purity hydrogen.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of copper oxide-magnesium hydroxide is 0.06-0.10. The magnesium hydroxide crystal grain size of the copper oxide-magnesium hydroxide circulating carrier is 10-100 nm. In step (i), the molar ratio of water to methanol in the methanol aqueous solution is 0.3-1. Preferred Conditions: In step (i), the preheating temperature is 150-200 degrees C. In step (iii), the reaction temperature is 200-300 degrees C. In step (iii), the temperature of the reaction environment is 350-400 degrees C. Preferred Method: In step (iii), the reaction is carried out under an oxygen atmosphere and air atmosphere to capture high-purity carbon dioxide.

PT P
PN CN111072517-A; CN111072517-B
TI Preparing 4-amino-2-(trifluoromethyl)benzonitrile            comprises e.g. carrying out Grignard reaction of            5-fluoro-2-bromobenzotrifluoride, introducing carbon            dioxide, carrying out hydrolysis, heating and            dehydrating.
AU LI H
AE CHANGZHOU WATSON FINE CHEM CO LTD (CHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202038034Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing                4-amino-2-(trifluoromethyl)benzonitrile (I)                comprises (i) carrying out Grignard reaction of                5-fluoro-2-bromobenzotrifluoride (II), introducing                carbon dioxide, and carrying out hydrolysis to                obtain 4-fluoro-(2-trifluoromethyl)benzoic acid                (III), (ii) adding (III) into a autoclave, reacting                the liquid ammonia under the action of a catalyst                to produce 4-amino-(2-trifluoromethyl)benzamide                (IV), and (iii) heating (IV), adding dehydrating                agent and dehydrating to obtain final                product.
   USE - The method is useful in preparing                4-amino-2-(trifluoromethyl)benzonitrile.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: does not use highly toxic cuprous                cyanide; reduces the safety risk of production;                produces three wastes do not contain cyanide ions                and large amount of copper ions; and is easier to                handle and less harmful to the environment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing                4-amino-2-(trifluoromethyl)benzonitrile of formula                (I) comprises (i) carrying out Grignard reaction of                5-fluoro-2-bromobenzotrifluoride of formula (II),                introducing carbon dioxide, and carrying out                hydrolysis to obtain                4-fluoro-(2-trifluoromethyl)benzoic acid of formula                (III), (ii) adding (III) into a autoclave, reacting                the liquid ammonia under the action of a catalyst                to produce 4-amino-(2-trifluoromethyl)benzamide of                formula (IV), and (iii) heating (IV), adding                dehydrating agent and dehydrating to obtain final                product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (i), the carbon                  dioxide temperature is -10 to 50 degrees C after                  the Grignard reaction. In step (ii), the reaction                  pressure is 0.1-5 MPa. In the step (ii), copper                  salt or copper oxide is also added as catalyst.                  Preferred Components: In step (i), the solvent                  used is tetrahydrofuran and/or                  2-methyltetrahydrofuran. The molar ratio between                  (II) and carbon dioxide is 1:1-10. The acid used                  for hydrolysis after the reaction is hydrochloric                  acid or sulfuric acid, and the addition molar                  ratio between (II) and acid is 1:1-10. In step                  (ii), the reaction solvent is methanol, ethanol,                  isopropanol, tetrahydrofuran and/or                  2-methyltetrahydrofuran. In step (ii) directly                  use the hydrolyzed layered organic phase in step                  (i) to directly pass ammonia reaction. In step                  (iii), the dehydrating agent used is thionyl                  chloride, phosphorus oxychloride, phosphorus                  pentoxide, methanesulfonyl chloride and/or                  trifluoroacetic anhydride.

AE MARTIN U (MART-Individual)
   MARTIN U (MART-Individual)
GA 202009812G
AB    NOVELTY - Method for supplying aquatic plants and microorganisms present in water, preferably biological purification of the water, in a stagnant water with carbon-containing compounds such as carbon dioxide, and possibly with macro- and micronutrients, involves providing biodegradable, water-insoluble organic carbon substrates as nutrient depots.
   USE - The method is useful for supplying aquatic plants and microorganisms present in the water, preferably biological purification of the water, in stagnant water with carbon-containing compounds such as carbon dioxide, and possibly with macro- and micronutrients.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables a more efficient denitrification of the pond water and an efficient phosphate elimination in a cost-effective manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) use of biodegradable, water-insoluble organic carbon substrates as nutrient depots in the form of aggregates or in the form of special filter substrates with a large surface area, and optionally enriched with macro and micronutrients for supplying microorganisms in biofilters for performing denitrification and phosphate binding/elimination in standing waters chosen from swimming ponds, bathing ponds, natural pools and/or aquariums;
   (2) microbial bioreactor for generating carbon-containing compounds; and
   (3) device for performing the above-mentioned method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The biodegradable, water-insoluble organic carbon substrates are at least two components chosen from a first component of a rapidly degradable carbon source, which also functions as an adhesive and sealant, and a second structure-forming component of a slowly degradable carbon source. The rapidly degradable carbon source is starch and the slowly degradable carbon source is cellulose. Preferred Method: The method involves introducing biodegradable, water-insoluble organic carbon substrates as water-insoluble biodegradable carbon sources, in the form of polymers, preferably biopolymers and/or fatty acids/waxes and/or polysaccharides, or in the form of complex natural substrates such as in the form of wood pellets, hemp fibers, flax fibers or cotton fibers become. The biodegradable, water-insoluble organic carbon substrates, preferably in the form of biopolymers, fatty acids/waxes, polysaccharides and/or complex natural substrates with additional macro and micronutrients incorporated into the substrates. The method involves conveying through-flow water through at least one pipe, preferably drainage pipe, and/or hose, through the soil substrate having the nutrient depot e.g. swamp/shallow water area and/or planted deep water area into the water body of the planted deep water area, preferably pumped or sucked. The method involves introducing biodegradable, water-insoluble carbon sources, preferably biopolymers, fatty acids/waxes, polysaccharides and/or complex natural substrates into the perforated depot tubes placed in the bottom substrate of the swamp/shallow water area and/or the planted deep water area, which allow later refilling by flushing in new carbon sources. The method involves enriching the water, preferably water supplied to the deep water area with carbon dioxide in a bioreactor using microbial degradation of biodegradable, water-insoluble organic carbon substrates, preferably biopolymers, stearin, cellulose, and starch e.g. sago or potato starch.

PT P
PN CN109908086-A
TI Preparing a GelMA nano dry powder spray for rapid hemostasis and protection of wounds, involves dissolving gelatin methacrylate and a photoinitiator in a solvent to obtain a GelMA solution.
AU CHEN A
   WANG S
   CHEN Z
   CHEN B
AE UNIV HUAQIAO (UYHQ-C)
GA 201957370R
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a GelMA nano dry powder spray for rapid hemostasis and protection of wounds, involves dissolving gelatin methacrylate and a photoinitiator in a solvent to obtain a GelMA solution, where the gelatin methyl group has mass percentage concentration of the acrylate is 0.1-0.5%. The GelMA solution prepared is placed in a supercritical preparation device for granulation, and the preparation conditions are a flow rate of the GelMA solution of 0.1-3 mL/min, and a temperature of 32-55 degrees C. The pressure is 80-120 bar, the carbon dioxide flow rate is 15-50 g/min, and it is placed in a supercritical carbon dioxide anti-solvent reaction kettle. After the reaction is finished, the supercritical carbon dioxide is used for further washing for 0.3-2 hours, and the GelMA nanoparticles are collected. The GelMA nanoparticles are transferred to a dry powder sprayer to obtain a dry powder spray.
   USE - Method for preparing a GelMA nano dry powder spray.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing GelMA nano dry powder spray, which comprises a dry powder sprayer and internally filled GelMA nanoparticles, where the GelMA nanoparticles have a particle size of 60-200 nm.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: Solvents include ethanol, methanol, hexafluoroisopropanol, dimethyl sulfoxide, dimethylformamide, formamide or water. The photoinitiator in the middle includes lithium phenyl-2,4,6-trimethylbenzoylphosphonate. The mass percentage concentration of lithium phenyl-2,4,6-trimethylbenzoylphosphonate in the GelMA solution is 0.001-0.003%. The dry powder sprayer is a high pressure dry powder sprayer or a normal pressure dry powder sprayer. The photoinitiator is added to the GelMA nanoparticles by physical blending. The dissolution of GelMA nanoparticles at the wound absorbs water to form a gel, which allows the wound to quickly stop bleeding, and then the GelMA gel is rapidly solidified by photocrosslinking to achieve water and wound protection. The photocrosslinked light source includes ultraviolet, visible, and infrared light sources.

PN CN113926454-A
TI Preparation of cobalt-aluminum layered double hydroxide/porous carbon nitride foam three-dimensional heterojunction composite material, by reacting porous carbon nitride foam three-dimensional net-like structure in hydrotalcite precursor aqueous solution.
AU BAO X
   ZHU B
   XU Q
   QIN Y
AE UNIV GUANGXI NORMAL (UNGT-C)
GA 202217838Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of cobalt-aluminum layered double hydroxide (CoAl-LDH)/porous carbon nitride foam three-dimensional heterojunction composite material, comprises (1) preparing the melamine porous carbon nitride foam three-dimensional net-shaped structure; (2) preparing hydrotalcite precursor aqueous solution; (3) placing the porous carbon nitride foam three dimensional net-like structure into the hydrotalcite precursor aqueous solution to react to prepare the porous carbon nitride foam/hydrotalcite three-dimensional heterojunction material.
   USE - Cobalt-aluminum layered double hydroxide (CoAl-LDH)/ porous carbon nitride foam three-dimensional heterojunction composite material is used for catalytic reduction of carbon dioxide, where the carbon dioxide catalytic is catalytically reduced to carbon monoxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and uses easily available raw materials, short test period, high efficiency, easy to realize scale production. The composite material has good photo-catalytic stability and excellent photo-catalytic performance, carbon monoxide yield can reach 75.33 mol/g, about 2.26 times of pure LDHs powder material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for use of cobalt-aluminum layered double hydroxide (CoAl-LDH)/porous carbon nitride foam three-dimensional heterojunction composite material for catalytic reduction of carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The specific process of step 1 is as follows: heating the melamine sponge in nitrogen atmosphere at a heating rate of 10degrees Celsius/minute to 600degrees Celsius for 100 minutes, naturally cooling to room temperature, de-ionized water and ethanol alternately washing, drying to obtain the graphitic carbon nitride-rich porous carbon nitride foam, melamine porous carbon nitride foam exhibits a smooth three-dimensional net-shaped structure, the large hole of the three-dimensional net-shaped structure is 30-300 microns. The specific process of step 2 is as follows: providing 0.291-0.873 g cobalt nitrate nonahydrate, 0.188-0.563 g aluminum nitrate hexahydrate, 1.201 g in 70 ml de-ionized water, stirring to form uniform reaction liquid, placing into 100 ml polytetrafluoroethylene lined reaction kettle. The specific process of step 3 is as follows: adding melamine porous carbon nitride foam three-dimensional net shaped structure into the reaction liquid in a reaction kettle and ultrasonically processing for 30 minutes, sealing and placing in 120degrees Celsius oven for 8 hours for hydrothermal treatment, after finishing reaction, naturally cooling to room temperature, taking out and using de-ionized water and anhydrous ethanol respectively wash for 3 times to remove the impurity absorbed on the surface of the composite material, drying at 60degrees Celsius in the oven to obtain the porous carbon nitride foam/hydrotalcite three-dimensional heterojunction material. The specific process of catalytic reduction of carbon dioxide is as follows: placing the composite material in the reactor, heating the water bath boiler to generate steam, introducing the mixed gas of water vapor and carbon dioxide into the reactor by introducing carbon dioxide gas into the reactor, blowing for a certain time, and then adding illumination at the outer side of the reactor, sampling 1 ml gas product from the reaction chamber every hour, then using gas chromatography with FID and TCD detector for analysis. Before introducing the mixed gas, firstly introducing carbon dioxide gas to purge for 1.5 hours to remove the air in the reactor, the ventilation speed is 10 ml/minute. The reactor is a transparent quartz reactor, using 300W xenon lamp simulate sunlight xenon lamp the distance of the catalytic reduction material is 8 mm, the volume of the reactor is 200 ml. The mixed gas is equilibrated for 30 minutes in the atmosphere to ensure complete adsorption of the gas molecules.

PN KR2021150025-A
TI Device for reducing leakage of high-pressure working fluid e.g. supercritical carbon dioxide, has shroud-integrated impeller that minimizes leakage by forming appropriate gap with labyrinth seal.
AU WOO L
AE JINSOL TURBO MACHINERY CO LTD (JINS-Non-standard)
GA 2021E3110Q
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a labyrinth seal leak reduction device (400). A labyrinth seal (300) is installed immediately downstream of the leakage reducing device. A housing is provided with the labyrinth seal leakage reducing device and labyrinth seal, and with a space for accommodating a shroud-integrated impeller (100). A shroud-integrated impeller minimizes leakage by forming an appropriate gap with the labyrinth seal.
   USE - Device for reducing leakage of high-pressure working fluid e.g. supercritical carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The device eliminates speed of swirling component when a part of the fluid compressed through the shroud-integrated impeller passes through the space between the impeller and the housing, so that dynamic pressure of incoming flow is reduced and leakage flow rate through the labyrinth seal is reduced, thus consequently improving efficiency of a compressor. The device maintains a uniform pattern in circumferential direction by maintaining density of the working fluid such as supercritical carbon dioxide so as to improve stability of a rotating body rotating at high speed.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of a labyrinth seal leakage reduction device.
   Shroud-integrated impeller (100)
   Labyrinth seal (300)
   Labyrinth seal leak reduction device (400)

PN CN113663716-A; CN113663716-B
TI Indium oxide supported metal single-atom catalyst            for producing acetic acid, is prepared by dissolving            mixture of indium salt and aminophthalic acid in            N,N-dimethylformamide, separately dissolving metal            acetylacetone and aminated metal-organic framework            material in solvent, mixing, and carbonizing.
AU WAN H
   LI X
   WU J
   ZHANG W
   DING J
   GUAN G
AE UNIV NANJING TECHNOLOGY (UNTY-C)
GA 2021D7084M
AB    NOVELTY - Indium oxide supported metal single-atom                catalyst is prepared by (i) adding a mixture of                indium salt and aminophthalic acid to                N,N-dimethylformamide solution to dissolve,                uniformly stirring, reacting, filtering, washing,                and drying to obtain an aminated metal-organic                framework (MOF) material (MIL-68(In)-NH2), (ii)                separately adding an acetylacetone metal organic                compound and the aminated MOF material to a solvent                to dissolve, then uniformly mixing and reacting                under the protection of nitrogen, and (iii)                filtering the product obtained after the reaction,                washing, vacuum drying and carbonizing in a tube                furnace under a protective atmosphere for 1-5 hours                to obtain the product.
   USE - The catalyst is used in the direct production                of acetic acid from methane and carbon dioxide                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The indium oxide supported metal single-atom                catalyst catalyzes the co-conversion of methane and                carbon dioxide to directly produce acetic acid, and                has excellent catalytic activity and selectivity                under low pressure conditions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for the                application of the catalyst in the direct                production of acetic acid from methane and carbon                dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: In step (i), the                  reaction temperature is 80-140degrees Celsius,                  and the reaction time is 1-6 hours. In step (ii),                  the reaction temperature is 25-70degrees Celsius,                  and the reaction time is 4-12 hours. In step                  (iii), the carbonization temperature is                  700-900degrees Celsius, the heating rate is                  2-10degrees Celsius.minute-1, the flow rate is                  10-60 ml.minute-1, and the protective atmosphere                  is nitrogen, argon, or helium.Preferred Composition: In step (i), the mass                  ratio of the indium salt, aminophthalic acid and                  added N,N-dimethylformamide solution is                  (1-5):1:(60-80). In step (ii), the mass ratio of                  acetylacetone metal organic compound, aminated                  MOF material and solvent is                  1:(1-10):(10-40).Preferred Components: In step (i), the                  indium salt is indium nitrate, indium sulfate,                  indium chloride, or indium acetate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The application process                  involves (a) mixing the catalyst and quartz sand,                  and adding to a stainless steel microfluidic                  fixed-bed reactor with an inner diameter of 6-10                  mm, (b) purging with nitrogen, heating,                  performing hydrogen reduction and heating to                  reaction temperature, or purging with nitrogen                  and heating to reaction temperature, and (c)                  injecting methane and carbon dioxide at a certain                  flow rate to react.Preferred Conditions: During the application                  process: the reaction purged with nitrogen is                  heated to 300-450degrees Celsius at a rate of                  2-10degrees Celsius.minute-1; the hydrogen                  reduction flow rate is 10-60 ml.minute-1, and the                  reduction time is 3-12 hours; finally the                  reaction is heated to a reaction temperature of                  400-600degrees Celsius at a rate of 2-10degrees                  Celsius.minute-1; the volume flow ratio of                  methane to carbon dioxide is 1:(1-3); and the                  reaction pressure is 1-10 atmospheres.Preferred Components: In step (i), the                  aminophthalic acid is 2-aminoterephthalic acid,                  3-aminophthalic acid, or 4-aminophthalic acid. In                  step (ii), the acetylacetonate metal organic                  compound is iron acetylacetonate, cobalt                  acetylacetonate, manganese acetylacetonate,                  copper acetylacetonate, zinc acetylacetonate, or                  nickel acetylacetonate. In step (ii), the solvent                  is methanol, N,N-dimethylformamide, toluene, or                  chloroform.Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of                  catalyst to quartz sand is 1:(100-150).

PN CN113564630-A
TI Use of metal aerogel catalytic material for            preparing anion ion membrane electrolytic cell            electro-catalytic reduction carbon dioxide            reaction.
AU LIU J
   LU R
   GONG S
   WANG W
   LV X
AE UNIV JIANGSU (UYJS-C)
GA 2021C5719M
AB    NOVELTY - Use of metal aerogel catalytic material for                preparing anion ion membrane electrolytic cell                electro-catalytic reduction carbon dioxide (CO2)                reaction. The method comprises (i) respectively                dissolving metal precursor and reducing agent in                ultra-pure water, fresh prepared reducing agent                aqueous solution, rapidly adding metal precursor                solution, and continuously stirring, allowing to                stand at room temperature, aging to obtain                hydrogel, then freezing and drying to obtain metal                aerogel catalytic material for anionic membrane                electrolytic cell, adding metal gas gel or                composite material containing metal gas gel to                alcohol organic solvent containing Nafion                (Sulfonated tetrafluoroethylene) solution to form                uniform catalyst ink, using the cathode flow field                i.e. directly introducing dry CO2 as raw material                into the cathode of the gas transmission layer,                gaseous CO2, liquid electrolyte and solid catalyst                of the three-phase interface CO2 reduction                reaction.
   USE - The metal aerogel catalytic material is useful                for preparing anion ion membrane electrolytic cell                electro-catalytic reduction carbon dioxide reaction                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The metal aerogel catalytic material: has                excellent catalytic activity of electro-catalysis                reducing CO2 reaction, has three-dimensional                structure of the catalyst that avoids agglomeration                of catalyst and inactivation caused by corrosion                poisoning and forms cathode gas diffusion electrode                using the catalyst with high Faraday efficiency and                current density.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal precursor in                  the step (i) is silver salt, gold salt, palladium                  salt, bismuth salt and tin salt, the reducing                  agent is sodium borohydride, potassium                  borohydride, ascorbic acid or hydrazine hydrate.                  The metal precursor is nitrate of silver,                  sulfate, acetate, chloride of gold, chloride of                  palladium, nitrate of bismuth and chloride of                  tin. The saturated aqueous phase electrolyte of                  CO2 in the step (iv) comprises saturated sodium                  bicarbonate solution of CO2, potassium                  bicarbonate solution, potassium chloride                  solution, potassium sulfate solution, phosphate                  buffer solution or borate buffer solution.Preferred Method: The method further                  comprises (ii) adding the metal gas gel prepared                  in the step (i) or the composite material                  containing metal gas gel to the alcohol organic                  solvent containing Nafion (Sulfonated                  tetrafluoroethylene) solution to form uniform                  catalyst ink, coating the obtained catalyst ink                  on the hydrophobic carbon paper by dipping or                  spraying to form cathode gas diffusion electrode;                  (iii) according to the cathode flow field plate,                  cathode gasket, cathode gas diffusion electrode,                  anion membrane, anode material, anode gasket,                  anode flow field plate are assembled in order to                  keep good surface contact the adjacent                  components; and (iv) providing the device                  assembled in the step (iii) i.e. orderly                  connected with the gas-liquid pipeline, electrode                  contact and temperature control device, dipping                  the gas diffusion electrode in the CO2 saturated                  water phase electrolyte overnight, taking out and                  using ultra-pure water to wash to remove the                  residual electrolyte, using the cathode flow                  field i.e. directly introducing dry CO2 as raw                  material into the cathode of the gas transmission                  layer, gaseous CO2, liquid electrolyte and solid                  catalyst of the three-phase interface CO2                  reduction reaction.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon material                  comprises conductive carbon black, carbon                  nano-fiber, carbon nano-tube or graphene, and the                  alcohol organic solvent comprises methanol,                  ethanol and isopropanol.

AE UNIV HENAN NORMAL (UYNR-C)
GA 2021B8044G
AB    NOVELTY - A nanowire structured electrocatalyst is prepared by dissolving chloroauric acid, copper chloride dihydrate and glucose in water, stirring and mixing to obtain a solution (A), dissolving oleylamine in water, stirring and mixing to obtain a solution (B), adding the solution (A) to the solution (B) at room temperature, ultrasonically treating for 30 minutes, stirring and mixing at room temperature, transferring the obtained suspension to an autoclave lined with polytetrafluoroethylene, sealing the autoclave, reacting at 120degreesC, and centrifugally washing with ethanol and deionized water at 10000 rpm. The synthesis method has simple operation, high reaction efficiency and low energy consumption.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - The molar ratio of the chloroauric acid and dihydrate copper chloride is 1:1.

PT P
PN KR2021074618-A
TI Preparing polyether carbonate polyol by            copolymerizing carbon dioxide, propylene oxide, and            polyether polyol in the presence of double metal            cyanide catalyst.
AU HWAN H
   KIM I
   JEONG J
AE HYUNDAI MOTOR CO LTD (HYMR-C)
   KIA CORP (KIAK-C)
GA 202172357D
AB    NOVELTY - A polyether carbonate polyol is prepared by                copolymerizing carbon dioxide, propylene oxide, and                polyether polyol in the presence of double metal                cyanide catalyst (I).
   USE - Preparation of polyether carbonate polyol                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The polyether carbonate polyol can be prepared                even under a pressure of 3-5 bar, where the                copolymerization reaction pressure is low by using                the double metal cyanide catalyst (I).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A polyether carbonate polyol is prepared by                copolymerizing carbon dioxide, propylene oxide, and                polyether polyol in the presence of double metal                cyanide catalyst of formula (I).
   M1=Zn(II), Fe(II), Ni(II), Mn(II), Co(II),                Sn(II), Pb(II), Fe(III), Mo(IV), Mo(VI), Al(II),                V(V), V(IV), Sr(II), W(IV), W(VI), Cu(II), or                Cr(III);
   M2=potassium hexacyanocobaltate (III),                potassium hexacyanoferrate (II), potassium                hexacyanoferrate (III), calcium hexacyanocobaltate                (II), or lithium hexacyanoferrate (II); and
   CA1=organic complexing agent containing ketone                and OH.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) production of double metal cyanide                catalyst (I) which involves preparing 1st mixed                solution containing organic complexing agent, metal                salt, and distilled water, preparing 2nd mixed                solution containing metal cyanide salt and                distilled water, and mixing 1st mixed solution and                2nd mixed solution to prepare the catalyst                (I);
   (2) double metal cyanide catalyst (I);                and
   (3) polyether carbonate polyol.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: Organic complexing                  agent is 4-hydroxy-methyl-2-pentanone. Molar                  ratio of double metal cyanide catalyst (I) to                  propylene oxide is 5000:1-50000:1.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: Polyether polyol is a                  polypropylene glycol block copolymer having a                  number average molecular weight of 400 g/mol.                  Polyether polyol is polypropylene glycol block                  copolymer, polyethylene glycol block copolymer,                  polytetramethylene ether glycol block copolymer,                  block copolymer of ethylene oxide and propylene                  oxide, butylene oxide polymer, and/or                  hyperbranched polyglycitol. Carbon dioxide,                  propylene oxide, and polyether polyol form a                  polyethercarbonate polyol having a carbonate                  content of 15-19% and number average molecular                  weight (Mn) of 3000-4000 g/mol. Preferred                  Processes: The method further comprises                  introducing the double metal cyanide catalyst (I)                  into a trap installed inside the reactor; purging                  the inside of the reactor using carbon dioxide                  gas; injecting a solution containing propylene                  oxide and polyether polyol into the reactor;                  injecting carbon dioxide into the reactor to                  increase the reactor internal pressure and                  temperature to 3-5 bar and 90-110 degrees C;                  increasing stirring speed up to 300 rpm to drop                  the double metal cyanide catalyst (I) into a                  solution to polyether carbonate polyol; and                  initiating polymerization.
   EXAMPLE - 10 mg Double metal cyanide catalyst was                introduced at the top of a 170 ml high-pressure                reactor, and the inside of the reactor was purged                with carbon dioxide gas. 5 ml Polypropylene glycol                and 20 ml propylene oxide dissolved in 10 ml                toluene solution was injected into the reactor. At                this time, the catalyst exists in a state trapped                in a trap in the reactor and in a state separated                from propylene oxide. The reactor internal pressure                was increased from 1 bar to 3 bar, and the reactor                temperature was increased from room temperature to                90 degrees C. The pressure inside the reactor was                maintained at 3 bar by continuously supplying                carbon dioxide, and by increasing the stirrer speed                to 300 rpm, the catalyst trapped in the trap was                dropped into the solution, and polymerization was                carried out by contacting the catalyst with                propylene oxide. After reacting for 1 hour, the                supply of carbon dioxide was stopped, the unreacted                carbon dioxide inside the reactor was discharged to                the outside, and the temperature of the reactor was                cooled to room temperature. At this time, the                viscosity of the product inside the reactor was                lowered by using a chloroform solvent, and the                catalyst and the polymerization product were                separated using a vacuum glass filter. By drying at                80 degrees C and a vacuum of 30 inHg, unreacted                cyclohexene oxide was removed, and polyether                carbonate polyol was recovered.
DC A95 (Transport - including vehicle parts, tyres and armaments.); A23 (Polyamides; polyesters. (including polycarbonates, polyesteramides); alkyds; other unsaturated polymers.); A25 (Polyurethanes; polyethers.)
MC A02-A00A; A05-E09; A10-D05
IP C08G-064/34
PD KR2021074618-A   22 Jun 2021   C08G-064/34   202163   Pages: 11

AU VENDITTI M
   MAHOUTIAN M
   MERDAD M
   MARIO B
AE CARBICRETE INC (CARB-Non-standard)
   CARBICRETE INC (CARB-Non-standard)
   CARBICRETE INC (CARB-Non-standard)
GA 2021644245
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves casting and demoulding the                concrete product. The concrete product is                positioned on a base (102). The opening is sealed                using a cover plate (108). A gas containing carbon                dioxide (CO2) is introduced into the cavity to                execute carbonation of the concrete product after                the casting and demoulding of the concrete product.                The opening is unsealed in response to the concrete                product attaining a target specification. The                cavity is pressurized to a first pressure for a                first period of time followed by increasing the                pressure in the cavity to a second pressure for a                second period of time. The first and second                pressures are balanced with the cover plate.
   USE - Method for curing a concrete product, such as                pipes, manholes and culverts used in building                structures.
   ADVANTAGE - Method for curing a concrete product reduces                curing times and cost. Strength of the concrete                product is increased. Carbon dioxide is prevented                from escaping through the outer layer of concrete                product. The integrity of the concrete product is                protected at early ages.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a                system for curing a concrete product.

   Seal (104)
   Cover plate (108)
   Pressure gauge (112)
   Flow control valves (114)

PT P
PN CN112920418-A; CN112920418-B
TI New bromine and uncoordinated groups containing honeycomb two-dimensional metal organic polyhedral framework compound useful for capturing carbon dioxide in flue gas of thermal power plant.
AU DU L
   MA M
   MIAO Y
   XU L
   ZHANG J
AE UNIV NANJING FORESTRY (UNJF-C)
GA 2021657330
AB    NOVELTY - Bromine and uncoordinated groups containing honeycomb two-dimensional metal organic polyhedral framework compound (I) is new. (I) is prepared by taking 5-((3-bromo-5-carboxyphenyl)ethynyl)isophthalic acid as ligand and N,N-dimethylformamide and water as solvent, involving all the carboxylic acid groups in the ligand in coordination and forming dual-nuclear paddle-shaped copper cluster with the divalent copper ion, thus constructing a bromine and uncoordinated group containing two-dimensional network structure.
   USE - (I) is useful for capturing carbon dioxide in flue gas of thermal power plant (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - (I): exhibits high carbon dioxide adsorption selectivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Bromine and uncoordinated groups containing honeycomb two-dimensional metal organic polyhedral framework compound of formula (C45.5H27.5Br2.5Cu3.75NO18.75) (I) is new. (I) is prepared by taking 5-((3-bromo-5-carboxyphenyl)ethynyl)isophthalic acid as ligand and N,N-dimethylformamide and water as solvent, involving all the carboxylic acid groups in the ligand in coordination and forming dual-nuclear paddle-shaped copper cluster with the divalent copper ion, thus constructing a bromine and uncoordinated group containing two-dimensional network structure. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparation of (I).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of (I), comprises (i) dissolving 5-((3-bromo-5-carboxyphenyl)ethynyl)isophthalic acid as ligand and copper(II) nitrate trihydrate in N,N-dimethylformamide, adding concentrated nitric acid, performing ultrasonic vibration to dissolve completely, (ii) transferring and sealing the prepared solution, performing constant temperature solvothermal reaction at 60-80 degrees C, cooling to room temperature to obtain blue-green pyramid-shaped crystals, (iii) washing with N,N-dimethylformamide and water, and drying to obtain final product. Preferred Method: The ligand is prepared by (a) dissolving methyl 3,5-dibromobenzoate (1a) in tetrahydrofuran-triethylamine under the protection of nitrogen, adding bis(triphenylphosphine)palladium(II) dichloride and cuprous iodide, stirring, adding 5-ethynyl-isophthalic acid dimethyl ester (1b) to react, filtering the filtrate and draining under reduced pressure to obtain black solid, introducing through a column, and drying to obtain dimethyl 5-((3-bromo-5-(methoxycarbonyl)phenyl)ethynyl)isophthalate (II), and (ii) dissolving (II) in a methanol-water mixture, and adding lithium hydroxide for reaction to obtain white solid of 5-((3-bromo-5-carboxyphenyl)ethynyl)isophthalic acid. Preferred Components: In formation of (I), the uncoordination of the bromine causes the formation of elongated triangular gyroscopic nanocages, and the triangular gyroscopic nanocages of adjacent layers are close to the pi-pi interaction force between the bromobenzene rings of the ligands of the adjacent layers and the bromine-pi interaction force between the bromine atom of the ligand of the adjacent layer and the benzene ring of the dicarboxylate is connected. The crystal of (I) belongs to the triclinic system, has P-1 space group and unit cell parameters (a is 15.2383(7) Angstrom , b is 15.2536(7) Angstrom , c is 19.9407(9) Angstrom , alpha is 85.996(1) degrees , beta is 70.847(1) degrees and gamma is 71.275(1) degrees ). In step (i), the molar ratio between ligand and copper(II) nitrate trihydrate is 1:2-5, preferably 1:5; the dosage ratio of ligand to N,N-dimethylformamide and concentrated nitric acid is 0.012 mmol: 2 ml: 0.04-1.8 ml, preferably 0.012 mmol: 2 ml: 0.06 ml.
DC E14 (Aromatics - i.e. containing at least one benzene ring.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).); X11 (Power Generation and High Power Machines)
MC E05-L03B; E10-C02B; E11-F03; E11-F11; E11-K03; E11-Q02C; E11-W; E31-N05C; L03-H01; L03-J; N07-D03; N07-H; N07-K01; X11-A08

PT P
PN IN201911047258-A
TI Integrated electrochemical-chemical process for preparation of adipic acid used for production of polymers, involves performing electrolysis of carbon-dioxide, butadiene saturated in dimethyl formamide with tetrabutylammonium bromide.
AU CHANDRASEKARAN N K
   KULANDAINATHAN M A
AE COUNCIL SCI & IND RES INDIA (COUI-C)
GA 202159431U
AB    NOVELTY - Integrated electrochemical and chemical process for preparation of adipic acid from carbon dioxide (CO2) and butadiene involves performing electrolysis of CO2 and butadiene saturated in dimethyl formamide (DMF) with tetrabutylammonium bromide (TBABr) as supporting electrolyte by applying potential of 3-6 Volt for 3 hours between aluminum anode and Nickel/Spent Raney Nickel cathode with constant stirring of electrolyte at rate of 600-1500 rotation/minute, temperature of 25-45 degrees C and pressure of 0.15-3 Megapascal to obtain 70-90% pure hexenedioate; performing chemically hydrogenating which results to obtain hexenedioate product by dissolving in sulphuric acid and ethanol for duration of 5 hour using Raney nickel catalyst at pressure of 1-3 bar hydrogen (H2) and at temperature of 25-45 degrees C, where adipic acid is extracted using petroleum ether.
   USE - Integrated electrochemical and chemical process for preparation of adipic acid from carbon dioxide and butadiene used for production of polymers, such as nylon and polyurethane.
   ADVANTAGE - The process enables to prepare acid by integrating electrochemical and chemical processes which operates at ambient pressure and temperature with less energy, high yield and no production of toxic chemicals.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The cathode includes perforated or porous at least one of iron (Fe), nickel (Ni), copper (Cu), stainless steel (SS), where cathode includes spent Raney Ni. The anode includes aluminum (Al), magnesium (Mg), zinc (Zn), where no separator or membrane is needed to separate cathode and anode compartments. Preferred Process: The integrated electrochemical and chemical process for preparation of adipic acid from CO2 and butadiene involves extracting crude product predominantly contains hexenedioate at bottom of electrochemical reactor, where crude product treated with certain amount of Raney Nickel catalyst in mixture of sulphuric acid and ethanol for duration of 5 hours at temperature 25-45 degrees C, and 1-3 bars of H2 pressure to obtain solution of ethanol ester of adipic acid.

   STATE GRID TIANJIN ELECTRIC POWER CORP (SGCC-C)
   STATE GRID ELECTRIC POWER RES INST (SGCC-C)
   STATE GRID CORP CHINA (SGCC-C)
GA 2021223646
AB    NOVELTY - Grease comprises 60-120 pts. wt. silicone oil, 0.1-30 pts. wt. graphene, 0.1-5 pts. wt. silane coupling agent and supercritical carbon dioxide (CO2). The supercritical CO2 makes the pressure inside the pressure tank 10-100 atm.
   USE - Grease used for live working robot wire clamp contact parts.
   ADVANTAGE - The grease prevents the surface of the electrical connection fittings from being damaged by the continuous temperature rise and heating, has the performance that the contact resistance decreases when the ambient temperature rises, has simple preparation method, strong operability, high storage stability, sprayed directly on site, and improves the safety and stability of grid operation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing a grease, which involves:
   (A) placing graphene, silicone oil and silane coupling agent at 100-160 degrees C;
   (B) kneading under the condition of -0.05 to-0.09 megapascal (MPa) for 2-3 hours to obtain semi-finished products, and then grounding 3-5 times with three rollers;
   (C) placing the semi-finished product after the above three-roller grinding in the tank;
   (D) injecting supercritical carbon dioxide until the pressure in the tank reaches 10-100 atm; and
   (E) preparing a grease.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The silicone oil includes a mixture of methyl silicone oil, methyl phenyl silicone oil and hydroxy silicone oil. The silane coupling agent includes phenyltrimethoxysilane, phenyltriethoxysilane or vinyltriethoxysilane. The silane coupling agent includes phenyltrimethoxysilane or phenyltriethoxysilane. Preferred Condition: The silicone oil has a viscosity of 50,000-200,000 cst, and it includes at least one of methyl silicone oil, hydroxy silicone oil or methyl phenyl silicone oil. The number of layers of the graphene is less than 10.

GA 2021186173
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining a blasting parameter. Blasting hole distance is calculated according to increase rock strength selecting blasting cylinder type. PT P
PN CN110961109-A
TI Preparing catalyst for hydrogenation of carbon dioxide, by adding nickel nitrate hexahydrate, gamma-aluminum oxide and polyvinylpyrrolidone to water, transferring to microwave-ultrasonic reactor, drying and performing reduction.
AU SONG F
   CAO Y
   LIU Y
   YANG B
   CHEN T
   YAN J
   XU Q
AE YANCHENG INST TECHNOLOGY (YANH-C)
GA 202029013N
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing nickel-aluminum oxide catalyst, involves (i) adding nickel nitrate hexahydrate, gamma -aluminum oxide and polyvinylpyrrolidone to 60 ml deionized water, uniformly stirring, and transferring to a 100 ml PTFE reactor, (ii) transferring the reactor of step (i) to a microwave-ultrasonic combined reactor, setting the microwave power to 400-600 W, the ultrasonic power to 800-1000 W and temperature at 120-150 degrees C, and performing the constant temperature reaction under autogenous pressure for 15-30 minutes, (iii) cooling the reaction product of step (ii) to room temperature, centrifuging the product, drying, and calcining at 400-500 degrees C for 4 hours to obtain nickel oxide-aluminum oxide precursor, and (iv) performing reduction on the nickel oxide-aluminum oxide precursor at 400 degrees C in an atmosphere of hydrogen/nitrogen mixed gas to obtain a highly dispersed nickel-aluminum oxide catalyst.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing nickel-aluminum oxide catalyst used for hydrogenation of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method solves the problems of long reaction time and uneven dispersion of active components during the preparation of the catalyst, and improves the dispersibility of the catalyst and activity in the carbon dioxide hydrogenation reaction.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (i) involves mixing nickel nitrate hexahydrate and gamma -aluminum oxide, where the ratio of nickel and gamma -aluminum oxide is 10-30 wt.%, pouring to a PTFE reactor provided with a pressure sensor, adding 60 ml 0.1 mol/l polyvinylpyrrolidone solution, and magnetically stirring for 5 minutes. The step (iv) involves performing reduction on the nickel oxide-aluminum oxide precursor in the hydrogen/nitrogen mixed gas processed at 400 degrees C for 30-60 minutes, where the volume ratio of the hydrogen/nitrogen mixed gas is 20/30. Preferred Conditions: In step (iii), the heating rate during calcination is maintained at 2 degrees C/minute.
DC J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

In step (c), the pumping pressure of supercritical carbon dioxide when recovering condensate is 75-100 MPa. Preferred Properties: The thickness of hydrophobic oleophylic film layer is 10-12 mu m. The water control rate of water control agent is greater than or equal to 90%, and the particle diameter is 16-300 mesh.

PN CN109777597-A
TI Extracting grape seed essential oil, comprises removing impurities from grape seed, washing, freeze-drying, powdering into granules, extracting, pumping liquid carbon dioxide into the vaporizer, separating and purifying the grape seed oil.
AU SU J
AE SU J (SUJJ-Individual)
GA 201948248F
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting grape seed essential oil, comprises (i) removing impurities from grape seed, washing and drying; (ii) freeze-drying the grape seed, powdering into 40-60 mesh granules; (iii) passing the granules into an extraction vessel for extraction at 35-40 degrees C, pressure 22-30 MPa, for 100-120 minutes; pumping liquid carbon dioxide into the vaporizer during the extraction process, where gasification is in a critical fluid state, passing into the extractor to extract the grape seed material at 10-18 MPa, 30-40 degrees C, passing the mixed fluid of the extracted carbon dioxide and grape seed oil into a separator to be separated under reduced pressure to obtain a grape seed oil; (iv) purifying the grape seed oil by molecular distillation at 18-23 degrees C and a pressure of 5-10 MPa; and (v) decolorizing the grape seed oil to obtain a grape seed essential oil having a purity of 99.9 wt.%.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting grape seed essential oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has high extraction rate, produces oil with good flavor and no quality problem.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (iii), the separator is a conical condenser, the condenser is provided with purified water, the water temperature is constant at 18-23 degrees C, and the oil-water mixed gas is sent to the condenser at a temperature of 18-23 degrees C, then condensed into a liquid, allowed to stand, layering the oil and water due to the difference in specific gravity, the oil phase rises into the n-shaped elbow at the upper end of the conical condenser, and the grape seed net oil having a content of 98 wt.% or more is collected by the n-shaped elbow.

PN CN109704383-A
TI Cubic crystalline calcium carbonate used in industries e.g. plastics, is prepared by modulating calcium chloride and ammonia aqueous solution, adding crystal type guiding agent, mixing, carbonizing, aging, dehydrating, washing and drying.
AU TONG Z

   LI L
   LIAO D
   QIN L
   ZHOU W
   ZHONG L
   MENG Y
AE GUANGXI CALCIUM CARBONATE INDUSTRIALIZAT (UYGU-C)
   UNIV GUANGXI (UYGU-C)
GA 201942321J
AB    NOVELTY - Cubic crystalline calcium carbonate is prepared by modulating calcium chloride and ammonia aqueous solution, adding a crystal type guiding agent, fully mixing, introducing carbon dioxide to carbonize, aging, dehydrating, washing and drying, where the calcium carbonate has particle size of 1-30 microns.
   USE - The cubic crystalline calcium carbonate is useful in plastics, rubber, papermaking, foods, medicines and cosmetics industries (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The cubic crystalline calcium carbonate does not need surface treatment, adopts simple and feasible preparation method and low production cost and has excellent dispersibility and economic and social benefits.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing the cubic crystalline calcium carbonate involving (i) preparing calcium chloride and ammonia aqueous solution, adding the crystal type guiding agent, (iii) carrying out temperature regulation, introducing carbon dioxide gas for carbonization reaction, judging the reaction is ended when the pH value is 6.5-7 and stopping introducing carbon dioxide gas and (iv) aging after the carbonization reaction is completed, filtering, washing and drying to obtain the cubic crystalline calcium carbonate, filtering, washing and recycling of ammonium chloride solution obtained by filtering and washing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (i) involves preparing the calcium chloride and ammonia aqueous solution by dissolving calcium chloride in water, adding aqueous ammonia, reacting calcium hydroxide slurry with ammonium chloride or aqueous solution of ammonium chloride to prepare the calcium chloride and ammonia aqueous solution, reacting carbide slag or other substances containing calcium hydroxide with ammonium chloride to prepare the calcium chloride and ammonia aqueous solution, where the molar ratio of calcium chloride to aqueous ammonia is 1:2 and the aqueous ammonia can be used slightly excess. The method involves fully dissolving calcium chloride in water, aging, filtering out insoluble substances to remove impurities, adding aqueous ammonia into the calcium chloride solution to prepare the calcium chloride and ammonia aqueous solution, reacting calcium hydroxide slurry with ammonium chloride to prepare the calcium chloride and ammonia aqueous solution, filtering to remove insoluble substances to obtain a clear calcium chloride and ammonia aqueous solution, where the content of calcium chloride in the calcium chloride and ammonia aqueous solution is 8-20%. The step (ii) involves adding the crystal type guiding agent which is a phosphoric acid crystal type guiding agent specifically comprising sodium phosphate, potassium phosphate, ammonium phosphate, sodium tripolyphosphate, potassium tripolyphosphate, sodium pyrophosphate and potassium pyrophosphate, where the addition amount of the crystal type guiding agent is 0.2-5 %mass based on the mass of calcium chloride. The method involves controlling temperature of the calcium chloride and ammonia aqueous solution to 5-40 degrees C. The step (iv) involves aging can be non-aging or room temperature aging, where the aging can be accelerated by proper heating. The step (iv) involves filtering to obtain the ammonium chloride solution, where the ammonium chloride solution can be used for dissolving calcium carbide slag or calcium hydroxide and reacting to generate the calcium chloride and ammonia aqueous solution, and realizing cyclic utilization. Preferred Composition: The carbon dioxide gas has a concentration of 5-100%, where the carbon dioxide gas can be a kiln gas used for purifying a lime kiln to purify a certain carbon dioxide gas, pure gas of carbon dioxide and mixed gas of carbon dioxide and other gases.

PT P
PN CN109513445-A; CN109513445-B
TI Ternary metal oxide thin film catalyst useful for catalytic oxidation of volatile organic pollutants and hydrogenation of carbon dioxide, comprises substrate, and ternary metal oxide thin layer deposited on substrate.
AU TIAN Z
   YU D
AE INST ENG THERMOPHYSICS CHINESE (CAET-C)
GA 2019295631
AB    NOVELTY - A ternary metal oxide thin film catalyst comprises a substrate and a thin layer of ternary metal oxide deposited on the substrate. The ternary metal oxide thin layer comprises three metal oxides separately chosen from precious metals, transition metals or oxide of rare earth metals.
   USE - The ternary metal oxide thin film catalyst is useful for the catalytic oxidation of volatile organic pollutants and hydrogenation of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The ternary metal oxide thin film catalyst not only can catalyze the oxidative removal of volatile organic pollutants, but also has a certain catalytic effect on the hydrogenation conversion of carbon dioxide, and large specific surface area.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing the ternary metal oxide thin film catalyst, which involves: (1) preparing a metal organic compound solution of three metals, and then mixing to-be-used as a precursor solution, where the three metals are independently chosen from a noble metal, a transition metal or a rare earth metal; and (2) forming a thin layer of uniform ternary metal oxide on the substrate by pulse atomization thermal evaporation chemical vapor deposition.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (2) involves: (21) adjusting the evaporation temperature, the substrate temperature, and the degree of vacuum in the pulse atomization thermal evaporation chemical vapor deposition apparatus, and introducing nitrogen gas and oxygen into the pulse atomization thermal evaporation chemical vapor deposition apparatus; (22) spraying the precursor solution obtained in the step (1) into the apparatus for evaporation to obtain precursor vapor; and (23) transporting the precursor vapor to the surface of the substrate by the nitrogen and oxygen to carry out a chemical reaction to grown a thin layer of the ternary metal oxide on the substrate. Preferred Conditions: In the step (21), the evaporation temperature is 100-200 degrees C, the substrate temperature is 200-700 degrees C, and the pressure is in the order of kPa. In the step (22), the precursor solution is sprayed at a frequency of 0.5-20 Hz and a pulse width of 0.1-10 ms. Preferred Properties: The ternary metal oxide thin layer has a thickness of 100-200 nm. Preferred Components: The ternary metal oxide thin layer is a thin layer made of zinc oxide-zirconium dioxide-metal oxide compound of formula: ZnO-ZrO2-MOx (I). The molar content of the zinc element in the ternary metal oxide thin layer is greater than 0 and less than or equal to 90%. The molar content of the zirconium element is greater than 0 and less than or equal to 90%. The substrate is chosen from metal mesh, high-temperature resistant flakes (both preferred), molecular sieves, honeycomb ceramics or activated carbon. The ternary metal oxide thin layer is used in an amount of less than 2 wt.% the entire catalyst. In the step (1), the concentration of the metal organic compound solution is in the order of mmol/l, and the component content of the ternary metal oxide is adjusted by adjusting the concentration ratio of the three metal organic compounds.M = Cu or Co.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN CN113527097-A
TI Refining of carbonate e.g. dimethyl carbonate involves before distilling or rectifying crude carbonate product, filling saturated concentration carbon dioxide in crude product, absorbing and dissolving material liquid in carbon dioxide, and distilling or rectifying.
AU ZHONG R
AE ZHONG R (ZHON-Individual)
GA 2021C5118E
AB    NOVELTY - Refining of carbonate involves before distilling or rectifying the crude carbonate product, filling 5-100% carbon dioxide in the crude product, absorbing and dissolving the material liquid in carbon dioxide, and distilling or rectifying to obtain carbonate products. The carbonate is dimethyl carbonate, ethylene carbonate or propylene carbonate.
   USE - Method for refining carbonate e.g. dimethyl carbonate, ethylene carbonate or propylene carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can inhibit the decomposition of dimethyl carbonate, ethylene carbonate and propylene carbonate during distillation or rectification, improves the effect of distillation or rectification, increases product purity, and reduces material loss.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The crude carbonate product is non-standard purity carbonate product. The crude carbonate product is a product obtained from a carbonate synthesis process, or is a crude product obtained from a carbonate synthesis process after flashing treatment, or it is a carbonate fraction whose purity is not up to the standard after one or more distillations or rectifications, or a mixture of multiple of the above.Preferred Property: The purity of the carbonate does not meet the standard due to the inclusion of moisture, alcohol components or other impurity components in the crude carbonate product.Preferred Composition: The added amount of carbon dioxide is 50-100% of the saturation concentration of carbon dioxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The carbon dioxide can be charged under normal pressure or under pressure.Preferred Property: The distillation or rectification is atmospheric distillation, vacuum distillation, atmospheric rectification or vacuum rectification.

PT P
PN CN112341567-A; CN112341567-B
TI Thickener used for supercritical carbon dioxide            oil displacement is prepared by taking            fluorine-containing acrylate, tert-amine-containing            acrylate and styrene as polymerization monomers, and            performing free radical bulk polymerization.
AU SONG K
   YU Q
   HOU J
   JIANG S
   BAI M
AE UNIV CHINA PETROLEUM BEIJING (UPEB-C)
   UNIV NORTHEAST PETROLEUM (UYPT-C)
GA 202117440H
AB    NOVELTY - Thickener is prepared by taking                fluorine-containing acrylate, tert-amine-containing                acrylate and styrene as polymerization monomers,                and performing free radical bulk                polymerization.
   USE - The thickener is used for supercritical carbon                dioxide oil displacement or supercritical carbon                dioxide oil displacement agent (all claimed) in oil                field.
   ADVANTAGE - The thickener has low cloud point pressure and                excellent viscosity increasing performance and                improves oil recovery rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) preparation of the thickener comprising                polymerizing at 50-55 degrees C for 12-24 hours;                and
   (2) supercritical carbon dioxide oil                displacement agent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Component: The content of                  fluorine-containing acrylate monomer is 55-60                  mol.%, preferably 58 mol.% of the total mol                  amount of the polymerization monomers. The                  content of the tert-amine-containing acrylate                  monomer is 10-15 mol.% of the total mol amount of                  the polymerization monomers. The content of                  styrene monomer is 27-32 mol.% of the total molar                  amount of the polymerization monomers. The                  fluorine-containing acrylate monomer is                  trifluoroethyl (meth)acrylate, hexafluorobutyl                  (meth)acrylate, or preferably dodecafluoroheptyl                  (meth)acrylate. The tert-amine-containing                  acrylate monomer is 2-(dimethylamino)ethyl                  methacrylate. The free radical bulk                  polymerization uses azo compound as initiator,                  which is added at 2.0x 10-5-4.8x 10-3 mols based                  on the total content of the polymerization                  monomers. The azo compound is                  azobisisobutyronitrile or azobisisoheptonitrile.                  The supercritical carbon dioxide oil displacement                  agent has cloud point pressure of 12 MPa and                  viscosity of 2.0 mPa.s.

PT P
PN CN109081936-A
TI Preparing fluoropolymer cell material involves placing fluoropolymer in closed container, raising temperature of closed container, introducing supercritical carbon dioxide into closed vessel, and maintaining for predetermined time T.
AU LIU F
   LIN H
   FAN D
   FAN Y
   ZHANG L
AE NINGBO LVSEFANGZHOU ENVIRONMENT TECHNOLO (NING-Non-standard)
GA 2019015854
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing fluoropolymer cell material involves placing the fluoropolymer in a closed container, raising the temperature of the closed container to 150-360 degrees C, introducing the supercritical carbon dioxide into the closed vessel to a pressure of 10-40 megapascal, and maintaining the pressure for a predetermined time T to uniformly distribute the supercritical carbon dioxide in the fluoropolymer, depressurizing the sealed container to foam supercritical carbon dioxide in the fluoropolymer to obtain a fluoropolymer cell material, where the pressure of the sealed container is greater than 8 megapascal/second.
   USE - Method for preparing fluoropolymer cell material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The fluoropolymer is polyvinylidene fluoride and/or polyperfluoroethylene propylene. The volume of the fluoropolymer is less than 30% of the volume of the closed vessel. The temperature of closed container is 180-360 degrees C. The pressure of introducing supercritical carbon dioxide into the closed container to the closed container reaches 25-40 megapascal. The predetermined time T is greater than or equal to 8 minutes. The pressure relief speed of the closed container is greater than 10 megapascal/second. The fluoropolymer cell material comprises multiple openings, where the opening has a pore size of 1 nm to 100 micrometer, and the fluoropolymer cell material has a density of 0.3-1.4 g/cm3.

TI Method for separating lignocellulose component, involves adding water to lignocellulosic material, subjecting to supercritical carbon dioxide blasting treatment and extracting liquid comprises hemicellulose and pectin.
AU FENG Y
   ZHANG M
   ZHONG H
AE UNIV SOUTH CHINA TECHNOLOGY (UYSC-C)
GA 201864226D
AB    NOVELTY - A lignocellulose component separating method involves adding water to the lignocellulosic material and mixing uniformly, the lignocellulosic material is subjected to supercritical carbon dioxide blasting treatment, after heating, introducing the carbon dioxide, pressurizing, maintaining and releasing the pressure after being balanced, adding water to the lignocellulosic material after supercritical carbon dioxide blasting treatment and extracting the extracting liquid, where the extracting liquid comprises hemicellulose and pectin, separating lignin and cellulose into the remaining materials by adding solvent extraction and extraction.
   USE - Method for separating lignocellulose component.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables separating lignocellulose component with low energy consumption and high processing efficiency.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The method further involves adding water to lignocellulosic material after the supercritical carbon dioxide blasting treatment, where the mass to volume ratio g/ml of the lignocellulosic material to the water is 1:20-1:50, heated to 80-140 degrees C and extraction 30-120 minutes, filtering the extract and remaining materials and adding ethanol to the extract for centrifuging and discarding the supernatant to obtain hemicellulose and pectin.

PT P
PN CN114561201-A
TI Preparing neutral blocking remover for operating            and repairing well, involves adding main agent raw            materials into reaction kettle, adding water, heating            and stirring mixture until the solid is completely            dissolved to obtain main agent mixed solution.
AU SHI X
   WANG G
   LI X
AE TIANJIN YICHUANG ENERGY TECHNOLOGY CO (TIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2022772174
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing neutral blocking remover involves                adding main agent raw materials into reaction                kettle, adding water into the reaction kettle,                heating and stirring the mixture until the solid is                completely dissolved to obtain a main agent mixed                solution. The treating agent is added into the main                agent mixed solution, heated and cooled to room                temperature to obtain a first mixed solution.                Corrosion inhibitor and oxidant are added into the                first mixed solution to obtain a second mixed                solution. High-density carbon dioxide gas is                introduced into the second mixed solution for                dispersion treatment to obtain the treatment fluid.                The cosolvent is added into the treatment fluid and                the pH of the solution is adjusted to 7 to obtain                the neutral blocking remover.
   USE - Method for preparing neutral blocking remover                for operating and repairing a well.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare neutral blocking                remover having high sulfur dissolving ability, can                removed large amount of dissolved sulfur, has low                flammability, is safe to use, and can efficiently                and deeply remove plugging, improve the efficiency                of plugging removal. The neutral blocking remover                does not cause corrosion to the oil casing and                lifting equipment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing neutral blocking remover involves:                i. weighing the main agent raw materials, adding                the main agent raw materials into a reaction                kettle, adding a proper amount of water into the                reaction kettle, heating and continuously heating                the mixture, and stirring the mixture until the                solid is completely dissolved to obtain a main                agent mixed solution; ii. weighing 3-5 pts. wt.                treating agent, adding the treating agent into the                main agent mixed solution, fully stirring the                treating agent, heating the treating agent to                dissolve the treating agent, and cooling the                solution to room temperature to obtain a first                mixed solution; iii. weighing 2-4 pts. wt.                corrosion inhibitor and 4-6 pts. wt. oxidant,                sequentially adding the corrosion inhibitor and the                oxidant into the first mixed solution, setting the                stirring speed and the stirring time to fully mix                the corrosion inhibitor and the oxidant to obtain a                second mixed solution, and simultaneously                introducing high-density carbon dioxide gas into                the second mixed solution for dispersion treatment                to obtain the treatment fluid; iv. weighing 7-9                pts. wt. cosolvent, and adding the cosolvent into                the treatment fluid for pressure maintaining and                heat preservation; v. stirring the mixture after                pressure maintaining and heat preservation to fully                mix the substances in the mixture, adjusting the pH                value of the solution to 7, and obtaining the                neutral blocking remover.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The main agent raw                  material is prepared from 13-16 pts. wt.                  polyepoxysuccinic acid sodium salt, 12-15 pts.                  wt. earth acid, 6-8 pts. wt. potassium chloride,                  9-12 pts. wt. glycerol, 11-15 pts. wt. glutamine                  and 8-12 pts. wt. sodium hydroxysuccinate.                  Preferred Components: The treating agent                  comprises a surfactant and a polymeric alcohol in                  weight ratio of 1:2, and the surfactant is a                  quaternary ammonium compound. The corrosion                  inhibitor is imidazoline. The oxidant is selected                  from potassium sulfate or sodium persulfate. The                  cosolvent is selected from 2-methoxyethanol,                  diethylene glycol monomethyl ether and ethyl                  lactate.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); H01 (Obtaining crude oil and natural gas - including exploration, drilling, well completion, production and treatment. TI Purification of biogas used as fuel, involves purifying by removing hydrogen sulfide, sulfur dioxide, and sulfur trioxide, which are sulfides in collected biogas generated from food waste, sewage sludge, and landfills by adding iron chelate solution containing water-soluble iron salt.
AU JEONG C
AE JEONG C (JEON-Individual)
GA 202271415T
AB    NOVELTY - Purification of biogas involves collecting biogas generated from food waste, sewage sludge, and landfills and performing primary purification by removing hydrogen sulfide, sulfur dioxide, and sulfur trioxide, which are sulfides in the collected biogas, by adding iron chelate solution and performing secondary purification by mixing a reaction agent with an amine-based enhancer to an absorbent obtained by mixing sodium hydroxide, a reaction continuation agent, and magnesium oxide, a reaction retardant, in an aqueous calcium hydroxide solution prepared by dissolving water in quicklime, which is calcium oxide, and biogas, and reacting with carbon dioxide contained in the biogas to produce calcium carbonate and purifying into high-purity methane gas. The iron chelate solution contains water-soluble iron salt of iron nitrate, iron chloride and iron sulfate, organic acid and its conjugate salt and pH stabilizer and other additives.
   USE - Purification of biogas used as fuel for cooking and transportation, automobiles, power generation facilities and boiler.
   ADVANTAGE - The method contributes to reduction of air pollutants and waste and greenhouse gas, which is the cause of global warming.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Purification of biogas involves collecting biogas generated from food waste, sewage sludge, and landfills and performing primary purification by removing hydrogen sulfide, sulfur dioxide, and sulfur trioxide, which are sulfides in the collected biogas, by adding iron chelate solution and performing secondary purification by mixing a reaction agent with an amine-based enhancer to an absorbent obtained by mixing sodium hydroxide, a reaction continuation agent, and magnesium oxide, a reaction retardant, in an aqueous calcium hydroxide solution prepared by dissolving water in quicklime, which is calcium oxide, and biogas, and reacting with carbon dioxide contained in the biogas to produce calcium carbonate and purifying into high-purity methane gas. The process of condensing the purified biogas is performed by storing the condensed purified biogas. In the primary purification process, nitrilotriacetate, cyclohexanediaminetetraacetate or diethylenetriaminepentaacetate. The iron chelate solution contains 0.0001-1.0 M water-soluble iron salt of iron nitrate, iron chloride and iron sulfate, 0.0001-2.4 M organic acid and its conjugate salt and 0.0001-2.0 M pH stabilizer and other additives. The pH stabilizer is made by mixing sodium monohydrogen phosphate, sodium dihydrogen phosphate, potassium monohydrogen phosphate and potassium dihydrogen phosphate. The organic acid is any one or more of acetic acid, lactic acid, tartaric acid, and malic acid. The reaction agent is a combination of 1.5-2.3 pts. wt. amine-based enhancer based on 100 pts. wt. of the absorbent, and the amine-based enhancer includes piperazine.
