PN CN114482807-A
TI Preparation of high temperature resistant and low            radiation coated glass by preparing glass, annealing,            cutting, putting to vacuum evaporation coating            equipment, coating both sides with silver-tin coating            layers, and stacking.
AU LIN J
AE TG CHANGJIANG GLASS CO LTD (TGCH-Non-standard)
GA 2022712963
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of high temperature resistant and                low radiation coated glass includes taking quartz                sand, soda ash, calcite, limestone, boron compound,                barium carbonate and lead oxide, crushing,                grinding, pouring into mixing equipment, stirring,                adding to glass melting furnace, heating, pouring                into forming mold, and placing the steel wire mesh                to obtain glass; taking out the glass, annealing,                cutting annealed glass into glass substrates,                putting to vacuum evaporation coating equipment,                coating both sides with silver-tin coating layers,                stacking two glass substrates, putting rubber                gaskets on the surrounding edges, putting to heat                sealing machine, and hot press sealing to form                glass main board; and extracting air in inner                compartment of the glass main board to form a                vacuum state, filling with carbon dioxide gas,                adhering both sides with first heat insulating film                and second heat insulating film, and wrapping                protective frame on the outside of the glass main                board.
   USE - The method is for preparation of high                temperature resistant and low radiation coated                glass
   ADVANTAGE - The high temperature resistant low-radiation                coated glass preparation solves the problem that                the existing glass has simple structure, poor                protective property, high temperature resistance                and low radiation resistance, which is not good for                the stable glass. The glass substrate has high                reflectivity to the infrared ray, and has good heat                insulation performance, increases the                radiation-proof performance and high temperature                performance of the glass main board, and the inner                part of the two-side glass substrate is provided                with a steel wire net, through the setting of the                steel wirenet, which can improve the structure                strength of glass substrate, when it is hit by                violent impact, it will not be penetrated.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of high temperature resistant and                low radiation coated glass comprises taking quartz                sand, soda ash, calcite, limestone, boron compound,                barium carbonate and lead oxide, adding to crusher                respectively, crushing, grinding into powder,                pouring into mixing equipment, stirring, adding to                glass melting furnace, heating at high temperature                to obtain glass liquid without bubbles, pouring                into forming mold, and placing the steel wire mesh                into the liquid glass to obtain glass; taking out                the glass, putting to annealing furnace, annealing,                cutting annealed glass into glass substrates,                putting to vacuum evaporation coating equipment,                coating both sides with silver-tin coating layers,                stacking two glass substrates, putting rubber                gaskets on the surrounding edges, putting to heat                sealing machine, and hot press sealing to form                glass main board; and extracting air in inner                compartment of the glass main board to form a                vacuum state, filling with carbon dioxide gas,                adhering both sides with first heat insulating film                and second heat insulating film, and wrapping                protective frame on the outside of the glass main                board.

PN CN114343012-A
TI Preparing non-ferrous deoxidizer for packaging nut            e.g. pecans, involves mixing organic matter main agent,            alkali metal carbonate, salt substance, water retaining            agent and gas adsorbent to prepare premix, spraying            catalyst aqueous solution until premix is uniformly            mixed, and packaging.
AU LIU J
AE JIANGSU OUKAI PACKAGING TECHNOLOGY CO (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202259469X
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation method of a non-ferrous                deoxidizer for nut packaging involves: (S1)                dissolving 0-5wt.% of a catalyst in 6-20 wt.% of                distilled water, and uniformly stirring to form a                catalyst aqueous solution; (S2) mixing 20-70 wt.%                of an organic matter main agent, 0-20 wt.% of an                alkali metal carbonate, 0-20 wt.% of a salt                substance, 5-18 wt.% of a water retaining agent and                0-5 wt.% of a gas adsorbent to prepare a premix,                and spraying the catalyst aqueous solution while                stirring the premix until the premix is uniformly                mixed to form a non-ferrous deoxidizer for nut                packaging; and (S3) packaging the nonferrous                deoxidizer into an active packaging packet by using                an oil-proof, anti-seepage and water-proof                packaging material with a special structure through                a packaging machine.
   USE - Method for preparing a non-ferrous deoxidizer                for packaging seasoning nuts with thicker shells                such as macadamia nuts and pecans.
   ADVANTAGE - The preparation method of a special                non-ferrous deoxidizer for nut packaging can meet                the capacity of effectively deoxidizing and                simultaneously releasing sufficient carbon dioxide                to keep the taste of nut, and can maintain the                appearance, quality and taste of canned nut food                through metal detection and prolong the shelf life                of the food. The preparation method adopts specific                salt substances to reduce the water activity value,                adopts the water retention agent with strong water                retention capacity to improve the water retention                property of the raw materials of the content and                inhibit the water loss. The applicability to nut                packages of different varieties and different                tastes is wide. The method can effectively remove                oxygen to reduce the oxygen concentration in the                package to 0%, has small influence on the                proportion of released carbon dioxide by external                factors, and is not easy to shrink the can.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a process flow chart for                preparing non-ferrous deoxidizer for nut packaging.                (Drawing includes non-English language                text).
   S1Dissolving a catalyst in distilled water,                and uniformly stirring to form a catalyst aqueous                solution
   S2Mixing an organic matter main agent, an                alkali metal carbonate, a salt substance, a water                retaining agent and a gas adsorbent to prepare a                premix, and spraying the catalyst aqueous solution                while stirring the premix until the premix is                uniformly mixed to form a non-ferrous deoxidizer                for nut packaging
   S3Packaging the nonferrous deoxidizer into an                active packaging packet by using an oil-proof,                anti-seepage and water-proof packaging material                with a special structure through a packaging                machine
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic matter                  main agent is ascorbate, polyvalent phenols or                  tannic acid.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The salt substance is                  sodium chloride, calcium chloride or sodium                  bromide. The alkali metal carbonate is sodium                  carbonate, sodium bicarbonate or potassium                  carbonate. The catalyst comprises ferrous ions                  and copper ions. The water-retaining agent                  comprises diatomite, white carbon black, modified                  starch or water-absorbent resin. The gas                  adsorbent comprises activated carbon, zeolite or                  molecular sieve.

PN IN202141056294-A
TI Composition used to enhance rumen fermentation and mitigate methane emission in dairy cattle by e.g. identifying critical nutrients by making volatile fatty acid, gas and microbial biomass and extrapolating effect of mixture supplementation.
AU BALAKRISHNAN V
   MURUGESWARI R
AE UNIV TAMILNADU VETERINARY & ANIMAL SCI (UYTA-Non-standard)
GA 2022107783
AB    NOVELTY - Composition to enhance rumen fermentation and mitigate methane emission in dairy cattle comprising: identifying critical nutrients by means of production of volatile fatty acid from basal diet; identifying critical nutrients by means of production of gas and microbial biomass from the basal diet, where the basal diet along with 0.8% critical nutrients glucose, 22.5% urea, 40 mg copper sulfate mixed with requires quantity of portable water is incubated 6 hours, thus reducing the total gas, methane production, butyric acid, acetate to propionate ratio and non glucogenic ratio and increasing carbon dioxide production, total volatile fatty acid, acetic acid, propionic acid, energetic efficiency, apparent dry matter digestibility, true dry matter digestibility and ruminal feed degrading support biomass production; and extrapolating the effect of critical nutrients mixture supplementation to the basal diet by means of rumen simulation technique (RUSITEC).
   USE - The composition is useful for enhancing rumen fermentation and mitigating methane emission in dairy cattle (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composition: promotes rumen fermentation by facilitating readily available critical nutrients at rumen; enhances nutrient uptake to facilitate increase in milk production; enhances digestibility of feeds and fodder consumed by low yielding lactating cattle; increases milk production by systematic scientific experiments conducted in vitro as well as in vivo; and utilizes the critical nutrients mixture of the glucose, urea, copper sulfate cobalt shows the significant improvements on increased the in vitro ruminal pH and ammonical nitrogen, the dry matter and nitrogen degradability, the true digestibility which increases the ruminal feed degrading support biomass production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Composition to enhance rumen fermentation and mitigate methane emission in dairy cattle comprising: identifying critical nutrients by means of production of volatile fatty acid from basal diet, where the basal diet is incubated for 6 hours along with 0.8% critical nutrients glucose, 22.5% urea, 40 mg copper sulfate and 3 mg cobalt sulfate; identifying critical nutrients by means of production of gas and microbial biomass from the basal diet, where the basal diet along with 0.8% critical nutrients glucose, 22.5% urea, 40 mg copper sulfate mixed with requires quantity of portable water is incubated 6 hours, thus reducing the total gas, methane production, butyric acid, acetate to propionate ratio and non glucogenic ratio and increasing carbon dioxide production, total volatile fatty acid, acetic acid, propionic acid, energetic efficiency, apparent dry matter digestibility, true dry matter digestibility and ruminal feed degrading support biomass production; and extrapolating the effect of critical nutrients mixture supplementation to the basal diet by means of rumen simulation technique (RUSITEC), where the basal diet is bajra and Napier variety of carbon tetroxide grass.
TF EXAMPLE - Identify the critical nutrients for effective rumen fermentation by the production of volatile fatty acid from basal diet. This study was based on Tilley and Terry (1963), where the basal diet was incubated along with critical nutrients e.g. glucose, urea, copper sulfate and cobalt sulfate to study the apparent digestibility of basal diet and volatile fatty acid production. The basal diet (bajra X Napier variety carbon tetroxide grass) of 0.5 g used in the experiment was ground to pass through 1 mu pore size. The rumen liquor for the experiment was collected from three grazing animals, pooled and strained through four-layer cheese cloth. The in vitro ruminal pH and apparent digestibility of the basal diet with the treatments and compared for the volatile fatty acid production. Identified critical nutrients mixture for rumen fermentation by the production of gas and microbial biomass from basal diet was based on in vitro gas production techniques, where the basal diet and the basal diet along with critical nutrients mixture (0.8% glucose, 22.5% urea, 40 mg copper sulfate and 3 mg cobalt sulfate mixed with requires quantity of portable water) were incubated in six sequential runs to study the apparent digestibility of basal diet and volatile fatty acid production. The basal diet (bajra X Napier variety carbon tetroxide grass) of 0.5 g used in the experiment was ground to pass through 1 mu pore size. The rumen liquor for the experiment was collected from three grazing animals, pooled and strained through four-layer cheese cloth. Respective control and its strategic supplementation to basal diet were evaluated for apparent, true dry matter digestibility, production of total volatile fatty acids, acetic acid, propionic acid, butyric acid, total gas, methane, carbon dioxide and ruminal feed degrading support biomass at 6 hours incubation. The 200 mg basal diet was transferred into 100 ml calibrated glass syringes and were subjected to in vitro gas production studies by adding 30 ml buffered rumen fluid. The glass syringes were then placed in a shaking incubator. The promising results that emerged in the in vitro trial were re-examined in semi continuous culture using rumen simulation technique (RUSITEC) to validate the batch culture results. This experiment was conducted to extrapolate the effect that critical nutrients supplementation has on basal diet. The study was carried out using the RUSITEC, where rumen liquor from cattle was used. The rumen liquor needed for the experiment was collected from three cattle maintained on grazing and pooling them. The pooled ruminal fluid was filtered through 4 layers of muslin cloth and stored in pre warmed container at 39 degrees C till its use. In vitro semi continuous culture in RUSITEC with six reaction vessels was initiated within half an hour from rumen fluid collection. Three reaction vessels were assigned to control and another three reaction vessels for treatment. The experiment was repeated twice to achieve six replications for each measurement. All the operations were carried out under continuous carbon-di-oxide flushing. About 500 ml of this strained rumen liquor was immediately transferred into each one of reaction vessel and 200 ml of artificial saliva that was prepared as per McDougall (1948) was also added. The solid inoculum of 80 g rumen cud was placed in a nylon bag. The feed was taken in another bag and both these bags were placed inside the perforated cage. The remaining space in the reaction vessel was filled with distilled water. On the subsequent day the solid content was removed and in its place a bag with feed was placed. The space between the lid and the liquor was 50-70 ml and this was filled with carbon dioxide through gas valve to maintain an anaerobic environment. About 800 ml of artificial saliva per 24 hours was infused through the inlet at the bottom of the reaction vessel. The dilution rate was adjusted at 0.55 ml/minutes. The apparatus was run maintaining under strict anaerobic condition over a period of 24 hours. After 24 hours i.e. at the end of the first day the solid inoculum was replaced by a new bag containing test material. On subsequent days those bags that have spent two days inside the reaction vessels were replaced by a new bag till the 10th day. The collection period was started from the 10th day. Accurately weighed quantity of 5.5 g basal diet was placed in nylon bags to ensure 5 g of substrate on dry matter basis. The bag was incubated for 6 and 12 hours in the reaction vessel of the RUSITEC. After removal, the incubated bags were washed twice with 40 ml of artificial saliva, the washed saliva was poured back into the appropriate reaction vessel. In order to ensure the culture of bacteria associated with solids in the reaction vessel, 3 bags were simultaneously incubated during the experimental period. Two bags were dedicated for culture maintenance by alternatively placing and rejecting bags after 48 hours of incubation. The third bag was incubated to study the rate of degradation at specified time intervals. The bags collected after the incubation periods were washed in a washing machine for 15 minutes, then they were dried in a hot air oven at 60 degrees C for 48 hours. The bags were cooled in desiccators and weighed. At the end of incubation periods in vitro ruminal pH, ammonia nitrogen, dry matter degradability, nitrogen degradability, potential ruminal feed degrading support biomass production, total volatile fatty acids, acetic, propionic, butyric acid, lactic acid, total gas, methane, carbon dioxide and carbon dioxide:methane ratio were determined. On the last 5 days (i.e. collection period), immediately before feeding, fermentation fluid was sampled directly from each vessel through a three way valve using a syringe and it was immediately determined for pH using a pH-meter. The ammonia nitrogen concentration was determined. Loss in weight of nylon bag after 6 hours of incubation in RUSITEC after washing and drying was recorded to calculate dry matter disappearance. The nitrogen content of test samples was estimated. The residual dry matter in the nylon bag was generally contaminated with significant amount of microbial nitrogen. This contaminated nitrogen was estimated by incubation of nitrogen free cellulosic materials in the nylon bag under similar condition and making appropriate correction prior to calculating effective nitrogen degradability.
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).); P31 (Diagnosis, surgery (A61B).)
MC D03-G; P31-A05
IP A23K-050/10; A23K-050/15; A23K-020/132; A61B-005/145; A23K-020/158

TI Nutritional inducing reagent for male and female hemp plant, supplementing glucose content to Chinese hemp plant, increasing plant chlorophyll content, improving activity of enzyme, and promoting penetration of carbon dioxide comprises triacontanol, gibberellin, silver thiosulfate, and glucose.
AU TIAN Y
   SUN K
   GAO Y
   ZHAO Y
   WANG X
   HAN C
   CAO K
   BIAN J
   SUN Y
   ZHANG X
AE HEILONGJIANG ACAD AGRIC SCI DAQING BRANC (HEIL-Non-standard)
GA 2021E8710F
AB    NOVELTY - Nutritional inducing reagent comprises 0-5 mg/l triacontanol, 0-10 mg/l gibberellin, 0.2-2 mmol/l silver thiosulfate, 0-100 g/l glucose, where silver thiosulfate is generated by the reaction of silver nitrate and sodium thiosulfate.
   USE - The reagent is useful for supplementing glucose content to Chinese hemp plant, increasing absorption and synthesis chlorophyll of phosphorus-potassium element of hemp, increasing plant chlorophyll content, improving activity of enzyme, and promoting penetration of carbon dioxide, and in the field of crop breeding.
   ADVANTAGE - The method ensures photosynthesis more vigorous; can increase plant stress resistance (cold resistance, drought resistance); can promote plant growth and propagation; and improves fruit yield and quality.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMs are also included for:
   (1) Preparing Nutritional inducing reagent comprising (i) taking the needed concentration of gibberellin and triacontanol, firstly dissolving in alcohol, diluting the volume to 100 ml, pouring the triacontanol solution into the gibberellin solution, and dissolving to 250 ml, (ii) taking needed concentration of glucose, glycine, alanine, diluting the volume to 50 ml, pouring the glycine, and alanine into glucose solution, dissolving to 250 ml, (iii) taking the needed concentration of silver nitrate and sodium thiosulfate in 250 ml distilled water, fully dissolving the two agents, pouring the silver nitrate solution into the sodium thiosulfate solution at a speed of 13-17 ml/minute, inverting one side, quickly stirring one surface to prepare the solution of silver thiosulfate, where the volume ratio of the two is 1:1, and (iv) pouring the solution of step (i) and (ii) in turn into the step (iii), and mixing uniformly.
   (2) Use of the nutritional inducing reagent comprising (a) spraying the main stem of the hemp plant and the young tissue of the side branch before the fast growth post or the bud of the hemp, taking the fully wet and uniform (not dripping water) as the standard, performing the second spraying treatment for 7 days, processing in the evening or in cloudy day, ensuring that the sun is not solarizing in 3-4 hours after processing, (b) processing the male plant for 30 days, removing the male plant of the bud, leaving the female plant, inducing male flower bud on the female hemp plant of 35-50 days, changing into male and female same plant, powdering the male flower, ensuring free pollination, obtaining the Chinese hemp seed of all-female, (c) planting the induced whole female seed, spraying the nutritional inducing reagent without removing the male plant to obtain a large amount of Chinese hemp seed of all female.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Method: The step (iv) comprises immediately spraying or storing nutritional inducing reagent in brown glass bottle or black plastic barrel. The step (a) comprises ensuring air relative humidity of not less than 70%. The method comprises cutting seedlings of female plants, exposing 20-70% of the female flower, using inducer for spraying main stem of hemp plant and side branch young tissue for spraying treatment, fully wetting uniformly, performing the second spraying treatment after 7 days, processing in the evening or in cloudy day during nutritional inducing reagent, ensuring that the sun is not solarizing in 3-4 hours after processing.

PN CN112246219-A
TI Recycling and reusing of backwashing powdered carbon used as biological carrier, involves injecting washed impurities, powdered charcoal and water into filter, separating impurities from charcoal, cleaning charcoal, and drying.
AU JIANG X
   GUO T
   LIU L
AE SHANDONG TAIYA ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021111612
AB    NOVELTY - Recycling and reusing of backwashing powdered carbon involves e.g. backwashing granular activated carbon after the granular activated carbon is used for advanced treatment in the sewage treatment plant for 12-24 hours, to wash away the trapped impurities on the filter layer, injecting the washed impurities, powdered charcoal and water into the filter, separating the impurities from the powdered charcoal by filtering distribution, cleaning the powdered charcoal from the filter screen and recovering, and placing the recovered powdered charcoal into the oven, drying the activated carbon after backwashing to remove the volatile components on the activated carbon and placing the activated carbon into the reactor, vacuumizing in the inside of oven, increasing the temperature of the reactor, and introducing gas such as carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide, hydrogen and water vapor into the reactor to clean the micropores of the activated carbon.
   USE - Recycling and reusing of backwashing powdered carbon used for biochemical aerobic tank of sewage treatment plant for adsorbing water pollutants in biochemical aerobic pool, and as biological carrier to form biological activity.
   ADVANTAGE - The method effectively increases the chemical oxygen demand removal rate of the biochemical aerobic tank, achieves the reuse of waste resources, and saves the biochemical operating cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Recycling and reusing of backwashing powdered carbon involves backwashing granular activated carbon after the granular activated carbon is used for advanced treatment in the sewage treatment plant for 12-24 hours, to wash away the trapped impurities on the filter layer for controlled rate of 3.8-4.2 l/m2.second and time of 10-30 minutes, injecting the washed impurities, powdered charcoal and water into the filter, separating the impurities from the powdered charcoal by filtering distribution, cleaning the powdered charcoal from the filter screen and recovering, and placing the recovered powdered charcoal into the oven, drying the activated carbon after backwashing to remove the volatile components on the activated carbon and placing the activated carbon into the reactor, vacuumizing in the inside of oven, increasing the temperature of the reactor to 800-900 degrees C, introducing gas such as carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide, hydrogen and water vapor into the reactor to clean the micropores of the activated carbon and restore the adsorption performance, recovering the powdered carbon generated in the reactor, and placing the dried powdered carbon into powdered carbon, so that the powdered carbon is recycled, pumping the recovered powdered carbon to the biochemical aerobic tank of the sewage treatment plant, and adsorbing water pollutants in the biochemical aerobic pool, and using as a biological carrier to form biological activity, which can increase the chemical oxygen demand removal rate of the biochemical aerobic pool by about 10%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The length of the backwash time is determined according to the turbidity of the backwash drainage, when the backwash turbidity is more, and the backwash time is longer. The heating time for activated carbon is 1-1.5 hours Preferred Components: The filter includes a filter box main component, in which on top side of the filter box component is connected with an injection pipe through it, a clamping assembly fixed on one side of the injection tube, and a filter assembly fixed inside one side of the filter box main component. The clamping assembly includes a lifting tube sleeved on the outside of one side of the injection tube, a sliding block symmetrically welded and fixed inside one side of the lifting tube, a sliding groove corresponding to the sliding block one side of the injection pipe, a spring welded and fixed to the groove surface at the bottom end of one side of the sliding groove, and the other end of the spring welded to the side of the slider, in which one side of the injection tube is symmetrically sleeved and fixed with a connecting plate, a spring (s2) symmetrically welded and fixed on the outside between the connecting plates, and limit plate symmetrically welded and fixed on one side of the connecting plates, and a guide groove provided on one side of the lifting pipe corresponding to the limiting plate. The filter assembly includes a fixed ring welded inside one side of the filter box main component, a sleeve symmetrically inlaid on one side of the fixing ring, and frame installed inside the filter box component located on both sides of the fixing ring, a connecting rod fixed to the plate surface between the frames by symmetric welding, in which one end of the connecting rod penetrates the inside of the sleeve, top end of the fixing ring is fixed with a spring (s3) symmetrically welded, and the other end of the third spring is welded to the side surface of the frame, a motor installed symmetrically on one side of the fixing ring, a cam installed on the bottom plate surface of the frame, in which one end of the output shaft of the motor is fixedly connected inside the cam. The frame is inlaid and connected with filter screen (S1) above the fixing ring. The frame is inlaid and connected with filter screen (S2) below the fixing ring. One side of the filter box main component is connected with a box door through a hinge rotation, and one side of the box door is welded and fixed with a door handle. In the filter box main component, water outlet pipe is connected through the bottom end side of one side of the filter box component, and a valve is installed inside one side of the water outlet pipe.

TI Improving oxidation resistance of iron-silicon alloy steel comprises e.g. reheating iron-silicon alloy steel slab, subjecting reheated slab to high-pressure water descaling, and adding zinc fluoroborate and sodium fluoride into solution.
AU GAO X
   WANG J
AE MAANSHAN XINLONG SPECIAL STEEL CO LTD (MAAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020A1967V
AB    NOVELTY - Improving oxidation resistance of iron-silicon alloy steel comprises e.g. (i) reheating iron-silicon alloy steel slab, heating at 1140-1180 degrees C, preheating, heating and soaking, feeding heating section with mixture of argon and carbon dioxide into the furnace, where flow rate is 220-230 ml/minutes, and the heating rate is 8-10 degrees C/minutes, and target temperature is 80-100 degrees C, reducing heating rate to 4-4.5 degrees C/minutes, heating to the target temperature, (ii) subjecting reheated slab to high-pressure water descaling, rough rolling, finishing rolling, cooling, bending straightening and performing heat treatment, placing slab in annealing furnace for heating, pouring nitrogen into annealing furnace, annealing in a nitrogen-containing atmosphere for 60-65 minutes, cooling, and then performing water cooling, and (iii) pickling heat-treated slab, adding zinc fluoroborate and sodium fluoride into the basic acid solution.
   USE - The method is useful for improving oxidation resistance of iron-silicon alloy steel.
   ADVANTAGE - The method inhibits formation speed of oxide layer and bonding strength with alloy steel matrix, and solves the problem of performance degradation caused by easy oxidation of iron-silicon alloy steel.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Improving oxidation resistance of iron-silicon alloy steel comprises (i) reheating iron-silicon alloy steel slab, heating at 1140-1180 degrees C, preheating, heating and soaking, where preheating section time is 80-85 minutes, heating rate is 6.5-7 degrees C/minutes, feeding heating section with mixture of argon and carbon dioxide into the furnace, where flow rate is 220-230 ml/minutes, and the heating rate is 8-10 degrees C/minutes, and target temperature is 80-100 degrees C, reducing heating rate to 4-4.5 degrees C/minutes, heating to the target temperature, and the holding time of the soaking section is 20-25 minutes, (ii) subjecting reheated slab to high-pressure water descaling, rough rolling, finishing rolling, cooling, bending straightening and performing heat treatment, placing slab in annealing furnace for heating, and quickly heating annealing furnace to between 610-620 degrees C at a rate of 20-22 degrees C/seconds, pouring nitrogen into annealing furnace at a flow rate of 290-300 ml/minutes, annealing in a nitrogen-containing atmosphere for 60-65 minutes, cooling quickly at a rate of 55-60 degrees C/seconds to 280-300 degrees C, and then performing water cooling, and (iii) pickling the heat-treated slab, adding zinc fluoroborate and sodium fluoride into the basic acid solution, where mass ratios are 0.52-0.55%, 0.23-0.26%, pickling temperature is 55-60 degrees C, and the pickling speed is 40-45 m/minutes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (iii): basic acid solution is composed of 35-40 pts. wt. hydrochloric acid having concentration of 185-190 g/l, 15-20 pts. wt. hydrofluoric acid having concentration of 40-45 g/l, 7-9 pts. wt. concentrated sulfuric acid having concentration of 110-112 g/l, 2-5 pts. wt. concentrated phosphoric acid having concentration of 74-78 g/l and 120-140 pts. wt. water.

TI Gas liquid absorption device useful for mixing,            infusing or carbonating beverage liquids e.g. coffee,            tea, milk, and industrial fluids with gas, comprises            gas inlet, liquid inlet, gas/liquid mixing foamer, and            mixing chamber.
AU PATEL A
   TRAN D
AE FLOW CONTROL LLC (FLOW-Non-standard)
   FLOW CONTROL LLC (FLOW-Non-standard)
   FLOW CONTROL LLC (FLOW-Non-standard)
GA 2020971475
AB    NOVELTY - Gas liquid absorption device (GLAD) comprises                a gas inlet manifold having a gas inlet configured                to receive and provide an inlet gas, and has a gas                foamer cavity formed in the device and coupled                fluidically to the gas inlet to receive the inlet                gas, a liquid inlet manifold having a liquid inlet                configured to receive and provide a non-infused                liquid, and has a liquid foamer cavity formed in                the device and coupled fluidically to the liquid                inlet to receive the non-infused liquid, a                gas/liquid mixing foamer configured between the gas                inlet manifold and the liquid inlet manifold and                arranged in the gas foamer cavity and the liquid                foamer cavity, and a mixing chamber configured to                receive the dispersed inlet gas and the dispersed                non-infused liquid, infuse the dispersed inlet gas                and the dispersed non-infused liquid, and provide a                foamed gas/liquid mixture from the mixing                chamber.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Gas liquid absorption device (GLAD) comprises                a gas inlet manifold having a gas inlet configured                to receive and provide an inlet gas, and has a gas                foamer cavity formed in the device and coupled                fluidically to the gas inlet to receive the inlet                gas, a liquid inlet manifold having a liquid inlet                configured to receive and provide a non-infused                liquid, and has a liquid foamer cavity formed in                the device and coupled fluidically to the liquid                inlet to receive the non-infused liquid, a                gas/liquid mixing foamer configured between the gas                inlet manifold and the liquid inlet manifold and                arranged in the gas foamer cavity and the liquid                foamer cavity, where the gas/liquid mixing foamer                has a gas foamer wall configured to form a gas                receiving foamer chamber i.e. fluidically coupled                to the gas foamer cavity, and the gas foamer wall                has gas provisioning holes formed to provide                dispersed inlet gas from the gas receiving foamer                chamber, and the gas/liquid mixing foamer has a                liquid foamer wall configured to form a liquid                receiving foamer chamber i.e. fluidically coupled                to the liquid foamer cavity, and the liquid foamer                wall has liquid provisioning holes formed to                provide dispersed non-infused liquid from the                liquid receiving foamer chamber, and a mixing                chamber configured to receive the dispersed inlet                gas and the dispersed non-infused liquid, infuse                the dispersed inlet gas and the dispersed                non-infused liquid, and provide a foamed gas/liquid                mixture from the mixing chamber.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Device: The mixing chamber is                  configured in at least part of the gas inlet                  manifold. The gas inlet manifold includes a gas                  manifold wall configured to form the mixing                  chamber. The foamer includes a center wall                  configured to separate fluidically the gas                  receiving foamer chamber and the liquid receiving                  foamer chamber. The GLAD comprises a gas O-ring                  configured between the gas foamer wall and the                  gas foamer cavity. The GLAD comprises a liquid                  O-ring configured between the liquid foamer wall                  and the liquid foamer cavity. The GLAD comprises                  a gas inlet fitting configured in the gas inlet                  to provide the inlet gas, where the gas inlet                  fitting is a quick-connect gas inlet fitting. The                  GLAD comprises a check valve assembly having an                  inlet check valve configured in the gas inlet, or                  having a foamer check valve configured in the gas                  receiving foamer chamber, or having both the                  inlet check valve configured in the gas inlet and                  the foamer check valve configured in the gas                  receiving foamer chamber. The GLAD comprises a                  gas pressure sensor configured in the gas inlet                  manifold to sense gas pressure of the inlet gas                  provided to the gas/liquid foamer. The GLAD                  comprises a liquid outlet fitting coupled to the                  gas inlet manifold and configured to provide the                  foamed gas/liquid mixture from the mixing                  chamber, where the liquid outlet fitting is a                  liquid outlet quick-fitting. The GLAD comprises a                  pressure relief valve coupled to the gas inlet                  manifold and configured to sense mixing chamber                  pressure of the foamed gas/liquid mixture in the                  mixing chamber. The GLAD comprises a liquid inlet                  fitting configured in the liquid inlet manifold                  to provide the inlet liquid, where the liquid                  inlet fitting is a liquid inlet quick-fitting.                  The GLAD comprises a liquid pressure sensor                  configured in the liquid inlet manifold to sense                  liquid pressure of the inlet liquid provided to                  the gas/liquid foamer. The gas provisioning holes                  include four gas provisioning holes. The four gas                  provisioning holes are configured in an                  equi-distant and symmetrical arrangement around                  the gas foamer wall. The liquid provisioning                  holes include eight liquid provisioning holes,                  where either liquid provisioning holes are                  configured in an equi-distant and symmetrical                  arrangement around the liquid foamer wall.

PN CN111519207-A; CN111519207-B
TI Preparation of tin electrode for electrochemical            reduction of carbon dioxide, involves treating carbon            felt or carbon paper as base layer, electrodepositing            tin on base layer with electrolyte containing stannous            sulfate, reducing and drying.
AU ZHONG H
   PAN L
   ZHANG J
AE UNIV DALIAN (UYDV-C)
GA 202078661M
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of tin electrode involves treating                carbon felt or carbon paper at 200-600 degrees C in                air, degreasing and cleaning with ethanol and                acetone to obtain gas diffusion layer or treated                carbon felt or carbon paper as base layer, mixing                0.01-0.5 mol/L stannous sulfate solution, 0.03-2.5                mol/L sulfuric acid and 0.05-0.3 mol/L, preferably                0.05-0.15 mol/L sulfonate-containing polymer to                form mixed solution as electrolyte solution where                molar ratio of stannous sulfate and sulfuric acid                is 3:2-1:8, electrodepositing tin on base layer                with electrolyte solution under the protection of                an inert atmosphere and at different constant                current densities, cleaning with ethanol and water                and drying to obtain electrodeposited tin catalyst                layer, subjecting base layer with electrodeposited                tin catalyst layer to reduction reaction in                0.05-0.8 mol/L post-treatment electrolyte at                voltage of -1.1 to -1.8 V for 30-700 seconds,                washing reduced electrodeposited tin catalyst layer                and drying in vacuum oven.
   USE - Preparation of tin electrode used as cathode                for electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method can uniformly graft the polymer                material on tin surface of catalyst, improves the                stability of the electrode and increases proton                concentration on electrode surface.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of tin electrode involves treating                carbon felt or carbon paper at 200-600 degrees C in                air, degreasing and cleaning with ethanol and                acetone to obtain gas diffusion layer or treated                carbon felt or carbon paper as base layer, mixing                0.01-0.5 mol/L stannous sulfate solution, 0.03-2.5                mol/L sulfuric acid and 0.05-0.3 mol/L, preferably                0.05-0.15 mol/L sulfonate-containing polymer to                form mixed solution as electrolyte solution where                mass ratio of tin and sulfonate-containing polymer                is 1:30-100:1 and the molar ratio of stannous                sulfate and sulfuric acid is 3:2-1:8, preferably                1:1-1:5, electrodepositing tin on base layer with                electrolyte solution under the protection of an                inert atmosphere and at different constant current                densities, cleaning with ethanol and water and                drying to obtain electrodeposited tin catalyst                layer, subjecting base layer with electrodeposited                tin catalyst layer to reduction reaction in                0.05-0.8 mol/L post-treatment electrolyte at                voltage of -1.1 to -1.8 V for 30-700 seconds,                washing reduced electrodeposited tin catalyst                layer, and drying in vacuum oven at 80-200 degrees                C to form electrode with sulfonate-containing                polymer functionalized tin catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of tin                  catalyst and sulfonate-containing polymer is                  1:30-100:1, preferably 1:2-30:1. The loading                  amount of tin catalyst in electrode is 0.1-8.0                  mg/cm2. Preferred Components: The post-treatment                  electrolyte is one of hydrochloric acid,                  phosphoric acid, sodium bicarbonate, sodium                  hydroxide, sodium chloride, potassium chloride,                  potassium bicarbonate and potassium hydroxide.                  The inert gas atmosphere is one or more of                  nitrogen, argon and helium gas.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Component: The                  sulfonate-containing polymer is one of                  perfluorosulfonic acid resin, sulfonated                  polysulfone resin, sulfonated polyphenylene                  sulfide resin, sulfonated polybenzimidazole,                  sulfonated polyphosphazene, sulfonated polyimide                  resin, sulfonated polystyrene resin, sulfonated                  polyether ether ketone resin and                  aminobenzenesulfonic acid.

PT P
PN CN111468733-A; CN111468733-B
TI Preparing powder metallurgy bushing useful for car VVT cover comprises pressing forming in the upper and lower female molds, heating, carburizing, grinding and post-engineering treatment to ensure the tolerance of the shaft sleeve size.
AU LU D
   DENG E
   BEI Q
   CHEN J
AE PORITE YANGZHOU TECHNOLOGY & IND CO LTD (PORI-Non-standard)
GA 2020745792
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing powder metallurgy bushing comprises (1) pressing forming in the upper and lower female molds, heating the negative film and demolding blank body, combining the upper punch with the upper mandrel the punch ensures the stability of the R-arc working surface and the axial dimension of the shaft sleeve without adjustment and ensuring female mold the bearing press-fitting and guiding, (2) placing the blank body in a high temperature environment with RX atmosphere for sintering and controlling the carbon dioxide, (3) heating the carburizing furnace and carburizing the workpiece, (4) placing the heat-treated workpiece into the vibration grinding machine, adding grinding stones and grinding to remove the burrs on the surface of the workpiece, (5) using centerless grinding technology to finish the inner and outer surfaces of the workpiece and (6) post-engineering treatment to ensure the tolerance of the shaft sleeve size and outer diameter.
   USE - The method is useful for car VVT cover (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: satisfies the needs of the lock pin; has good product performance parameters; and reduces the cost of press-fitting steel parts for the lock pin hole.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing powder metallurgy bushing comprises (1) pressing forming in the upper and lower female molds, heating the negative film to 120 degrees C and demolding blank body, combining the upper punch with the upper mandrel the punch ensures the stability of the R-arc working surface and the axial dimension of the shaft sleeve without adjustment and ensuring female mold the bearing press-fitting and guiding, (2) placing the blank body in a high temperature environment with RX atmosphere for sintering at 1130 plus minus 10 degrees C, sintering speed is 100 mm/minutes and controlling the carbon dioxide at 0.3-0.5 vol.% during the sintering, (3) heating the carburizing furnace to 850 plus minus 10 degrees C for 120 minutes and carburizing the workpiece, where the carbon potential of the carburizing treatment 1.0 plus minus 0.05%, carry out oil quenching at 850 degrees C to ensure that the depth of the quenched hardened layer is 0.5 mm, surface micro-hardness HV0.1650-800 and surface macro-hardening HRC42, (4) placing the heat-treated workpiece into the vibration grinding machine, adding grinding stones and grinding to remove the burrs on the surface of the workpiece, (5) using centerless grinding technology to finish the inner and outer surfaces of the workpiece and (6) post-engineering treatment to ensure the tolerance of the shaft sleeve size and outer diameter.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The powder metallurgy components of the shaft sleeve are 0.2-0.4 wt.% carbon, 1.3-1.7 wt.% copper, 1.5-2.0 wt.% nickel, 0.4-0.6 wt.% molybdenum, 0.5-0.7 wt.% lubricant and remaining iron powder.

PT P
PN CN111448962-A
TI Breeding virus-free potato minitubers by cutting selected potato seedlings, cutting below axillary buds into seedbed having induction culture medium, incubating, cultivating, spraying nutrient solution, planting seedlings, and harvesting.
AU HU B
   HU R
   SU C
AE YUNNAN BENGLONG POTATO PLANTING CO LTD (YUNN-Non-standard)
GA 202072842L
AB    NOVELTY - Breeding virus-free potato minitubers, involves (a) selecting virus-free sterile potato seedlings by enzyme-linked immunoassay, cutting the seedlings, and cutting the portion below the axillary buds into a seedbed containing induction culture medium, (b) cutting the cuttings, watering the seedbed before cutting, incubating, stopping watering during the slow seedling period, shading, and controlling the temperature, maintaining the relative humidity, and cultivating, (c) cultivating, spraying nutrient solution to keep the substrate moist, and controlling the substrate humidity, (d) planting the detoxified seedlings, applying carbon dioxide gas fertilizer, and stopping using before ventilation, and (e) harvesting after flowering or when the lower leaves of the plant reach one-third of the leaves turn yellow, controlling the growth period, placing in a ventilated and shady place to dry, after harvesting, adding to a seed bag, affixing label, and storing.
   USE - The method is useful for breeding virus-free potato minitubers.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has short period and low production cost, and improves the yield and quality of the minitubers.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Breeding virus-free potato minitubers, involves (a) selecting virus-free sterile potato seedlings by enzyme-linked immunoassay, cutting the seedlings into 1-2 cm stem segments with 1-3 axillary buds, and cutting the portion below the axillary buds into a seedbed containing induction culture medium, (b) cutting the cuttings according to the spacing of 5x 7 cm, watering the seedbed before cutting, incubating for 20-26 hours in dark, stopping watering during the slow seedling period, shading for 5-10 days after cutting, and controlling the temperature to 18-21 degrees C, maintaining the relative humidity at 95-100%, and cultivating under a light intensity of 1800-2200 lux for 12-16 hours per day, (c) cultivating for 7-20 days, spraying 0.2-0.3% nutrient solution for 4-6 times according to the seedling conditions to keep the substrate moist during the growth of the new seedlings, with a water holding capacity of 60-70%, and controlling the substrate humidity to 75-80% at 17-25 degrees C, when the stolon begun to expand, (d) slowly planting the detoxified seedlings, applying 250-330 parts per million carbon dioxide gas fertilizer, when the leaves grow to close to adult size, 3 weeks after planting, and stopping using before ventilation, and (e) harvesting 20 days after flowering or when the lower leaves of the plant reach one-third of the leaves turn yellow, controlling the growth period within 70-80 days, placing in a ventilated and shady place to dry for 7-10 days, after harvesting, adding to a seed bag, affixing label, and storing at a low temperature of 2-4 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Composition: The induction medium comprises 80-90 pts. wt. cassava residue, 0.2-0.4 pt. wt. potassium fulvic acid, 1.2-2.5 pts. wt. microbial agents, 0.1-0.3 pt. wt. indole butyric acid, 5-20 pts. wt. perlite, 0.3-0.5 pt. wt. Bacillus thuringiensis rooting powder, 0.1-0.2 pt. wt. naphthalene acetic acid and 5-15 pts. wt. vermiculite. Preferred Components: The sterile potato seedlings are free of potato virus Y, potato leaf roll virus, potato virus X, potato S virus and/or potato late blight virus in the test tube vaccine. Preferred Conditions: In step (a), the cutting depth of the stem segment is that one section is inserted into the inside of the substrate, and one section is exposed outside the substrate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The nutrient solution comprises 1-3 %mass calcium nitrate, 3-5 %mass potassium nitrate, 1-3 %mass magnesium sulfate, 2-5 %mass urea, 1-3 %mass potassium dihydrogen phosphate, 1-3 %mass ammonium sulfate, 1-3 %mass calcium chloride, 0.2-0.5 %mass disodium edetate, 0.5-1 %mass copper sulfate, 0.05-0.1 %mass salicylic acid and remainder of distilled water.

PN CN111420638-A; CN111420638-B
TI Magnetic nanocomposite material used for forming magnetic effervescent tablet, comprises core comprising nickel ferrite magnetic nanoparticles, and outer composite which forms covalent organic framework polymer.
AU LI Y
   LI J
   DONG G
   WANG X
   LIU W
   WANG H
   GAO M
   TAN C
AE UNIV WENZHOU MEDICAL (WEZN-C)
GA 202070320J
AB    NOVELTY - A nickel ferrite-covalent organic framework magnetic nanocomposite material comprises a core comprising nickel ferrite magnetic nanoparticles, and an outer composite which forms a covalent organic framework polymer.
   USE - Magnetic nanocomposite material is used for forming magnetic effervescent tablet for performing magnetic effervescent enhanced micro-extraction in detection of endocrine disruptors in tap water, bottled water, river water, human urine sample and human serum (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The magnetic nanocomposite material can be prepared under mild reaction conditions using economical and easy-to-use catalyst. The magnetic effervescent tablet formed using the magnetic nanocomposite material enables magnetic effervescent enhanced micro-extraction with high extraction recovery rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) magnetic effervescent tablet, which is formed by mixing the magnetic nanocomposite material and an effervescent precursor, and compressing;
   (2) preparation of the nickel ferrite-covalent organic framework magnetic nanocomposite material, which involves preparing (i) nickel ferrite magnetic nanoparticles, and adding (ii) the nickel ferrite magnetic nanoparticles, 1,3,5-tris(4-aminophenyl)benzene and terephthaldehyde in a mass ratio of 3:2:1, dissolving in 30-60 mL dimethyl sulfoxide, ultrasonically-processing for 5 minutes, adding 1-5 mL acetic acid as a catalyst, heat-preserving at constant temperature for 0.5-3 hours, separating the obtained yellow-green solid with a magnet, sequentially washing three times with tetrahydrofuran and methanol, vacuum-drying the solid, cooling, and grinding after reaching room temperature;
   (3) magnetic effervescent enhanced micro-extraction method, which involves using the magnetic effervescent tablet to replace effervescent tablet of usual reaction-enhanced micro-extraction method. The magnetic nanocomposite material is used as an independent adsorbent. The covalent organic framework outer layer of the magnetic nanocomposite material provides larger surface area and more active sites; and
   (4) detection of endocrine disruptors in tap water, bottled water, river water, human urine sample and human serum, which involves placing 5 mL sample in a water bath and heating at 30 degrees C to obtain water sample, placing the magnetic effervescent tablet into a test tube, continuously rising carbon dioxide gas produced by acid-base reaction from the bottom to top, reacting within 3 minutes, uniformly dispersing the magnetic nanocomposite material in water sample using carbon dioxide gas produced during the reaction, using the magnetic nanocomposite material to extract six endocrine disruptors, applying a magnetic field to recover the magnetic nanocomposite material that has absorbed endocrine disruptors, using the sample using 100 mu l acetonitrile, separating the magnetic nanocomposite material and acetonitrile eluent comprising the sample with a magnet, filtering with 0.22 mu m filter membrane, finally drying using nitrogen, and placing in a high-performance liquid chromatography-fluorescence detector system for injection.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of the effervescent precursor with respect to magnetic nanocomposite material is 452:5-15. Preferred Process: The nickel ferrite magnetic nanoparticles are prepared by dissolving a certain amount of iron(III) chloride hexahydrate and nickel(II) chloride hexahydrate in a ratio of 1:1-10 in 30-60 mL water, magnetically stirring for 10-25 minutes to completely dissolve, transferring the mixed solution into a high-temperature and high-pressure stainless steel reactor with polytetrachloroethylene liner, reacting at 160-180 degrees C for 9-16 hours, placing the reaction product in a constant-temperature blast drying box, cooling to room temperature, collecting the obtained solids with a magnet, washing three times with ultrapure water and ethanol, vacuum-drying, cooling to room temperature, and grinding.

PN CN111349224-A; CN111349224-B
TI Producing polycarbonate-polyether polyol involves pumping raw materials from the inlet of the pipelined reactor, where the piping reactor includes a heating section group and a cooling section group.
AU MAO H
   WANG H
   WANG Z
AE HANGZHOU PULI MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HANG-Non-standard)
GA 2020638648
AB    NOVELTY - Producing polycarbonate-polyether polyol involves pumping raw materials from the inlet of the pipelined reactor, where the piping reactor includes a heating section group and a cooling section group, placing heating section group at the inlet end of the piping reactor, and placing cooling section group at the outlet end of the piping reactor. The raw materials include initiator, epoxy compound, catalyst and carbon dioxide are contacted in the pipelined reactor to form a copolymerization reaction system in presence of the catalyst. The polymerization reaction is carried out, and a polymerization reaction product stream containing polycarbonate-polyether polyol and intermediate products is obtained. The polymerization reaction product stream is flowed through the cooling section group to separate portion of the polycarbonate-polyether polyol.
   USE - Method for producing polycarbonate-polyether polyol.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing polycarbonate-polyether polyol involves pumping raw materials from the inlet of the pipelined reactor, where the piping reactor includes a heating section group and a cooling section group, placing heating section group at the inlet end of the piping reactor, and placing cooling section group at the outlet end of the piping reactor. The raw materials include initiator, epoxy compound, catalyst and carbon dioxide are contacted in the pipelined reactor to form a copolymerization reaction system in presence of the catalyst. The polymerization reaction is carried out, and a polymerization reaction product stream containing polycarbonate-polyether polyol and intermediate products is obtained. The polymerization reaction product stream is flowed through the cooling section group to separate portion of the polycarbonate-polyether polyol. The polycarbonate-polyether polyol product stream is formed, the remaining polymerization reaction product stream continues to undergo polymerization reaction in the pipelined reactor, or is recycled. The catalyst is a zinc-cobalt double metal cyanide complex catalyst obtained by reacting water-soluble metal salts of zinc and cobalt in a water-soluble solvent. The catalyst is modified by mixed acid during synthesis. The mixed acid comprises organic acid and water-soluble inorganic acid, and the ratio of the water-soluble inorganic acid to the organic acid is 1:10-10:1. The water-soluble inorganic acid is selected from Dilute sulfuric acid, dilute hydrochloric acid, and the pH value is between 0-5. The dilute sulfuric acid refers to an aqueous solution of H2SO4, which can be diluted with concentrated sulfuric acid and deionized water to obtain a pH value between 0-5. The dilute hydrochloric acid refers to an aqueous solution of HCl, which is diluted by adding deionized water with concentrated hydrochloric acid to obtain a pH value between 0 and 5. The organic acid is selected from succinic acid, glutaric acid, phthalic acid, iminodiacetic acid, pyromellitic acid and butanetetracarboxylic acid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The raw materials premixed in the premix tank further include carbon dioxide, and the raw materials are mixed in a static mixer and then pumped into the pipelined reactor. The initiator is selected from ethylene glycol, diethylene glycol, 1,2-propanediol, 1,3-propanediol, 1,4-butanediol, 1,5-pentanediol, 1,6-hexanediol , 1,4-cyclohexanedimethanol, neopentyl glycol, glycerin, trimethylolpropane, trimethylolethane, 1,2,4-butanetriol, 1,2,6-hexanetriol, pentaerythritol, dipentaerythritol, succinic acid, glutaric acid, adipic acid, pimelic acid, suberic acid, azelaic acid, decane Diacid, dodecanedioic acid, Terephthalic acid, isophthalic acid, phthalic acid, trimesic acid, pyromellitic acid, catechol, resorcinol, and hydroquinone. The epoxide is selected from ethylene oxide, propylene oxide, butylene oxide and epichlorohydrin. Preferred Conditions: The raw materials are mixed into a reaction liquid in a pipelined reactor, and carbon dioxide is added to the heating section group of the pipelined reactor to make the pipelined reactor. The pressure of the reactor is 1-20 mega pascal.
DC A23 (Polyamides; polyesters. (including polycarbonates, polyesteramides); alkyds; other unsaturated polymers.); A25 (Polyurethanes; polyethers.)

PT P
PN US2020165128-A1; FR3088925-A1; EP3659966-A1; CN111217331-A; EP3659966-B1; FR3088925-B1
TI Producing hydrogen from light hydrocarbon source            involves generating synthesis gas by steam reforming of            light gaseous hydrocarbons, desulfurizing hydrocarbons            for reforming, steam-reforming desulfurized            hydrocarbons in tubular reactors.
AU BASIN M
   GARY D
   TADIELLO J
AE AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
GA 202045491P
AB    NOVELTY - Producing hydrogen from light hydrocarbon                source involves generating a synthesis gas by steam                reforming of light gaseous hydrocarbons,                desulfurizing hydrocarbons for reforming,                steam-reforming the desulfurized hydrocarbons in                tubular reactors installed in a furnace of a                reformer heated by burners which are fed with fuel                by at least a secondary fuel gas. The hydrogen is                produced from the synthesis gas generated. The                synthesis gas is enriched by conversion of carbon                monoxide according to the exothermic conversion                reaction. The synthesis gas is purified in a                pressure swing adsorption unit to give pure                hydrogen product and a residual gas mixture                containing methane, carbon monoxide, hydrogen and                carbon dioxide. The secondary fuel gas comprises                some of the residual gas mixture produced in the                hydrogen production and a primary fuel gas.
   USE - Method for producing hydrogen from light                hydrocarbon source.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to produce hydrogen from                light hydrocarbon source that reduces the                consumption of fuel.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing hydrogen from light hydrocarbon                source involves generating a synthesis gas by steam                reforming of light gaseous hydrocarbons,                desulfurizing hydrocarbons for reforming,                steam-reforming the desulfurized hydrocarbons in                tubular reactors installed in a furnace of a                reformer heated by burners which are fed with fuel                by at least a secondary fuel gas. The hydrogen is                produced from the synthesis gas generated. The                synthesis gas is enriched by conversion of carbon                monoxide according to the exothermic conversion                reaction. The synthesis gas is purified in a                pressure swing adsorption unit to give pure                hydrogen product and a residual gas mixture                containing methane, carbon monoxide, hydrogen and                carbon dioxide. The secondary fuel gas comprises                some of the residual gas mixture produced in the                hydrogen production and a primary fuel gas. The                conversion reaction is carried out in a cooled                conversion reactor in which some of the heat                produced is transferred by indirect heat exchange                in the reactor with a first gaseous fluid so as to                preheat the first gaseous fluid before the first                gas fluid is used in the synthesis gas generation,                and in that the hydrogen-enriched synthesis gas                leaving the conversion reactor is cooled by                indirect heat exchange with a second gaseous fluid                before the second gaseous fluid is used as the                secondary fuel gas. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is                included for a plant suitable for implementing the                process, which comprises a cooled conversion                reactor, where cooled conversion reactor is                configured to admit the first fluid for preheating                before the first fluid is used, cooled conversion                reactor comprises reactor-internal units for                circulating first fluid for preheating and for heat                exchange with the synthesis gas for cooling, and                units for exiting of preheated first fluid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The first gaseous fluid                  for preheating is the residual gas mixture                  feeding the burners of the reformer, and the                  second gaseous fluid for preheating comprises the                  light hydrocarbons for reforming. The cooled                  conversion reactor is configured to admit the                  first fluid for preheating before the first fluid                  is used, where the cooled conversion reactor                  comprises reactor-internal unit for circulating                  first fluid for preheating and for heat exchange                  with the synthesis gas for cooling, and unit for                  exit of preheated first fluid. The cooled                  conversion reactor is a plate-corrugated sheet                  reactor. The cooled conversion reactor is a                  shell-and-tube reactor and is adapted for                  circulating the synthesis gas in catalyst-filled                  tubes and for circulating first fluid for                  preheating in the shell.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).)

PN CN110934352-A
TI Preparing children's yoga wear fabrics involves adding lime emulsion to super gravity reactor, adding soluble starch, stirring well under constant temperature conditions, filtering and drying calcium carbonate slurry obtained by reaction.
AU SHEN S
AE HUZHOU RUIGEQI CLOTHING CO LTD (HUZH-Non-standard)
GA 2020265016
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing children's yoga wear fabrics involves adding 1-3 wt.% lime emulsion to the super gravity reactor at 2-5 degrees C, adding soluble starch, stirring well under constant temperature conditions, introducing carbon dioxide-containing gas with a volume content of 6-12 wt.%, until the pH value of the slurry is 6.5-7.0, filtering and drying the calcium carbonate slurry obtained by the reaction to obtain spherical calcium carbonate, and performing wet grinding the pearl powder to grind the particle size below 200 microns. The dispersant and obtained pearl powder are added and stirred well, performed ball milling to make a slurry to make the particle size of the powder reach 200-500nm. The bamboo pulp is added to the slurry and dilute it to a concentration of 15-25 wt.%. The filtered slurry is added into viscose fiber spinning slurry and spherical calcium carbonate according to the formula amount, and then mixed, filtered, spin, drawn and shaped into pearl fiber.
   USE - Method for preparing children's yoga wear fabrics.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare children's yoga wear fabrics is significantly reduces added amount of pearl powder, helps to improve its anti-UV performance, it is also conducive to improving the emissivity and uniformity of far infrared emission, and also improves the breathability and dyeing performance of the fabric. It is more environmentally friendly and healthy, and more suitable for children to wear.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing children's yoga wear fabrics involves adding 1-3 wt.% lime emulsion to the supergravity reactor at 2-5 degrees C, adding soluble starch, stirring well under constant temperature conditions, introducing carbon dioxide-containing gas with a volume content of 6-12 wt.%, until the pH value of the slurry is 6.5-7.0, filtering and drying the calcium carbonate slurry obtained by the reaction to obtain spherical calcium carbonate, and performing wet grinding the pearl powder to grind the particle size below 200 microns. The dispersant and obtained pearl powder are added and stirred well, performed ball milling to make a slurry to make the particle size of the powder reach 200-500nm. The bamboo pulp is added to the slurry and dilute it to a concentration of 15-25 wt.%. The filtered slurry is added into viscose fiber spinning slurry and spherical calcium carbonate according to the formula amount, and then mixed, filtered, spin, drawn and shaped into pearl fiber. The pearl fiber and nylon fiber are blended and dyed with natural plant dyes to obtain high-grade yoga clothing fabrics.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The nylon fiber is a profiled ultra-fine nylon fiber. Preferred Compositions: The mass ratio of the pearl fiber and the nylon fiber is (8-15):1. The spherical calcium carbonate has a particle size of 500-1200 nm. The pearl fiber includes 2-5 wt.% spherical calcium carbonate, 0.2-0.5 wt.% bamboo pulp, 0.5-0.8 wt.% dispersant, 0.5-1 wt.% pearl powder and balance is viscose fiber.

PN CN110877895-A
TI Integrated process of conversion of coal to            hydrogen and sulfur-tolerant methanation in refinery            includes entering gasified crude synthesis gas into            sulfur-resistant shift methanation unit with reactor,            and performing partial methanation.
AU HU L
   PANG R
   ZHANG W
   GUO W
   LI Z
   LIU J
   WANG L
AE SINOPEC NINGBO ENG CO LTD (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC NINGBO TECHNOLOGY INST CO LTD (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC ENG GROUP CO LTD (SNPC-C)
GA 2020222116
AB    NOVELTY - Integrated process of conversion of coal to                hydrogen and sulfur-tolerant methanation in                refinery includes entering gasified crude synthesis                gas into sulfur-resistant shift methanation unit to                undergo shift reaction, while performing partial                methanation, cooling reaction gas, entering acid                gas removal unit to remove hydrogen sulfide and                carbon dioxide, sending purified gas to Pressure                Swing Adsorption (PSA) unit to separate high-purity                hydrogen for export, pressurizing high-calorie PSA                exhaust gas and sending to fuel gas pipe                network.
   USE - The method is for integrated process of                conversion of coal to hydrogen and sulfur-tolerant                methanation in refinery.
   ADVANTAGE - The method adopts sulfur-resistant methanation                reactor that can meet methanation reaction under                different loads, and effectively avoid risk of                over-temperature of bed under low load. It                eliminates circulation compressor, reduces                equipment investment and has improved energy                recovery rate. It makes synthesis gas cascade pass                through sulfur-tolerant methanation reactor in                order, which simplifies process and system control,                and is conducive to achieving better economic                benefits. The pressure of hydrogen pipeline network                and secondary circuit of sulfur-resistant                methanation reactor are controlled in chain, and                opening degree of secondary circuit is adjusted                online by pressure to realize stable operation of                hydrogen pipeline network, thus ensuring long-term                stable operation of oil refining device. The                fluctuation of load of gasification unit due to                fluctuation of hydrogen pipeline network is                eliminated, which is beneficial to maintaining                long-term stable operation of gasification                unit.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Integrated process of conversion of coal to                hydrogen and sulfur-tolerant methanation in                refinery includes entering gasified crude synthesis                gas into sulfur-resistant shift methanation unit to                undergo shift reaction, while performing partial                methanation, cooling reaction gas, entering acid                gas removal unit to remove hydrogen sulfide and                carbon dioxide, sending purified gas to PSA unit to                separate high-purity hydrogen for export,                pressurizing high-calorie PSA exhaust gas and                sending to fuel gas pipe network. The                sulfur-resistant shift methanation unit is operated                by sending crude synthesis gas to detoxification                tank after being heated by preheater, entering                desulfurized crude synthesis gas to first                sulfur-resistant conversion furnace, reacting,                dividing high-temperature gas into two streams                after heat is recovered by high-pressure steam                generator, such that one enters sulfur-resistant                methanation reactor and reacted gas is mixed with                another unreacted gas, replenishing boiler water,                reducing temperature and humidifying gas into                second sulfur-resistant conversion furnace,                reacting, replenishing high-temperature gas into                boiler water after heat exchange, sending to third                sulfur-resistant conversion furnace, recovering                reacted gas by waste heat, cooling and sending to                acid gas removal unit.

Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. PN CN110628539-A
TI Preparing malt of caramel and sweet comprises e.g. choosing barley, using a sorter to remove straw, wheat husk, dust, sieving, removing small grains, flushing with compressed air and incubating and controlling fresh air temperature.
AU LUO F
   YANG Z
   HAN Y
   ZHANG L
   SUN W
AE GDH YONGSHUNTAI GUANGZHOU MALT CO LTD (GDHY-Non-standard)
GA 2020025587
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing malt of caramel and sweet comprises e.g. choosing barley, and using a sorter to remove the straw, wheat husk and dust, and sieving with 2.5 mm graded sieve, and removing small grains, filling clean water for cleaning, simultaneously continuously filling compressed air washing, dipping water and simultaneously starting the exhaust fan, and the generating carbon dioxide (CO2) kernel by breathing, controlling fresh air temperature, passing in water, mixing with food-grade sodium hydroxide with a weight of wheat, and continuing to introduce compressed air, draining the immersed wheat water, turning on the exhaust fan at the same time, and drawing in the CO2 produced by the respiration of the wheat kernels, cleaning water and continuously flushing with compressed air and incubating, draining the soaked wheat water, turning on the exhaust fan at the same time, drawing in the CO2 produced by the respiration of the wheat kernels, and controlling fresh air temperature.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing malt of caramel and sweet.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: produces malt, which has both flavor of burnt malt and crystal malt, harmonious in flavor and mouth feel, and the sense of hierarchy is more obvious.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing special malt of caramel and sweet comprises (i) choosing barley, and using a sorter to remove the straw, wheat husk and dust, and sieving with a 2.5 mm graded sieve, and removing small grains; (ii) filling 18 degrees C clean water for cleaning, simultaneously continuously filling compressed air washing, and maintaining for 5 hours; (iii) dipping water and simultaneously starting the exhaust fan, and the generating carbon dioxide (CO2) kernel by breathing, controlling fresh air temperature at 18 degrees C for 9 hours; (iv) passing in 18 degrees C water, mixing with food-grade sodium hydroxide with a weight of 0.5% wheat, and continuing to introduce compressed air and maintaining for 3 hours; (v) draining the immersed wheat water, turning on the exhaust fan at the same time, and drawing in the CO2 produced by the respiration of the wheat kernels; (vi) cleaning water at 18 degrees C and continuously flushing with compressed air and incubating for 3 hours; (vii) draining the soaked wheat water, turning on the exhaust fan at the same time, drawing in the CO2 produced by the respiration of the wheat kernels, and controlling the fresh air temperature at 18 degrees C; (viii) performing germination process; (ix) performing drying and moisture-removing process; (x) preheating quartz sand and saccharification and uniform heating of malt; and (xi) performing roasting and heat preservation process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: In the step (viii), the germination process includes: 0-24 hours, germination temperature is 18 degrees C, fresh air; 24-48 hours, germination temperature is 18 degrees C, fresh air; 48-72 hours, germination temperature is 20 degrees C, fresh air return air ratio is 7:3; 72-84 hours, germination temperature is 20 degrees C, fresh air return ratio is 6:4 and total germination time is 84 hours. In the step (ix), the drying and exhausting process includes: drying is performed by low temperature and high wind speed to discharge moisture, where the fan is fully on, the inlet air temperature is 50 degrees C, the drying time is 8-10 hours, when the water content of malt drops to 25-30%, and transferred to the roasting furnace. In the step (x), the weight ratio of the quartz sand to the malt is 3:1, and the temperature of the quartz sand is 100 degrees C. In the step (xi), the roasting and heat preservation is carried out by: after mixing, controlling wheat temperature to 60-70 degrees C, where the closed roasting oven is kept for 15 minutes, exhaust the lid, slowly increasing the temperature to 130 degrees C at a rate of 1 degrees C/minute, uniformly spraying the malt to 10 wt.% water, incubating at 130 degrees C for 30 minutes, discharging cold air blowing and cooling.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)
