PT P
PN CN110354832-A
TI Regenerating activated carbon comprises dispersing            the waste activated carbon, heating, volatilizing,            filling inert gas, performing carbonization, refluxing,            filtering, washing to neutral, drying, air-cooling            charcoal and post-treating.
AU HU Z
   CHEN H
AE NANJING ANJIETE ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (NANJ-Non-standard)
GA 201990017B
AB    NOVELTY - Regenerating activated carbon comprises (i)                dispersing the waste activated carbon with                sufficient microporous adsorption and exposing in                the sunlight; (ii) washing the waste activated                carbon with pure water to ensure the cleaning of                visible impurities and soluble micro-impurities and                drying; (iii) heating the washed wet carbon to                evaporate the water adsorbed in the carbon and                volatilizing; (iv) placing the dried charcoal into                a roaster, filling inert gas under air-tight                conditions, turning over continuously and passing                through the guiding arm to the tail; (v) placing                the pre-carbonized charcoal into a carbonization                furnace, controlling the negative pressure; (vi)                introducing uniformly the carbonized carbon into                the activation furnace, filling the furnace with a                mixture of water vapor and carbon dioxide; (vii)                placing the activated carbon into the activated                carbon column, refluxing, filtering, washing to                neutral and drying; and (viii) air-cooling the                charcoal and post-treating.
   USE - The method is useful for regenerating                activated carbon.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is environmentally friendly; and                has lower temperature and generates less exhaust                gas and the activated carbon has increased                adsorption and reuse times.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Regenerating activated carbon comprises (i)                dispersing the waste activated carbon with                sufficient microporous adsorption and exposing in                the sunlight for 24-48 hours; (ii) washing the                waste activated carbon with pure water to ensure                the cleaning of visible impurities and soluble                micro-impurities and drying at room temperature to                a moisture content of 40-45%; (iii) heating the                washed wet carbon at 100-150 degrees C to evaporate                the water adsorbed in the carbon and volatilizing                some low-boiling organic substances; (iv) placing                the dried charcoal into a roaster, filling inert                gas under air-tight conditions, turning over                continuously and passing through the guiding arm to                the tail, where the exhaust gas in the furnace is                led out through the induced draft fan; (v) placing                the pre-carbonized charcoal into a carbonization                furnace, controlling the negative pressure 30-100                P, and the heating rate at 9 degrees C/minute and                turning over in the furnace to make it uniformly                heated in the furnace; (vi) introducing uniformly                the carbonized carbon into the activation furnace                through the feeder, filling the furnace with a                mixture of water vapor and carbon dioxide,                contacting with charcoal and setting the furnace                temperature to 800-900 degrees C and reacting for                5-10 hours; (vii) placing the activated carbon into                the activated carbon column, inputting the                activation liquid into the activation furnace and                refluxing at 110-120 degrees C for 10 hours,                filtering, washing to neutral and drying at 120                degrees C for 7-8 hours under vacuum; and (viii)                air-cooling the charcoal for 4-6 hours and                post-treating.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (iv), the                  pre-carbonization temperature is 150-400 degrees                  C and in the step (v), the carbonization                  temperature is 400-500 degrees C. The volume                  ratio of water vapor and carbon dioxide is                  2:1-3:1. The heating source of the activation                  furnace is natural gas. The activation solution                  is formed by mixing water, concentrated nitric                  acid, hydrochloric acid and zinc chloride                  solution in a volume ratio of 5-6:6:2:1:1. In the                  step (vii) the degree of vacuum is 500-600                  mmHg.

Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PN CN110349497-A; CN210039417-U
TI Evaporation enhancement three-dimensional advertisement word comprises e.g. glyph plate having primary color-shaped area and vapor deposition zone in front portion, and evaporation zone plated with evaporation hook layer on the font plate.
AU ZHAO L
AE ZHAO L (ZHAO-Individual)
GA 201989004E
AB    NOVELTY - Evaporation enhancement three-dimensional advertisement word comprises a glyph plate provided with primary color-shaped area and vapor deposition zone in the front portion. The outer contour surface edge and inner contour surface edge of primary color font area, and the inner contour end surface edge and the outer contour end surface edge are both arranged as vapor deposition zone. The evaporation zone is plated with an evaporation hook layer on font plate by vacuum evaporation process or nano-plating process. The evaporation hooking layer is integrally formed with font plate, and the back surface of the font plate comprises mounting groove arranged along glyph-shaped contour. A three-dimensional contour plate is defined in the mounting groove and is adapted to the mounting groove.
   USE - Used as evaporation enhancement three-dimensional advertisement word.
   ADVANTAGE - The three-dimensional advertising word: can be integrated without being welded or bonded, and the font plate and the vapor deposition hook layer can be integrated, which simplifies the manufacturing process. The method: improves the quality of the three-dimensional advertising word, avoids the technical problems in the advertising industry, and the manufacturing process of the luminous three-dimensional advertising word.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing evaporation enhancement three-dimensional advertisement word comprising (a) drawing the advertisement words to be produced by using vector software, marking the outline position of font shape of advertisement word at the time of drawing and carrying out typesetting on acrylic plate, (b) cutting the protective film covered by the surface of the acrylic sheet by a numerically controlled carbon dioxide laser machine to outline the edge between primary color font region and vapor deposition zone, where the numerically controlled carbon dioxide laser machine is only at the position of the mark point, and cutting the acrylic sheet while removing the protective film covered on the evaporation zone, (c) matching the position of mark by the CCD numerical control patrol engraving and milling machine, engraving the mounting groove on the back surface of acrylic plate, simultaneously cutting the acrylic sheet into acrylic sheet having outline of the advertisement word, (d) hooking the acrylic sheet, forming the vapor deposition hook layer on the surface of acrylic plate-shaped plate of evaporation zone by a vacuum evaporation process or a nano-spraying process, and (e) using an aluminum alloy plate to form a three-dimensional contour plate adapted to the acrylic plate, and binding the three-dimensional contour plate into mounting groove by an adhesive.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the evaporation enhancement three-dimensional advertisement word.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further comprises turning off the protective film covered on the primary color-shaped area when the vapor-deposited three-dimensional advertisement word is installed. Preferred Components: The gating plate is an acrylic plate which is cut by an acrylic plate. The three-dimensional outline plate is a three-dimensional outline plate surrounded by an aluminum alloy plate, and one end of the three-dimensional outline plate is bonded into the mounting groove by an adhesive. The contour of the vapor deposition layer is the same as the contour of the font plate. The adhesive is a structural adhesive, a glass adhesive, a alfa-cyanalocacrylicresinadhesive (502 adhesive) or cyanoacrylate adhesive (505 adhesive).
DC M23 (Soldering; welding - including brazing, flame cutting and scarfing, cutting and welding rods, soldering and unsoldering apparatus and solder compositions (B23K).); A88 (Mechanical engineering and tools e.g. valves, gears and conveyor belts.); T01 (Digital Computers); X24 (Electric Welding)

PN CN110140754-A
TI Preserving puffer fish, by washing puffer fish, naturally drying, packing to high-resistant polyvinylidene chloride vacuum packaging bag, transferring to modified atmosphere packaging machine, vacuuming, aerating, heat sealing and storing.
AU XIE J
   LI P
   MEI J
   WANG J
   TAN M
   YU W
   WANG X
AE UNIV SHANGHAI OCEAN (USHO-C)
GA 201974083B
AB    NOVELTY - Preserving puffer fish, involves (i) selecting puffer fish, increasing oxygen using foam box to keep the fish fresh and alive, and transporting to laboratory, (ii) cutting fish belly by scissors, removing fish gill and internal organs, tearing off fish skin, removing eyes, extruding residual blood in the spine of fish and washing, (iii) horizontally paving disposable preservative film on an experiment table, placing slaughtered and washed fish and naturally drying, (iv) randomly dividing treated fish into 3 groups and packaging to high-resistant polyvinylidene chloride vacuum packaging bags, subjecting 1 group to non-modified atmosphere packaging which is used as reference, and subjecting the other 2 groups to modified atmosphere packaging, (v) transferring the modified atmosphere packaged component to an modified atmosphere packaging machine, setting carbon dioxide, oxygen and nitrogen range, mixing, vacuuming, aerating and heat sealing, and (vi) storing packaged puffer fish.
   USE - The method is useful for preserving puffer fish.
   ADVANTAGE - The method inhibits growth and reproduction of microorganisms during storage of puffer fish, ensures high sensory score, physical and chemical index and quality of the fish, and is safe, healthy and easy to operate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preserving puffer fish, involves (i) selecting 250-300 g puffer fish with uniform size, smooth body surface and bright color and luster, increasing oxygen using a foam box to keep the fish fresh and alive, and transporting to laboratory, (ii) rapidly cutting fish belly by a sharp scissors, removing fish gill and internal organs using fingers, trimming the residual internal organs by scissors, tearing off fish skin along the section line of the upper abdomen, removing eyes by scissors, extruding residual blood in the spine of the fish, and washing the fish with ice water, (iii) horizontally paving a disposable preservative film on an experiment table, placing the slaughtered and washed fish and naturally drying for 20-50 minutes, (iv) randomly dividing the treated fish into 3 groups and packaging to high-resistance polyvinylidene chloride vacuum packaging bags, subjecting 1 group to non-modified atmosphere packaging which is used as reference, and subjecting the other 2 groups to modified atmosphere packaging, (v) transferring the modified atmosphere packaged component to a modified atmosphere packaging machine, setting the gas proportion at a range of 40-60% carbon dioxide, 40% oxygen and 0-20% nitrogen, where the ratio of the volume of gas (ml) to the weight of puffer fish (g) is 2:1-4:1, mixing, vacuuming, aerating and heat sealing, and (vi) storing the packaged puffer fish at 4 plus minus 1 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Composition: The ratio of the volume of gas to the weight of puffer fish is 3 ml:1 g. The ratio of the gas in the modified atmosphere packaging bag is 40% carbon dioxide, 40% oxygen and 20% nitrogen. Preferred Components: The modified atmosphere packaging bag is a high-resistant polyvinylidene chloride vacuum packaging bag. Preferred Conditions: In step (iii), the natural drying is carried out for 30 minutes.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).)

PN CN110028763-A; CN110028763-B
TI Preparation of low density high rate epoxy resin            microporous material, involves reacting polyether            monoamine, bisphenol A type epoxy resin, hyperbranched            epoxy resin and 2-ethyl-4-methylimidazole to obtain            sample and placing in ice water.
AU ZHANG G
   FAN X
   SHI X
   GAO Q
   QIN J
   FENG Y
AE UNIV NORTHWESTERN POLYTECHNICAL (UNWP-C)
   UNIV NORTHWESTERN POLYTECHNICAL (UNWP-C)
GA 201965125Y
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation of low density high rate epoxy                resin microporous material, involves (1) adding                polyether monoamine and                3-glycidoxypropyltrimethylsiloxane absolute                ethanol, drying to obtain yellow waxy viscous                fluid, and labeling obtained sample as (A), (2)                adding bisphenol A type epoxy resin, hyperbranched                epoxy resin and 2-ethyl-4-methylimidazole, and                stirring to obtain mixed solution, and labeling                mixed solution as sample as sample (B), (3) adding                samples (A) and (B) to sample (B), stirring, drying                to obtain epoxy resin powder, and labeling resin                powder as sample (C), (4) placing sample (C)in hot                press, heating, and natural cooling to room                temperature to obtain sample (D), (5) placing                sample (D) in autoclave, injecting carbon dioxide                gas, controlling pressure, temperature and time and                relieving pressure to obtain sample (E), and (6)                placing sample (E) in silicone oil bath, foaming at                constant temperature and quickly placing sample (E)                into ice water to cool the shape.
   USE - Preparation of low density high rate epoxy                resin microporous material.
   ADVANTAGE - The low density high rate epoxy resin                microporous material has uniform structure.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The preparation of low density high rate epoxy                resin microporous material, involves (1) adding                polyether monoamine and                3-glycidoxypropyltrimethylsiloxane to 300-350 g of                absolute ethanol at mass ratio of 10:1-8:1,                reacting under magnetic stirring conditions of                40-50 degrees C for 10-15 hours, drying the                reactant solution to obtain yellow waxy viscous                fluid, and labeling obtained sample as (A), (2)                adding bisphenol A type epoxy resin, hyperbranched                epoxy resin and 2-ethyl-4-methylimidazole to                dichloromethane at mass ratio of 90:10:4.54, and                stirring at speed of 500-1000 rpm on magnetic                stirrer for 4-6 hours to obtain mixed solution, and                labeling obtained sample as (B), (3) adding samples                (A) and (B) to sample (B) according to the mass                ratio of 1-10:100, and carrying out magnetic                stirring for 3-5 hours, drying mixed solution in                vacuum oven at 30-35 degrees C to obtain an epoxy                resin powder, and labeling obtained sample as (C),                (4) placing sample (C)in hot press, heating at                100-125 degrees C with pressure of 10-20 MPa for                4-8 hours, and natural cooling to room temperature                to obtain sample (D), (5) placing sample (D) in an                autoclave, injecting carbon dioxide gas,                controlling pressure of autoclave to 20-25 MPa,                maintaining temperature at 40-60 degrees C, and                time for 70-100 hours and relieving pressure to                obtain sample (E), and (6) placing sample (E) in a                silicone oil bath, foaming at constant temperature                of 90-120 degrees C and quickly placing the sample                (E) into ice water to cool the shape.

PT P
PN WO2019098433-A1; KR2019057609-A
TI Air cleaner used in confined space e.g. government office, has air compressor that compresses air to be mutually interacting with granules in granule bath to reduce toxic gas among air and increase amount of oxygen among air.
AU KIM Y G
AE KIM Y G (KIMY-Individual)
GA 2019457172
AB    NOVELTY - The air cleaner (100) has an air compressor (130) which are provided for compressing the air flowed in from outside. A granule bath (140) is connected with the air compressor and filled with the granule obtained by processing the alkali metal in inside. The air compressed by the air compressor and the granules in the granule bath are mutually interacted to reduce the toxic gas among air and increase the amount of oxygen among air. An air filtration filter (110) is filled with the granule. A sound pressure generating fan (120) is provided between the air filtration filter and the air compressor for adding the sound pressure to the air filtered by the air filtration filter in order to move the filter to the air compressor.
   USE - Air cleaner used in confined space such as government office, congestion facility and building.
   ADVANTAGE - The toxic gas among air is removed with multiple granule baths which are selectively usable and the carbon dioxide in the air is changed to oxygen so that the quality of the indoor space is improved.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The air compressor is covered with a soundproof case for reducing noise generated during operation of air compressor. A cooling fan is provided for cooling the heat generated in the air compressor operation. A solenoid valve is mounted in the air compressor in order to control the operation of air compressor by controlling the amount of air provided to the air compressor. The air is selectively provided to the granule bath from the air compressor. The granule bath is provided with an outlet for discharging the oxygen-containing air generated by reaction with the granule. The inner diameter of the outlet is smaller than the inner width of the internal space of the granule bath. A control module is configured for controlling the operation of the granule bath. The control module comprises a main control unit that is provided for selectively controlling the operation of the granule bath. A power control unit is configured for controlling the supply of electric power. A sensing control unit is configured for sensing the carbon dioxide concentration, oxygen content or toxic gas concentration of the space in which the air cleaner is arranged. A central control application is portable to the user. The granule bath is controlled selectively by wireless transmission-reception between the control module and the central control application.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of the configuration of air cleaner. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Air cleaner (100)
   Air filtration filter (110)
   Sound pressure generating fan (120)
   Air compressor (130)
   Granule bath (140)

PN CN109331557-A
TI Purification of cement kiln bypass air flue gas comprises exhausting of bypass gas from suction point, filtering of dust in flue gas, reacting of nitrogen oxide with reducing agent ammonia, dioxin decomposing and deacidifying.
AU SHI Y
   MU L
   MAO Z
   GAN H
   LIAO Y
   GUO X
   TAO W
   ZHU X
   SHEN L
AE HEFEI ZHONGYA ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (CHNB-C)
GA 201917873V
AB    NOVELTY - Purification of cement kiln bypass air flue gas comprises (i) exhausting of bypass gas from the suction point out of the kiln tail smoke chamber, initially entering into the primary cooler, cooling the high temperature smoke gas to 450-550 degrees C and introducing into high temperature bag dust remover; (ii) filtering of dust in flue gas to separate dust and other contaminants and reducing chlorine ion dust; (iii) introducing filtered high temperature smoke gas into secondary cooler and cooling; (iv) introducing gas into the selective catalytic reduction reactor, reacting of nitrogen oxide x (NOx) in the flue gas with the reducing agent ammonia to form nitrogen gas and water and decomposing of dioxins are into hydrochloric acid, carbon dioxide and water in the selective catalytic reduction reactor; (v) introducing into the quench tower for fast cooling, adding into bag-type dust remover, deacidifying and reducing amount of hydrochloric acid in flue gas.
   USE - The method is useful for purification of cement kiln bypass air flue gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces waste treatment difficulty of setting the reaction device of denitration and the dioxin.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Purification of cement kiln bypass air flue gas comprises (i) exhausting of bypass gas from the suction point out of the kiln tail smoke chamber, initially entering into the primary cooler, cooling the high temperature smoke gas to 450-550 degrees C and introducing into high temperature bag dust remover; (ii) filtering of dust in high temperature flue gas to separate dust and other contaminants, reducing the chlorine ion dust, where high-temperature bag-type dust collector is provided with a filter bag made of ceramic fiber fabric; (iii) introducing the filtered high temperature smoke gas into the secondary cooler, cooling to 210-230 degrees C, rapidly cooling the flue gas through secondary cooler and avoiding the dioxin re-synthesis temperature, which is beneficial to reduce the re-production of dioxins; (iv) introducing gas into selective catalytic reduction reactor, reacting of nitrogen oxide x (NOx) in the flue gas with the reducing agent ammonia to form nitrogen gas and water and decomposing of dioxins are into hydrochloric acid, carbon dioxide and water in the selective catalytic reduction reactor; (v) introducing into the quench tower for fast cooling to 170-190 degrees C, adding into bag-type dust remover, deacidifying flue gas by the sprayed sodium bicarbonate powder in a bag filter, reducing the amount of hydrochloric acid in the flue gas and reaching of gas pollutants in the outgas of the bag filter to requirements of ultra-clean exhaust.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The primary cooler and the secondary cooler both use an air-cooled cooler, and the air-cooled cooler is connected to an external fan. The air-cooled cooler has a built-in cooling pipe, and the flue gas passes through the cooling pipe to cool down. The inlet of the selective catalytic reduction reactor is provided with an ammonia water tank connected with the spraying device. The dust discharging the high-temperature bag type dust collector outlet is less than 1 mg/m3. The bypass gas exhaust point from the kiln tail smoke chamber, and incorporated into a cold air, and then into the first-stage cooler.

PT P
PN CN109203526-A
TI Preparing hemodialysis medical catheter comprises adding polytetrafluoroethylene, polyethylene, polymethyl methacrylate into reaction furnace, heating, pressurizing, reacting, recovering, sterilizing catheter, drying and packaging.
AU FEI Y
AE FEI Y (FEIY-Individual)
GA 201907555C
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing hemodialysis medical catheter comprises (i) adding polytetrafluoroethylene, polyethylene, polymethyl methacrylate into reaction furnace, heating, mixing, adding antifoaming agent, pressurizing and defoaming, reacting, recovering normal pressure, static placing, (ii) adding epoxy soybean oil, sodium acetate and copper sulfate, heating, mixing, reducing to room temperature, filling carbon dioxide gas, static placing, discharging, pressurizing heating, maintaining temperature, (iii) injecting reaction liquid obtained in the step (i) and (ii) into medical guide co-extruder, (iv) sequentially washing extruded conduit casting with sodium citrate solution and ultrapure water, (v) subjecting dried catheter casting to surface activation, sonicating, (vi) soaking surface activated conduit in the solidifying liquid, (vii) washing catheter and drying naturally, (viii) carrying out ultrasonic cleaning, and (ix) sterilizing the catheter high pressure, drying and packaging.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing hemodialysis medical catheter.
   ADVANTAGE - The catheter is safe, no toxicity, anticoagulant and antithrombotic, and has high compatibility.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing hemodialysis medical catheter comprises (i) adding 10-15 pts. wt. polytetrafluoroethylene, 3-9 pts. wt. polyethylene, 4-10 pts. wt. polymethyl methacrylate, 1-4 pts. wt. poly-perfluoroethylene-propylene into reaction furnace and uniformly mixing, heating the reaction to 180 degrees C, adding 1-4 pts. wt. antifoaming agent, pressurizing and de-foaming, continuously reacting for 30-50 minutes, recovering to normal pressure, static placing for 1 hours, (ii) adding 2-5 pts. wt. epoxy soybean oil, 1-2 pts. wt. sepiolite powder, 1-2 pts. wt. wollastonite powder, 2-5 pts. wt. sodium acetate, and 1-2 pts. wt. copper sulfate into the reaction furnace, mixing, heating to 140 degrees C, reacting for 3 minutes, reducing the temperature to room temperature, filling carbon dioxide gas to the reaction furnace, static placing for 10-25 minutes, discharging at room temperature, pressurizing heating the reaction furnace to 150 degrees c, maintaining the temperature for 30 minutes, (iii) injecting the reaction liquid obtained in the step (i) and reaction liquid obtained in the step (ii) into medical guide co-extruder, where the co-extruding machine adopts needle valve-type nozzle of hot runner system to extrude the material, (iv) sequentially washing the extruded conduit casting obtained in step (iii) with sodium citrate solution and ultrapure water, and drying at 65 degrees C, (v) subjecting the dried catheter casting obtained in step (iv) to surface activation, sonicating for 30-35 minutes, (vi) soaking the surface activated conduit obtained in the step (v) in the solidifying liquid to perform curing, (vii) washing the catheter obtained in step (vi) with ultrapure water and drying naturally, (viii) carrying out ultrasonic cleaning and (ix) sterilizing the catheter in high pressure, drying, packaging to obtain finished product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the antifoaming agent is phenethyl alcohol oleate, phenylacetic acid lauryl alcohol ester, dimethyl silicone oil and/or polyoxypropylene glyceryl ether.In the step (vi), the solidifying liquid is soybean oil, glycerol, sodium citrate, glycine, 2-methyl imidazole, arabic gum, water mixture, the mass ratio is 5:3:3:1:2:6:30. In the step (viii), the ultrasonic medium is 0.8% sodium acetate solution. Preferred Method: In the step (i), the pressure is 3-5 MPa. In the step (ii), the air speed of the carbon dioxide is 0.3 cm3/minute, the pressure of the carbon dioxide gas in the final holding reaction furnace is 2-3 MPa. In the step (iii) the parameters of traditional Chinese medical guide is: the screw temperature is 220-260 degrees C, the cutting power is 1KW. In the step (v), the ultrasonic medium is linear alkyl benzene sulfonate, fatty alcohol polyoxyethylene ether sodium sulfate, coconut oil alcohol sodium sulfate, glycerol, dimethyl sulfoxide, water mixture, the mass ratio is 3:4:1:7:1:25. In the step (v) and (viii), the ultrasonic parameter is ultrasonic power of 300 KW, and the ultrasonic time is 30 minutes. In the step (ix), the drying temperature is 65 degrees C.

PN CN108887630-A
TI Preparing halogenated lotus root comprises e.g. cutting fresh lotus root, mixing brine and complex mixture, boiling, adding lotus, marinating, carrying out halogenation, extracting, concentrating, spraying, cooling, slicing and packing.
AU SUN H
   WANG Y
   ZHOU Q
   LIU L
   SUN L
   QIAN H
   YAO W
   CHENG Y
AE JIANGSU NIHAOYA FOOD CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201897649V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing halogenated lotus root comprises (i) cleaning fresh lotus root and cutting into sections to obtain whole section, cleaning lotus root, and removing surface and internal precipitate and debris, (ii) adding brine to boiling pot, adding complex mixture, mixing, boiling, adding lotus and marinating, carrying out halogenation for 10-45 minutes, removing core, crushing, extracting, concentrating, drying and spraying, and pickling greengage to obtain sour organic acid extract, which is a powdery substance with an acidity of 25-30%, (iii) cooling, (iv) slicing the lotus root slice to the thickness of 4-6 mm, and (v) performing air-conditioning preservative packaging using packaging machine, filling gas with mixture of 69% nitrogen and 31% carbon dioxide, and packing.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing halogenated lotus root.
   ADVANTAGE - The lotus root has good taste and color. The method saves time; reduces waste; and prolongs shelf life.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing halogenated lotus root comprises (i) cleaning fresh lotus root and cutting into sections to obtain whole section, cleaning lotus root, and removing surface and internal precipitate and debris, (ii) adding brine to boiling pot, adding complex mixture, which accounts for two thousandths of the weight of the raw material, uniformly mixing, boiling, adding lotus and marinating, carrying out halogenation for 10-45 minutes, where the complex mixture comprises 3-12 pts. wt. functional red yeast rice powder, 2-8 pts. wt. noni fruit extract, 10-22 pts. wt. galangal extract, 40-65 pts. wt. FSCY86 modified starch, 20-35 pts. wt. water, 10-20 pts. wt. maltodextrin and 3-15 pts. wt. organic acid extract of green plum, removing core, crushing, extracting, concentrating, drying and spraying, and pickling greengage to obtain sour organic acid extract, which is a powdery substance with an acidity of 25-30%, (iii) cooling at 15-20 degrees C, 5-10 degrees C and 0-4 degrees C to central temperature of 8-10 degrees C, and using different control times depending on the size of the crucible and the number of layers placed on the crucible, where each time the center temperature control is the end point, the cool end point in the 15-20 degrees C environment is the center temperature of 35-40 degrees C, 5-10 degrees C environment, the cool end point is 20-25 degrees C, 0-4 degrees C in the environment, and the cool end point is the center temperature of 8-10 degrees C, (iv) slicing the lotus root slice to the thickness of 4-6 mm, and (v) performing air-conditioning preservative packaging using packaging machine, filling gas with mixture of 69% nitrogen and 31% carbon dioxide, and packing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: The complex mixture comprises 5 pts. wt. functional red yeast rice powder, 4 pts. wt. noni fruit extract, 14 pts. wt. galangal extract, 48 pts. wt. FSCY86 modified starch, 30 pts. wt. water, 15 pts. wt. maltodextrin and 7 pts. wt. organic acid extract of green plum. Preferred Method: The preparation of the complex mixture comprises (i) adding 5 pts. wt. functional red yeast rice powder, 4 pts. wt. noni fruit extract and 14 pts. wt. galangal extract to 30 pts. wt. water respectively and stirring until completely dissolved to obtain mixture number one, and stirring with manual slow mixing or stirring with a blender at a speed of lower than 200 revolutions/minute, (ii) mixing 48 pts. wt. FSCY86 modified starch, 15 pts. wt. maltodextrin and 7 pts. wt. organic acid extract of green plum with the mixture number one, uniformly stirring to obtain mixture number two, and stirring with manual slow mixing or stirring with a blender at a speed of lower than 200 revolutions/minute, and (iii) adding the mixture number three to twin-screw extruder, setting the pre-extrusion zone temperature to 60-65 degrees C and the rear zone temperature to 85-100 degrees C and extruding, hot air-drying to a moisture content of 10%, and crushing to 40 meshes or less. The preparation of the noni fruit extract comprises cleaning fresh noni fruit, cooling under natural wind until the surface is dry, no water droplets on the surface and no obvious loss of water, (ii) pulping the surface of the noni fruit using pulping machine, taking the noni fruit pulp, and discarding the material exported from the pulping machine waste residue, (iii) mixing the noni fruit pulp and salt according to the mass ratio of 100:1, stirring and mixing until the salt is completely dissolved, and incubating the mixture in an incubator at 25-30 degrees C for 24 hours, and (iv) drying the mixture in the oven to the moisture content of 6-8%, and crushing in crusher.

PN CN108641781-A
TI Preparing magnetorheological fluid of porous carbon nanosphere embedded with ferroferric oxide comprises dissolving ferrocene powder in acetone, adding hydrogen peroxide, oxidizing, drying, grinding, calcining and dispersing in mineral oil.
AU ZHANG W
   QIU Y
AE UNIV QINGDAO (UNQD-C)
GA 2018812897
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing magnetorheological fluid of a porous carbon nanosphere embedded with ferroferric oxide comprises dissolving ferrocene powder in acetone, stirring, adding hydrogen peroxide to the acetone solution, vigorously stirring, thoroughly mixing to obtain a reaction liquid, transferring into PTFE inner tank, fully oxidizing, cooling, destroying ferrocene structure and oxidizing to form triiron tetroxide to obtain a black mud, sonicating the black mud, re-dispersing the precipitate in the solution to facilitate subsequent washing, adding acetone for centrifugal washing, washing away excess ferrocene, oven drying to obtain a black solid, thoroughly grinding black solid product with an agate mortar, placing in a tubular atmosphere furnace, calcining in a carbon dioxide atmosphere to be carbonized, naturally cooling to room temperature to obtain embedded iron(II, III) oxide porous carbon nano ball material, grinding, dispersing in mineral oil to obtain a suspension, and oscillating.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing a magnetorheological fluid of a porous carbon nanosphere embedded with ferroferric oxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes easily available raw materials; is simple and environmentally friendly; and provides magnetorheological fluid with high-stability and high yield.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing magnetorheological fluid of a porous carbon nanosphere embedded with ferroferric oxide comprises (a) dissolving ferrocene powder in acetone, stirring, adding hydrogen peroxide to the acetone solution according to a volume ratio of hydrogen peroxide to acetone of 1:15-1:25, and vigorously stirring for 20-30 minutes, where the hydrogen peroxide and ferrocene are thoroughly mixed to obtain a reaction liquid, (b) transferring the homogeneous solution into PTFE inner tank, fully oxidizing at 200-300 degrees C for 10-40 hours, cooling to room temperature, destroying ferrocene structure and oxidizing to form triiron tetroxide to obtain a black mud, (c) sonicating the black mud for 20-30 minutes, re-dispersing the precipitate in the solution to facilitate subsequent washing, adding acetone (1-3 times) for centrifugal washing for three times, at a rotating speed of 6000-12000 revolutions/minute for 1-10 minutes, washing away excess ferrocene, and drying in a drying oven at 60-80 degrees C for 12-16 hours to obtain a black solid, (d) thoroughly grinding black solid product with an agate mortar, placing in a tubular atmosphere furnace, and calcining at 500 degrees C for 4 hours in a carbon dioxide atmosphere to be carbonized, and naturally cooling to room temperature to obtain embedded iron(II, III) oxide porous carbon nano ball material and (e) grinding in-situ iron(II, III) oxide porous carbon nanosphere material in an agate mortar, dispersing in 35 vol.% mineral oil to obtain a suspension, and oscillating to form a uniform magnetorheological fluid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The molar ratio of the ferrocene to acetone is 10 pts. wt. of ferrocene and 25 pts. wt. of acetone.

PN CN108117932-A; CN108117932-B
TI Recycling tuna leftover involves crushing cleaned tuna leftover, adding water to tuna leftover, adding pepsin to slurry, carrying out enzymolysis, introducing nitrogen into slurry, and performing high voltage pulse electric field process.
AU LU Z
   WEI C
   HUANG Y
AE GUANGXI NANNING XIUPO BIOTECHNOLOGY CO (GUAN-Non-standard)
   GUANGXI NANNING XIUPO BIOTECHNOLOGY CO (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201845489D
AB    NOVELTY - The method for recycling tuna leftover involves (a) cleaning tuna leftover with water, crushing the material, adding water to the tuna leftover to form slurry and stirring slurry, (b) adding pepsin to the slurry, carrying out enzymolysis, adding pepsin to the slurry, introducing nitrogen into the slurry to obtain enzymolysis solution (I), (c) placing enzymolysis solution (I) in a vessel, continuously introducing carbon dioxide gas into the enzymolysis solution, performing high voltage pulse electric field process, continuously introducing carbon dioxide gas to obtain material liquid, (d) processing the material liquid, then adding neutral protease to carry out enzymolysis, adding neutral protease to the material liquid, introducing nitrogen into the slurry to obtain enzymolysis solution (II), and (e) adding hydrochloric acid to the enzymolysis solution (II), adjusting pH value of the enzymolysis liquid (II) to 4-5, adding anhydrous ethanol to the enzymolysis solution (II) and leaving.
   USE - The method is useful for recycling tuna leftover.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the extracting rate of fish oil, and increases the content of eicosapentaenoic acid and docosahexaenoic acid in fish oil.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The method involves (f) centrifugally separating the material liquid in a centrifugal device at rotating speed of 8000-9000 rpm for 26-33 minutes. The water is used in an amount of 0.5-2 times. The enzymolysis in the step (b) is carried out at temperature of 30-35 degrees C for 9-10 hours and pH value of 2-3. The pepsin is used in amount of 2-2.5 %mass. The introducing in the step (b) is carried out at flow rate of 1.5-2.5 m/second. The introducing in the step (c) is carried out at flow rate of 4-5 m/second for 1-2 hours. The high voltage pulse electric field process is carried out at strength of 55-60 kV/cm, frequency of 100-150 Hz and pulse width of 20-25 mu s and time of 5-10 seconds. The processing in the step (d) is carried out for 30-60 minutes. The enzymolysis in the step (d) is carried out at temperature of 40-45 degrees C for 3-4 hours and pH value of 8-9. The neutral protease is used in an amount of 2-3 %mass. The introducing in the step (d) is carried out at flow rate of 3.5-5.5 m/second. The neutral protease comprises trypsin, papain and subtilisin protease in mass ratio of 4-5:1-2:0.5-1. The hydrochloric acid is used in an amount of 2-3 %mass. The leaving step is carried out for 20-23 hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method involves carrying out ultrasonic treatment at ultrasonic frequency of 800-950 kHz, preferably 700 kHz.

PT P
PN WO2018073400-A1; CA3040849-A1; IN201917015234-A; EP3529252-A1; US2019256528-A1; CN110167948-A; JP2020500158-W; EP3529252-B1; US10882870-B2; ES2857699-T3; CN110167948-B; JP7000424-B2
TI New crystalline metal organic framework comprising            metal-ligand repeating units comprising a metal and two            ligands used as separation agent for e.g. mixture of            substances in gas phase including carbon dioxide or            methane, and as catalyst.
AU CORELLA OCHOA M D L N
   LILLO GARCIA V
   GALAN MASCAROS J R
   LILLO G V
   GALAN M J R
   CORELLA O M D L N
AE FUNDACIO INST CATALA INVESTIGACIO QUIMIC (CATA-Non-standard)
   INST CATALANA RECERCA & ESTUDIS AVANCATS (CATA-Non-standard)
   FUNDACIO INST CATALA DINVESTIGACIO QUIMI (CATA-Non-standard)
   FUNDACIO CATALA DINVESTIGACIO QUIMICA (CATA-Non-standard)
   CATALANA RECERCA & ESTUDIS AVANCATS INST (CATA-Non-standard)
   FUNDACIO INST CATALA DINVESTIGACIO QUIMI (CATA-Non-standard)
   CATALANA RECERCA & ESTUDIS AVANCATS INST (CATA-Non-standard)
   ICREA INST CATALANA RECERCA I ESTUDIS AV (ICRE-Non-standard)
   FUNDACIO CATALA DINVESTIGACIO QUIMICA (CATA-Non-standard)
   FUNDACIO INST CATALA DINVESTIGACIO QUIMI (CATA-Non-standard)
   CATALANA RECERCA & ESTUDIS AVANCATS INST (CATA-Non-standard)
   FUNDACIO INST CATALA DINVESTIGACIO QUIMI (CATA-Non-standard)
   ICREA INST CATALANA RECERCA & ESTUDIS AV (ICRE-Non-standard)
   ICREA INST CATALANA RECERCA I ESTUDIS AV (ICRE-Non-standard)
   CATALA FOUND CHEM RES INST ICIQ (CATA-Non-standard)
   FUNDACIO INST CATALA INVESTIGACIO QUIMIC (CATA-Non-standard)
   INST CATALANA RECERCA & ESTUDIS AVANCATS (CATA-Non-standard)
GA 2018332204
AB    NOVELTY - Crystalline metal organic framework (I)                comprising metal-ligand repeating units (IA), or                alternatively metal-ligand repeating units (IB),                comprising a metal M2 and two ligands L1, is                new.
   USE - (I) are useful: as separation agent for a                mixture of substances in gas phase comprising                carbon dioxide or methane, and in the                enantioselective separation of a mixture of                enantiomers of a chiral compound, where the                enantioselective separation is performed in the                presence of a solvent, the chiral compound is                active ingredient i.e. ibuprofen; and as a catalyst                (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - (I) exhibit easy post-reaction separation,                catalyst reusability (recycling), and high                stability, high efficiency and selectivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Crystalline metal organic framework (I)                comprising metal-ligand repeating units of formula                (IA), or alternatively metal-ligand repeating units                of formula (IB), comprising a metal M2 and two                ligands L1, is new.
   L1 = anion comprising                (R)-midazole-5-ylmethyl)-(1,2,4-triazol-4-yl)acetate                or                (S)-imidazole-5-ylmethyl)-(1,2,4-triazol-4-yl)acetate;
   M2 = divalent metal cation;
   S1 = solvent molecule comprising water,                methanol, ethanol, isopropanol, tert-butanol,                diethyl ether, 1,4-dioxane, tetrahydrofuran,                N,N-dimethylformamide, acetonitrile, acetone,                1,2-ethyleneglycol, dimethylsulfoxide or pyridine;                and
   arrows = coordination bond between the metal                and N of ligand L1, where one of the coordination                bonds is between the metal and the nitrogen atom N1                of one L1 ,and the other coordination bond is                between the metal and the nitrogen atom N2 of the                other L1, and between the metal and the solvent                molecules S1, thus the repeating unit has e.g.                carboxylate derivatives of formula (RR)-(IA), or                alternatively, carboxylate derivatives of formula                (SS)-(IA), and the repeating unit in the metal                organic framework is connected to other repeating                units in so that the metal of one repeating unit is                connected to two additional repeating units of                formula (IA), or alternatively of formula (IB),                through coordination bonds represented by between                the metal M2 of the first repeating unit and the                nitrogen atoms of the ligands L1 of the additional                repeating units, and one of the additional                coordination bonds is between the metal M2 of the                first repeating unit and the nitrogen atom N1 of an                L1 of an additional repeating unit, and the other                additional coordination bond is between the metal                M2 of the first repeating unit and the nitrogen                atom N2 of an L1 of another additional repeating                unit.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) preparing (I); and
   (2) a composition comprising (I) and at least                one carrier.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): Preparation of (I)                  comprises (a) adding a solution of a solvent S1                  and of a salt of formula (MA2) to a solution of a                  solvent S1 and of the compound e.g.                  (R)-midazol-5-ylmethyl)-(1,2,4-triazol-4-yl)acetic                  acid, or alternatively, of the compound                  (S)-midazol-5-ylmethyl)-(1,2,4-triazol-4-yl)acetic                  acid, (b) maintaining the solution obtained in                  step (a) at 20-35 degrees C for the necessary                  time to crystallize the metal organic framework,                  and (c) isolating (I).A = monovalent anion A- comprising organic                  anion or inorganic anion.Preferred Process: The process comprises:                  (d) submitting the metal organic framework having                  e.g. repeating units of formula (RR)-(IB), or                  alternatively of formula (SS)-(IB) at such                  reaction conditions to remove at least both                  solvent molecules S1; (f) contacting (I) having                  repeating units (RR)-(IA), or alternatively                  (SS)-(IA) with solvent S1 and under such reaction                  conditions to coordinate the two solvent                  molecules S1 to the metal. Preferred Components:                  (I) is an enantiomerically enriched metal organic                  framework. (I) has: a X-ray diffractogram that                  comprises characteristic peaks at 6.2, 7.6, 9.8,                  10.7, 14.5, 15.2, 17.6, 18.1, 19.2, 19.7, 20.2,                  20.6, 22.5, 22.9, 23.7, 24.2, 26.1, 27.2, 28.3,                  30, 32.6, 33.2, 33.5, 34.1, 35 and or 37 plus                  minus 0.2 degrees 2 theta measured in an X-ray                  diffractmeter with copper K- alpha radiation                  (1.5406asterisk10-10 m ( Angstrom ));                  crystallographic parameters measured by single                  X-ray diffraction, crystal system is cubic, space                  group is P4(3)/32, unit cell dimensions a is                  20.172(2)x 10-10 m( Angstrom ), alpha is 90                  degrees , b is 20.172(2)x 10-10 m( Angstrom )                  beta is 90 degrees , c is 20.172(2)x 10-10 m(                  Angstrom ), gamma is 90 degrees , volume is                  8208(2)x 10-10 m3( Angstrom ) and density is                  1.359 Mg/m3; a Brunauer-Emmett-Teller surface                  area of 250-1800 (preferably 850-1050) m2/g; and                  a pore volume from 0.1-0.7 (preferably 0.3-0.4)                  cm3/g. The carrier is a porous solid carrier                  comprising a porous organic solid carrier and a                  porous inorganic solid carrier. The porous                  polymeric organic carrier is e.g. poly(vinyl                  esters), poly(ethylenimine), polyimides,                  polysulfones, poly(methyl methacrylate),                  poly(vinylacetate), poly(pyrroles),                  poly(acetylenes) or poly(thiophenes). Preferred                  Composition: The composition comprises 20-90                  (preferably 60-70) wt.% (I).
   Preferred Definitions:
   M2 = Cu2+, Zn2+, V2+, Pd2+, Pt2+, Ni2+, Ru2+,                Hg2+, Sn2+, Co2+, Fe2+, Mn2+ Cr2+, Cd2+, Be2+, Mg2t                Ca2+, Ba2+, Zr2+, Gd2+, Eu2+ or Tb2+.
   EXAMPLE - 0.48 g Copper(II) acetate was dissolved in 50                ml water and the resultant solution was slowly                added to an aqueous solution containing 1 g                (S)-imidazol-5-ylmethyl)-(1,2,4-triazol-4-yl)acetic                acid. The final solution was filtered and stored at                room temperature to give 1.1 g crystalline metal                organic framework.

PT P
PN CN107896666-A
TI Planting Magnolia cylindrica useful for improving survival rate, by grafting Magnolia cylindrica and Magnolia cylindrica seedling, combining and covering wound with functional liquid, wrapping and planting grafted seedlings in nursery beds.
AU JU G
AE BENGBU HONGRUI GARDEN CO LTD (BENG-Non-standard)
GA 201830232S
AB    NOVELTY - Method of planting Magnolia cylindrica involves mixing loess and organic fertilizers and adding mixed content in greenhouse nursery beds, sowing variable temperature seed, intercept root of M.cylindrica seedling and Magnolia grandiflora seedlings, soaking scion and rootstock in bactericidal solution and turpentine, combining and covering the wound with functional liquid, wrapping using plastic wrap to 3 layers to obtain grafted seedlings, planting grafted seedlings in nursery beds, spraying growth promoting solution and sodium hypochlorite solution.
   USE - The method is useful for planting M.cylindrica which is useful for improving survival rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method of planting Magnolia cylindrica involves (a) selecting and sterilizing M.cylindrica and Magnolia grandiflora seed, mixing seeds with organic fertilizer and storing mixed content at 4-7 degrees C for 2-3 months to obtain stored M.cylindrica and M.grandiflora seed, (b) performing variable temperature mutagenesis by soaking stored seed obtained in warm water, then cooked soaked seeds in absolute ethanol at 42-45 degrees C for 34-38 minutes, placing seeds in thermostat for temperature changes, storing seeds at 25-28 degrees C for 15-18 minutes, subjecting seeds to magnetization processing using 0.2-0.4 MHz magnetizer, then cooling seeds to 4-7 degrees C for 8-10 minutes, alternately preserving seeds for 22-24 hours to obtain a variable temperature seed, (c) mixing loess and organic fertilizers and adding mixed content in greenhouse nursery beds, sowing variable temperature seed in November, irrigating the growth promoting solution for greenhouse cultivation, maintaining concentration of carbon dioxide at 0.08-0.1 vol.%, first 15 days after sowing, maintaining temperature at 11-13 degrees C, after 15 days, maintaining and raising temperature to 23-27 degrees C, until the seedling grows to height of 8-10 cm to obtain M.cylindrica and M.grandiflora seedlings, (e) intercept root of M.cylindrica seedling at level of 0.3-0.5 cm, then semi-circular cutting root into 2 at 180 degrees , then cutting scions along the middle of the branches and cutting 1 cm at the upper end of M.grandiflora seedlings, slashing rootstock roots on both sides at 15 degrees , soaking scion and rootstock in bactericidal solution and turpentine for 15-20 minutes, combining and covering the wound with functional liquid, use plastic wrap to wrap 3 layers to obtain grafted seedlings and (e) planting grafted seedlings in nursery beds, buried thickness of soil is lower than the wound site, maintaining greenhouse temperature at 17-20 degrees C and the carbon dioxide concentration at 0.1-0.12%, spraying growth promoting solution each day for 1 week and spraying sodium hypochlorite solution once every 2-3 days until wound healing after transplantation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Method: In the step (a), sterilizing seeds using antibacterial solution which is a sodium hypochlorite solution having a mass concentration of 40 ppm. In the step (b), soaking seeds in warm water at 33-36 degrees C for 6-8 hours. Preferred Composition: The organic fertilizer comprises 43-47 pts. wt. cereal straw, 27-30 pts. wt. rice husk, 18-22 pts. wt. wheat bran, 14-17 pts. wt. maize peel, 11-14 pts. wt. corn meal, 10-13 pts. wt. sweet potato vine, 8-12 pts. wt. Eucalyptus leaves, 7-10 pts. wt. bamboo and 4-7 pts. wt. humic acid. Preferred Components: The mass ratio of loess and organic fertilizer is (3-4):1. The growth-promoting liquid comprises gibberellin, zeatin and naphthaleneacetic acid in a ratio of 3:1:1, where its concentration is 2-3 %mass. Preferred Process: The functional liquid is prepared by weighing and uniformly mixing 17-20 pts. wt. Aloe gel, 6-9 pts. wt. xylitol, 4-7 pts. wt. bamboo juice, 3-6 pts. wt. catalytic growth liquid, 3-6 pts. wt. sodium hypochlorite solution and 1-3 pts. wt. guar gum to obtain the product.
   ACTIVITY - Fertilizer.

PN CN107597140-A; CN107597140-B
TI Preparation of low-temperature            desulfurization-denitration catalyst for use in            low-temperature desulfurization and denitration process            of flue gas, involves preparing activated carrier using            semi-coke particles and calcium oxide powder.
AU LI J
AE LI J (LIJJ-Individual)
   GUANGDONG LIWEIDA MINING CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018074536
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of low-temperature desulfurization                denitration catalyst, involves (i) mixing semi-coke                particles and calcium oxide powder to obtain a                mixture, (ii) placing the mixture in closed                container, introducing mixture of steam and carbon                dioxide, heat-preserving at 420-450 degrees C,                warming to 800-850 degrees C, heat-preserving,                cooling, washing, drying to obtain activated                carrier, (iii) adding copper nitrate, manganese                nitrate and sodium chloride to obtain precursor                solution of copper nitrate, manganese nitrate and                sodium chloride, (iv) impregnating activated                carrier with precursor solution.
   USE - Preparation of low-temperature                desulfurization-denitration catalyst for use in                low-temperature integrated desulfurization and                denitration process of flue gas (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The prepared catalyst has high desulfurization                and denitration efficiency, sulfur capacity and                long catalytic activity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of low-temperature                desulfurization-denitration catalyst, involves (i)                mixing semi-coke particles and calcium oxide powder                in a mass ratio of (8-10):1 to obtain a mixture,                (ii) placing the mixture in a closed container,                introducing mixture of steam and carbon dioxide,                heat-preserving at 420-450 degrees C for 30-40                minutes, then warming to 800-850 degrees C,                heat-preserving for 2-3 hours, then cooling to room                temperature, washing, drying to obtain activated                carrier, (iii) adding copper nitrate, manganese                nitrate and sodium chloride in a mass ratio of                (7.0-8.0):(1.5-2.5):0.5 to obtain precursor                solution of copper nitrate, manganese nitrate and                sodium chloride, (iv) impregnating the activated                carrier with the precursor solution so that the                total supported mass of cupric oxide and                manganese(IV) oxide on the activated carrier                reaches 4.0-6.0% by the same volume impregnation                method, where impregnating process is carried out                by ultrasonically dispersing for 30-40 minutes, and                then maintaining for 8-12 hours and aging in a                protective atmosphere at 400-450 degrees C for                2-2.5 hours, and cooling to room temperature to                obtain low-temperature desulfurization denitration                catalyst. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                low-temperature integrated desulfurization and                denitration method for flue gas using                low-temperature desulfurization and denitration                catalyst, which involves (a) placing                low-temperature desulfurization and denitration                catalyst in a desulfurization and denitration                reactor, spraying ammonia gas into the flue gas to                be treated, desulfurizing and denitrifying the flue                gas carrying the ammonia gas at a space velocity of                4000-5000 hour-1 through the catalyst from bottom                to top at a reaction temperature of 130-160 degrees                C, (b) feeding deactivated low-temperature                desulfurization and denitration catalyst into a                regeneration reactor, introducing ammonia into the                regeneration reactor, regenerating the catalyst at                200-240 degrees C, recycling the regenerated                catalyst to the desulfurization and denitration                reaction of step (i).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The particle size of                  semi-coke particles is 8-16 mesh. The calcium                  oxide powder has particle size of 200-300 mesh.                  Preferred Composition: The volume ratio of steam                  to carbon monoxide in the mixed gas is                  (3-4):(6-7). Preferred Conditions: The mixed gas                  is introduced at a flow rate of 40-60 ml/minute                  per 1 g of mixture. The drying temperature in                  step (ii) and step (iv) is 105-115 degrees C. In                  step (a), the low-temperature desulfurization                  denitration catalyst is placed in a fixed bed in                  desulfurization-denitration reactor where the                  packing density of the catalyst in the fixed bed                  is 540-560 kg/m3. The deactivation of the                  low-temperature desulfurization and denitration                  reactor is judged when the desulfurization and                  denitrification reactor desulfurization                  efficiency is less than 70%. In step (ii), the                  space velocity of the regeneration reaction in                  the regeneration reactor is 800-1500 hour-                  1.

PT P
PN US2022307369-A1
TI Method for integrating measurements of subsurface            fluid pressure information of different types and from            diverse sources, involves identifying a set of            geological boundaries for a group of multiple wells in            a region that includes a reference well.
AU STEWART S A
AE SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
GA 2022C46840
AB    NOVELTY - The method (400) involves identifying a set of                geological boundaries for a group of wells in a                region that includes a reference well (402), where                each well of the group of wells comprises                geological boundaries at different depths from same                geological boundaries in the reference well. The                geological boundaries of the group of wells                including identifying tops and bases of geological                layers for each well of the group of wells are                matched (404). Depth shifts for depths comprising                corresponding pressure data are determined (406)                using the matched geological boundaries, where the                depth shifts are redatumed based on differences in                depth of the geological layers of each well. A                display of a graph mapping pressures of the group                of wells at different depths is presented (408) in                a graphical user interface (GUI) based on                redatuming of each well of the group of wells to a                datum of the reference well.
   USE - Method for effectively integrating                measurements of subsurface pore fluid pressure                information of different types from diverse sources                i.e. deep wells, for subsurface projects. Uses                include but are not limited to oil and gas                exploration, geothermal energy, water extraction,                and geological carbon dioxide sequestration                applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables effectively integrating                measurements of subsurface fluid pressure                information of different datum types and from                diverse sources into a single geological reference                frame, thus revealing key information that supports                well design and project commerciality for oil and                gas, carbon dioxide sequestration, geothermal, and                water management projects.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The pressures are measured pore fluid                pressures. The set of geological boundaries define                a porous layer that varies in depth for each well                of the group of wells.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (a) Non-transitory computer-readable medium                comprising a set of instructions for effectively                integrating measurements of subsurface pore fluid                pressure information of different types from                diverse sources;
   (b) System for effectively integrating                measurements of subsurface pore fluid pressure                information of different types from diverse                sources.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating                a method for effectively integrating measurements                of subsurface pore fluid pressure information of                different types from diverse sources.
   400Method for effectively integrating                measurements of subsurface pore fluid pressure                information of different types from diverse                sources
   402Identifying set of geological boundaries                for group of wells in region that includes                reference well
   404Matching geological boundaries of group of                wells including identifying tops and bases of                geological layers for each well of group of                wells
   406Determining depth shifts for depths                comprising corresponding pressure data using                matched geological boundaries

AE SICHUAN XINGSHENG LITHIUM CO LTD (SICH-Non-standard)
GA 2022807168
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing anhydrous lithium hydroxide involves charging, putting lithium hydroxide monohydrate into storage hopper, drying, adding the lithium hydroxide monohydrate in storage hopper into the vertical disc drying machine through the feeding spiral pipe, and the lithium hydroxide monohydrate in the vertical tray dryer, the indirect heat exchange is carried out with the heating medium, so that the monohydrate lithium hydroxide absorbs the hot water to evaporate to obtain anhydrous lithium hydroxide. The anhydrous lithium hydroxide is cooled by the water-cooled jacketed screw conveyor, removed iron from the cooled anhydrous lithium hydroxide by an iron remover.
   USE - Method for preparing anhydrous lithium hydroxide used for lithium-based lubricating grease preparation alkaline storage battery electrolyte solution and carbon dioxide absorbent.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has small labour intensity, ensures the continuous production of anhydrous lithium hydroxide, increased yield, and reduces energy consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing anhydrous lithium hydroxide involves charging, putting lithium hydroxide monohydrate into storage hopper, drying, adding the lithium hydroxide monohydrate in storage hopper into the vertical disc drying machine through the feeding spiral pipe, and the lithium hydroxide monohydrate in the vertical tray dryer, the indirect heat exchange is carried out with the heating medium, so that the monohydrate lithium hydroxide absorbs the hot water to evaporate to obtain anhydrous lithium hydroxide. The anhydrous lithium hydroxide is cooled by the water-cooled jacketed screw conveyor, removed iron from the cooled anhydrous lithium hydroxide by an iron remover, bagged the anhydrous lithium hydroxide after iron removal, sieved, loaded the ton bag of anhydrous lithium hydroxide into the linear vibrating screen storage hopper, and sieved out large pieces of material for bagging. The fine materials after sieving are removed by the electromagnetic iron remover to obtain high-magnetic anhydrous lithium hydroxide from the magnetic discharge port of electromagnetic iron remover. The rest material is low magnetic anhydrous lithium hydroxide.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an anhydrous lithium hydroxide continuous production device, which comprises a storage hopper, vertical disc drying machine, water cooling jacket spiral conveye, iron remover, linear vibration sieve bin and an electromagnetic iron remove, the discharge hole of the storage hopper is connected with the feeding port of the vertical disc drying machine through the feeding spiral pipe, the discharging port of the vertical disc drying machine is connected with the feeding port of the water cooling jacket spiral conveyer through a discharging pipe, the iron removing device is installed on the discharging end of the water cooling jacket spiral conveyer, the linear vibration sieve bin is used for sieving the material after removing iron into large block and fine material.

GA 2022652414
AB    NOVELTY - A sintered container comprises a container                body (1) and a container cover for snapping and                covering the container body. The container body                includes an open first box (11), and an open second                box (12) arranged in the open first box and shares                the same bottom plate (110) with the open first                box. The side wall (111) of open first box, the                side wall of open second box (121) and the bottom                plate enclose a groove. The container cover                includes a top plate, and a side wall provided with                through-holes. The side wall and the top plate                enclose an open accommodating cavity used for                accommodating the container body. The total area of                through-holes is 5-20% with respect to the total                area of the side wall of the container cover. The                container body and container cover are made of                aluminum oxide.
   USE - Container used for sintering                lithium-lanthanum-zirconium oxide solid electrolyte                material (claimed) used in lithium-ion                battery.
   ADVANTAGE - The sintering container has the function of                adjusting internal atmosphere, and removing the                atmosphere not required for the reaction on the                premise of maintaining the required atmosphere                inside the sintering container, so that the                sintering reaction generates pure phase. The gas                generated by the decomposition of the raw material                can pass through the through-hole on the side wall                of the container cover to avoid impurities that                affect the sintering effect. The sintering                container can discharge the atmosphere (carbon                dioxide and water) not needed for the reaction and                can also fill the atmosphere (e.g.                lithium-containing oxide) needed for the reaction                in a certain space, to ensure the smooth progress                of the reaction and effectively solves the problem                of sintering of the existing crucible. If the                bottom powder is sintered with a cover, impurity                will be generated due to insufficient                volatilization of water and carbon dioxide, hence,                open sintering is performed to volatilize surface                powder too quickly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for sintering                of lithium-lanthanum-zirconium oxide solid                electrolyte material, which involves placing the                mixed powder of lanthanum source and zirconium                source or the mixed powder of lanthanum source,                zirconium source and doping source in the second                box of container body, placing lithium source in                the groove, buckling the container cover to cover                the container body, and sintering at a certain                temperature.

TI New brominated iridium complex used as photosensitizer in photocatalytic reduction reaction of carbon dioxide, and for organometallic chemistry applications.
AU ZHANG M
   RUAN Q
   DENG Y
   LUN Z
   CHEN M
   GUAN Y
   TAN G
AE UNIV DONGGUAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDU-C)
GA 2022049813
AB    NOVELTY - A brominated iridium (III) complex (1), is new.
   USE - New compound (1) used as photosensitizer in photocatalytic reduction reaction of carbon dioxide, and for organometallic chemistry applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The compound (1) has excellent blue light absorption ability, and excellent efficiency of converting photon energy into chemical reaction energy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A brominated iridium (III) complex of formula (1), is new.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included forpreparation of the compound (1), which involves placing polished magnesium in 5 mL dry tetrahydrofuran organic solvent under the protection of nitrogen, dripping bromothiophene dissolved in 20 mL of tetrahydrofuran under stirring for 2 hours, cooling to -78degrees Celsius with liquid nitrogen-acetone bath, dripping 15 mL tetrahydrofuran solution of trimethyl borate at low temperature, stirring to return to room temperature, then carrying out acidification and hydrolysis, and then washing with saturated brine, evaporating and concentrating, and finally adding n-hexane for recrystallization to obtain (thiophen-2-yl)boronic acid, dissolving 2-bromopyridine and (thiophen-2-yl)boronic acid in tetrahydrofuran, adding tetrakistriphenylphosphine palladium and potassium carbonate solution, Suzuki coupling at 75degrees Celsius for 48 hours under the protection of nitrogen, and extracting the reaction solution with ethyl acetate, washing with saturated brine, concentrating and evaporating with n-hexane as eluent, silica gel column separating and purifying, extracting with ethyl acetate, washing with saturated brine, concentrating and evaporating with n-hexane and ethyl acetate as eluent to obtain light yellow crystal of 2-(thiophen-2-yl)pyridine as intermediate product, adding 2-(thiophen-2-yl)pyridine to 45 ml chloroform to dissolve, adding N-brominated succinimide every 3 minutes to chloroform in small amounts and multiple times in an ice-water bath, reacting in dark under stirring for 8 hours in the dark under the conditions, drying the reaction solution a rotary evaporator, using n-hexane as the eluent, separating and purifying to obtain a white powdery solid of 2-(5-bromothiophen-2-yl)pyridine as intermediate product, adding 2-(5-bromothiophen-2-yl)pyridine and hydrated iridium trichloride to a mixed solution of 12 mL of ethylene glycol ether and water, heating to 90degrees Celsius under nitrogen protection, and stirring for 12 hours to obtain tetrabromo-dispiro(dichlora-2,4-diiridacyclobutane-tetrakis(thia-aza-iridatricyclo)-dodecane)-icosaene, adding tetrabromo-dispiro(dichlora-2,4-diiridacyclobutane-tetrakis(thia-aza-iridatricyclo)-dodecane)-icosaene and acetylacetone to sodium carbonate in 15 mL of ethylene glycol ether, heating to 100degrees Celsius, reacting for 10 hours under nitrogen, extracting the reaction solution with dichloromethane, washing with saturated aqueous sodium chloride solution, concentrating and evaporating, using n-hexane and dichloromethane as eluents, and the product was separated and purified by silica gel chromatography to obtain a red solid product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The acidification hydrolysis involves adding 10% hydrochloric acid to pH 1-2.Preferred Composition: The addition ratio of the 2-bromopyridine and 2-thiopheneboronic acid is 1:2. The ratio of the n-hexane to ethyl acetate is 6:1. The ratio of the ethylene glycol ether to water is 3:1. The ratio of the n-hexane to dichloromethane is 1:2.

PN LU500173-B1
TI Fresh-keeping used for inhibiting apple browning and prolonging storage time, by sprinkling 1-methylcyclopropene aqueous solution, transporting apples to storage place, cutting and soaking in calcium chloride solution, packing with foam net, placing gauze-wrapped soda lime in bag, and cooling fruit.
AU WANG Z
   JIA C
   WANG W
   JIANG Y
AE CHINESE ACAD AGRIC SCI POMOLOGY RES INST (CAGS-C)
GA 2021F08700
AB    NOVELTY - Fresh-keeping method for inhibiting apple browning and prolonging the storage time involves (i) pre-harvesting by sprinkling 1-methylcyclopropene (MCP) aqueous solution, (ii) picking ripe apples of uniform size and peel color, (iii) transporting picked apples to the storage place, (iv) cutting the apple stems and soaking in calcium chloride solution, (v) packing by covering the fruit treated with foam net, (f) putting apples processed immediately, (vii) placing gauze-wrapped soda lime in the fresh-keeping bag, and (viii) cooling the fruit to -2 to -1 degrees C slowly for normal management, and controlling the oxygen concentration in the bag at 19-21%, carbon dioxide concentration at 0.3% or less, ethylene concentration at 5 mu l/l or less and relative humidity at 85-90%.
   USE - The fresh-keeping method for inhibiting apple browning and prolonging storage time. The apple is chosen from Mengfu apple.
   ADVANTAGE - The method maintains color, hardness, soluble solids, titratable acid, vitamin C and flavor of stored fruits, improves storability of the fruit and its commodity rate, satisfies needs of long-distance transportation and long-term storage, and reduces storage risks and costs.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Fresh-keeping method for inhibiting apple browning and prolonging the storage time involves (i) pre-harvesting by sprinkling 1-methylcyclopropene (MCP) aqueous solution in 5-7 days before apple harvest, (ii) picking 80-90% ripe apples of uniform size and peel color, free of lumps, no diseases and insect pests that are processed in step (i) in 145-155 days after the full blooming period and building a shelter in the field to protect picked apples from sunburn, (iii) transporting picked apples to the storage place as soon as possible, (iv) cutting the apple stems short and soaking in the calcium chloride solution, removing and drying stem, (v) packing by covering the fruit treated with foam net and placing in a cardboard box or plastic fruit box lined with polyethylene (PE) film fresh-keeping bag for apples, which is placed open, with ventilation holes, (f) putting apples processed immediately into the storage for pre-chill, (vii) placing the gauze-wrapped soda lime in the fresh-keeping bag after pre-chill and immediately tying the bag tightly, and (viii) cooling the fruit to -2 to -1 degrees C slowly for normal management, and controlling the oxygen concentration in the bag at 19-21%, carbon dioxide concentration at 0.3% or less, ethylene concentration at 5 mu l/l or less and relative humidity at 85-90%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The concentration of 1-MCP aqueous solution is 200-400 mu l/l. The 80-90% ripeness is defined as fruit hardness of 6 kg/cm2 or more, and the soluble solid content is 14% or more. The concentration of the calcium chloride solution is 5-7%, and the soaking time is 8-10 minutes. The pore diameter of the ventilation holes on the fresh-keeping bag is 20-40 mu l/l, the density of the ventilation holes is 1-2 holes/cm2, and the thickness of the fresh-keeping bag is 0.015-0.02 mm. The pre-chill temperature in step (f) is 6-8 degrees C, and the pre-chill time is 2-3 days. The weight of the packaged soda lime in the step (g) is 50-80 g, which absorbs carbon dioxide generated by the respiration of the fruit and prevents the fruit from being damaged by carbon dioxide. The specific operation of the slow cooling is to drop 1 degrees C every 3 days to 0 degrees C in 18-24 days, storing at 0 degrees C for 10-12 days, and dropped from -2 to -1 degrees C for normal management storage until the end. In method, the gas composition in the bag and the ambient temperature and humidity are regularly checked, the oxygen, carbon dioxide and ethylene gas composition are measured by the American F-950 analyzer, and the temperature and humidity are measured by German testo 174 thermohygrometer.
   EXAMPLE - None given.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).)
MC A04-G02E2; A12-P01A; A12-P06B; A12-W09; D03-A04
IP A23B-007/04; A23B-007/157
