PN CN113501523-A
TI Preparing superfine particle tungsten carbide e.g. used in metal cutting processing, by continuously adding tungsten trioxide to rotary furnace that is divided into six reaction temperature areas, ball milling, spraying, and carbonizing.
AU LIN Z
   LI Z
   ZHU Y
   WEI B
   ZHANG Y
AE GANZHOU JXTC SUMMIT AT & M NEW MATERIALS (GANZ-Non-standard)
GA 2021C4338L
AB    NOVELTY - The method comprises (a) continuously adding tungsten trioxide to rotary furnace that is divided into six reaction temperature areas, carrying out reduction to obtain the superfine particle tungsten powder, (b) adding into ball mill, adding ultra-high purity thermal cracking powdered carbon black and polyvinyl alcohol, (c) spraying and granulating obtained slurry, (d) adding obtained mixed material into rotary furnace, at the same time, introducing hydrogen and carbon dioxide gas into the rotary furnace for carbonization protection, dividing the rotary furnace into three reaction temperature areas, and (e) crushing the superfine tungsten carbide powder.
   USE - Method for preparing superfine particle tungsten carbide used for metal cutting processing, printed circuit board (PCB) processing, military equipment and electronics.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare superfine particle tungsten carbide powder, which has high purity, high purity thermal cracking powder carbon black and polyvinyl alcohol, and provides uniform crystal grain, and uniform granularity distribution of superfine TPC powder finished product.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The method comprises (a) continuously adding tungsten trioxide to rotary furnace at a speed of 15-75 kg/hour, and dividing the rotary furnace into six reaction temperature areas, from inlet to outlet, i.e. 580-640degrees Celsius, 640-660degrees Celsius, 670-690degrees Celsius, 690-710degrees Celsius, 880-920degrees Celsius and 950-1050degrees Celsius, controlling the furnace tube rotating speed to 2-6 revolutions/minute (rpm), controlling the flow rate of hydrogen into the rotary furnace to 200-600 m3/hour, carrying out reduction to obtain the superfine particle tungsten powder, (b) adding the superfine particle tungsten powder obtained by rotary reduction into the ball mill, and adding ultra-high purity thermal cracking powdered carbon black with 5.8-6.2 mass% superfine tungsten powder, and polyvinyl alcohol with 1.0-1.8 mass% superfine tungsten powder as binder, adding alcohol to the ball mill, ball milling and preparing carbon, obtaining slurry, (c) spraying and granulating the slurry obtained by ball milling to obtain tungsen+carbon (W+C) mixed material with uniform material particles, (d) adding W+C mixed material into the rotary furnace, at the same time, introducing hydrogen and carbon dioxide gas into the rotary furnace for carbonization protection, dividing the rotary furnace into three reaction temperature areas, controlling the temperature range of the three reaction temperature areas to be 1150-1250degrees Celsius, and controlling the feeding amount of W+C mixed material as 30-50 kg/hour, controlling the rotating speed of the furnace tube of the rotary furnace to 2.5-6 rpm, carbonizing to obtain the superfine tungsten carbide powder product, and (e) crushing the superfine tungsten carbide powder obtained by carbonization to obtain uniform grain, uniform granularity distribution of superfine tungsten carbide powder finished product.

TI Preparing graphite-phase carbon nitride            three-dimensional scaffold loaded with metal particles            used in e.g. carbon dioxide reduction, by depositing            metal particles on graphite-phase carbon nitride            (g-C3N4) three-dimensional scaffold, depositing metal            particles, and performing thermal polymerization.
AU LIN Q
   LIU M
   WU X
   DING Y
   MIAO Z
   LIANG D
   WANG Z
   WANG W
   ZHANG X
AE JINLING INST TECHNOLOGY (JINL-C)
GA 202199762N
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing a graphite-phase carbon                nitride (M/g-C3N4) three-dimensional scaffold                loaded with metal particles, involves taking                graphite-phase carbon nitride (g-C3N4)                three-dimensional scaffold, depositing metal                particles (M) on surface of g-C3N4                three-dimensional scaffold, depositing the metal                particles (M) on a three-dimensional stent formed                by foaming amino resin by magnetron sputtering, and                subjecting to high-temperature thermal                polymerization, preparably (i) uniformly mixing                amino resin, foaming agent, emulsifier and curing                agent, rinsing with 0.5% aqueous ammonia or                ammonium chloride solution, washing, and drying to                obtain amino resin three-dimensional scaffold, (ii)                installing metal (M) target in magnetron sputtering                machine, placing amino resin three-dimensional                support, and sputtering, and (iii) placing amino                resin three-dimensional scaffold sputtered with                metal (M) in a high-temperature furnace, heating,                heat-preserving, and naturally cooling.
   USE - The method is used for preparing                graphite-phase carbon nitride (M/g-C3N4)                three-dimensional scaffold loaded with metal                particles in carbon dioxide reduction,                photodecomposition hydrogen production, and                photocatalytic degradation of organic pollutant                (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enhances separation efficiency of                photogenerated carriers, and improves                photocatalytic performance of the M/gC3N4                three-dimensional scaffold.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing a graphite-phase carbon                nitride (M/g-C3N4) three-dimensional scaffold                loaded with metal particles, involves depositing                graphite-phase carbon nitride (g-C3N4)                three-dimensional scaffold and metal particles (M)                on surface of g-C3N4 three-dimensional scaffold,                depositing the metal particles (M) on a                three-dimensional stent formed by foaming amino                resin by magnetron sputtering, and subjecting to                high-temperature thermal polymerization to prepare                an M/g-C3N4 three-dimensional stent, preparably (i)                controlling temperature at 30-60 degrees C,                uniformly mixing amino resin, foaming agent,                emulsifier and curing agent, ultrasonically                treating for 30-60 minutes, placing in a microwave                foaming furnace at 70-95 degrees C for 60-120                seconds, after the volume is stable, placing in an                oven at 80-200 degrees C for 20-60 minutes to form                a three-dimensional scaffold, rinsing with 0.5%                aqueous ammonia or ammonium chloride solution,                washing with ultrapure water, and drying to obtain                amino resin three-dimensional scaffold, (ii)                installing the metal (M) target in the magnetron                sputtering machine, placing the amino resin                three-dimensional support with a thickness of 2-20                mm in the magnetron sputtering machine, and                sputtering the metal (M) on both sides, and (iii)                placing the amino resin three-dimensional scaffold                sputtered with metal (M) in a high-temperature                furnace, heating at 500-700 degrees C at a constant                speed under the protection of nitrogen,                heat-preserving for 2-5 hours, and naturally                cooling to obtain M/g-C3N4 three-dimensional                scaffold.

PN CN112516203-A
TI Preparing tongshu granule comprises e.g. adopting medicinal materials and extract using supercritical fluid extraction technology, centrifuging the extract to obtain supernatant, concentrating centrifugal supernatant, stirring and cooling.
AU FAN F
   HU R
   PENG C
   LIU H
AE JIANGXI JIMIN KEXIN PHARM CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
   JIANGXI JINSHUIBAO PHARM CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202131721D
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing tongshu granule comprises (1) adopting 1250 pts. wt. radix-polygoni multiflori, 833 pts. wt. Angelica, 833 pts. wt. radix paeoniae rubra, 833 pts. wt. fructus cannabis, 833 pts. wt. mulberry, taking medicinal materials and extract them using supercritical fluid extraction technology to obtain an extract, where the supercritical fluid extraction parameters are the extraction pressure is 25 MPa, the extraction temperature is 35 degrees C, the extraction time is 1 hour, and the carbon dioxide flow rate is 20 kg/hour, (2) centrifuging the obtained extract to obtain supernatant, where the parameters of the centrifugation are the temperature of the liquid medicine is 75-90 degrees C, the centrifugal speed is 6900 revolutions/minute, and the feed speed is 2 m3/hours, (3) concentrating the centrifugal supernatant to a relative density of 1.08 (60-70 degrees C), and cooling to room temperature, and (4) adding ethanol to make the ethanol concentration in the solution reach 60-80% and stirring.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing tongshu granule.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing tongshu granule comprises (1) adopting 1250 pts. wt. radix-polygoni multiflori, 833 pts. wt. Angelica, 833 pts. wt. radix paeoniae rubra, 833 pts. wt. fructus cannabis, 833 pts. wt. mulberry, taking medicinal materials and extract them using supercritical fluid extraction technology to obtain an extract, where the supercritical fluid extraction parameters are the extraction pressure is 25 MPa, the extraction temperature is 35 degrees C, the extraction time is 1 hour, and the carbon dioxide flow rate is 20 kg/hour, (2) centrifuging the obtained extract to obtain the supernatant, where the parameters of the centrifugation are as the temperature of the liquid medicine is 75-90 degrees C, the centrifugal speed is 6900 revolutions/minute, and the feed speed is 2 m3/hours, (3) concentrating the centrifugal supernatant to a relative density of 1.08 (60-70 degrees C), and cooling to room temperature, (4) adding ethanol to make the ethanol concentration in the solution reach 60-80%, stirring well, allowing to stand, precipitating and filtering to obtain the supernatant, (5) concentrating the supernatant into a clear cream with a relative density of 1.15-1.20 (60-70 degrees C), (6) vacuum belt drying to obtain particles, where the parameters of vacuum belt drying are the heating system temperature is 1 zone of 95 degrees C, 2 zone of 90 degrees C, 3 zone of 75 degrees C, 4 zone of 30 degrees C, feeding speed is 20 l/hours, and crawler speed is 8 HZ, and (7) adding dextrin and steviosin, and preparing into granules to obtain the product.

TI Generation of energy products for power generation, involves feeding feedstock into reactor, mixing bi-functional reforming nanocatalyst, purging nitrogen gas/an inert gas, condensing gaseous products, and regenerating used nanocatalyst.
AU DWIVEDI P
   TIWARY D
   MISHRA P K
   CHAKRABORTY J P
   KUMAR R
   SINGH N
AE INDIAN TECHNOLOGY VARANASI INST (INTE-Non-standard)
GA 202113118F
AB    NOVELTY - Generation of energy products from arranged absorbent materials involves drying and chopping/shredding a polymeric waste feedstock obtained from arranged absorbent materials for personal hygiene, feeding a predefined amount of the dried and chopped/shredded feedstock into a reactor, mixing the bi-functional reforming nanocatalyst with the feedstock in the reactor in a predefined ratio, purging the reactor using nitrogen gas/an inert gas for removing oxygen, adjusting temperature, residence time and heating rate of the reactor for pyrolytic breakdown of feedstock to obtain gaseous products and solid residue, condensing gaseous products to obtain condensed liquid products and a non-condensable portion, collecting the non-condensable portion obtained as gaseous product using a gas trapping equipment, collecting the solid residue from the reactor after cooling, and regenerating the used nanocatalyst.
   USE - Generation of energy products from arranged absorbent materials for personal hygiene such as baby diapers, adult diapers, sanitary pads, and sanitary napkins (all claimed) used as alternative fossil fuels for power generation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables generation of energy products with excellent pyrolytic conversion efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Generation of energy products from arranged absorbent materials involves drying and chopping/shredding a polymeric waste feedstock obtained from arranged absorbent materials for personal hygiene, feeding a predefined amount of the dried and chopped/shredded feedstock into a reactor, mixing the bi-functional reforming nanocatalyst with the feedstock in the reactor in a predefined ratio, purging the reactor using nitrogen gas/an inert gas for removing oxygen, adjusting temperature, residence time and heating rate of the reactor for pyrolytic breakdown of feedstock to obtain gaseous products and solid residue, condensing gaseous products to obtain condensed liquid products and a non-condensable portion, collecting the non-condensable portion obtained as gaseous product using a gas trapping equipment, collecting the solid residue from the reactor after cooling, and regenerating the used nanocatalyst, where the bi-functional reforming nanocatalyst is preferably palladium/yttrium-zeolite, the predefined ratio (weight/volume) of yttrium-zeolite support and purified palladium nanoparticle suspension is 1:10, to have a minimal palladium loading of -0.6948 wt.%, that progresses polymer conversion into gaseous products, liquid products and solid residues and reduces the emission of carbon monoxide by 4-5% and carbon dioxide by 15%, the gaseous products obtained are 64-68% and includes hydrogen, methane, nitrogen, carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide, the liquid product obtained is 12-13% and includes liquid fractions close to gasoline with upgraded octane number of hydrocarbons, the solid residue obtained is 22-23% and includes char, and the energy products obtained are hydrogen, ammonia and liquid products.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The polymeric waste feedstock obtained from adsorbent article is sodium polyacrylate (PAAS). Preferred Conditions: The adjusted temperature is 350-550 degrees C, residence time is 0-30 minutes and heating rate is from 10-60 degrees C/minute for pyrolytic breakdown of feedstock. The regeneration of used nanocatalyst is carried out by heating the obtained solid residue under nitrogen flow to a temperature of 550 degrees C or more, maintaining the temperature for 30 minutes thereafter eliminating coke by combustion at a temperature of 550 degrees C or more under oxygen flow till detecting no weight loss.

PN EP3736415-A1; WO2020229328-A1; CA3135963-A1; CN113784921-A; EP3966435-A1; US2022082032-A1; US11293310-B1; IN202117052381-A
TI Steam network assembly for industrial plant e.g. power plant, has turbines that are configured to deliver power to syngas compressor and carbon dioxide compressor in ammonia-producing unit and urea-producing unit of plant respectively.
AU FRANCEUS D
   PORRO L G
AE YARA INT ASA (YARI-C)
   YARA INT ASA (YARI-C)
   YARA INT ASA (YARI-C)
GA 2020B0200H
AB    NOVELTY - The steam network assembly has an ammonia-producing unit and a urea-producing unit. The two medium-pressure steam lines (2, 3) and a condensing-type turbine (10,11) and counter-pressure type turbine are connected to the high-pressure steam line (1) for supplying both with high-pressure steam. The condensing-type turbine is supplied with high pressure steam by the high-pressure steam line with extraction into one of the two medium-pressure steam lines (2), and configured to deliver power to a syngas compressor in the ammonia-producing unit of the plant. The counter-pressure type turbine is supplied with high-pressure steam by the high-pressure steam line with extraction connected to the two medium pressure steam lines and configured to deliver power to a carbon dioxide (CO2) compressor in the urea-producing unit of the plant.
   USE - Steam network assembly for industrial plant e.g. power plants to produce electricity.
   ADVANTAGE - The installation of devices to produce the steam is reduced, thus reducing the amount of steam extracted from the condensing turbine, and reducing the operating and maintenance cost of the plant.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The network assembly has high-pressure steam line that includes steam ranging from 8.0 MPa to 12.0 MPa, and from 9.0 MPa to 11.0 Mpa. The two medium-pressure steam lines include steam ranging from 2.0 MPa to 6.0 MPa, and from 3.0 MPa to 4.0 Mpa. The one of the two medium-pressure steam line includes steam ranging from 1.0 MPa to 5.0 MPa, and from 1.5 MPa to 3.0 MPa. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for distributing high-pressure steam in a steam network assembly for a plant involves supplying high-pressure steam from the highpressure steam line to a condensing-type turbine with extraction into one of the two medium-pressure steam lines and a counter-pressure type turbine with extraction connected to the two medium-pressure steam lines; and
   (2) a method for revamping a steam network assembly for a plant involves replacing the condensing-type steam turbine configured to deliver power to a CO2 compressor with a counter-pressure type turbine with extraction connected to the two medium-pressure steam lines and removing the condensers (20,21) connected to the condensing-type turbine.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic views of the steam network assembly.
   High-pressure steam line (1)
   Medium-pressure steam lines (2,3)
   Condensing-type turbine (10,11)
   Condensers (20,21)
   Users (40,41)

AE JIANGXI YIJIE ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION T (JIAN-Non-standard)

AB    (CN211595374-U)   NOVELTY - The utility model claims a sewage treatment device of suspension biological film and deep filtration, comprising an inclined tube settling zone, a pre-hypoxia zone, an anaerobic zone, an anoxic zone, a contact oxidation zone, a vertical flow zone, a filter cloth filtering zone, a clean water zone, an ultraviolet sterilizing zone and a device room; the water enters the inclined tube settling zone from the total water inlet, an anoxic zone, a contact oxidation zone, a vertical flow zone, a filter cloth filtering zone, a clean water zone and an ultraviolet disinfection zone; the contact oxidation zone comprises a suspension type filler, a micro-pore aeration system and a dark aeration main tube connected with the micro-pore aeration system. the sewage treatment device of the suspension biological film and deep filtration; the contact oxidation area reduces the generation amount of sludge, reduces the loading of the biological film; The microporous aeration system increases dissolved oxygen, and also takes away the carbon dioxide and other harmful gas in the water, prevents the gas from jacking the biological film because of buoyancy, so that the biological film is not easy to fall off.

The contact oxidation zone including suspended filler and a micro porous aeration system and a dark aeration main pipe is connected with the micro porous aeration system.

PN CN111471619-A
TI Culture medium for laboratory separation and culture of Avibacterium paragallinarum used for detection of infectious rhinitis infection in poultry, comprises chocolate agar medium includinga nutrient agar medium, healthy chicken whole blood.
AU ZHENG Y
   SUN S
   WANG H
   SHEN Y
   NING J
   HOU L
   WANG Y
   CUI G
AE BEIJING POULTRY BREEDING CO LTD (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 202081251L
AB    NOVELTY - Culture medium comprises chocolate agar medium including 100 pts. wt. nutrient agar medium, 10 pts. wt. healthy chicken whole blood. The chocolate agar medium is prepared by sterilizing the prepared nutrient agar medium at high temperature and heat it at 70-85 degrees C, adding healthy chicken whole blood to the nutrient agar medium, heating at 70-85 degrees C for 5-10 minutes, and shaking to obtain healthy chicken whole blood culture. A sterile plate is prepared using the medium added with healthy chicken whole blood to obtain chocolate agar medium.
   USE - Culture medium for laboratory isolation and culture of Avibacterium paragallinarum used for detection of infectious rhinitis infection in poultry (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The culture medium brings great convenience to the preparation of the culture medium for separating and cultivating Avibacterium paragallinarum, and reduces the cost input of the poultry breeding industry.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for preparing a culture medium for laboratory separation and culture of Avibacterium paragallinarum, which involves manually collecting aseptic blood under the wings of healthy chickens, collecting hole blood of healthy chickens and using immediately, preparing hocolate agar medium by sterilizing the prepared nutrient agar medium at high temperature and heating at 70-85 degrees C, adding 10 pts. wt. healthy chicken whole blood to the nutrient agar medium, heating at 70-85 degrees C for 5-10 minutes, shaking well while heating to obtain a medium with healthy chicken whole blood, preparing sterile plate using the medium added with healthy chicken whole blood to obtain chocolate agar medium; and
   (2) a method for laboratory isolation and culture of Avibacterium paragallinarum, which involves aseptically taking one of the infraorbital sinus secretion and the nasal sinus secretion of the suspected chicken as a collected sample, taking sample-inoculation loop collected and inoculating on a chocolate agar medium plate, and incubating in a 37 degrees C, 5% CO2 carbon dioxide incubator for 18-24 hours, and cultivating strain A, cross-stitching the strain A and the pre-culturing staphylococci on the blood agar medium plate at the same time, and cultivating for 18-24 hours in a 37 degrees C, 5%CO2 carbon dioxide incubator, detecting whether there are satellites around the staphylococcal colonies on the blood agar medium plate, if the satellite phenomenon appears on the blood agar medium plate, indicates that the strain A cultivated is Avibacterium paragallinarum, which proves that avian infectious rhinitis has occurred in the poultry, and if there is no satellite phenomenon on the blood agar medium plate, which indicates strain A cultivated is not Avibacterium paragallinarum and no avian infectious rhinitis infection in poultry.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The whole blood of the healthy chicken is obtained by artificially performing sterile blood under the wings of healthy chickens and healthy chicken whole blood is used in a current way. The nutrient agar medium is prepared by dividing raw material composition ratio, mixing and sterilizing at 121 degrees C for 15-20 minutes. The laboratory isolation and culture of Avibacterium paragallinarum is performed by inoculating Avibacterium paragallinarum on a chocolate agar medium plate, and culturing for 18-24 hours in a 37 degrees C, 5% CO2 carbon dioxide incubator, and culturing Avibacterium paragallinarum. Preferred Composition: The nutrient agar medium comprises 9.0-11.0g/L peptone, 2.5-3.5g/L beef extract powder, 4.0-5.5g/L sodium chloride, and 11.0-17.0g/L agar.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); C06 (Biotechnology - including plant genetics and veterinary vaccines.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

PN CN111289639-A
TI Determining hexavalent chromium in solid waste            leaching solution involves preparing solid waste            leaching solution by weighing solid waste, and placing            in sodium hypochlorite with a mass concentration in the            solution bottle.
AU ZHONG G
   HU Z
   ZHOU Q
   TU X
AE UNIV GUANGXI (UYGU-C)
GA 2020563847
AB    NOVELTY - Determining hexavalent chromium in solid waste                leaching solution involves preparing solid waste                leaching solution by weighing 4.5-5.5 g solid                waste, and placing in sodium hypochlorite with a                mass concentration of 8.0-12% in the solution                bottle, then shaking at 55-70 degrees C for 70-90                minutes, filtering and taking the supernatant,                adding the leaching solution to a small test tube,                and then placing into a headspace bottle with a pH                of 1.50 tetrapotassium oxalate solution, the                headspace bottle, and inverting and shaking to                fully mix the leaching solution with tetrapotassium                oxalate, placing the sealed headspace bottle in the                headspace gas chromatograph and measure the carbon                dioxide signal value of the sample, where the                working conditions of the headspace autosampler                includes oven temperature 50-100 degrees C, sample                circuit temperature of 60-80 degrees C, conveyor                temperature of 70-90 degrees C, interval time 3-5                minutes, column temperature (GS-Q, Agilent, US)                30-40 degrees C, and operating time 2-4                minutes.
   USE - Method for determining hexavalent chromium in                solid waste leaching solution.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to determine hexavalent                chromium in solid waste leaching solution has good                reproduction quality, high accuracy, recovery rate                is between 107-92.5%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Determining hexavalent chromium in solid waste                leaching solution involves preparing solid waste                leaching solution by weighing 4.5-5.5 g solid                waste, and placing in sodium hypochlorite with a                mass concentration of 8.0-12% in the solution                bottle, then shaking at 55-70 degrees C for 70-90                minutes, filtering and taking the supernatant,                adding the leaching solution to a small test tube,                and then placing into a headspace bottle with a pH                of 1.50 tetrapotassium oxalate solution, the                headspace bottle, and inverting and shaking to                fully mix the leaching solution with tetrapotassium                oxalate, placing the sealed headspace bottle in the                headspace gas chromatograph and measure the carbon                dioxide signal value of the sample, where the                working conditions of the headspace autosampler                includes oven temperature 50-100 degrees C, sample                circuit temperature of 60-80 degrees C, conveyor                temperature of 70-90 degrees C, interval time 3-5                minutes, column temperature (GS-Q, Agilent, US)                30-40 degrees C, and operating time 2-4 minutes,                used pure nitrogen as the carrier gas for gas                chromatography with a flow rate of 20-30mL/minutes,                calculated the hexavalent chromium, where A6 and A7                are the chromatographic signals generated by 6                minutes and 7 minutes, S and a respectively Based                on the slope and intercept of the calibration curve                of the standard dichromate solution, the average                value of k is 0.954.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The S and a are based                  on the slope and intercept of the calibration                  curve of the standard dichromate solution, make a                  peak database of 0-100mg/L low concentration                  hexavalent chromium standard solution, make a                  peak database of 0-10000mg/L high concentration                  hexavalent chromium standard solution. The                  standard solutions with different Cr(VI) contents                  are measured by headspace gas chromatography to                  obtain calibration curves of low concentration                  and high concentration respectively. By linear                  regression fitting the above standard curves, the                  following equations are obtained respectively,                  namely: A=1634( divide by 43)m-246( divide by                  239)(n=7, R2=0.997), A=1112( divide by                  13)m+10021( divide by 5552)(n=8, R2=0.999) are                  linear regression equations for the standard                  curve of low content of 0-100 mg/L and high                  content of 0-10000 mg/L, respectively. The                  retention time of the carbon dioxide peak                  measured by the headspace gas chromatograph is                  1-3 minutes. The solid waste sample is                  pretreated: the fresh solid waste sample                  retrieved is air-dried at room temperature to                  remove visible plant debris, stones and                  microplastic debris, ground and sieved through                  2mm. The solid waste includes tanning sludge,                  chromium contaminated soil, electroplating waste,                  and printing and dyeing sludge.

TI Method for producing electric power and synthesis gas from coal fuel by emission-free coal-nuclear synergy technology, involves carrying out coal fuel gasification and high-temperature electrolysis sub-processes in emission-free technology.
AU WRZESINSKI Z
AE WRZESINSKI Z (WRZE-Individual)
GA 202044021S
AB    NOVELTY - The method (11-00) involves producing coal fuel (11-15) gasification sub-process (11-10) in a gasifier (11-16). A thermal energy recovery sub-process (11-20) is performed in which high pressure steam (6) is generated. A sub-process (11-30) of purification of the gas generated in the coal fuel gasification sub process, is performed in at least one gasifier. A high-temperature electrolysis sub-process (111-00) is performed, in which an oxygen for supplying the coal fuel gasification sub-process is produced in the electrochemical electrolysis reaction. A carbon monoxide (CO) is produced in the cool fuel gasification sub-process. A separate sub-process (11-40) is performed for producing synthesis gas, which is a mixture of two gaseous components such as the carbon monoxide and a hydrogen (H). The coal fuel gasification sub-process and the high-temperature electrolysis sub-process are carried out in emission-free technology, without emission of any carbon dioxide (CO2).
   USE - Method (UPW) 11-00 for producing electric power and synthesis gas in the methane oxidation sub-process (IV-00) by catalytic partial oxidation (CPOx) method from coal fuel by emission- free coal-nuclear synergy technology or the Fischer-Tropsch process (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The combustion or gasification of a coal in pure oxygen ensures much higher efficiency of coal-fired power plant, and much smaller amount of exhaust gas that amount is even three times lower in relation to the amount of exhaust gas produced during combustion of a coal fuel in the air, and the amount of exhausted harmful nitrogen oxides is practically reduced to zero. The high performance and efficiency of the process are ensured and fuel costs are reduced with simultaneously reduced amount of harmful substances produced as by-products, such as compounds containing sulphur and nitrogen oxides, and reduced carbon dioxide emission.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system for producing electric power and a synthesis gas from a coal fuel by the method for producing electric power and a synthesis gas from a coal fuel by the emission-free coal-nuclear synergy technology.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the system for generating electric power and a synthesis gas.

   Separate sub-process block (11-40)
   High-temperature electrolysis sub-process block (111-00)

The synthesis gas is hydrocarbon gas (CnH2n+n), where n is natural number, preferably 1 to 42.

PT P
PN CN111072097-A
TI Treatment device used for removing organic matter in chlorine-containing wastewater includes liquid inlet pipe, ozone generating device, ring-shaped UV lamp, stirring paddle, ring-shaped anode electrode, liquid outlet pipe, and casing.
AU WANG Y
   FAN X
AE BEIJING BGJY ENVIRONMENT TECHNOLOGY CO (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 202038044F
AB    NOVELTY - Treatment device comprises liquid inlet pipe (1), ozone generating device (2), ring-shaped UV lamp (3), stirring paddle (4), ring-shaped anode electrode (5), liquid outlet pipe, casing (8), and cathode electrode. The casing has on upper side portion provided with liquid inlet pipe, has middle side portion provided with liquid outlet pipe, and top portion provided with ozone generating device having lower portion provided with ozone exhaust pipe extending to middle and lower portion of casing. The ozone exhaust pipe has lower end uniformly distributed with ozone exhaust holes, and has outer side provided with stirring paddle above ozone exhaust hole. The ring-shaped UV lamp is installed on top of casing and outside of ozone exhaust pipe. The ring-shaped anode electrode surrounds inner wall of casing, while cathode electrode is internally provided at center of bottom of casing.
   USE - The treatment device is used for removing organic matter in chlorine-containing wastewater.
   ADVANTAGE - The device carries all reaction steps, thus saving floor space and conveying period. It improves organic oxidation efficiency via oxidation and hotocatalysis. It completely oxidizes organic matter into water and carbon dioxide to achieve removal of organic matter, while reducing electro-oxidation period and saving power consumption. The electro-oxidation electrode can effectively improve oxidation effect of organic matter, while avoiding consumption and corrosion of electrode.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for treatment method for removing organic matter in chlorine-containing wastewater by adding titanium dioxide to casing; filtering chlorine-containing wastewater containing high-concentration organic matter; turning on ozone generating device and stirring paddle to produce ozone bubbles that fully contact with solution; turning on ring-shaped UV lamp to prduce high energy photons to excite titanium dioxide to generate hydroxyl groups and hydrogen peroxide; turning on power of ring-shaped anode electrode and cathode electrode to quickly oxidize organic substances that cannot be oxidatively decomposed by ozone and photocatalysis into carbon dioxide and water, while discharging generated chloride ions as chlorine gas, which is hydrolyzed to form hypochlorous acid and hypochlorite after hydrolysis; turning off ozone generator, stirring paddle, ring-shaped UV lamp, ring-shaped anode electrode and cathode electrode; standing for 5-100 minutes to precipitate titanium dioxide in solution; and slowly discharging treated solution that meets requirements for organic matter removal and meets three-level standard of comprehensive sewage discharge.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of treatment device.
   Liquid inlet pipe (1)
   Ozone generating device (2)
   Ring-shaped uv lamp (3)
   Stirring paddle (4)
   Ring-shaped anode electrode (5)
   Casing (8)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The stirring blade is operated at 10-1000 revolutions per minute. The ring-shaped UV lamp is operated at 5-500 W at wavelength of 254-365 nm. The ring-shaped anode electrode and cathode electrode have votage controlled at 1-10 V. Preferred Component: The titanium dioxide has particle size of 0.1-100 nm and is added at 0.1-50 mass% of treatment solution.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Component: The ring-shaped anode electrode is ruthenium-iridium-plated ring-shaped titanium electrode. The cathode electrode is small protruding ruthenium-iridium-plated titanium electrode.

PN IN201811031862-A
TI Fly ash based geopolymer concrete pavement used for making e.g. concrete pavement/roads, paver blocks, paving tiles/blocks, pothole repair and precast slabs, comprises wearing surface layer and dry lean geopolymer concrete sub-base layer.
AU MALIK M
   SAXENA S K
   SATYA P
   SRIVASTAVA P
   SONI N
   SINGH B
   GANESAN I
   ATHAR H
   PASWAN R
   RAHMAN M R
   DESHWAL S
   KHAN J
   SINGH S K
AE NTPC LTD (NTPC-Non-standard)
   COUNCIL SCI & IND RES INDIA (COUI-C)
GA 202020196A
AB    NOVELTY - Fly ash based geopolymer concrete pavement comprises wearing surface layer and dry lean geopolymer concrete sub-base layer.
   USE - The fly ash based geopolymer concrete pavement useful for making concrete pavement/roads, paver blocks, paving tiles/blocks, white topping, pothole repair, precast slabs and other items like cement concrete (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The pavement has high early flexural strength, less shrinkage, excellent workability properties, adequate durability in aggressive environment compared to ordinary Portland cement and excellent heat resistance, and does not require Portland cement, water curing and temperature curing, reduces carbon dioxide emission in comparison to high carbon dioxide emission in cement, provides rapid controllable setting and better hardening properties, utilizes bulk fly ash, and is stable even at 800 degrees C.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a process for preparing the fly ash based geopolymer concrete pavement, involving (a) grinding fly ash and residue in a grinder to obtain a powder mix, (b) mixing activating solution with superplasticizer, and stirring to obtain a solution, (c) mixing the powder mix, solution, aggregates, chemical admixtures, fibers, and shrinkage controlling agent in a mixer to obtain a geopolymer concrete pave mix, (d) setting a steel rail framework over a uniformly compacted subgrade, and laying a dry lean geopolymer concrete by vibro-compaction, (e) applying polythene separation layer over the dry lean geopolymer concrete, (f) placing dowel bar chairs at the location of transverse joint, and pouring the geopolymer concrete pave mix, (g) spreading and compacting the geopolymer concrete pave mix using a vibrator to obtain a uniform cast slab surface, (h) texturing and spraying a evaporation reducer solution onto the cast slab surface, (i) covering the cast slab surface with a moist textile sheet or plastic sheet, and curing at room temperature, and (j) using saw cutting transverse and horizontal joints, and filling a sealing compound to obtain the concrete pavement; and
   (2) a fly ash based geopolymer concrete pavement mix comprising the wearing surface layer composition comprising wearing surface layer comprises 10-15 wt.% fly ash, 4-8 wt.% residue, 7-10 wt.% activating solution, 0.1-0.2 wt.% superplasticizer, 74-78 wt.% aggregates, 0.15-0.18 wt.% chemical admixtures, 0.01-0.02 wt.% fibers, 0.06-0.08 wt.% shrinkage controlling agent and 0.6-1 wt.% water, and the dry lean geopolymer concrete sub-base layer comprising 10-15 wt.% fly ash, 2-4 wt.% residue, 5-7 wt.% activating solution, 74-80 wt.% aggregates and 0.5-0.7 wt.% water.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The fly ash is low calcium fly ash comprising 0.7-4 wt.% calcium oxide. The aggregates comprise a coarse aggregate and fine aggregate in a weight ratio of 2-3. The chemical admixtures comprise 0.15-0.18 wt.% sodium silicofluoride and 0.15-0.18 wt.% boric acid. The concrete pave mix comprises 15-20 wt.% powder mix, 0.1-0.2 wt.% superplasticizer, 7-10 wt.% activating solution, 74-78 wt.% aggregates, 0.15-0.18 wt.% chemical admixtures, 0.01-0.02 wt.% fibers and 0.06-0.08 wt.% shrinkage controlling agent. The dry lean geopolymer concrete comprises 10-15 wt.% fly ash, 2-4 wt.% residue, 5-7 wt.% activating solution, 74-80 wt.% coarse and fine aggregates and 0.5-0.7 wt.% water Preferred Components: The residue is a ground granulated blast furnace residue. The shrinkage controlling agents are the chemicals as per AS 1478 Type SN. The sealing compound is polysulfide sealant. Preferred Conditions: The fly ash and residue is mixed in a ratio of 80:20, 70:30, 60:40 or 50:50. 18. In step (a), the grinding is carried out for 2-10 minutes. In step (c), mixing is carried out for 2-10 minutes. In step (e), the polythene separation layer is applied over the dry lean geopolymer concrete for 2-7 days. In step (g), the spreading and compacting the geopolymer concrete pave mix is carried out for 1-3 minutes. In step (i), the curing is carried out for 5-10 hours. Preferred Method: The fly ash is obtained by grinding to retain less than 34 wt.% on a 45 mu m sieve. The activating solution is prepared by mixing sodium hydroxide and sodium silicate in a ratio of 1:1-1:3. The chemical admixtures are obtained by mixing sodium silicofluoride and boric acid. The step (f) involves pouring the geopolymer concrete pave mix with the help of a tipper, vibrator which is a needle and plate vibrator and grinder which is a ball mill. The texturing is done using a fiber broom, and spraying the evaporation reducer solution using a hand held sprayer. Preferred Properties: The powder mix has an average particle size 7-25 mu m.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The fiber comprises polypropylene fibers, polyester fibers and/or their mixture. The superplasticizer is a polycarboxylate-based superplasticizer.

PN CN110706753-A
TI Construction of n-nonane low-temperature            combustion mechanism model for predicting value of            combustion characteristics of n-nonane in aero-engine            comprises using ReaxGen program and AramcoMech 1.3            combustion core mechanism.
AU GUO J
   PENG W
   ZHANG S
   LEI J
   JING J
   LIU M
AE GUIZHOU TECHNOLOGY INST (GZTI-C)
GA 2020082182
AB    NOVELTY - Construction of n-nonane low-temperature                combustion mechanism model comprises using ReaxGen                program to build combustion mechanism model for C5                and higher hydrocarbon systems and combining with                AramcoMech 1.3 combustion core mechanism to obtain                class I model, performing simulation prediction                experiment of shock retardation of shock tube,                comparing obtained combustion characteristics data                with n-nonane combustion value, eliminating                reaction model corresponding to value to obtain                class II model; performing simulation prediction                experiment of species concentration curve,                obtaining data on changes in n-nonane, carbon                monoxide, methane, carbon dioxide, hydrogen, and                water, and eliminating reaction type with small                difference to obtain class III model; adopting                reaction path flow analysis for class III model,                obtaining second-order interaction coefficients of                each reaction, constructing third-order model; and                performing ignition delay sensitivity analysis and                determining core reaction network.
   USE - The method is for construction of n-nonane                low-temperature combustion mechanism model for                predicting value of combustion characteristics of                n-nonane in aero-engine (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses automatic generation program                ReaxGen of hydrocarbon fuel mechanism coupled with                combustion core mechanism AramcoMech 1.3 as                high-carbon hydrocarbon mechanism model, being                optimized, to fully refine model of low-temperature                combustion of n-nonane.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The method comprises using ReaxGen program to                build combustion mechanism model for C5 and higher                hydrocarbon systems, combining with AramcoMech 1.3                combustion core mechanism to obtain preliminary low                temperature combustion optimization model of                n-nonane, and optimizing preliminarily to obtain                class I model of low-temperature combustion                mechanism of n-nonane; performing simulation                prediction experiment of shock retardation of shock                tube using non-nonane low-temperature combustion                mechanism I model, comparing obtained combustion                characteristics data with n-nonane combustion                value, eliminating reaction model corresponding to                value that has large deviation from n-nonane                combustion value, to obtain class II model;                performing simulation prediction experiment of                species concentration curve of jet stirrer using                class II model, obtaining data on changes in                n-nonane, carbon monoxide, methane, carbon dioxide,                hydrogen, and water during reaction, comparing                obtained data with corresponding values in                combustion of n-nonane, and eliminating reaction                type with small difference in corresponding                numerical value from existing low-temperature                combustion mechanism to obtain class III model;                adopting reaction path flow analysis for class III                model, obtaining second-order interaction                coefficients of each reaction, constructing                third-order model of n-nonane low-temperature                combustion skeleton mechanism by second-order                interaction coefficients; and performing ignition                delay sensitivity analysis of n-nonane low                temperature combustion skeleton mechanism III model                to obtain sensitivity coefficients of each                reaction, and determining core reaction network in                III model by difference in sensitivity                coefficients.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Method: The preliminary                  low-temperature combustion model of n-nonane                  includes paraffin reaction types comprising                  addition of alkyl with air molecules;                  isomerization reaction of alkyl peroxy radical;                  alkyl peroxy radical reaction with hydrogen                  dioxide and hydrogen peroxide; oxygen                  homocracking reaction of peroxane; alkoxy                  cleavage reaction; hydroperoxyalkyl and air                  addition reaction; beta -cleavage reaction of                  peroxyalkyl groups forming smaller alkyl groups;                  addition reaction of peroxyalkyl and air;                  tetraoxyalkyl isomerization reaction;                  diperoxyalkyloxy oxygen homogeneous reaction;                  peroxocyclic ether formation reaction;                  peroxyalkyl ketone cleavage reaction;                  carbon-based cleavage reaction; hydrogen                  extraction reaction of cyclic ether; cleavage                  reaction of peroxycyclic ether; hydrogen                  extraction reaction of aldehyde and ketone; and                  free radical cleavage reaction of ketone.

PN CN110699321-A; CN110699321-B
TI Inducing neural stem cells for differentiating            into neurons, by performing neural stem cell primary            culture using embryonic rat brain tissue, subculturing            neural stem cell, collecting neurosphere, and inducing            differentiation.
AU ZHOU Q
   HAN Y
   ZHOU Y
   LIU L
   YANG N
   WANG Y
   LIANG P
AE UNIV XINYANG NORMAL (UXYN-C)
GA 202009726U
AB    NOVELTY - Method for inducing neural stem cells for                differentiating into neurons in vitro, involves (a)                performing primary culture of neural stem cells                under aseptic conditions using embryonic rat brain                tissue of 14-15 days pregnant ICR rats as raw                materials, (b) subculturing neural stem cells by                collecting neurospheres, digesting with Accutase                (RTM: natural enzyme mixture with proteolytic and                collagenolytic enzyme activity), and adding                Dulbecco's modified Eagle medium (DMEM)/F12 medium                for terminating reaction, and (c) inducing                differentiation of neural stem cell, preparing                single cell suspension by digesting the fourth or                fifth generation neurospheres, plating on a 12-well                cell culture plate coated with poly ornithine and                laminin, culturing overnight, and adding 1 ml                differentiation medium for performing                differentiation culture the next day by replacing                fresh culture medium every 3 days after 7 days of                differentiation.
   USE - The method is useful for inducing neural stem                cells for differentiating into neurons in                vitro.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, has good                reproducibility, increases differentiation ratio of                neural stem cells and neurons, and promotes purity                of neurons.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for inducing neural stem cells for                differentiating into neurons in vitro, involves (a)                performing primary culture of neural stem cells                under aseptic conditions using embryonic rat brain                tissue of 14-15 days pregnant ICR rats as raw                materials, repeatedly blowing into single cell                suspension with a sterile pipette tip, filtering                the single-cell suspension using 140 mesh nylon                sieve, transferring into 50 ml centrifuge tube                after counting the cells, adjusting the cell                density to 1x 106 to 2x 106 cells/ml in growth                medium, inoculating cells in a culture flask, and                culturing in 5% carbon dioxide incubator at 37                degrees C to form neurospheres in the culture flask                after 3 days, (b) subculturing neural stem cells by                collecting the neurospheres, removing the                supernatant after low speed centrifugation, washing                pellet using phosphate-buffered saline (PBS),                digesting with Accutase (RTM: natural enzyme                mixture with proteolytic and collagenolytic enzyme                activity), adding Dulbecco's modified Eagle medium                (DMEM)/F12 medium for terminating reaction,                performing second centrifugation, removing the                supernatant, adding growth medium to the pellet,                adjusting the cell density to 5x 105 cells/ml,                inoculating in a culture flask and culturing in 5%                carbon dioxide incubator at 37 degrees C, and (c)                inducing differentiation of neural stem cell,                preparing single cell suspension by digesting the                fourth or fifth generation neurospheres, plating on                a 12-well cell culture plate coated with poly                ornithine and laminin at a cell density of 5x 104                to 5x 106 cells/ml, culturing overnight, and adding                1 ml differentiation medium for performing                differentiation culture the next day by replacing                fresh culture medium every 3 days after 7 days of                differentiation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: In step (b), the low-speed                  centrifugation is carried at a rotation speed of                  100 g for 1 minute. In step (b), the second                  centrifugation is carried out at a rotation speed                  of 200 g for 4 minutes. In step (c), the plated                  cells has concentration of 5x 105 cells/ml. In                  step (c), the 12-well cell culture plate is                  prepared by coating 12-well plate with 0.02 mg/ml                  poly ornithine, incubating at 37 degrees C in                  incubator for 1 hour, recovering poly ornithine,                  washing excess poly ornithine with sterilized                  double distilled water, coating 12-well plate                  with 0.01 mg/ml laminin, incubating at 37 degrees                  C in incubator for 2 hours, recovering laminin                  after coating, adding 1x PBS to 12-well plate and                  placing in a dry place. Preferred Composition:                  The formulation of growth medium in steps (a) and                  (b), comprises 1% nitrogen, 1% B27 (RTM: Not                  defined), 20 ng/ml basic fibroblast growth factor                  (bFGF), 20 ng/ml epidermal growth factor (EGF)                  and 97% DMEM/F12. The formulation of                  differentiation culture solution in step (c)                  comprises 2% B27, 97% Neurobasal (RTM: Basal                  medium designed for long-term maintenance and                  maturation of pure post-natal and adult brain                  neurons) medium, and 2 mM GlutaMAX (RTM:                  L-glutamine supplement)-I supplement.

TI Composite welding butt joint method involves using bridge steel of different materials, forming transition bevel at bevel of steel plate, welding both sides of joint and filling cover surface by automatic submerged arc welding.
AU LIANG H
   LI H
   HUANG Y
   LI F
   GUO H
   ZHU D

   GAO B
   CHEN Q
   LIU X
   XU W
   WANG Z
AE CHINA RAILWAY JIUJIANG BRIDGE ENG CO LTD (CREN-C)
GA 202006247Y
AB    NOVELTY - A composite welding butt joint method involves using bridge steel of different materials and plate thickness, forming transition bevel at bevel of thick steel plate, so that thickness of two steel plates is same and gradient ratio of transition bevel is 1:8, welding both sides of the joint by gas shielded welding with controlled interlayer temperature of 120-150 degrees C, gas shielded welding current of 250 A, welding voltage of 28 V, welding speed of 30 cm/minute, welding line energy of 14 kJ/cm, using argon-rich gas comprising 20 vol.% carbon dioxide and 80 vol.% argon as protective gas, controlling gas flow under the condition of 20-25 L/minute for welding, filling cover surface by automatic submerged arc welding with controlled interlayer temperature of 120-150 degrees C, arc welding current of 650 A, welding voltage of 31 V, welding speed of 40 cm/minute and welding line energy of 30 kJ/cm.
   USE - Composite welding butt joint method.
   ADVANTAGE - The welding butt joint method effectively prevents cracking and breaking of large steel structures of different thick plates during welding.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A composite welding butt joint method involves using Q500qE and Q690qENH bridge steel of different materials and plate thickness, forming transition bevel at bevel of thick steel plate, so that thickness of two steel plates is same and gradient ratio of transition bevel is 1:8, where bevel adopts X-shaped asymmetric bevel with angle of deep groove of 60 degrees , angle of shallow groove of 65 degrees and blunt edge diameter of 2 mm, welding both sides of the joint by gas shielded welding with controlled interlayer temperature of 120-150 degrees C, gas shielded welding current of 250 A, welding voltage of 28 V, welding speed of 30 cm/minute, welding line energy of 14 kJ/cm, using argon-rich gas comprising 20 vol.% carbon dioxide and 80 vol.% argon as protective gas, controlling gas flow under the condition of 20-25 L/minute for welding, filling cover surface by automatic submerged arc welding with controlled interlayer temperature of 120-150 degrees C, arc welding current of 650 A, welding voltage of 31 V, welding speed of 40 cm/minute and welding line energy of 30 kJ/cm. The Q500qE steel has yield strength of 500 MPa or more, tensile strength of 630 MPa or more, elongation of 18% or more and impact energy of 120 J or more at -40 degrees C. The Q690qENH steel has yield strength of 690 MPa or more, tensile strength of 810 MPa or more, elongation of 14% or more and impact energy of 120 J or more at -40 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Property: The gas shielded welding wire and automatic submerged welding wire have tensile strength of more than 630 MPa or more than 810 MPa. The diameter of the gas shielded welding wire is phi 1.2 mm and the diameter of automatic submerged welding wire is phi 4.0 mm. Preferred Composition: The gas shielded welding wire comprises 0.071 wt.% carbon, 1.63 wt.% silicon, 0.057 wt.% manganese, 0.013 wt.% phosphorus, 0.003 wt.% sulfur, 1.03 wt.% nickel and balance iron, or 0.084 wt.% carbon, 0.269 wt.% silicon, 1.378 wt.% manganese, 0.011 wt.% phosphorus, 0.004 wt.% sulfur, 0.407 wt.% chromium, 2.775 wt.% nickel, 0.574 wt.% molybdenum, 0.164 wt.% copper, 0.008 wt.% vanadium and balance iron. The automatic submerged arc welding wire comprises 0.096 wt.% carbon, 0.054 wt.% silicon, 1.84 wt.% manganese, 0.010 wt.% phosphorus, 0.0065 wt.% sulfur, 0.94 wt.% nickel, 0.30 wt.% molybdenum, 0.20 wt.% copper and balance iron, or 0.063 wt.% carbon, 0.388 wt.% silicon, 1.902 wt.% manganese, 0.012 wt.% phosphorus, 0.003 wt.% sulfur, 0.364 wt.% chromium, 2.755 wt.% nickel, 0.501 wt.% molybdenum, 0.049 wt.% copper, 0.008 wt.% vanadium and balance iron.

PT P
PN CN110512589-A; CN110512589-B
TI Method for curing shallow super soft foundation,            involves measuring basic physical and chemical            indicators of ultra-soft foundation soil, followed by            dividing excavation and drainage ditch of super soft            foundation site.
AU CAI G
   LIU S
   DU G
   LI J
   ZHAO Z
   CHEN J
   RUAN J
   LIU C
   WANG Z
AE UNIV NANJING FORESTRY (UNJF-C)
GA 2019A1439X
AB    NOVELTY - A shallow super soft foundation curing method                involves measuring the basic physical and chemical                indicators of ultra-soft foundation soil, dividing                the excavation and drainage ditch of the super soft                foundation site, cleaning and leveling the                periphery of the site, laying a sand mat on an                ultra-soft foundation and inserting the vertical                drainage plate into the pretreatment area. The                upper part of the vertical drainage plate is                connected to the vacuum pump, followed by laying a                non-woven geotextile and a sealing film, measuring                the water content and depth of the sludge, removing                the sealing film and geotextile, turning on the                homomixing equipment to stir the sand, determining                the amount of curing agent, turning on the curing                agent controller, stirring the powdery curing agent                and silt soil, and forming a pretreatment                foundation, laying a non-woven geotextile and a                sealing film, and adjusting the gas pressure                regulating valve for ventilation and carbonization                treatment.
   USE - Method for curing shallow super soft                foundation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables curing the shallow super                soft foundation with better treatment effect, and                effectively realizes the industrial slag,                construction waste and resource utilization of                carbon dioxide in the soft foundation processing,                in rapid and eco-friendly manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A shallow super soft foundation curing method                involves utilizing vacuum drainage and magnesium                oxide carbonization, measuring the basic physical                and chemical indicators including site area, mud                depth, water content, liquid-plastic limit, organic                matter content and pH value of ultra-soft                foundation soil, dividing the excavation and                drainage ditch of the super soft foundation site,                cleaning and leveling the periphery of the site,                laying a sand mat on an ultra-soft foundation and                inserting the vertical drainage plate into the                pretreatment area. The upper part of the vertical                drainage plate is connected to the vacuum pump                through a horizontal suction pipe, followed by                laying a non-woven geotextile and a sealing film in                order, measuring the water content and depth of the                sludge after drainage, removing the sealing film                and geotextile, removing the horizontal suction                pipe, turning on the homomixing equipment to stir                the sand and silting of the upper cushion evenly,                determining the amount of curing agent according to                the physical index of the secondary measurement,                the stirring time and stirring rate per unit volume                of soft soil, turning on the curing agent                controller, stirring the powdery curing agent and                silt soil evenly, leveling the site after stirring,                and forming a pretreatment foundation, hermetically                connecting the vertical drainage plate to the                ventilation main through the ventilation branch                pipe, connecting the ventilation main pipe to a                high-pressure carbon dioxide gas tank, laying a                non-woven geotextile and a sealing film, and                adjusting the gas pressure regulating valve for                ventilation and carbonization treatment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The curing agent is                  composed of 50-60% active magnesium oxide, 30-60%                  slag, 10-20% slag, 5-10% quick lime and 5-10%                  anhydrous magnesium chloride powder.
DC Q42 (Hydraulic engineering, soil shifting and sewerage (E02, 3))
MC Q42-A10

PT P
PN CN110452180-A
TI Synthesizing ethyl 2-chloro-5-amino-6-pyrimidine carboxylate by mixing fuming nitric acid and whey acid, pouring into ice water, stirring, mixing with ethyl nitrolactalate and organic alkali, adding water to quench, reaction and filtering.
AU WANG X
AE NANJING PURUIDA PHARM TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (NANJ-Non-standard)
GA 2019980145
AB    NOVELTY - Method for synthesizing ethyl 2-chloro-5-amino-6-pyrimidine carboxylate by mixing fuming nitric acid, concentrated sulfuric acid and whey acid, slowly pouring into ice water, stirring, filtering, washing the filter cake to neutral, digging out a filter cake, and drying to obtain nitrolactalbumin, dripping concentrated sulfuric acid at room temperature, heating, and filtering to obtain ethyl nitrolactalate, mixing ethyl nitrolactalate, phosphorus oxychloride and organic alkali, carrying out chlorination reaction, cooling, performing reduced pressure concentration to remove redundant phosphorus oxychloride, adding water to quench, extracting with organic solvent, drying and concentrating to obtain the intermediate product 2,4-dichloro-5-nitro-6-pyrimidinecarboxylic acid ethyl ester, and adding 2, 4-dichloro-5-nitro-6-pyrimidinecarboxylic acid ethyl ester, carbon dioxide and magnesium oxide, adding tetrahydrofuran for reaction, filtering, and carrying out column chromatography.
   USE - Method for synthesizing ethyl 2-chloro-5-amino-6-pyrimidine carboxylate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is convenient and simple in operation, mild in reaction, small in pollution, has high yield and purity of the pyrimidine, and can be applied in large-scale production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for synthesizing ethyl 2-chloro-5-amino-6-pyrimidine carboxylate, involves (i) mixing fuming nitric acid, concentrated sulfuric acid and whey acid in a weight ratio of 1:(1-5):(2-5), reacting, cooling to room temperature after the reaction is ended, slowly pouring into ice water, stirring for 1 hour, filtering, washing the filter cake to neutral, digging out a filter cake, and drying to obtain nitrolactalbumin, (ii) dripping concentrated sulfuric acid at room temperature, where the weight ratio of concentrated sulfuric acid to nitroemulsion is 1:(0.5-2), heating at 70-80 degrees C, carrying out reflux reaction, cooling to room temperature after the reaction is ended, and filtering to obtain ethyl nitrolactalate, (iii) mixing ethyl nitrolactalate, phosphorus oxychloride and organic alkali in a weight ratio of 1:(5-10):(3-7), carrying out chlorination reaction at 25-100 degrees C for 2-5 hours, cooling the mixture, carrying out reduced pressure concentration to remove redundant phosphorus oxychloride, adding water to quench, extracting with organic solvent, drying and concentrating to obtain the intermediate product 2,4-dichloro-5-nitro-6-pyrimidinecarboxylic acid ethyl ester, and (iv) adding 2, 4-dichloro-5-nitro-6-pyrimidinecarboxylic acid ethyl ester, carbon dioxide and magnesium oxide in a weight ratio of 1:(0.1-1):(0.1-0.4), adding 10-25 times volume tetrahydrofuran for reaction, filtering after the reaction is ended, evaporating the filtrate to obtain a crude product, and carrying out column chromatography.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (i) involves mixing fuming nitric acid and concentrated sulfuric acid, cooling the outside with ice water, adding the orotic acid at 5-25 degrees C, stirring at room temperature for 1 hour, heating at 30-60 degrees C and reacting for 3-6 hours. The step (iii) involves quenching by adding water is to pour 5 times the amount of phosphorus oxychloride to the mixture from which removing and stirrring phosphorus oxychloride for 2 hours. Preferred Conditions: The reaction time under elevated temperature and reflux in step (ii) is 16-24 hours. The reaction condition in step (iv) is hydrogenation reaction under normal pressure for 7-10 hours. Preferred Components: The organic alkali comprises triethylamine, diisopropylethylamine, triisopropylamine, N, N-dimethylaniline, N, N-diethylaniline, N, N-dimethylpyridylamine, pyridine and/or 1,8-diazabicyclo (5,4,0) undecene-7.

PT P
PN CN110373205-A
TI Preparation of soil remediation agent for high concentration cobalt and lead-contaminated soil, by placing biomass raw material in extruder, heating bentonite, yellow stone, biomass charcoal, fruit peel, sawdust and slag and drying.
AU XIANG J
AE ANHUI YILUHONG MODERN AGRIC TECHNOLOGY (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 201990254N
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of soil remediation agent involves placing biomass raw material in numerically controlled continuous steam explosion machine, carrying out continuous steam explosion process for 1-1.4 minutes, placing the biomass raw material in extruder, weighing bentonite, yellow stone and modified process liquid, placing in extruder, extruding for 30-38 minutes, filtering, collecting filter residue, placing in pyrolysis reactor, performing pyrolysis carbonization reaction for 42-46 minutes to obtain carbonized particles, placing in ultrasonic cleaner, ultrasonically processing for 1-1.4 hours, weighing biomass charcoal, dried vegetable, fruit peel, sawdust, slag and sterile water, placing the raw materials in stirred tank, heating at 200-240 degrees C, continuously introducing carbon dioxide gas into the stirred tank, stirring at 400-600 rpm for 40-60 minutes, placing in microwave oven, carrying out microwave activation process for 40-60 minutes, placing in oven and drying at 55-65 degrees C.
   USE - The method is useful for preparation of soil remediation agent, which is useful for high concentration cobalt and lead-contaminated soil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of soil remediation agent with microporous structure and high specific surface area. The soil remediation agent can significantly reduce migration and bioavailability of heavy metal contaminants in the environment, and reduces concentration of cobalt and lead in the soil.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of soil remediation agent involves placing biomass raw material in numerically controlled continuous steam explosion machine, carrying out continuous steam explosion process for 1-1.4 minutes, placing the biomass raw material in extruder, weighing 20-24 wt.% bentonite, 13-16 wt.% yellow stone and 160-200 wt.% modified process liquid, placing in extruder, extruding for 30-38 minutes, filtering, collecting filter residue, placing in pyrolysis reactor with oxygen concentration of 5-7%, performing pyrolysis carbonization reaction for 42-46 minutes to obtain carbonized particles, placing in ultrasonic cleaner, ultrasonically processing for 1-1.4 hours, weighing 45-55 pts. wt. biomass charcoal, 10-13 pts. wt. dried vegetable, 12-14 pts. wt. fruit peel, 17-19 pts. wt. sawdust, 5-7 pts. wt. slag and 42-46 pts. wt. sterile water, placing the raw materials in stirred tank, heating at 200-240 degrees C under pressure of 6-8 MPa, continuously introducing carbon dioxide gas into the stirred tank, stirring at 400-600 rpm for 40-60 minutes to obtain mixed raw materials, placing in microwave oven, carrying out microwave activation process for 40-60 minutes to obtain activated product, placing in oven and drying at 55-65 degrees C under moisture content of 4-8%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The biomass raw material comprises 30-34 pts. wt. corn straw, 13-17 pts. wt. cottonseed hull, 7-11 pts. wt. wheat straw, 10-14 pts. wt. willow branch, 14-18 pts. wt. Cedrela sinensis leaves and 18-24 pts. wt. pine cypress.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The usage amount of carbon dioxide gas is 8-10 g/m3.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The pressure in the steam explosion machine is 4-6 MPa during the steam explosion process. The relative air humidity in the steam explosion machine is 86-90%. The extruding step is carried out at 140-180 degrees C under pressure of 3-4 MPa and speed of 800-1000rpm. The pyrolysis carbonization reaction step is carried out at 800-900 degrees C. The ultrasonically processing step is carried out at frequency of 67-77 kHz. The microwave activation process step is carried out at power of 500-600 W. Preferred Components: The modified process liquid comprises 18-19 pts. wt. ammonium nitrate, 12-14 pts. wt. tartaric acid, 10-13 pts. wt. tetradecyltrimethylammonium bromide, 7-10 pts. wt. sodium hexadecyl sulfate, 0.7-0.9 pt. wt. ethylenediamine, 2-3 pts. wt. silane coupling agent, 30-40 pts. wt. anhydrous ethanol and 120-160 pts. wt. sterile water.

PT P
PN CN110305838-A
TI Improving body fat content by treating human subcutaneous fat tissue, digesting using collagenase I medium, inoculating cells, incubating, adding autologous platelet-rich plasma and recombinant growth factor and implanting into subject.
AU WU W
AE WU W (WUWW-Individual)
GA 201996204G
AB    NOVELTY - Method for improving body fat content, involves (i) taking human subcutaneous fat tissue under sterile conditions, transferring to a dish pre-filled with D-Hanks solution, rinsing, and removing blood vessels and connective tissue visible in fat tissue, (ii) shearing the tissue block, homogenizing to chyle shape, adding to collagenase I medium, digesting, and shaking, (iii) removing undigested tissue blocks and large volume of fat cells, centrifuging the cell suspension, discarding the supernatant, add cell culture medium, centrifuging to obtain cell suspension, counting under the microscope, inoculating the cells to the culture dish or culture flask, and incubating in 5% carbon dioxide incubator, (iv) taking fat cell group in a Petri dish and adding sterile membrane stabilizing reagent, (v) adding autologous high-concentration platelet-rich plasma and recombinant human vascular endothelial growth factor-C (VEGF-C)to the fat cell population, and (vi) implanting the fat cell population.
   USE - The method is useful for improving body fat content.
   ADVANTAGE - The method increases the survival rate of the graft of the fat cell population before, during or after delivery or administration to the subject, and greatly increases the body fat content.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for improving body fat content, involves (i) taking human subcutaneous fat tissue under sterile conditions, transferring to a dish pre-filled with D-Hanks solution, rinsing 5-8 times, and removing blood vessels and connective tissue visible in fat tissue using ophthalmic scissors, (ii) shearing the tissue block to 8-10 mm 3 small pieces, homogenizing with a tissue homogenizer at a speed of 180-200 rpm to chyle shape, adding to 0.1% collagenase I medium, digesting for 30 minutes, and shaking once every 5 minutes, (iii) removing undigested tissue blocks and large volume of fat cells through a 200 mesh stainless steel cell sieve, centrifuging the cell suspension at 2000 rpm for 5 minutes, discarding the supernatant, add 1 time cell culture medium, centrifuging at 2000 rpm for 10 minutes to obtain cell suspension, counting under the microscope, inoculating the cells to the culture dish or culture flask at 5x 104 cells/ml, and incubating at 37 degrees C under saturated humidity in 5% carbon dioxide incubator, (iv) taking 100 pts. wt. fat cell group in a Petri dish under a sterile constant pressure environment and adding 5 pts. wt. of a 0.5% sterile membrane stabilizing reagent to the culture dish, (v) adding autologous high-concentration platelet-rich plasma and recombinant human vascular endothelial growth factor-C (VEGF-C) to the fat cell population, and (vi) implanting the fat cell population treated in steps (iv) and (vi) into the subject.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: In step (ii), the collagenase I medium is prepared by dissolving 100 mg collagenase I in 100 ml of D-Hanks solution, adjusting the pH to 7.2-7.4 with a 5.6% sodium bicarbonate solution, filtering through a 0.22 micron filter, and storing at -20 degrees C until use. In step (iii), the cell culture medium is prepared by adding 10% fetal bovine serum to Dulbecco's modified eagle medium: nutrient mixture F-12 solution, filtering through a 0.22 micron filter, and storing at 4 degrees C until use. Preferred Conditions: The volume ratio of the fat cell population to the autologous high-concentration platelet-rich plasma is 3:1. The dose of the recombinant human VEGF-C is 16 fat cells per injection, corresponding to 1500 IU of recombinant human VEGF-C. Preferred Cells: The fat cell population comprises autologous cells and non-autologous cells, where the non-autologous cells comprise allogeneic cells and xenogeneic cells derived from a mammal.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The sterile membrane stabilizing agent comprises sodium chloride, calcium chloride, potassium chloride, EDTA and distilled water.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); D22 (Sterilising, bandages, dressing and skin protection agents - including sterilising agents (other than for food), sutures, plaster casts, bioactive prostheses, contact lenses, diapers, animal litter, timber, preservatives, disinfectants, bactericidal detergents, deodorants, insect repellent compounds, moth proofers, sheep dip (A61L).); P34 (Sterilising, syringes, electrotherapy (A61L, M, N).)
MC B04-B04D4; B04-F02; B04-H06M0E; B04-L05; B11-A04A; B11-C04G; D05-H08B; D09-C01B; P34-A02
