PT P
PN CN110170187-A
TI Steam-water separation device useful for gas treatment of geothermal fluid, comprises e.g. shell, separation plate, water inlet pipe, exhaust pipe, an air outlet pipe, spiral blade, horizontal flow separation plate and vertical rotating rod.
AU CHEN L
   YUAN X
   WANG R
   CHEN W
   TAO Z
   LIU Z
   YU C
AE FIRST GEOLOGICAL ENVIRONMENT SURVEY HENA (FIRS-Non-standard)
GA 201975312W
AB    NOVELTY - Steam-water separation device comprises a shell (1) whose upper side of is provided with a downwardly inclined separation plate (2) and the side wall is provided with a water inlet pipe (3). The side of the shell of the separating plate is in the air outlet chamber provided with an exhaust pipe (4). The lower end of separating plate is provided with an air outlet pipe (5) connected to an air chamber. The outer side of the air outlet pipe wall is provided with a spiral blade. A horizontal flow separation plate is disposed in the shell below the air outlet pipe. A vertical rotating rod is arranged on the separation plate. Many scrapers are evenly distributed on the rotating rod set at the upper end of the separation plate. A stirring paddle is arranged on the rotating rod. A water outlet pipe is arranged on the shell below the dividing. A drain valve is arranged on the water outlet pipe.
   USE - The device is useful for gas treatment of geothermal fluid (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The device can effectively avoid the fouling of the water inlet, separating plate and the water outlet; and has simple gas treatment process flow which requires less equipment investment, and purity of the carbon-dioxide gas is more than 99%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for gas treatment of geothermal fluid, comprising (a) separating geothermal fluid by a first-stage steam separator to obtain hot water and a mixed gas; (b) cooling the mixed gas through the first condenser, liquefying the steam contained in the mixed gas, and then conveying into a second-stage steam separator to remove steam contained in the mixed gas; (c) conveying the mixed gas treated in the step (b) into a compressor, pressurizing to 2.5-3 MPa, then conveying to a second condenser and cooling to 35-45 degrees C to liquefy hydrogen sulfide, conveying into a three-stage steam separator for gas-liquid separation to obtain liquid hydrogen sulfide; (d) allowing absorption of gas separated in the step (c) by using a polyethylene glycol dimethyl ether solution to remove the hydrogen sulfide contained in the gas to obtain purified carbon-dioxide gas, concentrating polyethylene glycol dimethyl ether containing hydrogen sulfide, drying the liquid under reduced pressure, flashing, heating to 135-145 degrees C for analysis, releasing hydrogen sulfide gas; and (e) recovering liquid hydrogen sulfide of hydrogen sulfide gas in the step (c) and (d).
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the steam-water separation device.
   Shell (1)
   Separation plate (2)
   Water inlet pipe (3)
   Exhaust pipe (4)
   Air outlet pipe (5)

PN CN109908180-A
TI Preparing endometrial stem cell exosome            concentrate gel composition useful for treating            endometrial damage, comprises choosing human            endometrial stem cells P2-P8, inoculating in cell            culture, adding electrolyte, and starvation            culturing.
AU ZHANG B
   HAN H
   LU Z
   MOU X
   XU Y
AE TIANJIN XINPUSAIER BIOMEDICAL TECHNOLOGY (TIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201957368J
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing endometrial stem cell exosome                concentrate gel composition, comprises (i) choosing                human endometrial stem cells P2-P8, inoculating in                cell culture; (ii) after 2-4 days of inoculation                culture, when the cell fusion rate reaches 80-95%,                discarding all the culture supernatant, and washing                the cells, adding electrolyte, and placing in a                carbon dioxide incubator for starvation culture;                (iii) recovering all the culture supernatants and                filtering, centrifuging the filtered culture                supernatant, and recovering centrifuge supernatant;                (iv) centrifuging the recovered supernatant, gently                removing the centrifuge tube, and pipetting the                upper 70-90% liquid, and mixing the lower part                liquid and the precipitate and using for preparing                a therapeutic composition; and (v) taking the                composition containing medicinal polymer                stabilizer, moisturizer, transdermal absorption                enhancer, and endometrial stem cell exosome                concentrate, and balance of compound electrolyte                injection.
   USE - The composition is useful for treating                endometrial damage (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing endometrial stem cell exosome                concentrate gel composition, comprises (i) choosing                human endometrial stem cells P2-P8 with good growth                and microbial tests are negative, inoculating in                cell culture at an initial density of 3-5x 104/cm2                in a bottle or a cell culture dish, where the                medium is a culture system containing a fetal                bovine serum having a volume ratio of 5-15% in a                basal medium; (ii) after 2-4 days of inoculation                culture, when the cell fusion rate reaches 80-95%                and the cell growth state is good, discarding all                the culture supernatant, and washing the cells 2-4                times with medical compound electrolyte injection,                adding electrolyte, making up the solution to a                height of 2-3 mm, and placing in a carbon dioxide                incubator for starvation culture; (iii) after 18-30                hours of starvation culture, recovering all the                culture supernatants and filtering through a 0.22                mu m pore size filter, centrifuging the filtered                culture supernatant for 10-20 minutes under a                centrifugal force of 3000-5000 g, and recovering                centrifuge supernatant; (iv) centrifuging the                recovered supernatant at 200000-500000 g for 30-60                minutes, gently removing the centrifuge tube after                centrifugation, and pipetting the upper 70-90%                liquid, and mixing the lower part of 10-30% liquid                and the precipitate and using as a raw material for                preparing a therapeutic composition for endometrial                damage; and (v) taking the composition containing                2-10 g medicinal polymer stabilizer, 5-10 ml                moisturizer, 5-10 ml transdermal absorption                enhancer, and 10-50 ml of 100 ml endometrial stem                cell exosome concentrate, and balance of compound                electrolyte injection. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also                included for endometrial stem cell exosome                concentrate gel composition.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The composition is                  administered by direct injection into the                  interior of the uterus.

PN CN109852808-A
TI Extracting tin from silver-separating residue comprises e.g. taking required amount of silver residue for crushing treatment and mixing crushed silver residue and flocculating agent to obtain mixed solution of silver residue and water.
AU CHEN P
   ZHOU L
   CHEN H
AE LIUZHOU GUANGHUA TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (LIUZ-Non-standard)
GA 201953305L
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting tin from silver-separating residue comprises e.g. taking required amount of silver residue for crushing treatment, mixing crushed silver residue and flocculating agent uniformly to obtain mixed solution of silver residue and water, injecting mixed solution of silver residue and water into mixing furnace and raising temperature, stirring, using high pressure filter group to perform filtration treatment on mixed solution, subjecting filtered silver slag to low-temperature oxidation roasting treatment, and adding silver slag subjected to low-temperature oxidative roasting to the reactor and adding flocculating agent, allowing to stand, adding required amount of phosphoric acid to reactor, heating for leaching reaction, adding calcium dihydrogen phosphate to the reactor, heating and reacting to obtain mixture A, adding mixture A to melting furnace, heating, stirring under nitrogen, introducing carbon dioxide gas, analyzing other components in silver slag and filtering.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting tin from silver-separating residue.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: ensures sufficient filtration of impurities in the silver slag and improves purity of the obtained product after repeated filtration, improves purity of obtained product, has stable progress of the chemical reaction, is simple in preparation and economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Extracting tin from silver-separating residue comprises (i) taking required amount of silver residue for crushing treatment, mixing crushed silver residue and flocculating agent in the ratio of 1:10, and mixing uniformly to obtain mixed solution of silver residue and water; (ii) injecting mixed solution of silver residue and water into mixing furnace and raising temperature to 560-850 degrees C and stirring for 20 minutes, allowing to stand for 3-6 hours after stirring; (iii) using high pressure filter group to perform filtration treatment on mixed solution in the step (ii) and first filtration speed is 40 m/hours, second filtration speed is 45 m/hours, filtering third time at a speed of 50 m/hours and filtering fourth time at a speed of 55 m/hours; (iv) subjecting filtered silver slag to low-temperature oxidation roasting treatment, and adding silver slag subjected to low-temperature oxidative roasting to the reactor and adding flocculating agent, allowing to stand for 5-8 hours, adding required amount of phosphoric acid to reactor, heating to 260 degrees C for leaching reaction for 6-8 hours, adding calcium dihydrogen phosphate to the reactor, heating to 100 degrees C and reacting for 2-4 hours to obtain mixture A; and (v) adding mixture A to melting furnace, heating to 800 degrees C, stirring under nitrogen for 30 minutes, introducing carbon dioxide gas at a rate of 20 l per minute, analyzing other components in silver slag and filtering.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises controlling temperature in low temperature oxidation roasting within 50 degrees C and feeding rate of oxygen-enriched air is 6-8 l/minutes. The rate of nitrogen injection is 20-38 l/minutes. Preferred Components: The flocculating agent is at least one of aluminum sulfate, aluminum chloride, iron sulfate and ferric chloride. The particle size after pulverization is less than 0.3 cm. In the step (ii) phosphoric acid filtrate density is 0.25 g/cm. The purity of carbon dioxide gas is between 97-99%.
DC M25 (Production and refining of metals other than iron - including ore treatment, extraction, working up scrap, obtaining specific metals, control testing methods (C22B).)
MC M25-A01; M25-E; M25-G05; M25-G22; M25-G23; M25-H; M25-J

PN RU2017139062-A; RU2691579-C2
TI Cultivation of black sea red alga Gelidium spinosum by providing weekly cyclic cultivation, including rest phase in aquarium, placing nutrient medium with Gelidium fragments, stimulating growth of subject and growing in cultivators.
AU BELYAEV B N
   BEREGOVAYA N M
AE AS RUSSIA MARINE BIOLOGICAL RES INST (ASMA-Non-standard)
GA 2019517169
AB    NOVELTY - Black sea red alga Gelidium spinosum (Grev.) (Rhodophyta) is cultivated by providing weekly cyclic cultivation, including rest phase in an aquarium at 10 12 degrees C and daylight of 0.2-0.5 klx, preparing a nutrient medium based on filtered black sea water, bringing to salinity, placing the medium with Gelidium fragments, stimulating the growth of the subject and growing in cultivators with illumination on the water surface of 18-20 klx in 16-day, 8-night mode, securing from the start of the cycle, bubbling the medium with compressed air, ensuring volumetric rotation of the algae, maintaining the pH of the medium at 7.9-8.2 by adding carbon dioxide and in nutrient channel, which is saturated with nutrients in the form of salts containing nitrogen, phosphorus, iron, manganese and magnesium, and chelated iron, pre-boiled in distillate together with sodium (Na2)-EDTA, and harvesting and selection for subsequent cycles of the most complete and clean from fouling algae.
   USE - The method is useful for cultivation of black sea red alga Gelidium spinosum (Grev.) (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Black sea red alga Gelidium spinosum (Grev.) (Rhodophyta) is cultivated by providing weekly cyclic cultivation, including rest phase in an aquarium at 10 12 degrees C and daylight of 0.2-0.5 klx, preparing a nutrient medium based on filtered black sea water, bringing to salinity, placing the medium with Gelidium fragments with an initial density of 2-2.5 kg/m2, stimulating the growth of the subject and growing in cultivators with illumination on the water surface of 18-20 klx in 16-day, 8-night mode, nutrient medium temperature of 15-19 degrees C in February, March and November, 19-23 degrees C in April, September and October, and 23-27 degrees C in the summer months, securing it within 36-48 hours from the start of the cycle near the upper range limits, bubbling the medium with compressed air, ensuring volumetric rotation of the algae, maintaining the pH of the medium at 7.9-8.2 by adding carbon dioxide at the rate of 25-30 g/kg algae/day, and in nutrient channel, which is saturated with nutrients in the form of salts containing nitrogen, phosphorus, iron, manganese and magnesium, and chelated iron, pre-boiled in distillate together with sodium (Na2)-EDTA, and harvesting and selection for subsequent cycles of the most complete and clean from fouling algae. The salts containing nitrogen, phosphorus, iron, manganese, and magnesium contribute 44 mg/L potassium nitrate (KNO3), 6 mg/L phosphoric acid (H3PO4), 5.57 mg/L iron sulfate heptahydrate (FeSO4.7H2O), 2.7 mg manganese chloride tetrahydrate (MnCl2.4H2O), and 1.03 g magnesium sulfate heptahydrate (MgSO4.7H2O).

PN CN109673411-A
TI High-yield planting method of Cucumis melo by mixing loess and nutrient fertilizer, sterilizing, sowing seeds, culturing, transplanting in greenhouse, spraying amino acid nutrient solution and supporting using parallel rows of cotton lines.
AU YANG Z
AE YANG Z (YANG-Individual)
GA 2019408374
AB    NOVELTY - High-yield planting method of Cucumis melo comprises sterilizing nutrient fertilizer, mixing with Cucumis melo seeds, putting into cloth bag, placing in freezer and storing; mixing loess and nutrient fertilizer to prepare culture soil, spreading in seedling box, sterilizing culture soil, airing, sowing seeds, culturing at indoor pressure of 76-80 kPa for 8-12 days, culturing at indoor pressure of 55-60 kPa for 14-18 days, introducing oxygen and carbon dioxide and culturing to obtain seedling; transplanting in greenhouse and spraying 5-7 wt.% amino acid nutrient solution; keeping planting shed daytime temperature at 27-30 degrees C and humidity at 63-68% during seedling growing period, spraying 240-270 mL/box amino acid nutrient solution once every 7-10 days, spraying 310-340 mL/box amino acid nutrient solution during flowering period and spraying 380-430 mL/box amino acid nutrient solution during fruiting period; and (E) supporting using parallel rows of cotton lines.
   USE - The method is for high-yield planting of Cucumis melo.
   ADVANTAGE - The Cucumis melo has high seed germination rate, strong disease resistance, high sweetness degree, fresh meat quality, delicious taste and improved planting economic income of more than 26%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - High-yield planting method of Cucumis melo comprises: (A) sterilizing nutrient fertilizer, mixing with Cucumis melo seeds at mass ratio of 50-60: 1, putting into cloth bag, placing in freezer at - 14 to - 10 degrees C and storing at 1-3 degrees C for 3-4 days; (B) mixing loess and nutrient fertilizer at mass ratio of 3-4: 1 to prepare culture soil, spreading in seedling box to thickness of 11-15 cm, sterilizing culture soil, airing for 4-5 days, sowing stored seeds to depth of 3-4 cm, culturing at indoor pressure of 76-80 kPa for 8-12 days, culturing at indoor pressure of 55-60 kPa for 14-18 days, introducing 18-23% vol.% oxygen and 0.04-0.06 vol.% carbon dioxide and culturing to obtain seedling; (C) transplanting in greenhouse provided with three-dimensional planting frame and spraying 5-7 wt.% amino acid nutrient solution; (D) keeping planting shed daytime temperature at 27-30 degrees C and humidity at 63-68% during seedling growing period, spraying 240-270 mL/box amino acid nutrient solution once every 7-10 days, spraying 310-340 mL/box amino acid nutrient solution once every 6-8 days during flowering period and spraying 380-430 mL/box amino acid nutrient solution once every 4-5 days during fruiting period; and (E) supporting using parallel rows of cotton lines when plant claw length is 3-4 cm long.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The amino acid nutrient solution comprises 70-80 pts. wt. sterile water, 19-23 pts. wt. amino acid component, 4-6 pts. wt. effective microorganism bacteria, 7-10 pts. wt. potassium sulfate, 0.2-0.4 pt. wt. magnesium chloride, 0.3-0.4 pt. wt. zinc sulfate and 3-5 pts. wt. calcium gluconate. The amino acid component comprises glycine, leucine, threonine, tryptophan, lysine and glutamic acid at mass ratio of 5: 2: 0.5: 2: 1: 3.

PN CN109453774-A; CN109453774-B
TI Preparing nickel-based bimetallic nano-material            comprises e.g. mixing nickel metal salt with other            metal salts, deionized water and absolute ethanol,            adding a mixed solution of sodium hydroxide and            hydrazine hydrate, stirring and reacting.
AU LU J
   ZENG S
   WANG H
   WANG S
   YANG L
AE UNIV EAST CHINA NORMAL (UYEN-C)
GA 201925441F
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nickel-based bimetallic                nano-material comprises mixing nickel metal salt                with other metal salts, deionized water and                absolute ethanol in a molar ratio of                1:0.2-5:0.6-2.8:0.3-1.8, adding a mixed solution of                sodium hydroxide and hydrazine hydrate, stirring at                50-75 degrees C, reacting, ultrasonic centrifuging                product washed with deionized water and absolute                ethanol, collecting clear and transparent                supernatant and vacuum drying at 50-80 degrees C to                obtain nickel-based bimetallic nano material having                a molar ratio of two metals of 0.2-5, where nickel                metal salt is nickel nitrate, nickel sulfate,                nickel chloride or nickel acetate; other metal salt                is silver nitrate, copper sulfate, ferrous sulfate,                ferric chloride, cobalt nitrate or cobalt sulfate;                and mixed solution of sodium hydroxide and                hydrazine hydrate is composed of 50 wt.% sodium                hydroxide and 50 vol.% hydrazine hydrate in a                volume ratio of 3:1-3:5.
   USE - The nickel-based bimetallic nano-material is                useful in electro-catalytic field (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The material: has excellent magnetism,                excellent stability, high temperature thermal                stability, excellent electrochemical properties and                chemical catalytic performance; and method: has                simple preparation process and low cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for use                method of nickel-based bimetallic nano-material in                electro-catalytic field comprising (i) pressing                nickel-based bimetallic nano material into a solid                electrode having a diameter of 2 cm by using a                tableting abrasive under a pressure of 1-10 MPa, or                mixing the nickel-based bimetallic nano material                with the sodium carboxymethyl cellulose sol in a                weight ratio of 1 g:0.1-0.5 ml, applying ultrasonic                dispersion to both sides of the carbon paper,                drying at room temperature to obtain rectangular                solid electrode of 2 x 2.2 cm, and (ii) using solid                electrode prepared in step (i) as a cathode of a                one-chamber electrolytic cell with magnesium rod                anode, mixing acetophenone with a supporting                electrolyte, chiral inducer, auxiliary agent,                acetonitrile or N,N-dimethylformamide in a molar                ratio of 1:1-5:0.01-0.1:0.01-0.1:1-2, then placing                in a one-chamber electrolytic cell at room                temperature under constant pressure carbon dioxide                atmosphere, performing asymmetric                electrocarboxylation of acetophenone and carbon                dioxide at a constant current density of 0.5-4.5                mA/cm2, where target product is                2-hydroxy-2-phenylpropionic acid; supporting                electrolyte is quaternary ammonium salt,                tetra-n-butyl-ammonium iodide or tetra ethyl                ammonium iodide; chiral inducer is cinchonine,                cinchonidine, quinine, quinidine, L-(+)-tartaric                acid or D-(-)-tartaric acid; and auxiliary agent is                n-butanol, n-propanol or isopropanol.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The nickel-based                  bimetallic nanomaterial has a microspherical                  particle of 180-220 nm. The molar ratio between                  sodium hydroxide and hydrazine hydrate is 1:14.                  The nickel metal salt is nickel nitrate. The                  molar ratio between two metal elemental                  components of the nickel-based bimetallic nano                  material nickel/M is 0.2-5, preferably 5-3, where                  M is silver, copper, iron or cobalt.
DC J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); A85 (Electrical applications.); X25 (Industrial Electric Equipment)

PT P
PN CN109402060-A
TI Separating and culturing primary tumor cell comprises placing fresh tumor tissue in culture dish, placing cancer tissue in culture dish, transferring tissue block, observing under microscope and digesting primary tumor cell.
AU QIAN C
AE QIAN C (QIAN-Individual)
GA 201922644U
AB    NOVELTY - Separating and culturing primary tumor cell comprises (i), placing fresh tumor tissue in culture dish, adding proper amount of phosphate buffered saline, removing blood (blood clot), fat, necrotic tissue and connective tissue on tissue; (ii) placing cancer tissue in another culture dish, adding small amount of Dulbecco's modified eagle medium, cutting tissue into pieces with ophthalmic scissors, transferring centrifuge tube; (iii) transferring tissue block into culture bottle, adding compound enzyme solution, blowing tissue fragments, adjusting pH; (iv) observing under microscope, transferring centrifuge tube; (v) adding suspension into culture flask; (vi) introducing carbon dioxide and oxygen slowly into culture flask, transferring culture into incubator, discarding adherent fibroblasts, suspending unattached cancer cell suspension; and (vii) changing medium regularly, digesting primary tumor cells with trypsin and passing through differential sticking wall purification.
   USE - The method is useful for separating and culturing primary tumor cell.
   ADVANTAGE - The method achieves more than 80% culture success rate and more than 95% clinical coincidence rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Separating and culturing primary tumor cell comprises (i), placing fresh tumor tissue in culture dish, adding proper amount of phosphate buffered saline, removing blood (blood clot), fat, necrotic tissue and connective tissue on tissue by using ophthalmic forceps and ophthalmic scissors, retaining tumor cell-rich region, washing 2-3 times phosphate buffered saline containing antibiotics; (ii) placing cancer tissue in another culture dish, adding small amount of Dulbecco's modified eagle medium, cutting tissue into pieces with ophthalmic scissors, transferring into 15 ml centrifuge tube, rinsing 3-5 times with phosphate buffered saline and removing phosphate buffered saline after tissue block is automatically sunk; (iii) transferring tissue block into culture bottle, adding 5 ml compound enzyme solution, blowing tissue fragments, adjusting pH to 7-8, placing culture bottle on constant temperature shaker at 37 degrees C for 4-10 minutes under lower digestion treatment, adding fetal bovine serum to terminate digestion to obtain cell suspension; (iv) observing under microscope for every 20-30 minutes, transferring to 15 ml centrifuge tube for centrifugation for 5 minutes, when light transmittance of tissue block is good and flocculated and discarding supernatant; (v) adding suspension containing serum, penicillin and streptomycin extracted from tumor patient into culture flask containing cell suspension and re-suspending precipitate by using differential adherence method cell; (vi) introducing carbon dioxide and oxygen slowly into culture flask for 120-150 minutes, transferring culture into incubator for 30-40 minutes, discarding adherent fibroblasts, suspending unattached cancer cell suspension, moving unattached cancer cell suspension to another clean culture bottle and into incubator for further cultivation; and (vii) changing medium regularly, digesting primary tumor cells with trypsin after overgrown and passing through differential sticking wall purification.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The tumor tissue is breast cancer tissue, liver cancer tissue or lung cancer tissue. The amount of antibiotic 4-6% of mass of phosphate buffered saline and antibiotic is penicillin or streptomycin. Preferred Method: In the step (ii), cutting cancer tissue into pieces of 0.5-1 mm3. In the step (iii), preparing composite enzyme solution by adding 200 U/ml collagenase solution, 0.2-0.35% trypsin and 0.3-0.5% hyaluronidase and uniformly mixing ultrasonically. In the step (iv), centrifuging at 1200-1500 rotations/minute. In the step (v), culture medium is prepared by adding 10-15% serum extracted from tumor patient, 5-8% penicillin and 3-7% streptomycin into Dulbecco's modified eagle medium, mixing uniformly. In the step (vi), introducing carbon dioxide at rate of 60-90 ml/minute and oxygen at rate of 40 ml/minute.

PN CN108935660-A
TI Preparing liquid preservative film useful for preserving household probiotics, comprises e.g. mixing milk protein casein, glycerol, propolis, chitosan, bovine jelly and distilled water, filtering, filling, capping, cooling and storing.
AU JIN B
AE JIN B (JINB-Individual)
GA 2018A0809W
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing liquid preservative film comprises (i) taking milk protein casein, glycerol, propolis, chitosan, bovine jelly and distilled water, (ii) disinfecting all container surfaces with disinfectant, and illuminating, (iii) dissolving chitosan in acetic acid, adding probiotics, and extracting active factor from bovine bone, (iv) dissolving milk protein casein and glycerol in distilled water, heating and soaking, and stirring, (v) placing the mixture A, chitosan solution and propolis in blender and stabilizer, adding white sugar and citric acid, heating and stirring, and passing carbon dioxide gas, (vi) controlling the temperature in the mixing stirrer for degassing operation of the wrap film, and discharging the air in the plastic wrap, (vii) filtering the wrap film liquid, discharging into high-temperature sterilizer, sterilizing, and placing in a clean sterile high-pressure spray device under aseptic filling conditions, and (viii) filling, capping and cooling, and storing.
   USE - The film is useful for preserving household probiotics (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and convenient to operate. The film is safe to use, stable, biodegradable, smooth, transparent and fully edible; and has water-resistant.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing liquid preservative film comprises (i) taking 30-40% milk protein casein, 5-15% glycerol, 3-6% propolis, 15-25% chitosan, 10-30% bovine jelly and distilled water (remaining amount), (ii) disinfecting all container surfaces with disinfectant, and completely illuminating by ultraviolet light for 5-10 minutes, (iii) dissolving chitosan in acetic acid, adding probiotics, and extracting uniform and transparent active factor from bovine bone, (iv) dissolving milk protein casein and glycerol in distilled water, heating at 80-90 degrees C and soaking for 1-2 hours, and stirring until completely dissolved to obtain mixture A, (v) placing the mixture A, chitosan solution and propolis in blender and stabilizer, adding white sugar and citric acid, heating the wrap film at 60-70 degrees C and stirring for 15-25 minutes, and passing 20-30% of carbon dioxide gas into the interior of the mixer for 3-5 hours, (vi) controlling the temperature in the mixing stirrer to maintain the temperature of 10-20 degrees C for degassing operation of the wrap film for 4-6 minutes, and discharging the air in the plastic wrap, (vii) completing degassing, filtering the wrap film liquid, discharging into high-temperature sterilizer, sterilizing at 115-120 degrees C for 10-15 minutes, and placing in a clean sterile high-pressure spray device under aseptic filling conditions, and (viii) filling, capping and cooling the probiotic preservative film liquid in stages at 38-40 degrees C, and storing at 15-20 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The stabilizer is xanthan gum, agar and/or sodium alginate. The disinfectant in the step (ii) is an alcohol having a purity of 70-75%. Preferred Method: The speed of the stirrer in the mixing stirrer is 100-300 revolutions/minute, and a fine mesh screen is movably connected to the bottom end of the bottom discharge port of the mixing agitator. The relative humidity of the ultraviolet irradiation environment in the step (ii) is 50-60% and the temperature is 15-20 degrees C. The UV intensity should not be lower than 70 mu W/cm2.

PN CN108417837-A
TI Preparing lithium battery anode material aqueous polyimide adhesive comprises e.g. adding N,N-dimethylacetamide, dibasic anhydride and isophorone diisocyanate into reaction kettle under an inert atmosphere, and heating.
AU WU Y
   YUE L
   SU R
AE WU Y (WUYY-Individual)
GA 201866982W
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing lithium battery anode material aqueous polyimide adhesive comprises (1) adding N,N-dimethylacetamide, dibasic anhydride and isophorone diisocyanate into reaction kettle under an inert atmosphere, heating until the solid is completely dissolved; (2) adding organic silver catalyst to the reaction kettle, heating until no carbon dioxide is released; cooling, weighing, and converting to N,N-dimethylacetamide; (3) filtering to obtain polyimide resin solution having solid content of 14-16 wt.%; (4) weighing the polyimide resin solution obtained in the step (3), dropwise adding lithium dodecylbenzenesulfonate solution thereto, stirring and mixing, (5) heating the mixed solution obtained in the step (4), and dropwise adding deionized water while stirring; (6) weighing the mixed solution obtained in step (5), adding de-ionized water while stirring, to obtain transparent polyimide solution having solid content of 1-2 wt.%.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing lithium battery anode material aqueous polyimide adhesive.
   ADVANTAGE - The polyimide adhesive: reduces effect of adhesive on capacitance of anode material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing lithium battery anode material aqueous polyimide adhesive comprises (1) adding N,N-dimethylacetamide, dibasic anhydride and isophorone diisocyanate into reaction kettle under an inert atmosphere, heating the system to 50-60 degrees C until the solid is completely dissolved; where the molar ratio of the dibasic anhydride to diisocyanate is 1:1; (2) adding organic silver catalyst to the reaction kettle, heating until no carbon dioxide is released; cooling to 60-80 degrees C, weighing, and converting to N,N-dimethylacetamide; (3) filtering to obtain polyimide resin solution having solid content of 14-16 wt.%; (4) weighing the polyimide resin solution obtained in the step (3), dropwise adding lithium dodecylbenzenesulfonate solution thereto, stirring and mixing, where the concentration of the lithium dodecylbenzenesulfonate solution is 35-40%, and the pH is 12-12.5; where used amount of lithium dodecylbenzenesulfonate solution is 15-18 wt.% of the polyimide resin solution; (5) heating the mixed solution obtained in the step (4) to 65-85 degrees C, and dropwise adding deionized water while stirring; (6) weighing the mixed solution obtained in step (5), adding de-ionized water while stirring, to obtain transparent polyimide solution having solid content of 1-2 wt.%; and (7) respectively weighing the solution obtained in the step (6), lithium iron phosphate and graphene, where the mass ratio of polyimide resin, lithium iron phosphate and graphene is 2:98:4.9, adding lithium iron phosphate to solution, stirring evenly, adding graphene, mixing well, adding deionized water, and adjusting the viscosity. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for the lithium battery anode material aqueous polyimide adhesive prepared by above mentioned method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The dibasic acid anhydride is 1,2,4,5-cyclohexanetetracarboxylic dianhydride, pyromellitic dianhydride, and diphenyl ether tetracarboxylic dianhydride; preferably 1,2,4,5-cyclohexanetetracarboxylic dianhydride. In the step (2), the added amount of the organic silver catalyst is 0.3-0.4 wt.% of the dibasic acid anhydride. In the step (2), the organic silver catalyst is CAT-100E. In the step (5), adding previously 80-85 degrees C heated deionized water. In the step (5), the added amount of deionized water is 125 wt.% of polyimide resin solution. Preferred Method: In the step (7), the process of adding lithium iron phosphate comprises dividing the lithium iron phosphate into five equal parts, orderly adding solution, stirring and mixing the mixture after each addition. In the step (7), the process of adding graphene comprises dividing the graphene into five equal parts, orderly adding solution, stirring and mixing the mixture after each addition.

GA 2018637891
AB    NOVELTY - Method for modeling iatrogenic damage of bile ducts in an experimental animal, involves (a) sedating the experimental animal, fixing the animal on a laboratory table in a position on the back with dilated sides and fixed limbs, and providing anesthesia and infusion therapy, (b) forming carboxyperitoneum by Veresh needle over navel with a partial pressure of carbon dioxide, (c) introducing 4 trocar for manipulators by introducing a trocar above the navel, 2 trocar at a distance of 3 cm from each other under xiphoid process, and a trocar in the right hypochondrium region, (d) partially isolating holedoch using a soft clamp and a dissector, from hepatic-duodenum ligament at the site of the confluence of the bladder duct into the common hepatic duct, (e) inserting a clitoris and clipping a holedoh clip through a separate puncture of the anterior abdominal wall under the xiphoid process, and (f) establishing a dissection mode at a given current strength for all diathermic influences.
   USE - The method is useful for modeling iatrogenic damage of bile ducts in experimental animal.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for modeling iatrogenic damage of bile ducts in an experimental animal, involves (a) sedating the experimental animal, fixing the animal on a laboratory table in a position on the back with dilated sides and fixed limbs, and providing anesthesia and infusion therapy, (b) forming carboxyperitoneum by Veresh needle over navel with a partial pressure of carbon dioxide of 14 mmHg in conditions of general combined anesthesia with intubation of the trachea and artificial ventilation, (c) introducing 4 trocar for manipulators by introducing a trocar with a diameter of 10 mm above the navel, 2 trocar with a diameter of 5 mm at a distance of 3 cm from each other under xiphoid process, and a trocar with a diameter of 5 mm in the right hypochondrium region, (d) partially isolating holedoch using a soft clamp and a dissector, from hepatic-duodenum ligament at the site of the confluence of the bladder duct into the common hepatic duct, (e) inserting a clitoris with a diameter of 10 mm and clipping a holedoh clip through a separate puncture of the anterior abdominal wall under the xiphoid process with a diameter of 10 mm, where the fixation of 2 clips are proximal from the place of the confluence of cystic duct and the 1st clip distal, and the common bile duct completely crosses below the place of confluence into the cystic duct, between the second proximal and first distal clip, with mechanical damage to the LMC being crossed using endoscopic scissors, and with thermal damage using L-shaped electrode for dissection, under the influence of a monopolar diathermic generator, and (f) establishing a dissection mode at a given current strength of 70 A for all diathermic influences.

TI Preparing melamine plate, comprises e.g. crushing and humidifying wheat straw by crusher, gluing, drying, paving, hot pressing, cooling, edge cutting and sanding.
AU ZHANG W
AE HEZHOU BAOXING NEW MATERIALS CO LTD (HEZH-Non-standard)
GA 2018455300
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing melamine plate, comprises raw material pre-treatment, gluing, drying, paving, hot pressing, cooling, edge cutting and sanding steps, where in the pre-treatment process, crushing and humidifying wheat straw by crusher, adding melamine solution into the humidifying material; slicing cotton stalk and wood chips, and spraying the melamine solution; in the gluing process, adding the melamine modified urea-formaldehyde glue; and the melamine prepared by quenching melamine melt, removing ammonia and carbon dioxide, adding alkali, mixing, allowing to stand, and crystallizing to obtain pure melamine; the melamine modified urea-formaldehyde glue prepared by adding formaldehyde into a reaction kettle, heating, then adding urea, and mixing to obtain a reaction solution, heating, measuring pH value, reacting, adjusting pH value, observing end point of the reaction, measuring viscosity, adding the second batch of urea, stirring, adding melamine, stirring, adjusting pH value, and cooling.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing melamine plate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes wheat straw, cotton stalk and wood chips; reduces formaldehyde content in plate; is environmentally friendly; has low cost; and reduces energy consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing melamine plate, comprises raw material pre-treatment, gluing, drying, paving, hot pressing, cooling, edge cutting and sanding steps, where in the pre-treatment process, crushing and humidifying wheat straw by crusher, adding melamine solution into the humidifying material; slicing cotton stalk and wood chips, and spraying the melamine solution; in the gluing process, adding the melamine modified urea-formaldehyde glue; and the melamine prepared by quenching melamine melt with greater than 95% purity water, removing ammonia and carbon dioxide from the obtained melamine solution, adding alkali into the melamine solution, mixing, allowing to stand, and crystallizing to obtain pure melamine; the melamine modified urea-formaldehyde glue prepared by adding 61-65 parts 37% formaldehyde into a reaction kettle, heating to 30-50 degrees C, adjusting pH value to 6.5-8.5, then adding 34-37 parts urea accounting for 50-70% of the total weight of urea, and mixing to obtain a reaction solution, heating the reaction solution to 70 degrees C, heating to 90 degrees C by exothermic, measuring pH value does not less than 5.5-7.5, reacting for 30 minutes, adjusting pH value of the reaction liquid to 4-5, observing end point of the reaction, measured viscosity by coating -4 cup reaches to 25-30 seconds/25 degrees , when pH value is adjusted to 6.5-7.5, adding the second batch of urea i.e. remaining 34-37 pts. wt. accounting for 30-50% total weight of urea, stirring for 30 minutes, adding 2-5 parts melamine, stirring uniformly, then adjusting pH value to 7.5-8, and cooling to 30 degrees C by cold water.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The melamine solution has a concentration of 8-12%, preferably 10%. The added amount of melamine solution is 8-12% (preferably 10%) weight of the material.

PN WO2022107033-A1
TI Producing carbonate or bicarbonate of alkali metal in solid form from sulfate of alkali metal includes e.g. reacting sulfate of alkali metal with alkaline earth metal sulfides in aqueous medium and reacting the sulfides with carbon dioxide.
AU VAN VUUREN D S
   MAREE J P
AE UNIV PRETORIA (UYPR-Non-standard)
   UNIV LIMPOPO (UYLI-Non-standard)
GA 2022730603
AB    NOVELTY - Producing a carbonate or a bicarbonate of an alkali metal in solid form from a sulfate of the alkali metal, includes: reacting a sulfate of an alkali metal with at least one alkaline earth metal sulfides in aqueous medium, thus forming an aqueous solution of the sulfides of the alkali metal and the sulfates of earth metal in solid form in a first reaction step; reacting sulfides of alkali metal with carbon dioxide (CO2) in gaseous form in the aqueous solution of the sulfides of the alkali metal, thus forming an aqueous solution of bicarbonate of the alkali metal and gaseous hydrogen sulfide in a second reaction step; recovering carbonate or the bicarbonate of alkali metal in solid form from aqueous solution of bicarbonate of metal in recovery step; and regenerating the metal sulfides by subjecting the sulfates of the earth metal formed in first reaction step to carbothermal reduction, thus obtaining regenerated alkaline earth sulfides in earth metal sulfide regeneration step.
   USE - The method is useful for producing carbonate or a bicarbonate of alkali metal in solid form from a sulfate of the alkali metal.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes barium sulfate that has a lower solubility in water compared to gypsum, which would be formed when reacting the alkali metal sulfate with barium sulfide or barium hydrosulfide; and utilizes significantly less energy to reduce barium sulfite than that which is required to reduce calcium sulfate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for an alkali metal carbonate and/or an alkali metal bicarbonate in solid form produced by the method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The alkaline earth metal sulfides include a hydrosulfide of the alkaline earth metal and the method includes producing the hydrosulfide of the alkaline earth metal as an aqueous solution of it by reacting sulfide of the alkaline earth metal in water with gaseous hydrogen sulfide produced in the second reaction step. The method includes separating the sulfates of the alkaline earth metal, produced in the first reaction step in solid form, from the aqueous solution of sulfides of the alkali metal in a first separation step ahead of the second reaction step. The method includes recovering residual alkaline earth metal that is dissolved in the aqueous solution of the sulfides of the alkali metal by reacting residual alkaline earth metal with carbon dioxide in gaseous form, alkali metal carbonate, and alkali metal bicarbonate, thus producing a precipitate comprising a carbonate of the alkaline earth metal in solid form. The residual alkaline earth metal recovery step is performed ahead of the second reaction step and the method includes separating the precipitate comprising the carbonate of the alkaline earth metal in solid form from the solution of the sulfides of the alkali metal or the residual alkaline earth metal recovery step is combined with the second reaction step and separating the precipitate comprising the carbonate of the alkaline earth metal in solid form from the aqueous solution of the bicarbonate of the alkali metal. The alkaline earth metal is calcium and the alkaline earth metal sulfide that is regenerated in the regeneration step is calcium sulfide and reacting regenerated calcium sulfide in water with gaseous hydrogen sulfide produced in the second reaction step, thus producing an aqueous solution of regenerated calcium hydrosulfide. The alkaline earth metal is barium and alkaline earth metal sulfide that is regenerated in the regeneration step is barium sulfide, and dissolving the regenerated barium sulfide in water, thus producing an aqueous solution of regenerated barium sulfide. The alkali earth metal is calcium and the carbonate of the alkaline earth metal, separated form from the aqueous solution of the bicarbonate of the alkali metal in the second separation step and regenerating calcium hydrosulfide by subjecting the calcium carbonate to thermal treatment, thus producing calcium oxide (CaO) and reacting the CaO with the hydrogen sulfide formed in the second reaction step to produce regenerated calcium di(hydrogensulfide). The alkali metal is barium and the carbonate of the alkaline earth metal, separated form from the aqueous solution of the bicarbonate of the alkali metal in the second separation step and reacting the barium carbonate with alkali metal sulfate ahead of the first reaction step, thus producing insoluble barium sulfate and recovering the residual alkaline earth metal that was dissolved in the aqueous solution of the sulfides of the alkali metal. The method includes performing the first reaction step using the regenerated alkaline earth metal sulfide or calcium sulfide or calcium hydrosulfide or barium sulfide as the alkaline earth metal sulfide. The recovery step includes subjecting the aqueous solution of the bicarbonate of the alkali metal to one of evaporative crystallization, cooling crystallization, and eutectic freeze crystallization to recover the carbonate or bicarbonate of the alkali metal in solid form from the aqueous solution of the bicarbonate of the alkali metal. The evaporative crystallization is performed and the bicarbonate of the alkali metal in the aqueous solution of the bicarbonate of the alkali metal is sodium bicarbonate, and sodium carbonate is recovered in solid form or the bicarbonate of the alkali metal in the aqueous solution of the bicarbonate of the alkali metal is potassium bicarbonate, and potassium bicarbonate is recovered in solid form. The bicarbonate of the alkali metal is recovered in solid form and the method includes subjecting the bicarbonate of the alkali metal in solid form to heat treatment, thus producing a carbonate of the alkali metal in solid form. Preferred Components: The alkali metal comprises sodium or potassium, the alkaline earth metal comprises calcium or barium and the alkaline earth metal sulfide is/are sulfide and/or a hydrosulfide of the alkaline earth metal.
   EXAMPLE - The examples provide details of drawings covered in the description of drawing section that do not support the claims.

PN CN114506839-A
TI Preparing conductive aerogel comprises filling the            sol into hydrophilic glass mold and filling the            container, soaking and washing wet gel with absolute            ethyl alcohol, subjecting alcoholic gel for carbon            dioxide supercritical drying, and performing high            temperature treatment.
AU YUAN Z
   CUI Z
   CHENG F
   MA D
   LI E
   SHEN Y
AE UNIV XIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYXT-C)
GA 202271492P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing conductive aerogel comprises (i)                mixing resorcinol, formaldehyde aqueous solution,                sodium carbonate and water, and stirring for 1-2                hours by magnetic force to form mixed solution;                (ii) allowing to stand the mixed solution in a                constant temperature box for 1-2 days to obtain                sol; (iii) washing and soaking the glass mold with                diluted aqueous ammonia, washing with deionized                water, and drying to obtain hydrophilic glass mold;                (iv) filling the sol into hydrophilic glass mold                and filling the container, covering with                hydrophilic glass cover, sealing and allowing to                stand to obtain wet gel; (v) soaking and washing                the wet gel with absolute ethyl alcohol to obtain                alcoholic gel; (vi) subjecting alcoholic gel for                carbon dioxide supercritical drying to obtain                organic aerogel; (vii) placing a quartz mold                containing organic aerogel in a tubular furnace,                further performing high temperature treatment under                inert atmosphere protection to remove residual                organic matter in the sample.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing conductive                aerogel.
   ADVANTAGE - The aerogel has porous and fractal structure,                and its physical and chemical properties are                obviously different from those of dense solids of                the same material. At the same time, the material                of aerogel is rich in diversity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                electrically conductive aerogel sheet obtained by                using the above preparation method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart of a                conductive aerogel preparation method (Drawing                includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the                  temperature of the constant temperature box is                  45-50degreesC. In the step (iv), the temperature                  of the constant temperature box is 45-50degreesC.                  In the step (v), soaking and washing with                  absolute ethanol for 3-4 days, and replacing the                  solvent every 1 day. In the step (vi), the                  temperature of supercritical drying is set to                  40-42degrees Celsius, the air pressure is set to                  10-11 MPa, the heating rate is set to                  0.5-1degreesC/minute, the constant temperature                  time is 3-4 hours, and the decompression rate is                  1-3 MPa/hour. In the step (vii), the quartz mold                  is composed of two quartz plates of the same size                  and two quartz gaskets of the same size, and the                  heat treatment temperature is 850-1050degrees                  Celsius, the inert atmosphere is high-purity                  nitrogen, the heating rate is 8-10degrees                  Celsius/minute, and the constant temperature time                  is 3-4 hours.Preferred Components: In the step (i), the                  formaldehyde solution is formaldehyde solution                  with a mass fraction of 38%, and the mass ratio                  of resorcinol, formaldehyde solution, sodium                  carbonate and water is 1:1.5:0.001-0.01:1-10. In                  the step (iii), the concentration of dilute                  aqueous ammonia is 0.5-1 M, and the soaking time                  is 12-24 hours.

PN CN114369213-A
TI Preparing polyethylene glycol terephthalate            tackifier useful in wind power, automobile, ship and            aerospace fields, comprises e.g. performing            esterification reaction under action of first catalyst,            and reacting epoxidized and methacrylic acid under            action of third catalyst.
AU DUO M
   LIU L
   TANG Y
AE HEBEI MINGRUN COMPOSITE MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO              LTD (HEBE-Non-standard)
GA 202265298Q
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing polyethylene terephthalate (PET)                tackifier comprises (a) mixing terephthalic acid                and ethylene glycol, and performing esterification                reaction under the action of first catalyst to                obtain polyethylene glycol terephthalate, (b)                reacting the polyethylene glycol terephthalate and                epoxy resin under the effect of second catalyst,                (c) reacting epoxidized polyethylene glycol                terephthalate and methacrylic acid under the action                of the third catalyst, so as to obtain vinyl                polyethylene glycol terephthalate, and (d)                performing copolymerization reaction on the vinyl                polyethylene glycol terephthalate, glycidyl                methacrylate and initiator to obtain PET                tackifier.
   USE - The tackifier is useful in wind power,                automobile, ship and aerospace fields.
   ADVANTAGE - The PET foaming material has excellent                mechanical property and bubble hole shape, and is                environment-friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   PET tackifier prepared by the preparation                method;
   PET foaming material, comprising 100 pts. wt.                PET resin, 0.1-3 pts. wt. nucleating agent, 1.5-2.5                pts. wt. foaming agent, which is carbon dioxide and                0.1-3 pts. wt. tackifier, where the tackifier is                the tackifier; and
   preparing the PET foaming material, comprising                (1) premixing the PET resin, nucleating agent and                tackifier to obtain mixed raw materials, and (2)                carrying out extrusion foaming of the mixed raw                material and foaming agent so as to obtain the PET                foaming material, where the foaming agent is carbon                dioxide, and the tackifier is the tackifier.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: In the step (1), the                  molar mass of terephthalic acid is greater than                  the molar mass of ethylene glycol, and obtained                  the polyethylene terephthalate with carboxy at                  both ends of the molecular chain. The first                  catalyst is antimony-based catalysts and/or                  titanium-based catalysts. The mixed reaction of                  terephthalic acid and ethylene glycol is carried                  out, and the esterification reaction is carried                  out by heating to 220-260&#8451; under                  nitrogen protection, the temperature is raised to                  above 270&#8451; after the reaction system is                  uniform and transparent, and pre-polycondensation                  is carried out by vacuuming, so as to obtain                  polyethylene terephthalate. In the step (2), the                  epoxy resin is bisphenol A epoxy resin, aliphatic                  glycidyl ether epoxy resin, glycidyl ester type                  epoxy resin and/or polyphenol type glycidyl ether                  epoxy resin. The second catalyst quaternary                  ammonium salt or triphenylphosphine. The added                  amount of the second catalyst is 5% of the sum of                  the mass of the polyethylene terephthalate and                  the epoxy resin. The third catalyst is quaternary                  ammonium salt or triphenylphosphine. The addition                  amount of the third catalyst is 5% of the sum of                  the mass of the epoxidized polyethylene                  terephthalate and methacrylic acid. In the step                  (4), the initiator described is benzoyl peroxide                  (BPO) and/or azobisisobutyronitrile (AIBN). The                  addition amount of the initiator is 5-5% of the                  sum of the mass of the vinyl polyethylene                  terephthalate and glycidyl methacrylate. In the                  step (2), (3) and (4), the solvents are acetone,                  benzene, toluene, xylene, trichloroethane and/or                  carbon tetrachloride. The intrinsic viscosity of                  the PET resin is greater than 0.6 dl/g,                  preferably 0.8-1.5 dl/g. The nucleating agent is                  talc, calcium carbonate, silicon dioxide and/or                  modified nano-montmorillonite. The foaming agent                  is liquid carbon dioxide.Preferred Method: The method drying the PET                  resin at 120-140&#8451; for 6-8 hours before                  premixing the PET resin with the nucleating agent                  and the tackifier, the air injection pressure of                  the foaming agent is 10-20 MPa, and the                  temperature of the extrusion foaming is                  230-270&#8451;, the mixed raw materials are                  added to the extruder for melt blending, and the                  foaming agent is injected into the extruder for                  continuous extrusion foaming with the mixed raw                  materials, and the PET foam material is obtained                  after foaming, shaping and cooling. The intrinsic                  viscosity of the PET resin is greater than 0.6                  dl/g. The nucleating agent component comprises                  talc, calcium carbonate, silicon dioxide and/or                  modified nano-montmorillonite. The foaming agent                  is liquid carbon dioxide.

AU WANG J
   XIONG B
   CHEN J
   LUO Z
   LI S
   XIONG Y
   DING X
   ZHANG J
AE JIANGXI DANXIA BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202246762A
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting chlorophyll from Spirulina comprises (i) selecting dry Spirulina powder; (ii) pouring the Spirulina powder in 5-10 times of 85-90% ethanol at 2-10degrees Celsius, filling nitrogen, extracting for 3-5 hours in an outer wall freezing reflux extracting device, and filtering to obtain leaching filtrate; (iii) vacuum concentrating the leaching filtrate at 40-45degrees Celsius until the original volume is 50-60% to obtain extract concentrated solution; (iv) passing the concentrated solution through an adsorption resin for enriching and removing impurities, then using an eluent to analyze to obtain chromatographic viscous liquid; (v) extracting chromatography viscous liquid by supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device for removing fat at 40-45degrees Celsius under pressure of 9-12 MPa to obtain chlorophyll semifinished product; and (vi) drying the chlorophyll semifinished product by using a low temperature continuous vacuum drier.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting chlorophyll from Spirulina.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes the outer wall freezing reflux extraction device for leaching, adsorption resin for enrichment and impurity removal, supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device for fat removal, and low-temperature continuous vacuum dryer for drying; has mild process conditions; requires less device investment; utilizes recyclable reagents; is suitable for continuous and large-scale industrial production; and maximizes the chlorophyll content while ensuring the yield. The leached Spirulina waste can be used as feed raw material, with little pollution and short production cycle.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The adsorption resin is CD-8, HP2MGL, SP850, FPA90CL and/or SP70. The eluent is acetone and/or ethyl acetate.Preferred Method: The drying temperature of the low-temperature continuous vacuum dryer is 40-50degreesC, and the continuous drying time is 1-2 hours.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The composition further comprises one or more silicates. The silicates comprise clay and zeolite species. The clay and zeolite species comprise halloysite, kaolinite, illite, montmorillonite, talc, sepiolite, pyrophyllite, erionite, stilbite, mordenite or their combinations. Preferred Composition: The composition comprises 1-20 wt.% silicates.

The mechanical refining is done using a conical refiner or a disc refiner. The method further involves removing impurities from the biogas before removing the carbon dioxide, where the impurities comprise at least one of ammonia and hydrogen sulfide. The renewable natural gas is produced at a rate of at least 2000 MMBTU per day. The method further involves producing a controlled release fertilizer using the digestate, reducing nitrogen emissions by applying the controlled release fertilizer as a soil amendment, separating the biogas to produce an acid-rich stream, producing a controlled release fertilizer using the acid-rich stream and the digestate, and separating water from the digestate for recycle. When the biogas comprises methane, the process further involves reacting at least a portion of the methane with steam at a pressure of 3-15 bar to produce hydrogen. The controlled release fertilizer preparation composition further involves adding an acid to the solids fraction to produce a humates sludge paste, adding a base to the solids fraction to produce a fulvates sludge paste, treating the liquid fraction to produce brine solids. The addition of the acid reduces the pH to 2-4. The addition of the base increases the pH to 7-9. Preferred Composition: The solid content of digestate is 2-5%, preferably about 3.5%. The methane concentration of the renewable natural gas is at least 90%. The feedstock comprises 90% or more poultry waste. Preferred Components: The acid comprises sulfuric acid. The base comprises potassium hydroxide. The brine solids are polyhalite brine solids. Preferred Properties: The feedstock has a moisture content of 94%. The slurry has an average particle size of 10 mu m or less. The process reduces nitrogen emissions sufficient to offset at least about 550000 tons per year of carbon dioxide equivalent. The nutrients absorbed by the controlled release fertilizer are released from the controlled release fertilizer at the rate of 15% or less in 24 hours or 75% or less in 28 days. The nitrogen emissions are reduced by at least 10%, preferably at least 25%.

TI Calcining calcium oxide by carbide slag, comprises            e.g. hot flue gas provided by hot-blast stove, after            non-metal heat exchanger and air for heat exchange, and            then desulfurization, denitration, collecting dust and            discharging into atmosphere.
AU LAI R
   ZHU M
   LU J
   HUANG X
   LI H
AE XINJIANG KAISHENG BUILDING MATERIAL DESI (CHNB-C)
GA 2022055057
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves sending a calcium carbide                slag powder to a first-stage preheater C1 and a                second-stage preheater C2 in turn through the                conveying equipment. The temperature of the exhaust                gas from the first-stage preheater C1 is less than                or equal to 250degreesC, which is filtered by the                dust collector and discharged into the atmosphere.                The carbide slag entering the decomposition furnace                is decomposed into calcium oxide and water vapor                after being calcined by the hot air suspension                method at 600-650 degreesC. The decomposed calcium                oxide enters a tertiary separator C3 together with                water vapor for gas-solid separation. The calcium                oxide enters the primary cooler L1 after the                calcium oxide and water vapor are separated, and                the water vapor enters the secondary preheater. The                calcium oxide after heat exchange in the primary                cooler enters the secondary cooler and the tertiary                cooler in turn to continue cooling, and the cooled                calcium oxide is stored for use.
   USE - Method for calcining calcium oxide from                carbide slag.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses hot air as firing heat source,                where carbon dioxide content is very low, which                effectively avoids the phenomenon that carbide slag                is decomposed into calcium oxide, and again reacts                with carbon dioxide to generate calcium                carbonate.

The lye is at least one aqueous solution of sodium hydroxide, ammonia, ammonium bicarbonate or sodium bicarbonate. PN WO2021204310-A1
TI Artificial turf system for use in e.g. private garden, has elastic layer built up on rock layer, where artificial turf on elastic layer is added, and layer of filler material is interspersed and brushed in before.
AU WILSHUSEN A
AE 7SEVENRELAX GROUP GMBH (SEVE-Non-standard)
   WILSHUSEN A (WILS-Individual)
GA 2021B81602
AB    NOVELTY - The artificial turf system has a rock layer (11) that is arranged on a substrate (10) consisting of rock material. An elastic layer (12) is built up on the rock layer, and an artificial turf (14) is accommodated on the elastic layer. A filler material layer has broken rock material. The rock layer contains rock olivine to a predominant part of its volume. The volume fraction of the rock oxide is 100% or more. The grain size of rock layer is 1-3 mm, and that of filler layer is 0.250-0.500 mm. The layers are designed in such a way that the water permeability through the layers is less than or equal to 60 liters per minute/m2.
   USE - Artificial turf system used in private gardens and in sports field construction, and in commercial sector.
   ADVANTAGE - The rock olivine stone is excellent as a rock layer but also suitable as a filler material layer in the production of the artificial turf system, since the product olivines is suitable for absorbing and binding carbon dioxide to a high degree. The olivin absorbs greater than or equal to 1 times its own weight in carbon dioxide, and occurs everywhere on earth.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for production of artificial turf system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a cross-sectional view of the artificial turf system.
   Substrate (10)
   Rock layer (11)

   Infill material (13)

PN CN113210021-A
TI Preparation of transition metal-based composite catalyst used for promoting desorption of carbon dioxide-rich liquid by heating solution prepared by adding benzene-1,3,5-tricarboxylic acid into ethanol water solution.
AU WU L
   AN S
   QI T
   WANG Q
   ZHAO Y
   ZENG B
AE UNIV NORTH CHINA ELECTRIC POWER (UYHD-C)
GA 202194093F
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of transition metal-based composite catalyst comprises adding 4.2 g benzene-1,3,5-tricarboxylic acid into ethanol water solution, and preparing solution A; adding 8.68 g cerium nitrate hexahydrate (Ce(NO3)3.6H2O) into water to prepare solution B; heating the solution A at 60 degrees C, pouring the solution B, and quickly stirring for 1 hour; filtering and collecting the precipitates, and washing and drying the precipitates; putting the dried product in a muffle furnace and calcining at 350 degrees C for 2 hours to obtain calcined product; and adding water in the calcined product for dispersing treatment, adding phosphotungstic acid aqueous solution, where mass ratio of phosphotungstic acid and calcined product is 15:100, stirring and mixing for 20 minutes, filtering, and drying.
   USE - The method is for preparation of transition metal-based composite catalyst used for promoting desorption of carbon dioxide (CO2)-rich liquid (claimed). The catalyst is used as proton donor.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst promotes proton transfer in CO2 regeneration reaction, so as to realize CO2 high-efficiency desorption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) transition metal-based composite catalyst; and
   (2) application of the transition metal-based composite catalyst for promoting the desorption of CO2-rich liquid comprising absorbing flue gas in ethanolamine solution to be saturated, and obtaining ethanolamine rich solution, adding the transition metal-based composite catalyst, and desorbing at 80-90 degrees C so as to realize the desorption of ethanolamine rich liquid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The ethanol aqueous solution is formed by mixing 20 ml water (H2O) and 20 ml ethanol. The solution B is prepared by adding 90 ml H2O in 8.68 g Ce(NO3)3.6H2O. The washed precipitate is dried for 8 hours at 70 degrees C. The drying in the sixth step is conducted at 100 degrees C for 8 hours. The concentration of transition metal-based composite catalyst in the ethanolamine rich solution is 1 mass%.

AE UNIV SEOUL IND COOP GROUP (USEO-C)
GA 202182418K
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst structure having core-shell structure including a core with ceramic particles or a metal and a shell formed on the core, is new. The shell is a metal double-layered hydroxide structure made of metal hydroxide based compound (I) or composite metal oxide structure derived from compound (I).
   USE - The catalyst is useful in Fischer-Tropsch, natural gas reforming reactions, methanation of carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide oxidation, and hydrogen production reactions (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst structure can generate catalyst nanoparticles with uniform size and high surface dispersion on the surface of the micro-sized structure of the catalyst structure, and has excellent physicochemical properties and catalytic performance of the catalyst; is simple and very efficient in preparation; has very remarkable catalytic properties and heat and material transfer properties, so it can be used very well in many processes using the catalyst.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Catalyst structure having core-shell structure including a core including ceramic particles or a metal and a shell formed on the core, is claimed. The shell is a metal double-layered hydroxide structure made of metal hydroxide based compound of formula ((M(2+)1-xM(3+)x(OH)2)(x+)(A(n-))x/n.mH2O) (I) or composite metal oxide structure derived from compound (I).
   M(2+) and M(3+) = cations of divalent metal and trivalent metal, respectively;
   A(n-) = intercalated anion;
   x = surface charge determined by the ratio of the two metal cations (M(3+)/(M(2+)+M(3+));
   x = 0-1;
   n = 1-3; and
   m = 0.1-15.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for producing catalyst structure.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation method of catalyst structure comprises (a) performing hydrothermal reaction by adding ceramic particles or metal particles for a core to an aqueous solution of a catalyst precursor salt. Preferred Components: The ceramic particles or metals included in core have a thermal conductivity of more than 50 W/(m-K). The ceramic particles or metal included in the core is silicon carbide (SiC), iron (Fe), aluminum (Al), and/or copper (Cu). The M2+ is at least one of nickel (II) ion, magnesium (II) ion, cobalt (II) ion, manganese (II) ion, zinc (II) ion, copper (II) ion, and iron (II) ion. The M3+ is at least one of iron (III) ion, aluminum (III) ion, chromium (III) ion, manganese (III) ion, gallium (III) ion, cobalt (III) ion, cerium (III) ion, and nickel (III) ion, where A n- is at least one of chloride ion, bromide ion, hydroxide ion, nitrate ion, carbonate ion, sulfate ion, phosphate ion, hydrogen phosphate ion, and dihydrogen phosphate ion. The metal double-layered hydroxide structure or a composite metal oxide structure derived is grown on the core along the ab-dimension perpendicular to the surface of the core to form a layered nanosheet. The shell comprises essentially of layered nanosheets. The catalyst prepared by hydrogen (H2) reduction of the catalyst structure has a core-shell structure, and contains metal oxide and metal catalyst particles. The shell includes a layered nanosheet formed on the core along the ab-plane perpendicular to the surface of the core, where metal catalyst particles are dispersed in the layered nanosheet. The metal catalyst particles contain at least one metal of nickel, magnesium, cobalt, manganese, zinc, copper, and iron. The metal oxide is a single oxide of one metal or a complex oxide of two metals generated from at least one of a divalent cation (M2+) and a trivalent cation (M3+) of the double-layer hydroxide structure. The aqueous solution of the catalyst precursor salt comprises a salt of at least one metal of nickel, magnesium, cobalt, manganese, zinc, copper, and iron. Preferred Method: The step (c) of preparing a catalyst comprises reducing the catalyst structure prepared under hydrogen.
   EXAMPLE - The examples provide details of synthesis of nickel catalyst on aluminum core particles that do not support the claims.

TI Preparation method of ferrocene carboxylic acid,            involves centrifugal filtering hydrolyzed mixture with            centrifuge, washing filter cake with deionized water to            specified pH, drying filter cake naturally and drying            at specified temperature.
AU SUN X
   SUN W
   LIU Y
   ZHANG J
AE ANHUI ZESHENG TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 202168837E
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves adding toluene to the                reactor one by one, starting stirring, add                ferrocene and toluene, then vacuuming, continuing                stirring and passing cooling water to adjust the                internal temperature to 15-18 degrees C, then                passing carbon dioxide gas 30-40min. The anhydrous                aluminum chloride to the aforementioned reaction                kettle and carbon dioxide gas is continuously                introduced. A portion of crushed ice and deionized                water are added to the hydrolysis kettle, and the                above reaction mixture is carefully transferred to                the hydrolysis kettle. The hydrolyzed mixture is                centrifugal filtered with a centrifuge, and the                filter cake is washed with deionized water to pH                equal to 6, then the filter cake is dried                naturally, and then dried at 90-100 degrees C 6                8hr, then the solid is crushed with a crusher. The                aforementioned pulverized solid is poured into a                washing pot filled with a mixed solution of toluene                and deionized water, and fully stirred for 2 to 3                hours.
   USE - Preparation method of ferrocene carboxylic                acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The nitrogen gas is introduced during the                hydrolysis process to isolate oxygen and put in                crushed ice in batches to continuously control the                temperature of the hydrolysis kettle to ensure that                the materials are not oxidized before hydrolysis                and reduce the generation of impurities.

AE KLUNGNESS J H (KLUN-Individual)

AB    NOVELTY - Making paper (M1) comprises combining carbon dioxide, calcium carbonate, and water in a reactor to produce calcium bicarbonate, providing a cellulosic fibrous material, comprising many elongated fibers having a fiber wall surrounding a hollow interior, the fibrous material having moisture content, adding calcium bicarbonate solution to the cellulosic fibrous material to form a pulp mixture, the calcium bicarbonate solution containing 0.5-16 wt.% solids of calcium bicarbonate, the resulting pulp mixture having 0.1-65 wt.% total solids, refining the pulp mixture so that at least some of the calcium ions become associated with the reactive sites in the fiber walls, forming a web from the pulp mixture and wet pressing the web.
   USE - The methods are useful for making paper.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: increases brightness, opacity, bonding properties, and run-nability of the paper machine; produces lightweight high-opacity newsprint; reduces grammage and drying energy costs, hence cost effective; and utilizes web shrinkage which improves water removal for fiber-loaded pulps as compared to the conventional method.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for making paper (M2) comprising providing the cellulosic fibrous material, adding the calcium bicarbonate solution to the cellulosic fibrous material to form a pulp mixture, the resulting pulp mixture having 0.1-5 wt.% total solids, refining the pulp mixture, forming a web from the pulp mixture and wet pressing the web.

   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

Preferred Method: In the step (c), the reaction temperature is 120-180 degrees C, and the reaction time is 1-48 hours. In the step (d), the reaction temperature is 80-120 degrees C, and the reaction time is 0.5-4 hours.

   (C) entering acid gasses of the latter entering process flow comprising mixture of carbon dioxide and hydrogen sulfide; and
   (D) entering the latter flow comprising acid gasses carbon dioxide and hydrogen sulfide.

An outer side of the nuclear fuel block is provided with a reactive and radioactive control ring and provided with a heat discharging device. The interior of the container is provided with an aerodynamic flow passage, and is filled with supercritical carbon dioxide or nitrogen.

The use of electricity allows for                significant operational advantages during start-up                or shut-down, or to respond to process variability.                The most advantageous operation is achieved by                eliminating the most expensive and/or polluting                fuel sources. The carbon dioxide emissions are                reduced, and excess steam production is reduced or                eliminated and/or the energy efficiency of the                furnaces are increased. The plant has capital cost,                environmental permitting and monitoring, ease of                operation, and/or heating designed to minimize coke                formation. The olefin synthesis plant increases                energy efficiency.

TI Nitrogen-doped porous carbon material, e.g. used            for preparing supercapacitor material and activated            carbon electrode.
AU HAN K
   LI J

   WANG M
