   NIU S
   REN K
   JI T
   ZHANG X
   ZHANG J
   LI M
   CAO Y
AE UNIV SHANDONG (USHA-C)
   UNIV SHANDONG (USHA-C)
GA 202075193F
AB    NOVELTY - A nitrogen-doped porous carbon material which                contains 13.6-19.3 wt.% nitrogen atoms. The carbon                material has specific surface area of 1600-3500                m2/g, 2-50 nm mesoporous diameter for 20-40% of all                pores, and average pore diameter of 2-20 nm.
   USE - The nitrogen-doped porous carbon material used                for preparing supercapacitor material and activated                carbon electrode. The nitrogen-doped porous carbon                material is also used as carbon dioxide adsorbent                (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon material has larger specific                capacitance of about 847 F/g at a current density                of 0.1 A/g and better capacitance retention rate of                about 99.7% after 5000 cycles of charging and                discharging, and good carbon dioxide adsorption                performance due to its excellent pore structure                distribution.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) preparation of the carbon material                comprising washing, drying and crushing carbon                precursor to obtain biomass powder, high                temperature carbonizing to 600-800 degrees C under                protective gas or ammonia atmosphere to obtain                carbonized product, adding saturated solution of                chemical activator and nitrogen source material,                ultrasonically mixing, soaking, and heating under                inert atmosphere;
   (2) an activated carbon electrode comprising                nitrogen-doped porous carbon material, conductive                agent and binder; and
   (3) preparation of the activated carbon                electrode comprising mixing raw materials to obtain                slurry, coating on current collector and drying; or                heating slurry, and shaping.
   The nitrogen source material is melamine,                polyaniline or pyridine. The conductive agent is                conductive carbon black, acetylene black, graphite                additives or carbon nanotube additive. The binder                is PTFE, polyvinylidene difluoride, polyvinyl                alcohol, sodium light methyl cellulose, polyolefin,                rubber or polyurethane. The current collector is                copper foil, aluminum foil, nickel mesh or                stainless steel foil.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The carbonization is done                  for 1.5-2.5 hours. The ultrasonic mixing is done                  at frequency of 10-50 kHz and power of 80-150 W                  for 4-8 minutes. The heating is done at 750-800                  degrees C for 2-2.5 hours. The method further                  comprises washing carbon product with 10-20 wt.%                  hydrochloric acid and with deionized water to                  neutralize. Preferred Components: The saturated                  solution of chemical activator is potassium                  hydroxide saturated solution. The mass ratio of                  carbonized product, saturated solution of                  chemical activator and nitrogen source material                  is 1-3:1-5:0.1-2.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon precursor                  include but not limited to garlic braid,                  sargassum, wood chips, husk and straw.

TI Composition used e.g. to produce oils and fuels, comprises bacterial cell in culture medium i.e. in contact with feedstock comprising e.g. syngas, gaseous carbon dioxide, inorganic carbon and/or solid products of pyrolytic reaction.
AU KUREK I
   DYSON L
   JANSSON C
   REED J S
AE KIVERDI INC (KIVE-Non-standard)
GA 2020503864
AB    NOVELTY - Composition comprises a bacterial cell in a culture medium that is in contact with a feedstock comprising syngas, gaseous carbon dioxide, other 1C compounds, inorganic carbon, liquid, gaseous, and/or solid products of pyrolytic reactions. The bacterial cell: chemoautotrophically converts the feedstock into organic compounds comprising at least one lipid or hydrocarbon that comprises fatty alcohols, alkenes, unsaturated fatty acids, diacids or hydroxy acids; comprises at least one exogenous nucleic acid sequence encoding an enzyme comprising a fatty acid aldehyde acyl-acyl carrier protein (ACP) reductase, a fatty acid aldehyde decarbonylase, a thioesterase, a fatty acyl-coenzyme (Co)A/fatty acyl-ACP reductase, a fatty acid hydroxylase, a desaturase, a cytochrome P450 enzyme, or a NADH or NADPH cytochrome P450 reductase; and comprises Ralstonia sp., Alcaligenes sp., or Cupriavidus sp..
   USE - The composition is useful for: producing organic compounds comprising lipid or hydrocarbon that comprises fatty alcohols, alkenes, unsaturated fatty acids, diacids or hydroxy acids; or producing oils, fuels, and oleochemicals.
   ADVANTAGE - The composition: is capable of producing and/or secreting at least 60 wt.% of the lipids or hydrocarbons; utilizes the culture medium, which does not comprise an organic carbon source as a primary source of carbon for microbial growth or biosynthesis; improves lipid yield and/or lowers production costs; and is not dependent upon light, and does not require any type of sugar, organic compound or fixed carbon to grow, metabolize and/or synthesize lipid molecules.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The bacterial cell comprises: Rhodococcus DSM 43205, Rhodococcus DSM 43206, Rhodococcus DSM 3346, Cupriavidus DSM 531, or Cupriavidus DSM 541; and at least a first and a second exogenous nucleic acid sequence but not greater than 5 exogenous nucleic acid sequences. The exogenous nucleic acid sequence consists of: a first, second, and a third exogenous nucleic acid sequence, where the first exogenous nucleic acid sequence encodes fatty acid aldehyde acyl-ACP reductase, the second exogenous nucleic acid sequence encodes a fatty acid aldehyde decarbonylase, and the third exogenous nucleic acid sequence encodes a thioesterase; and an exogenous nucleic acid sequence that encodes a thioesterase. The lipids or hydrocarbons comprise: at least one organic molecule having a carbon chain length of at least 8C and at least one carbon-carbon double bond; a mixture of lipids having at least one unsaturated fatty acid molecule having a carbon chain length of 8-30C; and at least one alkene, alkyne, hydroxy acid, dicarboxylic acid, and/or unsaturated fatty acid at a level higher than the quantity of the alkene, alkyne, hydroxy acid, dicarboxylic acid, and/or unsaturated fatty acid in the same bacterial cell not comprising the at least first exogenous nucleic acid sequence. The lipids comprise: at least one hydroxy acid molecule having a carbon chain length of at least 6C; at least one diacid acid molecule having a carbon chain length of at least 6C; a mixture of lipids having at least one hydroxy acid molecule having a carbon chain length of 8-24C; and a mixture of lipids having at least one dicarboxylic acid molecule having a carbon chain length of 6-18C. The hydrocarbon comprises a mixture of hydrocarbons having at least one desaturated hydrocarbon molecule having a carbon chain length of 8-30C. Less than 10 wt.% organic compounds produced is organic acid with carbon chain length of less than or equal to 4C. The feedstock comprises: gaseous hydrogen that is utilized by the bacterial cell as an energy source for the chemoautotrophic conversion into organic compounds and that is generated or recycled using renewable, alternative, or conventional sources of power comprising at least one of photovoltaics, solar thermal, wind power, hydroelectric, nuclear, geothermal, enhanced geothermal, ocean thermal, ocean wave power, or tidal power; and syngas that is generated from lignocellulosic energy crops, crop residue, bagasse, saw dust, forestry residue, food waste, municipal solid waste, biogas, landfill gas, or stranded natural gas. The gaseous feedstock comprises carbon dioxide, hydrogen and/or carbon monoxide, and oxygen.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for cultivating new strain of Grifola frondosa 86 (T5Q9), which involves:
   (A) preparing liquid strains, inoculating activated new strain of Grifola frondosa 86 (T5Q9) into fungal liquid medium, and culturing at 140-160 revolution per minute for 14-16 days at 25 degrees C, cultivating mushrooms, preparing medium package, and then sterilizing, waiting for temperature of medium package to fall below 25 degrees C, using liquid strain prepared for inoculation, inoculation amount is (8-11)mL/kg, sealing, placing in culture room at 20-25 degrees C and keeping away from light, and relative humidity is 60-70%, after 40-50 days of cultivation, covering hyphae with mycelium and physiological maturity becomes white;
   (B) transferring to the mushroom outlet room for mushroom management, cutting mushroom bag, temperature of the mushroom outlet room is 18-20 degrees C, humidity is 90-95%, light intensity is 200-500lx during day, no light at night, carbon dioxide concentration is 1000mL/m3, cultivating to harvest, composition of culture medium includes 30-45 pts. wt. cottonseed hulls, 35-45 pts. wt. chestnut shavings, 10-20 pts. wt. bran, 1-2 pts. wt. brown sugar, 1-2 pts. wt. gypsum; and
   (C) making up to water content of 60-65%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Compositions: The activation medium is PDA plus rich medium, medium composition includes 200g/L potato, 10g/L bran, 2g/L yeast powder, 1g/L potassium dihydrogen phosphate, 1g/L anhydrous magnesium sulfate, 3g/L peptone, 20g/L glucose, 20g/L agar, remaining water. The fungal liquid medium is PDA plus rich medium without agar. Preferred Conditions: The inoculation amount of the medium package is 8.5 mL/kg. Preferred Process: The method involves selecting v shaped outlet on the wall of mycelium to cut out mushrooms, each side is 3cm long, and cutting edge is facing upward.

AB    NOVELTY - The method involves starting a temperature                monitoring device (16), a pressure monitoring                device (17), a multi-component gas monitoring                device (18) and a gas injection pump (19). The gas                injection pump is used to mix a certain proportion                of air from a gas mixing tank (21) to inject into a                large-sized telescopic copper tube (5) and a                small-sized telescopic copper tube (6) through a                gas injection tube (20) and finally release it at a                position of a multifunctional assembly device (8)                and makes full contact with a broken coal. A                electrode firearm ignites the broken coal. An                actual temperature and various gas concentrations                are monitored in real time through a temperature                detector and a multi-gas concentration detector. A                negative pressure fan is used to extract a free gas                and a smoldering-derived gas when the concentration                of both carbon dioxide (CO2) gas and methane (CH4)                gas is detected to rise sharply.

TI Method for monitoring and identifying mountain fire based on meteorological satellites, involves generating fire model to perform image recognition, and monitoring mountain fires through infrared detector and signal emitter.
AU QIU H
   DING Y
   CHEN J
   LI Q
AE MLOG WUHAN TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (MLOG-Non-standard)
GA 202016347T
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves transmitting fire signal to a satellite through a signal emitter when fire is detected by a monitoring device (S1). Peripheral image signals are obtaining after the satellite receives the signal from the monitoring device. The image signal is forwarded (S2) to fire rescue department to perform data modeling process based on the image signal. A fire model is generated (S3) to perform image recognition such that specific fire condition is displayed at fire rescue department. A carbon dioxide detecting device is fixed in a shell to control switch of the signal emitter. Mountain fires are monitored through an infrared detector, the carbon dioxide detecting device and the signal emitter. A communication module is fixed with a GPRS unit. A H.265 HDMI high-definition encoder is converted video picture into video digital signal.
   USE - Method for monitoring and identifying mountain fire based on meteorological satellites.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables reducing false alarms caused by external environmental factors such as lights, lightning and sunlight, and reducing unnecessary troubles to worker, and improving working efficiency of the worker, and enhancing using effect of monitoring devices.

TI Water cultivating system with anaerobic denitrifying device useful for high-density industrial circulating water breeding of various freshwater and marine high-quality fish species comprises circulating water breeding pond, and micro filter.
AU ZHANG L
AE ZHANG L (ZHAN-Individual)
GA 202010640A
AB    NOVELTY - Water cultivating system with anaerobic denitrifying device comprises a circulating water breeding pond (1), a micro filter (2) which filters particulate matter and residual bait excrement in water, a large-flow low-head circulating water pump (3), carbon dioxide removal drip flow biological filter (5) located above front end of a mobile biological filter bed, ultraviolet sterilizing machine and high-efficiency pure oxygen mixer. A cotton-free biological filter ball is arranged in biological filter. An anaerobic denitrifying device (4) is connected to pump pool, and removes harmful substances e.g. nitrite and nitrate by anaerobic denitrifying reaction of microorganisms. The water body purified by filter bed enters sterilizing machine which sterilizes water, and removes microorganisms that are harmful to fish. The water flowing from sterilizing machine enters oxygen mixer, after pure oxygen mixing, and it flows it back to breeding pond to form closed circulating water breeding system.
   USE - The system is useful for high-density industrial circulating water breeding of various freshwater and marine high-quality fish species.
   ADVANTAGE - The system: reuses supernatant of precipitating system of precipitating tank, and precipitate for pressing to make vegetable organic fertilizer; and achieves zero discharge of industrialized circulating water cultivating system.

PT P
PN WO2019195296-A1; US2021095122-A1
TI Polymer blend for separating target gas from gas stream, comprises thermally rearranged ortho-functionalized polyimide homo or copolymer which is polymer comprising phenylene heterocyclic group, and polybenzimidazole homo or copolymer.
AU FREEMAN B D
   PAUL D R
   MOON J D
AE UNIV TEXAS SYSTEM (TEXA-C)
   UNIV TEXAS SYSTEM (TEXA-C)
GA 2019854030
AB    NOVELTY - A polymer blend comprises a thermally rearranged ortho-functionalized polyimide homo or copolymer and a polybenzimidazole homo or copolymer. The thermally rearranged polyimide homo or copolymer is a polymer comprising a phenylene heterocyclic group.
   USE - Polymer blend is used for separating target gas e.g. oxygen, carbon dioxide or nitrogen from gas stream e.g. exhaust gas from hydrocarbon decomposition reaction, and dehydrating gas stream (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The polymer blend has improved gas transport properties, and gas selectivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) method of forming polymer blend, which involves dissolving an ortho-functionalized polyimide homo or copolymer and a polybenzimidazole homo or copolymer in a solvent to form a polymer solution, contacting a support with the polymer solution, and evaporating the solvent to provide a thin layer comprising the polymer blend on the support;
   (2) method of separating a target gas from a gas stream, which involves contacting the polymer blend with the gas stream comprising carbon dioxide, methane, ethane, ethylene, propane, propylene, butane, carbon monoxide, nitrogen, helium, argon, or air at 0-400 degrees C, such that the target gas selectively permeates the polymer blend, thereby being separated the gas stream; and
   (3) method of dehydrating a gas stream, which involves contacting the polymer blend with the gas stream comprising air, ethylene glycol, acetic acid, or synthesis gas, such that water permeates the polymer blend, thereby being separated from the gas stream.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The inorganic compatibilizer is chosen from lithium chloride, lithium bromide, sodium chloride, sodium bromide, potassium chloride, potassium bromide, calcium chloride, calcium bromide, copper chloride, metal organic framework, and zeolitic imidazolate framework.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The solvent is chosen from N-methylpyrrolidone, N,N-dimethyl acetamide, methylene chloride, tetrahydrofuran, acetone, N,N-dimethylformamide, dimethyl sulfoxide, toluene, dioxanes, 1,3-dioxolane, acetonitirile, chloroform, trichloroethylene, sulfuric acid, formic acid, methanesulfonic acid, and trifluoroacetic acid.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The thermally rearranged polyimide homo or copolymer comprises a polybenzoxazole, polybenzothiazole, and/or polybenzimidazole. The polymer blend is a homogeneous, miscible blend, or an immiscible blend comprising a dispersed phase and a continuous phase. TI Welding all-steel cold tower tripod steel beam gas supply welding mechanism comprises a lower cone support for welding all-steel structure air cooling tower, a triangular frame steel beam and an intermediate splint.
AU HAN J
   LIANG J
   LI Y
   SONG H
   HE D
   LIU Y
   YANG X
AE CHINA ENERGY ENG CORP SHANXI ELECTRIC (CECG-C)
GA 201982586U
AB    NOVELTY - Welding all-steel cold tower tripod steel beam gas supply welding mechanism comprises a lower cone support for welding all-steel structure air cooling tower. A top end loop is set at the top end of the lower cone support of the all-steel structure air cooling tower. A triangular frame steel beam (25) to be welded is arranged on the lower cone support. An intermediate splint (26) is provided between adjacent two tripod steel beams. The tripod steel beam to be welded is perpendicular to the intermediate clamp. A welding bevel of the tripod steel beam to be welded has 25 degrees . A weld ceramic liner is set between the lower bottom surface of the tip of the welded steel beam welding bevel to be welded and the welded joint surface of the intermediate plate. The intermediate splint has a thickness of 20-25 mm. A compressed air duct for root cleaning of back side of the weld is respectively set between adjacent two tripod steel beams and carbon dioxide weld protection gas welding pipe (32).
   USE - Used as a welding all-steel cold tower tripod steel beam gas supply welding mechanism.
   ADVANTAGE - The mechanism has simple structure.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for construction of welding all-steel cold tower tripod steel beam gas supply welding mechanism.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The diagram shows a schematic representation of the welding all-steel cold tower tripod steel beam gas supply welding mechanism.
   Steel beam (25)
   Intermediate splint (26)
   Carbon dioxide weld protection gas welding pipe (32)

TI Pipe fillet weld automatic welding tooling, has bracket which is arranged on roller frame, and automatic welding oscillator is arranged on bracket and bracket is located on one side of rolling friction wheel.

   CHENGXI SHIPYARD CO LTD (CHSB-C)
GA 201991530F
AB    NOVELTY - The tooling has a roller frame (2) which is arranged on the sliding rail (1). The two roller frames are provided, and each roller frame is rotated to have two rolling friction wheels (3). A bracket (4) is arranged on the roller frame, and an automatic welding oscillator is arranged on the bracket. The bracket is located on one side of the rolling friction wheel. The two rolling friction wheels on one roller frame are driven wheels, and the two rolling friction wheels on one roller frame are the driving wheels.
   USE - Pipe fillet weld automatic welding tooling.
   ADVANTAGE - The pipe fillet weld automatic welding tooling realizes automatic carbon dioxide gas shielded welding, has convenient adjustment and strong adaptability, ensures that the fillet weld angles of different pipe diameters meet the requirements of the drawings, and the tooling reduces the welding time by nearly half compared with the manual carbon dioxide welding, and the welded weld seam is beautifully formed and polished free. The tooling greatly reduces the labor intensity of the welder, improves the welding efficiency, and relaxes the requirements of the welder's professional skills. The tooling ensures simple operation, convenient and flexible, and is promoted and applied in order to promote the reduction of welding and polishing activities.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a plan view of pipe fillet weld automatic welding tooling.

   Roller frame (2)

PN FR3077008-A1; WO2019145491-A1; WO2019145491-A9; EP3743195-A1
TI Use of homologous brucite-like structure or lamellar double hydroxide-type solid material in electrochemical cell for capture and/or selective release of gas, such as sulfur oxide, and anionic species e.g. divalent carbonate ions.
AU BETELU S
   IGNATIADIS I
   SERON A
AE BRGM (BRGM-Non-standard)
   BRGM (BRGM-Non-standard)
GA 201964594R
AB    NOVELTY - Use of homologous brucite-like structure or lamellar double hydroxide-type solid material in electrochemical cell for capture and/or selective release of anionic species is claimed.
   USE - Use of homologous brucite-like structure or lamellar double hydroxide-type solid material in electrochemical cell for capture and/or selective release of anionic species e.g. divalent carbonate ions, divalent sulfate ions, nitrate ions, borate ions, chlorate ions, divalent tungstate ions, divalent arsenate ions, dichromate ions and chromate ions, and gas, such as carbon dioxide, sulfur oxide, nitrogen oxide and mercaptan (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The homologous brucite-like structure or lamellar double hydroxide-type solid material has excellent durability, and enables efficient capture and/or selective release of anionic species with high selectivity and reduced electrical power consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) method of capturing and releasing anionic species using electrochemical cell, which involves circulating an electrolytic medium comprising suspension of the solid material particles between a compartment (a) comprising an electrode (a1) and a compartment (b) comprising an electrode (b1), both connected to a current generator, such that the electrolytic medium is in contact with the electrodes, and applying potential difference or electrolysis potential;
   (2) device, which comprises the electrochemical cell comprising the electrode (a1) and an electrode (b1) connected to the current generator, and the liquid electrolytic medium comprising the solid material; and
   (3) method of capturing and/or releasing gas, such as carbon dioxide, sulfur oxide, nitrogen oxide and mercaptan, which involves transforming the gas into corresponding anionic species.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method further involves concentrating the anionic species captured by the material. The solid material is a manganese-iron hydroxide-carbonate hydrate of formula: Mg8.2Fe2(OH)18.4(CO3)2.(4H2O). When the gas is carbon dioxide, the anionic species is divalent carbonate ions or mixture of divalent carbonate ions and hydrogen carbonate ions. Preferred Components: The device further comprises a low-resistive porous membrane provided between the compartments, a unit for connecting the two electrodes to the current generator, a unit for circulating the medium between the compartments, and a unit for concentrating the solid material positioned between the compartments. One among the compartment (a) and compartment (b) is anode compartment, and other is cathode compartment.

TI Device for producing methanol and co-producing ethylene glycol and LNG by coal gasification coupled coal coking, has two hydrogen outlet that is connected to hydrogen inlet of ethylene glycol synthesis unit through pipe.
AU QIAN Y
   CHEN J
   YANG S
AE UNIV SOUTH CHINA TECHNOLOGY (UYSC-C)
GA 201963530T
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a coal gasification unit that is provided with gasification raw material coal inlet, oxygen inlet and coal gasification crude syngas outlet. The coke oven gas purification unit is provided with a clean coke oven gas outlet and a tar outlet. The PSA separation unit is provided with a mixed gas outlet and two hydrogen outlets. The mixed gas outlet is connected to the intake pipe of the methane conversion unit through a pipeline. The one of the two hydrogen outlets is connected to the hydrogen inlet of the ethylene glycol synthesis unit through pipe, and other is connected to the hydrogen inlet of the methanol synthesis unit through pipe.
   USE - Device for producing methanol and co-producing ethylene glycol and liquefied natural gas (LNG) by coal gasification coupled coal coking.
   ADVANTAGE - The change unit of the coal-to-methanol process is removed. The load of the acid gas removal unit is greatly reduced without the water gas shift unit. The carbon dioxide emissions are effectively reduced and resource and energy utilization and system flexibility are significantly increased.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for producing methanol by co-production of ethylene glycol.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the block diagram of the device for producing methanol and co-producing ethylene glycol and LNG. (Drawing includes non-English language text)

PT P
PN RU2692006-C1
TI Method for cyclic gas nitriding of parts from high alloy steels has production of parts operating at high temperatures in conditions of friction with large contact loads.
AU ALEKSANDROV V A
   PETROVA L G
   BREZHNEV A A
   DEMIN P E
AE UNIV MOSC STATE TECH AUTOMOBILE ROADS (UYMO-Non-standard)
GA 201964658R
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to metallurgy and machine building, namely to chemical-thermal treatment, in particular to cyclic gas nitriding of high-alloy steels, and can be used in production of parts operating at high temperatures in conditions of friction with large contact loads. Method of cyclic gas nitriding of parts from high-alloy steels involves preliminary application of catalyst coating on surface of part, heating in furnace to temperature of 540-650 degrees C in atmosphere of ammonia and carbon dioxide with ratio of their volumes of 1:1, isothermal holding at temperature of said heating, during which saturation atmosphere is replaced cyclically in two stages in each cycle, and further cooling together with furnace in ammonia atmosphere. First stage of the isothermal holding cycle is carried out in a saturating atmosphere of ammonia in a mixture with water vapor at a ratio of volumes of said components of 1:1. Second stage of said cycle is carried out in a saturating atmosphere of ammonia in a mixture with carbon dioxide at a ratio of their volumes of 1:1. Said cyclic substitution of saturating atmosphere is repeated until nitrided layer of preset thickness is obtained. Catalytic coating used is a slurry coating containing chromium oxide Cr2O3.
   USE - Metallurgy.
   ADVANTAGE - Increased thickness of the diffused nitrogen-containing layer obtained on the surface of parts from high-alloy steels with reduction of duration of the nitriding process and without reduction of its hardness.
1 cl, 3 dwg, 1 tbl

TI Aquaponics and greenhouse system for producing            mushroom fruit, has water wall thermal mass unit housed            within solar greenhouse, and air ventilation system            housed within solar greenhouse, and providing misted            air into mushroom growing area.
AU VILLAMAR C R
AE VILLAMAR C R (VILL-Individual)
GA 201950613S
AB    NOVELTY - The system has an insulated solar greenhouse                provided with a glazing on a sun facing side at an                angle to maximize winter sunlight, and housing a                fish tank housed within the solar greenhouse. A                plant growing area is housed within the solar                greenhouse. A mushroom growing area is housed                within the solar greenhouse. A water wall thermal                mass unit is housed within the solar greenhouse and                located between the plant growing area and the                mushroom growing area. A natural air ventilation                system is housed within the solar greenhouse, and                provides misted air into the mushroom growing area,                where oxygen generated by the plant growing area is                received by the natural air ventilation system and                provided to the mushroom growing area, and carbon                dioxide generated by the mushroom growing area is                provided to the plant growing area.
   USE - Aquaponics and greenhouse system for producing                mushroom fruit.
   ADVANTAGE - The system increases robustness and                performance of devices and subsystems. The system                ensures that suction from air pumps used to power                geyser pumps is used to extract carbon dioxide from                a mushroom substrate and mushroom fruits, thus                increasing fresh air exchange, and producing                mushroom fruits with desirable characteristics as                the system is fully air powered.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a cross-sectional view of a                hard filter employed in systems for solar                greenhouse aquaponics and black soldier fly                composter and auto fish feeder.

PT P
PN CN109694078-A; CN109694078-B
TI Preparation of fluorine crystal mica used for preparation of two-dimensional crystal, involves adding sodium fluoride and potassium chloride to suspension to stir reaction, calcining precipitate and using supercritical carbon dioxide.
AU ZHANG M
AE CHANGCHUN TAIYUAN FLUOROPHLOGOPITE CO LTD (CHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201941939S
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation of fluorine crystal mica involves (1) mixing magnesite, montmorillonite and quartz sand into a millimeter fine sand mixture by a high-speed mechanical impact mill, (2) adding a nitric acid solution to the fine sand mixture to obtain a suspension, (3) adding sodium fluoride and potassium chloride to the suspension to stir the reaction, adjusting the pH of the solution to neutral by sodium hydroxide solution, centrifuging to obtain a precipitate, and calcining the precipitate and gradually decreasing the temperature to obtain a fluorine crystal mica crude product and (4) using a supercritical carbon dioxide to strip the crude fluorine crystal mica.
   USE - Preparation of fluorine crystal mica for preparation of two-dimensional crystal (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The mica has low impurity content, excellent stability and high flatness.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for application of mica, which involves (1) performing plasma etching on the fluorine crystal mica to obtain a patterned fluorine crystal mica matrix, (2) depositing a topological insulator on the fluorocrystalline mica substrate by a pulsed laser deposition method to prepare a two-dimensional semiconductor film material and/or a topological insulator film material, the plasma etching power is 80-100 W, the etching time is 10-15 minutes, the pulsed laser deposition temperature is 500-600 degrees C and the deposition time is 10-20 minutes. The topological insulator comprises bismuth selenide, indium(III) selenide, bismuth(III) selenide, and molybdenum disulfide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of magnesite, montmorillonite, quartz sand, sodium fluoride and potassium chloride is (6-12):(1-2):(3 -6):(4-8):(2-4). The concentration of nitric acid solution is 1-2 mol/l and the concentration of sodium hydroxide solution is 0.5-1 mol/l. Preferred Property: The particle diameter of grit mixture 0.01-1 mm. The thickness of fluorine crystal mica is 10-60 mu m. Preferred Condition: The calcination temperature is 500-800 degrees C and the calcination time is 5-10 hours. Preferred Process: The method for gradually decreasing the temperature involves holding the muffle furnace for 12-24 hours, and then gradually cooling to room temperature at a rate of 1 degrees C/min. The preparation of fluorine crystal mica specifically involves placing the crude fluorine crystal mica in a closed autoclave, introducing supercritical carbon dioxide into the autoclave to strip the crude fluorine crystal mica to promote smooth and smooth surface, preferably, the set temperature of the autoclave is 50-100 degrees C, the set pressure of the autoclave is 10-20 MPa, and the peeling time is 3-5 hours.
DC L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).); U11 (Semiconductor Materials and Processes)

TI Method for adjusting drilling characteristic of            downhole in e.g. carbon dioxide sequestration            applications, involves controlling adjustable element            of drilling device with control system in response to            one of obtained characteristics.
AU FANG L
   GRYMALYUK S
   SCHIMANSKI M
   GRYMALIYUK S
AE BAKER HUGHES A GE CO LLC (BAKO-C)
   FANG L (FANG-Individual)
   GRYMALYUK S (GRYM-Individual)
   SCHIMANSKI M (SCHI-Individual)
   BAKER HUGHES A GE CO LLC (BAKO-C)
   BAKER HUGHES INC (BAKO-C)
GA 201923936J
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining first drilling                characteristic associated with a first drilling                operation device (300) that is part of a drilling                system on a drill string. Second drilling                characteristic associated with a second drilling                operation device located apart from the first                drilling operation device along the drill string is                obtained at the control system. An adjustable                element of the first drilling operation device is                controlled with the control system in response to                one of the obtained first drilling characteristic                and the second drilling characteristic, where                adjustment of the adjustable element causes a                change in one of the first and second drilling                characteristics.
   USE - Method for automatically adjusting drilling                characteristic of downhole in carbon dioxide                sequestration, geothermal production and                hydrocarbon exploration and production                applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables performing real-time                decision making process downhole autonomously                through a closed loop communication to self-adjust                aggressiveness of first or second disintegrating                device to achieve optimal distribution of weight                and torque regardless of formation characteristics                of a formation being drilled. The method enables                minimizing risk that a pipe or drill string stuck                due to hole collapse. The method enables preventing                cuttings made by a pilot bit, a under reamer and/or                a reamer bit from entering space or annulus between                an inner structure and an outer structure.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                system for automatically adjusting drilling                characteristic in downhole operation.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a                drilling operation device.
   Drilling operation device (300)
   Tool body (302)
   Disintegrating blades (304)
   Cutting element (306)
   Dedicated drive mechanism (308)

PN CN109260322-A
TI Plant essential oil useful for e.g. promoting blood circulation, relieving swelling and pain, comprises safflower, Salvia miltiorrhiza, sappan wood, agarwood, myrrh, pepper, Eucommia bark, garden balsam stem and pseudo-ginseng.
AU GUO X
AE GUO X (GUOX-Individual)
GA 201912068D
AB    NOVELTY - Plant essential oil comprises 30-50 pts. wt. safflower, 10-25 pts. wt. Salvia miltiorrhiza, 15-20 pts. wt. sappan wood, 8-12 pts. wt. agarwood, 10-20 pts. wt. myrrh, 5-14 pts. wt. pepper, 8-16 pts. wt. Eucommia bark, 2-8 pts. wt. garden balsam stem and 6-15 pts. wt. pseudo-ginseng.
   USE - The essential oil is useful for promoting blood circulation, relieving swelling and pain of neck, shoulder, elbow, waist, knee pain and various rheumatoid arthritis, and degenerative diseases of the joints e.g. joint pain, joint swelling, joint wear, facet joint, hyperosteogeny, acute and chronic sprain, contusions, injuries from falls and gout and alleviating the pain of various factors.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing plant essential oil, comprising screening the raw materials, washing, pulverizing with a pulverizer, soaking the pulverized raw material component in a soaking tank, mixing and stirring by adding 50-75% ethanol solution, heating to 60-65 degrees C for 20-60 minutes, cooling, soaking for 24 hours, repeated heating and cooling for five times, filtering, heating the filtrate, refluxing for extraction, recovering ethanol, the filtrate after recovery of ethanol used, supercritically extracting the filtrate after recovering ethanol with carbon dioxide, the pressure is 25-35 MPa, the temperature is 450-550 degrees C, the time is 2-4 hours, the flow rate of carbon dioxide is 10-18 kg/hour, and collecting the volatile oil.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Composition: The plant essential oil comprises 40 pts. wt. safflower, 18 pts. wt. Salvia miltiorrhiza, 20 pts. wt. sappan wood, 10 pts. wt. agarwood, 10 pts. wt. myrrh, 10 pts. wt. pepper, 12 pts. wt. Eucommia bark, 6 pts. wt. garden balsam stem and 8 pts. wt. pseudo-ginseng. Preferred Method: The use of plant essential oil comprises spraying plant essential oil on the affected part, and perfoming the massage for five minutes.
   ACTIVITY - Analgesic; Antiarthritic; Antirheumatic; Osteopathic.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.)

PN CN109181084-A
TI Preparing modified polymer material involves pulverizing wormwood powder by pulverizer, and adding wormwood powder into supercritical carbon dioxide extraction tank for extraction and subjecting extract to secondary extraction.
AU MA Z
AE SUZHOU DEHE YAXIN TEXTILE TECHNOLOGY CO (SUZH-Non-standard)
GA 201909323J
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing modified polymer material involves pulverizing wormwood powder by pulverizer, and adding wormwood powder into a supercritical carbon dioxide extraction tank for extraction for 2-4 hours to obtain an extract, and subjecting extract to secondary extraction and separation with diethyl ether. The wormwood liquid layer is dried by a blast dryer to obtain wormwood extract for use. The 20-40 pts. wt. natural rubber and 30-50 pts. wt. EVA5110 are put into an open mill for opening and smelting, where the roll length of the open mill is 1-3 millimeter, and the thin pass is 8-12 times to obtain refining material. The 30-50 pts. wt. open refinery, 5-8 pts. wt. percarbonamide, 6-8 pts. wt. zinc oxide and 3-5 pts. wt. light calcium carbonate are taken together into an internal mixer at 75-85 degrees C for 7-12 minutes to obtain mixture.
   USE - Method for preparing modified polymer material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The 15-25 pts. wt. wormwood extract, 30-50 pts. wt. hardened material, 6-9 pts. wt. zinc stearate, 7-13 pts. wt. L-butyl lactate, 8-12 pts. wt. tert-butyl malonate ester and 3-5 pts. wt. azodicarbonamide are added to the mixer for mixing, and the temperature of the front section, the middle section and the back section of the mixer is set to 90-100 degrees C, 105-110 degrees C, 120-140 degrees C, and crushed them at 60-80 revoluiton per minute for 40-60 minutes, after cooling to obtain modified polymer new material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The wormwood powder is added into the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction tank for extraction for 3 hours. The 30 pts. wt. natural rubber and 45 pts. wt. EVA-5110 are put into an open mill for opening, and the roll of the open mill is 2 millimeter, and the pass is 9 times. The 40 pts. wt. the open material, 7 pts. wt. percarbonamide, 7 pts. wt. zinc oxide and 4 pts. wt. light calcium carbonate are added together to the internal mixer at 82 degrees C for 10 minutes. The 20 pts. wt. wormwood extract, 45 pts. wt. hardened material, 7 pts. wt. zinc stearate, 10 pts. wt. butyl lactide, 11 pts. wt. t-butyl ethyl malonate, and 4 pts. wt. azodicarbonamide are added and mixed. Preferred Conditions: The temperature of the front section, the middle section and the back section of the mixer are 95 degrees C, 108 degrees C, and 130 degrees C, respectively, and blended at 75 revolution per minute for 50 minutes.

   LONG Z
   ZHOU H
   ZHOU C
   XU Z
   HUANG H
AE JINKO ENERGY CO LTD (ZJJS-C)
   ZHEJIANG JINKO SOLAR CO LTD (ZJJS-C)
GA 201898883A
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a crucible cover, comprising a plate body, a thermal conductive plate, a first mounting plate and a second mounting plate, the plate body upper end equipped with a limiting hole, the limiting hole comprises an exhaust hole and an air inlet hole, an air inlet hole opposite to the quartz crucible, hole diameter of the exhaust hole is less than the hole diameter of the air inlet hole, and the aperture of the air inlet hole is orderly decreased toward the inlet hole from the contact end of the baffle body outer wall and inner wall of the vent hole is set with a limiting post, the flow limit column back inner wall convex exhaust hole, two sides of the heat conducting plate with the main body of the plate body is attached, This utility model through the design of the exhaust hole and air inlet hole, which effectively reduces the contact area of the crucible cover is reduced with the quartz crucible by crucible cover in contact with the quartz crucible produced by carbon dioxide gas, at the same time, the through hole diameter of the exhaust hole is less than the hole diameter of the air inlet hole, reduces the external carbon dioxide gas toward the flow of the quartz crucible so as to reach the effect of reducing carbon content in silicon ingot, finally reducing the carbon content in the silicon wafer.

PN WO2018210617-A1; EP3624921-A1; US2020391153-A1; EP3624921-B1; US11285425-B2
TI Separation unit for separating carbon dioxide from            mixture of different gas e.g. air, has two stacked            layers comprising two sheets of flexible fabric            material that are gas permeable and impermeable to            loose particulate sorbent material.
AU SAUERBECK S
   GEBALD C
   WURZBACHER J A
AE CLIMEWORKS AG (CLIM-Non-standard)
   CLIMEWORKS AG (CLIM-Non-standard)
   CLIMEWORKS AG (CLIM-Non-standard)
GA 201891350Y
AB    NOVELTY - The separation unit has two stacked layers                provided in-which each layer comprises two sheets                (15) of a flexible fabric material that are gas                permeable and impermeable to a loose particulate                sorbent material. The sheets are arranged                essentially parallel for defining an inlet face of                the layer and an outlet face of the layer, where                the sheets are arranged with distance in a range of                0.3-1 1 centimeter or 0.3-5 centimeter. The sheets                are mounted on a rectangular circumferential frame                structure, where the mean distance between the                inlet face and/or the outlet faces defining                channels is measured in direction essentially                perpendicular to main gas inflow direction and main                gas outflow direction, respectively.
   USE - Separation unit for separating gas i.e. carbon                dioxide, from mixture of different gas such as air,                flue gas, biogas (all claimed), exhaust gas, and                industrial waste gas, by using cyclic                adsorption/desorption process.
   ADVANTAGE - The separation unit prevents sagging of                flexible fabric layers and transports the sorbent                material under influence or airflow in an efficient                manner. The separation unit improves stability of                the frame structure and reduces hole formation,                thus increasing flexibility of arms.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                method for producing a layer for a gas separation                unit.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of an edge                portion of a frame structure at a heat exchange                medium inlet edge.
   Primary heat exchange pipe (1)
   Secondary heat exchange piping (5)
   Heat exchange inlet (7)
   Sheets (15)
   Outer ridge (18)

PT P
PN WO2018146006-A1; AR110798-A1; AU2018219405-A1; CA3050057-A1; KR2019112104-A; IN201917033933-A; CN110267712-A; EP3579928-A1; US2019380927-A1; JP2020506953-W; BR112019016318-A2; EP3579928-B1; MX2019009448-A1; RU2019128051-A; RU2750777-C2; MX387112-B; ES2878848-T3; CN110267712-B; IN408803-B
TI Cosmetic composition having UV-A and/or UV-B            protection, useful as sunscreen product, eye make-up            product, facial makeup product, hair care product and            lip care product, comprises inorganic UV filter, and            surface-reacted calcium carbonate.
AU BUDDE T
   HECKER A
AE OMYA INT AG (OMYA-C)
   OMYA INT AG (OMYA-C)
   OMYA INT AG (OMYA-C)
GA 201863776D
AB    NOVELTY - Cosmetic composition having UV-A and/or UV-B                protection, comprises: at least one inorganic UV                filter; and surface-reacted calcium carbonate                having a volume median particle size (d50) of                0.1-90 mu m, where the surface-reacted calcium                carbonate is a reaction product of natural ground                calcium carbonate or precipitated calcium carbonate                with carbon dioxide and at least one hydronium ion                donor and the carbon dioxide is formed in situ by                the hydronium ion donor treatment and/or is                supplied from an external source.
   USE - The cosmetic composition is useful as a                sunscreen product, an eye make-up product, a facial                makeup product, a lip care product, a hair care                product, a hair styling product, a nail care                product, a hand care product and/or a skin care                product (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The cosmetic composition: utilizes the                surface-reacted calcium carbonate that boosts the                sun protection factor (SPF) and improves the                sensory properties of the composition (claimed);                exhibits improved sunscreen efficiency and improved                appearance and provides an improved UV filter                performance, thus reducing the quantity of UV                filter agent without affecting the envisaged SPF;                exhibits reduced whitening effect when applied to                the skin; is easily applied to the skin, forms an                even and uniform film on the skin, is less greasy                and sticky, and exhibits a good spreadability and                dries fast; and is derivable from natural sources,                thus easily biodegradable.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                producing the composition, mixing the inorganic UV                filter with the surface-reacted calcium                carbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The inorganic UV                  filter comprises titanium dioxide, zinc oxide,                  hydroxyapatite (all preferred), iron oxide,                  cerium oxide, calcium-doped cerium oxide and/or                  cerium phosphate. The inorganic UV filter is in                  form of particles having a weight median particle                  size (d50) of 10-1000 (preferably 20-400) nm. The                  inorganic UV filter comprises 1-50 (preferably                  10-25) wt.%, based on the total weight of the                  cosmetic composition. The surface-reacted calcium                  carbonate has a volume median particle size (d50)                  of 0.1-75 (preferably 1.5-15) mu m. The                  surface-reacted calcium carbonate has a specific                  surface area of 15-200 (preferably 30-140) m2/g,                  measured using nitrogen and the                  Brunauer-Emmett-Teller method. The natural ground                  calcium carbonate comprises marble, chalk and/or                  limestone and the precipitated calcium carbonate                  comprises precipitated calcium carbonates having                  an aragonitic, vateritic and/or calcitic crystal                  form. The hydronium ion donor comprises                  phosphoric acid, hydrochloric acid, sulfuric                  acid, sulfurous acid, citric acid, oxalic acid,                  acidic salt, acetic acid and/or formic acid, and                  preferably hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid,                  sulfurous acid, phosphoric acid, oxalic acid,                  dihydrogen phosphate, being at least partially                  neutralized by a cation comprising lithium                  cation, sodium cation and/or potassium cation,                  hydrogen phosphate, being at least partially                  neutralized by a cation selected from comprising                  lithium cation, sodium cation and/or potassium                  cation, magnesium cation and/or calcium cation.                  The surface-reacted calcium carbonate comprises                  0.1-50 (preferably 3-6) wt.%, based on the total                  weight of the cosmetic composition. The                  surface-reacted calcium carbonate is associated                  with at least one active agent of                  pharmaceutically active agents, biologically                  active agents, disinfecting agents,                  preservatives, flavoring agents, surfactants,                  oils, fragrances and/or essential oils.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Composition: The composition                  further comprises: at least one organic UV filter                  comprising derivatives of cinnamic acid and its                  salts, derivatives of salicylic acid and its                  salts, benzophenones, derivatives of aminobenzoic                  acid and its salts, dibenzoylmethanes,                  benzylidene camphor derivatives, benzimidazole                  derivatives, diphenylacrylate derivatives,                  acrylamide derivatives, benzotriazole                  derivatives, triazine derivatives, benzalmalonate                  derivatives, aminobenzoate derivatives and/or                  octocrylene, preferably derivatives of cinnamic                  acid and its salts, benzophenones and/or                  octocrylene; and at least one additive of                  bleaching agents, thickeners, stabilizers,                  chelating agents, preserving agents, wetting                  agents, emulsifiers, emollients, fragrances,                  colorants, skin tanning compounds, antioxidants,                  pigments, oils and/or water.
   EXAMPLE - Typical cosmetic composition comprised of: 3                wt.% isohexadecane and disteardimonium hectorite                and propylene carbonate; 11 wt.% isononyl                isononanoate; 7 wt.% isohexadecane; 17 wt.%                titanium dioxide (nano) and alumina and dimethicone                and aqua; 3 wt.% surface-reacted calcium carbonate;                7 wt.% polyglyceryl-4 isostearate and cetyl                polyethylene glycol/polypropylene glycol-10/1                dimethicone hexyl laurate; 1.5 wt.% cetyl                dimethicone; 7 wt.% zinc oxide and                triethoxycaprylylsilane; 0.58 wt.% CI 77492 and                isostearyl sebacate and disodium stearoyl glutamate                and aluminum hydroxide; 0.09 wt.% CI 77491 and                isostearyl sebacate and disodium stearoyl glutamate                and aluminum hydroxide; 0.03 wt.% CI 77499 and                isostearyl sebacate and disodium stearoyl glutamate                and aluminum hydroxide; 36.55 wt.5 aqua                (demineralized water); 2 wt.% glycerin; 3 wt.%                propanediol; 0.25 wt.% chlorphenesin and aqua; 0.15                wt.% phenoxy ethanol; 0.8 wt.% sodium chloride; and                0.05 wt.% magnesium sulfate.
DC D21 (Preparations for dental or toilet purposes - including filling alloys, compositions for dentures or dental impressions, anti-caries chewing gum, plaque disclosing compositions, toothpastes, cosmetics, shampoos, topical anti-sunburn compositions and toilet soaps (A61K).); E19 (Other organic compounds general - organic compounds of unknown or indefinite structure; general mixtures of many types; organic reactions (e.g. nitration, resolution) when applied generally.); E37 (Mixtures of many components; inorganic reactions and processes of general applicability.)
MC D08-B; E06-D05; E06-D08; E07-D13B; E10-A15F; E10-C02A; E10-C02D1; E10-C04C; E10-C04J1U; E10-C04J2U; E10-D03D; E10-E02F1; E10-F02A1; E10-F02A2; E31-B03D; E31-F05; E31-K05A; E31-K05C; E31-K05D; E31-N05C; E34-D03A; E34-D03B; E34-E02A; E35-C02; E35-K02; E35-U02

PN CN108267381-A; CN208012993-U
TI Gas-liquid-solid multi-phase flow erosion-corrosion experimental installation, has test pipe whose one end is connected with dry gas conveying pipe and other end is connected with soft pipe.
AU SONG X
   LUO P
   ZHANG T
   ZHONG X
   WANG X
   LIU L
AE UNIV SOUTHWEST PETROLEUM (USWP-C)
GA 201857475K
AB    NOVELTY - The installation has a frequency converter (1) that is electrically connected with the fan (2). The humidity and temperature monitor (15) are arranged on gas conveying pipe (22). The gas conveying pipe is provided with a through hole. The sand adding tank (11) is connected with the sand feeding valve (17). The liquid storage tank (5) is provided with carbon dioxide filling pipe (3). The input port of conveying pump (8) is connected with liquid storage tank. The fog nozzle (16) is connected with the moisture conveying pipe (23). The right end of moisture conveying pipe is set in the right end of through hole. The one end of test pipe (14) is connected with dry gas conveying pipe (22) and other end is connected with hose (12). The hose is arranged in waste processing tank (13).
   USE - Gas-liquid-solid multi-phase flow erosion-corrosion experimental installation.
   ADVANTAGE - The erosion and corrosion comprehensive experiment of different material test in specific flow field under condition of gas-liquid-solid multiphase flow is finished to analyze corrosion wear rate and surface morphology of the material test piece under different conditions.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of gas-liquid-solid multi-phase flow erosion-corrosion experimental installation.
   Frequency converter (1)
   Fan (2)
   Carbon dioxide filling pipe (3)
   Sand adding tank (11)
   Hose (12)
   Waste processing tank (13)
   Test pipe (14)
   Humidity and temperature monitor (15)
   Fog nozzle (16)
   Sand feeding valve (17)
   Gas conveying pipe (22)
   Moisture conveying pipe (23)

PN CN108192022-A
TI Viscosity modifier useful for adjusting viscosity of cathode slurry, comprises aqueous solution of amide-based polymer containing tertiary amine group.
AU KANG L
   TIAN L
   WU K
   RAO M
   LI J
AE SHENZHEN OPTIMUMNANO ENERGY CO LTD (OPEG-C)
GA 2018519622
AB    NOVELTY - Viscosity modifier comprises aqueous solution of amide-based polymer containing tertiary amine group, where the amount of aqueous solution of amide-based polymer containing tertiary amine group is 2.5-4 wt.%; when the carbon dioxide gas is introduced into cathode slurry containing the viscosity modifier, then viscosity of cathode slurry is increase; and when the carbon dioxide gas is discharged from cathode slurry containing the viscosity modifier, then viscosity of cathode slurry is reduced.
   USE - The viscosity modifier is useful for adjusting viscosity of cathode slurry (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The viscosity modifier: has simple preparation process; and is environmentally friendly; and does not cause secondary pollution to the environment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing viscosity modifier, comprising (i) dissolving acrylamide and N-3-dimethylaminopropyl methacrylamide as a monomer in a molar ratio of 3.5-4.5:1 in deionized water to obtain mixed solution, where the mass of monomer in the mixed solution is 20-35 wt.%, (ii) adding initiator into mixed solution, and performing radical polymerization reaction at 35-45 degrees C for 4-6 hours, (iii) repeatedly washing product after the polymerization reaction and immersing with absolute ethanol to obtain solid of amide-based polymer containing tertiary amine group; placing solid of amide-based polymer containing tertiary amine group in dry box and drying at 55-70 degrees C for 26-30 hours to obtain amide polymer powder containing a tertiary amine group, and (iv) dissolving the tertiary amine group-containing amide polymer powder in deionized water to obtain viscosity modifier having a mass fraction of 2.5-4 wt.%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The tertiary amine group-containing amide-based polymer is N-(3-(dimethylamino)propyl)isobutyramide compound of formula (I). The molar ratio between acrylamide and N-3-dimethylaminopropyl methacrylamide is 4:1. The amount of monomer is 25 wt.%. The initiator is sodium bisulfite and ammonium persulfate in a ratio of 3:2. The ratio between total mass of sodium bisulfite and ammonium persulfate: total mass of acrylamide and N-3-dimethylaminopropyl methacrylamide is 1.5-2.5:1000 (preferably 2:1000).n and m = not defined.

AU PAVEL P M
AE MUKHORTOV P P (MUKH-Individual)
GA 2018471395
AB    NOVELTY - The life saving wristband has a buoyant elastic buoyancy chamber (floating pocket) tightly curved and placed in a bag along the periphery of the wristband. A gas generator releases a required amount of carbon dioxide gas to fill the buoyant elastic buoyancy chamber. The activation of the inflation system operates on the basis of manual compression, auxiliary strap or lever arm, excessive pressure at a set water depth, use of a timer device or signal data of the sensor.

The buoyancy chamber in which the gas is filled protects the swimmer from drowing at the state of crisis and the rippling of water accident is prevented.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a gas generator.

PN CN107986296-A; CN107986296-B
TI High silica-alumina A-type molecular sieve for e.g. selective adsorption of water, carbon dioxide and ammonia, has preset silica-alumina ratio and specific surface area, and is synthesized in preset time by seed induction method.
AU WANG S
   WEI X
   SUN T
   CHEN H
   ZHAO S
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 2018366582
AB    NOVELTY - A high silica-alumina A-type molecular sieve has silica-alumina ratio of 1.5-8, specific surface area of 400-1000 m2/g and grain size of 0.2-20 mu m, and is synthesized in 3-240 hours by seed induction method.
   USE - High silica-alumina A-type molecular sieve is used for selective adsorption of water, carbon dioxide and ammonia, ammonia-selective catalytic reduction denitrification process after cation exchange of divalent copper ion and divalent iron ion, and air separation after cation exchange of lithium ion and silver ion.
   ADVANTAGE - The high silica-alumina A-type molecular sieve has strong water resistance, excellent hydrothermal stability and high specific surface area, and is prepared in short period of time with high yield and reduced consumption of organic template.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of high silica-alumina A-type molecular sieve, which involves preparing seed crystals by pickling, sequentially adding organic template agent, aluminum source, silicon source, inorganic structure directing agent, water and seed crystals to the reactor, stirring at room temperature for 1-10 hours until the mixture is homogeneous, crystallizing at 60-125 degrees C for 24-72 hours, centrifuging, washing and drying. The mass ratio of silica as silicon source, organic template, aluminum hydroxide as aluminum source, inorganic structure directing agent and water is 8-20:6.2-13:0.5-3:0.5-3:120-240. The content of seed crystals is 0.5-30% with respect to content of silica.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The silicon source is silica sol and/or silicon oxide powder. The aluminum source is aluminum sec-butoxide, aluminum isobutoxide, aluminum sol, pseudo-aluminum and/or aluminum hydroxide. The inorganic structure directing agent is alkali metal or alkaline-earth metal salt, preferably salt of sodium, potassium, magnesium, calcium and barium.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The organic templating agent is tetramethylammonium hydroxide, tetraethylammonium hydroxide and/or tetrapropylammonium hydroxide.

PN JP2018053047-A; JP6540645-B2
TI Thermal decomposition of organic substance for manufacture of gaseous substance involves contacting organic substance and gas containing hydrogen and carbon dioxide in reactor, polymerizing portion of products and re-introducing to reactor.
AU MURAI R
   ISHII J
   HYAKUNO K
   WASHIMI I
AE JFE STEEL CORP (JFES-C)
GA 201826143Y
AB    NOVELTY - An organic substance is thermally decomposed by contacting organic substance with mixed gas (g) which contains hydrogen and carbon dioxide in reactor (A), carrying out polymerization of greater than or equal to 1 portion of thermal decomposition products which are liquid at normal temperature out of the thermal decomposition products of organic substance taken out from the reactor, and introducing again into the reactor to undergo thermal decomposition.
   USE - Thermal decomposition of organic substance for manufacture of gaseous substance (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method eliminates the need for special measuring device, thermally decomposes organic substance at low reaction temperature, and greatly improves yield of gas product. The thermal decomposition installation has low installation cost and simple installation process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) manufacture of gaseous substance which involves collecting thermal decomposition products which are generated in thermal decomposition method as gas at normal temperature and recovering as useful gaseous substance; and
   (2) thermal decomposition installation of organic substance which comprises reactor; gas containing thermal decomposition product of organic substance discharged from reactor; separation apparatus (B) which liquefies a portion of thermal decomposition product of organic substance contained in gas and separating from gas; polymerization processing apparatus (C) which carries out polymerization of liquid thermal decomposition product separated by separation apparatus; and supply unit (D) to supply liquid thermal decomposition product subjected to polymerization process to reactor.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of the thermal decomposition installation. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
   Reactor (A)
   Separation apparatus (B)
   Polymerization processing apparatus (C)
   Supply unit (D)
   Gas (g)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: Mixed gas further contains water vapor. Mixed gas comprises 20-70 vol.% water vapor, 10-40 vol.% hydrogen, and 10-40 vol.% carbon dioxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Processes: Gas containing thermal decomposition product of organic substance discharged from the reactor is cooled to normal temperature or normal temperature vicinity, a portion of the thermal decomposition product of organic substance contained in gas is liquefied, and polymerization of liquid thermal decomposition product is carried out. Preferred Devices: Reactor is fluidized bed reactor. Preferred Components: Organic substance is chosen from waste plastic, oil-impregnated sludge, waste oil, and/or biomass.

PT P
PN RU2642201-C1
TI Inflatable rescue raft.
AU CHERNYAVETS V V
AE CHERNYAVETS V V (CHER-Individual)
GA 2018089668
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to life-saving appliances, namely to life rafts used by aircraft vehicles for delivery to a site of disaster or water accident. Inflatable life raft is provided with inflatable sides, which are one common chamber, divided by diaphragms into four compartments, connected by bypass tubes, ensuring the gas filling of all compartments from one cylinder of carbon dioxide or from a hand-held fur. Handrails are made in form of handles, pasted on sides, on left side mounted hand-made fur, allowing air to inflate shell. On the outside, a water ballast bag is placed on the bottom to give the inflatable raft stability. To remove gas from the inflatable raft sheath on it there is a threaded bushing with a stopper, in the bow in the pocket is placed a cylinder with carbon dioxide, which is connected to the inflatable raft, the hood with the hood is glued to the sides from the inside and is divided into two parts, fastened together by a textile fastener, both parts of the canopy can be filled with air through the tubes, one available on the left and right of its troughs. Inflatable raft also contains a soft floating anchor, a sponge for removing water from raft, a bag of uranium, a cord for anchoring anchor and a carrying case with various things. Raft in non-working (static) state is placed inside paddle, articulated with pilot's combinzon. Pouf is provided with a zipper closure with a stopper connected to an electromechanical switch articulated with a mechanism for starting a source of compressed gas.
   USE - Rescue means.
   ADVANTAGE - Simplifies the design of the raft while maintaining its basic functional membership.

PN CN107611393-A; CN107611393-B
TI Preparation of thiophene/three-dimensional porous            carbon composite used as positive electrode material            for lithium-sulfur battery, involves grinding            grapefruit peel-derived three-dimensional porous carbon            material with sulfur and reacting.
AU GUO J
   WANG Z
   ZHENG P
   AN Y
   WANG J
   ZHANG Y
AE GUO J (GUOJ-Individual)
   UNIV SHANGLUO (UNSG-C)
GA 201808403D
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of thiophene/three-dimensional                porous carbon composite, involves (i) adding                grapefruit peel to 100 ml reactor, introducing                carbon dioxide gas into reactor so that pressure                reaches 80 atmosphere, (ii) heating the reactor at                50 degrees C and reacting for 10 hours, than                placing the reactor to atmospheric pressure and                room temperature and obtaining grapefruit                peel-derived three-dimensional porous carbon                material which is rich in oxygen-functional groups                on the surface, grinding three-dimensional porous                carbon material with sulfur, mixing and reacting in                an inert atmosphere.
   USE - Preparation of thiophene/three-dimensional                porous carbon composite for use as positive                electrode material for lithium-sulfur                battery.
   ADVANTAGE - The obtained thiophene/three-dimensional                porous carbon composite material has high specific                capacity and cyclic stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of thiophene/three-dimensional                porous carbon composite, involves (i) weighing                0.1-10.0 g grapefruit peel, adding to 100 ml                reactor, introducing carbon dioxide gas into                reactor so that the pressure reaches 80 atm, (ii)                heating the reactor at 50 degrees C and reacting                for 10 hours, after the reaction is completed,                opening the reactor to atmospheric pressure and                room temperature and obtaining grapefruit                peel-derived three-dimensional porous carbon                material which is rich in oxygen-functional groups                on the surface, taking 1 pt. wt. three-dimensional                porous carbon material and 2 pts. wt. sulfur,                grinding and mixing for half an hour, placing the                mixture in a closed glass reactor, and reacting at                155 degrees C in an inert atmosphere for 2                hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The grapefruit peel is                  chosen from pomelo, Pingshan pomelo, Shatian                  pomelo, Citrus maxima, shaddock, and Xiangxiang                  pomelo.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The inert atmosphere                  is argon or nitrogen atmosphere.

PT P
PN WO2018013349-A1
TI Conversion of methane to hydrocarbons containing two carbons involves contacting methane with oxygen in the presence of catalyst to produce reaction product, separating, and contacting stream with oxygen and additional carbon dioxide.
AU WEST D
   MAMEDOV A K
   SARSANI S
   LIANG W
AE SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
   WEST D (WEST-Individual)
GA 201805616G
AB    NOVELTY - Methane is converted to C2 hydrocarbons by contacting methane with oxygen in the presence of 1st catalyst in 1st reactor to produce 1st reaction product containing C2 hydrocarbons and carbon dioxide (CO2), separating 1st reaction product to produce 1st stream containing C2 hydrocarbons and 2nd stream containing methane, carbon monoxide (CO), and CO2, and contacting 2nd stream with oxygen and additional CO2 in the presence of 2nd catalyst in 2nd reactor to produce 2nd reaction product containing hydrogen (H2) and CO.
   USE - Conversion of methane to C2 hydrocarbons.
   ADVANTAGE - The method exhibits carbon efficiency, where CO2 is converted into high value product, which is syngas. Syngas can be converted to methanol, olefins, and oxo-synthesis products, which can increase efficiency of the total process significantly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for integrated system comprising 1st reactor for contacting methane with oxygen in the presence of 1st catalyst disposed within 1st reactor to produce 1st reaction product containing C2 hydrocarbons and CO2, separations column for separating 1st reaction product to produce 1st stream containing C2 hydrocarbons and 2nd stream containing methane, CO, and CO2, and 2nd reactor suitable for contacting 2nd stream with oxygen and additional CO2 in the presence of 2nd catalyst disposed within 2nd reactor to produce 2nd reaction product containing H2 and CO.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of methane conversion to C2 hydrocarbons integrated with the production of oxo-synthesis gas composition.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The 1st catalyst comprises magnesium oxide (MgO), calcium oxide (CaO), MgO-CaO, lanthanum(III) oxide (La2O3), and cerium(III) oxide (Ce2O3), samarium(III) oxide (Sm2O3), and/or Sm2O3-La2O3-Ce2O3. The 2nd catalyst comprises nickel-containing catalyst. Nickel-containing catalyst is supported on alumina (Al2O3) or La2O3 support. The 2nd catalyst comprises supported NiO.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Parameters: Conversion of methane in the 1st reactor is greater than or equal to 30% with C2 selectivity of greater than or equal to 55%. Preferred Conditions: Contacting methane with oxygen occurs at 700-850 degrees C, preferably 720-820 degrees C. Contacting 2nd stream with oxygen occurs at 650-750 degrees C, preferably 660-725 degrees C. Contacting methane with oxygen occurs in the presence of nitrogen (N2). Preferred Processes: The 2nd reaction product is reacted to produce methanol. The methanol is further reacted to produce 5-11C hydrocarbons. Preferred Component: The C2 hydrocarbons comprise ethylene and ethane.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst comprises copper nickel metal supported catalyst. The active component of the copper nickel metal supported catalyst comprises carrier and loaded on carrier. The active component comprises copper and nickel. The carrier comprises zinc oxide, magnesium oxide, lanthanum oxide, ceric oxide and Zirconium dioxide. The reactor comprises one of a batch reactor and a continuous reactor. The nitrogen is used as carrier gas. The chemical comprises 2-5C diol. Preferred Composition: The total loading of copper and nickel is 10-90%. The molar percentage of the carrier is 10-90%. The solid-liquid ratio of the xylose to water is 1-50 g/100 ml. The ratio of xylose and catalyst is 1-5. The pressure of the hydrogen is between 1.0-8.0 megapascal. The partial pressure of the nitrogen gas is between 0-7.0 megapascal. The partial pressure of the hydrogen is not less than 1.0 megapascal. The reaction temperature is 423-573 kelvin. The reaction time is 30-360 minutes.
