PT P
PN CN114163303-A
TI Preparing 3-methyl-3-buten-1-ol, involves obtaining reaction solution by continuously reacting isobutene and formaldehyde, transferring reaction solution to rectifying tower, carrying out gas-liquid separation of obtained light.
AU AN L
   ZHANG Y
   SHEN Y
   ZHU X
   ZHANG H
   LIU Y
   PANG J
AE WANHUA CHEM GROUP CO LTD (WHCH-C)
GA 202241755T
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing 3-methyl-3- buten-1-ol involves obtaining the reaction solution by continuously reacting isobutene and formaldehyde under high temperature and high pressure conditions, where the total content of carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide and hydrogen in the raw isobutene is 0.05-0.5 wt.%, preferably 0.05-0.35 wt.%; transferring the reaction solution to a rectifying tower, and the tower top light components comprises unreacted isobutene, carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide, hydrogen are separated and obtained, and the column bottom comprising the 3-methyl-3-butene-1-ol product is obtained, the heavy components are separated and refined to obtain 3-methyl-3-buten-1-ol products from the heavy components at the bottom of the tower; carrying out gas-liquid separation of the obtained light fraction at the top of the tower, and continuously extracting the non-condensable gas containing isobutene.
   USE - Method for preparing 3-methyl-3-buten-1-ol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to improve the selectivity and solves the problem of device blockage problem in the production of preparing 3-methyl-3-buten-1-ol, improves the device operation stability, cost-effective, reduces carbon emmision and improves the carbon atom economical efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing 3-methyl-3- buten-1-ol involves obtaining the reaction solution by continuously reacting isobutene and formaldehyde under high temperature and high pressure conditions, where the total content of carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide and hydrogen in the raw isobutene is 0.05-0.5 wt.%, preferably 0.05-0.35 wt.%; transferring the reaction solution to a rectifying tower, and the tower top light components comprises unreacted isobutene, carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide, hydrogen are separated and obtained, and the column bottom comprising the 3-methyl-3-butene-1-ol product is obtained, the heavy components are separated and refined to obtain 3-methyl-3-buten-1-ol products from the heavy components at the bottom of the tower; carrying out gas-liquid separation of the obtained light fraction at the top of the tower, and continuously extracting the non-condensable gas containing isobutene; the extracted non-condensable gas is subjected to cryogenic separation to obtain a liquid phase containing unreacted isobutene, which is mixed with the remaining liquid gas-liquid separation, and applied to the isobutene raw material; separating cryogenically the remaining mixed gas phase containing carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and hydrogen, and reacting under the action of a composite modified zirconia catalyst to obtain isobutene, which is applied to the isobutene raw material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The isobutene raw material comprises fresh isobutene, recycled isobutene, and waste gas recovered isobutene, based on the total mass of the raw isobutene being 100%, where 100 wt.% fresh isobutene, ratio of 5-20 wt.%, preferably 10-15 wt.%, 80-95 wt.% recycling of isobutene, preferably 85 to 90 wt.%, and 0-10 wt.% waste gas recovery of isobutene, preferably 0.5-5 wt.%, more preferably 1-3 wt.%; the fresh isobutene, controlling the total content of carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide and hydrogen less than or equal to 0.03%, preferably, the carbon dioxide content less than or equal to 0.01%, the carbon monoxide content less than or equal to 0.01%, and the hydrogen content less than or equal to 0.01%; the recycling isobutene in the liquid phase containing unreacted isobutene is obtained by cryogenic separation and the mixed solution of the remaining liquid in gas-liquid separation; preferably, carbon dioxide in the mixed solution content less than or equal to 0.04%, carbon monoxide content less than or equal to 0.03%, hydrogen content less than or equal to 0.03%; the waste gas recovery isobutene in cryogenic separation of the remaining mixed gas-phase reaction containing carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and hydrogen, preferably, where the carbon dioxide content less than or equal to1.2%, the carbon monoxide content less than or equal to 1 %, hydrogen content less than or equal to 0.8%. The formaldehyde raw material is selected from one or at least two of formaldehyde solution, paraformaldehyde, methylal, and hemiacetal A combination of species, preferably or a combination of two in formaldehyde solution, methylal, and hemiacetal; preferably, the molar ratio of isobutene to formaldehyde is 3-30:1, more preferably 8-25:1. The reaction temperature is 160-400degrees Celsius, preferably 180-320degrees Celsius, and the reaction pressure is 5-30 MPaG, preferably 10-25MPaG, the reaction residence time is 5-45min, preferably 15-30min; preferably, the reaction is carried out under a solvent condition, and the solvent is selected from water, methanol, ethanol, tert-butanol, butanol, n-butanol, octanol, pentanol, and prenol or A combination of two, more preferably or a combination of at least two of methanol, tert-butanol, n-butanol, and prenyl alcohol; preferably, the solvent dosage is 0.5-3 times the quality of formaldehyde; preferably, in the reaction process, the carbon dioxide content in the reaction system is less than or equal to 0.2%, the carbon monoxide content is less than or equal to 0.15%, and the hydrogen content is less than or equal to 0.15%. The theoretical plate number of the rectifying column is 10-35, preferably 25-30, and the pressure at the top of the column is controlled at 300-1000KPaG, preferably 400-700 KPaG, the temperature is 35-85degrees Celsius, preferably 45-70degrees Celsius, the temperature of the tower is 80-160degrees Celsius, preferably 100-150degrees Celsius. The mixed gas phase containing carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and hydrogen is composed of 10-20 vol.% carbon dioxide, preferably 12-16 vol.%, carbon monoxide 30-40 vol.%, preferably 32-36 vol.%, hydrogen 40-60 vol.%, preferably 45-55 vol.%. The composite modified zirconia catalyst comprises a main catalyst zirconia, a co-catalyst and a modifier. The cocatalyst is selected from a combination of nickel oxide, chromium oxide, copper oxide, zinc oxide, aluminum oxide, and palladium oxide, preferably any one of nickel oxide, zinc oxide, and aluminum oxide or a combination of at least two. The modifier is selected one or a combination of at least two of calcium phosphide, zinc phosphide, aluminum phosphide, copper phosphide and magnesium phosphide, preferably zinc phosphide, copper phosphide, phosphide or a combination of two of magnesium.
   EXAMPLE - The examples provide details of chemical or comparative chemical preparations that do not support the claims.

PN CN113801335-A
TI Metal-organic framework materials useful in catalytic cycloaddition reaction of carbon dioxide and epoxide.
AU WU P
   BAO G
   ZHENG R
   TIAN X
   WANG J
AE UNIV JIANGSU NORMAL (UJSN-C)
GA 2022036461
AB    NOVELTY - Metal-organic framework materials, is claimed. The metal-organic framework material with carbon dioxide catalytic conversion application uses metal ion Zn2+ as node, using flexible propeller-shaped 4,4',4''-tricarboxytriphenylamine as the organic linking main ligand, 3,5-bis(pyridin-4-yl)-4H-1,2,4-triazol-4-amine as functional auxiliary ligand to prepare double interpenetrating metal-organic framework material Zn-TDA by solvothermal method
   USE - The metal-organic framework material is useful in catalytic cycloaddition reaction of carbon dioxide and epoxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The material has excellent size selective catalytic performance to the fixed reaction of carbon dioxide and epoxide, simple preparation technique, high catalytic efficiency, good circulation stability, easy to prepare in large scale.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Metal-organic framework materials, is claimed. The metal-organic framework material with carbon dioxide catalytic conversion application uses metal ion Zn2+ as node, using flexible propeller-shaped 4,4',4''-tricarboxytriphenylamine as the organic linking main ligand, 3,5-bis(pyridin-4-yl)-4H-1,2,4-triazol-4-amine as functional auxiliary ligand to prepare double interpenetrating metal-organic framework material Zn-TDA by solvothermal method; and the metal-organic framework materials is of formula ((Mm(L1)n(L2)b).(DMAC) q(H2O)p.
   L1 = 4,4',4''-tricarboxytriphenylamine of formula (I), referred to as H3tca;
   L2 = 3,5-bis(pyridin-4-yl)-4H-1,2,4-triazol-4-amine of formula (II), referred to as dpta;
   M = metal ion Zn2+;
   DMAC = N,N-dimethylacetamide;
   m = 3;
   n = 2;
   b = 1;
   q = 2; and
   p = 1.
   Provided that: m, n, b, q and p are the number of metal ions, organic linking main ligand H3tca, functional auxiliary ligand dbpt, DMAC and water molecules, respectively.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing (i) adding 4,4',4''-tricarboxytriphenylamine, 3,5-bis(pyridin-4-yl)-4H-1,2,4-triazol-4-amine, and soluble salt of zinc in a molar ratio of 1:1.6-1.8:3.3-3.5 into a glass sample bottle, adding 3 ml mixed solvent with a volume ratio of N,N-dimethylacetamide and water of 2:1-1.2, and stirring evenly, (ii) placing the glass sample bottle in the hydrothermal synthesis reaction kettle, and then placing the reaction kettle in an electric heating blast drying oven, and reacting at a constant temperature of 85-95degrees Celsius for 70-74 hours, and (iii) cooling obtains light yellow block crystals after the reaction, and then carrying out centrifugation, washing with deionized water and drying in sequence to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The molar ratio between 4,4',4''-tricarboxytriphenylamine, 3,5-bis(pyridin-4-yl)-4H-1,2,4-triazol-4-amine and soluble salt of zinc is 1:1.7:3.4. The soluble salt of zinc is zinc nitrate hexahydrate.Preferred Method: In the step (iii), the centrifugal speed is 10000 revolutions per minute, the deionized water washing method is 3 times, 5 ml each time, the drying temperature is 95-105degrees Celsius, and the drying time is 6-8 hours.

PT P
PN CN113651343-A
TI Preparing high-purity lithium carbonate by recovering lithium hydroxide for air purification, by drying lithium hydroxide material, introducing carbon dioxide gas, converting lithium hydroxide into lithium carbonate, crushing, drying, adding calcium hydroxide, centrifuging, washing, and drying.
AU LI D
   LIU T
   YAN Y
   LIU J
AE 718TH RES INST CHINA SHIPBUILDING IND CO (CSHI-C)
GA 2021D5813N
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing high-purity lithium carbonate by recovering lithium hydroxide for air purification, involves (i) drying recovered air purification lithium hydroxide material, (ii) placing dried material in an airtight container, introducing industrial grade carbon dioxide gas, and converting lithium hydroxide into lithium carbonate, (iii) crushing lithium carbonate crude product, washing, oven drying and crushing to obtain a refined lithium carbonate powder, (iv) adding calcium hydroxide, adding lithium carbonate powder, mixing to carry out causticization reaction, performing coarse filtration, collecting filtrate, and performing fine filtration, (v) adding lithium hydroxide solution to reaction kettle, stirring, introducing industrial grade carbon dioxide gas, reacting, stirring, increasing temperature to 80-98degrees Celsius, and heat-preserving for 10-30 minutes to obtain a suspension, and (vi) centrifuging the suspension, stirring the solid, washing with pure water, and drying.
   USE - The method is used for preparing high-purity lithium carbonate by recovering lithium hydroxide for air purification for ceramic, glass, atomic, aerospace, lithium battery, lithium alloy and medicine applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple process, low cost, easy industrial production, and reduces the content of the impurity ion in the product, and effectively improves the purity of the product.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing high-purity lithium carbonate by recovering lithium hydroxide for air purification, involves (i) drying recovered air purification lithium hydroxide material to obtain a dried material, (ii) placing the dried material in an airtight container, introducing industrial grade carbon dioxide gas to react, and converting lithium hydroxide into lithium carbonate to obtain a lithium carbonate crude product, (iii) performing lithium carbonate refining by crushing the lithium carbonate crude product, stirring and washing with pure water, oven drying and crushing to obtain a refined lithium carbonate powder, (iv) adding calcium hydroxide while stirring to pure water, adding the lithium carbonate powder, completely mixing to carry out causticization reaction, performing coarse filtration, collecting filtrate, and performing fine filtration to obtain lithium hydroxide solution, (v) adding the lithium hydroxide solution to the reaction kettle, stirring, introducing industrial grade carbon dioxide gas, reacting under normal pressure, when the pH value of the reaction solution drops to 10, stopping the ventilation, continuously stirring, increasing the temperature to 80-98degrees Celsius, and heat-preserving for 10-30 minutes to obtain a suspension, and (vi) centrifuging the suspension, stirring the solid, washing with pure water, and drying to obtain the high-purity lithium carbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (ii) involves placing the dried material in an airtight container, arranging an air inlet above the airtight container, arranging an air outlet below, introducing the industrial-grade carbon dioxide gas to the airtight container through the air inlet to carry out the reaction, and when the flow rate of the industrial-grade carbon dioxide gas at the air outlet is constant, stopping the reaction to obtain the crude product of lithium carbonate with a purity of greater than or equal to 98.5%. The step (iv) involves maintaining the filtration precision during fine filtration to be 0.1 mum, when the metal impurity ion content in the filtrate is greater than 10 pats per million (ppm), adding a complexing agent to the filtrate to remove the impurity ions, and performing fine filtration.Preferred Conditions: The moisture content in the dried material in step (i) is less than or equal to 0.5 wt.%. In step (iii), the crushed particle size is greater than or equal to 30 mesh, and the stirring and washing is carried out at 80-100degrees Celsius for 30-60 minutes. The purity of the lithium carbonate powder in step (iii) is greater than or equal to 99%. In step (v), during the stirring and washing, the solid-liquid ratio is 0.3:1-0.5:1, and the purity of the high-purity lithium carbonate is more tha 4 N.Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of the lithium carbonate crude product and pure water during stirring and washing in step (iii) is 0.3:1-0.5:1. The mass ratio of calcium hydroxide and pure water in step (iv) is 0.03:1-0.05:1.

PN CN113304722-A; CN113304722-B
TI Cerium-potassium co-doped magnesium oxide-based            intermediate temperature carbon dioxide adsorbent            comprises monodisperse granular magnesium oxide            carrier, cerium-potassium, and auxiliary sodium nitrate            supported.
AU ZHOU Z
   CUI H
   DONG H
   NIU S
AE UNIV EAST CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYEC-C)
GA 2021A2678J
AB    NOVELTY - A cerium-potassium co-doped magnesium                oxide-based intermediate temperature carbon dioxide                adsorbent comprises monodisperse granular magnesium                oxide carrier co-doped with cerium-potassium, and                auxiliary sodium nitrate supported on                carrier.
   USE - Cerium-potassium co-doped magnesium                oxide-based intermediate temperature carbon dioxide                adsorbent.
   ADVANTAGE - The adsorbent has high adsorption capacity,                fast adsorption rate, unique monodisperse granular                structure, improved carbon dioxide adsorption                performance and simple preparation method.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A cerium-potassium co-doped magnesium                oxide-based intermediate temperature carbon dioxide                adsorbent comprises monodisperse granular magnesium                oxide carrier co-doped with cerium-potassium and                specific proportion of auxiliary sodium nitrate                supported on carrier. The adsorbent has chemical                formula (MgxCeyKz)-(Na')m (I).
   MgxCeyKz=cerium-potassium co-doped magnesium                oxide carrier;
   Na'=sodium nitrate;
   x=80-90;
   y, z=5-10; and
   m=15-25.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                preparation of the adsorbent comprising:
   (A) weighing magnesium salt, cerium salt and                potassium salt, and dissolving in deionized water                for 30 minutes until the solution is transparent                and clear,;
   (B) weighing citric acid and polyethylene                glycol with average molecular weight of 400, adding                to transparent and clear solution, transferring to                single-necked flask, mechanically stirring in a                constant temperature water bath at 80 degrees C and                at speed of 500 revolutions/minute for 4 hours to                obtain sol, taking out, placing in evaporating                dish, and oven drying to 150 degrees C for 5 hours                to obtain fluffy gel;
   (C) grinding solid into powder, adding to                muffle furnace, and calcining to 500 degrees C at                heating rate of 5 degrees C/minute for 3 hours to                obtain cerium-potassium doped magnesium oxide                carrier; and
   (D) weighing sodium nitrate, adding to                separate single-neck flask, adding anhydrous                methanol, stirring, adding cerium-potassium doped                magnesium oxide carrier, stirring for 2 hours, and                vacuum rotary evaporating at 60 degrees C to remove                methanol solvent.
   The magnesium salt comprises magnesium acetate                and/or magnesium nitrate. The cerium salt comprises                cerium acetate and/or cerium nitrate. The potassium                salt comprises potassium acetate, potassium nitrate                and/or potassium citrate. The number of moles of                magnesium salt per 100 mL of deionized water is                0.01-0.02. The molar ratio of citric acid and                magnesium ion is 1.2-1.5. The volume ratio of                polyethylene glycol 400 and deionized water is                0.05-0.10. The number of moles of sodium nitrate                added is 0.005-0.01 100 mL of anhydrous                methanol.

PN CN112481187-A
TI New recombinant Escherichia coli with e.g. formic            acid autotrophic ability, obtained by introducing            strain with e.g. formate dehydrogenase gene, and            overexpressing endogenous glycine lyase complex, used            for e.g. recycling carbon dioxide.
AU FANG Z
   SHA C
   ZHANG R
   YU Y
AE UNIV JIANGSU (UYJS-C)
GA 202130175X
AB    NOVELTY - A recombinant Escherichia coli with formic                acid and carbon dioxide autotrophic ability,                obtained by introducing the recombinant E.coli with                an exogenous formate assimilation gene cluster                fhs-fchA-folD and a formate dehydrogenase gene                CbFDH1, and overexpressing the endogenous glycine                lyase complex EcgcvTHP, is new.
   USE - The recombinant E.coli is useful in chemical,                pharmaceutical or environmental fields for                recycling carbon dioxide and promoting the de novo                synthesis of pyruvate.
   ADVANTAGE - The recombinant E.coli recycles carbon dioxide                with high-efficiency, and is the fastest growing                formic acid and CO2 autotrophic E.coli without the                aid of gene knockout and long-term domestication.                The method is simple, and has short cycle and high                versatility. The recombinant bacteria obtains a                maximum of 0.9 optical density (OD)600 biomass                within 56 hours, while the original bacteria hardly                grow.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following: (1) method for constructing recombinant                E.coli; (2) a modular plasmid pM6tac-fhs-fchA-folD                with formic acid assimilation function comprising a                8879 base pair sequence (SEQ ID NO: 5) fully                defined in the specification; (3) a modular plasmid                pACYC-EcgcvTHP-CbFDH1 with carbon dioxide (CO2)                assimilation function comprising a 9514 base pair                sequence (SEQ ID NO: 6) fully defined in the                specification; and (4) method for culturing                recombinant E.coli involves inoculating the                constructed recombinant E.coli single clone into                the liquid seed medium, culturing to a certain                amount of cells to obtain the seed liquid of the                recombinant bacteria, collecting the seed liquid                cells, washing with physiological saline,                inoculating into the fermentation medium with                formic acid and CO2 as the sole carbon source, and                culturing the formic acid and CO2 autotrophic                recombinant E.coli, where the fermentation medium                comprises 4 g sodium formate, 8 g sodium                bicarbonate, 6.8 g disodium hydrogen phosphate                heptahydrate, 3 g potassium dihydrogen phosphate,                0.5 g sodium chloride, 1 g ammonium chloride , 0.8                g magnesium sulfate heptahydrate, 0.24 g isopropyl                beta -d-1-thiogalactopyranoside (IPTG), 0.05 g                EDTA, 2 mg thiamine, 10 ml vitamin mixture, 10 ml                trace element mixture, 80 mg/l ampicillin, and 25                mg/l chloramphenicol, where pH value of the medium                is 7.2, where the vitamin mixture contains biotin,                folic acid, pyridoxine hydrochloride, riboflavin,                nicotinic acid, calcium D-(+)-pantothenate, vitamin                B12, p-aminobenzoic acid and thioctic acid, and the                trace element mixture comprises manganese sulfate,                zinc sulfate, calcium chloride, cobaltous chloride                hexahydrate, ferrous sulfate heptahydrate,                copper(II) chloride dihydrate, sodium selenite,                nickel(II) chloride hexahydrate and sodium                tungstate dihydrate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preparation (Claimed): The recombinant                  E.coli is prepared by (i) constructing a modular                  plasmid pM6tac-fhs-fchA-folD with formic acid                  assimilation function, (ii) constructing a                  modular plasmid pACYC-EcgcvTHP-CbFDH1 with CO2                  assimilation function, (iii) constructing a                  recombinant E.coli with formic acid and CO2                  autotrophic ability by transforming the                  constructed modular plasmid with formic acid                  assimilation function and the modular plasmid                  with CO2 assimilation function into E.coli by                  electroporation, and obtaining the recombinant                  E.coli was obtained by culture with double                  resistance plates of ampicillin and                  chloramphenicol. Preferred Biomolecule: The                  formate assimilation enzyme gene cluster                  fhs-fchA-folD comprises a 4030 amino acid                  sequence (SEQ ID NO: 1) fully defined in the                  specification. The formate dehydrogenase gene                  CbFDH1 comprises a 1097 amino acid sequence (SEQ                  ID NO: 2) fully defined in the specification. The                  glycine lyase complex gene EcgcvTHP comprises a                  4500 amino acid sequence (SEQ ID NO: 3) fully                  defined in the specification. The gene sequence                  of the chassis plasmid pM6tac comprises a 5206                  base pair sequence (SEQ ID NO: 4) fully defined                  in the specification. Preferred Method: The                  method involves constructing the modular plasmid                  pM6tac-fhs-fchA-folD with formic acid                  assimilation function by (i) taking the plasmid                  pETDuet-1 as the basic backbone, replacing the T7                  promoter with the tac promoter, introducing the                  restriction site AvrII in front of the tac                  promoter, and introducing the restriction sites                  SpeI and NheI before and after the T7 terminator                  to obtain the chassis pM6tac of the modular                  plasmid, (ii) respectively cloning the fhs, fchA,                  and folD gene fragments in the genome sequence of                  the formate assimilation gene cluster, and                  ligating to the pM6tac plasmid after restriction                  digestion to obtain 3 recombinant plasmids, and                  (iii) through the complementation and elimination                  of different restriction sites, by cutting and                  ligating the three recombinant plasmids obtained,                  the three formate assimilation genes fhs, fchA,                  and folD carrying the tac promoter were                  successively stacked on the chassis plasmid                  pM6tac, and obtaining the modular plasmid                  pM6tac-fhs-fchA-folD with formic acid                  assimilation function. The method involves                  constructing the modular plasmid                  pACYC-EcgcvTHP-CbFDH1 with CO2 assimilation                  function by using plasmid pACYCDuet-1 as the                  chassis plasmid, inserting the EcgcvTHP gene                  sequence at one of the multiple cloning sites of                  the plasmid to obtain plasmid pACYC-EcgcvTHP,                  inserting the CbFDH1 gene sequence at the other                  multiple cloning site of the plasmid to obtain                  plasmid pACYC-CbFHD1, performing double-enzyme                  digestion of the plasmid pACYC-EcgcvTHP and the                  plasmid pACYC-CbFHD1, recovering the linear                  plasmid fragment pACYC-CbFHD1 and the gene                  fragment EcgcvTHP, ligating, transforming, and                  screening on the resistance plate to obtain the                  modular plasmid pACYC-EcgcvTHP-CbFDH1 with CO2                  assimilation function.

PN IN201921011161-A; IN366418-B
TI Separating pollutant flue gases by collecting pollutant flue gases, cooling ambient temperature flue gases by vapor compression refrigeration system, separating nitrogen dioxide and sulfur dioxide to store, and compressing carbon dioxide.
AU KASHYAP V
AE KASHYAP V (KASH-Individual)
   KASHYAP V (KASH-Individual)
GA 2020A1554T
AB    NOVELTY - Method and apparatus for separation of pollutant flue gases by refrigeration, involve (a) collecting pollutant flue gases in flue gas chamber from exhaust pipe, (b) pre-cooling flue gases in water chamber by passing through coils, (c) cooling ambient temperature flue gases by vapor compression refrigeration system (VCRS) in separation chamber, (d) using VCRS system comprising compressor section creating high pressurize refrigerant, condenser to reduce the refrigerant temperature and change the phase of refrigerant, control valve for the flow of the refrigerant, and refrigerant passing through separation chamber and reduce the temperature of flue gases, (e) using supercooled flue gases in separation chamber consisting nitrogen dioxide, sulfur dioxide and carbon dioxide, separating the nitrogen dioxide in liquid form in can, separating sulfur dioxide in liquid form and storing in can, and (f) compressing carbon dioxide by compressor, separating in liquid form, and collecting in can.
   USE - The method and apparatus are used for separation of pollutant flue gases, which are used in chemical and food industries.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces air pollution, utilizes components of flue gas, reduces respiratory and skin issues caused due to acid rain, generates income by selling flue gas components, eliminates need of chimneys, eliminates need of flue gas treatment, and reduces carbon emission. The method circulates flue gases in multi-stages separation chamber to achieve required desired temperature for liquefaction. The sulfur dioxide and nitrogen dioxide are easily convertible from vapor to liquid without increasing their pressure.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method and apparatus for separation of pollutant flue gases by refrigeration method, involve (a) collecting pollutant flue gases in flue gas chamber from exhaust pipe, (b) pre-cooling flue gases in water chamber by passing through coils, (c) cooling ambient temperature flue gases by vapor compression refrigeration system (VCRS) in separation chamber, (d) using VCRS system comprising compressor section creating high pressurize refrigerant, condenser to reduce the temperature of the refrigerant and change the phase of refrigerant, control valve to control the flow of the refrigerant, and refrigerant passing through separation chamber and reduce the temperature of flue gases, (e) using supercooled flue gases in separation chamber consisting nitrogen dioxide, sulfur dioxide and carbon dioxide, separating the nitrogen dioxide in liquid form at 10-20 degrees C in can, separating sulfur dioxide in liquid form at -14 degrees C to -16 degrees C and storing in can, and (f) compressing carbon dioxide by compressor at 25-30 bar at -12 degrees C to -8 degrees C, separating in liquid form, and collecting in can.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The flue gases are collected in vapor form through chimneys, exhaust gases from the flue gases and any other flue gases resources. The flue gases pre-cool the flue gases at normal bar and normal temperature of the water in water chamber. The flue gases cool and reduce the temperature by use of separation chamber consists of evaporator. The refrigerant is compressed in compressor section without changing of temperature and velocity. The flue gases reduce the velocity and pressure by use of expansion valve. The first nitrogen dioxide is separated from the mixture of flue gases and stored in nitrogen dioxide tank at 10-20 degrees C. The second sulfur dioxide is separated from the mixture of flue gases and stored in sulfur dioxide tank at -14 degrees C to -16 degrees C. The third carbon dioxide is separated from the mixture of flue gases and stored in carbon dioxide tank at -12 degrees C to -8 degrees C at 25-30 bar pressure of the compressor.

TI Treating supercritical carbon dioxide fluid flash            explosion used for artificial fibers and products,            involves taking artificial fibers and their products as            processed samples, and filling the processed            samples.
AU SHI M
   CAI C
   ZHANG Z
   LONG J
AE ZHOUSHAN XINXIN CHEM FIBRE CO LTD (ZHOU-Non-standard)
   UNIV SOOCHOW (USWZ-C)
GA 202093641M
AB    NOVELTY - Treating supercritical carbon dioxide fluid                flash explosion involves taking artificial fibers                and their products as processed samples, and                filling the processed samples in the fluid flash                explosion processing unit. The flash explosion                treatment unit connected to the supercritical CO2                flash explosion treatment system. The CO2 medium                pressurizing device connected to fill the                supercritical CO2 flash explosion treatment system                with a fixed amount of CO2, and heated up at the                same time. The CO2 fluid circulation treatment                carried out on processed samples when the internal                temperature and pressure of the system reach the                preset value. The temperature and pressure of the                separation and recovery system adjusted to the                preset value according to the requirements of the                flash explosion process, and realizes flash                explosion with different temperature and pressure                differences.
   USE - Method for treating supercritical carbon                dioxide fluid flash explosion used for artificial                fibers and products.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to treat supercritical                carbon dioxide fluid flash explosion achieves                physical modification of artificial fibers and                their products, increases the voids inside the                fiber, enlarged the pore diameter, beneficial to                the subsequent yarn formation or dyeing, has better                subsequent spinning and textile wear performance,                has simple operation, low energy consumption, no                pollution, fibers, utilizes the space of the device                without mutual influence, and has high                efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Treating supercritical carbon dioxide fluid                flash explosion involves taking artificial fibers                and their products as processed samples, and                filling the processed samples in the fluid flash                explosion processing unit. The flash explosion                treatment unit connected to the supercritical CO2                flash explosion treatment system. The CO2 medium                pressurizing device connected to fill the                supercritical CO2 flash explosion treatment system                with a fixed amount of CO2, and heated up at the                same time. The CO2 fluid circulation treatment                carried out on processed samples when the internal                temperature and pressure of the system reach the                preset value. The temperature and pressure of the                separation and recovery system adjusted to the                preset value according to the requirements of the                flash explosion process, and realizes flash                explosion with different temperature and pressure                differences. The pressure relief valve of the                system is open, perform rapid or instant pressure                relief according to the process, and perform flash                explosion treatment on the processed samples. The                gas recovery pump of the separation recovery system                turn on to recover gas. The pressure reduces in the                flash explosion system to atmospheric pressure,                taken out the sample, and finished the flash                explosion treatment of artificial fiber or its                products.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The artificial fibers                  and their products includes chemical synthetic                  fibers, regenerated cellulose fibers, and                  regenerated protein fibers, and their yarns and                  fabric products. The synthetic fibers include                  polyester fibers, polyamide fibers, polylactic                  acid fibers, acrylic fibers, chlorinated fibers,                  vinylon, spandex, polyolefin elastic yarns and                  their modified special fibers. The regenerated                  cellulose fibers include ordinary viscose fibers,                  Tencel/Lyocell fibers, Modal/Modal fibers, bamboo                  fibers, cupra fibers, and acetate fibers. The                  regenerated protein fiber includes soybean fiber,                  peanut fiber, milk fiber, silkworm pupa fiber,                  sericin fiber. Preferred Conditions: The fluffy                  fiber is first extruded neatly and uniformly                  layer by layer under mechanical external force                  when the processed sample is fiber, filled it                  flatly in the fluid flash explosion treatment                  unit. The yarn is wound neatly and uniformly                  under the action of mechanical external force                  when the processed sample is yarn, then wind it                  uniformly and smoothly in the fluid flash                  explosion treatment unit. The fabric is firstly                  wound smoothly, neatly and uniformly under the                  action of mechanical external force when the                  processed sample is a fabric, then it is                  uniformly and smoothly wound in the fluid flash                  explosion treatment unit. The preset value of the                  internal pressure of the system is 0.1-30                  megapascal (MPa). The preset value of the                  internal temperature of the system is 90-180                  degrees C. The CO2 fluid circulation treatment                  includes the treatment time is 1-120 minutes. The                  time ratio of fluid circulation to static state                  is 1:5-10:1. The preset temperature and pressure                  of the separation and recovery system are 0-120                  degrees C and 0-25 megapascal (MPa),                  respectively. The rapid or instantaneous pressure                  relief and the pressure relief time is                  0.070-30s.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Response surface method optimized                supercritical carbon dioxide extraction process for                eucalyptus seed oil, comprises (i) drying                eucalyptus seed at a constant temperature, and                pulverizing and sieveing dried eucalyptus seed,                (ii) charging crushed eucalyptus seed into the                extraction kettle, and extraction temperature, the                extraction pressure, adjusting extraction time and                carbon dioxide flow rate to value, and connecting                receiving bottle to the outlet valve to collect the                eucalyptus seed oil for extraction and weighing                eucalyptus seed oil and calculating oil rate; (iii)                based on the single factor experiment, selecting                the extraction temperature A that has a significant                effect on the oil yield Y of eucalyptus seed, three                factors of extraction pressure B and extraction                time C are the factors of investigation, designing                response surface test and testing by the                Box-Behnken design method, (iv) analyzing and                processing the experimental data through software,                establishing mathematical models to optimize the                process conditions for extracting eucalyptus seed                oil, where prediction model is as given in the                specification A and above A is 45-65 degrees C and                B 20-30 Mpa and C is 30-90 minutes; and (v)                according to 3D map of the corresponding response                surface, analyzing influence of the three                investigation factors on the response rate of the                oil yield and intuitively analyzing the interaction                between the evaluation variables to determine the                final optimal process conditions

PN CN114317350-A
TI Device for producing hydrogen fuel cell based on activated sludge fermentation comprises sludge anaerobic fermentation module utilizes Clostridium P21, ferment via liquid medium of substrate and auxiliary materials and alkaline cleaning module for removing carbon dioxide and acid gas.
AU DING D
   HU P
AE SUZHOU GUTANLV HYDROGEN BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SUZH-Non-standard)
GA 202263714H
AB    NOVELTY - Device for producing hydrogen fuel cell based on activated sludge fermentation comprises a sludge anaerobic fermentation module used to utilize Clostridium P21, ferment through the liquid medium of substrate and auxiliary materials, and set the pH value to be constant to be greater than 7 and the fermentation temperature to be greater than 40degrees Celsius to produce a mixed gas containing hydrogen. The activated sludge comprising organic matter, nutrients, microorganisms and moisture. The mixed gas only contains hydrogen, carbon dioxide and acid gas with a purity of more than 98%. The Clostridium P21 is a rod-shaped alkalophilic Clostridium. The substrate includes the activated sludge. The auxiliary material comprises phosphate compound liquid. The water washing module receives the mixed gas, and washes off the suspended particles in the mixed gas. An alkaline cleaning module is for removing carbon dioxide and acid gas in the mixed gas by neutralization.
   USE - The device is useful for producing hydrogen fuel cell based on activated sludge fermentation.
   ADVANTAGE - The device ferments active sludge at high temperature and high alkaline condition to produce hydrogen with purity more than 98%, and processes high-purity hydrogen to supply power to the hydrogen electric fuel cell.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Device for producing hydrogen fuel cell based on activated sludge fermentation comprises a sludge anaerobic fermentation module used to utilize Clostridium P21, ferment through the liquid medium of substrate and auxiliary materials, and set the pH value to be constant to be greater than 7 and the fermentation temperature to be greater than 40degrees Celsius to produce a mixed gas containing hydrogen. The activated sludge comprising organic matter, nutrients, microorganisms and moisture. The mixed gas only contains hydrogen, carbon dioxide and acid gas with a purity of more than 98%. The Clostridium P21 is a rod-shaped alkalophilic Clostridium. The substrate includes the activated sludge. The auxiliary material comprises phosphate compound liquid. The water washing module receives the mixed gas, and washes off the suspended particles in the mixed gas. An alkaline cleaning module is for removing carbon dioxide and acid gas in the mixed gas by neutralization. An adsorption module removes the peculiar smell in the mixed gas through activated carbon adsorption, and outputs the mixed gas to supply power to the hydrogen fuel cell module.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The diagram shows a schematic view of the device for producing hydrogen fuel cell based on activated sludge fermentation (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: In the step (a), the phosphate complex solution comprises 1.4 g potassium dihydrogen phosphate, 1.1 g dipotassium hydrogen phosphate, 2 g ammonium sulfate, 0.5 g magnesium sulfate, 10 g yeast extract, 10 g morpholine ethanesulfonic acid, 40 mg nitrilotriacetic acid, 20 mg manganese sulfate monohydrate, 16 mg ferrous ammonium sulfate hexahydrate, 4 mg cobalt chloride hexahydrate, 0.04 mg zinc sulfate heptahydrate, 0.4 mg copper chloride dihydrate, 0.4 mg nickel chloride hexahydrate, 0.4 mg sodium molybdate, 0.4 mg sodium selenate, 0.4 mg sodium tungstate, and 0.3 g cysteine, where the Clostridium P21 stock solution is 10 ml.Preferred Method: The sludge anaerobic fermentation module includes feeding module located at the upper end of a fermentation tank, and used for regularly and quantitatively intermittently placing the liquid culture medium including substrate and auxiliary materials and the Clostridium P21 mother liquor into the fermentation tank, the substrate is carbohydrates, proteins, fats, sugars, oils, acids and/or organic acids included in the activated sludge for hydrogen production by fermentation, and the auxiliary materials also include the active nutrients contained in sludge, the nutrients are nitrogen salts, phosphorus salts and/or potassium salts, a deoxygenation module for purging the liquid culture medium from the bottom of the fermenter with inert gas or carbon dioxide gas to remove residual oxygen in the liquid culture medium, a temperature control module for introducing the liquid culture medium into a fermenter with a stirrer, and setting the fermentation temperature to a temperature between 50degrees Celsius and 80degrees Celsius, the pH value control module is used to control the pH value of the fermenter in the fermentation cycle to be a constant value between 8 and 12 through the acid solution and the alkaline solution and the residual material output module is located at the lower part of the fermentation tank, and used for outputting the fermentation residual material from the feeding port periodically and quantitatively and intermittently. The device further includes a pretreatment module for removing heavy metals and/or inorganic salts in the activated sludge before feeding and the inorganic salt is silicon oxide, iron oxide, aluminum oxide, calcium oxide and/or salt. The method further comprises removing heavy metals and/or inorganic salts before performing step (a), where the inorganic salt is silicon oxide, iron oxide, aluminum oxide, calcium oxide and/or salt. The step (a) further comprises purging the fermenter with inert gas or carbon dioxide. In the step (a), the timed and quantitative intermittent loading and unloading are every predetermined time, the 10% liquid medium of substrate and auxiliary materials and the mother liquor of Clostridium P21 are placed into the fermenter and the no more than 10% of the fermentation residue is output from the discharge port, the pH value is constantly adjusted by the acid solution being a 2 M hydrochloric acid solution and the basic solution being a 5 M sodium hydroxide solution. The fermentation is carried out at 60degrees Celsius with pH of 10.5.

PT P
PN WO2021119752-A1; AU2020404450-A1; EP4076727-A1
TI Storing energy by heating material having carbon            dioxide sorbed product and additive (that prevents            formation of crust on material) to desorb carbon            dioxide from material and convert carbon dioxide sorbed            product to carbon dioxide sorbent.
AU PASKEVICIUS M P
   MOLLER K T
   BUCKLEY C E
AE UNIV CURTIN (UYCU-Non-standard)
   UNIV CURTIN (UYCU-Non-standard)
   UNIV CURTIN (UYCU-Non-standard)
GA 2021703901
AB    NOVELTY - Storing energy involves heating a material                comprising a carbon dioxide (CO2) sorbed product                and an additive to desorb CO2 from the material and                convert the CO2 sorbed product to a CO2 sorbent.                The additive at least partially prevents during                heating: (i) sintering the CO2 sorbent and/or the                CO2 sorbed product; and (ii) the formation of a                crust on the material, the crust minimizing or                preventing the CO2 sorbent and CO2 from reacting                with one another to form the CO2 sorbed product in                a subsequent CO2 absorption step.
   USE - The method is useful for storing energy                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The combination of adding both ZrO2 and Al2O3                to CaCO3 has a positive effect as a cyclic                stability of greater than or equal to 80% was                achieved at rapid calcination/carbonation times,                i.e. 20 and 30 minutes, respectively. The reaction                kinetics are fast, especially during carbonation.                The calcination kinetics improved as the sample is                cycled. Combining different active properties of                additives/catalysts can enhance the cyclic                stability of a metal carbonate even further. This                opened up multiple new pathways for optimizing the                thermochemical energy storage properties of metal                carbonates.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) a composition used to sorb and desorb CO2                in a thermal battery, comprising a form of calcium                that is capable of absorbing or desorbing CO2 to,                respectively, form a CO2 sorbed product or CO2                desorbed product; an additive having a                concentration of 5-95 wt.% relative an amount of                the CO2 sorbed product; where the additive at least                partially prevents upon heating of the composition                sintering of the CO2 sorbed/desorbed product and                the formation of a crust that minimized or prevents                the CO2 desorbed product and CO2 from reacting with                one another to form the CO2 sorbed product;                and
   (2) a system for storing energy, comprising a                reactor comprising a material that is capable of                absorbing or desorbing CO2 to, respectively, form a                CO2 sorbed product or CO2 desorbed product, the                material having an additive that at least partially                prevents during heating: sintering of the CO2                sorbent/sorbed product; and the formation of a                crust on the material, the crust minimizing or                preventing the CO2 sorbent and CO2 from reacting                with one another to form the CO2 sorbed product;                and a CO2 source that is in fluid communication                with the reactor to allow a flow of CO2 between the                reactor and CO2 source during absorption or                desorption of CO2.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ELECTRICAL POWER AND ENERGY - Preferred Method: The step of desorbing CO2                  from the material is performed at 600-1200                  degrees C The method further involves mixing an                  additive precursor with the material which reacts                  with the CO2 sorbent to form the additive. During                  CO2 absorption, the additive allows the CO2                  sorbent to migrate through the particle from an                  inner region of the particle to a surface of the                  particle to react with CO2 present at the surface                  of the particle to form the CO2 sorbed product.                  The method further involves sorbing CO2 onto the                  CO2 sorbent to reform the CO2 sorbed product,                  thus releasing energy. Desorbing CO2 from the CO2                  sorbed product is carried out under a reduced                  pressure compared to a pressure used to sorb CO2                  to the CO2 sorbent. A cycle of desorbing CO2 from                  the material and absorbing CO2 onto the material                  is repeated at least 100 times. The method is                  carried out in the absence of water. The additive                  is formed by mixing the form of calcium that is                  capable of absorbing or desorbing CO2 with an                  additive precursor that upon heating reacts with                  the CO2 desorbed product to form the additive.                  Upon heating and performing CO2 absorption and                  desorption, the composition adopts a porous                  structure. Preferred Components: The CO2 sorbent                  is calcium oxide (CaO) and the CO2 sorbed product                  is calcium carbonate (CaCO3). The additive                  precursor includes aluminum oxide (Al2O3) and/or                  zirconium dioxide (ZrO2). The additive is a metal                  oxide having at least one metal. It includes                  calcium zirconate (CaZrO3) and/or a calcium                  aluminate. It includes Ca5Al6O14 and Ca9Al6O18.                  The CO2 is provided as a gas or supercritical                  fluid. The CO2 is provided as a gas or as a                  supercritical fluid, and optionally has a purity                  of 95-99.95%, and can optionally be entrained in                  a carrier gas. The CO2 sorbed product is CaCO3                  and the desorbed product is CaO. The additive                  precursor includes Al2O3 and/or ZrO2. The reactor                  comprises the composition. The CO2 source is one                  or more of: a vessel capable of storing CO2                  either in gas, liquid or supercritical form; a                  material capable of storing CO2; and a carrier                  gas, such as air, that has a component of CO2.                  The material capable of storing CO2 comprises one                  or more of: a molecular sieve such as a zeolite;                  a metal organic framework; a nanomaterial;                  activated carbon. Preferred Composition: Ratio of                  the additive to the CO2 sorbed product is 10-70                  wt.%. Ratio of Ca5Al6O14 to Ca9Al6O18 is 100:0 to                  0:100. Preferred Parameter: The pressure used for                  CO2 absorption and desorption is less than or                  equal to 60 bar.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); G04 (Miscellaneous compositions - including luminescent and tenebrescent materials, de-icing/de-misting compositions, mastics, heat transfer compositions and aerosol-can filling mixtures (C09H).); X15 (Non-Fossil Fuel Power Generating Systems)

TI Medicine useful for clearing heat, removing stagnation, detoxifying, and promoting digestion, comprises e.g. crushing Prunella spike into powder, introducing into carbon dioxide supercritical extraction device, liquefying and circulating.
AU DAI J
AE SHENZHEN GUFANG CHINESE HERBAL PIECES CO (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 202164400N
AB    NOVELTY - Medicine prepared by (i) preparing Prunella extract including crushing Prunella into powder, introducing into carbon dioxide supercritical extraction device, turning on device as required, liquefying and circulating carbon dioxide in device, controlling extraction conditions of extraction kettle, analyzing kettle I and analyzing kettle II, and circulating and extracting Prunella, (ii) preparing Polygonum spp. extract including crushing Polygonum, immersing in edible alcohol according to material-to-liquid ratio of 10:80-200 g/ml for 3-8 hours, refluxing for extracting under ultrasonic auxiliary of 15-40 KHz, mixing the extract, distilling, and recycling edible alcohol, (iii) preparing Gentiana extract including crushing Gentiana, soaking 300 g/ml in edible alcohol for 2-6 hours according to material-to-liquid ratio of 10:120, and heating and refluxing for extracting under microwave auxiliary of 350-600 W, is claimed.
   USE - The medicine is useful for clearing heat, removing stagnation (claimed), detoxifying, and promoting digestion.
   ADVANTAGE - The medicine: makes it easier for the human body to absorb these functional substances; and avoids the waste of medicinal materials.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Medicine prepared by (i) preparing Prunella extract including crushing Prunella spike into powder, introducing into carbon dioxide supercritical extraction device, turning on device as required, liquefying and circulating carbon dioxide in device, controlling extraction conditions of extraction kettle, analyzing kettle I and analyzing kettle II, and circulating and extracting Prunella, (ii) preparing Polygonum spp. extract including crushing Polygonum, immersing in edible alcohol according to material-to-liquid ratio of 10:80-200 g/ml for 3-8 hours, refluxing for extracting under ultrasonic auxiliary of 15-40 KHz, mixing the extract, distilling, and recycling edible alcohol, (iii) preparing Gentiana extract including crushing Gentiana, soaking 300 g/ml in edible alcohol for 2-6 hours according to material-to-liquid ratio of 10:120, heating and refluxing for extracting under microwave auxiliary of 350-600 W, combining extracts, and reducing pressure to remove edible alcohol, (iv) preparing adjuvant solution including placing washed gourd tea, green tea, mint, peony, clove flower, white turnip and balsam pear in crusher to crush into powder, placing in deionized water for boiling, and filtering, (v) preparing medicine powder particles including placing Prunella extract, Polygonum spp. extract, Gentiana extract and mixed solution in stirrer for stirring, concentrating, and spraying and drying concentrated solution, and (vi) preparing medicine including introducing medicine powder particles, lactose, 1-2% cyclodextrin inclusion powder and 0.1-1% magnesium stearate in stirrer for mixing, transferring to dry extrusion granulator for granulating, sieving and packaging.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the carbon dioxide flow rate is 15-35 l/hours, and the extracting is carried out at 40-60 degrees C under 24-40 MPa for 2-6 hours. The temperature of the analyzing reactor I at 40-50 degrees C under 12-20 MPa. The temperature of the analyzing kettle II at 50-60 degrees C under 5-12 MPa. In the step (ii), the extracting is carried out at 65-80 degrees C for 3-8 hours. In the step (iii), the extracting is carried out at 65-80 degrees C for 2-6 hours. In the step (v), the inlet air temperature of spray drying is 140-180 degrees C, the outlet air temperature is 60-90 degrees C, and the spray pressure is 1-3 kg/cm2. Preferred Components: In the step (iv), the mass ratio between gourd tea, green tea, mint, peony, clove flower, white turnip, balsam pear and deionized water is 8-15:1220:2.5-4:0.6-1:0.8-1.5 :6-10:1.5-2.5:100. In the step (v), the mass ratio among the Prunella extract, Polygonum spp. extract, Gentiana extract and the adjuvant solution is 10:8-20:12-30:180-300. The mass ratio between the medicine powder particles and lactose is 100:180-300.
   ACTIVITY - Antipyretic; Antidote; Gastrointestinal-Gen.

PN CN112169799-A; CN112169799-B
TI Use iron-based catalysts for carbon dioxide            hydrogenation to synthesize light olefins comprises at            least one iron element or oxides, oxides of cobalt,            nickel and/or magnesium, and oxides of lithium, sodium,            potassium, rubidium and/or cesium.
AU HUANG Y
   LIANG B
   DUAN H
   ZHANG T
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 202106275N
AB    NOVELTY - Use iron-based catalysts for carbon dioxide                hydrogenation to synthesize light olefins comprises                (a) at least one 5-88 wt.% iron element or oxides,                (b) oxides of cobalt, nickel and/or magnesium, and                the amount is 10-80 wt.%, (c) oxides of lithium,                sodium, potassium, rubidium, and/or cesium, and the                proportion is 0.001-20 wt.%.
   USE - The iron-based catalysts is useful for carbon                dioxide hydrogenation to synthesize light olefins                (i.e., carbon olefin product with                2-4C)(claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst obtains low-carbon olefins with                high selectivity and high stability, directly                converts carbon dioxide into low-carbon olefins,                the conversion rate of carbon dioxide reach 43.5%,                the selectivity of methane is not less than 10%,                and carbon-based selectivity of low-carbon olefins                is 46%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing iron-based catalyst comprising (i)                dissolving the soluble iron salt and soluble salt                of M in deionized water to obtain solution A, the                concentration of metal ions is 0.1-2 mol/L, where M                is cobalt, nickel, and/or magnesium, (ii)                dissolving alkali in deionized water, preparing                precipitation agent to obtain solution B with                concentration of 0.001-10 mol/L, (iii) adding                solution B in water bath at 20-60 degrees C, adding                solution A under stirring, precipitating to obtain                mixed solution, the volume ratio of solution B to                solution A is 0.5-10, adjusting the pH of the                system to 9-11, stirring the obtained mixture, and                aging in water bath at 60-95 degrees C for 0.5-24                hours, (iv) filtering and washing the obtained                turbid liquid, and drying the filter cake in the                air at 50-200 degrees C for 12-48 hours to obtain                hydrotalcite precursor C, (v) dissolving soluble                salts of lithium, sodium, potassium, rubidium                and/or cesium in deionized water to obtain solution                D, where the concentration of alkali metal ions is                0.001-10 mol/L, (vi) providing solid C, dropping                solution D into solid C or pouring into solid C,                where the volume ratio of solution D to solid C is                0.1-10, and evenly mixing, (vii) immersing the                mixture at room temperature for 0.5-48 hours,                (viii) drying the mixture at 60-120 degrees C for                4-24 hours, and (ix) calcining the dried mixture at                350-700 degrees C for 1-8 hours to obtain                iron-based catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the component (a),                  the iron oxide is ferrous ferric oxide and/or                  iron oxide, and the content is 25-70%. The                  component (b) is oxides of cobalt, nickel and/or                  magnesium, and the content is 20-70 wt.%. The                  component (c) is oxides of lithium, sodium,                  potassium and/or cesium, and the content is                  0.01-15 wt.%. In the step (i), the soluble iron                  salt is nitrate and/or chloride. The soluble salt                  of M is nitrate and/or chloride. In the step                  (ii), the alkali is aqueous solution of sodium                  hydroxide and sodium carbonate, the molar ratio                  of sodium hydroxide and sodium carbonate is                  0.5-2:1. In the step (v), the soluble salts of                  lithium, sodium, potassium, rubidium and cesium                  is nitrates. Preferred Method: In the step (ix),                  roasting in flowing or static air atmosphere,                  heating the program from room temperature or                  drying temperature to the roasting temperature,                  where the heating speed is 0.5-10 degrees                  C/minutes, the roasting temperature is 380-500                  degrees C, and the roasting time is 0.5-6 hours.                  In the method, reductively activating the                  catalyst as follows: the reducing gas is pure                  hydrogen, the molar purity is greater than or                  equal to 99.9%, the volumetric space velocity of                  reducing gas is 1000-3600 hour-1, the heating                  rate from room temperature to reduction                  temperature is 1-10 degrees C/minutes, the                  reduction temperature is 200-600 degrees C, the                  pressure is normal pressure, and the reduction                  time is 1-24 hours. In the method, the catalyst                  is used to fixed bed reactor for CO2                  hydrogenation to synthesize low-carbon olefins                  under the condition is as follows: the reaction                  gas is mixture of carbon dioxide and hydrogen,                  the molar ratio hydrogen: carbon dioxide =0.5-4,                  the volumetric space velocity is 600-30000                  ml/(gcatx hours), the reaction temperature is                  200-600 degrees C, and the reaction pressure is                  0.1-6 MPa.

PN CN111892126-A
TI Growth desorption filter membrane for sewage treatment, comprises desorption filter membrane installed on inner wall of sewage transmission pipeline and desorption filter membrane includes fixed filter membrane.
AU ZHANG L
AE SICHUAN JINCHENG JIAHE ECOLOGICAL ENVIRO (SICH-Non-standard)
GA 2020B3082Y
AB    NOVELTY - Growth desorption filter membrane comprises a desorption filter membrane installed on the inner wall of a sewage transmission pipeline (1), desorption filter membrane includes a fixed filter membrane (2) fixedly connected to the inner wall of the sewage transmission pipeline and a movable filter membrane (3) is fixedly connected to the upper end of the fixed filter membrane. The upper end of the pneumatic storage cylinder is fixedly connected with a rubber closure sleeve (5). The rubber closure sleeve is located between the fixed filter membrane and the movable filter membrane and is fixedly connected with the movable filter membrane. The inner wall of the pneumatic storage cylinder is fixedly connected with a waterproof and breathable membrane.
   USE - Growth desorption filter membrane for sewage treatment.
   ADVANTAGE - The growth desorption filter membrane realizes the desorption between the fixed filter membrane and the dynamic filter membrane, increases contact area between the entire desorption membrane and sewage, improves the filtering effect of sewage, reduces resistance to the flow of sewage, improves efficiency of sewage filtration, improves sealing effect between the pneumatic push ball and the pneumatic storage cylinder, and improves ability of the dynamic filter membrane to withstand the impact of sewage.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for using a growth-desorption filter membrane for sewage treatment, which involves:
   (A) impacting on the fixed filter membrane when the sewage flows can promote the escape of carbon dioxide in the carbon dioxide aqueous solution in the pneumatic storage cylinder;
   (B) pushing pneumatic push ball along the pneumatic storage cylinder to increase the air pressure in the rubber closure, prompting the rubber closure, when the pneumatic push ball contacts the anti-dropping ring as the carbon dioxide is gradually released, parts of the carbon dioxide is forced to push off the rubber seal and entering the pneumatic push ball;
   (C) releasing magnetic shielding state of the built-in magnet ball, and the repulsion between the built-in magnet ball and the anti-dropping ring is used to promote pneumatic push ball, sliding down to encourage carbon dioxide to dissolve in water; and
   (D) contracting rubber sealing sleeve and the dynamic filter membrane to complete the dynamic filter membrane from propping up to shrinking and circulating with the dynamic changes of the dynamic filter membrane.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows sectional view of growth desorption filter membrane for sewage treatment.
   Sewage transmission pipeline (1)
   Fixed filter membrane (2)
   Movable filter membrane (3)
   Rubber closure sleeve (5)

PN CN108842479-A
TI Preparation of environment-friendly crucian fish seed uniformly dyeing agent by washing waste crucian carp seed with clean water, quickly freezing, drying, and filling in extraction kettle of supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device.
AU YE J
AE YE J (YEJJ-Individual)
GA 201894307A
AB    NOVELTY - An environment-friendly crucian fish seed uniformly dyeing agent is prepared by collecting waste crucian carp seed, washing with clean water, quickly freezing, and drying in vacuum chamber in vacuum drier; filling in extraction kettle of supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device to extract, checking tightness, setting extracting temperature, opening carbon dioxide and nitrogen gas storage tank valves, and setting extracting pressure, and opening outlet valve to collect extract A; taking absolute ethanol as entrainer, setting flow speed, extracting, continuously extracting, collecting extract B, vacuum distilling to separate anhydrous ethanol, and vacuum drying in vacuum drying box; and mixing and stirring extract A, extract B, plant sterol and CO40 high efficiency solubilizer, adding remaining water, continuously stirring, high pressure homogenizing, and vacuum distilling.
   USE - Method for preparing environment-friendly crucian fish seed uniformly dyeing agent.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has uniform dyeing. The agent has good fabric dyeing effect, highest dry friction fastness level reaching 5, wet friction fastness to 5 level and highest water washing-resistant fastness reaching 5 level, and does not change color by water washing.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An environment-friendly crucian fish seed uniformly dyeing agent is prepared by:
   (A) collecting waste crucian carp seed, washing with clean water, quickly freezing at - 40 degrees C at speed of 0.6-0.8 degrees C/minute, and drying in vacuum chamber in vacuum drier at absolute pressure of 66-70 MPa, heating plate temperature of 35-37 degrees C and cold trap temperature of - 60 degrees C;
   (B) filling in extraction kettle of supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device to extract, checking tightness, setting extracting temperature at 45-55 degrees C, opening carbon dioxide and nitrogen gas storage tank valves, setting extracting pressure at 35-45 MPa, and opening outlet valve to collect extract A;
   (C) taking absolute ethanol as entrainer, setting flow speed at 1.8-2.0 mL/minute, extracting at 50-60 degrees C at pressure of 30-40 MPa, continuously extracting for 2.5-3 hours, collecting extract B, vacuum distilling to separate anhydrous ethanol, and vacuum drying in vacuum drying box at 40-50 degrees C; and
   (D) mixing and stirring 4-5 wt.% extract A, 8-10 wt.% extract B, 1-1.5 wt.% plant sterol and 2-3 wt.% CO40 high efficiency solubilizer for 20-30 minutes, adding remaining water, continuously stirring for 10-20 minutes, high pressure homogenizing 3-4 times at pressure of 70-80 MPa, and vacuum distilling.

PT P
PN CN108455633-A; CN108455633-B
TI Extracting and recycling synthetic ammonia desulfurization residue fluid involves passing residual liquid from previous stage to primary micro-vortex sedimentation groove through pipeline, and removing large particulate matter.
AU XU S
   ZHENG J
   SUN C
AE ANHUI JINHE IND CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 201872546B
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting and recycling synthetic ammonia desulfurization residue fluid involves passing residual liquid from previous stage to primary micro-vortex sedimentation groove through pipeline, and removing large particulate matter in residual liquid in primary sedimentation groove by using micro-vortex physical sedimentation. The desulfurization residual liquid is sent to secondary micro-vortex sedimentation groove from top for clarifying. The clarified supernatant liquid is pumped in plate filter by using height difference of the device. The supernatant liquid is further purified in the plate filter. The purified liquid is sent to clean liquid groove. The liquid in the clean liquid groove is sent to bubbling absorption tower by the clear liquid pump. The solution with high concentration from carbon dioxide post and carbon dioxide storage tank is circulated for carbon dioxide gas absorption and precipitation to obtain precipitate of sodium bicarbonate.
   USE - Method for extracting and recycling synthetic ammonia desulfurization residue fluid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to extract and recycle synthetic ammonia desulfurization residue fluid that solves scaling and corrosion of heat transfer equipment problem, saves cost, and in eco-friendly manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The sodium bicarbonate is added to centrifuge and solid matter is separated into sodium bicarbonate dryer for heating and decomposing to obtain finished product of sodium carbonate powder. The gas bubble and bicarbonate are sent to carbon dioxide storage tank. The solution is added into mother liquid evaporator for evaporating mother liquor by centrifuging, separated to obtain centrifugal mother liquor solution. The solution is evaporated to obtain mixture containing solid sodium bicarbonate. The sodium sulfate is dried at high temperature to decompos gas in carbon dioxide storagetank, and crystallized. The crystal is filtered to obtain crystal of sodium sulfate. The finished product of sodium sulfate solid powder and filtered mother liquor are added into mother liquor to circularly extracting. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a device for realizing the extracting and recycling synthetic ammonia desulfurization residue fluid, which comprises primary micro-vortex sedimentation groove, where upper section of desulfurization residue fluid is connected with primary micro-vortex sedimentation groove bottom through pipeline, primary micro-vortex sedimentation groove through pipeline is connected with secondary sedimentation groove top, secondary sedimentation groove bottom and middle are connected with plate-type filter through pipeline, outlet of plate-type filter is connected with clean liquid inlet through pipeline, clean liquid outlet through pipeline is connected with clean liquid pump and liquid is divided into two paths by the pipeline through which liquid pump is connected to the bottom of the top and bubbling absorption tower bubbling absorption tower.

GA 201850845V
AB    NOVELTY - Steel/aluminum dissimilar material laser welding process comprises (i) taking steel plates and aluminum alloys, cleaning oil on steel plates and aluminum alloys, and retaining the aluminum oxide layer on the surface of the aluminum alloy, (ii) taking nickel powder and boron powder separately, mixing nickel powder and boron powder evenly to obtain nickel/boron mixed powder, (iii) presetting the nickel/boron mixed powder on the surface of the aluminum alloy uniformly, (iv) placing the steel plate and the aluminum alloy preset with the nickel/boron mixed powder on the steel plate, placing the lap joint of the aluminum alloy on the workbench, and using the welding plate to make the steel plate and the aluminum alloy in close contact, (v) performing laser welding of the steel plate and the aluminum alloy under an atmosphere of an inert gas and carbon dioxide/oxygen mixed gas or a simple air atmosphere to obtain iron-aluminum intermetallic compound layer.
   USE - The method is useful for steel/aluminum dissimilar material laser welding.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves metallurgy in the reaction pool; prevents occurrence of aluminum metallurgy reaction; inhibits precipitation of iron-aluminum intermetallic compound welded joint at the interface; and improves the toughness of the steel.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Steel/aluminum dissimilar material laser welding process comprises (i) taking steel plates and aluminum alloys, cleaning oil on steel plates and aluminum alloys, and retaining the aluminum oxide layer on the surface of the aluminum alloy, (ii) taking nickel powder and boron powder separately, mixing nickel powder and boron powder evenly to obtain nickel/boron mixed powder, (iii) presetting the nickel/boron mixed powder on the surface of the aluminum alloy uniformly, (iv) placing the steel plate and the aluminum alloy preset with the nickel/boron mixed powder on the steel plate, placing the lap joint of the aluminum alloy on the workbench, and using the welding plate to make the steel plate and the aluminum alloy in close contact, (v) performing laser welding of the steel plate and the aluminum alloy under an atmosphere of an inert gas and carbon dioxide/oxygen mixed gas or a simple air atmosphere to obtain iron-aluminum intermetallic compound layer, where the carbon dioxide ratio in the carbon dioxide/oxygen mixed gas is 0-100%, the ratio of oxygen is 0-100%, and the sum of carbon dioxide and oxygen is 100%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (i), the steel plate and the aluminum alloy have a thickness of 0.8-2 mm, and the oil stain on steel plates and aluminum alloys is cleaned by acetone or alcohol. In step (ii), the nickel powder and the boron powder have a particle size of 20 nm to 200 mu m, and the purity is 90-99.99%. In step (ii), the mass fraction of boron powder in the nickel/boron mixed powder is 5-15%, and the nickel powder mass fraction is 95-85%. In step (ii), there are two ways to mix the Ni powder and the B powder, when the particle size of the nickel powder and the boron powder is 20 nm to 1 mu m, ultrasonic or sol-gel mixing is used; and when the particle sizes of the nickel powder and the boron powder are 1-200 mu m, mechanical mixing is employed. In step (iii), the thickness of the nickel/boron mixed powder uniformly pre-set on the surface of the aluminum alloy is 0.03-1 mm. In step (v), the inert gas is argon or nitrogen, the volume between the inert gas in the shielding gas formed by mixing the inert gas with the carbon dioxide/oxygen mixed gas is 80-95%, the volume of the carbon dioxide/oxygen mixed gas is 5-20%, and the welding under the air atmosphere is directly welded in an air environment. In step (v), the gas flow rate during the welding process is 10-15 l/minutes. In step (v), the iron-aluminum intermetallic compound layer has a thickness of 10-20 mu m. In step (v), the tensile strength of the laser welded joint formed after welding is 60-120 MP.
