TI Producing pluripotent stem cell population used            for treating e.g. lifestyle-related disease, by            performing suspension culture of pluripotent stem cells            in liquid medium in perfusion method by controlling            medium perfusion rate per unit time, and passaging            suspension-cultured pluripotent stem cells.
AU SHO K
   YOSHIKAZU K
   KANBAYASHI A
   KAWAI Y
AE KANEKA CORP (KANF-C)
GA 2022C44451
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing a pluripotent stem cell                population involves (a) performing suspension                culture of pluripotent stem cells in a liquid                medium in a perfusion method by controlling the                medium perfusion rate per unit time in the range of                1-100 vol.%, and (b) passaging the                suspension-cultured pluripotent stem cells.
   USE - The method is useful for producing pluripotent                stem cell population, which is used for treating                intractable diseases and lifestyle-related                diseases.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces pluripotent stem cell                population comprising a desired number of cells in                a less time-consuming and cost-effective manner,                precisely controls the medium perfusion rate and/or                the carbon dioxide gas supply rate, synergistically                improves culture efficiency and cell productivity,                prevents death of pluripotent stem cells, achieves                proliferation efficiency and maximum of pluripotent                stem cells, improves the arrival density and                efficiently produces a pluripotent stem cell                population.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method involves                  determining medium perfusion rate per unit time                  based on the culture volume multiplied by the                  ratio of the length of the unit time to 24 hours.                  The method involves controlling suspension                  culture based on culture variables, where the                  control causes the medium perfusion rate per unit                  to be proportional to the culture variables,                  respectively. The method involves controlling by                  increasing the medium perfusion rate per unit                  time based on the increase in the cell density                  increase rate, where the culture variables is the                  cell density increase rate, and the cell density                  increase rate indicates the ratio of the cell                  density to the cell density of the pluripotent                  stem cells at the start of the control. One of                  the culture variables is the pH of the culture                  medium in which the pluripotent stem cells are                  present, and the control changes the medium                  perfusion rate per unit time so as to suppress                  the decrease in the pH. The method involves                  suppressing the decrease in pH by increasing the                  medium perfusion rate per unit time based on the                  decrease in pH. The method involves maintaining                  the pH during the suspension culture to be 6.5 or                  higher. The method involves maintaining lactate                  production rate within the range of 2.5x 10-9-1x                  10-10mmol/cell/hour, where the culture variables                  are the lactate production rate of the                  pluripotent stem cells. The control increases the                  medium perfusion rate for any 6 hours of culture                  after the cell density of the pluripotent stem                  cells reaches 8x 105cells/ml, compared to the                  medium perfusion rate for the previous 6 hours of                  culture. The method involves changing the culture                  additive composition of the liquid medium used                  for perfusion in the perfusion method during the                  culture. The step (a) involves changing the                  amount of carbon dioxide supplied to the liquid                  medium based on one or more indicators by                  changing the carbon dioxide gas concentration in                  the gas supplied to the liquid medium in the                  range of 0-10%. One of the indicators is the pH                  of the culture medium in which the pluripotent                  stem cells are present, and the method involves                  changing the carbon dioxide concentration in the                  gas supplied to the liquid medium so as to                  suppress the decrease in the pH. The 6-hour                  average value of the carbon dioxide concentration                  is reduced to a range of 0-2.5% one day or more                  after the start of the change. The specific                  growth rate of the cells before passage is                  0.2-1.5/day. The seeding density in the                  suspension culture step is 0.01x 105-20x                  105cells/ml. The cell density of the pluripotent                  stem cells before the passage is 1x 106cells/ml                  or more. The method further involves performing                  suspension culture after the subculture step. The                  cell density of the pluripotent stem cells one                  day after the passaging step is 60% or more of                  the seeding density in the passaging step.                  Preferred Components: The culture medium                  containing the pluripotent stem cells at the                  start of the control comprises 10 mM or less                  lactic acid. The liquid medium comprises at least                  one chosen from L-ascorbic acid, insulin,                  transferrin, selenium and sodium bicarbonate. The                  liquid medium comprises fibroblast growth factor                  2 (FGF2) and/or transforming growth factor                  &#946; (TGF-&#946; )-1. The liquid medium                  comprises a Rho kinase (ROCK) inhibitor i.e.                  Y-27632. Preferred Cells: The pluripotent stem                  cell population comprises cell aggregates, and in                  the suspension culture step, the cell aggregates                  are collected. The pluripotent stem cell                  population comprises cell aggregates, and in the                  suspension culture step, perfusion in the                  perfusion method is started after the formation                  of the cell aggregates. The pluripotent stem cell                  population comprises cell clumps, where one of                  the culture variables is cell clump volume                  increase rate, and the cell aggregate volume                  increase rate indicates the volume ratio of the                  cell aggregate to the cell aggregate volume at                  the start of the control. The pluripotent stem                  cell population comprises cell clumps, and the                  carbon dioxide concentration in the gas supplied                  to the liquid medium starts to change before the                  formation of the cell clumps. In the pluripotent                  stem cell population, the ratio of                  octamer-binding transcription factor 4                  (OCT-4)-positive cells is 90% or more, the ratio                  of sex determining region Y (SRY)-box 2                  (SOX2)-positive cells is 90% or more, and the                  ratio of Nanog-positive cells is 90% or more. The                  pluripotent stem cells are embryonic stem (ES)                  cells and/or induced pluripotent stem                  cells.
   ACTIVITY - None given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - Cell therapy. Test details are described but                no results given.
   EXAMPLE - The examples provide details about production                of cell population through passage from suspension                culture that do not support the claims.

TI Production of polyurethane foam used as heat            insulating materials in vehicles, involves mixing and            reacting polyol composition comprising polyol and            catalyst with polyisocyanate composition, followed by            foaming and curing, and supplying blowing agent.
AU USHIMI T
AE SEKISUI CHEM CO LTD (SEKI-C)
GA 2022C4346W
AB    NOVELTY - Production of polyurethane foam involves                mixing and reacting a polyol composition containing                a polyol and a catalyst with a polyisocyanate                composition containing a polyisocyanate, followed                by foaming and curing, and supplying a blowing                agent (A) in a separate system from the polyol                composition and the polyisocyanate composition. PN CN114287472-A
TI Air-conditioning cold storage of fresh-keeping vegetables involves picking and selecting vegetables, pre-cooling, selecting film bag meeting ventilation requirement according to respiration characteristics of the vegetables selected, and filling vegetables into film bag.
AU ZHEN X
AE ZHENJIANG CHANGZHUO TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ZHEN-Non-standard)
GA 202262959L
AB    NOVELTY - Air-conditioning cold storage of fresh-keeping vegetables involves picking and selecting vegetables, pre-cooling, selecting a film bag meeting the ventilation requirement according to the respiration characteristics of the vegetables selected, and filling the vegetables into the film bag, extracting air, filling oxygen (O2) into the film bag and/or carbon dioxide (CO2) carrying out modified atmosphere packaging on the gas, sealing, and refrigerating.
   USE - Method for air-conditioning refrigerated fresh-keeping vegetable for preventing cardiovascular and cerebrovascular diseases.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces the vegetable respiration rate, and maintains the need of basic metabolism, optimizes the thickness of the film bag and the gas proportion in bag. The fresh-keeping period of the vegetable is greatly prolonged. The method does not need to adjust and control the humidity of the refrigerator after packaging, the device requirement is low, saves the device and management labor, and saves the cost.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The vegetables are lettuce, spinach, flowering cabbage, Chinese cabbage, lettuce, broccoli, cauliflower, garlic sprout, celery, pumpkin, balsam pear, towel gourd or green pepper. Preferred Conditions: The pre-cooling is carried out for 12-24 hours in an environment with the temperature of 0-3degrees Celsius and the humidity of 80-95%. The selection of the film bag involves calculating the oxygen consumption V of the selected vegetable using the formula V=Q'xm(ml/hour), calculating the oxygen permeability V' of the film bag in unit time using the equation V'=PxS (p1-p2)/D (ml/hour), and when the oxygen consumption V of the vegetables is equal to the oxygen permeability V 'of the film, namely V is equal to V', keeping the oxygen concentration in the bag balanced, and calculating the thickness of the film with the selected air permeability coefficient. The relation between the parameters is determined using the equation D=PxSx(p1-p2)/Q'xm, so that the film bags with the corresponding thicknesses matched with different vegetables are selected, where p is oxygen permeability of the film is expressed in ml.mum/(hour/m2) under 0.1 MPa pressure, S is the surface area of the film pouch in m2, Q' is the respiration rate of the vegetables and the volume of oxygen consumed by the vegetables per unit mass of time are ml/(kg/hour), d is the film thickness of the film bag is in mum; p1 is the partial pressure of oxygen in the atmosphere, in MPa, p2 is the partial pressure of oxygen in the bag expressed in MPa, m is vegetable mass expressed in kg, v is the unit is ml/hour, and oxygen consumption of certain quality vegetables in unit time is given. The method particularly involves exhausting for 1-9 seconds by using air conditioning packaging machine. The O2 concentration is 2-10 vol.% and CO2 concentration is 0-5 vol.%. The refrigeration temperature of the lettuce, spinach, flowering cabbage, Chinese cabbage, lettuce, broccoli, cauliflower, garlic sprouts and celery is 2-4degrees Celsius. The refrigeration temperature of the pumpkin, balsam pear, gourd and round green pepper is 9-11degrees Celsius. The picking involves fresh vegetables are picked at a time of the day, when the temperature is low, such as in the early morning without dew or in the evening.
   ACTIVITY - Cardiovascular-Gen; Cerebroprotective
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).); Q75 (Refrigeration, liquefaction (F25))

   CHEN Z

AB    NOVELTY - Preparing building aggregate by using life                rubbish, involves carrying out high-temperature                heating treatment on the slag burned by life                rubbish to obtain a first solid material. The temperature                of the second solid material and the humidity is                settled, and carbonized and maintain the carbon                dioxide concentration atmosphere to carbonize and                maintain to obtain the building aggregate.
   USE - Method for preparing building aggregate by                using domestic rubbish incinerator slag for solid                waste resource utilization and waste water                treatment of a building i.e. house.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can efficiently recycle and reuse                the domestic rubbish stirring station concrete                waste water and greenhouse gas CO2, further using                the physical and chemical properties of the waste,                preparing new high-quality building aggregate by                modifying treatment, reducing the risk of                generating durability defect and structural                defect.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The high temperature                  heating and low temperature tempering treatment                  to the domestic rubbish incineration slag                  comprises obtaining the first solid material                  comprising placing the domestic rubbish in the                  heating device raising the temperature to 680-750                  degrees Celsius at a rate of 10-30 degrees                  Celsius/minute and keeping the temperature for                  1-2 hour and then taking out the life rubbish and                  quickly cooling to normal temperature in the                  concrete waste water of the stirring station                  cooling to normal temperature of the domestic                  rubbish incineration slag at a rate of 10-30                  degrees C min heating to 180-220 degrees C low                  temperature tempering and keeping the temperature                  for 1-2hours and then putting the life rubbish                  incinerator slag in the stirring station concrete                  waste water rapidly cooling to normal temperature                  and drying to obtain the first solid material.                  The heating device is a muffle furnace. The                  solid-to-liquid ratio of the first solid material                  to the concrete waste water of the stirring                  station is 1.5-1.10.

The dipping                  ratio of phosphoric acid to paper is                  0.1:1-5:1.Preferred Method: Dipping is done for 10-24                  hours. PN CN113429563-A
TI Preparing polyether ether ketone comprises e.g. adding difluoromonomer into solvent, stirring, uniformly mixing diphenol monomer, anhydrous potassium carbonate and anhydrous sodium carbonate, adding into difluoro solution, heating, obtaining reaction product and purifying.
AU HE D
   QIAN X
   ZHOU R
   XIAO B
   ZOU L
   YOU Q
AE UNIV JIANGHAN (UYJJ-C)
GA 2021B5104V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing polyether ether ketone comprises (i) adding difluoromonomer into the solvent under an inert gas, continuously stirring at 25-60degrees Celsius to fully dissolve the difluoromonomer in the solvent, obtaining a difluoro solution, and synthesizing polyether ether ketone with 1,3-bis(2-methoxy-2-oxoethyl)imidazole chloride as a solvent; (ii) uniformly mixing diphenol monomer, anhydrous potassium carbonate and anhydrous sodium carbonate under inert gas, adding into the difluoro solution to make the diphenol monomer, dissolving anhydrous potassium carbonate and anhydrous sodium carbonate in the difluoro solution, obtaining the mixed solution, heating to 140-200degrees Celsius, terminating the reaction when no carbon dioxide gas is generated, obtaining the reaction product, purifying the reaction product to obtain the polyether ether ketone with a particle size distribution of 30-70 mum.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing polyether ether ketone.
   ADVANTAGE - The method ensures the product with smooth surface, spherical and high powder fluidity and meets the requirement of selective laser sintering technology.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The difluoro monomer is 4,4'-difluorobenzophenone, 3,3'-difluorobenzophenone, 2,3-difluorobenzophenone, 2,5-difluorobenzaldehyde, 2,6-difluorobenzaldehyde, bis(4-fluorophenyl) ether, 4,4'-difluorodiphenylmethane, 2,5-difluoroanisole, 2,5-difluorobenzophenone, 3,5-difluorobenzonitrile, methyl 2,5-difluorobenzoate, ethyl 2,4-difluorobenzoate, or 4,4'-difluorobenz piperazine. The diphenol monomer is 1,4-benzenediol, bisphenol A, 1,5-naphthalenediol, 2,6-naphthalenediol, tert-butylhydroquinone, 2-methoxyhydroquinone, trimethylhydroquinone, tetramethylhydroquinone, 2,5-dioctylhydroquinone, 2-teroctylhydroquinone, 2,5-diisooctylhydroquinone, 2,5-diphenylhydroquinone or 2,5-di-tert-amylhydroquinone.Preferred Method: The step (ii) comprises uniformly mixing diphenol monomer, anhydrous potassium carbonate and anhydrous sodium carbonate, dividing into the difluoro solution for 3-5 times with interval two adjacent addition of 20-40 minutes, and continuously stirring after each addition, heating to 120-140degrees Celsius at a rate of 5-20degrees Celsius/minute, heat preserving for 1-3 hours, completely dissolving diphenol monomer, the anhydrous potassium carbonate and the anhydrous sodium carbonate in difluoro solution to obtain a mixed solution, and heating to 140-200degrees Celsius at a rate of 2-10degrees Celsius/minute. The purification includes adding ethanol into the reaction product, filtering for the first time, obtaining the filter residue of the first filtration, washing the filter residue of the first filtration with ethanol and acetone for 1-2 times respectively, filtering, obtaining the re-filtered filter residue, and washing the re-filtered filter residue with deionized water twice, vacuum drying the washed product at 120degrees Celsius to obtain the polyether ether ketone in powder form.

The mass ratio between                  Y-type zeolite and alcohol solvent is 1-20:100,                  preferably 2-18:100. In the step (ii), the                  auxiliary agent is sodium dodecyl sulfate or                  cetyltrimethylammonium bromide. The mass ratio                  between solid phase material, auxiliary agent,                  and deionized water is 1-20:0.1-0.2:100,                  preferably 2-18:0.2-1.8:100. Preferred Method: In                  the step (i), the stirring operation is carried                  out in the presence of ultrasonic waves with                  ultrasonic wave frequency of 15KHz-10 MHz and                  power of 20-100 W/l for 0.5-10 hours, preferably                  1-8 hours. In the step (i) and step (ii), the                  low-temperature heat treatment is carried out at                  100-140 degrees C for 5-15 hours. In the step                  (ii), the mixing is carried out under stirring                  conditions, preferably under ultrasonic waves                  with ultrasonic wave frequency of 15-10 MHz and                  power of 20-100 W/l based on the volume of the                  solution for 0.5-10 hours, preferably 1-8 hours.                  In the step (iii), the flow rate of the carbon                  dioxide gas is 100-1000 ml/minute, preferably                  150-900 ml/minute and the high-temperature heat                  treatment is carried out at 200-400 degrees C for                  2-7 hours, preferably at 250-350 degrees C for                  3-6 hours.

AB    NOVELTY - A Mxene spinel ferrite heterojunction photoelectrode comprises a top layer, a middle layer and a bottom layer. The bottom layer is transparent conducting substrate which is used a support for the two layers as photoelectrodes.
   USE - Mxene spinel ferrite heterojunction photoelectrode used for photoelectrocatalytic cells for solar hydrogen production, and photodegradation of dyes and contaminants in water, photocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction, and photocatalytic self-cleaning surface (all claimed).

The Mxene spinel ferrite heterojunction photoelectrode greatly enhances the photocatalysis process without affecting the stability of the photocatalyst. The nanocomposite material has excellent optical and electrical properties.

PN CN113060741-A; CN113060741-B
TI Comprehensive utilization of boron and iron            tailings from boron-magnesium-iron ore, comprises e.g.            water leaching of magnetic separation tailings,            filtering, evaporating and concentrating leaching            solution to crystallize.
AU RAO M
   ZHANG X
   LI G
   JIANG T
   YOU J
   LUO J
   PENG Z
   XIAO T
   DUAN J
AE UNIV CENT SOUTH (UYCS-C)
GA 202180477E
AB    NOVELTY - Comprehensive utilization of boron and iron                tailings from boron-magnesium-iron ore, comprises                subjecting magnetic separation tailings to high                temperature and high pressure water leaching,                filtering product after water leaching to obtain                the leaching solution and leaching residue;                introducing carbon dioxide gas into leaching                solution, evaporating and concentrating the                leaching solution to crystallize step by step to                precipitate borax pentahydrate and sodium carbonate                crystal; forming and sintering the leaching residue                to prepare magnesia refractory material; performing                high-temperature high-pressure water immersion in                closed environment, where temperature of the water                immersion is greater than or equal to 150 degrees                C.
   USE - The method is useful for comprehensive                utilization of boron and iron tailings from                boron-magnesium-iron ore.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple; has high production                efficiency, and high product added value; and                realizes zero discharge of solid waste and                high-efficiency recovery and utilization of boron                and sodium, provides technical support for the                efficient and value-added utilization of                boron-magnesium-iron ore resource.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the method,                  high-temperature water immersion temperature is                  150-220 degrees C, preferably 150-180 degrees C,                  the solution -solid mass ratio is 6:1-8:1, and                  the leaching time is 1-6 hours. In the method,                  introducing carbon dioxide gas into the leaching                  solution to convert the boron in the solution                  into borax, and remaining sodium into sodium                  carbonate; heating the aqueous solution to 90-100                  degrees C to evaporate part of the water for                  concentration, cooling to 50-70 degrees C,                  crystallizing and precipitating some sodium                  carbonate, continuously cooling to 25-35 degrees                  C to precipitate the remaining sodium carbonate                  crystals, cooling filtered solution to 10-20                  degrees C to crystallize borax crystals, and                  returning small amount of boron and sodium to                  mother liquor for leaching. In the method, after                  sample preparation of aqueous leaching residue,                  preparing refractory materials by briquetting and                  sintering; sintering temperature is 1200-1350                  degrees C, sintering time is 2-3 hours, sintering                  atmosphere is air atmosphere. In the method,                  leaching rates of boron and sodium in the                  magnetic separation tailings are both greater                  than 95%. Preferred Components: The mass of                  diboron trioxide in the magnetic separation                  tailings is 1.2-1.8 wt.%, and magnetic separation                  tailings contain sodium magnesium silicate. In                  the magnetic separation tailings, the mass of                  -0.074 mm particles accounts for more than 90%.                  The obtained refractory material has                  refractoriness of 1600-1680 degrees C and                  apparent porosity of less than 2.2%.

TI Preparing polyurethane catalyst with stretch-resistant function for clothing, involves adding isooctanoic acid with sodium hydroxide solution to reactor to achieve saponification reaction, adding solvent oil, and stirring.
AU JIANG P
AE NANTONG NORSE ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (NANT-Non-standard)
GA 202165264S
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing polyurethane catalyst with stretch-resistant function for clothing involves preparing polyurethane catalyst which includes 25-30 wt.% isooctanoic acid, 16-20 wt.% solvent oil, 15-20 wt.% sodium hydroxide, and 15-20 wt.% carbon nanotubes; adding certain amount of isooctanoic acid and sodium hydroxide solution to reactor to achieve saponification reaction, then adding solvent oil to continue stirring, and obtaining zinc isooctanoate product after standing for stratification and distillation and dehydration; adding carbon nanotubes into zinc isooctanoate for mixing and stirring to obtain polyurethane catalyst.
   USE - Method for preparing polyurethane catalyst with stretch-resistant function for clothing.
   ADVANTAGE - The method for preparing polyurethane catalyst with stretch-resistant function for clothing is environment-friendly; promotes cross-linking of aliphatic isocyanates during production of leather garments, reduces curing time, improves tensile resistance of cured polyurethane material, reduces carbon dioxide emissions, and dibutyltin is not used, which is harmless to human body, avoids environmental pollution, which is beneficial to protect surrounding environment and adapt to sustainable development.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The isooctanoic acid is 2-ethylhexanoic acid, mineral oil is mineral oil, and sodium hydroxide is sodium hydroxide solution. The zinc isooctanoate is light yellow uniform transparent liquid, where ratio of isooctanoic acid to sodium hydroxide to mineral spirits is 1:3:2, and sodium hydroxide is added in excess. Preferred Conditions: The diameter of carbon nanotube is 2-3 mu m, and surface of carbon nanotube is distributed with burrs, and inside of carbon nanotube is hollow. The ratio of zinc isooctanoate to carbon nanotubes is 3:1-2, and temperature during mixing is room temperature, that is, 27-33 degrees C. The rotation speed of stirring and mixing chamber of zinc isooctanoate and carbon nanotubes is 100-500 revolutions per minute, and stirring duration is 1-2 hours. Preferred Method: The method for preparing polyurethane catalyst with stretch-resistant function for clothing involves stirring isooctanoic acid and sodium hydroxide, and mixing, where speed is 200-300 revolutions per minute and maintained for 3-4 hours, and temperature during stirring is 120-150 degrees C; adding mineral spirits, where rotation speed is 200-300 revolutions per minute for 1-3 hours, and temperature during stirring is 100 degrees C; sealing obtained polyurethane catalyst in can and stored in cool and dry environment.

PN CN112875711-A; CN112875711-B
TI Preparing gradient density hydrophobic silica aerogel to the fields of heat preservation, heat insulation, energy-saving and environment-friendly military and civil use, involves adding ethyl orthosilicate, functional modifier to ethanol.
AU REN H
   WANG L
   ZHU J
   BI Y
   LIN Z
   WANG S
AE UNIV SOUTHWEST SCI & TECHNOLOGY (USWS-C)
GA 202164302Q
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing gradient density hydrophobic silica aerogel involves adding 3-8 pts. wt. ethyl orthosilicate, 1-5 pts. wt. functional modifier to 15-40 pts. wt. ethanol and 3-8 pts. wt. deionized water, and stirring for 18-28 hours to obtain a mixed solution, adding 1-2.5 pts. wt. condensation catalyst to the mixed solution, stirring for 0.5-1 minutes, then casting in a mold and centrifuge, and leaving to stand to obtain a wet gel, leaving the wet gel to stand and age at room temperature for 24 hours, exchanging the aged wet gel with fresh acetone for ethanol, and exchanging three times for 24 hours each time. The wet gel after the exchange is supercritically dried with carbon dioxide to obtain product.
   USE - Method for preparing gradient density hydrophobic silica aerogel used to the fields of heat preservation, heat insulation, energy-saving and environment-friendly military and civil use.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has good moisture-proof performance.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The functional modifier is perfluorooctyltrichlorosilane. The condensation catalyst is ammonia water, where the concentration of the ammonia water is 0.5-1.5 mole/L. The functional modifier is perfluorooctyltrichlorosilane. Preferred Conditions: The rotation speed of the centrifugation is 4000-8000 r/second and the time of the centrifugation is 4-12 minutes. The standing time is 25-45 minutes. The reaction is carried out in an autoclave and includes adding 15-40 pts. wt. ethanol, 3-8 pts. wt. deionized water, 3-8 pts. wt. ethyl orthosilicate and 1-5 pts. wt. functional modifier into the autoclave, sealing the autoclave and vacuumizing, filling with carbon dioxide gas, stirring for 10-15 hours, and releasing the pressure. The pressure in the autoclave is 10-16 megaPascal, the temperature is 35-38 degrees C, and the pressure relief rate is 0.4-0.5 megaPascal/minutes, the stirring speed is 100-120 r/minutes. The UV irradiation is performed while stirring for 0.5-1 minutes. The ultraviolet radiation has an irradiation wavelength of 200-400 nanometer and an irradiation intensity of 20-80 mW/cm2. The obtained gradient density hydrophobic silica aerogel is reprocessed, and the process includes placing the gradient-density hydrophobic silica aerogel in a low-temperature plasma treatment apparatus for 5-8 minutes, and then using a high-pressure mercury lamp to irradiate the gradient-density hydrophobic silica aerogel under nitrogen for 20-30 minutes. The atmosphere of the low-temperature plasma treatment instrument is carbon tetrafluoride or carbon tetrachloride. The frequency of the low-temperature plasma treatment instrument is 40-80KHz, the power is 50-100W, and the pressure of argon is 40-90 Pascal when irradiated with a high-pressure mercury lamp, the intensity of light is 15-30 mW cm2, and the irradiation time is 30-40 minutes.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).)
MC E11-W; E12-C02; E31-P01
IP C01B-033/155; C01B-033/157; C01B-033/158

PN US10995027-B1
TI De-scaling of saline stream used for treating feed            stream, involves mixing saline stream with aluminum            source and releasing mixture in flashing stage to be            contacted with heat source, and forming precipitate            comprising calcium carbonate.
AU BADER M S
AE BADER M S (BADE-Individual)
GA 202143400G
AB    NOVELTY - De-scaling of saline stream involves (a)                mixing the saline stream with an aluminum source                and releasing the mixture in a flashing stage (S1)                to be contacted with a heat source to vent carbon                dioxide, forming a precipitate (a) comprising                calcium carbonate, calcium aluminate, or calcium                silicoaluminate, by forming pre-heated brine (i),                and (b) releasing the pre-heated brine (i) in a                flashing stage (S2) to be contacted with an organic                hydroxide source forming a precipitate (b)                comprising magnesium hydroxide, magnesium silicate                and/or magnesium silicoaluminate, recovering the                organic hydroxide source, and producing de-scaled                pre-heated brine. The saline stream comprises                calcium, bicarbonate, magnesium, and/or                silica.
   USE - De-scaling of saline stream used for treating                feed stream.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables de-scaling of saline stream                allows for more energy balance and efficient                thermal utilization without the need to fill a hot                wet emulsion separation system with more steam and                heat rejection devices to waste steam.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The saline stream is                  derived from a wet emulsion stream at ambient                  temperature. The heat source comprises blowdown                  steam, return condensate, letdown steam, and/or                  steam from a heat rejection device. The method                  further involves mixing the saline stream with                  aluminum source and releasing the mixture in                  first flashing stage (S1) to be contacted with                  the heat source and a portion of organic                  hydroxide source in the flashing stage (S1) to                  form a precipitate (a) comprising calcium                  carboaluminate, recovering the portion of the                  organic hydroxide source, and producing the                  pre-heated brine (i), de-oiling the saline stream                  by a hydrophobic membrane to de-scaling, using at                  least a portion of the de-scaled pre-heated brine                  in oil recovery systems, and distilling the                  de-scaled pre-heated brine by a recycle-brine                  multi-effect (RB-ME) train that operates at a                  temperature range, vapor arising from brine                  partial evaporation in a preceding higher                  temperature effect is passed into the boiling                  tubes of a subsequent lower temperature effect,                  where in condensing releases the latent heat to                  boil the brine in the subsequent effect, with the                  exception of vapor arising from the last effect                  which is condensed in a backward feed section                  (BFS) heat rejection condenser, by vapor                  condensating from each effect, spraying the BFS                  heated reject brine onto the boiling tubes of                  effect (e1) of the forward feed section (FFS),                  vapor arising from brine partial evaporation in a                  preceding higher temperature effect is passed                  into the boiling tubes of a subsequent lower                  temperature effect, where in condensing releases                  the latent heat to boil the brine in the                  subsequent effect, with the exception of vapor                  arising from the last effect where is condensed                  in a FFS heat rejection condenser, by vapor                  condensating from each effect after the effect                  (e1) in FFS passes out to the distillate line                  common to ME train, and mixing at least a portion                  of the reject brine with the de-scaled pre-heated                  brine to form a recycle brine stream to entering                  the last effect of BFS. The de-scaled brine is                  generated by mixing a saline stream with an                  aluminum source and releasing the mixture in a                  flashing stage (S1), recovering the portion (p1)                  of organic hydroxide source, and producing brine                  (i), releasing the brine (i) in a flashing stage                  (S2) to be contacted with a portion (P2) of                  organic hydroxide source, forming a recovered                  portion (p1) of organic hydroxide source from the                  flashing stage (S1) to at least maintain the pH                  in flashing stage ((S2) higher than the pH in the                  flashing stage (S1), to form a precipitate (b)                  comprising magnesium hydroxide, magnesium                  silicates, or magnesium silicoaluminate, and                  recovering organic hydroxide source, and                  producing de-scaled brine.Preferred Components: The aluminum source is                  selected from aluminum hydroxide, sodium                  aluminate, and/or aluminum chloride.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic hydroxide                  source is selected from isopropylamine,                  propylamine, dipropylamine, diisopropylamine,                  ethylamine, diethylamine, methylamine,                  dimethylamine, and/or ammonia.

PT P
PN WO2021073917-A1
TI Producing ammonia synthesis gas by steam reforming a hydrocarbon feed followed by oxygen reforming, and shifting the synthesis gas in a high temperature shift step over a high temperature shift catalyst.
AU DAHL P J
AE HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
GA 2021416933
AB    NOVELTY - An ammonia synthesis gas is produced by steam reforming a hydrocarbon feed followed by oxygen reforming to obtain a synthesis gas comprising methane (CH4), carbon monoxide (CO), carbon dioxide (CO2), hydrogen gas (H2) and water (H2O), and shifting the synthesis gas in a high temperature (HT) shift step over a promoted zinc-aluminum oxide based high temperature shift catalyst, where the steam/carbon ratio in the reforming and HT shift step is less than or equal to 2.6.
   USE - Production of ammonia synthesis gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables a process scheme utilizing proven reforming technology operating with low steam/carbon, enables operation of high temperature (HT) shift downstream the reforming section at the same low steam/carbon ratio as the reforming section, provides a process scheme without methanation section to remove residual carbon components in the make up synthesis gas for the ammonia synthesis, and provides an overall process layout enabling maximum single line capacity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Parameters: The temperature in high temperature shift step is 300-600 degrees C, preferably 345-550 degrees C. The space velocity in the oxygen reforming is less than or equal to 20000 Nm3 C/m3/hour, preferably less than or equal to 7000 Nm3 C/m3/hour. Preferred Components: The promoted zinc-aluminum oxide based HT shift catalyst comprises, in its active form, a zinc (Zn)/aluminum (Al) molar ratio of 0.5-1 and a content of alkali metal is 0.4-8 wt.% and a copper content is 0-10 wt.% based on the weight of oxidized catalyst. The steam/carbon ratio in the reforming step is 2.6-0.8, 2.4-1 or 2-1.2, preferably 1.8 or 1.5. Preferred Process: Reforming is carried out by tubular steam reforming followed by autothermal reforming with an oxygen source, where the oxygen source contains greater than or equal to 90%, preferably greater than or equal to 98% oxygen. Process further comprises a prereforming step. The high temperature shift step is greater than or equal to 1 high temperature shift steps in series, preferably with possibility for cooling and/or steam addition in between. Process further comprises greater than or equal to 1 additional shift step downstream the high temperature shift step. The greater than or equal to 1 additional shift steps are greater than or equal to 1 medium temperature shift steps and/or greater than or equal to 1 low temperature shift steps. The steam is optionally added to the synthesis gas before greater than or equal to 1 additional shift steps downstream the high temperature shift step. The synthesis gas leaving the greater than or equal to 1 additional shift step downstream the high temperature shift step is optionally washed with water to reduce the methanol content. Method further comprises a CO2 removal step of removing CO2 from the synthesis gas down to a level of less than or equal to 400 vppm CO2, preferably less than or equal to 20 vppm. Method further comprises a nitrogen gas (N2)-wash step. Process further comprises a pressure swing adsorption (PSA). The ammonia process loop is an inert free loop.
DC E35; J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

TI Preparation of carbon/silicon/calcium-magnesium composite material used for improving soil and removing heavy metals in polluted water, involves mixing dolomite and rice husk, grinding to obtain mixed powder, pyrolyzing and cooling.
AU ZHOU C
   LIU J
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG TECHNOLOGY (UYZT-C)
GA 202145516N
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of carbon/silicon/calcium-magnesium composite material, involves (1) mixing dolomite and rice husk, grinding to 40-100 mesh to obtain a mixed powder, the mass ratio of the dolomite and rice husk is 1:1-5, (2) pyrolyzing the mixed powder obtained in step (1) at 300-500 degrees C for 1-5 hours, and then cooling to room temperature.
   USE - Preparation of carbon/silicon/calcium-magnesium composite material used for improving soil and removing heavy metals in polluted water (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The addition of dolomite increases the porosity of the bed layer in the rice husk carbonization process, reduces the bed pressure drop, makes the carbonized product carbon dioxide easier to escape, and thus the rice husk carbonization process is more complete. The method for preparing the carbon/silicon/calcium-magnesium composite material has simple steps, cheap and easy-to-obtain raw materials, and the process is convenient for promotion and application.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The dolomite is a mixture of magnesite and calcite. The rice husk is corn stalk, coconut husk, or wheat stalk.

AU PEI J

AE UNIV TIANJIN (UTIJ-C)
GA 202137101B
AB    NOVELTY - Removal of formaldehyde comprises using constant temperature heating of positive temperature coefficient ceramic heater and transition metal oxide catalyst as a packed bed, realizing catalytic decomposition of formaldehyde in the air at room temperature, and realizing thermal catalytic oxidation decomposition to generate carbon dioxide and water. The method is done by using positive temperature coefficient ceramic heater which is provided with main body, transition metal oxide catalysts, thermal resistance, lead wire, temperature controller, heating switch, temperature sensing resistor, and temperature controller.
   USE - The method is used for removal of formaldehyde.

It not only can realize the catalytic decomposition reaction under the room temperature condition, but also can realize the thermal catalytic oxidation function. It has simple operation, stable heating temperature, and is safe and reliable. It can customize the size according to the use occasion.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the removal of formaldehyde.

PN CN112470930-A
TI Inducing Caragana in vitro plantlet to take root by subculturing selected Caragana rootless test-tube plantlet on Murashige and Skoog medium, and culturing subcultured Caragana rootless test-tube plantlet.
AU KANG H
   ZHU R
   LI M
   QIN P
AE INST BIOLOGY GANSU ACAD SCI (BIOL-Non-standard)
GA 202130858R
AB    NOVELTY - Method for inducing Caragana in vitro plantlets to take root involves (a) selecting Caragana rootless test tube seedlings with good growth and 2-3 stem nodes, (b) subculturing selected Caragana rootless test-tube plantlets on Murashige and Skoog (MS) medium supplemented with sugar 30 g.l-1 for 30 days, and (c) putting subcultured Caragana rootless test-tube plantlets on one half -MS medium comprising 6 g.l-1 agar, sealing with air-permeable sealing film, and culturing at a temperature of 25 degrees C/20 degrees C, a relative humidity of 80%, an intensity of 3300-10000 Lx, a rate of 8-14 hour/day, and carbon dioxide concentration of 800-3000 mu mol.mol-1 for 28 days.
   USE - The method is useful for inducing Caragana in vitro plantlets to take root.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is easy-to-operate, low in cultivation cost, ensures strong photosynthetic capacity, and high transplanting survival rate, and can provide high-quality Caragana seedlings for environmental protection and restoration.

Preferred Method: In the                  step (i), controlling the reaction temperature at                  -5 degrees C to 5 degrees C. In the step (ii),                  controlling the reaction temperature at -10                  degrees C to 10 degrees C. In the step (iv),                  controlling the reaction temperature at 20                  degrees C to -40 degrees C.

PN CN112225214-A
TI Preparing aza mesoporous carbon by copolymerization comprises e.g. taking phenol as main carbon source, and urea as nitrogen source, reacting with formaldehyde compounds, carbonizing and activating gas.
AU LI L
   LIU B
   LI J
   WANG X
   LI Z
   XU Z
AE TIANJIN VOCATIONAL INST (TJVI-C)
GA 202109164X
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing aza mesoporous carbon by copolymerization comprises taking phenol as main carbon source, and urea as nitrogen source, reacting with formaldehyde compounds to form block copolymer phenolic urea-formaldehyde resin, which is further carbonizing and activating gas to obtain nitrogen hybrid mesoporous carbon, where nitrogen content is 5-10 wt.%, and its carbon content is 75-85%, pore diameter is 4-6 nm, mesoporosity is 70-80%, and specific surface area is 1500-2500 m2/g, formaldehyde compound is formaldehyde solution, formaldehyde gas, paraformaldehyde, hexaformaldehyde tetramine or methanol formal; gas activation is air, water vapor, carbon dioxide or mixed gas activation; technical solutions adopted include phenolic oligomer synthesis, urea-formaldehyde oligomer synthesis, synthesis of block phenolic urea-formaldehyde copolymer, block phenolic urea-formaldehyde copolymer hardening and curing, and carbonization activation of block phenolic urea-formaldehyde copolymer.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing aza mesoporous carbon by copolymerization.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has low production cost and utilizes wide sources of raw materials and improves copolymer carbonization activation yield.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The synthesis method of block phenolic urea-formaldehyde copolymer comprises mixing aqueous phenolic oligomer solution and the aqueous urea-formaldehyde oligomer solution in the reactor, controlling the molar ratio of phenolic unit molecules to urea-formaldehyde unit molecules in the raw materials to be 1:1.5-2, placing reactor in a 60 degrees C water bath and heating it for 0.5-2 hours to obtain white precipitate of insoluble phenolic urea-formaldehyde copolymer. The hardening and curing method of block phenolic urea-formaldehyde copolymer comprises adding formaldehyde compound crosslinking agent into the reaction liquid, controlling the molar ratio of formaldehyde to phenolic monomer to 0-0.2:1, increasing temperature of the reaction solution to 80-100 degrees C, and cross-linking phenol-formaldehyde urea-formaldehyde copolymer and thermally curing to obtain non-meltable network phenol-formaldehyde urea-formaldehyde copolymer at 100 degrees C, filtering reticulated phenol-formaldehyde urea-formaldehyde copolymer, drying and curing in an oven at 150 degrees C for 0.5-2 hours, transforming reticulated phenolic urea-formaldehyde copolymer into a bulk phenolic urea-formaldehyde copolymer that does not melt at 500 degrees C. The carbonization activation method of the block phenol-formaldehyde urea-formaldehyde copolymer comprises placing the cured phenol-formaldehyde urea-formaldehyde copolymer in a high temperature furnace protected by activated gas, carrying out carbonization treatment at 550-600 degrees C for 2-4 hours, increasing temperature to 800-850 degrees C, and continuously activating carbonized material for 2-6 hours, where activated gas expands the micropores into mesopores to obtain high specific surface area nitrogen hybrid mesoporous carbon.

GA 2020A7036F
AB    NOVELTY - A lithium-ion secondary battery comprises a                cathode including a cathode current collector and a                cathode active material layer which is positioned                on the cathode current collector, a nonaqueous                electrolyte, and an anode. The cathode active                material layer includes a lithium transition metal                oxide particles. The lithium transition metal oxide                particles contain 60 mol% or more nickel with                respect to total of 100 mol% of transition metal                atoms. The nonaqueous electrolyte contains a                polyethylene glycol-based polymer.
   USE - Lithium-ion secondary battery used for mobile                devices, electric vehicle and hybrid electric                vehicle.
   ADVANTAGE - The lithium-ion secondary battery has                excellent durability by preventing swelling,                explosion, and deterioration in durability                characteristics caused by gas generated in the                battery in high temperature environment, stability                by preventing deterioration of stability of the                battery due to a swelling phenomenon, energy                density and capacity retention rate by decreasing                the amount of carbon dioxide generated after the                high temperature storage, during charging and                discharging.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ELECTRONICS - Preferred Properties: The cathode has                  electron density of 3.3-4.2 g/cc, preferably                  3.5-3.8 g/cc. After the lithium-ion secondary                  battery is charged in a constant current (CC)                  mode up to 4.2 V with a current of 0.3 C-rate at                  2 degrees C, it is shifted to a constant voltage                  (CV) mode, and completely charged under a cut-off                  condition of a current amount of 1/20 C, and                  stored at 60 degrees C for one week, then the                  amount of carbon dioxide generated is 50% or less                  of a total amount of gas generated in the                  battery, and then the amount of carbon dioxide                  generated in the battery is decreased by 75% or                  more of the amount of carbon dioxide generated                  when the polyethylene glycol-based polymer is not                  contained in the nonaqueous electrolyte.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The lithium transition                  metal oxide particles comprise preferably 88 mol%                  or more nickel with respect to total of 100 mol%                  of transition metal atoms. In the surface of the                  lithium transition metal oxide particles, a crack                  is present, in which the polyethylene                  glycol-based polymer is present in the crack. The                  cathode active material layer includes a lithium                  transition metal oxide particles comprising                  lithium-nickel-cobalt-manganese-metal oxide of                  formula: LiaNi1-x-yCoxMnyMzOb.a = 0.5-1.3;b = 1.9-2.1;x,y = 0-0.4;x+y = 0-0.4;M = Al, Mg, Zr, or B;andz = 0-0.2.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The polyethylene                  glycol-based polymer comprises a compound of                  formula (I). The polyethylene glycol-based                  polymer contains polyethylene glycol (PEG),                  and/or polyethylene glycol dimethyl ether                  (PEGDME), preferably PEGDME. The nonaqueous                  electrolyte contains 0.1-10 wt.% polyethylene                  glycol-based polymer with respect to 100 wt.% of                  the nonaqueous electrolyte.n = 5-100;andR1,R2 = H or 1-4C linear or branched                  alkyl.Preferred Property: The polyethylene                  glycol-based polymer has number average molecular                  weight of 50-2000 g/mol.

PN CN111802443-A
TI Preserving whole Larimichthys crocea by soaking cleaned fish body in prepared complex coating liquid, drying coated fish body, filling mixed gas into vacuum package with fish body, and sealing it, and freezing and storing it.
AU PANG J
   CHEN Y
   WU Z
   LIN L
   CHEN S
   ZHENG S
AE UNIV FUJIAN AGRIC & FORESTRY (UYFN-C)
GA 2020A6229E
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preserving whole Larimichthys crocea by coating and air conditioning, which involves (a) preparing complex coating liquid by taking water and konjak fine powder, stirring with magnetic stirrer, adding cinnamaldehyde and essential oil during stirring process, continuously stirring and adding glycerol, and continuously stirring and mixing uniformly, and putting in vacuum encapsulating machine to remove air bubbles to obtain complex coating liquid, (b) soaking cleaned fish body (i.e. whole fish body) in complex coating liquid for soaking and coating, drying coated fish body in ventilation room at 4 degrees C, (c) putting fish body after drying, respectively packing into each packaging bag, and vacuuming it, (d) using gas mixer to prepare mixed gas of carbon dioxide, oxygen and dinitrogen, filling it into vacuum package with fish body, and sealing it, and (e) freezing and storing the L.crocea.
   USE - The method is useful for preserving whole L.crocea.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: In the step (a), the weight ratio of water to konjak fine powder is 100:1. The step (a) involves stirring with magnetic stirrer at 45-55 degrees C and 500-700 revolution per minute (rpm) for 10-20 minutes. The step (a) involves adding 2 mu l/ml concentration of cinnamaldehyde and essential oil, and 0.2-0.6% cinnamaldehyde and essential oil based on weight of water and stirring for 30-40 minutes, adding 3-5 drops glycerin, and stirring continuously. The step (a) involves removing bubbles in vacuum encapsulating machine in 10-30 minutes. The step (b) involves soaking cleaned fish body in complex coating liquid at a ratio of 1:2-1:4, soaking the mixture for 10-30 minutes, drying coated fish body in ventilation room at 4 degrees C under shade for 30-60 minutes. In the step (d), the volume ratio of the gas carbon dioxide, oxygen, and dinitrogen is 6-7:1-1.5:1.5-2.5. The step (e) involves storing the L.crocea at 3-5 degrees C.

GA 202098340T
AB    NOVELTY - Pre-treating cyclohexane-decomposing                photocatalyst in oil field station, comprises                taking anatase titanium dioxide crystals and rutile                crystals mixed phase, first time calcining at                410-420 degrees C for 1-2 hours, and placing them                in a desiccator and cooling naturally, subjecting                the calcined titanium dioxide to first magnetic                stirring treatment in sulfuric acid, microwave                treating the titanium dioxide with sulfuric acid                under power of 640-660 W and radiation frequency of                2300-2500 Hz for 3-5 minutes, magnetically stirring                the titanium dioxide treated with microwave                radiation in sulfuric acid for second time, taking                out, then second time calcining at 300-310 degrees                C for 0.5-2 hours, and drying.
   USE - The method is useful for pre-treating                cyclohexane-decomposing photocatalyst in oil field                station.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes photocatalytic mechanism                to treat pre-treated titanium dioxide catalyst that                decomposes cyclohexane; improves photocatalyst                activity and cyclohexane degradation rate; prolongs                use time of the photocatalyst; and generates stable                final products of carbon dioxide and water.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The first time calcining                  temperature is 415 degrees C. The second time                  calcining temperature is 300 degrees C. Preferred                  Components: The average particle size of basic                  particles of titanium dioxide in the mixed phase                  of anatase crystals and rutile crystals is 18-25                  nm. The weight ratio between anatase crystals and                  rutile crystals is 71:29. In the first magnetic                  stirring, the concentration of sulfuric acid is 1                  mol/l, and the solid-liquid ratio between                  titanium dioxide and sulfuric acid is 1:1. In the                  second magnetic stirring, the concentration of                  sulfuric acid is 0.5 mol/l, and the solid-liquid                  ratio between titanium dioxide and sulfuric acid                  is 1:1.

TI Diesel engine for ships, controls combustion            chamber inside to form ammonia pre-mixed gas to which            ammonia concentration of inner side wall surface of            combustion chamber becomes deep rather than center            portion of combustion chamber.

   KUWAHARA T
   TAKAKI K
   KUWABARA T
AE JFE ENG CORP (JFES-C)
   JFE ENG CORP (JFES-C)
   JFE ENG CORP (JFES-C)
GA 202090884T
AB    NOVELTY - The engine has a fuel injection unit that                injects the fuel oil which becomes an ignition                source at combustion chamber inside and gaseous                ammonia to combustion chamber. The ammonia                pre-mixed gas is formed by combustion chamber                inside by the fuel injection unit, and a premixed                combustion is carried out. A control unit is                provided to control the combustion chamber inside                to form the ammonia pre-mixed gas to which the                ammonia concentration of the inner side wall                surface of the combustion chamber becomes deep                rather than center portion of the combustion                chamber.
   USE - Diesel engine for ships. Can also be used in                diesel engine which uses distillate as fuel, and                diesel and petrol combined engine.
   ADVANTAGE - The diesel engine which improves the                combustion rate of ammonia by comparatively easy                structure, and reduces carbon dioxide is                provided.

PN CN111621030-A; CN111621030-B
TI Preparing multi-stage porous metal-organic            framework material for drug loading and sustained            release, involves forming ionic liquid microemulsion,            weighing emulsifier and ionic liquid in reaction flask            in turn, mixing and stirring.
AU ZHAN G
   ZENG X
   ZHOU S
   SU R
   SHI X
   CHENG Y
   XIANG H
   LIN B
AE UNIV HUAQIAO (UYHQ-C)
GA 2020877286
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing multi-stage porous metal-organic                framework material involves forming ionic liquid                microemulsion, weighing emulsifier and ionic liquid                in reaction flask in turn, mixing and stirring for                10-20 minutes, adding alcohol solvent, stirring for                20-30 minutes to obtain ionic liquid microemulsion.                The certain amount of metal salt and organic ligand                are poured into above-mentioned ionic liquid                microemulsion in sequence, stirred and transferred                to the autoclave, poured in carbon dioxide, reacted                for 1-5 hours, after reaction is over. The washing,                and drying is performed. The metal organic material                HP-Cu-BTC is obtained after drying.
   USE - Method for preparing multi-stage porous                metal-organic framework material for drug loading                and sustained release.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare multi-stage                porous metal-organic framework material that has                simple operation, good repeatability, short                time-consuming, and green process route,                hierarchical pore structure, large specific surface                area, high drug loading, and ensures long-term slow                release of drugs.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The emulsifier is                  fatty alcohol polyoxyethylene ether-5, fatty                  alcohol polyoxyethylene ether-7, fatty alcohol                  polyoxyethylene ether-9, polyethylene glycol                  octyl phenyl ether, nonylphenol polyoxyethylene                  ether-10 and sorbitol fatty acid ester. The ionic                  liquid is 1-ethyl-3-methylimidazole                  tetrafluoroborate, 1-butyl-3-methylimidazole                  tetrafluoroborate, 1-butyl-3-methylimidazole                  hexafluoroborate, 1-butyl-3-methylimidazole                  hexafluorophosphate and 1-butyl-3-methylimidazole                  tetrafluorophosphate. The alcohol solvent is                  mixture of one or more of methanol, ethanol,                  ethylene glycol, propanol, propylene glycol and                  glycerol. The metal salt is one of anhydrous                  copper sulfate, copper sulfate pentahydrate,                  copper acetate anhydrous, copper acetate                  monohydrate, copper nitrate anhydrous and copper                  nitrate hexahydrate. The organic ligand is                  trimellitic acid. Preferred Conditions: The                  volume ratio of the ionic liquid and alcohol                  solvent is 1:0.2-5. The molar ratio of metal salt                  to organic ligand is 1:0.5-2.

PN WO2020172111-A1; CN113454019-A; EP3927656-A1; KR2021128468-A; US2022136117-A1; JP2022520657-W
TI Recovering hydrogen by reacting hydrocarbon, separating carbon compound, directing carbon compound to cathode and water to anode side of electrochemical cell, electrolyzing water, applying voltage, and reacting protons with carbon compound.
AU MOLTER T M
   ROY R
   ROBERT
AE SKYRE INC (SKYR-Non-standard)
   SKYRE INC (SKYR-Non-standard)
   SKYRE INC (SKYR-Non-standard)
GA 202085134B
AB    NOVELTY - Method for recovering hydrogen, involves (a) reacting a hydrocarbon to form a carbon compound and hydrogen in the presence of a catalyst, where the carbon compound comprises at least one of carbon dioxide or carbon monoxide, (b) separating the carbon compound from the hydrogen, (c) directing the carbon compound to a cathode side of an electrochemical cell and directing water to an anode side of the electrochemical cell, (d) electrolyzing the water on the anode side to form oxygen and protons, (e) applying a voltage to a membrane and electrode assembly in the electrochemical cell to cause the protons to traverse through a proton exchange membrane from an anode to a cathode on the cathode side, and (f) reacting the protons with the carbon compound to form an organic product.
   USE - The method is used for recovering hydrogen.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the efficiency of refining operation, and increases the hydrogen production rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a hydrogen recovery system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method involves directing an off-gas stream from a refinery comprising a carbon compound and hydrogen to an anode side of an electrochemical hydrogen separator, applying a voltage to a separation unit membrane and electrode assembly in the electrochemical hydrogen separator to cause the hydrogen at a separation unit anode to disassociate into protons and electrons and directing the protons from the separation unit anode through a separation unit proton exchange membrane to a separation unit cathode, where the protons recombine with the electrons at the separation unit cathode to form hydrogen, removing the hydrogen from a separation unit cathode side of the electrochemical hydrogen separator, and removing a separated carbon stream from the separation unit anode side. The step (a) comprises: at least one of steam reforming, partial oxidation, carbon dioxide reforming, or auto-thermal reforming; directing the hydrocarbon and water to a steam reformer and reacting the hydrocarbon with the water to form a reformate comprising the carbon compound and the hydrogen; and directing the hydrocarbon, water, and carbon dioxide to an autothermal reformer and reacting the hydrocarbon, water, and carbon dioxide to form a reformate comprising the carbon monoxide and the hydrogen. The step (b) comprises pressure swing adsorption. The step (b) comprises directing a reformate stream comprising the carbon compound and the hydrogen to an anode side of an electrochemical hydrogen separator, applying a voltage to a separation unit membrane and electrode assembly in the electrochemical hydrogen separator to cause the hydrogen at a separation unit anode to disassociate into protons and electrons and directing the protons from the separation unit anode through a separation unit proton exchange membrane to a separation unit cathode, where the protons recombine with the electrons at the separation unit cathode to form hydrogen, removing the hydrogen from a separation unit cathode side of the electrochemical hydrogen separator, and removing a separated carbon stream from the separation unit anode side. The step (e) comprises applying the voltage through a renewable energy source. The method comprises recovering water from the separation unit cathode side of the electrochemical hydrogen separator and is used in the reacting and/or is directed to the electrochemical cell.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The hydrocarbon comprises at least one of methane, ethane, ethylene, propane, propylene, butane, butadiene, cyclohexane, benzene, or toluene. The organic product comprises at least one of methane, carboxylic acid, an alcohol, formaldehyde, or carbon monoxide.

TI Intra-abdominal cavity environment monitoring device, has pneumoperitoneum machine for transmitting monitored data to display device, and air inlet channel for extracting redundant carbon dioxide in abdominal cavity through exhaust channel.
AU LI H
   WANG C
   WANG W
   LI S
AE WUHAN BOSHIMAN MEDICAL TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (WUHA-Non-standard)
GA 202083826X
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model relates to medical consumable technology field, specifically to a abdominal cavity environment monitoring device, for monitoring abdominal cavity environment in laparoscopic surgery, comprising a display device, further comprising a puncture outfit and pneumoperitoneum machine; the puncture outfit is provided with a sensing part; the puncture outfit is inserted into the patient body, using the pneumoperitoneum machine to fill the CO2 from the air inlet passage into the patient body to establish a pneumoperitoneum; monitoring the internal environment of the abdominal cavity by the sensing part on the puncture outfit, and transmitting the monitored data to the pneumoperitoneum machine; the pneumoperitoneum machine synchronously transmits the data to the display device for display, The user can charge CO2 into the abdominal cavity according to the monitored data, or extract the redundant CO2 in the abdominal cavity, so as to ensure the air pressure in the abdominal cavity; the temperature and humidity are kept balance, so as to improve the good surgical working environment.

PT P
PN CN111154097-A; CN111154097-B
TI Preparing polybutyrolactam comprises e.g. adding            butyrolactam monomer and basic catalyst into reaction            vessel under protection of protective gas, and carrying            vacuum distillation to remove small molecule            product.
AU ZHAO L
   CHEN T
   WANG L
   WANG C
AE UNIV EAST CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYEC-C)
   HI-TECH FIBER GROUP CORP (HITE-Non-standard)
GA 202044224R
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing polybutyrolactam comprises (1)                adding the butyrolactam monomer and basic catalyst                into the reaction vessel under the protection of                protective gas, carrying vacuum distillation to                remove small molecule product, to obtain                butyrolactam salt activation solution; (2) taking a                part of butyrolactam salt activation solution, and                passing high purity carbon dioxide into the                butyrolactam salt activation solution to obtain                butyrolactam carboxylate activation solution; and                (3) adding the dispersion medium, dispersant, ion                stabilizer and remaining butyrolactam salt                activation solution to the prepared butyrolactam                carboxylate activation solution under the                protection of protective gas, stirring and reacting                at 40-80 degrees C under 100-1000                revolutions/minute for 12-120 hours, adding an                extractant to the mixed solution after the reaction                to extract the dispersion medium, carrying                solid-liquid separation on mixed liquid to obtain                crude product, washing and drying the crude                product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing                polybutyrolactam.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has high yield. The                polybutyrolactam: has large molecular weight and                good thermal stability.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The basic catalyst is                  potassium hydroxide and/or potassium                  tert-butoxide; the dosage of the basic catalyst                  is 1-10 mol.% of butyrolactam monomer. The volume                  ratio of the butyrolactam salt activation                  solution used in step (2) and the butyrolactam                  salt activation solution used in step (3) is 1:                  0.5-5. In the step (2), the flux of the                  high-purity carbon dioxide is 1-10 mol.% of the                  butyrolactam monomer. In the step (2), in the                  step (3), the dispersion medium is aprotic                  reagent, the amount of the dispersion medium                  added is 1-10 times the volume of the                  butyrolactam monomer; the aprotic agent is                  saturated aliphatic hydrocarbons and/or saturated                  alicyclic hydrocarbon, preferably paraffin oil,                  normal-heptane, normal-hexane, cyclohexane and/or                  dimethylsilane. The dispersant is higher fatty                  acid salt, the dosage is 1-20 wt.% of the                  butyrolactam monomer; preferably the higher fatty                  acid salt is sodium dodecyl sulfate, sodium                  dodecyl sulfonate, sodium dodecylbenzene                  sulfonate and/or sodium cetyl sulfate. The ion                  stabilizer is crown ether ion stabilizer and/or                  quaternary ammonium salt ion stabilizer. The                  crown ether ion stabilizer is N-crown-M-ether,                  where N is 12-30, M is 4-10, the dosage is 40-100                  mol.% of basic catalyst. The quaternary ammonium                  salt ion stabilizer is tetramethylammonium                  chloride and/or tetraethylammonium chloride; the                  dosage is 1-10 mol.% of butyrolactam monomer.                  Preferred Method: In the method, the temperature                  of the vacuum distillation is 50-120 degrees C,                  and the time is 1-3 hours.
DC A23 (Polyamides; polyesters. (including polycarbonates, polyesteramides); alkyds; other unsaturated polymers.)
MC A02-A07; A05-F03; A08-A; A08-S01; A10-D03

PT P
PN CN111117215-A
TI Thermoplastic elastomer foam material for sports            shoes, comprises modified polyamide elastomer            comprising at least two polyamide elastomers,            antioxidant and nucleating agent.
AU WANG X
   LI R
   YU B
   LIN Y
   XU J
AE LINING CHINA SPORTS GOODS CO LTD (LINI-Non-standard)
GA 202041380Y
AB    NOVELTY - A thermoplastic elastomer foam material                comprises 100 pts. wt. modified polyamide                elastomer, 0.1-0.5 pt. wt. antioxidant and 0-10                pts. wt. nucleating agent. The modified polyamide                elastomer comprises at least two polyamide                elastomers.
   USE - Thermoplastic elastomer foam material for                sports shoes.
   ADVANTAGE - The foam shoe material has low density,                excellent resilience and deformation resistance,                and provides lightweight sports shoes with                excellent rebound.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                preparation of the thermoplastic elastomer foam                material, which involves drying polyamide elastomer                in oven, stirring with antioxidant and nucleating                agent in high-speed mixer, extruding in twin-screw                extruder, drying to obtain polyether block amide                (PEBA) material, placing polyether block amide                material in high-pressure reactor, passing nitrogen                or carbon dioxide gas, heating to 100-140 degrees C                under pressure of 15-60 MPa for 0.5-4 hours to                reach supercritical state, quickly releasing                pressure to normal pressure to obtain foamed beads,                molding foamed beads by steam compression to obtain                product, drying product at 50-60 degrees C for 1-5                hours and cooling.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The nucleating agent is                  one or more of talc, calcium carbonate,                  montmorillonite and white carbon black.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The antioxidant is one                  or more of antioxidant 1010                  (pentaerythritol-tetrakis-( beta                  )-(3,5-di-tert-butyl-4-hydroxyphenyl)-propionate),                  antioxidant 1098                  (N,N'-(hexane-1,6-diyl)bis(3-(3,5-di-tert-butyl-4-hydroxyphenyl)propanamide)),                  antioxidant 1076 (n-octadecyl- beta                  -(4-hydroxy-3,5-di-tert-butyl-phenyl)propionate)                  and antioxidant 168                  (tris(2,4-di-t-butylphenyl)phosphite).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Component: The polyamide elastomer                  is a long carbon chain polyamide elastomer. The                  long carbon chain polyamide elastomer is a block                  copolymer in which soft segments and hard                  segments are alternately present. The soft                  segments of block polymer are one or more of                  polytetramethylene ether, polyethylene oxide,                  polytetrahydrofuran, polyethylene glycol,                  polypropylene glycol, copolymer of polyether                  diol, polyether diamine, and                  polysiloxane-polyolefin. The hard segment of                  block copolymer is polyamide-11 and polyamide-12.                  The copolymer comprises one or more of Pebax 2533                  (RTM: polyether block amide), Pebax 3533 (RTM:                  polyether block amide), Pebax 4033 (RTM:                  polyether block amide), Pebax 5533 (RTM:                  polyether block amide), Pebax MV1074 (RTM:                  polyether block amide), XPA 9040 (thermoplastic                  polyamide elastomer), XPA 9048 (thermoplastic                  polyamide elastomer) and XPA 9055 (thermoplastic                  polyamide elastomer). Preferred Property: The                  long carbon chain polyamide elastomer has shore                  hardness of 20-60 D and hard segment                  crystallinity of 0.2-0.5.
DC A35 (Other processing and general - including vulcanisation, welding of plastics and adhesive processes. Testing.); A28 (Condensation polymers in general.); A83 (Clothing, footwear.); A86 (Fancy goods, games, sports, toys.); ; E11 (Containing P and/or Si.); P22 (Footwear (A43).)

PN CN111089763-A
TI Method for preparing critical exhaust catalyst of automobile e.g. car for on-board diagnostics (OBD) verification test, involves determining critical time and temperature according to specific surface area of automobile exhaust catalyst.
AU LI Z
   GAO J
   REN X
   SHAO Y
   WU H
   LI K
AE CHINA AUTOMOTIVE TECHNOLOGY & RES CENT (CAUM-C)
GA 2020387750
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves putting the automobile exhaust catalyst in the simulated automobile exhaust atmosphere for critical treatment. The treatment temperature is gradually increased from the initial temperature to the critical temperature, and the treatment time is gradually extended from zero to the critical time after reaching the critical temperature. The natural cooling of the automobile exhaust catalyst is carried out after the critical treatment. The critical time and the critical temperature are determined according to the relationship between the specific surface area of the automobile exhaust catalyst before and after the critical treatment. The simulative motor vehicle exhaust atmosphere comprises carbon monoxide (CO), propane (C3H8), propene (C3H6), nitric oxide (NO), carbon dioxide (CO2), hydrogen (H2), oxygen (O2), and water (H2O).
   USE - Method for preparing critical exhaust catalyst of automobile e.g. car for OBD verification test.
   ADVANTAGE - The preparation time of preparation method of the critical automobile exhaust catalyst for the OBD verification test is shortened. The determination and accuracy of OBD verification test is improved.

PN US2020101821-A1; US11400797-B2
TI Method for preventing exhaust gas intrusion in            passenger compartment of vehicle involves detecting            vehicle operating condition when detected heating            ventilation and air conditioning setting is            recirculating air mode.
AU PIKE C
   VANWORMER R
   LOPEZ Y
