TI Method for bitumen froth treatment tailings hydrocarbon recovery, involves introducing water and gas into flotation column through sparging, and removing overflow and underflow from flotation column.
AU ESMAEILI P
   YAZDI A Z
   BAZIUK O
   MIRMONTAZERI L
   ADEYINKA O B
AE IMPERIAL OIL RESOURCES LTD (IMOI-C)
GA 202024322H
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves providing tailings from a bitumen froth treatment using solvent. The tailings are introduced into a flotation column (104). The shearing device is used to break asphaltenes flocculant in the tailings. The water and gas are introduced into the flotation column through sparging. The overflow and underflow are removed from the flotation column. The gas comprises air, nitrogen, carbon dioxide, and methane. The advanced materials comprise carbon fiber, and graphene oxide. The collector additive comprises light hydrocarbons. The tailings comprise water, 0.5-7 wt.% hydrocarbon, and 5-20 wt.% solids. The tailings comprise water, 10-20 wt.% hydrocarbon, and 10-20 wt.% solids. The tailings comprise water, 0-0.5 wt.% hydrocarbon, and 1-5 wt.% solids.
   USE - Method for bitumen froth treatment tailings hydrocarbon recovery.
   ADVANTAGE - The recovery of valuable hydrocarbons from the flotation column is improved.

PN WO2020038985-A1; FR3085212-A1; FR3085212-B1; EP3841407-A1; US2021318459-A1
TI Method for detecting fluid e.g. hydrogen, in environment, involves performing measurement of fluid by sensor, calculating time consistency of samples by sensor, and determining presence of fluid by utilizing calculated time consistency.
AU VOISIN C
AE CENT NAT RECH SCI (CNRS-C)
   CENT NAT RECH SCI (CNRS-C)
   CENT NAT RECH SCI (CNRS-C)
   CENT NAT RECH SCI (CNRS-C)
GA 202017597J
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves performing measurement of fluid by a sensor (12) for a wave i.e. mechanical wave, propagating in an environment (11) to obtain measured signal. Cutting of the measured signal is performed at cutting time intervals of predefined duration for obtaining samples of the measured signal. Time consistency of the samples is calculated by the sensor. Presence of the fluid is determined by utilizing the calculated time consistency. Filtering and equalizing of the signal are performed in a predefined frequency range.
   USE - Method for detecting fluid e.g. liquid or gas, in an environment. Uses include but are not limited to petroleum, water, natural gases, carbon dioxide, hydrogen or methane.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables calculating time consistency of the samples by the sensor, so that precise detection of the fluid can be ensured.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a fluid detection system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of a fluid detection system.
   Environment (11)
   Sensor (12)
   Controller (14)
   Link (16)
   Frame (22)

PT P
PN CN110746750-A; CN110746750-B
TI Preparation of glass film involves mixing modified metal oxide solid particles prepared using e.g. salicylate compound and polyethylene terephthalate resin, coupling agent, additive and polyethylene terephthalate, molding, and drying.
AU ZHAO J
AE NINGBO TAIYIDE FILTRATION TECHNOLOGY CO (NING-Non-standard)
   HUNAN QUANXIANGHU INTELLIGENT TECHNOLOGY (HUNA-Non-standard)
GA 202011691A
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of glass film involves dispersing salicylate compound and polyethylene terephthalate resin in an aqueous ethanol solution, stirring, obtaining coating liquid, adding metal oxide solid particles in fluidized state to a fluidized bed reactor, closing inlet and outlet valves of the fluidized bed reactor, passing into supercritical carbon dioxide fluid entrained with the coating liquid, stirring in supercritical state for 1-8 minutes to reduce the outlet pressure, coating surface of metal oxide solid particles with salicylate compound and polyethylene terephthalate resin, filtering, washing, drying, obtaining modified metal oxide solid particles, mixing modified metal oxide solid particles, coupling agent, additive and polyethylene terephthalate, obtaining casting film solution, injecting the casting film solution into a mold, molding, placing in a drying box at 40-60 degrees C for 6-12 hours, cooling, and peeling off glass film.
   USE - Preparation of glass film.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of glass film having excellent thermal-insulating property, UV resistance and durability.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The metal oxide solid particles are titanium dioxide, zinc oxide, aluminum oxide, ferric oxide and/or lead oxide. Preferred Property: The particle size of the modified metal oxide solid particles is 50-500 nm.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The salicylate compound is subjected to ionic liquidization treatment by adding methylimidazole and 2,4,6-tribromophenylsalicylate in a molar ratio of 1-3: 1 to reactor containing toluene solvent, refluxing at 60-100 degrees C for 24 hours, cooling to room temperature, obtaining waxy solid, removing solvent, and reacting with silver hexafluorophosphate in acetone to obtain salicylate imidazole hexafluorophosphate ionic liquid. Preferred Composition: The salicylate compound is 2,3-dihydroxypropyl salicylate, 2-hydroxypropyl salicylate, 3-hydroxypropyl salicylate, 3-hydroxybutyl salicylate and/or 2,4,6-tribromophenylsalicylate. The coupling agent is KH-540 (RTM: 3-aminopropyltrimethoxysilane), KH-570 (RTM: gamma -methacryloyloxypropyltrimethoxysilane), JH-V171 (RTM: vinyltrimethoxysilane) and/or JH-M902 (RTM: 3-aminopropylmethyldiethoxysilane). The molar ratio of silver hexafluorophosphate and waxy solid is 1-3:1.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of salicylate compound, polyethylene terephthalate resin and aqueous ethanol solution is 5-25:1-8:100. The concentration of aqueous solution of ethanol is 90 wt.%. The mass ratio of modified metal oxide solid particles, coupling agent, additive and polyethylene terephthalate is 5-25:0.5-2.5:1-8:100. The additive contains polyethylene glycol, sodium silicate, aerogel carbon black and/or sodium salt of lauryl triethylene glycol ether sulfate in a ratio of 10-15:3-8:0.5-3.5:8-27.

PT P
PN CN110731898-A; CN110731898-B
TI Preparing moxa wax for moxibustion by pulverizing dried folium artemisiae argyi, extracting folium artemisiae argyi powder, heating extract, adding palmitic acid and xanthan gum, performing molecular distillation, collecting moxa oil.
AU WANG Y
   LIAO X
   WANG X
AE HENAN AIJIUTANG HEALTH CONSULTING CO LTD (HENA-Non-standard)
   GUANGZHOU JINAN BIOMEDICAL RES & DEV CEN (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 202012047H
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing moxa wax for moxibustion involves (a) pulverizing 5-10 kg dried folium artemisiae argyi to obtain folium artemisiae argyi powder, adding folium artemisiae argyi powder to supercritical extraction device, passing carbon dioxide into device, switching on compressor, extracting folium artemisiae argyi powder, and separating the extract from the separator, (b) allowing to stand the folium artemisiae argyi extract in a closed container for 0.5-2 hours, heating at constant temperature, adding palmitic acid, stirring for 20-60 minutes, adding xanthan gum and stirring to obtain wax, and (c) performing molecular distillation by heating the moxa wax at 50-70 degrees C when vacuum reaches 0.6-0.8 kPa, distilling wax, collecting the moxa essential oil using condenser and removing the residual liquid.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing moxa wax for moxibustion.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is safe and environmentally-friendly, and provides high yield of moxa wax.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Method: The step (a) involves extracting at 30-50 degrees C for 60-100 minutes, introducing carbon dioxide at a flow rate of 10-30 kg/hour. In step (a) the palmitic acid and folium artemisiae argyi extract are used in a weight ratio of 0.5-2:20. In step (b) the xanthan gum and folium artemisiae argyi extract are used in a weight ratio of 0.3-1:20. In step (b) heating is carried out at 60-80 degrees C. The molecular distillation is performed in scraped film molecular still. The step distillation is carried out at 100-120 degrees C, and the scraper has speed of 200-300 revolutions per minute (rpm). The method further involves placing 0.5-1 kg moxa wax in a container, heating for 5-7 minutes until it slowly melts, adding melted moxa wax into a mold pan, cooling to 45-55 degrees C, and applying cooled moxa wax on the affected area for performing moxa wax moxibustion treatment.

Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); P33 (Medical aids, oral administration (A61G, H, J).)

TI Production of aromatic nitrile compound used for            producing carbonate ester, involves dehydrating            aromatic amide compound by contacting aromatic amide            compound with catalyst in gas phase.
AU HIDEFUMI H
   TAKEHIKO I
   KEISUKE S
   RYOTARO U
   HARADA H
   ISOBE T
   SHIMOKAWA K
   UMEZU R
   SIMOKAVA K
AE MITSUBISHI GAS CHEM CO INC (MITN-C)
   MITSUBISHI GAS CHEM CO INC (MITN-C)
   MITSUBISHI GAS CHEM CO INC (MITN-C)
   MITSUBISHI GAS CHEM CO INC (MITN-C)
GA 2020095518
AB    NOVELTY - Production of an aromatic nitrile compound                involves dehydrating an aromatic amide compound by                contacting the aromatic amide compound with a                catalyst in a gas phase.
   USE - Production of aromatic nitrile compound used                for producing carbonate ester (claimed) used as                gasoline additive, and diesel fuel additive.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple and efficient                manufacture of carbonate ester with high yield,                excellent reaction rate and reaction time, and                suppresses the generation of by-products.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                production of carbonate ester, which involves                reacting alcohol and carbon dioxide in the presence                of the aromatic nitrile compound to produce a                carbonic ester and water, hydrating the generated                water to the aromatic nitrile compound to form the                aromatic amide compound, separating and dehydrating                the aromatic amide compound, and regenerating the                aromatic nitrile compound by the dehydration, and                hydrating at least a portion of the nitrile                compound.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a manufacturing apparatus                of carbonate ester.
   Buffer tank (1)
   Carbonate ester reactor (2)
   Recovery tower (3)
   Dehydrating agent separation column                (4)
   Carbonate ester recovery tower (5)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst comprises                  an alkali metal. The inert gas comprises at least                  nitrogen gas.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The contacting process                  further involves contacting an inert gas and/or a                  solvent in a vaporized state with the catalyst.                  The production of carbonate ester involves using                  a catalyst containing cerium oxide. Preferred                  Components: The aromatic amide compound comprises                  at least a heteroaryl amide compound, and the                  aromatic nitrile compound comprises at least a                  heteroaryl nitrile compound. The heteroaryl amide                  compound comprises 2-picolinamide, and the                  heteroaryl nitrile compound comprises                  2-cyanopyridine. The solvent is compatible with                  the aromatic amide compound. The solvent                  comprises a pyridine compound and/or a ketone                  compound. The alcohol comprises 1-6C alcohol.                  Preferred Property: The solvent has boiling point                  of 20-300 degrees C at normal pressure. Preferred                  Condition: In the contacting step, the aromatic                  amide compound is contacted with the catalyst at                  170-300 degrees C for 0.001-10 seconds.

PN CN110698988-A
TI Handheld quick bonding adhesive comprises component comprising alpha-ethyl cyanoacrylate and amine curing agent, and another component containing emulsified epoxy resin, castor oil, modifier, thickener and wetting agent.
AU DAI S
   MIAO J
   LIU F
AE CHANGZHOU WANQUAN NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOG (CHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202008400K
AB    NOVELTY - A handheld quick bonding adhesive comprises A component and B component at a weight ratio of 1:0.10-0.15. The B component comprises 30 wt.% alpha -ethyl cyanoacrylate and 70 wt.% amine curing agent. The A component contains 50-70 wt.% emulsified epoxy resin, 15-30 wt.% castor oil, 5-10 wt.% modifier, 3-5 wt.% thickener, and 5-15 wt.% wetting agent.
   USE - A handheld quick bonding adhesive.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation method of the quick bonding adhesive comprising emulsifying epoxy resin by low energy emulsification, subjecting castor oil to carbon dioxide supercritical fluid extraction, removing moisture in the castor oil, mixing the emulsified epoxy resin, castor oil, modifier, thickening agent, wetting agent, stirring and mixing to prepare A component; mixing alpha -cyanoethyl acrylate and amine curing agent under absolute vacuum condition to prepare B component; and mixing the A component and B component under anhydrous conditions at low temperature to prepare the adhesive.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The epoxy resin is 4,4'-dihydroxydiphenylsulfone diglycidyl ether epoxy resin, phloroglucinol triglycidyl ether epoxy resin, or p-aminophenol epoxy resin. The modifying agent is polysorbate-80 or isotridecyl polyoxyethylene ether compounds. The thickening agent is polymer water-soluble thickener, and/or acrylate emulsion. The wetting agent is propylene glycol, and/or glycerol. The particle diameter of the castor oil is 1-2 mm. The amine curing agent is aliphatic amine curing agent, polyetheramine curing agent, polyamide curing agent, or aromatic amine curing agent.

PN CN110451506-A
TI Preparation of graded holes activated coke with            needle-like mesoporous passage used in flue gas            desulfurization, by dispersing acicular nano magnesium            hydroxide and coal powder, heating, activating            insulation, cooling, and acid soaking.
AU ZHU Y
   LIU T
   GAO T
   LI H
   LIU H
   LIU C
AE UNIV TIANJIN CHENGJIAN (UTCJ-C)
GA 201998030J
AB    NOVELTY - A graded holes activated coke with needle-like                mesoporous passage is prepared by uniformly                dispersing acicular nano magnesium hydroxide and                coal powder, heating in inert protective gas                atmosphere from 20-25 degrees C to 800-900 degrees                C at rate of 5-10 degrees C/minute and inert                protective gas flow per 10 g of mixture at 350-450                mL/minute, changing atmosphere to mixed atmosphere                of carbon dioxide and inert protective gas,                activating insulation for 30-90 minutes, changing                atmosphere to inert protective gas, naturally                cooling with furnace to 20-25 degrees C, soaking by                acid, and removing magnesium oxide. The mass of                magnesium element provided by magnesium hydroxide                is 5-15% of the mass of coal powder. The coal                powder is low rank coal.
   USE - The method is used for preparing graded holes                activated coke with needle-like mesoporous passage                in flue gas desulfurization.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves activated coke adsorption                rate and sulfur capacity, and increases recycling                time.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The diameter of nano                  magnesium hydroxide is 10-30 nm. The inert                  protective gas is nitrogen, helium gas or argon                  gas.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The particle size                  distribution of coal powder is 100-200 mu m,                  preferably 100-160 mu m. The coal powder is                  lignite or sub-bituminous coal. The coke has                  micropore pore volume of not less than 0.4 cm3/g,                  average micropore pore volume of 0.4-0.5 cm3/g                  and micropore pore size of 1-2 nm, and mesopore                  pore volume of not less than 0.5 cm3/g, average                  pore volume of 0.5-0.6 cm3/g and mesopore pore                  size of 10-30 nm. Preferred Method: The method                  involves dispersing magnesium hydroxide and coal                  powder in deionized water (to form mixture),                  drying at 110-125 degrees C and drying to 80-85                  degrees C, or ball milling dispersing magnesium                  hydroxide and coal powder for 60-120 minutes at                  speed of 1000-2000 rad/minute, preferably 80-100                  minutes at speed of 1500-1800 rad/min. Soaking is                  done by using 1-2 mol/L hydrochloric acid for 1-5                  hours, and washing product with deionized water                  until there is no chlorine ion detected.

PN WO2019213393-A1; EP3788004-A1; US2021171352-A1
TI Aerogel for supercapacitor for use in a device comprises graphene oxide in which surface of graphene oxide is coated in at least one area with a conducting composite polymer.
AU RAM M K
AE UNIV SOUTH FLORIDA (UYSF-C)
   UNIV SOUTH FLORIDA (UYSF-C)
   UNIV SOUTH FLORIDA (UYSF-C)
GA 201993765G
AB    NOVELTY - An aerogel comprises graphene oxide, where surface of graphene oxide is coated in at least one area with a conducting composite polymer.
   USE - An aerogel for supercapacitor for use in a device (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method for forming graphene oxide-PVA-conducting composite polymer aerogels from coal does not release carbon dioxide into the atmosphere (as it does not require high temperatures). Graphene oxide from coal can be used to form graphene, both of which can be used with water-soluble compounds to form aerogels. The formation of graphene oxide, graphene, and their aerogels is very clean compared to other methods and, as such, has less potential impact on the environment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) supercapacitor comprising the aerogel;
   (2) preparation of aerogel which involves (a) reacting an amount of coal with acid, (b) reacting product from step (a) with a base, (c) reacting product from step (b) with a high concentration of an acid, and (e) washing product from step (c) with water; and
   (3) method which involves applying voltage to the supercapacitor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Dichalcogenide is selected from molybdenum sulfide (MoS2), molybdenium selenide (MoSe2), molybdenum telluride (MoTe2), tungsten sulfide (WS2), tungsten selenide (WSe2), and/or tungsten telluride (WTe2). Graphene oxide is derived from coal. Acid in step (a) is nitric acid. Base in step (b) is sodium hydroxide. Acid in step (c) is hydrochloric acid. Product of step (d) is graphene oxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: Conducting composite polymer comprises a polymer and a dichalcogenide. Polymer is selected from polyaniline, polypyrrole, polythiophene, polyaniline-polypyrrole copolymers, polyaniline-polythiophene copolymers, poly(ortho-anisidine), and poly(methyl aniline), poly(o-ethoxyaniline), and/or their permissible copolymers. Supercapacitor further comprises poly(vinyl alcohol) (PVA) gel electrolyte selected from PVA-gel, PVA-polypyrrole, PVA-polyaniline, PVA-polythiophene, PVA-polyethylenedioxythiophene, PVA-graphene-polypyrrole, PVA-graphene-polyaniline, PVA-graphene-polythiophene, PVA-graphene-polyethylenedioxythiophene, PVA-MoS2-polypyrrole, PVA-MoS2-polyaniline, and/or PVA-MoS2-polythiophene. Product of (e) is an aerogel. Preferred Processes: The method further includes (e) reacting the product of step (d) with poly(vinyl alcohol). The method further includes (f) coating product of step (e) in greater than or equal to 1 area with a conducting composite polymer.

PN CN110407706-A
TI Homogeneous catalyst system useful for preparing fatty tertiary amine comprises copper stearate and nickel stearate.
AU LI Y
   WANG Z
   LI J
AE CHINA DAILY CHEM CO LTD RES INST (CHDA-Non-standard)
GA 201995179G
AB    NOVELTY - Homogeneous catalyst system comprises copper stearate and nickel stearate according to the weight ratio of 3-6:1.
   USE - The homogeneous catalyst system is useful for preparing fatty tertiary amine (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst: has high catalytic activity, high yield of tertiary amine and less by-products; reduce energy consumption and reduce emissions of wastewater, salt, nitrogen oxides, carbon dioxide and solid waste.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for use of the homogeneous catalyst system for preparing fatty tertiary amine by fatty alcohol catalytic amination reaction comprising using C8-C18 single long-chain fatty alcohol as a raw material, adding the fatty alcohol to the reaction, adding the catalyst, and reducing the hydrogen under normal pressure with a hydrogen flow rate of 0.8-1 l/minutes at 160-180 degrees C for 30-60 minutes, carrying out amination reaction at 210-230 degrees C for 2-3 hours under normal pressure, and the flow rates of the introduced dimethylamine and hydrogen are 0.6-1 l/minutes and 0.2-0.5 l/minutes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The homogeneous catalyst system comprises calcium stearate, barium stearate, magnesium stearate and/or zinc stearate. The mass ratio of each component is copper stearate, nickel stearate, stearate M1, and stearate M2 according to the weight ratio of 3-6:1:0-1:0-1. The stearate M1 and stearate M2 respectively refer to any one of four kinds of stearate in the auxiliary component. The stearate is a commercially available chemically pure reagent which is used directly in the amination to prepare fatty tertiary amine without treatment. Preferred Method: The use method comprises using C8-C18 single long-chain fatty alcohol as a raw material, adding the fatty alcohol to the reaction, adding the catalyst, and reducing the hydrogen under normal pressure with a hydrogen flow rate of 0.9-1 l/minutes at 170-180 degrees C for 30-40 minutes, carrying out amination reaction at 210-220 degrees C for 2.5-3 hours under normal pressure, and the flow rates of the introduced dimethylamine and hydrogen are 0.8-1 l/minutes and 0.3-0.4 l/minutes. The amount of the catalyst is calculated by using a copper element in an amount of from 400-900 parts per million (ppm) of the fatty alcohol.

TI Preparation of layered structure calcium carbonate under optical drive involves dissolving anhydrous calcium chloride into deionized water, stirring, adding methanol, sealing and subjecting into static crystallization.
AU FU Z
   TANG J
   XIE J
   TAN T
   CHI W
AE UNIV WUHAN TECHNOLOGY (UYWL-C)
GA 2019771673
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of layered structure calcium carbonate under optical drive comprises dissolving anhydrous calcium chloride into deionized water at 20-35 degrees C environment and stirring to obtain clear and transparent solution-A; dissolving rhodamine-b into de-ionized water at 20-35 degrees C environment and stirring to obtain clear and transparent solution-B; adding obtained solution-B into solution-A, adding methanol, continuously keeping at 20-35 degrees C environment, adding crystallization mother liquid and rhodamine-b at mol ratio of 7-10:4-10 mol/L and stirring to obtain clear and transparent solution-C; and adding obtained solution-C into crystallization mother liquid, adding 0.1-10% methanol, 1-50 mmol/L calcium chloride, sealing crystal mother liquid into sealed container, subjecting into whole carbon dioxide atmosphere and xenon lamp illumination and static crystallization.

PN CN109735486-A; CN109735486-B
TI Primary melanocyte culturing useful in study of            erythema effect ultraviolet UVB irradiation induced            cell premature aging, comprises e.g. soaking skin            sample in ethanol and removing residual ethanol and            removing fat and subcutaneous tissue.
AU GAO L
   YAN J
   LIU Q
   TIAN M
   TIAN Y
   LU X
   LI S
   CAI T
AE CHINESE DISEASE CONTROL & PREVENTION CEN (CCDC-C)
GA 2019451569
AB    NOVELTY - Primary melanocyte culturing comprises (i)                soaking the skin sample in ethanol and removing                residual ethanol on sample after removal; (ii)                removing fat and subcutaneous tissue, transferring                the skin sample to covered vessel, and adding                trypsin solution to incubate overnight; (iii)                incubating, discarding digestive juice, washing                skin sample with phosphate buffered saline                solution, and discarding waste liquid; (iv)                separating dermis and epidermis of skin, placing                epidermis in phosphate buffered saline solution and                incubating at room temperature for 30-60 minutes;                (v) centrifuging cell suspension, discarding                supernatant and washing precipitate with phosphate                buffered saline solution; (vi) resuspending                suspension in melanocyte culture medium,                transferring to culture vessel and culturing in                carbon dioxide incubator for 48-72 hours to obtain                primary melanocytes; (vii) continuing to culture                and periodically changing culture solution; and                (viii) subculturing and freezing.
   USE - The primary melanocyte culturing method is                useful in the study of erythema effect ultraviolet                UVB irradiation induced cell premature aging                (claimed).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the                  skin sample is a male foreskin sample and the                  skin sample is immersed in a 70% ethanol solution                  for a period of 10-30 minutes. In the step (ii),                  the trypsin solution concentration is 0.25% and                  the incubation is carried out in a refrigerator                  at 4 degrees C for 14-20 hours. The skin sample                  is cut into strips having a width of 1-2 mm. The                  drop of phosphate buffered saline solution is                  added to the epidermis when separating the dermis                  and epidermis of the skin. Preferred Method: In                  the step (iv), the incubation refers to shaking                  incubation at 80-150 revolutions/minute. In the                  step (v), the centrifugation is performed at 1500                  revolutions/minute for 3 minutes. In the step                  (vi), the melanocyte medium is M254 medium                  supplemented with 1% (final concentration) human                  melanocyte growth supplement and 1% (final                  concentration) double antibody, which are                  penicillin and streptomycin.

   WANG Q
AE JINAN PENGBO BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (JINA-Non-standard)
   JINAN JIUAN ESTERS CHEM IND CO LTD (JINA-Non-standard)
GA 201928658E
AB    NOVELTY - A stable choline solution comprises choline hydroxide, water and choline carbonate.
   USE - Stable choline solution used in application such as circuit board cleaning agent, material preparation, electrochemistry and organic synthesis.
   ADVANTAGE - The preparation method does not use toxic and harmful substances, and eliminates the emission of volatile organic compound (VOC) in the production process, without using a catalyst, and the reaction conditions are mild. The side reaction is eliminated obviously with choline carbonate as a stabilizer, and no other substances are introduced. The production process is stable and safe.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of stable choline solution, which involves (1), adding trimethylamine to water, stirring and mixing, introducing carbon dioxide, stirring while adding, the pressure is less than 2kgf/cm2, the temperature is 10-40 degrees C to obtain solution containing trimethylamine and trimethylammonium carbonate, (2) adding ethylene oxide to the solution prepared in the step (1), reacting trimethylamine, ethylene oxide and water to form choline hydroxide, reacting trimethylammonium carbonate, ethylene oxide and water to form choline carbonate, cooling down and filtering to obtain stable choline solution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Property: A concentration of choline carbonate in the choline solution is 15-20%. A molar ratio of trimethylamine to carbon dioxide in step (1) is (8.5-10):1. The trimethylamine in the step (1) is aqueous solution of trimethylamine having concentration of 40-50% or liquefied trimethylamine having purity of 99%. A molar ratio of the ethylene oxide described in the step (2) to the trimethylamine described in the step (1) is 1:1. The temperature drop in step (2) is reduced to 30-60 degrees C. The cooling in step (2) is cooling water cooling or ice water cooling. Preferred Process: The ethylene oxide in step (2) is added at a temperature of 30-60 degrees C. The ethylene oxide in the step (2) is liquefied ethylene oxide having concentration of 99%.

TI Method for reclaiming amine agents used for e.g. removing acid gases, involves controlling temperature of fluid comprising degradation products in vessel by providing one or more fluid input stream and variable steam input to vessel.
AU ANDRIAN D
   ALSHAER A A
AE SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
GA 201919652J
AB    NOVELTY - Method for reclaiming amine agents involves controlling the temperature of a fluid in a vessel (324) by providing 1 or more fluid input streams and variable steam input to the vessel. The fluid comprises 1 or more degradation products formed from the reaction of the amine agents with 1 or more gases.
   USE - Method for reclaiming amine agents used for removing acid gases during gas sweetening process, and scrubbing carbon dioxide from flue gas in post combustion capture process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method efficiently reclaims the amine agent by an enhanced process without losing the agents, and improves temperature stability of the fluid.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) method for controlling temperature of the fluid in the vessel; and
   (2) system (300) for reclaiming the amine agents.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram of the reclaimer system.
   Reclaimer system (300)
   Level controller (304)
   Temperature controller (310)
   Variable steam input subsystem (318)
   Vessel (324)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Condition: The temperature is either increased or decreased and maintained at 355-365 degrees F, preferably 360 degrees F. Preferred Components: The fluid input stream comprises lean amine agent stream and water stream. The amine agents comprise primary amine, secondary amine or tertiary amine, preferably diglycolamine, monoethanolamine, diethanolamine, diisopropanolamine, triethanolamine, methyldiethanolamine, 2-amino-2-methyl-1-propanol and piperazine.

TI Intelligent environment controlling system for henhouse, has information collecting module comprising sensor unit, control module comprising automatic controller, and sensor unit fixedly connected to automatic controller by sensor circuit.
AU HE R
AE CHENGDU ANHENGDA INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY (CHEN-Non-standard)
GA 201920260F
AB    NOVELTY - The system has an environment information collecting module comprising a sensor unit and a sensor circuit. A control module comprises a button manual controller and an automatic controller. The sensor unit is connected to the automatic controller by a sensor circuit. The sensor unit comprises a carbon dioxide sensor, an ammonia sensor, a dust sensor, a wind speed sensor, a humidity sensor and an illumination sensor. An adjusting module comprises a ventilating device, a heating device, a cooling device, a spraying device and a filling device. An auxiliary module comprises a human-computer interaction interface, an external clock, a General Packet Radio Service (GPRS) wireless transmitter and an upper computer.
   USE - Intelligent environment controlling system for a henhouse.
   ADVANTAGE - The system has reduces morbidity and mortality of chicken, improves quality of chicken product, and satisfies the requirement of animal welfare, and can conductive to the competitiveness of the farm in the market.

TI Joint system for building, has two separate structures that are arranged with vibration property, shock absorption property, and sound absorption property, and special sealing compound arranged with set of closed air pores.
AU 
AE ULBRICH R (ULBR-Individual)
GA 201916897B
AB    NOVELTY - The system has two separate structures that are arranged with a vibration property, shock absorption property, and sound absorption property. A special sealing compound is arranged with a set of closed air pores. A joint group is provided with a density that is different from the density of the separate structures. The joint group is provided with specific shore hardness. A set of offset units is arranged between the separate structures, where the set of offset units is provided with a set of specific vertical heights, and the joint system conforms to 2002/44/EG standards.

PN CN109337704-A
TI Reduction of oil content of oily sludge pyrolysis residue for use in e.g. fuel rod preparation, involves screening and crushing pyrolysis residue, stir-mixing with pyrolysis water and oxidizing agent, washing, and drying.
AU LIU L
   WANG J
AE INNER MONGOLIA GANGYUAN CHEM CO LTD (INNE-Non-standard)
GA 201917727C
AB    NOVELTY - Reduction of oil content of oily sludge pyrolysis residue, involves screening and crushing pyrolysis residue to a particle diameter of less than 15 mm, stir-mixing the pyrolysis residue, pyrolysis water, and an oxidizing agent to obtain a mixed slurry, adding 15-35 vol.% mixed slurry to a reaction kettle, stirring continuously for preset time at 100-600 degrees C, opening the reaction kettle, washing the pyrolysis residue, and drying to a water content of less than 30%.
   USE - Reduction of oil content of oily sludge pyrolysis residue for use in preparation of fertilizer, soil improvement agent, repairing agent, and fuel rod.
   ADVANTAGE - The method effectively reduces oil content of pyrolysis residue, and provides treated residue completely free of organic matter and high in carbon content. The carbon- and nitrogen-containing organic matter in the residue can be oxidized to environmentally-friendly carbon dioxide and nitrogen.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for system for reducing oil content of oily sludge pyrolysis residue.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The oxidizing agent is oxygen, hydrogen peroxide, nitric acid, and/or ammonium nitrate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mixing ratio of pyrolysis residue, pyrolysis water, and oxidizing agent is 1:4-9:0.01-0.2.

TI Polyethylene/polystyrene interpolymer composite material comprises polyethylene as outer layer and polystyrene as inner layer.
AU NI Z
   HOU R
   CHEN M
   MA Z
AE UNIV JIANGNAN (UYJN-C)
   WUXI DAOSHI NEW MATERIAL CO LTD (WUXI-Non-standard)
GA 2019124029
AB    NOVELTY - Polyethylene/polystyrene interpolymer composite material comprises polyethylene as outer layer and polystyrene as inner layer, the inner layer of polystyrene is grafted onto the molecular chain of polyethylene, the graft ratio is 1-5 wt.%, and the material is granular.
   USE - Used as polyethylene/polystyrene interpolymer composite material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: increases the distance between the polyethylene molecular chains; provides more free volume for St swell; and the material: has high graft ratio.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing composite material comprising (i) adding styrene, polyethylene and initiator into the reaction kettle, mixing uniformly, then adding dry ice at 30-50 degrees C with a pressure of 1-4 Mpa, swelling for 1-4 hours, and releasing carbon dioxide, (ii) adding water, dispersant, emulsifier and aqueous phase polymerization inhibitor mixture into the reaction kettle of step (i), then adding ice while stirring and heating to 85 degrees C for 4-6 hours, continuing heating at 90 degrees C for 2-3 hours, then reducing the temperature to 30-50 degrees C, washing the reaction product with ethanol and water and drying to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The polyethylene is resin obtained by hydrogenating styrene-butadiene block copolymer or styrene-butadiene block copolymer by twin-screw extrusion, its melting point is 105-115 degrees C, and the melt index is 0.9-2.5 g/minutes. Preferred Components: In step (i), the mass ratio between styrene, polyethylene and initiator is 30-70:70-30:0.03-0.7; and the initiator is peroxide initiator or azo initiator. The initiator is benzoyl peroxide or azobisisobutyronitrile in an amount of from 0.1-1.5 wt.% of the styrene. The used amount of dry ice is 4 times the mass of the polyethylene. In step (ii), the dispersing agent is kaolin, calcium phosphate, polyvinyl alcohol, methyl cellulose and/or gelatin, the emulsifier is sodium dodecylbenzenesulfonate or sodium lauryl sulfate; and the aqueous phase polymerization inhibitor is methylene blue. In step (ii), the mass ratio between water, dispersing agent and emulsifier is 100:3-7:0.5-1; the amount of water is 1-4 times the mass of styrene, and the used amount of aqueous phase polymerization inhibitor is 0.01-0.05 wt.% based on the styrene.

GA 201913325F
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of fertilizer comprises using nitrogen fertilizer, phosphate fertilizer, potassium fertilizer and potassium carbonate as main raw materials, using polyethylene glycol 6000 as protecting agent for potassium carbonate, and citric acid as foaming agent, and dry granulating.
   USE - Preparation of fertilizer.
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes the carbon dioxide formed by the reaction of alkaline potassium carbonate fertilizer and citric acid, so that the fertilizer rapidly disintegrates, and the nutrient rapidly penetrates into the soil with water, reducing the nutrient loss. The method does not produce pollution.

PN CN109212041-A
TI Remote real-time calibration method for transformer oil online monitoring device, involves automatically replacing obtained calibration value by original calibration value of online monitoring device to complete calibration.
AU ZHANG Y
AE SHANGHAI JINYI INSPECTION TECHNOLOGY CO (BAOS-C)
GA 201907958X
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves sampling the transformer oil and sending to the laboratory to perform gas chromatography analysis. The gas composition and concentration content of the transformer oil is detected to obtain the laboratory test data. The online detection data of gas components and concentration levels in transformer oil are obtained on the day. It is determined whether the online detection data of the transformer oil deviates from the laboratory test data at the same time. The correction value for the gas component exceeding the judgment standard, is automatically calculated. The transformer oil online monitoring device is automatically corrected according to the correction value. The obtained calibration value is automatically replaced by the original calibration value of the online monitoring device to complete the calibration. The transformer oil gas component comprises hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, methane, ethylene, ethane, and acetylene.
   USE - Remote real-time calibration method for transformer oil online monitoring device.
   ADVANTAGE - The accuracy of online monitoring data is ensured. The online monitoring device is operated efficiently in real time.

AB    NOVELTY - The preparation of high white aluminum hydroxide involves pretreating high alumina fly ash to obtain high aluminum fly ash clinker, mixing high aluminum fly ash clinker with adjusting liquid to obtain crude sodium aluminate solution, performing desiliconization treatment (I) on crude sodium aluminate solution by using sodium silicon slag to obtain desiliconization solution (I), performing desiliconization treatment (II) on desiliconization solution (I) by using calcium oxide to obtain sodium aluminate semen, dividing sodium aluminate semen into sodium aluminate semen (I) and sodium aluminate semen (II), introducing carbon dioxide gas into sodium aluminate semen (I) for carbonation decomposition to obtain aluminum hydroxide, and using aluminum hydroxide to perform seed decomposition in sodium aluminate semen (II). The temperature of desiliconization treatment (I) is 90-170 degrees C. The temperature of desiliconization treatment (I) is 30-90 degrees C.
   USE - Preparation of high white aluminum hydroxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of high white aluminum hydroxide with low energy consumption and few by-product by simple process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The adjusting liquid comprises 10-50 g/l caustic alkali, 10-50 g/l carbon alkali, and 10-50 g/l aluminum oxide. The liquid-solid ratio of adjusting liquid and high alumina fly ash clinker is (2-15):1. The mass-to-volume ratio of sodium silicon slag to sodium aluminate crude solution in desiliconization process (I) is (20-120) g:1 l. The mass to volume ratio of calcium oxide to desiliconization solution (I) in desiliconization treatment (II) is (2030) g:1 l. The concentration of carbon dioxide gas in the carbonation decomposition process is 10-100%. The seed coefficient of the seed crystal decomposition is (2-7):1. Preferred Process: The pretreatment process is carried out by performing sequentially pre-desiliconization treatment and soda lime sintering treatment of the high alumina fly ash. Preferred Conditions: The elution temperature is 20-90 degrees C and the elution time is 5-60 minutes. The time of desiliconization treatment (I) is 30-200 minutes. The time of desiliconization treatment (II) is 30-200 minutes. The carbonation decomposition temperature is 50-90 degrees C, and the carbonation decomposition time is 30-200 minutes. The temperature at which the seed crystal is decomposed is 30-70 degrees C, and the time at which the seed crystal is decomposed is 20-50 hours.

PN CN109022139-A
TI Preparing Prinsepia utilis Royle oil used e.g. for preventing cancer comprises crushing P. utilis Royle dry seeds, extracting, heating extraction- and separation-kettle, cooling, adjusting flow rate and collecting extracting liquid.
AU UNCOLUMN I
AE QIDONG CHUANGLU NEW MATERIAL CO LTD (QIDO-Non-standard)
GA 2018A51534
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing Prinsepia utilis Royle oil comprises crushing Prinsepia utilis Royle dry seeds, sieving through 30-mesh sieve, adding 10-20 pts. wt. powder into a extracting kettle, extracting with 85-95% ethanol as a cosolvent, heating the extracting kettle and separation vessel, cooling a storage tank, turning on the high pressure pump for pressurizing when heating the extraction kettle and the separation vessel at 44-48 degrees C and 38-42 degrees C and raising the pressure to 27-29 MPa and 4-5 MPa respectively, starting circular extraction, adjusting the flow rate of carbon dioxide to 51-53 kg/hour, stabilizing the system, collecting the extraction liquid from the separation kettle for every 30 minutes and extracting for 3-4 hours.
   USE - The Prinsepia utilis Royle oil is useful in preparing medicine for preventing cancer, oxidation and inhibiting bacteria.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple; does not leave any residual solvent; and does not destroy the active components.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The cosolvent has 90 vol.% ethanol. The flow rate of carbon dioxide is 52 kg/hour. Preferred Method: The high pressure pump is opened to pressurize when heating the extracting kettle and separating kettle to 46 degrees C and 40 degrees C respectively. The pressure during extracting and separating is increased to 28 MPa and 4.5 MPa respectively. The extraction is carried out for 3.5 hours.
   ACTIVITY - Cytostatic; Antibacterial.

GA 2018A0557A

The generating device is provided with slag discharging port for discharging petroleum coke slag and the saturated absorbent formed by reacting petroleum coke with the gasifying agent. The fresh absorbent is delivered from the absorbent return port to the generating device. The absorbent comprises calcium oxide, calcium hydroxide and/or magnesium oxide. The acidic gas comprises carbon dioxide and sulfide.

TI Preparation of graphene-based nitrogen-doped carbon layer composite used as cathode, involves solvothermally reacting phthalic anhydride and 2,6-diaminoanthraquinone in N-methylpyrrolidone graphene solution, and carbonizing.
AU HUANG Y
   CHANG B
   HAN S
   LIU F
   LIN H
   ZHANG X
   SUN Y
   ZHU L
   CHEN H
   WEI H
   KANG J
   CHEN Y
AE SHANGHAI INST TECHNOLOGY (SHGH-C)
GA 201887743H
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of three-dimensional graphene-based nitrogen-doped carbon layer composite material involves dehydrating graphene oxide raw material, dissolving in N-methylpyrrolidone solvent, adding phthalic anhydride and 2,6-diaminoanthraquinone to the graphene oxide solution, reacting phthalic anhydride and 2,6-diaminoanthraquinone in a N-methylpyrrolidone solution of graphene, polymerizing polyimide layer on the graphene framework through solvothermal method, and carbonizing carbon dioxide under nitrogen atmosphere.
   USE - Preparation of graphene-based nitrogen-doped carbon layer composite material used as cathode of sodium ion battery (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, economical and has mild reaction conditions, and provides composite material having excellent electrochemical performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for usage method of composite material, which involves assembling composite material into a lithium ion button-type semi-battery as a cathode material of sodium ion battery, uniformly mixing composite material, carbon black super-P and polyvinylidene fluoride in a weight ratio of 7:2:1, and uniformly coating mixture on a pure copper foil to prepare an anode.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The solvothermal method is carried out at 150-200 degrees C, preferably 180 degrees C for 12-24 hours, preferably 24 hours. The carbonization is carried out at 400-800 degrees C for 5-10 hours. Preferred Process: The electrolyte in the battery in obtained by dissolving sodium hexafluorophosphate in mixed solution of ethylene carbonate or dimethyl carbonate, and performing electrochemical test using button type semi-battery.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of the phthalic anhydride to 2,6-diaminoanthraquinone is 1:1.08. The mass ratio of the phthalic anhydride to graphene oxide is 4:1.

TI Nitrogen oxide adsorber catalyst for emission treatment system, comprises layer comprising platinum group metals containing alloy of rhodium and platinum, support material and inorganic oxide and is arranged on another layer, and substrate.
AU CHANDLER G R
   PHILLIPS P R
   PRITZWALD-STEGMANN J
   REID S D
   STREHLAU W
   PRITZVALD S YU
   STREHLAU V
AE JOHNSON MATTHEY PLC (JOHO-C)
   JOHNSON MATTHEY PLC (JOHO-C)
   JOHNSON MATTHEY PLC (JOHO-C)
   JOHNSON MATTHEY INC (JOHO-C)
   JOHNSON MATTHEY PLC (JOHO-C)
GA 201877416L
AB    NOVELTY - A nitrogen oxide adsorber catalyst comprises a layer (l1) comprising one or more platinum group metal(s) containing a mixture or alloy of rhodium and platinum, a cerium-containing support material and an inorganic oxide (i1), a layer (l2), and a substrate having an axial length. The layer (l1) is arranged on the layer (l2).
   USE - Nitrogen oxide adsorber catalyst is used for system for treating stream of combustion exhaust gas from lean-burn diesel engine (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The nitrogen oxide adsorber catalyst has improved nitrogen oxide storage and carbon dioxide conversion properties.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) emission treatment system, which involves fluid communicating the lean burn diesel engine with the nitrogen oxide adsorber catalyst; and
   (2) method of treating an exhaust gas from lean burn diesel engine, which involves contacting the exhaust gas with the nitrogen oxide adsorber catalyst or the emission treatment system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The weight ratio of rhodium to platinum is 1:10-10:1, preferably 1:1. The total loading of the mixture or alloy of rhodium and platinum is 0.5-50 g/ft3. The mixture or the alloy of rhodium and platinum is arranged on the cerium-containing support material. The cerium-containing support material is chosen from ceria, ceria-zirconia mixed oxide and alumina-ceria-zirconia mixed oxide, preferably ceria. The inorganic oxide (i1) is chosen from alumina, ceria, magnesia, silica, titania, zirconia, niobium oxide, tantalum oxides, molybdenum oxides and tungsten oxides, or its mixed oxides or composite oxides, preferably alumina. The layer (l1) is free of alkali metals, alkaline earth metals or rare earth metals, which are different from ceria, preferably free of palladium. The catalyst further comprises one or more additional layer(s). The layer (l1), the layer (l2) and/or one or more additional layer(s) are extruded to form a flow or filter substrate.

PN CN108576058-A; ZA202200640-A
TI Extracting regenerated fulvic acid low metamorphic lignite comprises e. g. taking low-denatured lignite from coal and preparing low-grade lignite coal sample, selecting surface-modified nano-copper catalyst and adding coal sample.
AU GONG G
   ZHANG Y
   ZHAO Y
   ZHENG H
   SHI Y
   DENG B
   ZHANG A
   MA L
   LIU W
   XU W
   ZHANG S
   LI Z
   WANG Z
   LIANG S
   LI H
   LU S
   ZHANG F
   LIU P
   CHEN X
   LI Y
AE UNIV CHINA MINING & TECHNOLOGY XUZHOU (UYMT-C)
   UNIV CHINA MINING & TECHNOLOGY BEIJING (UYMB-C)
   BAOQING COAL POWER & CHEM CORP CHN ENERGY (BAOQ-Non-standard)
GA 2018798807
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting regenerated fulvic acid low metamorphic lignite comprises (i) taking low-denatured lignite from coal and preparing low-grade lignite coal sample; (ii) selecting surface-modified nano-copper catalyst having particle diameter of 100-1000 nm and adding the coal sample; (iii) preparing the low metamorphic lignite coal sample containing hydrogen peroxide and glacial acetic acid in the mass ratio of 1-10:1-10:1-10 by using hydrogen peroxide, glacial acetic acid and the low metamorphic lignite coal sample, passing protective gas into low metamorphic lignite coal sample mixture and performing microwave induced catalytic oxidative decomposition; and (iv) adding distilled water enriched in carbon dioxide gas into the oxidatively decomposed low-denatured lignite coal-type mixture, stirring and separating the low metamorphic lignite coal sample mixture and drying to obtain fulvic acid solid.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting regenerated fulvic acid low metamorphic lignite.
   ADVANTAGE - The product has good market prospect.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), a low-metamorphic lignite coal sample with mass ratio of water to coal of 0.5:1-10:1 is prepared by adding pure water into low-grade metamorphic lignite, the low-denatured lignite is crushed into particles below 300 mesh before the addition of pure water. In the step (ii), the surface-modified nano-copper catalyst has particle size of 100-500 nm, the surface-modified nano copper and the coal sample in a weight ratio of 1:50,000-1,000,000. In the step (iii), the mass ratio of the low-metamorphic lignite coal sample prepared by the hydrogen peroxide, glacial acetic acid is 1-5:1-5:1-5, the hydrogen peroxide has mass percent concentration of 20-30% and the shielding gas is inert gas. Preferred Method: The microwave induced catalytic oxidative decomposition has microwave power of 300-1000 watts. The microwave induced catalytic oxidative decomposition time is 20-50 minutes.
DC E13 (Heterocyclics.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

TI Production of polymer e.g. nylon polymer or            fabric, involves providing solar energy to production            process and supplying provided solar energy to            thermochemical reactor to produce polymer or fabric, or            intermediate products.
AU HAUSSENER S
   MODESTINO M A
AE ECOLE POLYTECHNIQUE FEDERALE LAUSANNE (EPFL-C)
   EPFL ECOLE POLYTECHNIQUE FEDERALE LAUSAN (EPFL-C)
   ECOLE POLYTECHNICQUE FEDERALE LAUSANNE (EPFL-C)
GA 2018759732
AB    NOVELTY - Production of polymer or fabric involves                providing solar energy as input energy to the                production process, and supplying the provided                solar energy and/or energy converted from the                provided solar energy to at least one                electrochemical system (201) and/or at least one                solar thermochemical reactor to produce the fabric                or polymer, or intermediate products.
   USE - Production of polymer or fabric e.g. nylon                6,6, nylon 6,10, nylon 6/66 and nylon 1,6 polymer                or fabric (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is energy intense process requiring                petrochemical input and economical and has improved                efficiency without using fossil fuel or fossil                energy resources and reduced energy input, carbon                dioxide emission and pollution output.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for polymer                or fabric production system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                process and system for production of nylon polymer                using solar energy input.
   Electrochemical system (201)
   Solar-thermochemical reactors                (202,203,204)
   Adiponitrile (405)
   Hexamethylenediamine (407)
   Adipic acid (408)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The electrochemical                  system is used for production of adiponitrile                  (405). Adiponitrile is subjected to hydrogenation                  to produce hexamethylenediamine (407) in                  thermochemical reactor (202). Adiponitrile is                  subjected to hydrolysis to produce adipic acid                  (408) in reactor (203). Adipic acid is                  polymerized with hexamethylenediamine to produce                  nylon polymer in reactor (204).

TI Manufacture of printed material used as flexible packaging material, involves printing ink on film having preset nitrogen element concentration in film surface, and irradiating active energy ray, after printing.
AU SADAKUNI HIRONOBU
   OHKURA MASATOSHI
   INOUE TAKEJIRO
   SADAKUNI H
   OHKURA M
   INOUE T
   HIRONOBU S
   MASATOSHI O
   TAKEJIRO I
   OKURA M
AE TORAY IND INC (TORA-C)
   TORAY IND INC (TORA-C)
   TORAY IND INC (TORA-C)
   TORAY IND INC (TORA-C)
GA 2018718742
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of printed material involves printing an ink on a film (1) having a nitrogen element concentration of 0.5-10 atomic% in the film surface, and irradiating an active energy ray, after printing.
   USE - Manufacture of printed material used as flexible packaging material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables manufacture of printed material with excellent adhesiveness between ink and film, and suppressed reduction of transferability, when ink is printed on film substrate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for printer, which is capable of printing the ink on the film by corona discharge-treating the film in an air atmosphere in a mixture gas atmosphere having an oxygen concentration of 0.1 vol.% or less. The volume ratio of nitrogen with respect to carbon dioxide in the mixture gas atmosphere is 99.5/0.5-50/50.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic view of printer. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Film (1)
   Roll (2)
   Film introduction unit (3)
   Surface-treatment unit (5)
   Printing unit (7)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - IMAGING AND COMMUNICATION - Preferred Components: The ink is an active energy ray hardening-type printing ink containing a pigment (a), a resin (b) containing an ethylenically unsaturated group, a (meth)acrylate (c), and a photoinitiator. The active energy ray is a bright line having a wavelength of 350-420 nm emitted from a light-emitting diode or an electron beam. Preferred Process: Manufacture of printed material further involves kneading an amine ester compound in the film, and printing the ink on the film using a planographic printing plate of roll-to-roll system.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The component (d) contains acyl phosphine oxide compound.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The surface layer of the film is a layer containing a nitrogen compound containing a melamine compound or a urethane compound. The film is a polyester film or a polyamide film. The component (b) further contains a carboxyl group. The component (c) contains an alicyclic skeleton or 6-18C aliphatic skeleton, a rosin frame, and a hydroxyl group. Preferred Properties: The component (b) has an acid value of 30-250 mg potassium hydroxide/g. The thickness of the film is 30 mu m or less.

Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. PN CN108479835-A; CN108479835-B
TI Preparation of aerogel composite material useful            as photocatalyst for e.g. photoelectrocatalytic            decomposition of water, involves reacting diamine and            dianhydride, adding crosslinking agent, acetic            anhydride and catalyst and drying.
AU ZHAO X
   YI X
   TIAN S
AE NEW MATERIALS INST SHANDONG ACAD SCI (UYQQ-C)
GA 201870751S
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation method of aerogel composite                material involves reacting diamine and dianhydride                as precursor, adding crosslinking agent to the                mixture and then carbonizing at high temperature,                acidifying using hydrolyzed trimethoxysilane                coupling agent and then carrying out graft                copolymerization, dissolving in organic solvent,                adding acetic anhydride, pyridine as dehydrating                agent and catalyst to the mixture and drying.                Diamine, dianhydride and crosslinking agent are                used in a molar ratio of 1-2:0.5-2:0.001-0.1.                Diamine, dianhydride, crosslinking agent, acetic                anhydride and pyridine are used in a molar ratio of                1-2:0.5-2:0.001-0.1:6-16:6-16.
   USE - The method is useful for preparation of                aerogel composite material, which is useful as                photocatalyst for photoelectrocatalytic                decomposition of water, degradation of organic                pollutants and antibiotics, reduction of carbon                dioxide and selective organic synthesis                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple preparation of                aerogel composite material with high yield and                excellent flexibility.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for aerogel                composite material prepared by the method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Diamine is                  2,2'-diaminobenzidine. Dianhydride comprises                  biphenyltetracarboxylic dianhydride and diphenyl                  ether tetracarboxylic acid dianhydride. The                  crosslinking agent is                  1,3,5-tris(4-aminophenoxy)benzene. The organic                  solvent is N-methylpyrrolidone.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)
MC E12-C02; E31-N05D; J03-B01; J04-E04A; J04-E04C; J04-E11; A02-A00A; A04-F05; A05-F05; A08-D03; A08-M01D; A08-S02; A08-S08; A10-C03A; A10-C03C; A10-E05B; A12-W11; A12-W11F; A12-W11K

PT P
PN WO2018141911-A1; EP3577064-A1; US2019375633-A1; US10882743-B2
TI Converting hydrocarbons in hydrocarbon feed into            hydrogen and carbon phase by contacting hydrocarbons            with molten salt to crack hydrocarbons and produce            hydrogen, cooling and separating carbon phase, and            re-heating and recycling molten salt.
AU OCONNOR P
AE ANTECY BV (ANTE-Non-standard)
   CLIMEWORKS AG (CLIM-Non-standard)
   CLIMEWORKS AG (CLIM-Non-standard)
GA 201861429K
AB    NOVELTY - Hydrocarbons in a hydrocarbon feed are                converted into hydrogen and carbon phase by (a)                contacting hydrocarbons with molten salt at a                temperature (T1) of greater than or equal to 250                degrees C, preferably 500-1000 degrees C, where                hydrocarbons are cracked to produce hydrogen, (b)                cooling and separating solid or liquid carbon phase                from molten salt at a temperature (T2) below T1 and                below 500 degrees C, preferably less than or equal                to 100 degrees C, (c) and re-heating and recycling                molten salt to contacting step (a).
   USE - Conversion of hydrocarbons in a hydrocarbon                feed into hydrogen and carbon phase.
   ADVANTAGE - The process avoids the emission of carbon                dioxide (CO2), making the hydrogen produced in this                way a zero CO2 emission fuel, and which also                produces a carbon product produced having a use                value. Compared to other processes, the pressure in                this method is not critical, allowing the use of                atmospheric or ambient pressure for                convenience.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: Molten salt is a                  metal halide selected from zinc chloride (ZnCl2),                  aluminum trichloride (AlCl3), antimony                  trichloride (SbCl3), and/or their hydrates and                  blends, preferably zinc chloride tetrahydrate                  (ZnCl2.4H2O) preferably additionally comprising                  zinc oxide (ZnO). Molten salt further comprises                  dehydrogenation catalyst metals different from                  the metal in the molten salt, selected from                  nickel (Ni), iron (Fe), zinc (Zn), or copper                  (Cu), to enhance hydrogen yield in the process,                  preferably in the form of a metal-organic                  complex, like metal-alkyls, metal-oxides, or                  metal-chloride complex, preferably in an amount                  of less than or equal to 10 mol.%, preferably                  less than or equal to 3 mol.% of the metal in the                  molten salt. Dehydrogenation catalyst metals are                  supported on a solid carbon source or its                  precursor. Solid carbon source or its precursor                  for use as seed in the separation or as support                  for the catalyst metal are same or different                  compound selected from carbon fiber, lignin,                  cellulosic fiber, carbon nanofiber, or carbon                  nanotube. Carbon phase produced comprises carbon                  nanofiber or its precursor. Preferred Processes:                  Formation of hydrogen in step (a) is performed at                  less than or equal to 800 degrees C, where                  separated carbon phase produced in step (b) is                  further processed in a separate step at greater                  than or equal to 800 degrees C to enhance the                  formation of higher quality carbon materials. The                  method further includes reacting hydrogen with                  carbon dioxide (CO2) captured from air and/or                  flue gas to produce a hydrocarbon, preferably                  methanol. The method further includes using                  obtained carbon phase to produce greater than or                  equal to 1 products selected from carbon sol                  fertilizer, carbon black, carbon fibers, carbon                  nanofibers, or their precursors.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: Hydrocarbon to be                  converted is oil-based, preferably methane; or                  hydrocarbon to be converted is carbohydrate,                  preferably from biomass waste feed. Amount of                  hydrocarbon added in step (a) is 0.1-50,                  preferably 1-10 wt.%; and amount of molten salt                  is 0.1-50, preferably 1-60 wt.% relative to the                  total weight of the molten salt and hydrocarbon.                  Solid carbon source or its precursor is added in                  step (a) and/or (b) as a seed to enhance the                  separation of the carbon phase from the molten                  salt phase, where amount of solid carbon source                  or its precursor is 0.1-10 wt.%, preferably 0.1-3                  wt.% relative to the total weight of the molten                  salt and the hydrocarbon. Preferred Processes:                  Energy required in the process is produced by the                  combustion of hydrogen produced in the process                  and/or produced from renewable sources. Energy                  required in the process for heating and                  conversion is added via electromagnetic waves,                  such as microwaves. In step (b), anti-solvent is                  added to improve the separation between the                  molten salt phase and the carbon phase.                  Separation between molten salt and carbon phase                  is performed by extrusion and/or spinning and                  adding solvent, coagulant, or anti-solvent to                  facilitate the separation.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)
MC E05-U05B; E11-A01; E11-A02; E11-F03; E11-G02; E11-W; E31-A02C; H04-E06; J04-X; N02-A01; N02-C01; N02-D01; N03-F01; N06-F; N07-D03; N07-D04; N07-F02; N07-F07; N07-F08; N07-J
IP C01B-003/24; C01B-032/05; C01B-032/158; C01B-003/26; C01B-003/34; C01B-003/40; C01B-032/15; C07C-001/12; C07C-029/151; D01F-009/127

AE PEUGEOT CITROEN AUTOMOBILES SA (CITR-C)

   PEUGEOT CITROEN AUTOMOBILES SA (CITR-C)
GA 2018580925
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves performing specific                operating process of a thermal engine when abnormal                combustion is detected. The specific operating                process is authorized when predetermined target                ratio of maximum mass of nitrogen oxide is                determined. Traversed period is determined during                rolling of a motor vehicle. Air in a combustion                chamber is cleaned. The nitrogen oxide masses are                calculated during operation of the thermal engine                according to past durations or emitted carbon                dioxide masses. Average quantity of the nitrogen                oxide is estimated during activation of specific                operation mode.
   USE - Method for authorization process of abnormal                combustions in a thermal engine i.e. petrol engine,                of a motor vehicle.
   ADVANTAGE - The wealth of catalyst is maintained so as to                preserve optimal process of pollution emissions and                protect the engine by limiting phenomena of                abnormal combustions.

PT P
PN CN108265168-A; CN108265168-B
TI Laser melting of vermicular graphite cast iron surface involves preheating preprocessed vermicular graphite cast iron, spraying surface with composite absorbent organic solution, and irradiating to form laser fused layer.
AU SHI Y
   CHEN K
   LI Y
   LIU J
AE UNIV CHANGCHUN SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYCY-C)
GA 201857522F
AB    NOVELTY - Laser melting of vermicular graphite cast iron surface comprises grinding and cleaning vermicular graphite cast iron surface; preparing composite absorbent material powder and immersing it in organic solvent to prepare composite absorbent organic solution; preheating vermicular graphite cast iron; spraying the composite absorbent organic solution into preheated vermicular graphite cast iron to form laser absorbing layer; and irradiating vermicular graphite cast iron with carbon dioxide laser to form laser fused layer.
   USE - Laser melting of vermicular graphite cast iron surface.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces laser fused layer with high hardness and has good wear resistance, corrosion resistance and impact resistance under high temperature working environment. The laser absorbing layer used has better carbon removal effect on surface of vermicular cast iron, so that defects including pores and surface quality of laser fused layer are controlled and mechanical properties and oxidation resistance are enhanced.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of a process for laser melting of vermicular graphite cast iron surface (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The composite absorbent material powder comprises 1-2 wt.% potassium sulfate less than or equal to 0.20 wt.% tin, 0.5-1.0 wt.% molybdenum, 2-3 wt.% vanadium, 2-3 wt.% titanium, and balance potassium aluminum fluoride; or 1-2 wt.% potassium sulfate, less than or equal to 0.10 wt.% rhenium, 0.5-1.0 wt.% molybdenum, 2-3 wt.% boron, 2-3 wt.% titanium, and balance potassium hexafluoraluminate; or 1-2 wt.% potassium sulfate, less than or equal to 1.0 wt.% chromium, 0.5-1.0 wt.% molybdenum, less than or equal to 2.0 wt.% copper, 2-3 wt.% vanadium, and balance potassium hexafluoraluminate. Preferred Method: The preheating step is performed at 350 degrees C for 10 minutes. The irradiation step is performed under laser output power of 2500-2700 W, wide spot width of 10 mm, negative defocus of 150-250 mm, scanning speed of 3-7 mm/s, and argon as shielding gas to produce laser fused layer with thickness of 0.9-1.5 mm without defects and cracks. The spraying step involves uniformly spraying composite absorbent organic solution on surface of vermicular cast iron to form 0.4-0.6 mm laser absorbing layer, and drying laser absorbing layer. The method also involves wire cutting cast iron into vermicular cast iron sample; polishing by sand paper; cleaning surface with acetone; and drying.

PN TW201824605-A
TI Method for manufacturing organic light-emitting component, organic light-emitting component and organic light-emitting material thereof achieving the effect of reducing the pollution and damage of the environment.
AU LIN J
AE LIN J (LINJ-Individual)
GA 2018765901
AB    NOVELTY - This invention discloses a method for manufacturing an organic light-emitting component, an organic light-emitting component and an organic light-emitting material thereof. The method mainly comprises the following steps: fermenting a fibrous raw material or a starchy raw material to form a sugar substance, and decomposing the sugar substance into a short fiber material comprising alcohols, carbon dioxide, aldehydes, phenols, and benzene rings; then mixing the short fiber material with an adhesive to form a colloid; and finally applying the colloid to a carrier with a positive electrode line and a negative electrode line so as to form an organic light-emitting material on the carrier, and further forming an organic light-emitting component by combining the carrier and the organic light-emitting material. This invention may be used to manufacture the light-emitting component from an organic and non-polluting plant material, so as to achieve the effect of reducing the pollution and damage of the environment.

PN CN108056156-A
TI Fresh-keeping method of kiwi fruit involves            picking fruits, removing mechanically damaged fruits            and malformed fruit, placing pre-cooled kiwi fruit in            atmosphere-controlled storage and removing            ethylene.
AU CHEN Y
   DI S
   ZHONG M
AE BEIJING FURUITONG SCI & TECHNOLOGY CO LT (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 201841850Y
AB    NOVELTY - Fresh-keeping method of kiwi fruit comprises                picking fruits, removing mechanically damaged                fruits and malformed fruits, choosing the fruits                with relatively uniform maturity and size where                maturity is 7-8 and shipping airlocks within 3-5                hours after picking, placing kiwi fruit callus in                cold storage at 10-12 degrees C, pre-cooling for 24                hours, placing pre-cooled kiwi fruit in                atmosphere-controlled storage where oxygen volume                concentration is 1-2% and carbon dioxide volume                concentration is 1-2%, controlling temperature at                0.5-1.5 degrees C and relative humidity to 90%,                removing ethylene once every 8 hours at rate of 30                minutes/time, measuring the changes of fruit                firmness and soluble solid content periodically and                measuring the hardness with durometer.
   USE - Method for fresh-keeping kiwi fruit.
   ADVANTAGE - The method inhibits the decrease of hardness                of kiwi fruit and ripening of fruit, maintains                higher level of soluble solids content and prolongs                storage and preservation period of the kiwi                fruit.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: Each fruit is measured for                  2 values for 3 times and the average value of                  hardness of fruit (kg/cm2) and total soluble                  solids content are determined using digital sugar                  meter where each fruit was measured 2 values for                  3 times and average is taken. The kiwi fruit                  released from the warehouse is placed under the                  simulated shelf life at room temperature and the                  hardness, soluble solids, weight loss rate and                  sensory properties are evaluated and measured.                  The mature fruit with maturity of 7-8 is selected                  and transported to air-conditioned pool within 4                  hours after picking. The kiwi fruit is pre-cooled                  in cold storage at 12 degrees C for 24 hours. The                  atmosphere-controlled storage is operated at                  0.5-1.5 degrees C with relative humidity of 90%,                  oxygen volume concentration of 1.5% and carbon                  dioxide volume concentration of 1.5%. The                  ethylene removal is done once every 8 hours at                  rate of 30 minutes/time.
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).)

PN CN108034634-A; CN108034634-B
TI Separating mesenchymal stem cells, by centrifuging upper plasma of menstrual blood, adding solution I, centrifuging, suspending precipitate B in cell culture medium, culturing, and digesting and passaging cells.
AU PAN R
   DU X
   CHEN X
   DAI L
   WANG J
   CHEN Q
AE HANGZHOU S-EVANS BIOSCIENCES CO LTD (HANG-Non-standard)
GA 201839840R
AB    NOVELTY - Method for separating mesenchymal stem cells from menstrual blood, involves collecting menstrual blood, centrifuging the upper plasma to obtain precipitate, adding solution I to precipitate A, rapidly mixing the solution, maintaining the solution for 1-3 minutes, adding solution II, centrifuging the mixture to obtain precipitate B, suspending the precipitate B in a cell culture medium, culturing the medium at 37 degrees C in 5% carbon dioxide incubator, removing non-adherent cells after 48 hours, changing the fresh cell culture medium for every 2-3 days, and digesting and passaging to collect third generation of cells, preferably endometrium mesenchymal stem cells, where the solution I is 1-3 g/l aqueous solution of sodium chloride, the solution II is 15-17 g/l aqueous solution of sodium chloride, and the cell culture medium comprises alpha -minimum essential medium (MEM) and 2% serum replacement basic medium.
   USE - The method is useful for separating mesenchymal stem cells from menstrual blood (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple to perform and cost-effective.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Composition: The volume ratio of solution I and solution II is 1:1. The precipitate B is suspended in a cell culture medium, and seeded at density of 2x 105/cm2 into 25 cm2 plastic cell culture flask. The volume ratio of solution I to precipitate A is 3-5:1. The solution I is 2 g/L aqueous solution of sodium chloride. The solution II is 16 g/l aqueous solution of sodium chloride.
