PT P
PN WO2018079619-A1; CN109689857-A; EP3505618-A1; JP2018547730-X; US2019309328-A1; EP3505618-A4
TI New recombinant cell i.e. anaerobic archaea capable of expressing e.g. gene encoding isoprene synthase, and/or gene encoding phytoene synthase as first foreign gene, useful for producing specific isoprene or terpene.
AU FURUTANI MASAHIRO
   MATSUSHIMA KANA
   KAWABATA KAZUFUMI
   FURUTANI M
   MATSUSHIMA K
   KAWABATA K
   MASAHIRO F
   KANA M
   KAZUFUMI K
AE SEKISUI CHEM CO LTD (SEKI-C)
   SEKISUI CHEM CO LTD (SEKI-C)
   SEKISUI CHEM CO LTD (SEKI-C)
GA 201834161N
AB    NOVELTY - A recombinant cell i.e. anaerobic archaea is new, where the recombinant cell is capable of expressing the first foreign gene and producing 10C, 15C, 20C, 30C or 40C isoprene or terpenes. The recombinant cell has a gene encoding isoprene synthase, gene encoding monoterpene synthase, gene encoding sesquiterpene synthase, gene encoding diterpene synthase, gene encoding squalene synthase, and/or gene encoding phytoene synthase as first foreign gene.
   USE - The recombinant cell is useful for producing 10C, 15C, 20C, 30C or 40C isoprene or terpene (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The recombinant cell is produced in an easier and cost-effective manner with improved productivity without causing microbial contamination.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for producing isoprene or terpene, which involves contacting at least one carbon source chosen from carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, methane, methanol, and acetic acid with the recombinant cell, and causing the recombinant cell to produce 10C, 15C, 20C, 30C or 40C isoprene or terpenes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preparation: No preparation method is given. Preferred Cell: The recombinant cell is capable of growing using at least one chosen from carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, methane, methanol, and acetic acid, preferably carbon dioxide, more preferably methanol, most preferably methane as sole carbon source, and hydrogen as an energy source. The recombinant cell comprises a carbon monoxide dehydrogenase. The recombinant cell has function of synthesizing acetyl coenzyme (Co)-A from methyl tetrahydropterin, carbon monoxide, and CoA, and methanogenic activity. Preferred Organism: The recombinant cell belongs to Methanosarcina, Methanococcus, Methanothermococcus, Methanothermobacter, Methanothrix, Thermococcus, Thermofilum, Archaeoglobus, Methanosarcina, Methanococcus, or Methanothermococcus. The Methanosarcina is M.barkeri, M.mazei and M.acetivorans. The Methanococcus is M.voltae, M.vannielii and M.maripaludis. The Methanothermococcus is M.thermolithotrophicus. Preferred Biomolecules: The recombinant cell further comprises gene encoding carbon monoxide dehydrogenase as a second foreign gene, a gene encoding an isopentenyl diphosphate isomerase as a third foreign gene, a gene encoding an isopentenyl diphosphate isomerase as a fourth foreign gene, a gene encoding at least one chosen from geranyl diphosphate synthase and farnesyl diphosphate synthase as fifth foreign gene, a gene encoding isopentenyl diphosphate isomerase as a sixth exogenous gene, a gene encoding geranyl diphosphate synthase as a seventh foreign gene, a gene encoding neryl diphosphate synthase as the eighth exogenous gene, a gene encoding copalyl diphosphate synthase as a ninth exogenous gene, a gene encoding an isopentenyl diphosphate isomerase as the tenth foreign gene, a gene encoding at least one chosen from geranyl diphosphate synthase, farnesyl diphosphate synthase, and geranylgeranyl diphosphate synthase as eleventh foreign gene, a gene encoding an isopentenyl diphosphate isomerase as the twelfth foreign gene, a gene encoding at least one chosen from geranyl diphosphate synthase and farnesyl, a gene encoding an isopentenyl diphosphate isomerase as fourteenth foreign gene, a gene encoding at least one chosen from geranyl diphosphate synthase, farnesyl diphosphate synthase and geranylgeranyl diphosphate synthase as fifteenth foreign gene, and a gene encoding at least one chosen from acetoacetyl CoA thiolase, 3-hydroxy-3-methylglutaryl-CoA (HMG-CoA) synthase, HMG-CoA reductase, mevalonate-5-kinase, 5- phosphomevalonate decarboxylase and isopentenyl phosphate kinase as sixteenth foreign gene. The isoprene synthase is derived from a plant. The isoprene synthase is a protein comprising amino acid sequence of SEQ ID NO: 1, an amino acid sequence in which 1-20 amino acids are deleted, substituted or added in SEQ ID NO: 1, and amino acid sequence having 90% or more sequence identity with SEQ ID NO: 1, where the protein has isoprene synthase activity. The sesquiterpene synthase is a farnesene synthase, or cyclic sesquiterpene synthase. The first foreign gene is a gene encoding sesquiterpene synthase capable of producing 15C terpenes. The farnesene synthase is a protein comprising amino acid sequence of SEQ ID NO: 4, an amino acid sequence in which 1-20 amino acids are deleted, substituted or added in SEQ ID NO: 4, and amino acid sequence having 90% or more sequence identity with SEQ ID NO: 4, where the protein has farnesene synthase activity. The first foreign gene is expressed under constitutive expression promoter. The first exogenous gene is a gene encoding a monoterpene synthase i.e. cyclic monoterpene synthase. The cyclic monoterpene synthase is phellandrene synthase. The phellandrene synthase is a (i) protein comprising amino acid sequence of SEQ ID NO: 2, an amino acid sequence in which 1-20 amino acids are deleted, substituted or added in SEQ ID NO: 2, and amino acid sequence having 90% or more sequence identity with SEQ ID NO: 2, where the protein has alpha -phellandrene synthase activity, (ii) protein comprising amino acid sequence of SEQ ID NO: 3, an amino acid sequence in which 1-20 amino acids are deleted, substituted or added in SEQ ID NO: 3, and amino acid sequence having 90% or more sequence identity with SEQ ID NO: 3, where the protein has beta -phellandrene synthase activity. The first exogenous gene is a gene encoding diterpene synthase capable of producing 20C terpenes, where the diterpene synthase is a cyclic diterpene synthase. The first foreign gene is a gene encoding a squalene synthetase capable of producing 30C terpenes. The first exogenous gene is a gene encoding phytoene synthase capable of producing 40C terpenes. Preferred Method: The method involves culturing the recombinant cell using the carbon source, and obtaining 10C, 15C, 20C, 30C or 40C isoprene or terpenes from the culture. The method involves continuously supplying and discharging of the culture medium, where the culture medium has cell concentration during stationary growth phase of 1 g dry cell/l or more. The method involves supplying a mixed gas comprising carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, and hydrogen to a culture tank, and culturing the recombinant cells in the culture tank, where the mixed gas comprises 20% or more carbon monoxide, 5% or more carbon dioxide, and 10 parts per million (ppm) or less hydrogen sulfide. The method further involves adding methanol and/or acetic acid to the culture solution. Sequences not defined here may be found at ftp://ftp.wipo.int/pub/published_pct_sequences/publication.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN TW201811181-A; TW587787-B1
TI Prevention and cure bait for Forcipomyia taiwana and Aedes capable of enhancing human body mimicry and smell effect, thereby achieving a higher trapping effect.
AU SU Y
AE SU Y (SUYY-Individual)
GA 201874114M
AB    NOVELTY - The invention relates to a prevention and cure bait for Forcipomyia taiwana and Aedes, which is mainly formed by mixing a liquid bait and a solid bait. The liquid bait is a liquid preparation including oleic acid, emulsifier, borax, benzoic acid and water which are evenly mixed with a predetermined ratio. The solid bait includes stinky tofu, pigskin, pig liver, pork, live enzyme bacteria, borax and benzoic acid which are refined and evenly mixed with a predetermined ratio. Then, the solid bait is dried in low temperature for producing solid block-shaped preparation, thereby enabling the solid bait to absorb liquid bait for being softened into human flesh-like tissue. Furthermore, through heating with suitable temperature, the bait can release carbon dioxide to simulate the breath of human body, and disperse body temperature and smell of human body to attract mosquitoes to come foraging, so as to achieve a better attraction effect thereby culling, stickily killing or electrically killing the mosquitoes to further enhance the killing efficiency.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The                  isocyanate-reactive composition further comprises                  20-80 wt.% filler comprising a silicate,                  titanate, glass fiber, carbon black, graphite,                  calcium carbonate, talc, mica, and/or clay.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The turf and the                  fibers in the fiber bundles are made of synthetic                  material comprising acrylic polymer, nylon,                  polyester, polyurethane, and/or polyolefin. The                  polyether carbonate polyol is the addition                  reaction product of carbon dioxide and alkylene                  oxide onto a hydrogen-functional starter in the                  presence of multimetal cyanide catalyst. The                  isocyanate-reactive composition further comprises                  a polyether polyol (p2) other than polyether                  carbonate polyol, having nominal functionality of                  at least 3 and an average number of hydroxyl                  groups of 20-100. The isocyanate-reactive                  composition further comprises polyether diol                  other than polyether carbonate diol, and a chain                  extender having two isocyanate-reactive groups                  per molecule. The isocyanate-reactive composition                  further comprises an infill (70) comprising                  rubber particles. Preferred Composition: The                  polyether carbonate polyol comprises 3-35 wt.%,                  preferably 10-28 wt.% carbonate (calculated as                  carbon dioxide). The isocyanate-reactive                  composition preferably comprises 10-50 wt.%                  polyether carbonate polyol. The                  isocyanate-reactive composition comprises 20-80                  wt.% polyol (p2), 5-25 wt.% polyether diol, and                  5-25 wt.% chain extender. Preferred Properties:                  The chain extender has a molecular weight of                  60-490 g/mol, and the polyether diol has a number                  average molecular weight of at least 1000                  g/mol.

PT P
PN CN107549331-A
TI Edible oil useful for children and teenagers comprises peanut kernel, walnut kernel, sunflower seed, olive oil, perilla seed, sea-buckthorn seed, peony seeds, fish oil and cod-liver oil.
AU DIAO E
   ZHENG D
AE SHANDONG LUPAI INT FOOD CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201805066X
AB    NOVELTY - Edible oil comprises 500-600 pts. wt. peanut kernel, 100-200 pts. wt. walnut kernel, 100-200 pts. wt. sunflower seed, 50-100 pts. wt. olive oil, 30-50 pts. wt. perilla seed, 30-50 pts. wt. sea-buckthorn seed, 30-50 pts. wt. peony seeds, 10-20 pts. wt. fish oil and 2-5 pts. wt. cod-liver oil.
   USE - The edible oil is useful for children and teenagers (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The edible oil has: 893 kcal/100g calories, 10.6-12.2 wt.% saturated fatty acid content, 37-39.3 % oleic acid content, 39-39.8 % linoleic acid content, 7.4-8.2 % linolenic acid content, 5.5-5.9 mg/100g content docosahexaenoic acid (DHA) and 5.0-5.4 mg/100 g eicosapentaenoic acid content.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing edible oil comprising carrying out supercritical extraction, maintaining carbon dioxide flow rate of 30 l/h, maintaining pressure at 30 MPa at 50 degrees C for 2 hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The supercritical extraction comprises adding perilla seed, seabuckthorn seed kernel, peony seed, removing impurity, drying at 45 degrees C until the water content is less than 3.5%, crushing under 40-mesh sieve. The method further comprises hot pressing, cooking at 100 degrees C for 20 minutes, maintaining water content to 10-12 %, frying at 160 degrees C for 30 minutes, discharging at 110-120 degrees C, and the water content is reduced to less than 3%. The hot pressing comprises selecting raw material, removing stone, grading and screening, soybeans, crushing, steaming and frying, squeezing and filtering. The softening comprises adding peanut kernel, walnut kernel, sunflower seed, using 5 g/l sodium chloride solution for 10 seconds, taking out and using the packaging sealing bag at 55-58 degrees C, pre-heating for 10 minutes at 1 degrees C/minutes, heating at 70-72 degrees C, keeping for 2 minutes, reducing the temperature to 25 degrees C, adjusting the pressure to 0.15-0.18 MPa, keeping for 10 minutes, returning to the normal pressure. The squeezing method comprises adding raw material 2/5 the volume total area, closing tank, filling nitrogen, maintaining pressure of the nitrogen at 0.8-1.0 MPa, at 90-95 degrees C, squeezing press area is set at 75-78 degrees C, pressing pressure at 15 MPa for 30 seconds, reducing the squeezing pressure to 12 MPa for 5-6 minutes.
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).)
MC D03-C01
IP A23D-009/007; A23D-009/04

AU ZHANG J
   MA Y
   REN T
   WANG L
   LI G
AE UNIV HENAN (UYEH-C)
GA 201804899B
AB    NOVELTY - N, N-dialkyl asymmetric pyrazole ionic liquid comprises pyrazole derivatives (I-V), where X is bromide or iodine.
   USE - N,N-dialkyl asymmetric pyrazole ionic liquid used for catalytically synthesizing ring-shaped carbonic ester (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The N,N-dialkyl asymmetric pyrazole ionic liquid is easy to prepare and environment-friendly under mild reaction condition, has high catalyst activity, exists in solid state at room temperature, increases large viscosity, and has yield of cyclic carbonate is 91.1%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - N, N-dialkyl asymmetric pyrazole ionic liquid comprises pyrazole derivatives thats are represented by group of formulae (I-V), where X is bromide or iodine. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for synthesizing ring-shaped carbonic ester, which involves adding pyrazole ionic liquid and epoxy compound in molar ratio of 1:100-500 into the reaction kettle, introducing carbon dioxide at pressure of 0.5-4 mega Pascal at 90-150 degrees C constant temperature for 1-5 hours, and post-treating to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The molar ratio of the pyrazolium ionic liquid to the epoxy compound is 1:100-300.R=H, CH3, CH2Cl, C6H5, CH2OH, CH2OCH2CH=CH2 Or CH2OC6H5.

PN CN107541294-A
TI Hydrocarbon gasification system, has shell-and-tube type raw material heat exchanger equipped with feeding pipeline of hydrocarbon raw material component, and top outlet connected with high-pressure flash tank.
AU DONG H
   ZHANG Q
AE GE SHENHUA GASIFICATION TECHNOLOGY CO (GESH-Non-standard)
GA 2018042205
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a shell-and-tube type raw material heat exchanger equipped with a feeding pipeline of a hydrocarbon raw material component. A top outlet is connected with a high-pressure flash tank. A gasification heat exchanger is connected with the raw material heat exchanger and arranged on an air inlet pipeline of the high-pressure flash tank. The raw material heat exchanger is provided with a high-pressure metering feed pump for increasing pressure of the hydrocarbon raw material component. A hydrocarbon gasification system body exchanges heat and increases temperature of gasifying agent.
   USE - Hydrocarbon gasification system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system reduces gasification agent, carbon dioxide emission, energy consumption and water consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The gasification heat exchanger is shell-and-tube type, sleeve type, fin-tube type, a tube type and plate types heat exchanger. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a flash gas hydrocarbon gasification system internal heat integrated utilization method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a hydraulic circuit diagram of a hydrocarbon gasification system.

TI Sliding element e.g. piston ring for internal            combustion engines, has sliding layer which comprises            nanoparticles of graphite incorporated within            predetermined combined matrix.
AU AVELAR ARAUJO J
   DA ROCHA MORDENTE P J
   AVELAR A J
   DA ROCHA M P J
   JOSE D R M P
   ARAUJO J A
AE MAHLE METAL LEVE SA (MAHL-C)
   MAHLE INT GMBH (MAHL-C)
   MAHLE INT GMBH (MAHL-C)
   MAHLE INT GMBH (MAHL-C)
   MAHLE METAL LEVE SA (MAHL-C)
   MAHLE METAL LEVE SA (MAHL-C)
GA 2018010953
AB    NOVELTY - The sliding element (10) has a base material                (12) which comprises an annular external surface                (11). A bonding layer (13) and a sliding layer (14)                composed of hard amorphous carbon (DLC) of a                combined matrix with Sp3/Sp2 bonds are deposited                sequentially upon the annular external surface. The                sliding layer comprises nanoparticles of graphite                incorporated within the combined matrix of                Sp33/Sp2.
   USE - Sliding element e.g. piston ring for internal                combustion engines (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The high hardness is ensured. The reduction in                friction, internal stresses and wear is realized.                The high durability of the piston ring is ensured.                The excellent performance and low emissions of                carbon dioxide is ensured. The coating is prevented                from experiencing incompatibilities with oil                additives.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for coating a sliding element for internal                combustion engine.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a cross-section of the                piston ring revealing the structure of the                coating.
   Sliding element (10)
   Annular external surface (11)
   Base material (12)
   Bonding layer (13)
   Sliding layer (14)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - The bonding layer is composed of one of the                  materials from among metallic chromium having a                  centered cubic structure, or nickel, or cobalt or                  tungsten, or chromium carbide (CrC).

GA 2022D15382
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves contacting liquid oil                stream with a 002-lean gas, so that a liquid oil                stream is produced with reduced 002 content and a                CO2-rich gas. The liquid oil stream is the liquid                phase of a production well stream from an oil                reservoir. The lean gas is a hydrocarbon gas. The                CO2-lean gas comprises 1 to 10 mol% 002, preferably                2 to 5 mark 002.
   USE - Method for treating liquid oil stream.
   ADVANTAGE - The required dilution gas rate is reduced when                the stripping gas rate is increased. The content in                the feed stream is reduced to a level which handled                by the existing processing facility. The content                creates a new facility upstream of the existing                production facility is reduced, and which employs a                gas/liquid separator operating at reduced pressure                to separate the gas phase from a well stream.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an                apparatus for treating liquid oil stream.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram of the                method for treating liquid oil stream.

PT P
PN CN115010944-A
TI Preparing metal-organic framework structure            material by using copper alkaline carbonate, involves            adding alkaline carbonate of copper into solution            containing acidic organic ligand for reaction, reacting            and washing, drying and filtering to obtain solid            product.
AU DONG L
   TAN L
   ZHANG L
   ZHOU C
   LI W
AE UNIV CHONGQING (UYCQ-C)
GA 2022B90242
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a metal-organic framework structure                material by using copper alkaline carbonate,                involves adding alkaline carbonate of copper into a                solution containing an acidic organic ligand for                reaction, reacting for 6-120 hours at                15-100&#8451;, and washing, drying and                filtering to obtain a solid product.
   USE - Method for preparing metal-organic framework                structure material by using copper alkaline                carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses basic carbonate of copper as a                template, through the in-situ reaction of the basic                carbonate of copper and the acidic organic ligand,                to obtain metal-organic framework materials (MOFs),                so that to complete the reaction without requiring                high temperature, and also has relatively mild                reaction conditions, short reaction time, and the                operation is simple and easy. The reaction products                are only carbon dioxide and water, no other                impurities, and does not cause further problems,                such as environmental pollution.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                metal-organic framework structure material prepared                by the method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alkaline carbonate                  of copper comprises chalcopyrite or malachite.                  The acidic organic ligand comprises phthalic                  acid, 2, 5-dihydroxyterephthalic acid,                  2-hydroxyterephthalic acid, 2-nitroterephthalic                  acid, 2-aminoterephthalic acid, 2,3,5,                  6-tetrachloroterephthalic acid, 2,3,5,                  6-tetrafluoroterephthalic acid, 2,                  5-dichloroterephthalic acid, 2,                  5-dibromoterephthalic acid, or 2,                  5-difluoroterephthalic acid. The solvent in the                  solution containing the acidic organic ligand                  comprises a mixed solvent formed by water,                  ethanol or N, N-dimethylformamide. The solvent                  used in washing process is ethanol, methanol or                  N, N-dimethylformamide. Preferred Compositions:                  The volume of component in the mixed solvent is                  not less than 20 vol.% total volume of the mixed                  solvent. The molar volume concentration of the                  acidic organic ligand in the solution containing                  the acidic organic ligand is 0.02-2.0 mol/L. PN CN114181896-A
TI Preparing nutrient solution for umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells, comprises e.g. isolating mesenchymal stem cells from corresponding tissue of human, adding fetal bovine serum-containing alpha-MEM medium, culturing mesenchymal stem cells, collecting cell suspension, and centrifuging.
AU WANG X
   ZHAO Y
   GAO H
   LI L
   LIU S
   HOU Z
   MENG M
AE HOU Z (HOUZ-Individual)
   MENG M (MENG-Individual)
GA 2022437266
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nutrient solution for umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells, comprises (1) isolating mesenchymal stem cells from the corresponding tissue of human or mammalian umbilical cord tissue, (2) adding 10% fetal bovine serum-containing alpha-MEM medium to the mesenchymal stem cells in the above steps, and culturing them for 3 days at 37degrees Celsius with 5% carbon dioxide, replacing the complete culture medium every 3 days, and obtaining the desired stem cells by passage, and (3) culturing the mesenchymal stem cells for 2-3 generations, adding 3 ml 0.25% trypsin, adding 10 ml of alpha-MEM medium containing fetal bovine serum to terminate the digestion, collecting the cell suspension, and centrifuging at normal room temperature for 5 minutes and the obtained supernatant is the cell nutrient solution.
   USE - The method for preparing nutrient solution for umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells useful to prepare the facial mask (claimed).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: In step (1), the mesenchymal stem cells are umbilical cord blood mesenchymal stem cells after 2-3 passages in vitro. The step (1), specifically comprises (1a) storing the obtained umbilical cord tissue in phosphate buffered saline buffer containing double antibody and bring back to the laboratory, (1b) rinsing the above-mentioned umbilical cord with phosphate buffered saline containing double antibody for more than 3 times to remove the blood on the surface of the umbilical cord, (1c) after removing the umbilical artery and umbilical vein, cutting into pieces of 1 cm size, (1d) placing the tissue block into a sterile 50 ml centrifuge tube, adding 15 ml of 0.1% type I collagenase, digesting in a 37degrees Celsius water bath for 1 hour, and shaking every 10 minutes, centrifuging at 1500 revolutions/minute for 5 minutes, discarding the supernatant, adding 1 mg/ml collagenase type I, digesting in a 37degrees Celsius incubator for 16 hour, (1e) adding 4 ml of alpha-MEM medium containing fetal bovine serum to terminate the digestion, at room temperature at 1500 revolutions/minute for 5 minute, discarding the supernatant, digesting the pellet with 2.5 g/l trypsin for 30 minutes, adding alpha-MEM medium containing fetal bovine serum to terminate the digestion, filtering with a 100-mesh sieve, and culturing in a carbon dioxide cell incubator with a volume fraction of 0.05 at 37degrees Celsius to obtain mesenchymal stem cells. The step (2) specifically comprises (2a) when the cells reach 80-90% confluence, the subculture can be carried out, aspirating the medium, and washing the cell surface twice with phosphate buffered saline, (2b) adding 3 ml 0.25% trypsin, and digesting for 1-2 minutes, adding 10 ml alpha-MEM medium containing 10% fetal bovine serum to terminate the digestion, and resuspending the cells repeatedly with a pipette liquid, centrifuging the cell suspension at 1000 revolutions/minute for 5 minute at room temperature, and using the maximum braking system to reduce the speed, discarding the supernatant and gently blowing off the precipitated cell mass, resuspending cells in alpha-MEM medium containing 10% fetal bovine serum and seeded in culture flasks, culturing the cells in a 37degrees Celsius, 5% carbon dioxide cell incubator for 3 days, replacing every 3 days, and passaging to obtain the desired stem cells. The step (3) specifically comprises (3a) culturing the mesenchymal stem cells for 2-3 generations, operating when the cells are most vigorously divided, (3b) adding 3 ml 0.25% trypsin, and digesting for 1-2 minutes, adding 10 ml alpha-MEM medium containing fetal bovine serum to terminate the digestion, and resuspending the cells repeatedly with a pipette, and collecting the cell suspension, and (3c) centrifuging the cell suspension at 1000 revolutions/minute for 5 minute at room temperature, reducing the speed with the maximum braking system, and collecting the supernatant for storage. The mask is prepared by immersing a piece of biological fiber cloth in cell culture solution.

GA 2021F0878T
AB    NOVELTY - Partial reduction of vanadium pentoxide using an ammonia solution involves preparing an ammonium vanadate powder by leaching vanadium pentoxide powder with an aqueous alkali salt solution of sodium hydroxide or potassium, separating by filtration, and reacting with an ammonia solution, heating the ammonium vanadate powder to 560-720 degrees C in an electric furnace or a horizontal furnace under nitrogen or argon atmosphere, and maintaining the heating temperature for 5 minutes to 2 hours for partial reduction to vanadium dioxide, and cooling the reduced vanadium dioxide powder to 50-120 degrees C under nitrogen or argon atmosphere in an electric furnace or a horizontal furnace.

PN EP3919606-A1; EP3919606-A4
TI Producing algal biomass for obtaining biomethane, involves applying all growth parameters applied in laboratory setting as well as intensive mixing, and applying specific wavelengths of artificial light at night and low sunlight periods.
AU 
AE ALGAE & ALGAE TECHNOLOGIES LTD (ALGA-Non-standard)
   ALGAE & ALGAE TECHNOLOGIES LTD (ALGA-Non-standard)
GA 2021D99560
AB    NOVELTY - Producing algal biomass involves applying all the growth parameters applied in a laboratory setting as well as intensive mixing. The specific wavelengths of artificial light are applied at night and low sunlight periods to lengthen the daily period of growth. Different strains of a chosen microalgae species are identified which will flourish in the local climatic conditions. The carbon dioxide is recycled from the biogas scrubbers to be fed into the raceways to promote maximum algae growth. The pulsed magnetic field (pmf) is applied to the algae to accelerate growth and preferentially favor lipid production.
   USE - Method for producing algal biomass for obtaining biomethane.
   ADVANTAGE - The method shows high yield industrial scale production of algal biomass, promotes a good cell density (stimulation of growth and metabolic cascades) by controlling biochemical pathways by pulsed magnetic field, and enhances phototropic cellular communication leading to higher percentages of light harvesting primary and accessory pigments.
